रूप्यकोपहितैः those inlaid with silver, चित्रैः by wonderful, हेमभूषितैः decorated with gold, तोरणैः archways, विचित्राभिः with colourful, कक्ष्याभिः with apartments, रुचिरैः with beauteous, द्वारैश्च with entrances, वृतम् surrounded by

#### Explanation

He saw beautiful silver archways decorated with gold, colourful inner apartments with beauteous entrances.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

गजास्थितैर्महामात्रैः शूरैश्च विगतश्रमैः। उपस्थितमसंहार्यैः र्हयैः स्यन्दनयायिभिः।।5.6.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

गजास्थितैः mounted on elephants, शूरैः by drivers, विगतश्रमैः unwearied, महामात्रैः great, असंहार्यैः irresistible, हयैः with horses, स्यन्दनयायिभिः  chariotriders, उपस्थितम् kept ready

#### Explanation

(He saw) archways guarded by riders mounted on majestic elephants and unwearied riders of chariots drawn by horses of irresistible speed kept ready.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

सिंहव्याघ्रतनुत्राणैर्दान्तकाञ्चनराजतैः। घोषवद्भिर्विचित्रैश्च सदा विचरितं रथैः।।5.6.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सिंहव्याघ्रतनुत्राणैः covered with skins of lions and tigers, दान्तकाञ्चनराजतैः encrusted with images of ivory, gold and silver, घोषवद्भि: ringing, विचित्रैः by wonderful, रथैः with chariots, सदा allover, विचरितम् moving about

#### Explanation

There were wonderful chariots covered with skins of lions and tigers, encrusted with ivory, gold and silver with ringing bells moving about.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

बहुरत्नसमाकीर्णं परार्थ्यसनभाजनम्। महारथसमावासं महारथमहास्वनम्।।5.6.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

बहुरत्नसमाकीर्णम् with many precious gems, परार्थ्यासनभाजनम् with excellent seats and vessels, महारथसमावासम् having halting places for big  chariots, महारथमहास्वनम् filled with deep sounds of great charioteers.

#### Explanation

The palace was embellished with excellent seats and vessels with many precious gems. There were spacious places to accommodate big chariorts filled with shouts of great charioteers.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

दृश्यैश्च परमोदारैस्तैस्तैश्च मृगपक्षिभिः। विविधै र्बहुसाहस्रैः परिपूर्णं समन्ततः।।5.6.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दृश्यैः beautiful, परमोदारैः pleasing, विविधैः of many kinds, बहुसाहस्रैः in many thousands, तैस्स्सैः by different, मृगपक्षिभिः with beasts and birds, समन्ततः all over, परिपूर्णम् filled

#### Explanation

It was pleasing with several kinds of beautiful beasts in their thousands all over.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

विनीतैरन्तपालैश्च रक्षोभिश्च सुरक्षितम्। मुख्याभिश्च वरस्त्रीभिः परिपूर्णं समन्ततः।।5.6.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विनीतैः by disciplined, अन्तपालैः by guards, रक्षोभिः by demons, सुरक्षितम् well guarded, मुख्याभिः important, वरस्त्रीभिः by beautiful shedemons, समन्ततः everywhere, परिपूर्णम् filled

#### Explanation

The place was full of beautful women, carefully protected by disciplined guards as well as demons.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

मुदितप्रमदारत्नं राक्षसेन्द्रनिवेशनम्। वराभरणसंह्रादैः समुद्रस्वनन्निस्वनम्।।5.6.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मुदितप्रमदारत्नम् jewels of joyous women, राक्षसेन्द्रनिवेशनम् the residence of the lord of demons, वराभरणसंह्रादैः by the jingling of ornaments and accessories, समुद्रस्वनन्निस्वनम् like the sound of the sea.

#### Explanation

Full of the jewels of joyous women, the residence of the lord of demons reverberated with the jingling of their ornaments and accessories like the sound of the sea.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

तद्राजगुणसम्पन्नं मुख्यैश्चागुरुचन्दनैः। महाजनैः समाकीर्णां सिंहैरिव महद्वनम्।।5.6.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राजगुणसम्पन्नम् endowed with royal traits, मुख्यैः with eminent demons, अगुरुचन्दनैः with the fragrance of agaru and sandal, सिंहैः by lions, महत् वनमिव like a huge forest, महाजनैः by great people, समाकीर्णम् full.

#### Explanation

The place was full of eminent demons with royal traits like a dense forest infested with lions. It carried the fragrance of agaru and sandal of good quality.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

भेरीमृदङ्गाभिरुतं शङ्खघोषनिनादितम्। नित्यार्चितं पर्वहुतं पूजितं राक्षसैः सदा।।5.6.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भेरीमृदङ्गाभिरुतम् filled with the sounds of trumpets, mridangam and conches and percussion, शङ्खघोषनिनादितम् echoed with sounds of conches, नित्यार्चितम् daily worshipped, पर्वहुतम् with sacrifices performed on new and fullmoon days, सदा ever, राक्षसैःby demons, पूजितम् worshipped

#### Explanation

Reverberating with the sounds of trumpets, mridangam and conches, there the demons daily performed worships and sacrifices on new and fullmoon days.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

समुद्रमिव गम्भीरं समुद्रमिव निस्स्वनम्। महात्मनो महाद्वेश्म महारत्नपरिच्छदम्।।5.6.13।। महारत्नसमाकीर्णं ददर्श स महाकपिः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

समुद्रमिव resembling the sea, गम्भीरम् deep, निस्स्वनम् sound, समुद्रमिव sealike, महारत्न परिच्छदम् gemstudded ornaments, महारत्न समाकीर्णम् full of precious gems, महात्मनः of the great self, महत् great, वेश्म residence, सः that(Hanuman), महाकपिः the great vanara, ददर्श saw

#### Explanation

The place produced deep sounds like the sea. Those mighty ogres wore gemstudded ornaments. The great vanara saw the residence of the great self, Ravana rich with precious gems.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

विराजमानं वपुषा गजाश्वरथसङ्कुलम्।।5.6.14।। लङ्काभरणमित्येव सोऽमन्यत महाकपिः। चचार हनुमांस्तत्र रावणस्य समीपतः।।5.6.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वपुषा in appearance, विराजमानम् was present, गजाश्वरथसङ्कुलम् full of elephants, horses and chariots, लङ्काभरणम् इत्येव considered the very jewel of Lanka, सः that, महाकपिःgreat vanara, अमन्यत thought, हनुमान् Hanuman, तत्र there, रावणस्य Ravana's, समीपतः near by, चचार moved

#### Explanation

Hanuman, the great monkey, saw the royal mansion looking brilliant with elephants, horses and chariots. He thought that the palace was the very ornament of Lanka. He moved unnoticed into the close quarters of Ravana.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

विराजमानं वपुषा गजाश्वरथसङ्कुलम्।।5.6.14।। लङ्काभरणमित्येव सोऽमन्यत महाकपिः। चचार हनुमांस्तत्र रावणस्य समीपतः।।5.6.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वपुषा in appearance, विराजमानम् was present, गजाश्वरथसङ्कुलम् full of elephants, horses and chariots, लङ्काभरणम् इत्येव considered the very jewel of Lanka, सः that, महाकपिःgreat vanara, अमन्यत thought, हनुमान् Hanuman, तत्र there, रावणस्य Ravana's, समीपतः near by, चचार moved

#### Explanation

Hanuman, the great monkey, saw the royal mansion looking brilliant with elephants, horses and chariots. He thought that the palace was the very ornament of Lanka. He moved unnoticed into the close quarters of Ravana.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

गृहाद्गृहं राक्षसानामुद्यानानि च वानरः। वीक्षमाणोऽह्यसंत्रस्तः प्रासादांश्च चचार सः।।5.6.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसानाम् of the demons', गृहात् गृहम् from one house to the other, उद्यानानि च and pleasure gardens, वानर: vanara, वीक्षमाणः observing, असंत्रस्तः undaunted, प्रासादांश्च and the mansions, चचार ranged

#### Explanation

Hanuman ranged undaunted from one mansion to another including the pleasure gardens.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

अवप्लुत्य महावेगः प्रहस्तस्य निवेशनम्। ततोऽन्यत्पुप्लुवे वेश्म महापार्श्वस्य वीर्यवान्।।5.6.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महावीर्यः mighty, महावेगः of great speed, प्रहस्तस्य Prahasta's, निवेशनम् house, अवप्लुत्य jumping from there, ततः then, महापार्श्वस्य Mahaparshva, अन्यत्  other, वेश्म houses, पुप्लुवे leaped

#### Explanation

Courageous and swift, Hanuman sprang from Prahasta's house to Mahaparshva's, and so on.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

अथ मेघप्रतीकाशं कुम्भकर्णनिवेशनम्। विभीषणस्य च तदा पुप्लुवे स महाकपिः।।5.6.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथ and then, सः that, महाकपिः great vanara, मेघप्रतीकाशाम् appearing like a cloud, कुम्भकर्णनिवेशनम् the residence of Kumbhakarna, तदा then, विभीषणस्य Vibhisana's, पुप्लुवे leaped

#### Explanation

Then the great vanara leaped from the residence of Kumbhakarna which looked like a cloud to Vibhishana's.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

महोदरस्य च गृहं विरूपाक्षस्य चैव हि। विद्युज्जिह्वस्य भवनं विद्युन्मालेस्तथैव च।।5.6.19।। वज्रदंष्ट्रस्य च तथा पुप्लुवे स महाकपिः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः that, महाकपिः great vanara, महोदरस्य Mahodara's, गृहं च home also, विरूपाक्षस्य चैव हि Virupaksha's home also, विद्युज्जिह्वस्य Vidyujjihva's, तथैव च likewise, विद्युन्मालेः Vidyunmali's, तथैव likewise, वज्रदंष्ट्रस्य Vajradanstra's, भवनम् mansion, पुप्लुवे leaped

#### Explanation

The great vanara leaped from Mahodara's to Virupaksha's, from Vidyujjihva's to Vidyunmali and then to Vajradanstra's.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

शुकस्य च महातेजाः सारणस्य च धीमतः।।5.6.20।। तथा चेन्द्रजितो वेश्म जगाम हरियूथपः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महातेजाः brilliant one, हरियूथपः leader of monkeys, शुकस्य Suka's, धीमतः of the intelligent, सारणस्य Sarana's, तथा similarly, इन्द्रजितः Indrajit's , वेश्म residence, जगाम went

#### Explanation

The brilliant leader of monkeys bounded to the house of Suka, to the clever Sarana's and to Indrajit's.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

जम्बुमालेः सुमालेश्च जगाम हरिसत्तमः।।5.6.21।। रश्मिकेतोश्च भवनं सूर्यकेतोस्तथैव च। वज्रकायस्य च तथा पुप्लुवे स महाकपिः।।5.6.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हरिसत्तमः the goble vanara, जम्बुमालेः Jambumali's, सुमालेश्च and Sumaali's, जगाम reached, सः he, महाकपिः great monkey, रमशिकेतोः Rashmiketu's, तथैव च and so also, सूर्यकेतोः Suryaketu's, तथा likewise, वज्रकायस्य Vajrakaya's, भवनम् mansion, पुप्लुवे jumped over

#### Explanation

The noble vanara jumped from Jambumali's to Sumali's and from  Rashmiketu's, Suryaketu's and to Vajrakaya's residence.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

जम्बुमालेः सुमालेश्च जगाम हरिसत्तमः।।5.6.21।। रश्मिकेतोश्च भवनं सूर्यकेतोस्तथैव च। वज्रकायस्य च तथा पुप्लुवे स महाकपिः।।5.6.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हरिसत्तमः the goble vanara, जम्बुमालेः Jambumali's, सुमालेश्च and Sumaali's, जगाम reached, सः he, महाकपिः great monkey, रमशिकेतोः Rashmiketu's, तथैव च and so also, सूर्यकेतोः Suryaketu's, तथा likewise, वज्रकायस्य Vajrakaya's, भवनम् mansion, पुप्लुवे jumped over

#### Explanation

The noble vanara jumped from Jambumali's to Sumali's and from  Rashmiketu's, Suryaketu's and to Vajrakaya's residence.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

धूम्राक्षस्य च सम्पातेर्भवनं मारुतात्मजः। विद्युद्रूपस्य भीमस्य घनस्य विघनस्य च।।5.6.23।। शुकनासस्य वक्रस्य शठस्य विकटस्य च। ब्रह्मकर्णस्य दंष्ट्रस्य रोमशस्य च रक्षसः।।5.6.24।। युद्धोन्मत्तस्य मत्तस्य ध्वजग्रीवस्य नादिनः। विद्युज्जिह्वेन्द्रजिह्वानां तथा हस्तिमुखस्य च।।5.6.25।। कराळस्य पिशाचस्य शोणिताक्षस्य चैव हि।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मारुतात्मजः son of the Windgod (Hanuman), धूम्राक्षस्य Dhumraksha's, सम्पातेः Sampati's, विद्युद्रूपस्य Vidyudrupa's, भीमस्य Bhima's, घनस्य Ghana's, विघनस्य च Vighana's, शुकनासस्य Shukanasa's, वक्रस्य Vakra's, शठस्य Shatha's, विकटस्य च and Vikata's, ब्रह्मकर्णस्य Brahmakarna's, दंष्ट्रस्य Damshtra's, रोमशस्य Romasa's, रक्षसः of the demons, युद्धोन्मत्तस्य Yuddhonmatta's, मत्तस्य Matta's, ध्वजग्रीवस्य Dhvajagriva's, नादिनः Nadi's, विद्युज्जिह्वेन्द्रजिह्वानाम् of Vidyujjihva's and Indrajihva's, तथा similarly, हस्तिमुखस्य च, Hastimukha's करालस्य Karala's, पिशाचस्य Pisacha's, शोणिताक्षस्य चैव Sonitaksha's also, हि indeed,

#### Explanation

Hanuman jumped the houses of Dhumraksha, Sampati, Vidyudrupa, Bhima, Ghana and Vighana, Sukanasa, Vakra, Shatha, Vikata, Brahmakarna, Damshtra Romasa, Yuddhonmatta, Matta, Dhvajagriva, Nadi, Vidyujjihva and Indrajihva Hastimukha, Karala, Pisacha and Sonitaksha.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

धूम्राक्षस्य च सम्पातेर्भवनं मारुतात्मजः। विद्युद्रूपस्य भीमस्य घनस्य विघनस्य च।।5.6.23।। शुकनासस्य वक्रस्य शठस्य विकटस्य च। ब्रह्मकर्णस्य दंष्ट्रस्य रोमशस्य च रक्षसः।।5.6.24।। युद्धोन्मत्तस्य मत्तस्य ध्वजग्रीवस्य नादिनः। विद्युज्जिह्वेन्द्रजिह्वानां तथा हस्तिमुखस्य च।।5.6.25।। कराळस्य पिशाचस्य शोणिताक्षस्य चैव हि।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मारुतात्मजः son of the Windgod (Hanuman), धूम्राक्षस्य Dhumraksha's, सम्पातेः Sampati's, विद्युद्रूपस्य Vidyudrupa's, भीमस्य Bhima's, घनस्य Ghana's, विघनस्य च Vighana's, शुकनासस्य Shukanasa's, वक्रस्य Vakra's, शठस्य Shatha's, विकटस्य च and Vikata's, ब्रह्मकर्णस्य Brahmakarna's, दंष्ट्रस्य Damshtra's, रोमशस्य Romasa's, रक्षसः of the demons, युद्धोन्मत्तस्य Yuddhonmatta's, मत्तस्य Matta's, ध्वजग्रीवस्य Dhvajagriva's, नादिनः Nadi's, विद्युज्जिह्वेन्द्रजिह्वानाम् of Vidyujjihva's and Indrajihva's, तथा similarly, हस्तिमुखस्य च, Hastimukha's करालस्य Karala's, पिशाचस्य Pisacha's, शोणिताक्षस्य चैव Sonitaksha's also, हि indeed,

#### Explanation

Hanuman jumped the houses of Dhumraksha, Sampati, Vidyudrupa, Bhima, Ghana and Vighana, Sukanasa, Vakra, Shatha, Vikata, Brahmakarna, Damshtra Romasa, Yuddhonmatta, Matta, Dhvajagriva, Nadi, Vidyujjihva and Indrajihva Hastimukha, Karala, Pisacha and Sonitaksha.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

धूम्राक्षस्य च सम्पातेर्भवनं मारुतात्मजः। विद्युद्रूपस्य भीमस्य घनस्य विघनस्य च।।5.6.23।। शुकनासस्य वक्रस्य शठस्य विकटस्य च। ब्रह्मकर्णस्य दंष्ट्रस्य रोमशस्य च रक्षसः।।5.6.24।। युद्धोन्मत्तस्य मत्तस्य ध्वजग्रीवस्य नादिनः। विद्युज्जिह्वेन्द्रजिह्वानां तथा हस्तिमुखस्य च।।5.6.25।। कराळस्य पिशाचस्य शोणिताक्षस्य चैव हि।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मारुतात्मजः son of the Windgod (Hanuman), धूम्राक्षस्य Dhumraksha's, सम्पातेः Sampati's, विद्युद्रूपस्य Vidyudrupa's, भीमस्य Bhima's, घनस्य Ghana's, विघनस्य च Vighana's, शुकनासस्य Shukanasa's, वक्रस्य Vakra's, शठस्य Shatha's, विकटस्य च and Vikata's, ब्रह्मकर्णस्य Brahmakarna's, दंष्ट्रस्य Damshtra's, रोमशस्य Romasa's, रक्षसः of the demons, युद्धोन्मत्तस्य Yuddhonmatta's, मत्तस्य Matta's, ध्वजग्रीवस्य Dhvajagriva's, नादिनः Nadi's, विद्युज्जिह्वेन्द्रजिह्वानाम् of Vidyujjihva's and Indrajihva's, तथा similarly, हस्तिमुखस्य च, Hastimukha's करालस्य Karala's, पिशाचस्य Pisacha's, शोणिताक्षस्य चैव Sonitaksha's also, हि indeed,

#### Explanation

Hanuman jumped the houses of Dhumraksha, Sampati, Vidyudrupa, Bhima, Ghana and Vighana, Sukanasa, Vakra, Shatha, Vikata, Brahmakarna, Damshtra Romasa, Yuddhonmatta, Matta, Dhvajagriva, Nadi, Vidyujjihva and Indrajihva Hastimukha, Karala, Pisacha and Sonitaksha.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

भवनम् क्रममाणः णोऽसौ हनुमान्मारुतात्मजः।।5.6.26।। तेषु तेषु महार्हेषु भवनेषु महायशाः। तेषामृद्धिमतामृद्धिं ददर्श स महाकपिः।।5.6.27।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मारुतात्मजः son of the Windgod, महायशाः illustrious, महाकपिः great monkey, सः हनुमान् Hanuman, महार्हेषु in lofty places, तेषु तेषु in several places, भवनेषु in mansions, क्रमेणैव in order, क्रममाणः while he was advancing, ऋद्धिमताम् of the wealthy ones, तेषाम् their, ऋद्धिम् wealth, ददर्श saw

#### Explanation

The illustrious son of the Windgod jumped from house to house in an order and saw the wealth of the wealthy in the lofty mansions.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

भवनम् क्रममाणः णोऽसौ हनुमान्मारुतात्मजः।।5.6.26।। तेषु तेषु महार्हेषु भवनेषु महायशाः। तेषामृद्धिमतामृद्धिं ददर्श स महाकपिः।।5.6.27।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मारुतात्मजः son of the Windgod, महायशाः illustrious, महाकपिः great monkey, सः हनुमान् Hanuman, महार्हेषु in lofty places, तेषु तेषु in several places, भवनेषु in mansions, क्रमेणैव in order, क्रममाणः while he was advancing, ऋद्धिमताम् of the wealthy ones, तेषाम् their, ऋद्धिम् wealth, ददर्श saw

#### Explanation

The illustrious son of the Windgod jumped from house to house in an order and saw the wealth of the wealthy in the lofty mansions.

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

सर्वेषां समतिक्रम्य भवनानि समन्ततः। आससादाथ लक्ष्मीवान् राक्षसेद्रनिवेशनम्।।5.6.28।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मीवान् the lucky, समन्ततः all over, सर्वेषाम् of all, भवनानि mansions, समतिक्रम्य passing, अथ then, राक्षसेन्द्रनिवेशनम् residence of lord of demons, आससाद reached

#### Explanation

Passing all the mansions, the lucky Hanuman reached the residence of the demon king, Ravana.

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

रावणस्योपशायिन्यो ददर्श हरिसत्तमः। विचरन्हरिशार्दूलो राक्षसीर्विकृतेक्षणाः।।5.6.29।। शूलमुद्गरहस्ताश्च शक्तितोमरधारिणी:।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हरिशार्दूलःtiger among vanaras, हरिसत्तमः noble vanara, विचरन् while moving about, रावणस्य Ravana's, उपशायिन्यः close by his bed, विकृतेक्षणाः of hideous eyes, शूलमुद्गरहस्ताश्च holding tridents and hammers, शक्तितोमरधारिणीः holding powerful javelines and iron cudgels, राक्षसीः rakshasas, ददर्श saw

#### Explanation

The heroic and powerful vanara saw at Ravana's residence demonesses with hideous eyes guarding the bed and an army of demons holding tridents, hammers, powerful javelins and iron cudgels.

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

ददर्श विविधान् गुल्मान् तस्य रक्षःपतेर्गृहे।।5.6.30।। राक्षसांश्च महाकायान्नानाप्रहरणोद्यतान्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तस्य his, रक्षः पते: demon king, गृहे at home, विविधान् several, गुल्मान् army troops, महाकायान् large, नानाप्रहरणोद्यतान् ready with different kinds of weapons, राक्षसांश्च demons also, ददर्श saw

#### Explanation

At his (Ravana's) residence, Hanuman also noticed troops of huge demons ready with different kinds of weapons.

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

रक्तान् श्वेतान् सितांश्चैव हरींश्चापि महाजवान्।।5.6.31।। कुलीनान् रूपसम्पन्नान् गजान्परगजारुजान्। निष्ठितान् गजशिक्षयामैरावतसमान्युधि।।5.6.32।। निहन्त्रून् परसैन्यानां गृहे तस्मिन् ददर्श सः। क्षरतश्च यथा मेघान् स्रवतश्च यथा गिरीन्।।5.6.33।। मेघसन्ततिनिर्घोषान् दुर्धर्षान् समरे परैः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः he, तस्मिन् in his, गृहे in the house, रक्तान् red, श्वेतान् white, सितांश्चैव pure white coloured, महाजवान् of good speed, हरींश्चापि horses also, कुलीनान् wellbred ones, रूपसम्पन्नान् goodlooking, परगजारुजान् those whch were not inferior to enemy's elephants, गजान् elephants, गजशिक्षयाम् in training elephants, निष्ठितान् exceedingly welltrained, ऐरावतसमान् equal to Airavata, युधि in battle, परसैन्यानाम् of enemy forces, निहन्त्रून् destroyers, क्षरतः  shedding, मेघान् यथा clouds like, स्रवतः pouring forth rut, गिरीन् यथा like mountains, मेघस्न्तिन्तिर्घोषान् trumpeting like a group of clouds, समरे in war, परैः by enemies, दुर्धर्षान् unassailable, गजान् elephants, ददर्श saw

#### Explanation

He saw horses of high speed in red, white and cream colours. He saw beautiful, wellbred elephants, which were not inferior to the enemy's. They were welltrained and had proved equal to Airavata (Indra's elephant) in battles. Resembling the thundering clouds, they were unassailable to the enemy in war and were trumpeting like a cluster of clouds.

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

रक्तान् श्वेतान् सितांश्चैव हरींश्चापि महाजवान्।।5.6.31।। कुलीनान् रूपसम्पन्नान् गजान्परगजारुजान्। निष्ठितान् गजशिक्षयामैरावतसमान्युधि।।5.6.32।। निहन्त्रून् परसैन्यानां गृहे तस्मिन् ददर्श सः। क्षरतश्च यथा मेघान् स्रवतश्च यथा गिरीन्।।5.6.33।। मेघसन्ततिनिर्घोषान् दुर्धर्षान् समरे परैः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः he, तस्मिन् in his, गृहे in the house, रक्तान् red, श्वेतान् white, सितांश्चैव pure white coloured, महाजवान् of good speed, हरींश्चापि horses also, कुलीनान् wellbred ones, रूपसम्पन्नान् goodlooking, परगजारुजान् those whch were not inferior to enemy's elephants, गजान् elephants, गजशिक्षयाम् in training elephants, निष्ठितान् exceedingly welltrained, ऐरावतसमान् equal to Airavata, युधि in battle, परसैन्यानाम् of enemy forces, निहन्त्रून् destroyers, क्षरतः  shedding, मेघान् यथा clouds like, स्रवतः pouring forth rut, गिरीन् यथा like mountains, मेघस्न्तिन्तिर्घोषान् trumpeting like a group of clouds, समरे in war, परैः by enemies, दुर्धर्षान् unassailable, गजान् elephants, ददर्श saw

#### Explanation

He saw horses of high speed in red, white and cream colours. He saw beautiful, wellbred elephants, which were not inferior to the enemy's. They were welltrained and had proved equal to Airavata (Indra's elephant) in battles. Resembling the thundering clouds, they were unassailable to the enemy in war and were trumpeting like a cluster of clouds.

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

रक्तान् श्वेतान् सितांश्चैव हरींश्चापि महाजवान्।।5.6.31।। कुलीनान् रूपसम्पन्नान् गजान्परगजारुजान्। निष्ठितान् गजशिक्षयामैरावतसमान्युधि।।5.6.32।। निहन्त्रून् परसैन्यानां गृहे तस्मिन् ददर्श सः। क्षरतश्च यथा मेघान् स्रवतश्च यथा गिरीन्।।5.6.33।। मेघसन्ततिनिर्घोषान् दुर्धर्षान् समरे परैः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः he, तस्मिन् in his, गृहे in the house, रक्तान् red, श्वेतान् white, सितांश्चैव pure white coloured, महाजवान् of good speed, हरींश्चापि horses also, कुलीनान् wellbred ones, रूपसम्पन्नान् goodlooking, परगजारुजान् those whch were not inferior to enemy's elephants, गजान् elephants, गजशिक्षयाम् in training elephants, निष्ठितान् exceedingly welltrained, ऐरावतसमान् equal to Airavata, युधि in battle, परसैन्यानाम् of enemy forces, निहन्त्रून् destroyers, क्षरतः  shedding, मेघान् यथा clouds like, स्रवतः pouring forth rut, गिरीन् यथा like mountains, मेघस्न्तिन्तिर्घोषान् trumpeting like a group of clouds, समरे in war, परैः by enemies, दुर्धर्षान् unassailable, गजान् elephants, ददर्श saw

#### Explanation

He saw horses of high speed in red, white and cream colours. He saw beautiful, wellbred elephants, which were not inferior to the enemy's. They were welltrained and had proved equal to Airavata (Indra's elephant) in battles. Resembling the thundering clouds, they were unassailable to the enemy in war and were trumpeting like a cluster of clouds.

---

### Shloka 34

#### Shloka Text

सहस्रं वाहिनीस्तत्र जाम्बूनदपरिष्कृताः।।5.6.34।। हेमजालपरिच्छन्नास्तरुणादित्यसन्निभाः। ददर्श राक्षसेन्द्रस्य रावणस्य निवेशने।।5.6.35।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तत्र there, राक्षसेन्द्रस्य demon king's, रावणस्य Ravana's, निवेशने in the residence, जाम्बूनदपरिष्कृताः bedecked with the gold, हेमजालपरिच्छन्नाः  protected by the rays emerging from, तरुणादित्यसन्निभाः like the rising Sun, सहस्रम् thousands, वाहिनीः troops, ददर्श saw

#### Explanation

He saw in the palace of Ravana, the demon king, thousands of troops adorned with gold, fully protected with armour of gold shining like the morning Sun.

---

### Shloka 35

#### Shloka Text

सहस्रं वाहिनीस्तत्र जाम्बूनदपरिष्कृताः।।5.6.34।। हेमजालपरिच्छन्नास्तरुणादित्यसन्निभाः। ददर्श राक्षसेन्द्रस्य रावणस्य निवेशने।।5.6.35।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तत्र there, राक्षसेन्द्रस्य demon king's, रावणस्य Ravana's, निवेशने in the residence, जाम्बूनदपरिष्कृताः bedecked with the gold, हेमजालपरिच्छन्नाः  protected by the rays emerging from, तरुणादित्यसन्निभाः like the rising Sun, सहस्रम् thousands, वाहिनीः troops, ददर्श saw

#### Explanation

He saw in the palace of Ravana, the demon king, thousands of troops adorned with gold, fully protected with armour of gold shining like the morning Sun.

---

### Shloka 36

#### Shloka Text

शिबिका विविधाकाराः स कपिर्मारुतात्मजः। लतागृहाणि चित्राणि चित्रशालागृहाणि च।।5.6.36।। क्रीडागृहाणि चान्यानि दारुपर्वतकानपि। कामस्य गृहकं रम्यं दिवागृहकमेव च।।5.6.37।। ददर्श राक्षसेन्द्रस्य रावणस्य निवेशने।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मारुतात्मजः son of the Windgod, सः कपिः that monkey (Hanuman), राक्षसेन्द्रस्य of the lord of demons, रावणस्य Ravana's, निवेशने in the dwelling, विविधाकाराः of several foms, शिबिकाः palanquins, चित्राणि colourful, लतागृहाणि bowers, चित्रशालागृहाणि homes with picture galleries, अन्यानि and other, क्रीडागृहाणि sporting homes, दारुपर्वतकानपि hillocks made of wood, कामस्य for lovemaking (romance), गृहकम् home, रम्यम् delighting, दिवागृहकमेव च for daytime activities, ददर्श saw

#### Explanation

The son of the Windgod saw at the palace of Ravana, the lord of demons, colourful palanquins of several kinds, bowers, picture galleries, spots for sporting, hillocks (artificial) made of wood, apartments for romance, for pleasures and day time activities.

---

### Shloka 37

#### Shloka Text

शिबिका विविधाकाराः स कपिर्मारुतात्मजः। लतागृहाणि चित्राणि चित्रशालागृहाणि च।।5.6.36।। क्रीडागृहाणि चान्यानि दारुपर्वतकानपि। कामस्य गृहकं रम्यं दिवागृहकमेव च।।5.6.37।। ददर्श राक्षसेन्द्रस्य रावणस्य निवेशने।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मारुतात्मजः son of the Windgod, सः कपिः that monkey (Hanuman), राक्षसेन्द्रस्य of the lord of demons, रावणस्य Ravana's, निवेशने in the dwelling, विविधाकाराः of several foms, शिबिकाः palanquins, चित्राणि colourful, लतागृहाणि bowers, चित्रशालागृहाणि homes with picture galleries, अन्यानि and other, क्रीडागृहाणि sporting homes, दारुपर्वतकानपि hillocks made of wood, कामस्य for lovemaking (romance), गृहकम् home, रम्यम् delighting, दिवागृहकमेव च for daytime activities, ददर्श saw

#### Explanation

The son of the Windgod saw at the palace of Ravana, the lord of demons, colourful palanquins of several kinds, bowers, picture galleries, spots for sporting, hillocks (artificial) made of wood, apartments for romance, for pleasures and day time activities.

---

### Shloka 38

#### Shloka Text

स मन्दरगिरिप्रख्यं मयूरस्थानसङ्कुलम्।।5.6.38।। ध्वजयष्टिभिराकीर्णं ददर्श भवनोत्तमम्। अनेकरत्नसङ्कीर्णं निधिजालं समन्ततः।।5.6.39।। धीरनिष्ठितकर्मान्तं गृहं भूतपतेरिव।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः he, मन्दरगिरिप्रख्यम् comaparable to mount Mandara, मयूरस्थानसङ्कुलम् places crowded with peacocks, ध्वजयष्टिभिः with flag staffs fixed, आकीर्णम् filled with, धीरनिष्ठितकर्मान्तम् built by skilled craftsmen with great care, भूतपतेः of Siva, the lord of all creatures, गृहमिव like home, भवनोत्तमम् magnificent mansion, समन्ततः every where, अनेकरत्नसङ्कीर्णम् full of gems, निधिजालम् treasure troves, ददर्श saw

#### Explanation

Comparable to mount Mandara, it was crowded with peacocks, set with flag staffs fixed all over, magnificent mansions built by skilled craftsmen with great efforts, decked with gems of several kinds, rich with treasures comparable to Kailasa, the home of Siva, the Lord of all beings.

---

### Shloka 39

#### Shloka Text

स मन्दरगिरिप्रख्यं मयूरस्थानसङ्कुलम्।।5.6.38।। ध्वजयष्टिभिराकीर्णं ददर्श भवनोत्तमम्। अनेकरत्नसङ्कीर्णं निधिजालं समन्ततः।।5.6.39।। धीरनिष्ठितकर्मान्तं गृहं भूतपतेरिव।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः he, मन्दरगिरिप्रख्यम् comaparable to mount Mandara, मयूरस्थानसङ्कुलम् places crowded with peacocks, ध्वजयष्टिभिः with flag staffs fixed, आकीर्णम् filled with, धीरनिष्ठितकर्मान्तम् built by skilled craftsmen with great care, भूतपतेः of Siva, the lord of all creatures, गृहमिव like home, भवनोत्तमम् magnificent mansion, समन्ततः every where, अनेकरत्नसङ्कीर्णम् full of gems, निधिजालम् treasure troves, ददर्श saw

#### Explanation

Comparable to mount Mandara, it was crowded with peacocks, set with flag staffs fixed all over, magnificent mansions built by skilled craftsmen with great efforts, decked with gems of several kinds, rich with treasures comparable to Kailasa, the home of Siva, the Lord of all beings.

---

### Shloka 40

#### Shloka Text

अर्चिर्भिश्चापि रत्नानां तेजसा रावणस्य च।।5.6.40।। विरराजाथ तद्वेश्म रश्मिमानिव रश्मिभिः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तत् that, वेश्म place, रत्नानाम् of the gems, अर्चिर्भिश्चापि by the wonderful hues of gems, रावणस्य Ravana's, तेजसा च and with brightness, अथ there, रश्मिभिः with rays, रश्मिमानिव like the brightness of Sunrays, विरराज shone

#### Explanation

The palace of Ravana looked splendid with gems of different hues and with Ravana's presence, it was shining like the radiant Sungod.

---

### Shloka 41

#### Shloka Text

जाम्बूनदमयान्येन शयनान्यासनानि च।।5.6.41।। भाजनानि च मुख्यानि ददर्श हरियूथपः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हरियूथपः vanara leader, जाम्बूनदमयान्येव made of Jambunada gold, शयनानि beds, आसनानि च seats, मुख्यानि chief, भाजनानि vessels, ददर्श saw

#### Explanation

The leader of the vanaras saw there beds, seats and main vessels made of gold.

---

### Shloka 42

#### Shloka Text

मध्वासवकृतक्लेदं मणिभाजनसङ्कुलम्।।5.6.42।। मनोरममसंबाधं कुबेरभवनं यथा। नूपुराणां च घोषेण काञ्चीनां निनदेन च।।5.6.43।। मृदङ्तलघोषैश्च घोषवद्भिर्विनादितम्। प्रासादसङ्घातयुतं स्त्रीरत्नशतसङ्कुलम्।।5.6.44। सुव्यूढकक्ष्यं हनुमान् प्रविवेश महागृहाम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ध्वासवकृतक्लेदम् drenched with liqour and other drinks, मणिभाजनसङ्कुलम् with gemencrusted vessels scattered all over, मनोरमम् very delightful, असम्बाधम् spacious, कुबेरभवनं यथा like the mansion of Kubera, नूपुराणाम् of anklets, घोषेण by the sound, काञ्चीनाम् of girdles, निनदेन with the sounds, घोषवद्भि:  filled with sounds of, मृदङ्गतल घोषैश्च with the sounds of drums, विनादितम् resonating, प्रासादसङ्घातयुतम् with rows of mansions, स्त्रीरत्नशतसङ्कुलम् filled with hundreds of exquisite women, सुव्यूढकक्ष्यम् with welllaid apartments, महागृहम् lofty palace, हनुमान् Hanuman, प्रविवेश entered

#### Explanation

Hanuman entered the delightful, lofty palace comparable to Kubera's mansion. It was filled with vessels encrusted with gems scattered all over. The floor was drenched with liquor. It was resonating with sounds of golden anklets of women and of drums. The rows of mansions, lofty palaces with welllaid apartments were full of hundreds of exquisite women. इत्यार्षे वाल्मीकीये श्रीमद्रामायणे आदिकाव्ये सुन्दरकाण्डे षष्ठस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the sixth sarga of Sundarakanda of the holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 7

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

स वेश्मजालं बलवान् ददर्श व्यासक्तवैडूर्यसुवर्णजालम्। यथा महत् प्रावृषि मेघजालं विद्युत्पिनद्धं सविहङ्गजालम्।।5.7.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

बलवान् powerful, सः he, व्यासक्तवैडूर्यसुवर्णजालम् fitted with golden lattices encrusted with vaidurya, प्रावृषि in the rainy season, विद्युत्पिनद्धम् with streaks of lightning, सविहङ्गजालम् with flocks of birds, महत् great, मेघजालं यथा like the abode of clouds, वेश्मजालम्  conglomeration of houses, ददर्श saw.

#### Explanation

Powerful Hanuman beheld the conglomeration of buildings with golden lattices encrusted with vaidurya, with flocks of birds comparable to a complex of clouds pierced by lightning during rainy season.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

निवेशनानां विविधाश्च शालाः प्रधानशङ्खायुधचापशालाः। मनोहराश्चापि पुनर्विशालाः ददर्श वेश्माद्रिषु चन्द्रशालाः।।5.7.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

निवेशनानाम् of the houses, विविधाः several, शालाः halls, प्रधानशङ्खायुधचापशालाः for storing valuable conches, weapons and bows, पुनः again, वेश्माद्रिषु on top of attics on the houses, मनोहराः delightful, विशालाः spacious, चन्द्रशालाः terraces, ददर्श saw.

#### Explanation

Attached to the spacious halls of the houses, were storehouses for valuable  conches, weapons, bows and arrows. On the top of the attics on these mansions, were large and delightful terraces open to the Moon.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

गृहाणि नानावसुराजितानि देवासुरैश्चापि सुपूजितानि। सर्वैश्च दोषैः परिवर्जितानि कपिर्ददर्श स्वबलार्जितानि।।5.7.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कपिः monkey, नानावसुराजितानि various kinds treasures, देवासुरैश्चापि even by gods and demons, सुपूजितानि worshipped by, सर्वैः by all, दोषैः defects, परिवर्जितानि devoid, स्वबलार्जितानि earned by his might, गृहाणि houses, ददर्श saw.

#### Explanation

The monkey saw numerous kinds of treasures, cherished by gods and demons, which were won by Ravana's might.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

तानि प्रयत्नाभिसमाहितानि मयेन साक्षादिव निर्मितानि। महीतले सर्वगुणोत्तराणि ददर्श लङ्काधिपतेर्गृहाणि।।5.7.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तानि those, प्रयत्नाभिसमाहितानि collected with great care and effort, साक्षात् directly, मयेन by Maya, निर्मितानीव as though built, महीतले on earth, सर्वगुणोत्तराणि of excellent quality, लङ्काधिपतेः of the lord of Lanka, गृहाणि mansions, ददर्श saw.

#### Explanation

The mansions of the lord of Lanka built with great care and  effort on earth were  by all means excellent  and appeared as though built personally by Maya (the divine architect wellknown for magical skills).

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

ततो ददर्शोछ्रचितमेघरूपं मनोहरं काञ्चनचारुरूपम्। रक्षोऽधिपस्यात्मबलानुरूपं गृहोत्तमं ह्यप्रतिरूपरूपम्।।5.7.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, उछ्रचितमेघरूपम् appearing like a towering cloud, मनोहरम् soulcaptivating, काञ्चनचारुरूपम् golden palace possessing splendour of molten gold, अप्रतिरूपरूपम् matchless, आत्मबलानुरूपम् worthy of the might of Ravana, रक्षोधिपस्य of the lord of demons, गृहोत्तमम् magnificent palace, ददर्श saw.

#### Explanation

Hanuman beheld the magnificent and soulcaptivating palace possessing the splendour of molten gold. Of matchless beauty, it looked like a towering cloud worthy of the might of Ravana, the lord of ogresses.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

महीतले स्वर्गमिव प्रकीर्णं श्रिया ज्वलन्तं बहुरत्नकीर्णम्। नानातरूणां कुसुमावकीर्णं गिरेरिवाग्रं रजसावकीर्णम्।।5.7.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महीतले on earth, प्रकीर्णम् scattered, स्वर्गमिव like heaven, श्रिया with splendour, ज्वलन्तम् while glittering, बहुरत्नकीर्णम् with many gems, नानातरूणाम् of all kinds of trees, कुसुमावकीर्णम् scattered with flowers, रजसा with dust, अवकीर्णम् covered, गिरेः mountain, अग्रमिव like the peak.

#### Explanation

It appeared splendid like a scattered piece of heaven on earth, glittering with countless gems. It looked like a mountain peak covered with pollen of flowers dropped from all kinds of trees.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

नारीप्रवेकैरिव दीप्यमानं तटिद्भिरम्भोदवदर्च्यमानम्। हंसप्रवेकैरिव वाह्यमानं श्रिया युतं खे सुकृतां विमानम्।।5.7.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नारीप्रवेकैः by resplendent women,दीप्यमानमिव as if lit up,तटिद्भिः with lightning, अम्भोदवत् like a raincloud, अर्च्यमानम् worshipped, हंसप्रवेकैः borne by swans, वाह्यमानम् इव as if being drawn by, श्रिया auspicious, युतम् endowed, खे in the sky, सुकृताम् royal, विमानम् aerial chariot.

#### Explanation

The aerial chariot was lit up by resplendent women just as a raincloud dazzles with flashes of lightning. It was auspicious, worthy of worship. It looked as if it was drawn by royal swans in the sky.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

यथा नगाग्रं बहुधातुचित्रं यथा नभश्च ग्रहचन्द्रचित्रम्। ददर्शयुक्तीकृतमेघचित्रं विमानरत्नं बहुरत्नचित्रम्।।5.7.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

युक्तीकृतमेघचित्रम् mass of colourful clouds, ग्रहचन्द्रचित्रम् looking colourful with planets and Moon, नभः यथा like the sky, बहुरत्नचित्रम् colourful on account of gems encrusted, बहुधातुचित्रम् colourful on account of many minerals, नगाग्रं यथा like the peak of a mountain, विमानरत्नम् jewel of a chariot, ददर्श saw.

#### Explanation

A jewel among aerial chariots, it looked colourful with many gems beset. It appeared like the summit of a mountain decorated with the colours of minerals, like the sky bespangled with planets and the Moon and like a mass of multicoloured clouds adorned by a rainbow.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

मही कृता पर्वतराजिपूर्णा शैलाः कृता वृक्षवितानपूर्णाः। वृक्षाः कृताः पुष्पवितानपूर्णाः पुष्पं कृतं केसरपत्रपूर्णम्।।5.7.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मही earth, पर्वतराजिपूर्णा filled with mountain ranges, कृता drawn, शैलाः mountains, वृक्षवितानपूर्णाः filled with a canopy of trees, कृताः drawn, वृक्षाः trees, पुष्पवितानपूर्णाः filled with a canopy of flowers, कृताः drawn on it, पुष्पम् flowers, केसरपत्रपूर्णम् filled with petals and filaments, कृतम् drawn.

#### Explanation

The whole  earth had been picturised on it. It was filled with rows of mountains  and a canopy of trees all over. The trees were filled with lovely flowers with petals and filaments drawn on them.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

कृतानि वेश्मानि च पाण्डुराणि तथा सुपुष्पाण्यपि पुष्कराणि। पुनश्च पद्मानि सकेसराणि धन्यानि चित्राणि तथा वनानि।।5.7.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पाण्डुराणि white in colour, वेश्मानि च houses, कृतानि drawn, तथा so also, सुपुष्पाणि with beautiful flowers, पुष्कराणि च ponds also, पुनश्च again, स केसराणि having filaments, पद्मानि lotuses, तथा so also, धन्यानि complete, चित्राणि paintings, वनानि gardens.

#### Explanation

There were pictures of mansions white in colour and pools of crystalclear water filled with lovely flowers. There were pictures of beautiful lotuses drawn on it.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

पुष्पाह्वयं नामविराजमानं रत्नप्रभाभिश्च विवर्धमानम्। वेश्मोत्तमानामपि चोच्यमानं महाकपिस्तत्र महाविमानम्।।5.7.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तत्र there, महाकपिः great vanara, पुष्पाह्वयम् Pushpaka chariot by name, नामविराजमानम् splendid indeed, रत्नप्रभाभिः glitter of gems, विवर्धमानम् glowing, वेश्मोत्तमानामपि of magnificent mansions, उच्यमानम् tall, महाविमानम् great chariot.

#### Explanation

There, the great Hanuman indeed saw a splendid chariot called Pushpaka. It was glowing with glittering gems. Taller than the biggest mansion, it was a great chariot.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

कृताश्च वैडूर्यमया विहङ्गाः रूप्यप्रवालैश्च तथा विहङ्गाः। चित्राश्च नानावसुभिर्भुजङ्गाः जात्यानुरूपास्तुरगाः शुभाङ्गाः।।5.7.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वैडूर्यमयाः made of vaidurya, विहङ्गाः birds, तथा similarly, रूप्यप्रवालैश्च made of silver and corals, विहङ्गाः birds, नानावसुभिः with various gems, चित्राः colourful, भुजङ्गाः serpents, जात्याः due to there good breeding, अनुरूपाः similar, शुभाङ्गाः with auspicious limbs, तुरगाः horses, कृताः drawn.

#### Explanation

Inside were birds made of vaidurya, silver and corals. There were colourful serpents made of various gems, and highclass horses of exquisite limbs drawn.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

प्रवालजाम्बूनदपुष्पपक्षाः सलीलमावर्जितजिह्मपक्षाः। कामस्य साक्षादिव भान्ति पक्षाः कृता विहङ्गाः सुमुखाः सुपक्षाः।।5.7.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

प्रवालजाम्बूनदपुष्पपक्षाः wings engraved with corals and gold, सुपक्षाः having beautiful wings, सलीलम् in a sportive way, आवर्जितजिह्मपक्षाः with their artificial wings bent down (tossed), विहङ्गाः birds, कृताः were drawn, साक्षात् truly, कामस्य cupid's, पक्षाः इव like wings, भान्ति seemed.

#### Explanation

It had curved figures of birds with beautiful wings carved out of corals and gold The wings were shining as if they were really the wings of allies of Cupid, the god of love.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

नियुज्यमानास्तु गजाः सुहस्ताः सकेसराश्चोत्पलपत्रहस्ताः। बभूवदेवी च कृता सुहस्ता लक्ष्मी स्तथा पद्मिनि पद्महस्ता।।5.7.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पद्मिनि in a lotuspond, सुहस्ता having beautiful hands, सकेसराश्च with filaments, उत्पलपत्रहस्ताः blue lotuses by their trunk carried, लक्ष्मीः goddess of wealth, नियुज्यमानास्तु stationed in place, गजाः elephants, तथा similarly, पद्महस्ता holding a lotus in hand, सुहस्ता of beautiful hand, देवी च goddess, कृता drawn, बभूव appeared.

#### Explanation

He saw on it lotus ponds, artificial elephants with shapely trunks, offering to Laxmi who herself was a lotus, holding in her beautiful hands blue lotuses shining with filaments.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

इतीव तद्गृहमभिगम्य शोभनम् सविस्मयो नगमिव चारुशोभनम्। पुनश्च तत्परमसुगन्धि सुन्दरम् हिमात्यये नगमिव चारुकन्दरम्।।5.7.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इतीव in that way, शोभनम् auspicious, चारुशोभनम् beautifully fashioned, नगमिव like a mountain, तद्गृहम् that palace, अभिगम्य having reached, सविस्मयः wonderstruck, परमसुगन्धि highly fragrant, सुन्दरम् beautiful, हिमात्यये at the end of winter, चारुकन्दरम् having beautiful caves, नगमिव like mountain, तत् that, पुनश्च once again.

#### Explanation

Thus he reached the auspicious palace appearing like a beautiful mountain and stood wonderstruck. Once again he looked at that beautiful palace which appeared like a mountain with lovely caverns in spring time filled with wonderful fragrance

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

ततः स तां कपिरभिपत्य पूजितां चरन् पुरीं दशमुखबाहुपालिताम्। अदृश्य तां जनकसुतां सुपूजितां सुदुःखितः पतिगुणवेगवर्जिताम्।।5.7.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, सः कपिः monkey, पूजिताम् worshipped, दशमुखबाहुपालिताम् ruled by the arms of the tenheaded king, तां पुरीम् that city, अभिपत्य having reached, चरन् while moving about, सुपूजिताम् venerable, पतिगुणवेगवर्जिताम् who was deprived of the benefit of her husband's virtuous conduct, तां जनकसुताम् that Janaka's daughter Sita, अदृश्य unable to find, सुदुःखितः was deeply sorrowful.

#### Explanation

Then Hanuman moved about that cherished city ruled by the strength of arms of the tenheaded king. Unable to find venerable Sita, who was deprived of the benefit of her husband's virtuous proximity, he became deeply sad.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

ततस्तदा बहुविधभावितात्मनः कृतात्मनो जनकसुतां सुवर्त्मनः। अपश्यतोऽभवदतिदुःखितं मनः सुचक्षुषः प्रविचरतो महात्मनः।।5.7.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः from there, तदा then, बहुविधभावितात्मनः thinking of many things in his mind, कृतात्मनः of a great soul, सुवर्त्मनः of the followers of righteous path, सुचक्षुषः foresighted, प्रविचरतः while he moved, जनकसुताम् daughter of Janaka, अपश्यत: unable to find, महात्मनः of the great self, मनः mind, अतिदुःखितम् very distressed, अभवत् became.

#### Explanation

Unable to find Sita, the disciplined and foresighted Hanuman, who always kept the righteous path, felt distressed while moving about, his mind oppressed by all kinds of thought. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये सुन्दरकाण्डे सप्तमस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the seventh sarga of Sundarakanda of the holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 8

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

स तस्य मध्ये भवनस्य संस्थितं महद्विमानं मणिवज्रचित्रितम्। प्रतप्तजाम्बूनदजालकृत्रिमं ददर्श वीरः पवनात्मजः कपिः।।5.8.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वीरः courageous, पवनात्मजः son of the Windgod, सः कपिः that vanara, तस्य भवनस्य of that mansion, मध्ये in the middle, संस्थितम् standing, मणिवज्रचित्रितम् rendered beautiful with gems and diamonds, प्रतप्तजाम्बूनदजालकृत्रिमम् adorned with latticed windows of barnished gold, महत् great, विमानम् aerial chariot, ददर्श saw.

#### Explanation

The courageous son of the Windgod, Hanuman, the vanara saw the aerial chariot kept in the middle of the mansion inlaid with gems and diamonds latticed with artificial windows of barnished gold.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

तदप्रमेयाप्रतिकारकृत्रिमं कृतं स्वयं साध्विति विश्वकर्मणा। दिवं गतं वायुपथप्रतिष्ठितं व्यराजतादित्यपथस्य लक्ष्मवत्।।5.8.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अप्रमेयाप्रतिकारकृत्रिमम् with images of incomparable beauty and immeasureable skill, विश्वकर्मणा by Visvakarma, स्वयम् himself, साध्विति product of excellent achievement, कृतम् fashioned, दिवम् sky, गतम् going, वायुपथप्रतिष्ठितम् which was placed in the aerial path, तत् that, आदित्यपथस्य in the orbit of the Sun, लक्ष्मवत् like a beaconlight, व्यराजत shone.

#### Explanation

The immeasurable Pushpaka fashioned by Visvakarma with images of incomparable beauty was not possible to describe. An excellent  product of his achievement, it was placed in the aerial path appearing as a beaconlight (signpost) in the orbit of the Sun.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

न तत्र किञ्चिन्न कृतं प्रयत्नतो न तत्र किञ्चिन्न महार्हरत्नवत्। न ते विशेषा नियताः सुरेष्वपि न तत्र किञ्चिन्न महाविशेषवत्।।5.8.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तत्र there, प्रयत्नतः by special effort, न कृतम् not only done, किञ्चित् anything, न not, तत्र there, न महार्हरत्नवत् with costly gems, किञ्चित् a litttle, न not, ते those, विशेषाः unique, सुरेष्वपि even to deities, न नियताः not present, तत्र there, न महाविशेषवत् very special, किञ्चित् न indeed it was.

#### Explanation

There was nothing in Pushpaka which was not executed carefully with special effort, nothing which was not made of precious stones. Such privileges were not available to even gods. Indeed there was nothing which was not unique.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

तपः समाधानपराक्रमार्जितं मनः समाधानविचारचारिणम्। अनेकसंस्थानविशेषनिर्मितं ततस्ततस्तुल्यविशेषदर्शनम्।।5.8.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तपः spiritual efforts, समाधानपराक्रमार्जितम् designed through concentration and prowess, मनः समाधानविचारचारिणम् which can reach the places desired by the thought of its master, अनेकसंस्थानविशेषनिर्मितम् built with many special seats, ततस्ततः at all places, तुल्यविशेषदर्शनम्  of equally good appearance

#### Explanation

(The Pushpaka) was designed by dint of divine efforts and prowess (of Kubera). It could touch any place its master commanded with a cool mind. It had many beautiful places of rest.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

विशेषमालम्ब्य विशेषसंस्थितं विचित्रकूटं बहुकूटमण्डितम्। मनोऽभिरामं शरदिन्दुनिर्मलं विचित्रकूटं शिखरं गिरेर्यथा।।5.8.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विशेषम् special, आलम्ब्य resorting to, विशेषसंस्थितम् designed in a unique manner, विचित्रकूटम् with wonderful peaks, बहुकूटमण्डितम् decorated with many peaks, मनोऽभिरामम् delightful to the mind, शरदिन्दुनिर्मलम् pleasing as autumnal fullmoon, गिरेः mountain's, विचित्रकूटम् wonderful towers, शिखरं यथा like the peak.

#### Explanation

The Pushpaka was specially designed in a unique manner. It was decorated with many colourful peaks. It was delghtful and pleasing to the mind and bright like the autumnal fullmoon. It looked like a mountain with many wonderful towers around.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

वहन्ति यं कुण्डलशोभिताननाः महाशना व्योमचरा निशाचराः। विवृत्तविध्वस्तविशाललोचनाः महाजवा भूतगणाः सहस्रशः।।5.8.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

यम् such (a chariot), कुण्डलशोभिताननाः faces brightened by the earrings, महाशनाः  gluttons, व्योमचराः ranging in the sky, निशाचराः nightrangers, विवृत्तविध्वस्त विशाललोचनाः with big rolling and frightening eyes, महाजवाः of tremendous speed, भूतगणाः groups of Bhutas, सहस्रशः in thousands, वहन्ति  drawing.

#### Explanation

The Pushpaka was borne by multitudes of Bhutas whose faces were shining with earrings, who were demons ranging in the night sky. They were gluttons endowed with great speed, and they had big, rolling and frightening eyes.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

वसन्तपुष्पोत्करचारुदर्शनं वसन्तमासादपि कान्तदर्शनम्। स पुष्पकं तत्र विमानमुत्तमं ददर्श तद्वानरवीरसत्तमः।।5.8.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तत् that, वानरवीरसत्तमः hero among monkeys, सः he, वसन्तपुष्पोत्करचारुदर्शनम् looking beautiful like a collection of blossoms of spring, वसन्तमासादपि more delightful than spring, कान्तदर्शनम् appearing beautiful, पुष्पकम् Pushpaka, उत्तमम् excellent, विमानम् aerial chariot, तत्र there, ददर्श saw.

#### Explanation

That hero among monkeys, beheld the aerial chariot looking beautiful like a colourful collection of spring blossoms. Its appearance was more pleasant than spring itself. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये सुन्दरकाण्डे अष्टमस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the eighth sarga of Sundarakanda of the holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 9

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

तस्यालयवरिष्ठस्य मध्ये विपुलमायतं। ददर्श भवनश्रेष्ठं हनुमान्मारुतात्मजः।।5.9.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मारुतात्मजः son of the Windgod, हनुमान् Hanuman, तस्य his, आलयवरिष्ठस्य of the main building, मध्ये in the centre, विपुलम् spacious, आयतम् huge, भवनश्रेष्ठम् excellent among mansions, ददर्श saw.

#### Explanation

The son of the Windgod, Hanuman, saw a huge, spacious and excellent of all mansions in the centre of the palace.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

अर्धयोजनविस्तीर्णमायतं योजनं हि तत्। भवनं राक्षसेन्द्रस्य बहुप्रासादसङ्कुलम्।।5.9.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसेन्द्रस्य of Indra among demons, तत् that, बहुप्रासादसङ्कुलम् of multitude of palaces, भवनम् mansion, अर्धयोजनविस्तीर्णम्  width of half yojana, योजनम् yojana, आयतम् long.

#### Explanation

The mansion of the lord of demons was a conglomeration of palaces half a yojana long and half a yojana wide.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

मार्गमाणस्तु वैदेहीं सीतामायतलोचनाम्। सर्वतः परिचक्राम हनूमानरिसूदनः।।5.9.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अरिसूदनः subduer of enemies, हनुमान् Hanuman, वैदेहीम् Vaidehi, आयतलोचनाम् broadeyed, सीताम् for Sita, मार्गमाणः तु  in search, सर्वतः all over, परिचक्राम went allround .

#### Explanation

Hanuman, the subduer of enemies, went  around in search of  the largeeyed Vaidehi.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

उत्तमं राक्षसावासं हनुमानवलोकयन्। आससादाथ लक्ष्मीवान् राक्षसेन्द्रनिवेशनम्।।5.9.4।। चतुर्विषाणैर्द्विरदैस्त्रिविषाणैस्तथैव च। परिक्षिप्तमसम्बाधं रक्ष्यमाणमुदायुधैः।।5.9.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथ now, लक्ष्मीवान् endowed with brightness, हनुमान् Hanuman, उत्तमम् best, राक्षसावासम् residence of demons, चतुर्विषाणैः fourtusked, तथैव च and similarly, त्रिविषाणैः threetusked, द्विरदैः twotusked, परिक्षिप्तम् surrounded by, असम्बाधम् spacious, उदायुधैः holding weapons, रक्ष्यमाणम् guarded by, राक्षसेन्द्रनिवेशनम् home of the lord of the ogres, अवलोकयन् seeing, आससाद reached.

#### Explanation

Searching the excellent residences of important demons, Hanuman finally reached  the palace of the lord of ogres guarded by armed demons with elephants with two or three or four tusks.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

उत्तमं राक्षसावासं हनुमानवलोकयन्। आससादाथ लक्ष्मीवान् राक्षसेन्द्रनिवेशनम्।।5.9.4।। चतुर्विषाणैर्द्विरदैस्त्रिविषाणैस्तथैव च। परिक्षिप्तमसम्बाधं रक्ष्यमाणमुदायुधैः।।5.9.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथ now, लक्ष्मीवान् endowed with brightness, हनुमान् Hanuman, उत्तमम् best, राक्षसावासम् residence of demons, चतुर्विषाणैः fourtusked, तथैव च and similarly, त्रिविषाणैः threetusked, द्विरदैः twotusked, परिक्षिप्तम् surrounded by, असम्बाधम् spacious, उदायुधैः holding weapons, रक्ष्यमाणम् guarded by, राक्षसेन्द्रनिवेशनम् home of the lord of the ogres, अवलोकयन् seeing, आससाद reached.

#### Explanation

Searching the excellent residences of important demons, Hanuman finally reached  the palace of the lord of ogres guarded by armed demons with elephants with two or three or four tusks.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

राक्षसीभिश्च पत्नीभी रावणस्य निवेशनम्। आहृताभिश्च विक्रम्य राजकन्याभिरावृतम्।।5.9.6।। तन्नक्रमकराकीर्णं तिमिङ्गिलझषाकुलम्। वायुवेगसमाधूतं पन्नगैरिव सागरम्।।5.9.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रावणस्य Ravana's, पत्नीभिः with wives, राक्षसीभिः च by demonesses also, विक्रम्य by winning, आहृताभिः brought, राजकन्याभिः by princesses, आवृतम् surrounded, निवेशनम् palace, तत् नक्रमकराकीर्णम् filled with crocodiles and sharks, तिमिङ्गिलझषाकुलम् with whales and fish, वायुवेगसमाधूतम् shaken by the force of the wind, पन्नगैः with serpents, सागरम् इव like an ocean, तत् he saw.

#### Explanation

Hanuman entered the palace of Ravana crowded with shedemons who were the wives of Ravana and the princesses brought by him after winning them in wars. To Hanuman, it looked like an ocean filled with crocodiles, sharks, whales, and a variety of fishes and snakes tossed by the force of the wind.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

राक्षसीभिश्च पत्नीभी रावणस्य निवेशनम्। आहृताभिश्च विक्रम्य राजकन्याभिरावृतम्।।5.9.6।। तन्नक्रमकराकीर्णं तिमिङ्गिलझषाकुलम्। वायुवेगसमाधूतं पन्नगैरिव सागरम्।।5.9.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रावणस्य Ravana's, पत्नीभिः with wives, राक्षसीभिः च by demonesses also, विक्रम्य by winning, आहृताभिः brought, राजकन्याभिः by princesses, आवृतम् surrounded, निवेशनम् palace, तत् नक्रमकराकीर्णम् filled with crocodiles and sharks, तिमिङ्गिलझषाकुलम् with whales and fish, वायुवेगसमाधूतम् shaken by the force of the wind, पन्नगैः with serpents, सागरम् इव like an ocean, तत् he saw.

#### Explanation

Hanuman entered the palace of Ravana crowded with shedemons who were the wives of Ravana and the princesses brought by him after winning them in wars. To Hanuman, it looked like an ocean filled with crocodiles, sharks, whales, and a variety of fishes and snakes tossed by the force of the wind.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

या हि वैश्रवणे लक्ष्मीर्या चेन्द्रे हरिवाहने। सा रावणगृहे सर्वा नित्यमेवानपायिनी।।5.9.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वैश्रवणे by Vaisravana, या whatever, लक्ष्मीः wealth हरिवाहने on greenish horse, the mount, इन्द्रे च of Indra, या as much, सा that, सर्वा everything, रावणगृहे in the residence of Ravana, नित्यमेव always, अनपायिनी present.

#### Explanation

Whatever wealth was possessed by Vaisravana (Kubera, the son of sage Vishrava) and also Indra who rides the Ucchaisrava green horse, was there in the palace of Ravana.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

या च राज्ञः कुबेरस्य यमस्य वरुणस्य च। तादृशी तद्विशिष्टा वा ऋद्धी रक्षोगृहेष्विह।।5.9.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राज्ञः of  the king, कुबेरस्य Kubera's, या whatever, यमस्य Yama's, वरुणस्य Varuna's, च and, या whatever, तादृशी such, तद्विशिष्टा वा or more exquisite than, ऋद्धि: wealth इह here, रक्षोगृहेषु in the houses of demons.

#### Explanation

The wealth stored in Ravana's palace was akin to what lay in the palaces of Kubera, Yama or Varuna, and ever more exquisite.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

तस्य हर्म्यस्य मध्यस्थं वेश्म चान्यत्सुनिर्मितं। बहुनिर्यूहसङ्कीर्णं ददर्श पवनात्मजः।।5.9.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पवनात्मजः son of the Windgod, तस्य of that, हर्म्यस्य of the palace, मध्यस्थम् in the centre, सुनिर्मितम् well built, बहुनिर्यूहसङ्कीर्णम् a complex many houses, अन्यत् another, वेश्म home, ददर्श saw.

#### Explanation

Hanuman saw in the centre of that palace a complex of many wellbuilt houses.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

ब्रह्मणोऽर्थे कृतं दिव्यं दिवि यद्विश्वकर्मणा। विमानं पुष्पकं नाम सर्वरत्नविभूषितम्।।5.9.11।। परेण तपसा लेभे यत्कुबेरः पितामहात्। कुबेरमोजसा जित्वा लेभे तद्राक्षसेश्वरः।।5.9.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सर्वरत्नविभूषितम् decorated with all kinds of gems, पुष्पकं नाम called Pushpaka, यत् that, दिव्यम् wonderful, विमानम् aerial chariot, विश्वकर्मणा by Visvakarma, दिवि in the sky, ब्रह्मणः अर्थे for Brahma, कृतम् built, यत् the same one, कुबेरः Kubera, पितामहात् from grandfather the creator, परेण with supreme, तपसा by penance, लेभे got, तत् that, राक्षसेश्वरः lord of demons, ओजसा by his might, कुबेरम् Kubera, जित्वा after defeating, लेभे acquired.

#### Explanation

There was a wonderful aerial chariot called Pushpaka embellished with all kinds of gems built by Visvakarma for Brahma. It was the same one that Kubera had won from his grandfather, the creator Brahma, through his supreme penance. The lord of ogres acquired it (as a memento for his victory) by defeating Kubera with his might.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

ब्रह्मणोऽर्थे कृतं दिव्यं दिवि यद्विश्वकर्मणा। विमानं पुष्पकं नाम सर्वरत्नविभूषितम्।।5.9.11।। परेण तपसा लेभे यत्कुबेरः पितामहात्। कुबेरमोजसा जित्वा लेभे तद्राक्षसेश्वरः।।5.9.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सर्वरत्नविभूषितम् decorated with all kinds of gems, पुष्पकं नाम called Pushpaka, यत् that, दिव्यम् wonderful, विमानम् aerial chariot, विश्वकर्मणा by Visvakarma, दिवि in the sky, ब्रह्मणः अर्थे for Brahma, कृतम् built, यत् the same one, कुबेरः Kubera, पितामहात् from grandfather the creator, परेण with supreme, तपसा by penance, लेभे got, तत् that, राक्षसेश्वरः lord of demons, ओजसा by his might, कुबेरम् Kubera, जित्वा after defeating, लेभे acquired.

#### Explanation

There was a wonderful aerial chariot called Pushpaka embellished with all kinds of gems built by Visvakarma for Brahma. It was the same one that Kubera had won from his grandfather, the creator Brahma, through his supreme penance. The lord of ogres acquired it (as a memento for his victory) by defeating Kubera with his might.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

ईहामृगसमायुक्त्तै: कार्तस्वरहिरमण्मयैः। सुकृतैराचितं स्तम्भैः प्रदीप्तमिव च श्रिया।।5.9.13।। मेरुमन्दरसङ्काशैरुल्लिखद्भिरिवाम्बरं। कूटागारैश्शुभाकारैस्सर्वतस्समलङ्कृतम्।।5.9.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ईहामृगसमायुक्तै: with images of wolves carved, कार्तस्वरहिरण्मयैः made of Kartaswara and Hiranmaya, two types of gold, सुकृतैः finely built, स्तम्भैः with pillars, आचितम् supported, श्रिया lustrous, प्रदीप्तमिव as though blazing, मेरुमन्दरसङ्काशैः resembling mountain Meru and Mandara, अम्बरम् sky, उल्लिखद्भिरिव as though scraping, शुभाकारैः by auspicious, कूटागारैः with multitude of pleasurehouses, सर्वतः everywhere, समलङ्कृतम् decorated.

#### Explanation

It was supported by finely built pillars of two types of gold (Kartaswara and Hiranmaya) with images of wolves carved on them and blazing. It was tall as if scraping the sky and resembled mountains like Meru and Mandara. It was graced with multitudes of  auspicious and decorated pleasurehouses everywhere.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

ईहामृगसमायुक्त्तै: कार्तस्वरहिरमण्मयैः। सुकृतैराचितं स्तम्भैः प्रदीप्तमिव च श्रिया।।5.9.13।। मेरुमन्दरसङ्काशैरुल्लिखद्भिरिवाम्बरं। कूटागारैश्शुभाकारैस्सर्वतस्समलङ्कृतम्।।5.9.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ईहामृगसमायुक्तै: with images of wolves carved, कार्तस्वरहिरण्मयैः made of Kartaswara and Hiranmaya, two types of gold, सुकृतैः finely built, स्तम्भैः with pillars, आचितम् supported, श्रिया lustrous, प्रदीप्तमिव as though blazing, मेरुमन्दरसङ्काशैः resembling mountain Meru and Mandara, अम्बरम् sky, उल्लिखद्भिरिव as though scraping, शुभाकारैः by auspicious, कूटागारैः with multitude of pleasurehouses, सर्वतः everywhere, समलङ्कृतम् decorated.

#### Explanation

It was supported by finely built pillars of two types of gold (Kartaswara and Hiranmaya) with images of wolves carved on them and blazing. It was tall as if scraping the sky and resembled mountains like Meru and Mandara. It was graced with multitudes of  auspicious and decorated pleasurehouses everywhere.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

ज्वलनार्कप्रतीकाशं सुकृतं विश्वकर्मणा। हेमसोपानसंयुक्तं चारुप्रवरवेदिकम्।।5.9.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विश्वकर्मणा by Visvakarma, सुकृतम् well built, ज्वलनार्कप्रतीकाशम् glowing like fire and the Sun, हेमसोपानसंयुक्तम् had stairways of gold, चारुप्रवरवेदिकम् had beautiful paltforms.

#### Explanation

It was well built by Visvakarma and and had golden stairways, with radiant platforms glowing like fire and Sun.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

जालवातायनैर्युक्तं काञ्चनैः स्स्फाटिकैरपि। इन्द्रनीलमहानीलमणिप्रवरवेदिकम्।।5.9.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

काञ्चनैः with gold, स्फाटिकैरपि with crystal also, जालवातायनैः by latticed windows, युक्तम् endowed, इन्द्रनीलमहानीलमणिप्रवरवेदिकम् platforms encrusted with saphires called Indraneela and Mahaneela.

#### Explanation

It had latticed windows made of gold inlaid with crystals. The platforms were encrusted with saphires called Indraneela and Mahaneela appropriately.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

विद्रुमेण विचित्रेण मणिभिश्च महाघनैः। निस्तुलाभिश्च मुक्ताभिस्तलेनाभिविराजितम्।।5.9.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विचित्रेण with colourful, विद्रुमेण with corals, महाघनैः by very precious, मणिभिश्च by gems even, निस्तुलाभिः with peerless (invaluable), मुक्ताभिः with pearls, तलेन on the surface, अभिविराजितम् shining very brightly.

#### Explanation

It was shining bright with colourful corals, precious gems and peerless pearls fixed on its floor.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

चन्दनेन च रक्तेन तपनीयनिभेन च। सुपुण्यगन्धिना युक्तमादित्यतरुणोपमम्।।5.9.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रक्तेन in red, तपनीयनिभेन च comparable to pure and polished gold, सुपुण्यगन्धिना with sweet fragrance, चन्दनेन with sandal, युक्तम् endowed, आदित्यतरुणोपमम् like the rising Sun.

#### Explanation

It was bright like molten gold, fragrant with red sandal comparable to the rising Sun.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

कूटागारैर्वराकारैर्विविधैस्समलङ्कृतम्। विमानं पुष्पकं दिव्यमारुरोह महाकपिः।।5.9.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाकपिः Hanuman, वराकारैः of excellent appearance, कूटागारैः with many corridors, समलङ्कृतम् decorated, दिव्यम् wonderful, पुष्पकं विमानम् Pushpaka chariot, आरुरोह ascended.

#### Explanation

Hanuman ascended the wonderful Pushpaka, welldecked with many corridors.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

तत्रस्थस्स तदा गन्धं पानभक्ष्यान्नसम्भवम्। दिव्यं सम्मूर्छितं जिघ्रद्रूपवन्तमिवानिलम्।।5.9.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

स: he, तदा then, तत्रस्थः present there, पानभक्ष्यान्नसम्भवम् rising from drinks and food, दिव्यम् divine, सम्मूर्छितम् stupefying, रूपवन्तम् having a solid form, आनिलमिव like wind, गन्धम् fragrance, जिघ्रत् smelt.

#### Explanation

There he smelt the stupefying, unearthly odour of food and drink. He felt as though the fragrance had taken a potentially solid form. (Note: The wind has no form. But when he carried the aroma of food and drink it appeared like the personification of wind which could be smelt. गन्धवती पृथवी fragrance comes from earth पृथवीतत्त्व the poet transmits the same to the wind who has got only स्पर्श sense of touch).

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

स गन्धस्तं महासत्त्वं बन्धुर्बन्धुमिवोत्तमम्। इत एहीत्युवाचेन तत्र यत्र स रावणः।।5.9.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

स: गन्धः that fragrance, महासत्त्वम् mighty, तम् him (Hanuman), उत्तमम् great, बन्धुम् relative, बन्धुरिव like a relative, सः रावणः that Ravana, यत्र wherever, तत्र there, इतः एहि come here, इति thus, उवाच इव as if told.

#### Explanation

The fragrance was as if inviting the mighty hero like a relative inviting his kith and kin, saying 'come here' and leading him to a place where Ravana rested.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

ततस्तां प्रस्थितश्शालां ददर्श महतीं शुभाम्। रावणस्य मनः कान्तां कान्तामिव वरस्त्रियम्।।5.9.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, प्रस्थितः he started, कान्ताम् beloved, वरस्त्रियमिव like his chief queen, रावणस्य Ravana's, मनः कान्ताम् dear his to heart, शुभाम् auspicious, महतीम् great, शालाम् hall, ददर्श saw.

#### Explanation

Then he proceeded to an auspicious hall, which was Ravana's favourite like his chief queen dear to his heart.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

मणिसोपानविकृतां हेमजालविभूषिताम्। स्फाटिकैरावृततलां दन्तान्तरितरूपिकाम्।।5.9.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मणिसोपानाविकृताम् staircase inlaid with special gems, हेमजालविभूषिताम् latticed with gold, स्फाटिकैः with crystals, आवृततलाम् floors covered with, दन्तान्तरितरूपिकाम् inlaid with silver set in ivory frame.

#### Explanation

The stairways of the mansion were inlaid with special gems, windows were latticed with gold, floors were covered with crystals, figures of ivory with silver.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

मुक्ताभिश्च प्रवालैश्च रूप्यचामीकरैरपि। विभूषितां मणिस्तम्भैस्सुबहुस्तम्भभूषिताम्।।5.9.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मुक्ताभिश्च with pearls, प्रवालैश्च with corals also, रूप्यचामीकरैरपि with silver and gold, मणिस्तम्भैः with pillars inlaid with gems, विभूषिताम् ornamented, सुबहुस्तम्भभूषिताम् with pillars.

#### Explanation

It was decorated with pillars of silver and gold inlaid with pearls and corals and gems.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

नम्रैरृजुभिरत्युच्चैस्समन्तात्सुविभूषितैः। स्तम्भै: पक्षैरिवात्युच्चैर्दिवं संप्रस्थितामिव।।5.9.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नम्रैः bent, ऋजुभिः straight ones, अत्युच्चै: with very lofty, समन्तात् all over, सुविभूषितैः well decorated, स्तम्भैः with columns, अत्युच्छैः long, पक्षैः with wings, दिवम् heaven, सम्प्रस्थितामिव as if started to reach

#### Explanation

It had highly ornamented pillars all over, some slightly bent and others straight. With lofty columns looking like wings it appeared as if the mansion had started on its journey to heaven.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

महत्या कुथयास्तीर्णां पृथिवीलक्षणाङ्कया। पृथिवीमिव विस्तीर्णां सराष्ट्रगृहमालिनीम्।।5.9.26।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पृथिवीलक्षणाङ्कया with several murals of earth, महत्या with expansive, कुथया with a carpet, आस्तीर्णाम् covered, सराष्ट्रगृहमालिनीम् having the garland of places and houses drawn on it, विस्तीर्णाम् expansive, पृथिवीमिव like the earth itself.

#### Explanation

The floor was covered with a wide carpet spread from wall to wall with designs of different murals of many places and houses drawn in a row (garland like). The big carpet appeared like the expanse of earth itself.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

नादितां मत्तविहगैर्दिव्यगन्धाधिवासिताम्। परार्ध्यास्तरणोपेतां रक्षोधिपनिषेविताम्।।5.9.27।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मत्तविहगैः with birds in heat, नादिताम् resounding, दिव्यगन्धाधिवासिताम् scented with divine fragrance, परार्ध्यास्तरणोपेताम् laid with valuable tapestries, रक्षोधिपनिषेविताम् inhabitted by the king of demons.

#### Explanation

The mansion was resounding with the noise of birds singing in heat. It had wonderful fragrance. It was laid with exquisite tapestries and served as a dwelling place for the king of demons.

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

धूम्रामगरुधूपेन विमलां हंसपाण्डुराम्। चित्रां पुष्पोपहारेण कल्माषीमिव सुप्रभाम्।।5.9.28।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अगरुधूपेन with smoke of agaru, धूम्राम् smoky, विमलाम् pure white, हंसपाण्डुराम् white like swan, पुष्पोपहारेण due to offering of flowers, चित्राम् colourful, सुप्रभाम् shining, कल्माषीमिव like the wishfulfilling cow.

#### Explanation

It was smoky with fumes of agaru incence though spotless like a swan. It was colourful and radiant like Kamadhenu, the wishfulfilling cow, because of the offering of flowers.

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

मनस्संह्लादजननीं वर्णस्यापि प्रसादिनीम्। तां शोकनाशिनीं दिव्यां श्रियः सञ्जननीमिव।।5.9.29।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मनः संह्लादजननीम् one which was soul ravishing, वर्णस्यापि with colour and complexion, प्रसादिनीम् pleasing, शोकनाशिनीम् banished sorrow, श्रियः of prosperity, सञ्जननीमिव like the source, दिव्याम् wonderful, ताम् it was.

#### Explanation

(The mansion) was so full of effulgence and soul ravishing that it served as an adornment even to splendour. It looked as though it banished all sorrow and was a source of prosperity.

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थैस्तु पञ्च पञ्चभिरुत्तमैः। तर्पयामास मातेव तदा रावणपालिता।।5.9.30।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रावणपालिता chamber of Ravana, तदा then, माता इव like a mother, उत्तमैः with excellent, पञ्चभिः with the five, इन्द्रियार्थैः with objects of the senses, पञ्च five, इन्द्रियाणि sense organs, तर्पयामास satisfied.

#### Explanation

The chamber of Ravana, like a mother, gratified all the five senses of Hanuman with proper objects of the senses.

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

स्वर्गोऽयं देवलोकोऽयमिन्द्रस्येयं पुरी भवेत्। सिद्धिर्वेयं परा हि स्यादित्यमन्यत मारुतिः।।5.9.31।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अयम् this, स्वर्गः heaven, अयम् this, देवलोकः world of gods, इयम् thus, इन्द्रस्य Indra's, पुरी city (of Amaravati), भवेत् may be, इयम् this one, परा supreme, सिद्धि: achievement, स्यात् may be, इति thus, मारुतिः Maruti, अमन्यत thought.

#### Explanation

'This may be heaven or world of gods. It may be Amaravati, the abode of Indra or else it may be the supreme achievment (of the Creator), thought Maruti৷৷

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

प्रध्यायत इवापश्यत्प्रदीपांस्तत्र काञ्चनान्। धूर्तानिव महाधूतैर्देवनेन पराजितान्।।5.9.32।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाधूर्तै: with expert gamblers, देवनेन by a game of dice, पराजिताम् defeated, धूर्तानिव like the gamblers, प्रध्यायत इव like one who is deep in thought, काञ्चनान् golden, प्रदीपान् lamps, आपश्यत् he saw.

#### Explanation

Seeing the golden lamps that stood burning steadily, Hanuman thought they looked like  gamblers in deep thought defeated by expert  gamblers in a game of dice.

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

दीपानां च प्रकाशेन तेजसा रावणस्य च। अर्चिर्भिर्भूषणानां च प्रदीप्तेत्यभ्यमन्यत।।5.9.33।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दीपानाम् of the lamps, प्रकाशेन by the glow, रावणस्य Ravana's, तेजसा च and by his brightness, भूषणानाम् of ornaments, अर्चिर्भिः च by the glow spread there, प्रदीप्ता इति as if glowing like fire, अमन्यत he thought in his mind.

#### Explanation

Hanuman fancied that the entire place was set ablaze with the glow of lamps, the lustre of Ravana and the brilliant glow of Ravana's ornaments (everything was shining).

---

### Shloka 34

#### Shloka Text

ततोऽपश्यत्कुथाऽऽसीनं नानावरायम्बरस्रजम्। सहस्रं वरनारीणां नानावेषविभूषितम्।।5.9.34।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, कुथासीनम् seated on carpets, नानावर्णाम्बरस्रजम् wearing clothes and garlands of different colours, नानावेषविभूषितम् dressed various clothes, वरनारीणाम् of beautiful women, सहस्रम् thousand, अपश्यत् he saw.

#### Explanation

He found thousands of beautiful women seated on carpets wearing different clothes and garlands and dressed in various attires.

---

### Shloka 35

#### Shloka Text

परिवृत्तेऽर्धरात्रे तु पाननिद्रावशं गतम्। क्रीडित्वोपरतं रात्रौ सुष्वाप बलवत्तदा।।5.9.35।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रात्रौ at night, क्रीडित्वा having sported, उपरतम् wearied, पाननिद्रावशं गतम् succumbed to drinks and sleep, तदा then, अर्धरात्रे when midnight, परिवृत्ते rolled away, बलवत् compelled, सुष्वाप to sleep.

#### Explanation

Drinking and sporting until midnight, they had dropped off to sleeptired.

---

### Shloka 36

#### Shloka Text

तत्प्रसुप्तं विरुरुचे निश्शब्दान्तरभूषणम्। निःशब्दहंसभ्रमरं यथा पद्मवनं महत्।।5.9.36।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नि:शब्दान्तरभूषणम् the sounds of ornaments stilled, प्रसुप्तम् slept, तत् that, निःशब्दहंसभ्रमरम् like silent swans and bees, महत् great, पद्मवनं यथा resembling a forest of lotuses, विरुरुचे looked beautiful.

#### Explanation

Fast asleep, sounds of their ornaments silenced, the young women looked like a large bed of lotuses with swans and bees sitting calm without their usual noise.

---

### Shloka 37

#### Shloka Text

तासां संवृतदन्तानि मीलिताक्षीणि मारुतिः। अपश्यत्पद्मगन्धीनि वदनानि सुयोषिताम्।।5.9.37।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मारुतिः son of the Windgod, तासां सुयोषिताम् of those wellbred young women, संवृतदन्तानि with teeth concealed, मीलिताक्षीणि closed eyes, पद्मगन्धीनि giving out smell of lotuses, वदनानि faces, अपश्यत् looked.

#### Explanation

Hanuman looked at the faces of the wellbred young women with closed eyes and concealed teeth, fragrant like lotuses.

---

### Shloka 38

#### Shloka Text

प्रबुद्धानीव पद्मानि तासां भूत्वा क्षपाक्षये। पुनस्संवृतपत्राणि रात्राविव बभुस्तदा।।5.9.38।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तासाम् of those women, क्षपाक्षये at the end of night, प्रबुद्धानि from early morning, पद्मानीव like lotuses, भूत्वा having been, पुनः again, रात्रौ at night, संवृतपत्राणिव closing up their petals, तदा then, बभुः looked.

#### Explanation

The faces of the women looked like blooming lotuses at daybreak, their petals closed up again at night.

---

### Shloka 39

#### Shloka Text

इमानि मुखपद्मानि नियतं मत्तषट्पदाः। अम्बुजानीव फुल्लानि प्रार्थयन्ति पुनः पुनः।।5.9.39।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मत्तषट्पदाः intoxicated bees, इमानि these, मुखपद्मानि lotuslike faces, फुल्लानि bloomed, अम्बुजानीव like lotuses, पुनः पुनः again and again, नियतम् always, प्रार्थयन्ति desire.

#### Explanation

The intoxicated bees would again and again long to enjoy these beautiful faces which were like blooming lotuses.

---

### Shloka 40

#### Shloka Text

इति चामन्यत श्रीमानुपपत्त्या महाकपिः। मेने हि गुणतस्तानि समानि सलिलोद्भवैः।।5.9.40।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

श्रीमान् the illustrious, महाकपिः great monkey, उपपत्त्या ascertained to be true, इति thus, अमन्यत felt, तानि those, गुणतः by their qualities, सलिलोद्भवैः with waterborn lotuses, समानि  similar मेने हि he thought.

#### Explanation

The illustrious Hanuman felt it to be correct to compare those faces with lotuses for their fragrance.

---

### Shloka 41

#### Shloka Text

सा तस्य शुशुभे शाला ताभिस्त्रीभिर्विराजिता। शारदीव प्रसन्ना द्यौस्ताराभिरभिशोभिता।।5.9.41।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तस्य his (Ravana's), सा शाला that mansion, ताभिः स्त्रीभिः by the women, विराजिता shone, ताराभिः with stars, अभिशोभिता very endearing, प्रसन्ना pleasant, शारदी autumnal, द्यौः इव sky like, शुशुभे seemed.

#### Explanation

Brightened by (the beauty of) the maidens, the mansion shone like the autumnal sky endearing  and pleasing with stars.

---

### Shloka 42

#### Shloka Text

स च ताभिः परिवृतश्शुशुभे राक्षसाधिपः। यथा ह्युडुपतिश्शीमांस्ताराभिरभिसंवृतः।।5.9.42।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ताभिः with them, परिवृतः surrounded, सः राक्षसाधिपः the lord of demons, ताराभिः with stars, अभिसंवृतः encircled, श्रीमान् beautiful, उडुपतिः यथा like the Moon, शुशुभे shone.

#### Explanation

The lord of demons surrounded by women shone like the beautiful Moon encircled by stars.

---

### Shloka 43

#### Shloka Text

याश्च्यवन्तेऽम्बरात्ताराः पुण्यशेषसमावृताः। इमास्तास्सङ्गताः कृत्स्ना इति मेने हरिस्तदा।।5.9.43।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

याः those, ताराः stars, पुण्यशेषसमावृताः with left over merits, अम्बरात् from the sky, च्यवन्ते fallen, ताः those, कृत्स्नाः entirely, इमाः these women, सङ्गताः collected together, इति thus, तदा then, हरिः the monkey, मेने thought.

#### Explanation

Seeing those women lying together Hanuman imagined them to be numerous meteors dropped from heaven after enjoying their merits, enjoyed and exhausted.

---

### Shloka 44

#### Shloka Text

ताराणामिव सुव्यक्तं महतीनां शुभार्चिषाम्। प्रभावर्णप्रसादाश्च विरेजुस्तत्र योषिताम्।।5.9.44।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तत्र there, योषिताम् of the young women, प्रभावर्णप्रसादाश्च lustre and grace, शुभार्चिषाम् releasing auspicious glow, महतीनाम् of the great, ताराणामिव like the stars, सुव्यक्तम् clearly, विरेजुः looked brilliant.

#### Explanation

It was clear that the grace and lustre of those women looked brilliant like the lustre and grace of brilliant stars releasing an auspicious glow.

---

### Shloka 45

#### Shloka Text

व्यावृत्तगुरुपीनस्रक्प्रकीर्णवरभूषणाः। पानव्यायामकालेषु निद्रापहृतचेतसः।।5.9.45।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पानव्यायामकालेषु drinking and sexual exercise, व्यावृत्तगुरुपीनस्रक्प्रकीर्णवरभूषणाः jewels scattered and garlands displaced, निद्रापहृतचेतसः minds dazed in slumber.

#### Explanation

Garlands displaced and jewels scattered, they were drowsy due to drink and sexual exercise.

---

### Shloka 46

#### Shloka Text

व्यावृत्ततिलकाः काश्चित्काश्चिदुद्भ्रन्तनूपुराः। पार्श्वे गलितहाराश्च काश्चित्परमयोषितः।।5.9.46।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कश्चित् a few, परमयोषितः other women, व्यावृत्ततिलकाः had vermilion mark (on the forehead) smudged, काश्चित् and a few, उद्भ्रान्तनूपुराः anklets let loose, काश्चित् a few, पार्श्वे on one side, गलितहाराश्च neckalces dislodged.

#### Explanation

A few young women had their circular vermilion mark (on the forehead) smudged, some had their anklets loosened and a few had their necklaces.

---

### Shloka 47

#### Shloka Text

मुक्ताहाराऽवृताश्चान्याः काश्चिद्विस्रस्तवाससः। व्याविद्धरशनादामाः किशोर्य इव वाहिताः।।5.9.47।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अन्याः others, मुक्ताहारावृताः had broken pearl strings on girdle, काश्चित् some, विस्रस्तवाससः had their clothes slipped off, व्यावद्धिरशनादामाः waist belts snapped, वाहिताः fallen to rest, किशोर्यः इव like young mares.

#### Explanation

Some had their strings of pearl broken, others had their clothes slipped off, and some had their waist belts snapped. They were like young mares fallen to rest after carrying heavy loads.

---

### Shloka 48

#### Shloka Text

सुकुण्डलधराश्चान्या विच्छिन्नमृदितस्रजः। गजेन्द्रमृदिताः फुल्ला लता इव महावने।।5.9.48।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सुकुण्डलधराः good eartops, अन्याः others, विच्छिन्नमृदितस्रजः with broken and crushed flower garlands, महावने in the dense forest, गजेन्द्रमृदिताः crushed by mighty elephants, फुल्लाः blooming, लताः इव like creepers.

#### Explanation

With their good eartops broken and flower garlands crushed some looked like blooming creepers in a dense forest crushed by mighty elephants.

---

### Shloka 49

#### Shloka Text

चन्द्रांशुकिरणाभाश्च हाराः कासांचिदुत्कटाः। हंसा इव बभुस्सुप्ताः स्तनमध्येषु योषिताम्।।5.9.49।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कासांचित् some, योषिताम् of young women, स्तनमध्येषु between their breasts, उत्कटाः large, चन्द्रांशुकिरणाभाश्च resembling the rays of the Moon, हाराः chains, सुप्ताः sleeping, हंसाः इव like the swans, बभुः appeared.

#### Explanation

The pearl chains, large and shining like rays of the Moon in the space between the breasts looked like sleeping swans.

---

### Shloka 50

#### Shloka Text

अपरासां च वैडूर्याः कादम्बा इव पक्षिणः। हेमसूत्राणि चान्यासां चक्रवाका इवाभवन्।।5.9.50।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

परासां च and for some, वैडूर्याः vaidurya, कादम्बाः kadamba, पक्षिणः इव like birds, अन्यासाम् of others, हेमसूत्राणि golden chains ,चक्रवाकाः इव like ruddy geese, अभवन् seemed.

#### Explanation

The vaidurya worn by women (on the breasts) looked like Kadamba birds while the golden chains put on by some others appeared like ruddy geese.

---

### Shloka 51

#### Shloka Text

हंसकारण्डवाकीर्णाश्चक्रवाकोपशोभिताः। आपगा इव ता रेजुर्जघनैः पुलिनैरिव।।5.9.51।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पुलिनैरिव like river banks, जघनैः with hips, ताः those women, हंसकारण्डवाकीर्णाः crowded with swans and Karandava birds, चक्रवाकोपशोभिताः abounded with ruddy geese, आपगा इव like rivers, रेजुः were shining.

#### Explanation

Their hips were like river banks crowded with swans, karandava and Chakravaka birds shining like rivers.

---

### Shloka 52

#### Shloka Text

किङ्किणीजालसङ्कोशास्ता हैमविपुलाम्बुजाः। भावग्राहा यशस्तीराः सुप्ता नद्य इवाऽऽबभुः।।5.9.52।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

किङ्किणीजालसङ्कोशाः tiny bells tied round the waist looking like buds, हैमविपुलाम्बुजाः bold golden ornaments looking like lotuses, भावग्राहाः their amorous gestures of love play like crocodiles, यशस्तीराः radiant beauty of silver banks, सुप्ताः sleeping, नद्यः इव resembled rivers, आबभुः appeared.

#### Explanation

With their tiny bells round their girdle looking like buds, the bold golden ornaments like lotuses, their gestures of love like crocodiles and their radiant beauty like silver banks, the women looked like smoky rivers.

---

### Shloka 53

#### Shloka Text

मृदुष्वङ्गेषु कासाञ्चित्कुचाग्रेषु च संस्थिताः। बभुर्वर्भूषणानीव शुभा भूषणराजयः।।5.9.53।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कासांचित् for some women, मृदुषु on their tender, अङ्गेषु limbs, कुचाग्रेषु च and on the nipples of breasts, संस्थिताः set, शुभाः beautiful, भूषणराजयः collections of ornaments, भूषणानीव like ornaments, बभूवुः appeared.

#### Explanation

The beautiful imprints made by the (heavy) ornaments on the tender limbs, breasts and nipples of some women appeared like many ornaments.

---

### Shloka 54

#### Shloka Text

अंशुकान्ताश्च कासाञ्चिन्मुखमारुतकम्पिताः। उपर्युपरि वक्त्राणां व्याधूयन्ते पुनः पुनः।।5.9.54।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कासाञ्चित् for some, मुखमारुतकम्पिताः shaken by the breath released by their mouth, अंशुकान्ताश्च the fringes of garments, वक्त्राणाम् on their faces, उपर्युपरि drawn, पुनः पुनः again and again, व्याधूयन्ते fluttered.

#### Explanation
