न रामेण वियुक्ता सा स्वप्तुमर्हति भामिनी। न भोक्तुं वाप्यलङ्कर्तुं न पानमुपसेवितुम्।।5.11.2।। नान्यं नरमुपस्थातुं सुराणामपि चेश्वरम्। न हि रामसमः कश्चिद्विद्यते त्रिदशेष्वपि।।5.11.3।। अन्येयमिति निश्चित्य पानभूमौ चचार सः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भामिनी lovely lady, रामेण about Rama, वियुक्ता separated, स्वप्तुम् to sleep, न अर्हति will not, भोक्तुम् न not to eat, अलङ्कर्तुम् to decorate, अपि also, न not, पानम् drinking, उपसेवितुम् to serve, सुराणाम् among gods, ईश्वरमपि even the lord, अन्यम् and others, नरम् man, उपस्थातुम् to approach, न not, हि indeed, रामसमः equal to Rama, कश्चित् any one, त्रिदशेष्वपि even among gods, न विद्यते हि not exist, इयम् this lady, अन्या other, इति thus, निश्चित्य having decided, सः he, पानभूमौ at the banquet, चचार strolled.

#### Explanation

'Lovely Sita, being away from Rama, will not sleep, will not eat or drink or adorn herself. How can she approach any other man even if he is the king of gods for there is none equal to Rama even among gods'. Having thus decided, Hanuman strolled about the banquet hall.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

क्रीडितेनापराः क्लान्ता गीतेन च तथाऽपराः।।5.11.4।। नृत्तेन चापराः क्लान्ताः पानविप्रहतास्तथा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पराः other women, क्रीडितेन by sporting, क्लान्ताः fatigued, तथा similarly, अपराः others, गीतेन due to singing, अपराः others, नृत्तेन by dancing, क्लान्ताः tired out, तथा so also, पानविप्रहताः  worn out due to continuous drinking.

#### Explanation

Some women were fatigued after sporting, similarly some were exhausted after singing. Others were tired through dancing and some were worn out by continuous drinking.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

मुरजेषु मृदङ्गेषु पीठिकासु च संस्थिताः।।5.11.5।। तथाऽऽस्तरणमुख्येषु संविष्टाश्चापराः स्त्रियः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मुरजेषु on murajas, मृदङ्गेषु on tabors, पीठिकासु च on hassocks, संस्थिताः resting, अपराः other, स्त्रियः women, आस्तरणमुख्येषु on exquisite beds, संविष्टाः lying.

#### Explanation

Some women reclined on tabors, some on murajas, and some on drums and hassocks while some slept on excellent couches.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

अङ्गनानां सहस्रेण भूषितेन विभूषणैः।।5.11.6।। रूपसल्लापशीलेन युक्तगीतार्थभाषिणा। देशकालाभियुक्तेन युक्तवाक्याभिधायिना।।5.11.7।। रताभिरतसंसुप्तं ददर्श हरियूथपः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विभूषणैः with ornaments, भूषितेन adorned, रूपसल्लापशीलेन good at arguments, युक्तगीतार्थभाषिणा versed in appreciation, देशकालाभियुक्तेन acquainted with the appropriateness of time and place, युक्तवाक्याभिधायिना good at appropriate expressions, अङ्गनानाम् of women, सहस्रेण thousands, रताभिरतसंसुप्तम् sleeping after long sexual intercourse, हरियूथपः monkey leader, ददर्श saw.

#### Explanation

The monkey leader saw thousands of  beautiful women asleep after long dalliance. Some of them were adorned with ornaments, some were good at arguments and versed in the appreciation of songs, some were aware of the appropriate time and place and some were adept in right expressions.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

अङ्गनानां सहस्रेण भूषितेन विभूषणैः।।5.11.6।। रूपसल्लापशीलेन युक्तगीतार्थभाषिणा। देशकालाभियुक्तेन युक्तवाक्याभिधायिना।।5.11.7।। रताभिरतसंसुप्तं ददर्श हरियूथपः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विभूषणैः with ornaments, भूषितेन adorned, रूपसल्लापशीलेन good at arguments, युक्तगीतार्थभाषिणा versed in appreciation, देशकालाभियुक्तेन acquainted with the appropriateness of time and place, युक्तवाक्याभिधायिना good at appropriate expressions, अङ्गनानाम् of women, सहस्रेण thousands, रताभिरतसंसुप्तम् sleeping after long sexual intercourse, हरियूथपः monkey leader, ददर्श saw.

#### Explanation

The monkey leader saw thousands of  beautiful women asleep after long dalliance. Some of them were adorned with ornaments, some were good at arguments and versed in the appreciation of songs, some were aware of the appropriate time and place and some were adept in right expressions.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

तासां मध्ये महाबाहुः शुशुभे राक्षसेश्वरः।।5.11.8।। गोष्ठे महति मुख्यानां गवां मध्ये यथा वृषः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तासाम् their, मध्ये in the midst, महाबाहुः longarmed, राक्षसेश्वरः lord of ogres, महति a big, गोष्ठे in a shed, गवाम् of cows, मध्ये in the midst, वृषः इव like a bull, शुशुभे  shone.

#### Explanation

In the midst of these women the longarmed lord of demons shone like a (majestic) bull in a big cowshed.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

स राक्षसेन्द्रः शुशुभे ताभिः परिवृतस्स्वयम्।।5.11.9।। करेणुभिर्यथाऽरण्ये परिकीर्णो महाद्विपः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ताभिः by those women, परिवृतः surrounded, सः राक्षसेन्द्रः the demon king, स्वयम् himself, महारण्ये in a great forest, करेणुभिः with female elphants, परिकीर्णः scattered, महाद्विपः यथा like a proud elephant, शुशुभे shone.

#### Explanation

Surrounded by the women, the demon king shone like a proud elephant attended by many female elephants scattered in a dense forest.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

सर्वकामैरुपेतां च पानभूमिं महात्मनः।।5.11.10।। ददर्श हरिशार्दूलस्तस्य रक्षःपतेर्गृहे।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हरिशार्दूलः tiger among vanaras, महात्मनः of the great, तस्य his, रक्षःपते: Ravana's, गृहे in the palace, सर्वकामैः with all his, उपेताम् provided, पानभूमिं च drinking place, ददर्श saw.

#### Explanation

The tiger among vanaras saw at the great Ravana's palace a drinking place where everything one desires was provided.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

मृगाणां महिषाणां च वराहाणां च भागशः।।5.11.11।। तत्र न्यस्तानि मांसानि पानभूमौ ददर्श सः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः he, तत्र there, पानभूमौ at the drinking hall, भागशः in portions, न्यस्तानि placed, मृगाणाम् on the deer, महिषाणां च buffaloes, वराहाणां च pigs, मांसानि meat, ददर्श saw.

#### Explanation

There at the drinking hall the meat of deer, buffaloes and boars portioned and placed.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

रौक्मेषु च विशालेषु भाजनेष्वर्धभक्षितान्।।5.11.12।। ददर्श हरिशार्दूलो मयूरान् कुक्कुटांस्तथा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हरिशार्दूलः tiger among vanaras, रौक्मेषु in golden, विशालेषु in large ones, भाजनेषु vessels, अर्धभक्षितान् half eaten, मयूरान् peacocks, तथा so also, कुक्कुटान् chicken, ददर्श saw.

#### Explanation

The tiger among vanaras also saw there meat of peacocks and chicken placed in large golden vessels, some half eaten.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

वराहवार्ध्राणसकान् दधिसौवर्चलायुतान्।।5.11.13।। शल्यान् मृगमयूरांश्च हनुमानन्ववैक्षत।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दधिसौवर्चलायुतान् marinated with yoghurt and special salt, वराहवार्ध्राणसकान् meat of pigs and jungle fowls, हनुमान् Hanuman, अन्ववैक्षत observed, शल्यान् bones, मृगमयूरांश्च deer and peacocks.

#### Explanation

Hanuman saw the meat of pigs and jungle fowls, deer, peacocks and bones marinated with yoghurt and special kinds of salt.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

क्रकरान्विविधान् सिद्धांश्चकोरानर्धभक्षितान्।।5.11.14।। महिषानेकशल्यांश्च छागांश्च कृतनिष्ठितान्। लेह्यानुच्चावचान्पेयान् भोज्यानि विविधानि च।।5.11.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विविधान् several types, सिद्धान् cooked, क्रकरान् fowls, अर्धभक्षितान् half eaten, चकोरान् ruddy geese, महिषान् buffaloes, एकशल्यांश्च fishes, छागांश्च goats, उच्चवचान् all sorts of them, लेह्यान् varieties of food that can be licked, पेयान् drinks, विविधानि of many kinds, भोज्यानि eatables.

#### Explanation

He saw several kinds of (nonvegetarian) food like cooked meat of fowls, ruddy geese, buffaloes, goats, fishes as well as food for licking. (Food is of four kinds  1) भक्ष्य which can be chewed 2) भोज्य ordinary solid, semisolid food which can be consumed  3) लेह्य food for licking  4) चोष्य liquid food (which can be sipped)).

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

क्रकरान्विविधान् सिद्धांश्चकोरानर्धभक्षितान्।।5.11.14।। महिषानेकशल्यांश्च छागांश्च कृतनिष्ठितान्। लेह्यानुच्चावचान्पेयान् भोज्यानि विविधानि च।।5.11.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विविधान् several types, सिद्धान् cooked, क्रकरान् fowls, अर्धभक्षितान् half eaten, चकोरान् ruddy geese, महिषान् buffaloes, एकशल्यांश्च fishes, छागांश्च goats, उच्चवचान् all sorts of them, लेह्यान् varieties of food that can be licked, पेयान् drinks, विविधानि of many kinds, भोज्यानि eatables.

#### Explanation

He saw several kinds of (nonvegetarian) food like cooked meat of fowls, ruddy geese, buffaloes, goats, fishes as well as food for licking. (Food is of four kinds  1) भक्ष्य which can be chewed 2) भोज्य ordinary solid, semisolid food which can be consumed  3) लेह्य food for licking  4) चोष्य liquid food (which can be sipped)).

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

तथाऽऽम्ललवणोत्तंसैर्विविधैरागषाडबैः। हारनूपुरकेयूरैरपविद्धैर्महाधनैः।।5.11.16।। पानभाजनविक्षिप्तैः फलैश्च विविधैरपि। कृतपुष्पोपहारा भूरधिकं पुष्यति श्रियम्।।5.11.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तथा so also, आम्ललवणोत्तंसैः seasoned with sour and salty ingredients, विविधैः with many types, रागषाडबैः with syrups, अपविद्धै: with discarded, महाधनैः with heavy, हारनूपुरकेयूरैः with chains, anklets, shoulder straps, पानभाजनविक्षिप्तै: with those drinks spilt from the glasses, विविधैः with many, फलैश्च fruits, कृतपुष्पोपहारा with the offerings flowers of, भूः the earth, अधिकम् greatly, श्रियम् rich, पुष्यति looked splendid.

#### Explanation

The banquet hall looked splendid with food seasoned with salt and sour ingredients. There was ragashadavas (syrup made with grapes, pomegranates, different juices like half ripe mangoes and seasoned with ginger cardomom, butter etc). Heavy chains, anklets and shouldersstraps were thrown aside. (The people relaxed while eating and drinking and discarded heavy ornaments). Many glasses were found scattered or drinks spilt on the floor after use.Many fruits and flowers were kept.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

तथाऽऽम्ललवणोत्तंसैर्विविधैरागषाडबैः। हारनूपुरकेयूरैरपविद्धैर्महाधनैः।।5.11.16।। पानभाजनविक्षिप्तैः फलैश्च विविधैरपि। कृतपुष्पोपहारा भूरधिकं पुष्यति श्रियम्।।5.11.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तथा so also, आम्ललवणोत्तंसैः seasoned with sour and salty ingredients, विविधैः with many types, रागषाडबैः with syrups, अपविद्धै: with discarded, महाधनैः with heavy, हारनूपुरकेयूरैः with chains, anklets, shoulder straps, पानभाजनविक्षिप्तै: with those drinks spilt from the glasses, विविधैः with many, फलैश्च fruits, कृतपुष्पोपहारा with the offerings flowers of, भूः the earth, अधिकम् greatly, श्रियम् rich, पुष्यति looked splendid.

#### Explanation

The banquet hall looked splendid with food seasoned with salt and sour ingredients. There was ragashadavas (syrup made with grapes, pomegranates, different juices like half ripe mangoes and seasoned with ginger cardomom, butter etc). Heavy chains, anklets and shouldersstraps were thrown aside. (The people relaxed while eating and drinking and discarded heavy ornaments). Many glasses were found scattered or drinks spilt on the floor after use.Many fruits and flowers were kept.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

तत्र तत्र च विन्यस्तै: सुश्लिष्टैश्शयनासनैः। पानभूमिर्विना वह्निं प्रदीप्तेवोपलक्ष्यते।।5.11.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पानभूमिः banquet hall, तत्र तत्र here and there, विन्यस्तै: arranged, सुश्लिष्टैः wellarranged, शयनासनैः with beds and seats, विना वह्निम् without fire, प्रदीप्तेव as if glowing, उपलक्ष्यते seemed.

#### Explanation

Good couches and different kinds of seats were wellarranged in the banquet hall. The place appeared as if it was glowing witout fire.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

बहुप्रकारैर्विविधैर्वरसंस्कारसंस्कृतैः। मांसैः कुशलसम्पृक्तैः पानभूमिगतैः पृथक्।।5.11.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

बहुप्रकारैः in many ways, विविधैः with many kinds, नरसंस्कारसंस्कृतैः by those seasoneed with many types of ingredients, कुशलसम्पृक्तै: cooked by experts, पृथक् separately, पानभूमिगतैः at the banquet hall, मांसैः with meat.

#### Explanation

In the banquet hall there were many types of meat arranged in a number of ways. They were cooked by experts by seasoning them with choicest ingredients.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

दिव्याः प्रसन्ना विविधाः सुराः कृतसुरा अपि। शर्कराऽसवमाध्वीकपुष्पासवफलासवाः।।5.11.20।। वासचूर्णैश्च विविधैर्मृष्टास्तैस्तैः पृथक् पृथक्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दिव्याः wonderful, प्रसन्नाः pleasing, विविधाः many, सुराः wines, शर्करासवमाध्वीकपुष्पासवफलासवाः sugercanejuices, honey, juices made from flowers and fruits, कृतसुराः अपि even though fermented, तैस्तै: as mentioned earlier, विविधैः with many, वासचूर्णैः with spice powders, पृथक् पृथक् diferent and, मृष्टाः were made delicious.

#### Explanation

There were many types of wonderful and pleasing wine extracted from sugarcane, honey , fruits and flowers. They were good even though fermented and made delicious by seasoning with aromatic spices.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

सन्तता शुशुभे भूमिर्माल्यैश्च बहुसंस्थितैः।।5.11.21।। हिरण्मयैश्च विविधैर्भाजनैः स्फाटिकैरपि। जाम्बूनदमयैश्चान्यैः करकैरभिसंवृता।।5.11.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

बहुसंस्थितैः arranged in varied styles, माल्यैश्च with garlands, संतता strectched, हिरण्मयैः with those made of gold, स्फाटिकैरपि and crystalware, विविधैः several kinds, भाजनैः with vessels, जाम्बूनदमयैः made of pure gold, अन्यैः by others, करकैः with jars, अभिसंवृता spread all over, भूमिः floor, शुशुभे looked  beautiful.

#### Explanation

(The banquet hall) appeared beautiful with many kinds of garlands arranged in different locations. Also there were vessels made of pure gold, crystalware and jars spread all over.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

सन्तता शुशुभे भूमिर्माल्यैश्च बहुसंस्थितैः।।5.11.21।। हिरण्मयैश्च विविधैर्भाजनैः स्फाटिकैरपि। जाम्बूनदमयैश्चान्यैः करकैरभिसंवृता।।5.11.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

बहुसंस्थितैः arranged in varied styles, माल्यैश्च with garlands, संतता strectched, हिरण्मयैः with those made of gold, स्फाटिकैरपि and crystalware, विविधैः several kinds, भाजनैः with vessels, जाम्बूनदमयैः made of pure gold, अन्यैः by others, करकैः with jars, अभिसंवृता spread all over, भूमिः floor, शुशुभे looked  beautiful.

#### Explanation

(The banquet hall) appeared beautiful with many kinds of garlands arranged in different locations. Also there were vessels made of pure gold, crystalware and jars spread all over.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

राजतेषु च कुम्भेषु जाम्बूनदमयेषु च। पानश्रेष्ठं तदा भूरि कपिस्तत्र ददर्श ह।।5.11.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कपिः monkey, तदा then, राजतेषु in those made of silver, जाम्बूनदमयेषु च in those made of pure gold, कुम्भेषु in pots, भूरि abundant, पानश्रेष्ठम् best of wines, ददर्श ह saw.

#### Explanation

Hanuman saw the best of wines in large quantities kept in silver and pure gold pots.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

﻿सोऽपश्यच्छातकुम्भानि सीधोर्मणिमयानि च। राजतानि च पूर्णानि भाजनानि महाकपिः।।5.11.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

﻿ स: महाकपिः that great monkey, शीधोः of wine, शातकुम्भानि made of gold, मणिमयानि च inlaid with gems also, राजतानि च and silver, पूर्णानि filled, भाजनानि vessels, अपश्यत् saw.

#### Explanation

﻿ The great monkey saw the wine jars made of gold and silver studded with crystals and gems.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

क्वचिदल्पावशेषाणि क्वचित्पीतानि सर्वशः। क्वचिन्नैव प्रपीतानि पानानि स ददर्श ह।।5.11.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

स he, क्वचित् in one place, अल्पावशेषाणि left over, क्वचित् at one place, सर्वशः fully, पीतानि drained, क्वचित् yet in another spot, नैव प्रपीतानि not touched even a little, पानानि drinks, ददर्श ह saw.

#### Explanation

He saw wine jars at one place, some fully drained, and at another place not touched at all.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

क्वचिद् भक्ष्यांश्च विविधान् क्वचित्पानानि भागशः। क्वचिदन्नावशेषाणि पश्यन्वै विचचार ह।।5.11.26।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

क्वचित् in one place, विविधान् many types, भक्ष्यांश्च eatables, क्वचित् in another place, भागशः separated, पानानि drinks, क्वचित् in another places, अन्नावशेषाणि left over food, पश्यन् while looking at, विचचार ह he moved about.

#### Explanation

He moved about looking at different types of eatables and drinks arranged separately along with the left over food.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

क्वचित्प्रभिन्नैः करकैः क्वचिदालोलितैर्घटैः। क्वचित्संपृक्तमाल्यानि जलानि च फलानि च।।5.11.27।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

क्वचित् in one place, प्रभिन्नैः with broken, करकैः with pots, क्वचित् at other place, आलोलितैः with rolling, घटैः with pots, क्वचित् somewhere else, संपृक्तमाल्यानि strewn and mixed up flower garlands, जलानि च and water, फलानि च and fruits.

#### Explanation

At some places he saw broken pots, at other places rolling pots and elsewhere flower garlands strewn and mixed up with water and fruits.

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

शयनान्यत्र नारीणां शुभ्राणि बहुधा पुनः। परस्परं समाश्लिष्य काश्चित्सुप्ता वराङ्गनाः।।5.11.28।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अत्र there, नारीणाम् women's, शयनानि beds, पुनः again, बहुधा severally, शुभ्राणि clean (unused) काश्चित् some, वराङ्गनाः lovely women, परस्परम् each other, समाश्लिष्य having embraced, सुप्ताः slept.

#### Explanation

Hanuman saw many beds some unused and some occupied by lovely women sleeping, embracing one another.

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

काश्चिच्च वस्त्रमन्यस्यास्स्वपन्त्याः परिधाय च। आहृत्य चाबलाः सुप्ता निद्राबलपराजिताः।।5.11.29।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

काश्चित् some, अबलाः women, निद्राबलपराजिताः succumbed to sleep, स्वपन्त्याः of sleeping women, अन्यस्याः others, वस्त्रम् clothes, आहृत्य after pulling, परिधाय covering their bodies with the same, सुप्ताः slept.

#### Explanation

Overcome with sleep some women pulled clothes of other women and covered their bodies with them.

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

तासामुच्छवासवातेन वस्त्रं माल्यं च गात्रजम्। नात्यर्थं स्पन्दते चित्रं प्राप्य मन्दमिवानिलम्।।5.11.30।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तासाम् their, गात्रजम् on their bodies, वस्त्रम् clothes, माल्यं च and garlands, उच्छवासवातेन by their exhaling, मन्दम् mildly, अनिलम् wind, प्राप्य इव as though caught, नात्यर्थम् by that, चित्रम् it is lovely, स्पन्दते moving.

#### Explanation

The lovely clothes and garlands on the bodies of women were moving mildly by their exhalations as if shaken by the gentle breeze.

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

चन्दनस्य च शीतस्य शीधोर्मधुरसस्य च। विविधस्य च माल्यस्य धूपस्य विविधस्य च।।5.11.31।। बहुधा मारुतस्तत्र गन्धं विविधमुद्वहन्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तत्र there, मारुतः the wind, शीतस्य of cool, चन्दनस्य  of  sandal, शीथोः of the wines, मधुरसस्य च of sweet smelling, विविधस्य of diverse, माल्यस्य and of garlands, विविधस्य of several types, धूपस्य च and of incense, विविधम् several, गन्धम् fragrances, बहुधा in many ways, उद्वहन्  blew.

#### Explanation

With the many scents of cool sandal, of sweetsmelling wines of diverse kinds, as well as flower garlands of several types and of incense burning in that hall, the cool wind blew spreading several fragrances all over.

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

स्नानानां चन्दनानां च धूपानां चैव मूर्छितः।।5.11.32।। प्रववौ सुरभिर्गन्धो विमाने पुष्पके तदा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, पुष्पके विमाने in the Pushpaka chariot, स्नानानाम् followed by bath, चन्दनानां च of sandals, धूपानां चैव also incense, सुरभिः sweet smelling wines, गन्धः fragrance, मूर्छितः spread, प्रववौ wafted through.

#### Explanation

The wind wafting through the Pushpaka chariot carried the fragrance of the cool sandalpaste used in bath and the sweet smell of wines and thick smoke of fragrance spread further.

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

श्यामावदातास्तत्रान्याः काश्चित्कृष्णा वराङ्गनाः।।5.11.33।। काश्चित् काञ्चनवर्णाङ्ग्यः प्रमदा राक्षसालये।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तत्र there, राक्षसालये in the abode of the ogres, अन्याः others, श्यामावदाताः of glowing dark complexion, काश्चित् some, वराङ्गनाः lovely women, कृष्णाः dark in colour, काश्चित् some, प्रमदाः women, काञ्चनवर्णाङ्ग्यः of golden complexion.

#### Explanation

There in the demons abode were women who were fair, some lovely with dark and others with golden complexion.

---

### Shloka 34

#### Shloka Text

तासां निद्रावशत्वाच्च मदनेन विमूर्छितम्।।5.11.34।। पद्मिनीनां प्रसुप्तानां रूपमासीद्यथैव हि।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

निद्रावशत्वाच्च overwhelmed with sleep, मदनेन च and due to dalliance, विमूर्छितम् exhausted, प्रसुप्तानाम् of the sleeping, तासाम् their, रूपम् charm, प्रसुप्तानाम् of the sleeping, पद्मिनीनाम् of lotus creepers, यथैव as, आसीत् was seen.

#### Explanation

Withered due to indulgence in sex they were overcome with sleep. The charm of the women was like the lotus creepers with closed lotuses .

---

### Shloka 35

#### Shloka Text

एवं सर्वमशेषेण रावणान्तः पुरं कपिः।।5.11.35।। ददर्श सुमहातेजाः न ददर्श च जानकीम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सुमहातेजाः brilliant, कपिः monkey, एवम् in that way, सर्वम् all, रावणान्तःपुरं harem of Ravana, अशेषेण thoroughly, ददर्श searched, जानकीं च Janaki, न ददर्श did not see.

#### Explanation

Brilliant Hanuman ransacked the harem of Ravana but found no Janaki.

---

### Shloka 36

#### Shloka Text

निरीक्षमाणश्च तदा ताः स्त्रियः स महाकपिः।।5.11.36।। जगाम महतीं चिन्तां धर्मसाध्वसशङ्कितः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, स्त्रियः women, निरीक्षमाणः while seeing, सः he, महाकपिः the great monkey, धर्मसाध्वसशङ्कितः thinking that he transgressed the moral code, महतीम् great, चिन्ताम् worry, जगाम experienced.

#### Explanation

The great monkey was very much worried as he thought he had transgressed the moral code by watching the women.

---

### Shloka 37

#### Shloka Text

परदारावरोधस्य प्रसुप्तस्य निरीक्षणम्।।5.11.37।। इदं खलु ममात्यर्थं धर्मलोपं करिष्यति।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

प्रसुप्तस्य of those sleeping, परदारावरोधस्य of the inner chambers of others' wives, इदम् this, मम on my part, निरीक्षणम् observing them, अत्यर्थम् very much, धर्मलोपम् trangression of moral code, करिष्यति will lead to.

#### Explanation

'Observing the wives of others in this manner in their inner chambers very much amounts to transgression of moral code  by me'. ( thought he)

---

### Shloka 38

#### Shloka Text

न हि मे परदाराणां दृष्टिर्विषयवर्तिनी।।5.11.38।। अयं चात्र मया दृष्टः परदारापरिग्रहः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मे my, दृष्टिः to view, परदाराणाम् others' wives, विषयवर्तिनी sensual, न हि not indeed, अत्र there, मया by me, अयम् this, परदारापरिग्रहः others' wives, दृष्टश्च were seen.

#### Explanation

'I did see these wives of another, but not with a sensual mind. I have only seen them but not with foul intention'.

---

### Shloka 39

#### Shloka Text

तस्य प्रादुरभूच्चिन्ता पुनरन्या मनस्विनः।।5.11.39।। निश्चितैकान्तचित्तस्य कार्यनिश्चयदर्शिनी।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मनस्विनः highly sensible, निश्चितैकान्तचित्तस्य struck with a bright idea in a single direction, तस्य his, पुनः again, कार्यनिश्चयदर्शिनी the decision regarding the task ahead, अन्या other, चिन्ता thought, प्रादुरभूत् arose.

#### Explanation

Another  bright idea struck the highly sensible Hanuman, who had a clear vision of the task ahead-- 'I was firm and singleminded in my direction regarding the task ahead' (thought he).

---

### Shloka 40

#### Shloka Text

कामं दृष्टा मया सर्वा विश्वस्ता रावणस्त्रियः।।5.11.40।। न हि मे मनसः किञ्चिद्वैकृत्यमुपजायते।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विश्वस्ताः loyal, सर्वाः all, रावणस्त्रियः wives of Ravana, मया by me, कामं दृष्टाः are seen indeed without any foul desire, मे my, मनसः minds, किञ्चित् even a little, वैकृत्यम् passionate feeling, न उपजायते हि indeed not arise.

#### Explanation

'It is true I saw all the loyal wives of Ravana but without any foul desire. Indeed in my mind no passionate feeling was ever aroused'.

---

### Shloka 41

#### Shloka Text

मनो हि हेतुः सर्वेषामिन्द्रियाणां प्रवर्तने।।5.11.41।। शुभाशुभास्ववस्थासु तच्च मे सुव्यवस्थितम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शुभाशुभासु for good and bad, अवस्थासु in all stages, सर्वेषाम् for all, इन्द्रियाणाम् of sense organs, प्रवर्तने in reaction, मनः mind, हेतुः is instrumental, मे my, तच्च that also, सुव्यवस्थितम्  is firmly eastablished.

#### Explanation

'Mind propels the sense organs to do good or bad deeds. (But) my mind is firmly esatablished in righteousness'.

---

### Shloka 42

#### Shloka Text

नान्यत्र हि मया शक्या वैदेही परिमार्गितुम्।।5.11.42।। स्त्रियो हि स्त्रीषु दृश्यन्ते सदा सम्परिमार्गणे।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वैदेही Vaidehi, अन्यत्र at other places, परिमार्गितुम् to search, मया by me, न शक्या हि not possible indeed, सदा always, सम्परिमार्गणे during search, स्त्रियः women, स्त्रीषु among women, दृश्यन्ते can be seen.

#### Explanation

'It is not possible for me to look for Vaidehi elsewhere. During such search operations one always looks for women among women.'

---

### Shloka 43

#### Shloka Text

यस्य सत्त्वस्य या योनिस्तस्यां तत्परिमार्ग्यते। न शक्या प्रमदा नष्टा मृगीषु परिमार्गितुम्।।5.11.43।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

यस्य whose, सत्त्वस्य of a being, या whichever, योनिः source, तत् that, तस्याम् in that, परिमार्ग्यते is searched, नष्टा lost, प्रमदा woman, मृगीषु among female deer, परिमार्गितुम् to search, न शक्या not possible.

#### Explanation

'One has to search a creature among its own species. It is not possible to find a lost woman in the herd of female deer'.

---

### Shloka 44

#### Shloka Text

तदिदं मार्गितं तावच्छुद्धेन मनसा मया।।5.11.44।। रावणान्तःपुरं सर्वं दृश्यते न तु जानकी।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तत् that, मया by me, शुद्धेन with a pure, मनसा mind, इदम् this, सर्वम् all, रावणान्तःपुरम् Ravana's harem, मार्गितम् searched, जानकी तु but Janaki also, न दृश्यते is not seen.

#### Explanation

'I searched all over Ravana's harem with a pure mind but I have not seen Janaki'.

---

### Shloka 45

#### Shloka Text

देवगन्धर्वकन्याश्च नागकन्याश्च वीर्यवान्।।5.11.45।। अवेक्षमाणो हनुमान्नैवापश्यत जानकीम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

देवगन्धर्वकन्याश्च daughters of gods, gandharvas also, नागकन्याश्च and daughters of nagas, अवेक्षमाणः looked, वीर्यवान् valiant, हनुमान् Hanuman, जानकीम् Janaki, नैवापश्यत did not find.

#### Explanation

Even while valiant Hanuman was looking among the daughters of gods, gandharvas and nagas he could not find Janaki.

---

### Shloka 46

#### Shloka Text

तामपश्यन्कपिस्तत्र पश्यञ्श्चान्या परस्त्रियः।।5.11.46।। अपक्रम्य तदा वीरः प्रध्यातुमुपचक्रमे।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वीरः hero, कपिः vanara, तत्र there, ताम् her, अपश्यन् not able to see, अन्याः others, परस्त्रियः the wives of others, पश्यञ्श्च and while seeing, तदा then, अपक्रम्य having moved aside, प्रध्यातुम् to contemplate, उपचक्रमे started.

#### Explanation

Heroic Hanuman, unable to find Sita among the women, moved aside and started thinking.

---

### Shloka 47

#### Shloka Text

स भूयस्तु परं श्रीमान् मारुतिर्यत्नमास्थितः।।5.11.47।। आपानभूमिमुत्सृज्य तद्विचेतुं प्रचक्रमे।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

श्रीमान् Illustrious, सः मारुतिः Maruti, भूयः once again, परम् great, यत्नम् effort, आस्थितः made, आपानभूमिम् banquet hall, उत्सृज्य leaving, तत् that, विचेतुम् to search, प्रचक्रमे departed.

#### Explanation

Illustrious Hanuman left the banquet hall to renew his effort in search of Sita. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीये आदिकाव्ये सुन्दरकाण्डे एकादशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the eleventh sarga of Sundarakanda of the holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 12

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

स तस्य मध्ये भवनस्य मारुति र्लतागृहांश्चित्रगृहान्निशागृहान्। जगाम सीतां प्रति दर्शनोत्सुको न चैव तां पश्यति चारुदर्शनाम्।।5.12.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः मारुतिः Maruti, तस्य of that, भवनस्य mansion's, मध्ये midst, सीतां प्रति towards Sita, दर्शनोत्सुकः with eagerness to find, लतागृहान् homes of creepers, चित्रगृहान् picture galleries, निशागृहान् dormitories for rest at night, जगाम went, चारुदर्शनाम् beautiful lady, ताम् that Sita, न चैव पश्यति did not find her.

#### Explanation

Anxious to find Sita, Hanuman revisited the bowers of creepers, picture galleries and domitories located in the midst of the mansion, but  did not find that beautiful Sita.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

स चिन्तयामास ततो महाकपिः प्रियामपश्यन्रघुनन्दनस्य ताम्। ध्रुवं हि सीता म्रियते यथा न मे विचिन्वतो दर्शनमेति मैथिली।।5.12.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, सः महाकपिः that great monkey, रघुनन्दनस्य Rama's, प्रियाम् beloved, ताम् her, अपश्यन् while being unable to find, चिन्तयामास started thinking, मैथिली Mythili, विचिन्वतः while searching, मे to me, यथा as, दर्शनम् her sight, न उपैति not appears, सीता Sita, ध्रुवम् surely, म्रियते dead.

#### Explanation

The great monkey, unable to find the beloved of Rama thought Sita might not be surviving. So he started thinking, ' I could not see her even though I have searched all over'.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

सा राक्षसानां प्रवरेण जानकी स्वशीलसंरक्ष्णतत्परा सती। अनेन नूनं प्रति दुष्टकर्मणा हता भवेदार्यपथे परे स्थिता।।5.12.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

परे best, आर्यपथे on a noble path, स्थिता follower, सा जानकी Janaki,              स्वशीलसंरक्षणतत्परा सती who wishes to protect her chastity, प्रति दुष्टकर्मणा by this evil mind, अनेन by him, राक्षसानां प्रवरेण by the demon king, हता slayed, भवेत् is, नूनम् surely.

#### Explanation

'Janaki, follower of noble path, anxious to protect her chastity must have been killed by the wicked demon king.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

विरूपरूपा विकृता विवर्चसो महानना दीर्घविरूपदर्शनाः। समीक्ष्य सा राक्षसराजयोषितो भयाद्विनष्टा जनकेश्वरात्मजा।।5.12.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सा that, जनकेश्वरात्मजा daughter of king Janaka, विरूपरूपाः distorted forms, विकृताः ugly, विवर्चसः dull, महाननाः women with huge faces, दीर्घविरूपदर्शनाः tall and deformed, राक्षसराजयोषितः wives of the demon king, समीक्ष्य on looking, भयात् out of fear, विनष्टा died.

#### Explanation

'The daughter of Janaka might have collapsed out of fear, looking at the distorted faces of ugly, tall and monstrous demon maids of Ravana.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

सीतामदृष्ट्वा ह्यनवाप्य पौरुषं विहृत्य कालं सह वानरैश्चिरम्। न मेऽस्ति सुग्रीवसमीपगा गतिः सुतीक्ष्णदण्डो बलवांश्च वानरः।।5.12.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीताम् Sita, अदृष्ट्वा without seeing, पौरुषम् pride of achievement, अनवाप्य without achieving, वानरैः सह accompanied by vanaras, चिरं कालम् for a long time, विहृत्य having passed time, मे to myself, सुग्रीवसमीपगा to approach Sugriva, गतिः way, नास्ति not possible, वानरः vanara, सुतीक्ष्णदण्डः  will punish severely, बलवांश्च powerful.

#### Explanation

'I have spent a long time with vanaras. I have not  succeded in my efforts to find Sita. I dare not see Sugriva now without finding Sita and without fulfilling my task. Sugriva will punish me severely. There is no way out.'

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

दृष्टमन्तःपुरं सर्वं दृष्टा रावणयोषितः। न सीता दृश्यते साध्वीवृथा जातो मम श्रमः।।5.12.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सर्वम् everywhere, अन्तःपुरम् the harem, दृष्टम् seen, रावणयोषितः Ravana's women, दृष्टाः are seen, साध्वी noble lady, सीता Sita, न दृश्यते is not seen, मम my, श्रमः efforts, वृथा wasted, जातः gone.

#### Explanation

'I have searched all over the harem. Ravana's women have been seen. (Yet) I have not been able to see noble Sita. My efforts have been wasted'.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

किं नु मां वानराः सर्वे गतं वक्ष्यन्ति सङ्गताः। गत्वा तत्र त्वया वीर किं कृतं तद्वदस्व नः।।5.12.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

गतम् when I reach, माम् me, सङ्गताः together, सर्वे all, वानराः vanaras, किं नु what indeed, वक्ष्यन्ति they will speak, वीर O hero, तत्र there, गत्वा after going, त्वया by you, किम् what, कृतम् is done, तत् that, नः for us, वदस्व you will say.

#### Explanation

'What will the vanaras say when I return? What should I speak when they ask me: O hero, what did you accomplish there?'

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

अदृष्ट्वा किं प्रवक्ष्यामि तामहं जनकात्मजाम्। ध्रुवं प्रायमुपैष्यन्ति कालस्य व्यतिवर्तने।।5.12.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ताम् her, जनकात्मजाम् Janaki, अदृष्टवा without seeing, किम् what, प्रवक्ष्यामि l am going to say, कालस्य for a long time, व्यतिवर्तने exceeded time limit, ध्रुवम् surely, प्रायम् उपैष्यन्ति they will sit down and wait until death.

#### Explanation

'Since the time limit has exceeded and Janaki is not spotted, surely the vanaras would sit and wait unto death. What am I going to say'?

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

किं वा वक्ष्यति वृद्धश्च जाम्बवानाङ्गदश्च सः। गतं पारं समुद्रस्य वानराश्च समागताः।।5.12.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

समुद्रस्य ocean's, पारम् other shore, गतम् on going, वृद्धः elderly, जाम्बवान् Jambavan, किं वा why or else, वक्ष्यति will say, सः he, अङ्गद: च Angada also, समागताः collecting together, वानराश्च and vanaras.

#### Explanation

'What will the elderly Jambavan say? What will young prince Angada and other vanaras collected there say when I reach the seashore to meet them'.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

अनिर्वेदः श्रियो मूलमनिर्वेदः परं सुखम्। अनिर्वेदो हि सततं सर्वार्थेषु प्रवर्तकः।।5.12.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अनिर्वेदः being free from despair, श्रियः prosperity, मूलम् cause, अनिर्वेदः being free from despondency, परम् supreme, सुखम् happiness, अनिर्वेदः being free from despair, सततम् always, सर्वार्थेषु in all objectives, प्रवर्तकः हि will lead the way.

#### Explanation

Wise Hanuman once again declared, 'being free from despair is the root cause of prosperity. Freedom from despondency gives supreme happiness and leads to success'.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

करोति सफलं जन्तोः कर्म यत्तत्करोति सः। तस्मादनिर्वेदकृतं यत्नं चेष्टेऽहमुत्तमम्।।5.12.11।। भूयस्तावद्विचेष्यामि देशान्रावणपालितान्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

यत् that, कर्म action, करोति that he will do, जन्तोः of a living being, तत् that, सफलम् fruitful, सः he, करोति will do, तस्मात् therefore, अहम् I, अनिर्वेदकृतम् without experiencing despair, उत्तमम् best, प्रयत्नम् effort, चेष्टे I act, अदृष्टान् not seen, रावणपालितान् ruled by Ravana, देशान् country, विचेष्यामि तावत् search everywhere.

#### Explanation

'The action of a living being certainly bears fruit. Therefore, I shall put forth my best effort without feeling despondent. I will once again search everywhere in Lanka ruled  by Ravana'.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

आपानशाला विचितास्तथा पुष्पगृहाणि च।।5.12.12।। चित्रशालाश्च विचिता भूयः क्रीडागृहाणि च। निष्कुटान्तररथ्याश्च विमानानि च सर्वशः।।5.12.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पानशालाः banquet halls, विचिताः searched, तथा so also, पुष्पगृहाणि च bowers of creepers, चित्रशालाश्च picture galleries, विचिताः searched, भूयः further, क्रीडागृहाणि च and sports galleries, निष्कुटान्तररथ्याश्च paths through the gardens and mansions, female apartments, विमानानि च of the chariot, सर्वशः everywhere.

#### Explanation

'I have searched the banquet halls, bowers of creepers, picture galleries and gymnasiums. I have searched everywhere through the paths of gardens and mansions, harems and the Pushpaka chariot also.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

आपानशाला विचितास्तथा पुष्पगृहाणि च।।5.12.12।। चित्रशालाश्च विचिता भूयः क्रीडागृहाणि च। निष्कुटान्तररथ्याश्च विमानानि च सर्वशः।।5.12.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पानशालाः banquet halls, विचिताः searched, तथा so also, पुष्पगृहाणि च bowers of creepers, चित्रशालाश्च picture galleries, विचिताः searched, भूयः further, क्रीडागृहाणि च and sports galleries, निष्कुटान्तररथ्याश्च paths through the gardens and mansions, female apartments, विमानानि च of the chariot, सर्वशः everywhere.

#### Explanation

'I have searched the banquet halls, bowers of creepers, picture galleries and gymnasiums. I have searched everywhere through the paths of gardens and mansions, harems and the Pushpaka chariot also.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

इति सञ्चिन्त्य भूयोऽपि विचेतुमुपचक्रमे। भूमीगृहांश्चैत्यगृहान् गृहातिगृहकानपि।।5.12.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इति thus, सञ्चिन्त्य thinking over well, भूमीगृहान् underground cells, चैत्यगृहान् temples, गृहातिगृहकानपि homes with many doorways also, भूयोऽपि once again, विचेतुम् to search, उपचक्रमे he started.

#### Explanation

Thinking thus, he started searching once again the basement cells, temples, homes within homes.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

उत्पतन्निष्पतंश्चापि तिष्ठन्गच्छन् पुनः पुनः। अपावृण्वंश्च द्वाराणि कवाटान्यवघाटयन्।।5.12.15।। प्रविशन्निष्पतंश्चापि प्रपतन्नुत्पतन्नपि। सर्वमप्यवकाशं स विचचार महाकपिः।।5.12.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः महाकपिः that great vanara, पुनः पुनः again and again, उत्पतन् jumping, निष्पतंश्चापि climbing down, तिष्ठन्  standing, गच्छन् going, द्वाराणि entrance, अपावृण्वन् opening, कवाटानि doors, अवघाटयन् crossing, प्रविशन् entering, निष्पतंश्चापि and coming out, प्रपतन् jumping down, उत्पतन्नपि climbing up, सर्वम् अपि and all over, अवकाशम् scope, विचचार searched.

#### Explanation

The great vanara searched all over again and again jumping up and down, stopping for a while and moving, opening and closing doors, by crossing, entering and exiting, jumping up and down. Thus he moved about and searched whereever there was scope to search

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

उत्पतन्निष्पतंश्चापि तिष्ठन्गच्छन् पुनः पुनः। अपावृण्वंश्च द्वाराणि कवाटान्यवघाटयन्।।5.12.15।। प्रविशन्निष्पतंश्चापि प्रपतन्नुत्पतन्नपि। सर्वमप्यवकाशं स विचचार महाकपिः।।5.12.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः महाकपिः that great vanara, पुनः पुनः again and again, उत्पतन् jumping, निष्पतंश्चापि climbing down, तिष्ठन्  standing, गच्छन् going, द्वाराणि entrance, अपावृण्वन् opening, कवाटानि doors, अवघाटयन् crossing, प्रविशन् entering, निष्पतंश्चापि and coming out, प्रपतन् jumping down, उत्पतन्नपि climbing up, सर्वम् अपि and all over, अवकाशम् scope, विचचार searched.

#### Explanation

The great vanara searched all over again and again jumping up and down, stopping for a while and moving, opening and closing doors, by crossing, entering and exiting, jumping up and down. Thus he moved about and searched whereever there was scope to search

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

चतुरङ्गुलमात्रोऽपि नावकाशः स विद्यते। रावणान्तःपुरे तस्मिन् यं कपिर्न जगाम सः।।5.12.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तस्मिन् in that, रावणान्तःपुरे in Ravana's inner chambers, सः कपिः monkey, यम् where, न जगाम did not go, सः he, अवकाशः scope, चतुरङ्गुलमात्रोऽपि even four fingers of space, न not विद्यते leaving.

#### Explanation

Even a space of four fingers in the inner chambers of Ravana was not left out in his search for Sita.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

प्राकारान्तररथ्याश्च वेदिकाश्चैत्यसंश्रयाः। दीर्घिकाः पुष्करिण्यश्च सर्वं तेनावलोकितम्।।5.12.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

प्राकारान्तररथ्याश्च lanes inside the boundaries, चैत्यसंश्रयाः around shrines, वेदिकाः altars, दीर्घिकाः wells, पुष्करिण्यश्च tanks, सर्वम् everywhere, तेन by him, अवलोकितम् was seen.

#### Explanation

He moved in the lanes inside the boundary walls, around the shrines, altars, wells and tanks whereever it was possible to look for.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

राक्षस्यो विविधाकारा विरूपा विकृतास्तथा। दृष्टा हनुमता तत्र न तु सा जनकात्मजा।।5.12.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विविधाकाराः of different types, विरूपाः ugly, तथा so also, विकृताः deformed, राक्षस्यः ogresses, तत्र there, हनुमता by Hanuman, दृष्टाः were seen, सा she, जनकात्मजा तु daughter of Janaka, न not found.

#### Explanation

Hanuman could see only ogresses of different typesugly, deformed but could not find the daughter of Janaka.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

रूपेणाप्रतिमा लोके वरा विद्याधरस्त्रियः। दृष्टा हनुमता तत्र न तु राघवनन्दिनी।।5.12.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रूपेण in beauty, लोके in the world, अप्रतिमाः incomparable, वराः best, विद्याधरस्त्रियः Vidyadhara women, तत्र there, हनुमता by Hanuman, दृष्टाः were seen, राघवनन्दिनी तु beloved of Raghava, न not find.

#### Explanation

Hanuman could see even the women of vidyadharas who were matchless in beauty among women in the world but not Janaki the beloved of Raghava.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

नागकन्या वरारोहाः पूर्णचन्र्दनिभाननाः। दृष्टा हनुमता तत्र न तु सीता सुमध्यमा।।5.12.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वरारोहाः lovely women with symmetrical limbs, पूर्णचन्र्दनिभाननाः moonfaced women, नागकन्याः daughters of nagas, तत्र there, हनुमता by Hanuman, दृष्टाः were seen, सुमध्यमा a woman of lovely waist, सीता तु Sita, न not found.

#### Explanation

Hanuman could see there moonfaced daughters of nagas with charming limbs but not the slimwaisted Sita.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

प्रमथ्य राक्षसेन्द्रेण नागकन्या बलाद्धृताः। दृष्टा हनुमता तत्र न सा जनकनन्दिनी।।5.12.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसेन्द्रेण by the demon king, प्रमथ्य defeating, बलात् forcefully, हृताः abducted, नागकन्याः daughters of nagas, तत्र there, हनुमता by Hanuman, दृष्टाः seen, सा that, जनकनन्दिनी न not the delight of Janaka.

#### Explanation

Hanuman saw the daghters of nagas kidnapped by Ravana forcibly after victory in war but not Sita, the delight of Janaka.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

सोऽपश्यंस्तां महाबाहुः पश्यंश्चान्या वरस्त्रियः। विषसाद मुहुर्धीमान् हनुमान् मारुतात्मजः।।5.12.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाबाहुः longarmed, धीमान् wise one, मारुतात्मजः son of the Wind god, सः हनुमान् Hanuman, ताम् her, अपश्यन् while being unable to see, अन्याः other, वरस्त्रियः great women, पश्यन् while seeing, मुहुः again, विषसाद was sorrowful.

#### Explanation

The longarmed Hanuman, son of the Windgod was able to see other great women but not Sita. So he became despondent.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

उद्योगं वानरेन्द्राणां प्लवनं सागरस्य च। व्यर्थं वीक्ष्यानिलसुतश्चिन्तां पुनरुपागमत्।।5.12.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वानरेन्द्राणाम् of the great vanara, उद्योगम् effort, सागरस्य ocean's, प्लवनं च in crossing, व्यर्थम् wasted, वीक्ष्य after seeing that, अनिलसुतः son of the Windgod, पुनः again, चिन्ताम् worry, उपागमत् experienced.

#### Explanation

The son of the Wind god, the great monkey realised the futility of his efforts in crossing the sea. He brooded again.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

अवतीर्य विमानाच्च हनुमान् मारुतात्मजः। चिन्तामुपजगामाथ शोकोपहतचेतनः।।5.12.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथ now, मारुतात्मजः son of the Windgod, हनुमान् Hanuman, विमानात् from the aerialchariot, अवतीर्य after descending, शोकोपहतचेतनः mind stricken with grief, चिन्ताम् worry, उपजगाम he experienced.

#### Explanation

Hanuman, son of the Windgod, came out of the aerial chariot, mind stricken with grief and started thinking again. इत्यार्षे  श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये सुन्दरकाण्डे द्वादशस्सर्गः। Thus ends the twelfth sarga of Sundarakanda of the holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 13

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

विमानात्तु सुसम्क्रम्य प्राकारं हरियूथपः। हनुमान्वेगवानासीद्यथा विद्युद्घनान्तरे।।5.13.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हरियूथपः vanara leader, हनुमान् Hanuman, विमानात् from the aerial chariot, प्राकारम् boundary wall, सुसम्क्रम्य jumped, घनान्तरे in the clouds, विद्युत् यथा like the lightning, वेगवान् speedily, आसीत् appeared.

#### Explanation

Hanuman, the vanara leader, jumped from the aerial chariot to the boundary wall like a flash of lightning in the clouds.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

सम्परिक्रम्य हनुमान्रावणस्य निवेशनात्। अदृष्ट्वा जानकीं सीतामब्रवीद्वचनं कपिः।।5.13.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कपिः vanara, हनुमान् Hanuman, रावणस्य Ravana's, निवेशनात् from the home, सम्परिक्रम्य going round, जानकीम् Janaka's daughter, सीताम् Sita, अदृष्ट्वा not seeing, वचनम् these words, अब्रवीत् spoke.

#### Explanation

Going round the palace of Ravana, unable to see Janaka's daughter Sita, Hanuman spoke thus to himself:

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

भूयिष्ठं लोलिता लङ्का रामस्य चरता प्रियम्। न हि पश्यामि वैदेहीं सीतां सर्वाङ्गशोभनाम्।।5.13.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामस्य Rama's, प्रियम् to please, चरता while doing, लङ्का Lanka, भूयिष्ठम् in and out, लोलिता checked thoroughly, सर्वाङ्गशोभनाम् of beautiful limbs, वैदेहीम् Vaidehi, सीताम् Sita, न हि पश्यामि I have not seen.

#### Explanation

"I have thoroughly surveyed the entire city of Lanka to please Rama, but I am unable to find Sita of flawless beauty.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

पल्वलानि तटाकानि सरांसि सरितस्तथा। नद्योऽनूपवनान्ताश्च दुर्गाश्च धरणीधराः।।5.13.4।। लोलिता वसुधा सर्वा न तु पश्यामि जानकीम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पल्वलानि waterlogs, तटाकानि tanks, सरांसि lakes, तथा similarly, सरितः streams, नद्यः rivers, अनूपवनान्ताश्च along the woods, दुर्गाः difficult places to reach, धरणीधराः mountains, सर्वा: all, वसुधा over the land, लोलिता searched, जानकीं तु but Janaki, न पश्यामि I have not seen.

#### Explanation

"I have searched for Sita thoroughly in waterlogs, lakes, tanks, streams and rivers and all over the woods and among mountains that are difficult to reach. I have searched all over the land but have not been able to find Janaki.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

इह सम्पातिना सीता रावणस्य निवेशने।।5.13.5।। आख्याता गृध्रराजेन न च पश्यामि तामहम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीता Sita, इह here, रावणस्य Ravana's, निवेशने in his palace, गृध्रराजेन by the king of vultures, सम्पातिना by Sampati, आख्याता has told, अहम् I, ताम् her, न च पश्यामि but have not seen her.

#### Explanation

"Sampati, king of vultures had said that Sita is at Ravana's palace. But I have not been able to see her.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

किं नु सीताऽथ वैदेही मैथिली जनकात्मजा।।5.13.6।। उपतिष्ठेत विवशा रावणं दुष्टचारिणम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथ now, वैदेही princess of Videha, मैथिली Mythili, जनकत्माजा Janaka's daughter, सीता Sita, विवशा dejected helpless, दुष्टचारिणम् a man of evilconduct, रावणम् to Ravana, किं नु उपतिष्ठेत  did she succumb?

#### Explanation

"Can it be that Mythili, daughter of Janaka, dejected and helpless succumbed to the evil intentions of Ravana.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

क्षिप्रमुत्पततो मन्ये सीतामादाय रक्षसः।।5.13.7।। बिभ्यतो रामबाणानामन्तरा पतिता भवेत्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामबाणानाम् of the arrows of Rama, बिभ्यतः out of fear, रक्षसः the demon, सीताम् Sita, आदाय having brought, क्षिप्रम् at great speed, उत्पततः while flying, अन्तरा in the middle, पतिता dropped, भवेत् may be, मन्ये I think.

#### Explanation

"Or, perhaps she might have been dropped midway from the hold of Ravana while he was flying at great speed, afraid of the deadly arrows of Rama?

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

अथवा ह्रियमाणायाः पथि सिद्धनिषेविते।।5.13.8।। मन्ये पतितमार्याया हृदयं प्रेक्ष्य सागरम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथवा or else, सिद्धनिषेविते frequented by siddhas, पथि on the path, ह्रियमाणायाः while being borne away, आर्यायाः of the noble lady, हृदयम् heart, सागरम् sea, प्रेक्ष्य on seeing, पतितम् fallen, मन्ये I think.

#### Explanation

"Or, perhaps while being borne away on the path frequented by siddhas, the beloved of Rama might have fallen down (dead), heart broken, on seeing the (vast) sea.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

रावणस्योरुवेगेन भुजाभ्यां पीडितेन च।।5.13.9।। तया मन्ये विशालाक्ष्या त्यक्तं जीवितमार्यया।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रावणस्य Ravana's, उरुवेगेन by the high speed in the flight, भुजाभ्याम् with the arms, पीडितेन च being under pressure, विशालाक्ष्या by the largeeyed one, तया by her, आर्यया by the noble lady, जीवितम् life, त्यक्तम् given up, मन्ये I think.

#### Explanation

"He thinks, on account of the high speed during the flight of Ravana under the pressure of his arms, the noble, largeeyed lady might have given up her life.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

उपर्युपरि वा नूनं सागरं क्रमतस्तदा।।5.13.10।। विवेष्टमाना पतिता सागरे जनकात्मजा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, सागरम् उपर्युपरि higher and higher over the sea, क्रमतः for him while he advanced, जनकात्मजा Janaki, विवेष्टमाना wriggling hard to extricate, सागरे in the sea, नूनम् surely, पतिता fallen.

#### Explanation

"Surely, she might have fallen into the sea while wriggling in her effort to extricate herself from the hold of Ravana as he was flying over the sea.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

अहो क्षुद्रेण वाऽनेन रक्षन्ती शीलमात्मनः।।5.13.11।। अबन्धुर्भक्षिता सीता रावणेन तपस्विनी।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहो Oh, आत्मनः of her self, शीलम् chastity, रक्षन्ती while protecting, अबन्धुः distanced from relatives, तपस्विनी a helpless woman, सीता Sita, क्षुद्रेण by the cruel one, अनेन by him, रावणेन by Ravana, भक्षिता eaten.

#### Explanation

"Oh poor Sita, distanced from relations, while protecting her chastity helplessly she might have been devoured by cruel Ravana.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

अथवा राक्षसेन्द्रस्य पत्नीभिरसितेक्षणा।।5.13.12।। अदुष्टा दुष्टभावाभिर्भक्षिता सा भविष्यति।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथवा or else, अदुष्टा a pious lady, असितेक्षणा blackeyed one, सा that, दुष्टभावाभिः by the cruelnatured, राक्षसेन्द्रस्य of the lord of rakshasas, पत्नीभिः by wives also, भक्षिता eaten, भविष्यति I think.

#### Explanation

"Or else, the pious, blackeyed Sita might have been eaten by the the cruelnatured wives of the lord of demons.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

सम्पूर्णचन्द्रप्रतिमं पद्मपत्रनिभेक्षणम्।।5.13.13।। रामस्य ध्यायती वक्त्रं पञ्चत्वं कृपणा गता।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कृपणा poor lady, सम्पूर्णचन्द्रप्रतिमम् like the fullmoon, पद्मपत्रनिभेक्षणम् with eyes like lotus petals, रामस्य Rama's, वक्त्रम् face, ध्यायती meditating on, पञ्चत्वम् death, गता attained.

#### Explanation

"Or, she might have attained death, meditating on Rama, whose face resembles the fullmoon and eyes the lotus petals.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

हा राम लक्ष्मणेत्येवं हाऽयोध्ये चेति मैथिली।।5.13.14।। विलप्य बहु वैदेही न्यस्तदेहा भविष्यति।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वैदेही Vaidehi, मैथिली Mythili, हा राम Alas, Rama, हा लक्ष्मण alas, Lakshmana, हा अयोध्या alas, Ayodhya, इति thus, बहु very much, विलप्य weaping, न्यस्तदेहा cast off life, भविष्यति may be.

#### Explanation

"Or, Vaidehi, the princess of Mithila might have cast off her life grieving intensely and crying, 'Alas, Rama, alas, Lakshmana, alas, Ayodhya'. (Mithila is the name of the  capital city and Videha is the country which was ruled by Janaka. Sita, the princess is known as Vaidehi, Mythili and Janaki).

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

अथवा निहिता मन्ये रावणस्य निवेशने।।5.13.15।। नूनं लालप्यते सीता पञ्जरस्थेव शारिका।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथवा or else, रावणस्य Ravana's, निवेशने in his palace, निहिता kept, सीता Sita, पञ्जरस्था in the cage, शारिका इव like myna, नूनम् surely, लालप्यते crying, मन्ये I think.

#### Explanation

"Or, I think Sita might have been imprisoned in a cage like a myna in Ravana's palace. She will be surely crying.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

जनकस्य सुता सीता रामपत्नी सुमध्यमा।।5.13.16।। कथमुत्पलपत्राक्षी रावणस्य वशं व्रजेत्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

जनकस्य Janaka's, सुता daughter, रामपत्नी Rama's wife, सुमध्यमा a lady of beautiful waist, उत्पलपत्राक्षी eyes like lotus petals, सीता Sita, रावणस्य Ravana's, वशम् fold, कथम् how, व्रजेत् can come under.

#### Explanation

"How could Janaki, the daughter of Janaka, Rama's wife, the lady with a beautiful waist and eyes like lotus petals, come under the fold of Ravana?

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

विनष्टा वा प्रणष्टा वा मृता वा जनकात्मजा।।5.13.17।। रामस्य प्रियभार्यस्य न निवेदयितुं क्षमम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

जनकात्मजा Janaka's daughter, विनष्टा वा whether lost, प्रणष्टा वा or whether irretrievable, मृता वा or dead, प्रियभार्यस्य for him who is fond of his wife, रामस्य of Rama, निवेदयितुम् to report, न क्षमम् not proper.

#### Explanation

"Whether Janaki is lost or irretrievable or dead is not known The news should not be  reported to Rama, who is very fond of his wife.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

निवेद्यमाने दोषस्स्याद्दोषस्स्यादनिवेदने।।5.13.18।। कथं नु खलु कर्तव्यं विषमं प्रतिभाति मे।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

निवेद्यमाने by informing this, दोषः mistake, स्यात् may be, अनिवेदने by not informing, दोषः mistake, स्यात् may be, कथम् how, कर्तव्यं नु खलु what to do, मे to me, विषमम् difficult, प्रतिभाति appears.

#### Explanation

"To carry or not to carry this news to Rama would be a mistake. What shall I do? I am facing a tough situation.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

अस्मिन्नेवंगते कार्ये प्राप्तकालं क्षमं च किम्।।5.13.19।। भवेदिति मतं भूयो हनुमान्प्रविचारयत्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अस्मिन् in this, कार्ये in this regard, एवं गते when things took such a turn, प्राप्तकालम् right time, क्षमम् proper, किम् what, भवेत् will be, इति thus, मतम् opinion, हनुमान् Hanuman, भूयः once again, प्रविचारयत् deliberated.

#### Explanation

"I wonder what the right course of action is when things have taken such a turn? What is proper for me to do? Hanuman once again deliberated.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

यदि सीतामदृष्ट्वाऽहं वानरेन्द्रपुरीमितः।।5.13.20।। गमिष्यामि ततः को मे पुरुषार्थो भविष्यति।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I, सीताम् Sita, अदृष्ट्वा without seeing, इतः from here, वानरेन्द्रपुरीम् city of vanaras, गमिष्यामि I reach, यदि if, ततः then, कः what (has been accomplished), पुरुषार्थः accomplishment, भविष्यति will happen.

#### Explanation

"If I return to Kishkinda without seeing Sita of what use is my effort? What have I accomplished? What will happen? (Dharma, Artha, Kama and Moksha are the four Purusharthas or accomplishments in life)

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

ममेदं लङ्घनं व्यर्थं सागरस्य भविष्यति।।5.13.21।। प्रवेशश्चैव लङ्कायाः राक्षसानां च दर्शनम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मम my, इदम् this, सागरस्य of the ocean, लङ्घनम् crossing, लङ्कायाः of Lanka, प्रवेशश्च entry, राक्षसानाम् of ogres, दर्शनम् seeing, व्यर्थम् futile, भविष्यति would be.

#### Explanation

"My crossing the ocean, my entry into Lanka and my survey of the demons are all futile.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

किं मां वक्ष्यति सुग्रीवो हरयो वा समागताः।।5.13.22।। किष्किन्धां समनुप्राप्तं तौ वा दशरथात्मजौ।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

किष्किन्धाम् at Kishkinda, समनुप्राप्तम् reached, माम् to me, सुग्रीवः Sugriva, किम् what, वक्ष्यति he says, समागताः collected, हरयः वा even vanaras, तौ those two, दशरथात्मजौ वा sons of Dasaratha.

#### Explanation

"What will Sugriva or even the vanaras and the two sons of Dasaratha say when I reach Kishkinda?

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

गत्वा तु यदि काकुत्स्थं वक्ष्यामि परमप्रियम्।।5.13.23।। न दृष्टेति मया सीता ततस्तक्ष्यति जीवितम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

गत्वा after reaching, काकुत्स्थम् Kakutstha, मया by me, सीता Sita, न दृष्टा not seen, इति like this, परम् most, अप्रियम् unpleasant, वक्ष्यामि यदि if I say, ततः then, जीवितम् life, त्यक्ष्यति he will give up.

#### Explanation

"If I report the most unpleasant news that Sita was not found, Rama will give up his life.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

परुषं दारुणं क्रूरं तीक्ष्णमिन्द्रियतापनम्।।5.13.24।। सीतानिमित्तं दुर्वाक्यं श्रुत्वा स न भविष्यति।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

परुषम् harsh, दारुणम् dreadful, क्रूरम् cruel, तीक्ष्णम् sharp, इन्द्रियतापनम् that which can scorch senses, सीतानिमित्तम् about Sita, दुर्वाक्यम् unwelcome words, श्रुत्वा after hearing, सः Rama, न भविष्यति will not live.

#### Explanation

None

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

तं तु कृच्छ्रगतं दृष्ट्वा पञ्चत्वगतमानसम्।।5.13.25।। भृशानुरक्तो मेधावी न भविष्यति लक्ष्मणः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कृच्छ्रगतम्  in painful state, पञ्चत्वगतमानसम् one who has almost given his life, तम् him, दृष्ट्वा seeing, भृशानुरक्तः deeply devoted, मेधावी wise, लक्ष्मणः Lakshmana, न भविष्यति will not survive.

#### Explanation

"Seeing Rama in such a painful state, wise Lakshmana who has given his life to Rama, to whom he is deeply devoted will also not survive.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

विनष्टौ भ्रातरौ श्रुत्वा भरतोऽपि मरिष्यति।।5.13.26।। भरतं च मृतं दृष्ट्वा शत्रुघ्नो न भविष्यति।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भ्रातरौ both brothers, विनष्टौ dead, श्रुत्वा after hearing, भरतोऽपि Bharata also, मरिष्यति will die, मृतम् dead, भरतम् of Bharata, दृष्ट्वा on seeing, शत्रुघ्नश्च even Satrughna, न भविष्यति will not live.

#### Explanation

"On hearing the death of both the brothers Bharata will also die. On seeing this Satrughna will also not live.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

पुत्रान्मृतान्समीक्ष्याथ न भविष्यन्ति मातरः।।5.13.27।। कौसल्या च सुमित्रा च कैकेयी च न संशयः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथ then, मातरः mothers, कौसल्या च and Kausalya, सुमित्रा च even Sumitra, कैकेयी च even Kaikeyi, पुत्रान् of sons, मृतान् of their death, समीक्ष्य on seeing, न भविष्यन्ति will not be able to live, संशयः न no doubt.

#### Explanation

None

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

कृतज्ञस्सत्यसन्धश्च सुग्रीवः प्लवगाधिपः।।5.13.28।। रामं तथा गतं दृष्ट्वा ततस्त्यक्ष्यति जीवितम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कृतज्ञः grateful, सत्यसन्धः truthful, प्लवगाधिपः leader of monkeys, सुग्रीवः Sugriva, तथा likewise, गतम् gone, रामम् Rama, दृष्ट्वा on seeing, ततः then, जीवितम् life, त्यक्ष्यति will give up.

#### Explanation

"On seeing that Rama has ended his life, the grateful and truthful monkey leader, Sugriva will give up his life.

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

दुर्मना व्यथिता दीना निरानन्दा तपस्विनी।।5.13.29।। पीडिता भर्तृशोकेन रुमा त्यक्ष्यति जीवितम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भर्तृशोकेन on account of her husband's grief, पीडिता tormented, दुर्मनाः disheartened, व्यथिता pained, दीना dejected, निरानन्दा unhappy, तपस्विनी helpless, रुमा Ruma, जीवितम् life, त्यक्ष्यति will give up.

#### Explanation

"Distressed, disheartened, pained and grieved on account of her husband's death, the virtuous lady Ruma (wife of Sugriva) will be unhappy and die.

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

वालिजेन तु दुःखेन पीडिता शोककर्शिता।।5.13.30।। पञ्चत्वं च गते राज्ञि ताराऽपि न भविष्यति।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वालिजेन caused by Vali, दुःखेन by sorrow, पीडिता troubled, शोककर्शिता fertured with grief, तारापि Tara too, राज्ञि when the king, पञ्चत्वम् gone to other world, गते having gone, न भविष्यति will not live

#### Explanation

"Tara who is troubled by Vali's death and tortured with grief will not live when king Sugriva dies.

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

मातापित्रोर्विनाशेन सुग्रीवव्यसनेन च।।5.13.31।। कुमारोऽप्यङ्गदः कस्माद्धारयिष्यति जीवितम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कुमारः prince, अङ्गदोऽपि Angada also, मातापित्रोः of his mother and father, विनाशेन by death, सुग्रीवस्य Sugriva's, व्यसनेन by calamity, जीवितम् life, कस्मात् why, धारयिष्यति will retain.

#### Explanation

"On account of his parents death and also because of Sugriva's calamity, Angada will give up his life.

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

भर्तृजेन तु दुःखेन ह्यभिभूता वनौकसः।।5.13.32।। शिरांस्यभिहनिष्यन्ति तलैर्मुष्टिभिरेव च।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नौकसः vanaras, भर्तृजेन on account of the events affecting their king, दुःखेन by grief, अभिभूताः overpowered, तलैः with their palms, मुष्टिभिरेव च and with their fists, शिरांसि their heads, अभिहनिष्यन्ति will hit themselves.

#### Explanation

"The grief due to the death of their king will make the vanaras hit their heads with their palms and fists.

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

सान्त्वेनानुप्रदानेन मानेन च यशस्विना।।5.13.33।। लालिताः कपिराजेन प्राणांस्त्यक्ष्यन्ति वानराः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

यशस्स्विना by the illustrious one, कपिराजेन by the king of vanaras, सान्त्वेन by good words, अनुप्रदानेन by offering gifts, मानेन by respecting, लालिताः reared, वानराः vanaras, प्राणान् life, त्यक्ष्यन्ति will give up.

#### Explanation

"The vanaras who were reared by the king's kind words, gifts and care will give up their life.

---

### Shloka 34

#### Shloka Text

न वनेषु न शैलेषु न निरोधेषु वा पुनः।।5.13.34।। क्रीडामनुभविष्यन्ति समेत्य कपिकुञ्जराः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कपिकुञ्जराः chiefs among vanaras, समेत्य collected together, वनेषु in the forest, क्रीडाम् play, न अनुभविष्यन्ति will not enjoy, शैलेषु on the mountains, न निरोधेषु वा or in the caves, पुनः again, न not.

#### Explanation

"The chiefs among vanaras together will not enjoy their life in the forest or mountains or caves.

---

### Shloka 35

#### Shloka Text

सपुत्रदारास्सामात्या भर्तृव्यसनपीडिताः।।5.13.35।। शैलाग्रेभ्यः पतिष्यन्ति समेषु विषमेषु च।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सपुत्रदाराः with sons and wives, सामात्याः with ministers together, भर्तृव्यसनपीडिताः tormented by the suffering of their king's death, शैलाग्रेभ्यः from the mountain peaks, समेषु on the plains, विषमेषु च and on uneven grounds, पतिष्यन्ति will fall (drop down).

#### Explanation

"Tormented by their king's death the vanaras with their wives, sons and ministers will drop down from the mountain peaks on the rough ground and die.

---

### Shloka 36

#### Shloka Text

विषमुद्बन्धनं वापि प्रवेशं ज्वलनस्य वा।।5.13.36।। उपवासमथो शस्त्रं प्रचरिष्यन्ति वानराः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वानराः vanaras, विषम् poison, उद्बन्धनं वापि or by hanging, ज्वलनस्य in the fire, प्रवेशं वा entering, उपवासम् by fasting, अथो or, शस्त्रम् weapon, प्रचरिष्यन्ति they will use to put an end.

#### Explanation

"The vanaras will put an end to their lives by poisoning or by hanging or by selfimmolation or by killing themselves with weapons or by fasting unto death.

---

### Shloka 37

#### Shloka Text

घोरमारोदनं मन्ये गते मयि भविष्यति।।5.13.37।। इक्ष्वाकुकुलनाशश्च नाशश्चैव वनौकसाम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मयि गते when I go, इक्ष्वाकुकुलनाशश्च destruction of the Ikshvakus, वनौकसाम् of vanaras, नाशश्चैव destruction, घोरम् dreadful, आरोदनम् weeping, भविष्यति will be, मन्ये I think.

#### Explanation

"If I go there (without finding Sita), it will cause destruction of the Ikshvakus and vanaras would weep. It would be dreadful.

---

### Shloka 38

#### Shloka Text

सोऽहं नैव गमिष्यामि किष्किन्धां नगरीमितः।।5.13.38।। न च शक्ष्याम्यहं द्रष्टुं सुग्रीवं मैथिलीं विना।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I, इतः from here, किष्किन्धां नगरीम् to the city of Kishkinda, नैव गमिष्यामि I will not go, अहम् I, मैथिलीं Mythili, विना without, सुग्रीवम् Sugriva, द्रष्टुम् to see, न च शक्ष्यामि not possible for me.

#### Explanation

"I will not go to the city of Kishkinda from here or to Sugriva without finding Mythili. It will not be possible for me to face him.

---

### Shloka 39

#### Shloka Text

मय्यगच्छति चेहस्थे धर्मात्मानौ महारथौ।।5.13.39।। आशया तौ धरिष्येते वानराश्च मनस्विनः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मयि when I, अगच्छति not go, इहस्थे stay here, धर्मात्मानौ righteous selves, महारथौ great warriors, तौ both, आशया with hope, धरिष्येते both will survive, मनस्विनः highsouled, वानराश्च vanaras also.

#### Explanation

Both the highsouled warrior princes treading a righteous path as well as the vanaras will survive with a hope of my return if I stay back.

---

### Shloka 40

#### Shloka Text

हस्तादानो मुखादानो नियतो वृक्षमूलिकः।।5.13.40।। वानप्रस्थो भविष्यामि ह्यदृष्ट्वा जनकात्मजाम्। सागरानूपजे देशे बहुमूलफलोदके।।5.13.41।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

जनकात्मजाम् Janaka's daughter, अदृष्ट्वा by not seeing, हस्तादानः food available to hand, मुखादानः and mouth, नियतः strictly, बहुमूलफलोदके filled with fruits roots and water, सागरानूपजे on the shore of the sea, देशे here, वृक्षमूलिकः surviving on roots of trees, वानप्रस्थः like a hermit, भविष्यामि I will be.

#### Explanation

"If Janaki is not found I will stay here and live like a hermit subsisting on whatever is available on this sea shore, which has abundance of roots, fruits and water.

---

### Shloka 41

#### Shloka Text

हस्तादानो मुखादानो नियतो वृक्षमूलिकः।।5.13.40।। वानप्रस्थो भविष्यामि ह्यदृष्ट्वा जनकात्मजाम्। सागरानूपजे देशे बहुमूलफलोदके।।5.13.41।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

जनकात्मजाम् Janaka's daughter, अदृष्ट्वा by not seeing, हस्तादानः food available to hand, मुखादानः and mouth, नियतः strictly, बहुमूलफलोदके filled with fruits roots and water, सागरानूपजे on the shore of the sea, देशे here, वृक्षमूलिकः surviving on roots of trees, वानप्रस्थः like a hermit, भविष्यामि I will be.

#### Explanation

"If Janaki is not found I will stay here and live like a hermit subsisting on whatever is available on this sea shore, which has abundance of roots, fruits and water.

---

### Shloka 42

#### Shloka Text

चितां कृत्वा प्रवेक्ष्यामि समिद्धमरणीसुतम्। उपविष्टस्य वा सम्यग्लिङ्गिनीं साधयिष्यतः।।5.13.42।। शरीरं भक्षयिष्यन्ति वायसाः श्वापदानि च।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

चिताम् pyre, कृत्वा having made, समिद्धम् lit, अरणीसुतम् fire sticks (fire), प्रवेक्ष्यामि will enter, वा or else, उपविष्टस्य seated here, लिङ्गिनीम् starving, साधयिष्यतः fast unto death, शरीरम् body, वायसाः crows, श्वापदानि च wild beasts, भक्षयिष्यन्ति  will eat.

#### Explanation

"I will prepare a pyre  and enter the fire  so that sea waves wash off the ashes or crows and beasts will eat up my body while i sit fasting unto death.

---

### Shloka 43

#### Shloka Text

इदं महर्षिभिर्दृष्टं निर्याणमिति मे मतिः।।5.13.43।। सम्यगापः प्रवेक्ष्यामि न चेत्पश्यामि जानकीम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इदम् this, महर्षिभिः by ascetics, दृष्टम्  seen, निर्याणम् ending of life, इति like this, मे my, मतिः thought, जानकीम् Janaki, न चेत्पश्यामि if I am not able to see, सम्यक् right, आपः water, प्रवेक्ष्यामि will enter.

#### Explanation

"I shall enter water and drown myself to death if I am not able to find Janaki. I think this way of ending life has been suggested by great ascetics.

---

### Shloka 44

#### Shloka Text

सुजातमूला सुभगा कीर्तिमाला यशस्विनी।।5.13.44।। प्रभग्ना चिररात्रीयं मम सीतामपश्यतः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीताम् Sita, अपश्यतः I could not find, मम for me, चिररात्रीयं this long night, सुजातमूला started well, सुभगा lovely, यशस्विनी glorious one, कीर्तिमाला famous one, प्रभग्ना is wasted.

#### Explanation

My glory which had a good start (in the form of bringing Rama to Sugriva) and brought fortune to Sugriva and fame to me has been snapped as I could not find Sita.

---

### Shloka 45

#### Shloka Text

तापसो वा भविष्यामि नियतो वृक्षमूलिकः।।5.13.45।। नेतः प्रतिगमिष्यामि तामदृष्ट्वासितेक्षणाम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नियतः strictly, वृक्षमूलिकः resting under the tree, तापसो वा or an ascetic, भविष्यामि I will remain, असितेक्षणाम् blackeyed, ताम् her, अदृष्टवा without seeing, इतः from here, न प्रतिगमिष्यामि I will not go back.

#### Explanation

"I will remain an ascetic and do penance under the tree practising selfcontrol and not go back without seeing the blackeyed Sita.

---

### Shloka 46

#### Shloka Text

यदीतः प्रतिगच्छामि सीतामनधिगम्य ताम्।।5.13.46।। अङ्गदस्सह तैस्सर्वैर्वानरैर्न भविष्यति।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ताम् her, सीताम् Sita, अनधिगम्य without finding, इतः from this place, प्रतिगच्छामि यदि if I go back, सर्वैः by all, तै: by those, वानरैः सह along with vanaras, अङ्गदः Angada, न भविष्यति will not live.

#### Explanation

"Besides, if I go back without finding Sita all the vanaras including Angada will die.

---

### Shloka 47

#### Shloka Text

विनाशे बहवो दोषा जीवन् भद्राणि पश्यति।।5.13.47।। तस्मात्प्राणान् धरिष्यामि ध्रुवो जीवितसङ्गमः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विनाशे by comitting suicide, बहवः many, दोषाः sins, जीवन् by living, भद्राणि auspicious events, पश्यति can be experienced, तस्मात् therefore, प्राणान् life, धरिष्यामि I will sustain, जीवितसङ्गमः only a living person, ध्रुवः is certain

#### Explanation

"If I commit suicide, I will earn numerous sins. By being alive, I can witness auspicious events. Therefore, I will live, for, happiness can come only to the living.

---

### Shloka 48

#### Shloka Text

एवं बहुविधं दुःखं मनसा धरायन्मुहुः।।5.13.48।। नाध्यगच्छत्तदा पारं शोकस्य कपिकुञ्चरः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कपिकुञ्जरः the elephant among monkeys, एवम् that way, बहुविधम् in many ways, दुःखम् grief, मुहुः over and over, मनसा in mind, धारयन् by holding, तदा then, शोकस्य sorrow, पारम् long, नाध्यगच्छात् did not reach of the end.

#### Explanation

The elephant among monkeys (Hanuman) worrying over and over again, holding his head for long could not reach the end of his grief.

---

### Shloka 49

#### Shloka Text

रावणं वा वधिष्यामि दशग्रीवं महाबलम्।।5.13.49।। काममस्तु हृता सीता प्रत्याचीर्णं भविष्यति।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वा or, दशग्रीवम् tenheaded one, महाबलम् endowed with great strength, रावणम् Ravana, वधिष्यामि I will, हृता dead, सीता Sita, कामम् अस्तु desire will be fulfilled, प्रत्याचीर्णम् fitting reply, भविष्यति will be.

#### Explanation

"I will kill Ravana, this mighty, tenheaded demon. Sita was indeed carried away by him. This (killing him) can be a fitting reply to his evil action. My desire would be fulfilled.

---

### Shloka 50

#### Shloka Text

अथवैनं समुत्क्षिप्य उपर्युपरि सागरम्।।5.13.50।। रामायोपहरिष्यामि पशुं पशुपतेरिव।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथवा otherwise, एनम् him, सागरम् उपर्युपरि over the ocean, समुत्क्षिप्य by lifting across (like a sacrificial prey), पशुपतेः to lord Siva, पशुमिव like a bull, रामाय to Rama, उपहरिष्यामि I shall offer.

#### Explanation

"Or else, I shall carry him across the sea and put him before Rama like a bull as if he is a sacrificial prey to lord Siva."

---

### Shloka 51

#### Shloka Text

इति चिन्तां समापन्नः सीतामनधिगम्य ताम्।।5.13.51।। ध्यानशोकपरीतात्मा चिन्तयामास वानरः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वानरः vanara, ताम् her, सीताम् Sita, अनधिगम्य unable to find, इति thus, चिन्ताम् worry, समापन्नः distressed, ध्यानशोकपरीतात्मा whose mind was overcome with anxiety, चिन्तयामास started thinking further.

#### Explanation

Distressed over his inability to find Sita, Hanuman's mind was overcome with anxiety and he started thinking further.

---

### Shloka 52

#### Shloka Text

यावत्सीतां हि पश्यामि रामपत्नीं यशस्विनीम्।।5.13.52।। तावदेतां पुरीं लङ्कां विचिनोमि पुनः पुनः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामपत्नीम् Rama's wife, यशस्विनीम् famed, सीताम् Sita, यावत् until, पश्यामि I see, तावत् till then, एताम् this, लङ्कां पुरीम् city of Lanka, पुनः पुनः again and again, विचिनोमि I will keep searching.

#### Explanation

"Till I find the virtuous Sita, wife of Rama in this Lanka, I shall keep searching everywhere again and again.

---

### Shloka 53

#### Shloka Text

सम्पातिवचनाच्चापि रामं यद्यानयाम्यहम्।।5.13.53।। अपश्यन् राघवो भार्यां निर्धहेत्सर्ववानरान्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सम्पातिवचनात् on Sampati's words, अहम् I, रामम् Rama, आनयामि यदि if I had brought, राघवः Raghava, भार्याम् wife, अपश्यन् unable to see, सर्ववानरान् all vanaras, निर्दहेत् would have burnt.

#### Explanation

"Had I brought Raghava following  the words of Sampati, Rama would have burnt all vanaras unable to find his wife.

---

### Shloka 54

#### Shloka Text

इहैव नियताहारो वत्स्यामि नियतेन्द्रियः।।5.13.54।। न मत्कृते विनश्येयुः सर्वे ते नरवानराः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नियताहारः restricting food, नियतेन्द्रियः controlling senses, इहैव here itself, वत्स्यामि I will stay, मत्कृते for my fault, ते those, नरवानराः naras and vanaras, न विनश्येयुः will not perish.

#### Explanation

"I will stay here itself, restricting my food and controlling my senses. Let not men and monkeys perish for my fault.

---

### Shloka 55

#### Shloka Text

अशोकवनिका चेयं दृश्यते या महाद्रुमा।।5.13.55।। इमामधिगमिष्यामि न हीयं विचिता मया।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाद्रुमा huge trees, या such, इयम् this, अशोकवनिका Ashoka grove, दृश्यते is seen, इमाम् there, अधिगमिष्यामि I will go, इयम् here, मया by me, न विचिता हि not looked for.

#### Explanation

"Here is the Ashoka grove with huge trees. I will go there. I have not looked for her in this place.

---

### Shloka 56

#### Shloka Text

वसून्रुद्रांस्तथाऽदित्यानश्विनौ मरुतोऽपि च।।5.13.56।। नमस्कृत्वा गमिष्यामि रक्षसां शोकवर्धनः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वसून् Vasus, रुद्रान् Rudras, तथा likewise, आदित्यान् Adityas, अश्विनौ Aswinis, मरुतोऽपि च Maruts also, नमस्कृत्वा after offering salutations, रक्षसाम् of  demons, शोकवर्धनः increase grief, गमिष्यामि will go.

#### Explanation

"I shall offer salutations to Vasus, Rudras Adityas and Aswinis and proceed to  increase (cause) the sorrow of the demons. (There are eight Vasus, eleven Rudras, twelve Adityas, two Ashwins and fortynine Windgods).

---

### Shloka 57

#### Shloka Text

जित्वा तु राक्षसान् सर्वानिक्ष्वाकुकुलनन्दिनीम्।।5.13.57।। सम्प्रदास्यामि रामाय यथा सिद्धिं तपस्विने।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सर्वान् all, राक्षसान् ogres, जित्वा तु having conquered, इक्ष्वाकुकुलनन्दिनीम् delight of Ikshvaku family, तपस्विने for the ascetic, सिद्धिं यथा an offering like, रामाय to Rama, सम्प्रदास्यामि I will offer.

#### Explanation

"I will conquer all the ogres and pass them to Sita, the delight of the Ikshvaku family  as an offering and give Rama to her as the fruit of her austerities."

---

### Shloka 58

#### Shloka Text

सः मुहूर्तमिव ध्यात्वा चिन्तावग्रथितेन्द्रियः।।5.13.58।। उदतिष्ठन्महातेजा हनुमान् मारुतात्मजः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महातेजाः brilliant, मारुतात्मजः son of the Windgod, सः हनुमान् Hanuman, चिन्तावग्रथितेन्द्रियः with senses overwhelmed by worries, मुहूर्तमिव for a while, ध्यात्वा after reflecting, उदतिष्ठत् got up.

#### Explanation

The brilliant son of the Windgod, Hanuman, whose senses were overwhelmed with worries, regained his senses in a short while and got up.

---

### Shloka 59

#### Shloka Text

नमोऽस्तु रामाय सलक्ष्मणाय देव्यै च तस्यै जनकात्मजायै। नमोऽस्तु रुद्रेंद्रयमानिलेभ्यो नमोऽस्तु चन्द्रार्कमरुद्गणेभ्यः।।5.13.59।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सलक्ष्मणाय along with Lakshmana, रामाय to Rama, नमः salutations, अस्तु I offer, देव्यै to the divine lady, तस्यै to her, जनकात्मजायै च to Janaka's daughter, रुद्रेन्द्रयमानिलेभ्यः to Rudra, Indra, Yama and Vayu, नमः salutations, अस्तु be, चन्द्रार्कमरुद्गणेभ्यः Sun, Moon and Marutas, नमः salutations, अस्तु I offer.

#### Explanation

"My salutations to Lakshmana, Rama and divine daughter of Janaka. Salutations to Rudra, Indra, Yama and Vayu. Salutations to Sun, Moon and Maruts"

---

### Shloka 60

#### Shloka Text

स तेभ्यस्तु नमस्कृत्य सुग्रीवाय च मारुतिः। दिशस्सर्वास्समालोक्य ह्यशोकवनिकां प्रति।।5.13.60।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

स: मारुतिः that son of the Windgod, तेभ्यः to them, नमस्कृत्य having saluted, सुग्रीवाय च to Sugriva, सर्वाः all, दिशः quarters, समालोक्य looking at, अशोकवनिकाम् Ashoka grove, प्रति towards.

#### Explanation

Having offered salutations to all gods as well as to Sugriva, he looked at all quarters and left for the Ashoka grove.

---

### Shloka 61

#### Shloka Text

स गत्वा मनसा पूर्वमशोकवनिकां शुभाम्। उत्तरं चिन्तयामास वानरो मारुतात्मजः।।5.13.61।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मारुतात्मजः Hanuman, सः वानरः the vanara, मनसा in his mind, पूर्वम् earlier, शुभाम् auspicious, अशोकवनिकाम् grove of Ashoka, गत्वा after going, उत्तरम् again, चिन्तयामास started thinking.

#### Explanation

Hanuman started thinking again about his task after reaching the auspicious Ashoka grove.

---

### Shloka 62

#### Shloka Text

ध्रुवं तु रक्षोबहुला भविष्यति वनाकुला। अशोकवनिका पुण्या सर्वसंस्कारसंस्कृता।।5.13.62।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शोकवनिका Ashoka grove, ध्रुवम् surely, रक्षोबहुला guarded by many demons, वनाकुला with trees, सर्वसंस्कारसंस्कृता carefully tended, पुण्या sacred, भविष्यति it will be.

#### Explanation

'This Ashoka grove filled with many trees, guarded by many demons is carefully tended. It must be sacred.

---

### Shloka 63

#### Shloka Text

रक्षिणश्चात्र विहिता नूनं रक्षन्ति पादपान्। भगवानपि सर्वात्मा नातिक्षोभं प्रवाति वै।।5.13.63।। संक्षिप्तोऽयं मयाऽत्मा च रामार्थे रावणस्य च।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अत्र there, विहिताः posted, रक्षिणश्च guards, पादपान् trees, नूनम् surely, रक्षन्ति they are protecting, सर्वात्मा all pervading, भगवानपि divinity also, नातिक्षोभम् not violent, प्रवाति वै is blowing, मया my, रामार्थे for Rama's sake, रावणस्य च and Ravana also, अयम् this, आत्मा my own self, संक्षिप्तः made small.

#### Explanation

"The guards posted there are surely protecting the grove. The divinity  pervading all over me (the wind) must also have been controlled as it is also not blowing violently. I have turned my body small for Rama's cause and also to save myself from Ravana.

---

### Shloka 64

#### Shloka Text

सिद्धिं मे संविधास्यन्ति देवाः सर्षिगणास्त्विह।।5.13.64।। ब्रह्मा स्वयंभूर्भगवान् देवाश्चैव दिशन्तु मे। सिद्धिमग्निश्च वायुश्च पुरुहूतश्च वज्रभृत्।।5.13.65।। वरुणः पाशहस्तश्च सोमादित्यौ तथैव च। अश्विनौ च महात्मानौ मरुतः शर्व एव च।।5.13.66।। सिद्धिं सर्वाणि भूतानि भूतानां चैव यः प्रभुः। दास्यन्ति मम ये चान्ये ह्यदृष्टाः पथि गोचराः।।5.13.67।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सर्षिगणाः (स ऋषिगणा:) along with all sages, देवाः gods, इह here, मे to me, सिद्धिंम् success, संविधास्यन्ति will give, स्वयंभूः selfborn, भगवान् god, ब्रह्मा Brahma, देवाश्चैव and also gods, अग्निश्च and Fire, वायुश्च even Wind, वज्रभृत् Indra, the wielder of thunderbolt, पुरूहूतश्च Indra who is invoked several times in sacrifices, पाशहस्तः holding a rope, वरुणश्च Varuna too, तथैव च so also,सोमादित्यौ Moon and Sun, महात्मानौ all great, अश्विनौ च twin Aswinis also, मरुतः Maruts, शर्वः एव च lord Siva, मे to me, सिद्धिम् success, दिशन्तु many give, सर्वाणि all, भूतानि beings, यः whoever, भूतानाम् of creatures, प्रभु: lord, अन्ये other, ये those, अदृष्टाः not seen, पथि on the way, गोचराः creatures moving on earth, मम to me, सिद्धिम् success, दास्यन्ति will ordain.

#### Explanation

May all sages, gods, the selfborn Brahma, Windgod, Indra, the wielder of thunderbolt whose name is invoked in sacrifices, Yama, who carries a rope, Varuna give success and Lord Siva help me. May the Sun, Moon, Aswinis  Marutas assist me. May the gods of the quarters and the lord of all beings help me to succeed. May those whom I could not see also bring success to me.

---

### Shloka 65

#### Shloka Text

सिद्धिं मे संविधास्यन्ति देवाः सर्षिगणास्त्विह।।5.13.64।। ब्रह्मा स्वयंभूर्भगवान् देवाश्चैव दिशन्तु मे। सिद्धिमग्निश्च वायुश्च पुरुहूतश्च वज्रभृत्।।5.13.65।। वरुणः पाशहस्तश्च सोमादित्यौ तथैव च। अश्विनौ च महात्मानौ मरुतः शर्व एव च।।5.13.66।। सिद्धिं सर्वाणि भूतानि भूतानां चैव यः प्रभुः। दास्यन्ति मम ये चान्ये ह्यदृष्टाः पथि गोचराः।।5.13.67।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation
