None

#### Translation

मैथिलि Mythili, एतत् this, एवम् in this manner, कामः passion, कामम् passion, यथाकामम् as it desires, मे  my, शरीरे  body, प्रवर्तताम् may sway, तु indeed, अकामाम् not force to accept my desire, त्वाम् to you, न स्प्रक्ष्यामि I will not touch.

#### Explanation

"Mythili  let passion hold its sway over my body as it will. But I shall not force you or touch you since you do not love me.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

देवि नेह भयं कार्यं मयि विश्वसि हि प्रिये। प्रणयस्व च तत्त्वेन मैवं भूः शोकलालसा।।5.20.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

देवि O lady, इह here, भयम् fear, न कार्यम् no need to feel, प्रिये O dear, मयि in me, विश्वसिहि trust me, तत्त्वेन truly, प्रणयस्व love me, एवम् that way, शोकलालसा entertaining sorrow, मा भूः you should not be.

#### Explanation

"O reverend lady, fear not me on that account. Have confidence in me. Show your love. Do not be swayed by grief .

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

एकवेणी धराशय्या ध्यानं मलिनमम्बरम्। अस्थानेऽप्युपवासश्च नैतान्यौपयिकानि ते।।5.20.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एकवेणी O lady with a single braid of hair, धराशय्या sleeping on the ground, ध्यानम् brooding, मलिनम् soiled, अम्बरम् clothes, अस्थाने without an occasion, उपवासश्च by fasting, एतानि these, ते to you, औपयिकानि not fit for you, न not.

#### Explanation

"O Lady, to wear a single braid, sleep on the ground, brooding, wearing soiled clothes and fasting without proper occasion does not suit you.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

विचित्राणि च माल्यानि चन्दनान्यगरूणि च। विविधानि च वासांसि दिव्यान्याभरणानि च।।5.20.9।। महार्हाणि च पानानि शयनान्यासनानि च। गीतं नृत्तं च वाद्यं च लभ मां प्राप्य मैथिलि।।5.20.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मैथिलि Mythili, माम् me, प्राप्य having secured, विचित्राणि wonderful, माल्यानि garlands, चन्दनानि sandal, अगरूणि incense, विविधानि several kinds, वासांसि च garments, दिव्यानि divine one, आभरणानि च ornaments, महार्हाणि rich, पानानि drinks, शयनानि bed also, आसनानि च and seats, गीतम् singing, नृत्तं च and dancing, वाद्यं च and musical instruments, लभ you may take

#### Explanation

"O Mythili having secured me, you get all these comforts wonderful garlands, sandal, incense, garments and beautiful ornaments, rich drinks, beds and seats.You can enjoy songs, dances accompanied by musical instruments.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

विचित्राणि च माल्यानि चन्दनान्यगरूणि च। विविधानि च वासांसि दिव्यान्याभरणानि च।।5.20.9।। महार्हाणि च पानानि शयनान्यासनानि च। गीतं नृत्तं च वाद्यं च लभ मां प्राप्य मैथिलि।।5.20.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मैथिलि Mythili, माम् me, प्राप्य having secured, विचित्राणि wonderful, माल्यानि garlands, चन्दनानि sandal, अगरूणि incense, विविधानि several kinds, वासांसि च garments, दिव्यानि divine one, आभरणानि च ornaments, महार्हाणि rich, पानानि drinks, शयनानि bed also, आसनानि च and seats, गीतम् singing, नृत्तं च and dancing, वाद्यं च and musical instruments, लभ you may take

#### Explanation

"O Mythili having secured me, you get all these comforts wonderful garlands, sandal, incense, garments and beautiful ornaments, rich drinks, beds and seats.You can enjoy songs, dances accompanied by musical instruments.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

स्त्रीरत्नमसि मैवं भूः कुरु गात्रेषु भूषणम्। मां प्राप्य हि कथं नु स्वास्त्वमनर्हा सुविग्रहे।।5.20.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

स्त्रीरत्नम् a jewel among women, असि you are, एवम् this way, मा भूः do not remain like this, गात्रेषु for your limbs, भूषणम् decoration, कुरु you do, सुविग्रहे lady of beautiful body, माम् me, प्राप्य having secured, त्वम् you, अनर्हा be undeserving, कथं नु how can you, स्याः be.

#### Explanation

"You are a, a jewel among women. You should not  remain like this. You  should adorn yourself properly. How can you remain deprived after having me?

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

इदं ते चारु सञ्जातं यौवनं व्यतिवर्तते। यदतीतं पुनर्नैति स्रोतः शीघ्रमपामिव।।5.20.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

चारु beautiful, सञ्जातम् created, इदम् this, ते यौवनम् your youth, व्यतिवर्तते will pass away, यत् since, शीघ्रम् fast, अतीतम् a past, अपाम् water, स्रोतः इव like flow, पुनः again, नैति does not come.

#### Explanation

"Your charming youth should not pass away (uselessly). A past event does not come back like a current of stream.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

त्वां कृत्वोपरतो मन्ये रूपकर्ता स विश्वसृक्। न हि रूपोपमा त्वन्या तवास्ति शुभदर्शने।।5.20.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शुभदर्शने lady of auspicious looks, रूपकर्ता creator, सः he, विश्वसृक् one who creates the world, त्वाम् you, कृत्वा having created, उपरतः he stopped, मन्ये I think, अन्या other lady, तव रूपोपमा comparable to your beauty, नास्ति हि indeed does not exist.

#### Explanation

"O lady  with auspicious looks Me thinks, the creator must have retired after creating your beautiful form. For there is no one comparable to you in beauty.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

त्वां समासाद्य वैदेहि रूपयौवनशालिनीम्। कः पुमानतिवर्तेत साक्षादपि पितामहः।।5.20.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वैदेहि Vaidehi, रूपयौवनशालिनीम् endowed with (extraordinary) beauty and (exuberant) youth, त्वाम्  you, समासाद्य after getting, साक्षात् personally, पितामहः grandsire, कः who, पुमान् man, अतिवर्तेत retain his balance of mind.

#### Explanation

"O Vaidehi you have (extraordinary) beauty  and (exuberant) youth. Who would not lose his balance of mind even if he were Brahma the grandsire himself after getting you?

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

यद्यत्पश्यामि ते गात्रं शीतांशुसदृशानने। तस्मिंस्तस्मिन् पृथुश्रोणि चक्षुर्मम निबध्यते।।5.20.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शीतांशुसदृशानने with face like the cool fullmoon, पृथुश्रोणि heavyhipped, ते your, यद्यत् whichever, गात्रम् limb, पश्यामि I see, तस्मिन् तस्मिन् एच whichever limb I behold, मम my, चक्षुः eyes, निबध्यते unable to extricate.

#### Explanation

"O one with a face like the cool fullmoon, with heavy hips. I am unable to extricate my eyes from any limb (of your body) I see.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

भव मैथिलि भार्या मे मोहमेनं विसर्जय। बह्वीनामुत्तमस्त्रीणामाहृतानामितस्ततः।।5.20.16।। सर्वासामेव भद्रं ते ममाग्रमहिषी भव।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मैथिलि O Mythili, मे my, भार्या wife, भव be, एनम् this, मोहम् delusion, विसर्जय give up, इतस्ततः from here and there, आहृतानाम् brought, बह्वीनाम् of many women, मम my, उत्तमस्त्रीणाम् among the best women, सर्वासामेव of all of them, अग्रमहिषी the chief consort, भव be, ते भद्रम् be blessed৷৷

#### Explanation

"O Mythili  be my wife. Give up this (folly due to) delusion. Be my chief consort among the several excellent women I have picked up here and there. Be blessed.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

लोकेभ्यो यानि रत्नानि सम्प्रमथ्याहृतानि वै।।5.20.17।। तानि मे भीरु सर्वाणि राज्यं चैतदहं च ते।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भीरु a timid lady, लोकेभ्यः from the worlds, यानि those, रत्नानि gems, सम्प्रमथ्य by force, आहृतानि brought, वै indeed, तानि those, सर्वाणि all of them, मे myself, एतत् this, राज्यम् this kingdom, अहं च and I, ते to you.

#### Explanation

"O timid lady, gems brought forcibly from all the worlds, this kingdom as well as myself are yours.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

विजित्य पृथिवीं सर्वां नानानगरमालिनीम्।।5.20.18।। जनकाय प्रदास्यामि तव हेतोर्विलासिनि।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विलासिनि a lovely lady, नानानगरमालिनीम् different cities forming a garland, सर्वाम् all, पृथिवीम् land, विजित्य winning, तव हेतोः for your sake, जनकाय to Janaka, प्रदास्यामि will give.

#### Explanation

"O lovely lady I will win all the cities and lands and give them to Janaka for your sake.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

नेह पश्यामि लोकेऽन्यं यो मे प्रतिबलो भवेत्। पश्य मे सुमहद्वीर्यमप्रतिद्वन्द्वमाहवे।।5.20.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

यः whoever, मे to me, प्रतिबलः a rival, भवेत् may be, अन्यम् and no other hero, इह लोके in this world, न पश्यामि I cannot see, आहवे in a battle, अप्रितिद्वन्द्वम् unrivalled, मे my, सुमहत् great, वीर्यम् virility, पश्य you may see.

#### Explanation

"I'm unrivalled in this entire world. Behold my matchless virility which permits no rival in war.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

असकृत्संयुगे भग्ना मया विमृदितध्वजाः।।5.20.20।। अशक्ताः प्रत्यनीकेषु स्थातुं मम सुरासुराः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मया by me, असकृत् again and again, संयुगे in battle, भग्नाः shattered, विमृदितध्वजाः their flags crushed, सुरासुराः gods and demons, मम my, प्रत्यनीकेषु in the wars against me, स्थातुम् to stand, अशक्ताः unable.

#### Explanation

"The gods and demons were unable to withstand my power in wars fought against me. They were often shattered and defeated with their flags crushed in war.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

इच्छ मां क्रियतामद्य प्रतिकर्म तवोत्तमम्।।5.20.21।। सप्रभाण्यवसज्यन्तां तवाङ्गे भूषणानि च। साधु पश्यामि ते रूपं संयुक्तं प्रतिकर्मणा।।5.20.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

माम् me, इच्छ love, अद्य today, तव your, उत्तमम् best, प्रतिकर्म decoration, क्रियताम् do, तव your, अङ्गे on limbs, सप्रभाणि radiant ones, भूषणानि च ornaments, अवसज्यन्ताम् wear, प्रतिकर्मणा युक्तम् by decorating yourself, ते your, रूपम् form, साधु pleasingly, पश्यामि I wish to see.

#### Explanation

"Offer your love to me. Get yourself decorated in an excellent way. Wear glowing ornaments on your limbs. Let me see you in pleasingly decorated form.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

इच्छ मां क्रियतामद्य प्रतिकर्म तवोत्तमम्।।5.20.21।। सप्रभाण्यवसज्यन्तां तवाङ्गे भूषणानि च। साधु पश्यामि ते रूपं संयुक्तं प्रतिकर्मणा।।5.20.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

माम् me, इच्छ love, अद्य today, तव your, उत्तमम् best, प्रतिकर्म decoration, क्रियताम् do, तव your, अङ्गे on limbs, सप्रभाणि radiant ones, भूषणानि च ornaments, अवसज्यन्ताम् wear, प्रतिकर्मणा युक्तम् by decorating yourself, ते your, रूपम् form, साधु pleasingly, पश्यामि I wish to see.

#### Explanation

"Offer your love to me. Get yourself decorated in an excellent way. Wear glowing ornaments on your limbs. Let me see you in pleasingly decorated form.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

प्रतिकर्माभिसंयुक्ता दाक्षिण्येन वरानने। भुंक्ष्व भोगान्यथाकामं पिब भीरु रमस्व च।।5.20.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वरानने O charming lady, भीरु timid lady, दाक्षिण्येन liberally, प्रतिकर्माभिसंयुक्ता decoration, यथाकामम् as you please, भोगान् luxuries, भुंक्ष्व enjoy, पिब drink, रमस्व च and enjoy.

#### Explanation

"O charming lady decorate yourself liberally. Enjoy these pleasures as you please. Drink and make merry.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

यथेष्टं च प्रयच्छ त्वं पृथिवीं वा धनानि च। ललस्व मयि विस्रब्धा धृष्टमाज्ञापयस्व च।।5.20.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्वम् yourself, पृथिवीम् the land, धनानि च and wealth also, यथेच्छम् as you wish, प्रयच्छ donate, विस्रब्धा being free, मयि with me, ललस्व enjoy, धृष्टम् boldly, आज्ञापयस्व च and command.

#### Explanation

"Give away land and wealth as you wish. Enjoy with me freely and command me boldly. I will obey you by all means.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

मत्प्रसादाल्ललन्त्याश्च ललन्तां बान्धवास्तव। ऋद्धिं ममानुपश्य त्वं श्रियं भद्रे यशश्च मे।।5.20.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मत्प्रसादात् by my grace, ललन्त्याः enjoying, तव your, बान्धवा relations, ललन्ताम् enjoy, भद्रे auspicious, त्वम् you, मम my, ऋद्धिम् affluence, मे my, यशश्च fame, अनुपश्य you will see.

#### Explanation

"O blessed lady, see my affluence and my fame. Seeing you enjoy (life) by my grace all your kinsmen will follow.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

किं करिष्यसि रामेण सुभगे चीरवाससा। निक्षिप्तविजयो रामो गतश्रीर्वनगोचरः।।5.20.26।। व्रती स्थण्डिलशायी च शङ्के जीवति वा न वा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सुभगे O beautiful lady, चीरवाससा person clad in bark robes, रामेण with Rama, किम् what, करिष्यसि will do, निक्षिप्तविजयः renounced triumph of warrior, गतश्रीः he lost his fortune, वनगोचरः wanderer in the forest, व्रती following asceticism, स्थण्डिलशायी च sleeping on the ground, रामः Rama, जीवति वा alive or, न वा not alive, शङ्के I am doubtful.

#### Explanation

"O beautiful lady Rama has renounced victory, he has lost his fortune. Clad in bark, he is wandering in the forest following asceticism and sleeping on the ground. What can he do  for you? I am in doubt if he is alive or not.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

न हि वैदेहि रामस्त्वां द्रष्टुं वा प्युपलप्स्यते। पुरोबलाकैरसितैर्मेघैर्ज्योत्स्नामिवावृताम्।।5.20.27।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वैदेहि Vaidehi, रामः Rama, त्वाम् you, पुरोबलाकैः cranes in front, असितैः dark , मेघैः clouds, आवृताम् veiled, ज्योत्स्नामिव like moonbeams, द्रष्टुं वापि even to see, न हि उपलप्स्यते not possible.

#### Explanation

"O Vaidehi I wonder if Rama can see you at all. You are like moonlight veiled by dark clouds and  cannot  be seen even by the flying cranes.

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

न चापि मम हस्तात्त्वां प्राप्तुमर्हति राघवः।।5.20.28।। हिरण्यकशिपुः कीर्तिमिन्द्रहस्तगतामिव।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हिरण्यकशिपुः Hiranyakasipu, इन्द्रहस्तगताम् from Indra, कीर्तिमिव like fame, राघवः Raghava, मम my, हस्तात् from the hands, त्वाम् you, प्राप्तुम् to win, न चापि अर्हति he will not be able.

#### Explanation

"Surely Raghava will not be able to win you back from my hands any more than the demon Hiranyakasipu was able to usurp the fame of Indra.

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

चारुस्मिते चारुदति चारुनेत्रे विलासिनि।।5.20.29।। मनो हरसि मे भीरु सुपर्णः पन्नगं यथा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

चारुस्मिते lady of charming smile, चारुदति lady of beautiful teeth, चारुनेत्रे beautifuleyed, विलासिनि luxurious, भीरु timid one, सुपर्णः Garuda, पन्नगं यथा like serpent, मे my, मनः mind, हरसि attract.

#### Explanation

"O lovely, timid lady your smile, your teeth and your eyes are bewitching. Just as Garuda snatches away a serpent, you ravish my mind by your beauty.

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

क्लिष्टकौशेयवसनां तन्वीमप्यनलङ्कृताम्।।5.20.30।। त्वां दृष्ट्वा स्वेषु दारेषु रतिं नोपलभाम्यहम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

क्लिष्टकौशेयवसनाम् wearing soiled silk clothes, तन्वीम् beautiful, त्वाम् you, अनलङ्कृतामपि even when you are not decorated, दृष्ट्वा after seeing, अहम् I, स्वेषु at my own, दारेषु wives, रतिम् love, नोपलभामि I find not.

#### Explanation

"Even when you are attired in soiled silk and are bereft of ornaments, I find no interest in my own wives.

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

अन्तःपुरनिवासिन्यः स्त्रियः सर्वगुणान्विताः।।5.20.31।। यावन्त्यो मम सर्वासामैश्वर्यम् कुरु जानकि।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

जानकि Janaki, मम my, अन्तःपुरनिवासिन्यः residing in the harem of the royal palace, सर्वगुणान्विताः endowed with all virtues, यावन्त्यः many of them, स्त्रियः women, सर्वासाम् for all of them, ऐश्वर्यम् authority, कुरु you may exercise.

#### Explanation

"O Janaki There are a number of virtuous consorts residing in the harem of my royal palace. You may exercise your authority on all of them.

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

मम ह्यसितकेशान्ते त्रैलोक्यप्रवराः स्त्रियः।।5.20.32।। तास्त्वां परिचरिष्यन्ति श्रियमप्सरसो यथा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

असितकेशान्ते lady of dark tresses, मम me, ताः those, त्रैलोक्यप्रवराः best among the women in the three worlds, स्त्रियः women, अप्सरस: apsaras, श्रियं यथा like goddess Lakshmi, त्वाम् you, परिचरिष्यन्ति will attend on you.

#### Explanation

None

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

यानि वैश्रवणे सुभ्रु रत्नानि च धनानि च।।5.20.33।। तानि लोकांश्च सुश्रोणि मां च भुङ्क्ष्व यथासुखम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सुश्रोणि heavyhipped, सुभ्रु lady of beautiful brows, वैश्रवणे with Vaisravana, यानि all such, रत्नानि gems, धनानि च and wealth, तानि those, लोकांश्च worlds also, मां च me as well, यथासुखम् be happy, भुङ्क्ष्व you may enjoy.

#### Explanation

"O Sita of heavy hips and beautiful brows Be happy. Enjoy all the worlds of mine, the gems and wealth of Ravana.

---

### Shloka 34

#### Shloka Text

न रामस्तपसा देवि न बलेन न विक्रमैः।।5.20.34।। न धनेन मया तुल्यस्तेजसा यशसापि वा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

देवि queen, रामः Rama, तपसा by penance, मया to me, न तुल्यः not equal, बलेन by prowess, न विक्रमैः nor by valour, न not, धनेन in wealth, तेजसा in brilliance, यशसापि वा or even in fame, न not.

#### Explanation

"O queen Rama is not equal to me  either in penance or prowess or valour or wealth or brilliance or even in fame.

---

### Shloka 35

#### Shloka Text

पिब विहर रमस्व भुङ्क्ष्व भोगान् धननिचयं प्रदिशामि मेदिनीं च। मयि लल ललने यथासुखं त्वं त्वयि च समेत्य ललन्तु बान्धवास्ते।।5.20.35।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ललने O delightful one, धननिचयम् heaps of riches, मेदिनीं च and land, प्रदिशामि will present you, त्वम् you, पिब  drink, विहर sport, रमस्व enjoy yourself, भोगान्  all pleasures, भुङ्क्ष्व you enjoy, यथासुखम् be happy, मयि with me, लल  sport, ते your, बान्धवाः relatives, समेत्य collectively, त्वयि in you, ललन्तु will enjoy.

#### Explanation

"O delightful one I will bestow you with heaps of riches and land. Therefore, enjoy with me drinking, sporting, and revelling. Accept me and be happy to sport with me.Let your elatives join you and enjoy all my wealth.

---

### Shloka 36

#### Shloka Text

कुसुमिततरुजालसन्ततानि भ्रमरयुतानि समुद्रतीरजानि। कनकविमलहारभूषिताङ्गी विहर मया सह भीरु काननानि।।5.20.36।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भीरु a timid lady, कनकविमलहारभूषिताङ्गी deck yourself with pure gold necklace, कुसुमिततरुजालसन्ततानि with stretches of trees filled with blossoms, भ्रमरयुतानि with swarm of bees, समुद्रतीरजानि grown on the sea shore, काननानि in the forest groves, मया सह  with me, विहर you may enjoy.

#### Explanation

"O timid lady embellish yourself with necklaces of pure gold and enjoy with me in the enchanting seaside forest groves full of trees with blossoms and swarming bees. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये सुन्दरकाण्डे विंशस्सर्गः। Thus ends the twentieth sarga of Sundarakanda of the holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 21

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

तस्य तद्वचनं श्रुत्वा सीता रौद्रस्य रक्षसः। आर्ता दीनस्वरा दीनं प्रत्युवाच शनैर्वचः।।5.21.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीता Sita, रौद्रस्य angry, तस्य his, रक्षसः of the demon, तत् those, वचनम् words, श्रुत्वा after hearing, आर्ता oppressed, दीनस्वरा in a sorrowful tone, दीनम् pitiable, शनैः slowly, वचः words, प्रत्युवाच replied.

#### Explanation

On hearing the demon's haughty words, oppressed, pitiable Sita replied slowly in a sorrowful tone.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

दुःखार्ता रुदती सीता वेपमाना तपस्विनी। चिन्तयन्ती वरारोहा पतिमेव पतिव्रता।।5.21.2।। तृणमन्तरतः कृत्वा प्रत्युवाच शुचिस्मिता।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दुःखार्ता afflicted by grief, तपस्विनी ascetic, वरारोहा a lady of beautiful hips, पतिव्रता chaste woman, पतिमेव only husband, चिन्तयन्ती thinking of, शुचिस्मिता with a gentle smile, सीता Sita, रुदती sobbing, वेपमाना trembling, तृणम् a blade of grass, अन्तरतः between (Ravana and her), कृत्वा  placed, प्रत्युवाच replied.

#### Explanation

Sita, austere, beautiful and chaste afflicted by grief kept sobbing. Brooding over her husband and trembling in fear, she placed a blade of grass in between her and Ravana and replied with a gentle smile on her face.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

निवर्तय मनो मत्तः स्वजने क्रियतां मनः।।5.21.3।। न मां प्रार्थयितुं युक्तं सुसिद्धिमिव पापकृत्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मत्तः from me, मनः mind, निवर्तय turn away, स्वजने in your own wives, मनः mind, क्रियताम् you may fix, पापकृत् sinner, सुसिद्धिमिव like blessedness, माम् me, प्रार्थयितुम् to desire, न युक्तम् it is not proper.

#### Explanation

"Turn your mind away from me and fix it on your wives. You should not expect to seek me just as a sinner cannot hope to seek blessedness.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

अकार्यं न मया कार्यमेकपत्न्या विगर्हितम्।।5.21.4।। कुलं सम्प्राप्तया पुण्यं कुले महति जातया।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महति high, कुले lineage, जातया born, पुण्यम् meritorious, कुलम् family, सम्प्राप्तया joined by virtue of marriage, एकपत्न्या a woman devoted to a single husband, मया by me, विगर्हितम् contemptible, अकार्यम् forbidden act, न कार्यम्  not to be done.

#### Explanation

"I was born of high lineage and joined a meritorious family by virtue of my marriage. I am devoted only to my husband. I should not commit this contemptible, forbidden act."

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्त्वा तु वैदेही रावणं तं यशस्विनी।।5.21.5।। राक्षसं पृष्ठतः कृत्वा भूयो वचनमब्रवीत्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

यशस्विनी glorious lady, वैदेही Vaidehi, तं रावणम् that Ravana, एवम् in that manner, उक्त्वा having spoken, राक्षसम् demon, पृष्ठतः at the back, कृत्वा having placed, भूयः further, वचनम् these words, अब्रवीत् spoke.

#### Explanation

Glorious Vaidehi having thus spoken to the demon turned her back and continued:

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

नाहमौपयिकी भार्या परभार्या सती तव।।5.21.6।। साधु धर्ममवेक्षस्व साधु साधुव्रतं चर।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

परभार्या other's wife, सती chaste, अहम् I am, औपयिकी obtained by efforts, भार्या wife, न not, धर्मम् righteous, साधु properly, अवेक्षस्व you may examine, तव your, साधुव्रतम् honesty, साधु honest, चर you may practise.

#### Explanation

"I cannot be your lawful wife obtainable by your personal efforts. I am a chaste woman married to another man. (Being a king) examine the dharma honestly and adopt a pious way of life.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

यथा तव तथान्येषां दारा रक्ष्या निशाचर।।5.21.7।। आत्मानमुपमां कृत्वा स्वेषु दारेषु रम्यताम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

निशाचर nightroamer (Ravana), तव your, यथा as such, अन्येषाम्  others, दाराः wives, तथा in the same way, रक्ष्याः should be protected, आत्मानम् yourself, उपमा an example, कृत्वा by placing, स्वेषु at your, दारेषु at wives, रम्यताम् you may enjoy.

#### Explanation

"O nightranger Enjoy your wives. Take care of them. Taking your example, others' wives also deserve to be protected.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

अतुष्टं स्वेषु दारेषु चपलं चलितेन्द्रियम्।।5.21.8।। नयन्ति निकृतिप्रज्ञं परदाराः पराभवम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

स्वेषु with your own, दारेषु in wives, अतुष्टम् unsatisfied, चपलम् unsteady, चलितेन्द्रियम् one with no control over the senses, निकृतिप्रज्ञम् evil minded, परदाराः others wives, पराभवम् insult, नयन्ति take.

#### Explanation

"The wives of others will insult an evilminded man who is not satisfied with his own wives, is fickleminded and has no control over his senses.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

इह सन्तो न वा सन्ति सतो वा नानुवर्तसे।।5.21.9।। तथाहि विपरीता ते बुद्धिराचारवर्जिता।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इह here, सन्तः pious, न वा सन्ति are not present, सतः of the saints, नानुवर्तसे वा you do not follow, तथाहि this way, आचारवर्जिता that which is a forbidden act, ते your, बुद्धिः mind, विपरीता perverse.

#### Explanation

"Are there no pious men around or are you not heeding their words? Your mind is perverted and inclined to commit a forbidden act.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

वचो मिथ्याप्रणीतात्मा पथ्यमुक्तं विचक्षणैः।।5.21.10।। राक्षसानामभावाय त्वं वा न प्रतिपद्यसे।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मिथ्याप्रणीतात्मा indulging in unrighteous act, त्वम् you, विचक्षणैः who have sense of discrimination, उक्तम् spoken, पथ्यम् proper advice, वचः words, राक्षासानाम् of demons, अभावाय for destruction, न प्रतिपद्यसे you do not agree.

#### Explanation

"You are indulging in unrighteous act and not listening to the advice of wise men who have a sense of discrimination. You are the cause of the destruction of demons.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

अकृतात्मानमासाद्य राजानमनये रतम्।।5.21.11।। समृद्धानि विनश्यन्ति राष्ट्राणि नगराणि च।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अकृतात्मानम् foolish, अनये in unfair means, रतम् engaged, राजानम् king, आसाद्य after obtaining, समृद्धानि prosperous, राष्ट्राणि countries, नगराणि च and cities, विनश्यन्ति are destroyed.

#### Explanation

"Even prosperous countries and cities are destroyed on account of indiscreet rulers engaged in unfair means.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

तथेयं त्वां समासाद्य लङ्का रत्नौघसङ्कुला।।5.21.12।। अपराधात्तवैकस्य नचिराद्विनशिष्यति।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तथा in that way, त्वाम् you, समासाद्य having got, रत्नौघसङ्कुला filled with gems, इयम् this, लङ्का Lanka, एकस्य a single person, तव your, अपराधात् for your crime, न चिरात् very soon, विनशिष्यति will be destroyed.

#### Explanation

"Having got you as its ruler, this Lanka filled with gems (riches) will perish without delay for the crime of one individual.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

स्वकृतैर्हन्यमानस्य रावणादीर्घदर्शिनः।।5.21.13।। अभिनन्दन्ति भूतानि विनाशे पापकर्मणः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रावण Ravana, अदीर्घदर्शिनः of a short sighted, स्वकृतैः by your own deed, हन्यमानस्य killed, पापकर्मणः of a sinner, विनाशे at the destruction, भूतानि living creatures, अभिनन्दन्ति will rejoice.

#### Explanation

"O Ravana living beings will welcome and enjoy the destruction of a shortsighted sinner killed by his own vile act.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

एवं त्वां पापकर्माणं वक्ष्यन्ति निकृता जनाः।।5.21.14।। दिष्ट्यैतद्व्यसनं प्राप्तो रौद्र इत्येव हर्षिताः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एवम् in that way, पापकर्माणम् sinner, त्वाम् you, निकृताः insulted, जनाः people, हर्षिताः will feel glad, रौद्रः angry one, दिष्ट्या luckily, एतत् thus, व्यसनम् doom, प्राप्तः met, इत्येव like this, वक्ष्यन्ति they will say.

#### Explanation

"Seeing you, getting ruined this way for your sin, those who were angry with you for their humiliation in your hands will feel glad and say 'Luckily this terrible fellow has met with his doom' .

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

शक्या लोभयितुं नाहमैश्वर्येण धनेन वा।।5.21.15।। अनन्या राघवेणाहं भास्करेण प्रभा यथा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I, ऐश्वर्येण by power, धनेन वा or even with wealth, लोभयितुम् to be tempted, न शक्या not possible, प्रभा light, भास्करेण यथा like that of the Sun, अहम् I, राघवेण by Raghava, अनन्या inseparable.

#### Explanation

"I cannot be lured by your power or wealth. I am inseparable from Raghava like light from the Sun.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

उपधाय भुजं तस्य लोकनाथस्य सत्कृतम्।।5.21.16।। कथं नामोपधास्यामि भुजमन्यस्य कस्य चित्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लोकनाथस्य of the lord of the people, तस्य his, सत्कृतम् an honourable one, भुजम् arm, उपधाय having used as a pillow, अन्यस्य others, कस्य चित् of any one, भुजम् arm, कथं नाम how can I, उपधास्यामि will I rest.

#### Explanation

"How can I pillow on somebody's arm after resting on the honourable arm of the great lord of the worlds ?

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

अहमौपयिकी भार्या तस्यैव वसुधापतेः।।5.21.17।। व्रतस्नातस्य विप्रस्य विद्येव विदितात्मनः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

व्रतस्नातस्य an ascetic who had ceremonial bath having observed vows, विदितात्मनः knower of the self, विप्रस्य brahmin's, विद्या इव like the knowledge, अहम् I, वसुधापतेः of the lord of the earth, तस्यैव him alone, औपयिकी fit, भार्या wife.

#### Explanation

"I am alone the lawful wife of the lord of the earth like the knowledge of the Vedas which rightfully belongs to a wise brahmin who has realised the self after having had his ceremonial bath (as a token of celibacy) and having observed strict vows during the period of his study.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

साधु रावण रामेण मां समानय दुःखिताम्।।5.21.18।। वने वाशितया सार्धं करेण्वेव गजाधिपम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रावण Ravana, दुःखिताम् grieving, माम् to me, गजाधिपम् with the king of elephants, वने in the forest, वाशितया by the separated, करेण्वेव female elephant, रामेण with Rama, सार्धम् along with, साधु it is proper, समानय you may unite me.

#### Explanation

"O Ravana it is proper for you to unite me in distress with Rama as one would unite a female elephant with the lordly elephant from which it is separated in the wilderness.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

मित्रमौपयिकं कर्तुं रामः स्थानं परीप्सता।।5.21.19।। वधं चानिच्छता घोरं त्वयासौ पुरुषर्षभः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

असौ this, पुरुषर्षभः bull among men, रामः Rama, स्थानम् it is proper, परीप्सता desirous of avoiding, घोरम् dreadful, वधम् death, अनिच्छता च by not willing, त्वया by you, मित्रम् friendship, कर्तुम् to do, औपयिकम् with due effort.

#### Explanation

"I wish you to build friendship with Rama, the bull among men. Surely it is the right thing for you to do, if you are desirous of avoiding a dreadful death.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

विदितः स हि धर्मज्ञः शरणागतवत्सलः।।5.21.20।। तेन मैत्री भवतु ते यदि जीवितुमिच्छसि।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

धर्मज्ञः one who knows dharma, सः he, शरणागतवत्सलः kind to those who seek his protection, विदितः हि famous, जीवितुम् to preserve your life, इच्छसि यदि if you so desire, ते to you, तेन with him, मैत्री friendship, भवतु  be built.

#### Explanation

"He is famous as a knower of dharma and is compassionate to those who seek his protection. If you desire to preserve your life build friendship with him.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

प्रसादयस्व त्वं चैनं शरणागतवत्सलम्।।5.21.21।। मां चास्मै नियतो भूत्वा निर्यातयितुमर्हसि।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्वम् you, शरणागतवत्सलम् one who is compassionate to those who seek refuge, एनम् him, प्रसादयस्व च propitiate, नियतः disciplined, भूत्वा after exercising, माम् me, अस्मै for your sake I, निर्यातयितुम् to return, अर्हसि it is proper for you.

#### Explanation

"Propitiate him. He is compassionate to those who seek refuge. Exercise control over your senses for your sake and it is proper for you to return me to him.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

एवं हि ते भवेत्स्वस्ति सम्प्रदाय रघूत्तमे।।5.21.22।। अन्यथा त्वं हि कुर्वाणो वधं प्राप्स्यसि रावण।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एवम् in that way, रघूत्तमे foremost in the Raghu family, सम्प्रदाय after giving away, ते to you, स्वस्ति welfare, भवेत् may be, रावण Ravana, त्वम् you, अन्यथा otherwise, कुर्वाणः doing so, वधम् death, प्राप्स्यसि will face.

#### Explanation

"It is good for you to give me back to Rama, the foremost in the Raghu dynasty. Otherwise you will face death.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

वर्जयेद्वज्रमुत्सृष्टं वर्जयेदन्तकश्चिरम्।।5.21.23।। त्वद्विधं तु न सङ्कृद्धो लोकनाथः स राघवः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्वद्विधम् a person like you, उत्सृष्टम् that which has been released, वज्रम् thunderbolt, वर्जयेत् may spare its target, चिरम् for a long time, अन्तकः Yama, वर्जयेत् may leave, सङ्कृद्धः angry, लोकनाथः lord of the world, सः that, राघवः Raghava, न will not.

#### Explanation

"A thunderbolt may spare its target, Yama, lord of death may spare for a long time the life of a person doomed. But Rama, the lord of the world, will not spare any one when enraged.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

रामस्य धनुषः शब्दं श्रोष्यसि त्वं महास्वनम्।।5.21.24।। शतक्रतुविसृष्टस्य निर्घोषमशनेरिव।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शतक्रतुविसृष्टस्य released by Indra who has performed a hundred yajnas, अशनेः of a thunderbolt, निर्घोषमिव like the rumble, रामस्य Rama's, धनुषः bow's, महास्वनम् great twang, शब्दम् sound, त्वम् you, श्रोष्यसि you will hear.

#### Explanation

"You will hear the terrible twang of Rama's bow resembling the rumble of the thunderbolt released by Indra who performed a hundred yajnas.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

इह शीघ्रं सुपर्वाणो ज्वलितास्या इवोरगा: ।।5.21.25।। इषवो निपतिष्यन्ति रामलक्ष्मणलक्षणाः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सुपर्वाणः well jointed arrows, शीघ्रं soon, ज्वलितास्याः with flaming fangs, उरगाः इव like the poisonous snakes, रामलक्ष्मणलक्षणाः marked with the names of Rama and Lakshmana, इषवः arrows, इह here, निपतिष्यन्ति will be raining.

#### Explanation

"Arrows marked with Rama and Lakshmana will soon rain on Lanka like the poisonous snakes with flaming fangs.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

रक्षांसि परिनिघ्नन्तः पुर्यामस्यां समन्ततः।।5.21.26।। असंपातं करिष्यन्ति पतन्तः कङ्कवाससः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कङ्कवाससः the shafts with feathers of Kanka bird, पतन्त: while falling, अस्याम् in this, पुर्याम् city, समन्ततः all over, रक्षांसि demons, परिनिघ्नन्तः killing, असम्पातम् covering all over, करिष्यन्ति will fall.

#### Explanation

"The shafts bound with feathers of Kanka birds (released by Rama and Lakshmana) will be killing the demons all over this Lanka. They will not give a chance for any other weapon to fall.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

राक्षसेन्द्रमहासर्पान् स रामगरुडो महान्।।5.21.27।। उद्धरिष्यति वेगेन वैनतेय इवोरगान्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महान् great, सः he, रामगरुडः Garuda of Rama, राक्षसेन्द्र demon king, महासर्पान् great serpents (in the form of demon chiefs), वैनतेयः son of Vinata or Garuda, उरगानिव like serpents, वेगेन swiftly, उद्धरिष्यति will carry up.

#### Explanation

"Just as Garuda swiftly extirpates the serpents, Rama will exterminate the demon leaders.

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

अपनेष्यति मां भर्ता त्वत्तः शीघ्रमरिन्दमः।।5.21.28।। असुरेभ्यः श्रियं दीप्तां विष्णुस्त्रिभिरिव क्रमैः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शीघ्रम् swiftly, अरिन्दमः subduer of enemies, भर्ता husband, माम् me, त्वत्तः from you, विष्णुः Visnu, त्रिभिः with three, क्रमैः strides, दीप्ताम् glowing, श्रियम् prosperity, असुरेभ्यः इव like that from the asuras, अपनेष्यति will take away.

#### Explanation

"My husband, the subduer of enemies, will soon take me away just as Lord Visnu in the form of Vamana took away the glowing prosperity from the demons with three strides.

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

जनस्थाने हतस्थाने निहते रक्षसां बले।।5.21.29।। अशक्तेन त्वया रक्षः कृतमेतदसाधु वै।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रक्षसाम् demons, बले in the army, निहते when it was killed, जनस्थाने in Janasthana, हतस्थाने spot of death,  रक्षः O demon, अशक्तेन having no capacity, त्वया  by you, एतत् this, असाधु dishonest, कृतं वै act is done.

#### Explanation

"When the army of demons as killed you had no capacity to protect and was destroyed at Janasthana, the place of death. You acted in this dishonest way.

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

आश्रमं तु तयोः शून्यं प्रविश्य नरसिंहयोः।।5.21.30।। गोचरं गतयोर्भ्रात्रोरपनीता त्वयाधम।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अधम mean creature, नरसिंहयोः two lions among men, तयोः those two, भ्रात्रोः brothers, गोचरम् not found, गतयोः when they went, शून्यम् desolate, आश्रमम् hut, प्रविश्य entered, त्वया you, अपनीता abducted.

#### Explanation

"You are a mean creature. Finding me in the vacant hut, when the two brothers, lions among men, were away, you  abducted me.

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

न हि गन्धमुपाघ्राय रामलक्ष्मणयोस्त्वया।।5.21.31।। शक्यं संदर्शने स्थातुं शुना शार्दूलयोरिव।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शुना by a dog, शार्दूलयोरिव like two tigers, रामलक्ष्मणयोः of both Rama and Lakshmana, गन्धम् smell, उपाघ्राय smelling, त्वया  by you, सन्दर्शने in their sight, स्थातुम् to stand there, न शक्यं हि it will not be possible.

#### Explanation

"Had you smelt the presence of Rama and Lakshmna, it would not have been possible for you to stand there, like a dog under the gaze of a pair of tigers.

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

तस्य ते विग्रहे ताभ्यां युगग्रहणमस्थिरम्।।5.21.32।। वृत्रस्येवेन्द्रबाहुभ्यां बाहोरेकस्य विग्रहे।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तस्य to him, ते you, ताभ्याम् both of them, विग्रहे battle, इन्द्रबाहुभ्याम् by the twoarmed Indra, वृत्रस्य Vritra's, एकस्य only one, बाहोः armed, विग्रहे इव like the body, युगग्रहणम् seize you, अस्थिरम्  not possible to win.

#### Explanation

"You will not be able to withstand them in a battle. You will meet the same fate as the onearmed Vritra in a battle with the twoarmed Indra.

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

क्षिप्रं तव स नाथो मे रामः सौमित्रिणा सह।।5.21.33।। तोयमल्पमिवादित्यः प्राणानादास्यते शरैः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मे नाथः my lord, सः रामः that Rama, सौमित्रिणा सह accompanied by Saumitri, आदित्यः the Sun, अल्पम्  shallow, तोयमिव like water, शरैः by arrows, तव your, प्राणान् life, क्षिप्रम् at once, दास्यते will take away.

#### Explanation

"My lord Rama accompanied by Saumitri will draw out your life by his arrows swiftly as Aditya sucks up shallow water at once by his radiance.

---

### Shloka 34

#### Shloka Text

गिरिं कुबेरस्य गतोऽथ वालयं सभां गतो वा वरुणस्य राज्ञः। असंशयं दाशरथेर्न मोक्ष्यसे महाद्रुमः कालहतोऽशनेरिव।।5.21.34।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कालहतः doomed by time, कुबेरस्य Kubera's, गिरिम् mountain, वा or else, आलयम् residence, गतः going, राज्ञः king, वरुणस्य Varuna's, सभाम् assembly, गतो वा or will go, महाद्रुमः mighty tree, अशनेरिव by lightning, दाशरथेः by Rama son of Dasaratha, न मोक्ष्यसे you do not escape.

#### Explanation

"Even if you fly to Kubera's mountain (Kailasa) or the residence of king Varuna (ocean) you will not escape Rama's arrows like a mighty tree cannot evade lightning. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये सुन्दरकाण्डे एकविंशस्सर्गः। Thus ends the twentyfirst sarga of Sundarakanda of the holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 22

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

सीताया वचनं श्रुत्वा परुषं राक्षसाधिपः। प्रत्युवाच ततः सीतां विप्रियं प्रियदर्शनाम्।।5.22.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसाधिपः lord of demons, परुषम् sharp, सीतायाः of Sita, वचनम् words, श्रुत्वा on hearing, ततः then, प्रियदर्शनम् is pleasing to see, विप्रियम् unpleasant, सीताम् to Sita, प्रत्युवाच replied.

#### Explanation

Hearing the sharp words of pleasing Sita, the lord of demons replied in unpleasant terms:

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

यथा यथा सान्त्वयिता वश्यः स्त्रीणां तथा तथा। यथा यथा प्रियं वक्ता परिभूतस्तथा तथा।।5.22.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

स्त्रीणाम् among women, यथा यथा more and more, सान्त्वयिता is courteous, तथा तथा so much so, वश्यः a passionate one, यथा यथा more and more, प्रियम् dear, वक्ता speaks, तथा तथा so much, परिभूतः will be humiliated.

#### Explanation

"The more the lover is courteous and loving and pleasing to women the more he is humiliated by them.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

सन्नियच्छति मे क्रोधं त्वयि कामः समुत्थितः। द्रवतोऽमार्गमासाद्य हयानिव सुसारथिः।।5.22.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्वयि in you, समुत्थितः arisen, कामः passion, सुसारथिः good charioteer, अमार्गम् wrong path, आसाद्य taking, द्रवतः speeding, हयानिव like the horses, मे my, क्रोधम् anger, सन्नियच्छति restrained.

#### Explanation

"My love for you has restrained my anger, just as a good charioteer controls the horses galloping on a wrong path.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

वामः कामो मनुष्याणां यस्मिन् किल निबध्यते। जने तस्मिंस्त्वनुक्रोशः स्नेहश्च किल जायते।।5.22.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मनुष्याणाम् in men, कामः passion, वामः partial, यस्मिन् in whom, जने people, निबध्यते bound, तस्मिन् in him, अनुक्रोशः compassion, स्नेहश्च friendship, जायते किल even though punishable.

#### Explanation

"Love is partial. It gets bound to those in whomsoever compassion and friendship is generated, even though they are punishable.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

एतस्मात्कारणान्न त्वां घातयामि वरानने। वधार्हामवमानार्हां मिथ्याप्रव्रजिते रताम्।।5.22.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वरानने O beautiful lady, एतस्मात् on this, कारणात् account, वधार्हाम् deserve to be killed, अवमानार्हाम् deserves to be put to shame, मिथ्याप्रव्रजिते in a pseudoascetic life, रताम् engaged, त्वाम् you, न घातयामि will not kill.

#### Explanation

"O beautiful one it is on this account that I do not kill you even though you deserve to be killed and insulted (for the words used),  even as you are engaged in a pseudoascetic life.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

परुषाणीह वाक्यानि यानि यानि ब्रवीषि माम्। तेषु तेषु वधो युक्तस्तव मैथिलि दारुणः।।5.22.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मैथिलि Mythili, इह here, माम् me, यानि यानि whatever, परुषाणि piercing, वाक्यानि statements, ब्रवीषि spoke, तेषु तेषु for each one, तव your, दारुणः dreadful, वध killing, युक्तः is right.

#### Explanation

"O Mythili for each of the piercing statements made you deserve dreadful death."

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्त्वा तु वैदेहीं रावणो राक्षसाधिपः। क्रोधसंरम्भसंयुक्तः सीतामुत्तरमब्रवीत्।।5.22.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसाधिपः lord of demons, रावणः Ravana, वैदेहीम् to Vaidehi, एवम् in that way, उक्त्वा having spoken, क्रोधसंरम्भसंयुक्तः stirred up with anger and exitement, सीताम् to Sita, उत्तरम् in reply, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

Ravana, the lord of demons, said these words in reply to Vaidehi who was overcome with  anger and exitement.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

द्वौ मासौ रक्षितव्यौ मे योऽवधिस्ते मया कृतः। ततः शयनमारोह मम त्वं वरवर्णिनि।।5.22.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मया by me, ते to you, यः that, अवधिः deadline, कृत: fixed, द्वौ two, मासौ months, मे to me, रक्षितव्यौ keep, वरवर्णिनि of beautiful hue, ततः thereafter, मम my, शयनम् bed, आरोह come to share.

#### Explanation

"I shall wait for two months, the deadline fixed. O lady of beautiful hue thereafter you have to share my bed.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

ऊर्ध्वं द्वाभ्यां तु मासाभ्यां भर्तारं मामनिच्छतीम्। मम त्वां प्रातराशार्थमालभन्ते महानसे।।5.22.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

द्वाभ्याम् those two, मासाभ्याम् two months, ऊर्ध्वम् after that, माम् me, भर्तारम् as husband, अनिच्छतीम् reluctant, त्वाम् you, मम my, प्रातराशार्थम् for breakfast, महानसे to the kitchen, आलभन्ते cook you.

#### Explanation

"If you are still reluctant to accept me as your husband after two months you will be sent to kitchen to be cooked as morning breakfast for me".

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

तां तर्ज्यमानां सम्प्रेक्ष्य राक्षसेन्द्रेण जानकीम्। देवगन्धर्वकन्यास्ता विषेदुर्विकृतेक्षणाः।।5.22.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसेन्द्रेण by the demon king, तर्ज्यमानाम् threatened, तां जानकीम् that Janaki, सम्प्रेक्ष्य observing, ताः those, देवगन्धर्वकन्याः daughters of gods and gandharvas, विकृतेक्षणाः distressed eyes, विषेदुः wept sadly.

#### Explanation

Observing Janaki, threatened by the demon king the daughters of gods and gandharvas shed tears with distressful eyes.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

ओष्ठप्रकारैरपरा वक्त्रनेत्रैस्तथापराः। सीतामाश्वासयामासुस्तर्जितां तेन रक्षसा।।5.22.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तेन by that, रक्षसा demon, तर्जिताम् threatened, ताम् her, सीताम् Sita, अपराः some of them, ओष्ठप्रकारैः with movement of lips, तथा likewise, अपराः still others, वक्त्रनेत्रैः with movement of eyes and faces, आश्वासयामासुः consoled her.

#### Explanation

When Sita was threatened by the demon king, the daughters of gods and gandharvas there consoled her through gestures of their lips and eyes.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

ताभिराश्वासिता सीता रावणं राक्षसाधिपम्। उवाचात्महितं वाक्यं वृत्तशौण्डीर्यगर्वितम्।।5.22.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ताभिः by them (the women), आश्वासिता having been consoled, सीता Sita, वृत्तशौण्डीर्यगर्वितम् proud of the power of her virtues, आत्महितम् in selfdefence, वाक्यम् words, राक्षसाधिपम् to the demon king, रावणम् to Ravana, उवाच said.

#### Explanation

Having thus been consoled, Sita, proud of the power of her virtues, said this to Ravana in selfdefence:

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

नूनं न ते जनः कश्चिदस्ति निःश्रेयसे स्थित:। निवारयति यो न त्वां कर्मणोऽस्माद्विगर्हितात्।।5.22.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

यः whoever, त्वाम् you, विगर्हितात् reproached, अस्मात् from this, कर्मणः action, निवारयति prevents, ते your, निःश्रेयसे who is interested in your welfare, रतः remain, जनः people, कश्चित् some, नास्ति destroyed, नूनम् surely.

#### Explanation

"Obviously there is no one among your people keen on your welfare since nobody is preventing you from this reproachable act.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

मां हि धर्मात्मनः पत्नीं शचीमिव शचीपतेः। त्वदन्यस्त्रिषु लोकेषु प्रार्थयेन्मनसापि कः।।5.22.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शचीपतेः Sachi's husband, Indra, शचीमिव like Sachi, धर्मात्मनः righteous self (Rama), पत्नीम् wife, माम् me, त्रिषु लोकेषु in the three worlds, त्वदन्यः none, कः what, मनसापि even in mind, प्रार्थयेत् would seek.

#### Explanation

"Who in all the three worlds other than you would, even in mind, seek this consort of the righteous self (Sri Rama) like  Sachi to Indra? .

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

राक्षसाधम रामस्य भार्याममिततेजसः। उक्तवानसि यत्पापं क्व गतस्तस्य मोक्ष्यसे।।5.22.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसाधम stupid demon, अमिततेजसः of undimmed brilliance, रामस्य Rama's, भार्याम् wife, यत् such, पापम् sin, उक्तवान् spoke, असि you are, तस्य its, क्व where, गतः gone, मोक्ष्यसे will you escape.

#### Explanation

"O stupid demon where can you go to escape the consequences of your sinful words spoken against Rama's consort of undimmed brilliance?

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

यथा दृप्तश्च मातङ्गः शशश्च सद्दृशो युधि। तथा मातङ्गवद्रामस्त्वं नीच: शशवत् स्मृतः।।5.22.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

युधि in war, सद्दृश: similar, दृप्तः arrogant, मातङ्ग: elephant, शश:च and a rabbit, यथा as, तथा in the same way, नीच: mean, रामः Rama, मातङ्गवद्राम: like the proud elephant, त्वम् you, शशवत् rabbit, स्मृतः as spoken by people.

#### Explanation

"It is like a proud elephant fighting in war with a rabbit. Rama is like an elephant and you are like an ordinary rabbit people speak of.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

स त्वमिक्ष्वाकुनाथं वै क्षिपन्निह न लज्जसे। चक्षुषोर्विषयं तस्य न तावदुपगच्छसि।।5.22.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः such, त्वम् you, इह here, इक्ष्वाकुनाथम् king of the Ikshvakus, क्षिपन् looking down, न लज्जसे you are not ashamed, तस्य this, चक्षुषोर्विषयं within his sight, तावत् until then, न उपगच्छसि  not yet caught

#### Explanation

"You are not ashamed of belittling Rama in his absence. You have not yet walked into his sight.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

इमे ते नयने क्रूरे विरूपे कृष्णपिङ्गले। क्षितौ न पतिते कस्मान्मामनार्य निरीक्षितः।।5.22.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अनार्य ignoble wretch, माम् me, निरीक्षतः while looking at, ते your, क्रूरे cruel, विरूपे uncouth, कृष्णपिङ्गले dark brown colour, इमे these, नयने two eyes, क्षितौ on the ground, कस्मात् why, न पतिते not dropping down?

#### Explanation

"O ignoble wretch I wonder why your dark brown, distorted eyes do not fall off (the socket) when you look at me (lustfully).

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

तस्य धर्मात्मनः पत्नीं स्नुषां दशरथस्य च। कथं व्याहरतो मां ते न जिह्वा व्यवशीर्यते।।5.22.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

धर्मात्मनः righteous, तस्य his, पत्नीम् wife, दशरथस्य Dasaratha's, स्नुषां च daughterinlaw also, माम् me, व्याहरतः while you speak, ते you, जिह्वा tongue, कथम् how is it, न व्यवशीर्यते not snap and fall down on the ground?

#### Explanation

"Why does not your tongue drop down while you speak such words against this consort of the righteous king (Rama) and daughterin law of Dasaratha?

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

असन्देशात्तु रामस्य तपसश्चानुपालनात्। न त्वां कुर्मि दशग्रीव भस्म भर्मार्ह तेजसा।।5.22.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भस्मार्ह  fit to be reduced to ashes, दशग्रीव O tennecked one, रामस्य Rama's, असन्देशात्  not received permission, तपसः penance, अनुपालनात् due to observing, तेजसा with my power, त्वाम् you, भस्म ashes, न कुर्मि I am not making.

#### Explanation

"O Ravana I can reduce you to ashes through the fire of my chastity. But I do not have Rama's permission and I want to preserve my power of ascetism even though you are fit to be consigned to the flames.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

नापहर्तुमहं शक्या त्वया रामस्य धीमतः। विधिस्तव वधार्थाय विहितो नात्र संशयः।।5.22.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

धीमतः of a wise one, रामस्य Rama's, अहम् I am, त्वया by you, अपहर्तुम् to abduct, न शक्या it is not possible, तव your, वधार्थाय for your ruin, विधिः providence, विहितः ordained, अत्र here, संशयः doubt, न no.

#### Explanation

"When I was with wise Rama, it was not possible for you to abduct me. No doubt Providence has ordained this to bring about your death.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

शूरेण धनदभ्रात्रा बलैः समुदितेन च। अपोह्य रामं कस्माद्धि दारचौर्यं त्वया कृतम्।।5.22.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शूरेण by a warrior, धनदभ्रात्रा by brother of Kubera, बलैः with forces, समुदितेन च endowed with, त्वया you, रामम् Rama, अपोह्य bypassing him, कस्मात् for what reason, दारचौर्यम् stealing of other's wife, कृतम् is committed.

#### Explanation

"You are a (great) warrior and a brother of Kubera. You possess an army. Then why did you abduct Rama's wife bypassing him?"

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

सीताया वचनं श्रुत्वा रावणो राक्षसाधिपः। विवृत्य नयने क्रूरे जानकीमन्ववैक्षत।।5.22.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसाधिपः lord of demons, रावणः Ravana, सीतायाः Sita's, वचनम् words, श्रुत्वा on hearing, क्रूरे cruel ones, नयने eyes, विवृत्य turning them dreadfully, जानकीम् at Janaki, अन्ववैक्षत looked at.

#### Explanation

On hearing Janaki, the lord of demons, Ravana looked at her with his cruel eyes opened wide and rolling.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

नीलजीमूतसङ्काशो महाभुजशिरोधरः। सिंहसत्त्वगतिः श्रीमान् दीप्त जिह्वाग्रलोचनः।।5.22.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नीलजीमूतसङ्काशः looked like a mass of dark cloud, महाभुजशिरोधरः who had strong arms and neck, सिंहसत्त्वगतिः gait like a lion, श्रीमान् prosperous, दीप्तजिह्वाग्रलोचनः with blazing eyes and tongue.

#### Explanation

Prosperous Ravana looked like a mass of dark cloud, with strong arms, stout neck, gait of a lion, a blazing tongue and burning eyes

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

चलाग्रमकुटप्रांशुश्चित्रमाल्यानुलेपनः। रक्तमाल्याम्बरधर तत्सङ्गदविभूषणः।।5.22.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

चलाग्रमकुटप्रांशुः tall with unsteady crown, चित्रमाल्यानुलेपनः with wonderful garlands and smeared with unguents, रक्तमाल्याम्बरधरः adorned with red flower garlands and clothes, तत्सङ्गदविभूषणः wearing shining ornamental armlets.

#### Explanation

He appeared taller with an unsteady crown. He was wearing wonderful red flower garlands and clothes and was smeared with unguents, shining in ornamental armlets.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

श्रोणिसूत्रेण महता मेचकेन सुसंवृतः। अमृतोत्पादनद्धेन भुजगेनेव मन्दरः।।5.22.26।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अमृतोत्पादनद्धेन waist band tied at the time of churning the milkocean for nectar, भुजगेन like serpent, मन्दरः इव like Mandara mountain, महता with huge, मेचकेन a dark coloured, श्रोणिसूत्रेण by a thread around his hips, सुसंवृतः wellwrapped.

#### Explanation

Wrapped well with a big black thread around his hips, he appeared like mount Mandara encompassed by a black serpent at the time of churning the milkocean for nectar.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

ताभ्यां स परिपूर्णाभ्यां भुजाभ्यां राक्षसेश्वरः। शुशुभेऽचलसङ्काशः शृङ्गाभ्यामिव मन्दरः।।5.22.27।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अचलसङ्काशः looked like a mountain, राक्षसेश्वरः lord of demons, परिपूर्णाभ्याम् with full, ताभ्याम् both of them, भुजाभ्याम् with two arms, शृङ्गाभ्याम् with two peaks, मन्दरः इव like the Mandara, सः he, शुशुभे shone.

#### Explanation

The lord of demons shone like mount Mandara, both his long two arms like two mountain peaks.

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

तरुणादित्यवर्णाभ्यां कुण्डलाभ्यां विभूषितः। रक्तपल्लवपुष्पाभ्यामशोकाभ्यामिवाचलः।।5.22.28।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तरुणादित्यवर्णाभ्याम् of the colour of the rising Sun, कुण्डलाभ्याम् with earrings, विभूषितः adorned, रक्तपल्लवपुष्पाभ्याम् having red leaves and flowers, अशोकाभ्याम् with two Ashoka trees, अचलः इव like a mountain.

#### Explanation

He stood like a mountain with two Ashoka trees clothed with red leaves and flowers in the form of earrings of the colour of the rising Sun.

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

स कल्पवृक्षप्रतिमो वसन्त इव मूर्तिमान्। श्मशानचैत्यप्रतिमो भूषितोऽपि भयङ्करः।।5.22.29।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कल्पवृक्षप्रतिमः resembling the wishyielding tree in heaven, सः he, मूर्तिमान् a personification, वसन्तः इव like the spring season, श्मशानचैत्यप्रतिमः resembled a memorial structure built on the cremation ground, भूषितोऽपि though adorned with jewels, भयङ्करः fierceful appearance.

#### Explanation

He was comparable to the wishfulfilling tree grown in heaven, a personified spring season and a memorial structure raised on the cremation ground. He looked frightening even though  welladorned.

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

अवेक्षमाणो वैदेहीं कोपसंरक्तलोचनः। उवाच रावणः सीतां भुजङ्ग इव निःश्वसन्।।5.22.30।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कोपसंरक्तलोचनः eyes red with anger, रावणः Ravana, वैदेहीम् Vaidehi, अवेक्षमाणः looking at, भुजङ्गः इव like a serpent, निःश्वसन् breathing heavily, सीताम् to Sita, उवाच said.

#### Explanation

Looking at Vaidehi, eyes red with anger, and breathing heavily like a histing snake, Ravana replied:

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

अनयेनाभिसम्पन्नमर्थहीनमनुव्रते। नाशयाम्यहमद्य त्वां सूर्यः सन्ध्यामिवौजसा।।5.22.31।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अनयेन with a wrong policy, अभिसम्पन्नम् endowed, अर्थहीनम् meaningless, अनुव्रते a lady following a vow of chastity, अद्य now, अहम् I, त्वाम् you, सूर्यः Sun, ओजसा with his bright light, सन्ध्यामिव like the morning twilight, नाशयामि dispel.

#### Explanation

"O Sita you are following a vow of chastity and treading a meaningless, wrong path. I will destroy you as the Sun dispels the morning twilight with his lustre".

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

इत्युक्त्वा मैथिलीं राजा रावणः शत्रुरावणः। सन्दिदेश ततः सर्वा राक्षसीर्घोरदर्शनाः।।5.22.32।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राजा king, शत्रुरावणः who torments enemies with his terrific voice, रावणः Ravana, मैथिलीम् to Mythili, इति thus, उक्त्वा having spoken, ततः then, घोरदर्शनाः women of fierceful looks, सर्वाः all, राक्षसीः shedemons, सन्दिदेश commanded.

#### Explanation

King Ravana, a tormentor of enemies, having thus threatened Mythili commanded the shedemons women of fiecrceful appearance as follows:

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

एकाक्षीमेककर्णां च कर्णप्रावरणां तथा। गोकर्णीं हस्तिकर्णीं च लम्बकर्णीमकर्णिकाम्।।5.22.33।। हस्तिपाद्यश्वपाद्यौ च गोपादीं पादचूलिकाम्। एकाक्षीमेकपादीं च पृथुपादीमपादिकाम्।।5.22.34।। अतिमात्रशिरोग्रीवामतिमात्रकुचोदरीम्। अतिमात्रास्यनेत्रां च दीर्घजिह्वामजिह्विकाम्।।5.22.35।। अनासिकां सिंहमुखीं गोमुखीं सूकरीमुखीम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एकाक्षीम् oneeyed, एककर्णां च with one year, तथा similarly, कर्णप्रावरणाम् whose ear was covered, गोकर्णीम् who had cow's ears, हस्तिकर्णीं च  who had ears like those of an elephant, लम्बकर्णीम् one who had long ears, अकर्णिकाम् who had no ears, हस्तिपाद्यश्वपाद्यौ च one with the feet of an elephant and another with the hoofs of the horse, गोपादीम् with cow's hoof, पादचूलिकाम् one with hair on feet, एकाक्षीम् oneeyed one, एकपादीं च with one foot, पृथुपादीम् one with broad feet, अपादिकाम्  who had no feet, अतिमात्राशिरोग्रीवाम् with a huge neck and head, अतिमात्राकुचोदरीम् of large breasts and belly, अतिमात्रास्यनेत्रां च  with big face and eyes, दीर्घजिह्वाम् with long tongue, अजिह्विकाम् with no tongue, अनासिकाम्  noseless, सिंहमुखीम् with a lion face, गोमुखीम् with the face of a cow, सूकरीमुखीम् a boar faced ogress.

#### Explanation

Of them one had one eye, the other one ear, another with ears covered, one had the ear of a cow, another had ears of an elephant and yet another, long, hanging ears. One had the feet of an elephant, another, hoofs of a horse and still another of a cow. While one had a single foot, another had broad feet and yet another no feet at all. One had a long neck and a large head. One had large breasts and belly. One had a big face and eyes, another had a long tongue and yet another no tongue at all. One had no nose and still another had a lion face and yet another the face of a cow.

---

### Shloka 34

#### Shloka Text

एकाक्षीमेककर्णां च कर्णप्रावरणां तथा। गोकर्णीं हस्तिकर्णीं च लम्बकर्णीमकर्णिकाम्।।5.22.33।। हस्तिपाद्यश्वपाद्यौ च गोपादीं पादचूलिकाम्। एकाक्षीमेकपादीं च पृथुपादीमपादिकाम्।।5.22.34।। अतिमात्रशिरोग्रीवामतिमात्रकुचोदरीम्। अतिमात्रास्यनेत्रां च दीर्घजिह्वामजिह्विकाम्।।5.22.35।। अनासिकां सिंहमुखीं गोमुखीं सूकरीमुखीम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एकाक्षीम् oneeyed, एककर्णां च with one year, तथा similarly, कर्णप्रावरणाम् whose ear was covered, गोकर्णीम् who had cow's ears, हस्तिकर्णीं च  who had ears like those of an elephant, लम्बकर्णीम् one who had long ears, अकर्णिकाम् who had no ears, हस्तिपाद्यश्वपाद्यौ च one with the feet of an elephant and another with the hoofs of the horse, गोपादीम् with cow's hoof, पादचूलिकाम् one with hair on feet, एकाक्षीम् oneeyed one, एकपादीं च with one foot, पृथुपादीम् one with broad feet, अपादिकाम्  who had no feet, अतिमात्राशिरोग्रीवाम् with a huge neck and head, अतिमात्राकुचोदरीम् of large breasts and belly, अतिमात्रास्यनेत्रां च  with big face and eyes, दीर्घजिह्वाम् with long tongue, अजिह्विकाम् with no tongue, अनासिकाम्  noseless, सिंहमुखीम् with a lion face, गोमुखीम् with the face of a cow, सूकरीमुखीम् a boar faced ogress.

#### Explanation

Of them one had one eye, the other one ear, another with ears covered, one had the ear of a cow, another had ears of an elephant and yet another, long, hanging ears. One had the feet of an elephant, another, hoofs of a horse and still another of a cow. While one had a single foot, another had broad feet and yet another no feet at all. One had a long neck and a large head. One had large breasts and belly. One had a big face and eyes, another had a long tongue and yet another no tongue at all. One had no nose and still another had a lion face and yet another the face of a cow.

---

### Shloka 35

#### Shloka Text

एकाक्षीमेककर्णां च कर्णप्रावरणां तथा। गोकर्णीं हस्तिकर्णीं च लम्बकर्णीमकर्णिकाम्।।5.22.33।। हस्तिपाद्यश्वपाद्यौ च गोपादीं पादचूलिकाम्। एकाक्षीमेकपादीं च पृथुपादीमपादिकाम्।।5.22.34।। अतिमात्रशिरोग्रीवामतिमात्रकुचोदरीम्। अतिमात्रास्यनेत्रां च दीर्घजिह्वामजिह्विकाम्।।5.22.35।। अनासिकां सिंहमुखीं गोमुखीं सूकरीमुखीम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एकाक्षीम् oneeyed, एककर्णां च with one year, तथा similarly, कर्णप्रावरणाम् whose ear was covered, गोकर्णीम् who had cow's ears, हस्तिकर्णीं च  who had ears like those of an elephant, लम्बकर्णीम् one who had long ears, अकर्णिकाम् who had no ears, हस्तिपाद्यश्वपाद्यौ च one with the feet of an elephant and another with the hoofs of the horse, गोपादीम् with cow's hoof, पादचूलिकाम् one with hair on feet, एकाक्षीम् oneeyed one, एकपादीं च with one foot, पृथुपादीम् one with broad feet, अपादिकाम्  who had no feet, अतिमात्राशिरोग्रीवाम् with a huge neck and head, अतिमात्राकुचोदरीम् of large breasts and belly, अतिमात्रास्यनेत्रां च  with big face and eyes, दीर्घजिह्वाम् with long tongue, अजिह्विकाम् with no tongue, अनासिकाम्  noseless, सिंहमुखीम् with a lion face, गोमुखीम् with the face of a cow, सूकरीमुखीम् a boar faced ogress.

#### Explanation

Of them one had one eye, the other one ear, another with ears covered, one had the ear of a cow, another had ears of an elephant and yet another, long, hanging ears. One had the feet of an elephant, another, hoofs of a horse and still another of a cow. While one had a single foot, another had broad feet and yet another no feet at all. One had a long neck and a large head. One had large breasts and belly. One had a big face and eyes, another had a long tongue and yet another no tongue at all. One had no nose and still another had a lion face and yet another the face of a cow.

---

### Shloka 36

#### Shloka Text

यथा मद्वशगा सीता क्षिप्रं भवति जानकी।।5.22.36।। तथा कुरुत राक्षस्यः सर्वाः क्षिप्रं समेत्य च।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षस्यः O rakshasa women, जानकी Janaki, सीता Sita, क्षिप्रम् quickly, यथा in a manner, मद्वशगा submits to me, भवति you do, तथा in that way, सर्वाः all of you, क्षिप्रम् at once, समेत्य च jointly, कुरुत you may try.

#### Explanation

"O demonesses, all of you jointly dispose Janaki towards me. See that she submits to me at once.

---

### Shloka 37

#### Shloka Text

प्रतिलोमानुलोमैश्च सामदानादिभेदनैः।।5.22.37।। आवर्जयत वैदेहीं दण्डस्योद्यमनेन च।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

प्रतिलोमानुलोमैश्च adopting means favourable or contrary to her will, सामदानादिभेदनैः by  gentle or conciliatory, deceptive or coercive means, दण्डस्य or punishment, उद्यमनेन च by adopting, वैदेहीम् Vaidehi, आवर्जयत attract her attention towards me.

#### Explanation

"By adopting means favourable or contrary to her will or through persuasion or coercion or through reward or punishment, draw Vaidehi's mind towards me." (said Ravana).

---

### Shloka 38

#### Shloka Text

इति प्रतिसमादिश्य राक्षसेन्द्रः पुनः पुनः।।5.22.38।। काममन्युपरीतात्मा जानकीं पर्यतर्जयत्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसेन्द्रः demon king, पुनः पुनः again and again, इति thus, प्रतिसमादिश्य repeated his instruction, काममन्युपरीतात्मा burning with passion and anger, जानकीम् Janaki, पर्यतर्जयत् threatened by pointing his finger at her

#### Explanation

The demon king repeatedly commanded the ogresses burning in anger and passion and threatened Sita again and again, pointing his finger at her.

---

### Shloka 39

#### Shloka Text

उपगम्य ततः शीघ्रं राक्षसी धान्यमालिनी।।5.22.39।। परिष्वज्य दशग्रीवमिदं वचनमब्रवीत्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, धान्यमालिनी Dhanyamalini, राक्षसी shedemon, शीघ्रम् quickly, उपगम्य having approached, दशग्रीवम् tennecked Ravana, परिष्वज्य having embraced, इदम् this, वचनम् word, अब्रवीत् spoke.

#### Explanation

An ogress called Dhanyamalini at once approached the tenheaded Ravana and embracing him, said:.

---

### Shloka 40

#### Shloka Text

मया क्रीड महाराज सीतया किं तवानया।।5.22.40।। विवर्णया कृपणया मानुष्या राक्षसेश्वर।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाराज O king, मया with me, क्रीड you may sport, राक्षसेश्वर lord of demons, विवर्णया pale, कृपणया with a wretched, मानुष्या with human, अनया with her, सीतया with Sita, तव to you, किम् what for.

#### Explanation

"O king of demons, sport with me, my lord. What purpose will be served with this pale wretched Sita, a human being after all?

---

### Shloka 41

#### Shloka Text

नूनमस्या महाराज न दिव्यान् भोगसत्तमान्।।5.22.41।। विदधात्यमरश्रेष्ठस्तव बाहुबलार्जितान्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाराज O king, अमरश्रेष्ठ: foremost of the immortals, तव your, बाहुबलार्जितान् earned by the strength of your arms, दिव्यान् divine, भोगसत्तमान् luxuries, अस्याः by her, न विदधाति not given , नूनम् surely

#### Explanation

"O king surely she is not ordained by Brahma, the foremost among the immortals to enjoy the luxuries earned by the strength of your arms.

---

### Shloka 42

#### Shloka Text

अकामां कामयानस्य शरीरमुपतप्यते।।5.22.42।। इच्छन्तीं कामयानस्य प्रीतिर्भवति शोभना।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अकामाम् a woman devoid of desire, कामयानस्य of a man who desires, शरीरम् body, उपतप्यते only is tormented, इच्छन्तीम् a lady who desires, कामयानस्य one who is desiring, शोभना is enjoyable, प्रीतिः pleasure भवति becomes

#### Explanation

None

---

### Shloka 43

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्तस्तु राक्षस्या समुत्क्षिप्तस्ततो बली।।5.22.43।। प्रहसन्मेघसङ्काशो राक्षसः स न्यवर्तत।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षस्या by the demon, एवम् in that way, उक्तः having been addressed, बली powerful, मेघसङ्काशः appearing like a cloud, सः he, राक्षसः demon, ततः then, समुत्क्षिप्तः turned away, प्रहसन् laughing heartily, न्यवर्तत went back.

#### Explanation

Having heard her words, the demon king who resembled a fierce dark cloud turned away, laughing heartily.

---

### Shloka 44

#### Shloka Text

प्रस्थितः स दशग्रीवः कम्पयन्निव मेदिनीम्।।5.22.44।। ज्वलद्भास्करवर्णाभं प्रविवेश निवेशनम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः he, दशग्रीवः tennecked, मेदिनीम् the ground, कम्पयन्निव as if shaking, प्रस्थितः departed, ज्वलद्भास्करवर्णाभम् blazing like the glow of the midday sun, निवेशनम् residence, प्रविवेश entered.

#### Explanation

Thereupon the tennecked demon departed, as if he was shaking the earth and entered his residence which was shining like the blazing midday Sun.

---

### Shloka 45

#### Shloka Text

देवगन्धर्वकन्याश्च नागकन्याश्च सर्वतः।।5.22.45।। परिवार्य दशग्रीवं विविशुस्तं गृहोत्तमम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

देवगन्धर्वकन्याश्च daughters of gods and gandharvas, नागकन्याश्च and daughters of nagas, सर्वतः from all over, दशग्रीवम् Ravana, परिवार्य having sorrounded, तम् him, गृहोत्तमम् excellent home, विविशुः entered.

#### Explanation

Then the daughters of gods, gandharvas and nagas gathered round and followed him into his excellent palace.

---

### Shloka 46

#### Shloka Text

स मैथिलीं धर्मपरामवस्थितां प्रपेपमानां परिभर्त्स्य रावणः। विहाय सीतां मदनेन मोहितः स्वमेव वेश्म प्रविवेश भास्वरम्।।5.22.46।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः he, रावणः Ravana, धर्मपराम् woman treading the righteous path, अवस्थिताम् steadfast, प्रवेपमानाम् trembling in fear, मैथिलीम् Mythili, परिभर्त्स्य having abused, सीताम् Sita, विहाय leaving, मदनेन मोहितः overpowered with passion, भास्वरम् glittering, स्वम् his, वेश्मैव dwelling, प्रविवेश entered.

#### Explanation

Having derided Mythili trembling in fear yet steadfast in treading the righteous path, Ravana, overpowered with passion left her and entered his glittering mansion. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये सुन्दरकाण्डे द्वाविंशस्सर्गः। Thus ends twentysecond sarga of Sundarakanda of the holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 23

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

इत्युक्त्वा मैथिलीं राजा रावणः शत्रुरावणः। सन्दिश्य च ततः सर्वा राक्षसीर्निर्जगाम ह।।5.23.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राजा king, शत्रुरावणः tormentor of enemies, रावणः Ravana, मैथिलीम् Mythili, इति thus, उक्त्वा having spoken, ततः thereafter, सर्वाः all, राक्षसीः demonesses, सन्दिश्य च having commanded, निर्जगाम departed.

#### Explanation

King Ravana, tormentor of enemies, having thus spoken to Mythili commanded the demonesses and departed.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

निष्क्रान्ते राक्षसेन्द्रे तु पुनरन्तःपुरं गते। राक्षस्यो भीमरूपास्ताः सीतां समभिदुद्रुवुः।।5.23.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसेन्द्रे the lord of demons, निष्क्रान्ते having left, पुनः again, अन्तःपुरम् to the inner chambers, गते gone, भीमरूपाः women of hideous appearance, ताः those, राक्षस्य: demonesses, सीताम् Sita, समभिदुद्रुवुः ran to her.

#### Explanation

As the demon king left for his abode all the demonesses of hideous looks rushed towards Sita and crowded around her.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

ततः सीतामुपागम्य राक्षस्यः क्रोधमूर्छिताः। परं परुषया वाचा वैदेहीमिदमब्रुवन्।।5.23.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, राक्षस्यः demonesses, सीताम् to Sita, उपागम्य went near, क्रोधमूर्छिताः  senses lost in anger, परम् very, परुषया harsh, वाचा words, वैदेहीम् Vaidehi, इदम् thus, अब्रुवन् spoke.

#### Explanation

The demonesses, overcome with anger, approached Sita and harshly said:

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

पौलस्त्यस्य वरिष्ठस्य रावणस्य महात्मनः। दशग्रीवस्य भार्या त्वं सीते न बहुमन्यसे।।5.23.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीते O Sita, पौलस्त्यस्य in Paulastya's, वरिष्ठस्य the distinguished, महात्मनः of the highsouled, दशग्रीवस्य tennecked, रावणस्य Ravana's, भार्यात्वम् to be his wife, न बहुमन्यसे not treating it as a privilege

#### Explanation

'O Sita don't you think it is a privilege to be the  wife of the highsouled, tennecked Ravana, who is the distinguished king of a high family of sage Paulastya?

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

ततस्त्वेकजटा नाम राक्षसी वाक्यमब्रवीत्। आमन्त्य्र क्रोधाताम्राक्षी सीतां करतलोदरीम्।।5.23.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, एकजटा नाम called Ekajata, राक्षसी shedemon, क्रोधाताम्राक्षी eyes red with anger, करतलोदरीम् with a belly of the size of the palm, सीताम् Sita, आमन्त्य्र having called, वाक्यम् these words, अब्रवीत् spoke.

#### Explanation

Then Ekajata, a shedemon of hideous looks, eyes red with anger and a belly of the size of the palm said to Sita:

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

प्रजापतीनां षण्णां तु चतुर्थो यः प्रजापतिः। मानसो ब्रह्मणः पुत्रः पुलस्त्य इति विश्रुतः।।5.23.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

षण्णाम् of the six, प्रजापतीनाम् of Prajapatis, यः he who was, चतुर्थः fourth, प्रजापतिः, ब्रह्मणः Brahma, मानसः born out of mind, पुत्रः a son, पुलस्त्यः Pulastya, इति thus, विश्रुतः very famous.

#### Explanation

'The mindborn son of Brahma is well known as Paulastya. He was the fourth  among the six Prajapatis (Maricha, Atri, Angira, Pulastya, Plaha, Kratu).

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

पुलस्त्यस्य तु तेजस्वी महर्षिर्मानसः सुतः। नाम्ना स विश्रवा नाम प्रजापतिसमप्रभः।।5.23.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नाम्ना with the name, विश्रवा नाम Vishravas by name, प्रजापतिसमप्रभः equal in splendour to Prajapati, तेजस्वी glorious, सः महर्षिः that sage, पुलस्त्यस्य Pulastya's, मानसः mindborn, सुतः son.

#### Explanation

'This glorious mindborn sage Pulastya's son is the famous Vishrava who, in glory and splendour was equal to Prajapatis.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

तस्य पुत्रो विशालाक्षि रावणः शत्रुरावणः। तस्य त्वं राक्षसेन्द्रस्य भार्या भवितुमर्हसि।।5.23.8।। मयोक्तं चारुसर्वाङ्गिः वाक्यं किं नानुमन्यसे।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विशालाक्षि a largeeyed lady, शत्रुरावण: a tormentor of enemies, रावणः Ravana, तस्य his, पुत्रः son, त्वम् you, राक्षसेन्द्रस्य lord of demons, तस्य his, भार्या wife, भवितुम् to become, अर्हसि you deserve, चारुसर्वाङ्गि a lady of beautiful limbs, मया by me, उक्तम् spoken, वाक्यम् words, किम् why, नानुमन्यसे  not heeding.

#### Explanation

'O largeeyes lady to become the wife of the lord of demons is a stroke of good luck. You deserve to be one. O lady of beautiful limbs why don't you accept his proposal?'

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

ततो हरिजटा नाम राक्षसी वाक्यमब्रवीत्।।5.23.9।। विवर्त्य नयने कोपान्मार्जारसदृशेक्षणा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, मार्जारसदृशेक्षणा cateyed, हरिजटा नाम called Harijata, राक्षसी ogress, कोपात् with anger, नयने both eyes, विवर्त्य rolling, वाक्यम् these words, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

Then an ogress called Harijata said this in anger, her cateyes rolling:

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

येन देवास्त्रयस्त्रिंशद्देवराजश्च निर्जिताः।।5.23.10।। तस्य त्वं राक्षसेन्द्रस्य भार्या भवितुमर्हसि।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

येन by him, त्रयस्त्रिंशत् thirtythree, देवाः gods, देवराजश्च Indra, king of the gods, निर्जिताः are conquered, तस्य his, राक्षसेन्द्रस्य of the lord of ogres, त्वम् you, भार्या wife, भवितुम् to become, अर्हसि you ought to be.

#### Explanation

'You ought to be the wife of the lord of ogres who has vanquished thirty three crores of gods including Indra, their king.'

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

ततस्तु प्रघसा नाम राक्षसी क्रोधमूर्छिता।।5.23.11।। भर्त्सयन्ती तदा घोरमिदं वचनमब्रवीत्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, प्रघसा नाम named Praghasa, राक्षसी ogress, क्रोधमूर्छिता overwhelmed with anger, तदा then, भर्त्सयन्ती deriding, घोरम् terrific manner, इदम् these, वचनं तु words also, अब्रवीत् spoke.

#### Explanation

Then an ogress named Praghasa, overwhelmed with anger, deriding Sita said these terrible words:

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

वीर्योत्सिक्तस्य शूरस्य सङ्ग्रामेषु निवर्तिनः।।5.23.12।। बलिनो वीर्ययुक्तस्य भार्यात्वं किं न लप्स्यसे।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वीर्योत्सिक्तस्य Valiant, शूरस्य hero's, सङ्ग्रामेषु in wars, अनिवर्तिनः hero who never retreats, बलिनः powerful, वीर्ययुक्तस्य mighty one, भार्यात्वम् to be a wife, किम् why, न लप्स्यसे not wishing.

#### Explanation

'Why don't you wish to be the wife of such a mighty and powerful king, a valiant hero who has never beaten a retreat in war?

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

प्रियां बहुमतां भार्यां त्यक्त्वा राजा महाबलः।।5.23.13।। सर्वासां च महाभागां त्वामुपैष्यति रावणः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राजा king, महाबलः mighty, रावणःRavana, प्रियाम् his dear, बहुमताम् favourrite lady, सर्वासाम् of all, महाभागाम् highly respectable, भार्याम् wife, त्यक्त्वा renouncing, त्वाम् you, उपैष्यति he is desirous

#### Explanation

'Even renouncing his highly respectable,  favourite wife among all, mighty Ravana is prepared to accept you.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

समृद्धं स्त्रीसहस्रेण नानारत्नोपशोभितम्।।5.23.14।। अन्तःपुरं समुत्सृज्य त्वामुपैष्यति रावणः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रावणः Ravana, स्त्रीसहस्रेण with a thousand wives, समृद्धम् rich, नानारत्नोपशोभितम् decorated with many gems, अन्तःपुरम् inner chambers, समुत्सृज्य abandoning, त्वाम् you, उपैष्यति is desiring.

#### Explanation

'Ravana is prepared to abandon a thousand wives in his prosperous harem  decorated with all kinds of gems in order to accept you.'

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

अन्या तु विकटा नाम राक्षसी वाक्यमब्रवीत्।।5.23.15।। असकृद्देवता युद्धे नागगन्धर्वदानवाः। निर्जिताः समरे येन स ते पार्श्वमुपागतः।।5.23.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विकटा नाम called Vikata, अन्या other, राक्षसी तु ogress also, वाक्यम् these words, अब्रवीत् spoke, येन by whom, असकृत् often, युद्धे in war, देवताः gods, समरे in war, नागगन्धर्वदानवाः nagas, gandharvas and demons, निर्जिताः vanquished, सः he, ते your, पार्श्वम् side, आगतः has come to you.

#### Explanation

Another ogress called Vikata said, ' Know that he who has often defeated gods in war and has vanquished nagas, gandharvas and demons has come to you (seeking your favour)'.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

अन्या तु विकटा नाम राक्षसी वाक्यमब्रवीत्।।5.23.15।। असकृद्देवता युद्धे नागगन्धर्वदानवाः। निर्जिताः समरे येन स ते पार्श्वमुपागतः।।5.23.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विकटा नाम called Vikata, अन्या other, राक्षसी तु ogress also, वाक्यम् these words, अब्रवीत् spoke, येन by whom, असकृत् often, युद्धे in war, देवताः gods, समरे in war, नागगन्धर्वदानवाः nagas, gandharvas and demons, निर्जिताः vanquished, सः he, ते your, पार्श्वम् side, आगतः has come to you.

#### Explanation

Another ogress called Vikata said, ' Know that he who has often defeated gods in war and has vanquished nagas, gandharvas and demons has come to you (seeking your favour)'.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

तस्य सर्वसमृद्धस्य रावणस्य महात्मनः। किमद्य राक्षसेन्द्रस्य भार्यात्वं नेच्छसेऽधमे।।5.23.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अधमे O foolish woman, सर्वसमृद्धस्य who has abundance of wealth, महात्मनः great self, राक्षसेन्द्रस्य of the lord of ogres, तस्य his, रावणस्य Ravana's, भार्यात्वम् to be wife, अद्य now, किम् why, नेच्छसे do not you desire

#### Explanation

'O foolish woman why don't you seek the wifehood of a glorious king of demons who has abundance of wealth?'

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

ततस्तु दुर्मुखी नाम राक्षसी वाक्यमब्रवीत्। यस्य सूर्यो न तपति भीतो यस्य च मारुतः।।5.23.18।। न वाति स्मायतापाङ्गे किं त्वं तस्य न तिष्ठसि।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, दुर्मुखी नाम named Durmukhi, राक्षसी ogress, वाक्यम् these words, अब्रवीत् spoke, आयतापाङ्गे lady of long side glaces, यस्य whose, भीतः by fear of whom, सूर्यः Sun, न तपति not scorch, यस्य by whose, भीतः by fear of whom, मारुतः wind, न वाति स्म not blow, तस्य his, त्वम् you, किम् why, न तिष्ठसि not yield

#### Explanation

Then an ogress called Durmukhi said, 'O lady of long sideglances why don't you yield to him, afraid of whom the Sun scorches not and strong winds blow not?

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

पुष्पवृष्टिं च तरवो मुमुचुर्यस्य वै भयात्।।5.23.19।। शैलाश्च सुभ्रु पानीयं जलदाश्च यदेच्छति। तस्य नैरृतराजस्य राजराजस्य भामिनि ।।5.23.20।। किं त्वं न कुरुषे बुद्धिं भार्यार्थे रावणस्य हि।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सुभ्रु lady with beautiful eyebrows, भामिनि radiant one, यस्य whose, भयात् fear, तरवः trees, पुष्पवृष्टिम् rain of flowers, मुमुचुः released, यदा whenever, इच्छति wishes, शैलाश्च even mountains, जलदाश्च clouds also, पानीयम् water, नैरृतराजस्य for the king of the southwest, Ravana, राजराजस्य king of kings, तस्य his, रावणस्य Ravana's, भार्यार्थे to become his wife, बुद्धिम् mind accepting, त्वम् you, किम् why, न कुरुषे not wishing?

#### Explanation

'O lady of beautiful brows why don't you make up your mind to accept the king of kings, afraid of whom the trees rain flowers, the mountains and clouds release water at his will? Ravana is the king of the southwest, direction Why don't you wish to be his wife?

---

## Sarga 24

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

ततः सीतामुपागम्य राक्षस्यो विकृताननाः। परुषं परुषा नार्य ऊचुस्तां वाक्यमप्रियम्।।5.24.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, परुषाः harsh, विकृताननाः hideous, राक्षस्यः नार्यः ogresses, तां सीताम् that Sita, उपागम्य having approached, अप्रियम् unbecoming, वाक्यम् words, परुषम् harshly, ऊचुः uttered.

#### Explanation

Then the hideous ogresses approached Sita and spoke these harsh words in an unbecoming manner:

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

किं त्वमन्तःपुरे सीते सर्वभूतमनोहरे। महार्हशयनोपेते न वासमनुमन्यसे।।5.24.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीते O Sita, त्वम् yourself, सर्वभूतमनोहरे delightful for all, महार्हशयनोपेते furnished with fine bedsteads, अन्तःपुरे in the women's chambers, वासम् residence, किम् why, नानुमन्यसे not willing?

#### Explanation

'O Sita why don't you agree to dwell in Ravana's inner apartment furnished with fine bedsteads, and which is delightful?

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

मानुषी मानुषस्यैव भार्यात्वं बहुमन्यसे। प्रत्याहर मनो रामान्न त्वं जातु भविष्यसि।।5.24.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मानुषी being a woman, मानुषस्यैव of a man only, भार्यात्वम् being wife, बहुमन्यसे you are holding this in high esteem, रामात् from Rama, मनः  mind, प्रत्यहर you may turn, त्वम् you, जातु indeed, न भविष्यसि will not live.

#### Explanation

'You are  a woman, wife of Rama, an ordinary mortal you are holding in high esteem. Withdraw your mind from Rama or else you will not survive.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

त्रैलोक्यवसुभोक्तारं रावणं राक्षसेश्वरम्। भर्तारमुपसङ्गम्य विहरस्व यथासुखम्।।5.24.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्रैलोक्यवसुभोक्तारम् enjoy the wealth of the three worlds, राक्षसेश्वरम् demon king, रावणम् Ravana, भर्तारम् as husband, उपसङ्गम्य after uniting yourself, यथासुखम् pleasure, विहरस्व sport with him.

#### Explanation

"Accept Ravana as your husband and after uniting with him enjoy all the pleasures of the three worlds.

---

### Shloka 5
