#### Shloka Text

मानुषी मानुषं तं तु राममिच्छसि शोभने। राज्याद्भ्र्रष्टमसिद्धार्थं विक्लबं त्वमनिन्दिते।।5.24.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शोभने O charming lady, अनिन्दिते blameless one, मानुषी a woman, त्वं तु you too, राज्यात् from the kingdom, भ्रष्टम् dethroned, असिद्धार्थम् one who is not successful, विक्लबम् distressed, मानुषम् human, तं him, रामम् Rama only, इच्छसि wanting.

#### Explanation

'O charming lady you have an irreproachable beauty and grace. Why do you think of human Rama only who has been dethroned from the kingdom. He is only a human being who is unsuccessful and distressful.'

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

राक्षसीनां वचः श्रुत्वा सीता पद्मनिभेक्षणा। नेत्राभ्यामश्रुपूर्णाभ्यामिदं वचनमब्रवीत्।।5.24.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पद्मनिभेक्षणा a lady with eyes like lotus petals, सीता Sita, राक्षसीनाम् of the ogresses, वचः words, श्रुत्वा on hearing, अश्रुपूर्णाभ्याम् those filled with tears, नेत्राभ्याम् with both eyes, इदम् this way, वचनम् words, अब्रवीत् spoke.

#### Explanation

Hearing the words of the ogresses, Sita with eyes like lotus petals full of tears replied this way:

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

यदिदं लोकविद्विष्टमुदाहरथ सङ्गताः। नैतन्मनसि वाक्यं मे किल्बिषं प्रतिभाति वः।।5.24.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सङ्गताः joined together, लोकविद्विष्टम् not acceptable to the world, यत् such, इदम् this, मे my, वाक्यम् words, उदाहरथ you spoke, एतत् this way, वः addressed by you, मनसि in mind, किल्बिषम् sinful, न प्रतिभाति not thought.

#### Explanation

"All of you joined together and spoke such words that are not acceptable to the world. Your sinful words of advice do not appeal to me.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

न मानुषी राक्षसस्य भार्या भवितुमर्हति। कामं खादत मां सर्वा न करिष्यामि वो वचः।।5.24.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मानुषी a human being, राक्षसस्य ogre's, भार्या wife, भवितुम् to become, न अर्हति ought not to be, सर्वाः all of you, कामम् freely, माम् me, खादत eat, वः addressed by you, वचः words, न करिष्यामि I will not do.

#### Explanation

' A human being ought not to become wife of a demon. Devour me if you want. I refuse to accept your advice.'

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

दीनो वा राज्यहीनो वा यो मे भर्ता स मे गुरुः। तं नित्यमनुरक्तास्मि यथा सूर्यं सुवर्चला।।5.24.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दीनो वा a dejected one, राज्यहीनो वा a king who lost his kingdom, यः he who, मे my, भर्ता husband, सः he, मे my, गुरुः lord, सुवर्चला Suvarchala, सूर्यं यथा just as to Sungod, नित्यम् ever, तम् him, अनुरक्ता devoted, अस्मि I am.

#### Explanation

'Dejected or dethroned from the kingdom, Rama is my lawful husband. I will ever remain devoted to Rama as Suvarchala, wife of the Sungod is to him.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

यथा शची महाभागा शक्रं समुपतिष्ठति। अरुन्धती वसिष्ठं च रोहिणी शशिनं यथा।।5.24.10।। लोपामुद्रा यथागस्त्यं सुकन्या च्यवनं यथा। सावित्री सत्यवन्तं च कपिलं श्रीमती यथा।।5.24.11।। सौदासं मदयन्तीव केशिनी सगरं यथा। नैषधं दमयन्तीव भैमी पतिमनुव्रता।।5.24.12।। तथाऽहमिक्ष्वाकुवरं रामं पतिमनुव्रता।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाभागा noble, शची Sachi, यथा like, शक्रम् Indra, समुपतिष्ठति is steadfadt, अरुन्धती Arundhati, वसिष्ठं च with Vasistha, रोहिणी Rohini, शशिनम् the moon, यथा likewise, लोपामुद्रा Lopamudra, अगस्त्यम् with Agastya, यथा similarly, सुकन्या Sukanya, च्यवनम् Chayavanam, यथा so also, सावित्री Savitri, सत्यवन्तम् with Satyavan, श्रीमती Srimati, कपिलम् with Kapilamuni, यथा so also, सौदासम् Soudasa, मदयन्तीव Madayanti, केशिनी Kesini, सगरं यथा with Sagara likewise, पतिम् as husband, नैषधम् Nala, king of Naishada, अनुव्रता devoted, भैमी Bhima's daughter, दमयन्तीव like Damayanti, तथा in the same way, अहम् I, इक्ष्वाकुवरम् foremost of Ikshvaku family, पतिम् a husband, रामम् Rama, अनुव्रता I am devoted.

#### Explanation

'I am steadfast (in love) to my husband Rama, the foremost of the Ikshvaku family in the same manner as noble Sachi to Indra, Arundhati to Vasistha, Rohini to the Moon, Lopamudra to Agastya, Sukanya to sage Chyavana, Savitri to Satyavan, Srimati to Kapilamuni Madayanti to Soudasu, Kesini to king Sagara and Bhima's daughter Damayanti to her lord Nala, (the king of Nishadas)'.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

यथा शची महाभागा शक्रं समुपतिष्ठति। अरुन्धती वसिष्ठं च रोहिणी शशिनं यथा।।5.24.10।। लोपामुद्रा यथागस्त्यं सुकन्या च्यवनं यथा। सावित्री सत्यवन्तं च कपिलं श्रीमती यथा।।5.24.11।। सौदासं मदयन्तीव केशिनी सगरं यथा। नैषधं दमयन्तीव भैमी पतिमनुव्रता।।5.24.12।। तथाऽहमिक्ष्वाकुवरं रामं पतिमनुव्रता।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाभागा noble, शची Sachi, यथा like, शक्रम् Indra, समुपतिष्ठति is steadfadt, अरुन्धती Arundhati, वसिष्ठं च with Vasistha, रोहिणी Rohini, शशिनम् the moon, यथा likewise, लोपामुद्रा Lopamudra, अगस्त्यम् with Agastya, यथा similarly, सुकन्या Sukanya, च्यवनम् Chayavanam, यथा so also, सावित्री Savitri, सत्यवन्तम् with Satyavan, श्रीमती Srimati, कपिलम् with Kapilamuni, यथा so also, सौदासम् Soudasa, मदयन्तीव Madayanti, केशिनी Kesini, सगरं यथा with Sagara likewise, पतिम् as husband, नैषधम् Nala, king of Naishada, अनुव्रता devoted, भैमी Bhima's daughter, दमयन्तीव like Damayanti, तथा in the same way, अहम् I, इक्ष्वाकुवरम् foremost of Ikshvaku family, पतिम् a husband, रामम् Rama, अनुव्रता I am devoted.

#### Explanation

'I am steadfast (in love) to my husband Rama, the foremost of the Ikshvaku family in the same manner as noble Sachi to Indra, Arundhati to Vasistha, Rohini to the Moon, Lopamudra to Agastya, Sukanya to sage Chyavana, Savitri to Satyavan, Srimati to Kapilamuni Madayanti to Soudasu, Kesini to king Sagara and Bhima's daughter Damayanti to her lord Nala, (the king of Nishadas)'.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

यथा शची महाभागा शक्रं समुपतिष्ठति। अरुन्धती वसिष्ठं च रोहिणी शशिनं यथा।।5.24.10।। लोपामुद्रा यथागस्त्यं सुकन्या च्यवनं यथा। सावित्री सत्यवन्तं च कपिलं श्रीमती यथा।।5.24.11।। सौदासं मदयन्तीव केशिनी सगरं यथा। नैषधं दमयन्तीव भैमी पतिमनुव्रता।।5.24.12।। तथाऽहमिक्ष्वाकुवरं रामं पतिमनुव्रता।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाभागा noble, शची Sachi, यथा like, शक्रम् Indra, समुपतिष्ठति is steadfadt, अरुन्धती Arundhati, वसिष्ठं च with Vasistha, रोहिणी Rohini, शशिनम् the moon, यथा likewise, लोपामुद्रा Lopamudra, अगस्त्यम् with Agastya, यथा similarly, सुकन्या Sukanya, च्यवनम् Chayavanam, यथा so also, सावित्री Savitri, सत्यवन्तम् with Satyavan, श्रीमती Srimati, कपिलम् with Kapilamuni, यथा so also, सौदासम् Soudasa, मदयन्तीव Madayanti, केशिनी Kesini, सगरं यथा with Sagara likewise, पतिम् as husband, नैषधम् Nala, king of Naishada, अनुव्रता devoted, भैमी Bhima's daughter, दमयन्तीव like Damayanti, तथा in the same way, अहम् I, इक्ष्वाकुवरम् foremost of Ikshvaku family, पतिम् a husband, रामम् Rama, अनुव्रता I am devoted.

#### Explanation

'I am steadfast (in love) to my husband Rama, the foremost of the Ikshvaku family in the same manner as noble Sachi to Indra, Arundhati to Vasistha, Rohini to the Moon, Lopamudra to Agastya, Sukanya to sage Chyavana, Savitri to Satyavan, Srimati to Kapilamuni Madayanti to Soudasu, Kesini to king Sagara and Bhima's daughter Damayanti to her lord Nala, (the king of Nishadas)'.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

सीताया वचनं श्रुत्वा राक्षस्यः क्रोधमूर्छिताः।।5.24.13।। भर्त्सयन्ति स्म परुषैर्वाक्यै रावणचोदिताः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रावणचोदिताः ordered by Ravana, राक्षस्यः by the ogresses, सीतायाः Sita's, वचनम् words, श्रुत्वा after hearing, क्रोधमूर्छिताः overtaken by anger, परुषैः with harsh, वाक्यैः with statements, भर्त्सयन्ति स्म threatened.

#### Explanation

Hearing the reply of Sita, the ogresses ordered by Ravana and overtaken by anger again began to threaten Sita using harsh words.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

अवलीनः स निर्वाक्यो हनूमान् शिंशुपाद्रुमे।।5.24.14।। यसीतां सन्तर्जयन्तीस्ता राक्षसीरशृणोत् कपिः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कपिः monkey, स: he, हनुमान् Hanuman, निर्वाक्यः speechless, शिंशुपाद्रुमे Simsupa tree, अवलीनः kept concealed, सीताम् Sita, सन्तर्जयन्तीः listened threatening, ताः those, राक्षसीः ogresses, अशृणोत् listened.

#### Explanation

Hanuman who kept himself concealed on the simsupa tree speechlessly listened to the ogresses threatening Sita.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

तामभिक्रम्य सङ्कृद्धा वेपमानां समन्ततः।।5.24.15।। भृशं संलिलिहुर्दीप्तान् प्रलम्बान् दशनच्छदान्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वेपमानाम् trembling in fear, ताम् her, समन्ततः from all around, अभिक्रम्य approaching, सङ्कृद्धाः enraged, दीप्तान् glowing, प्रलम्बान् hanging, दशनच्छदान् lips, भृशम् again and again, संलिलिहुः were licking.

#### Explanation

Sita was trembling in fear. The  enraged ogresses went on licking their lips hanging, again and again.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

ऊचुश्च परमक्रुद्धाः प्रगृह्याशु परश्वधान्।।5.24.16।। नेयमर्हति भर्तारं रावणं राक्षसाधिपम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

परमक्रुद्धाः very angry women, आशु immediately, परश्वधान् axes, प्रगृह्य taking up, ऊचुश्च spoke, इयम् this, राक्षसाधिपम् to the demon king, रावणम् Ravana, भर्तारम् as husband, नार्हति not fit to be.

#### Explanation

The enraged ogresses immediately picked up the axes and rose up saying, 'she is unfit to have the demon king, Ravana as her husband'.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

सा भर्त्स्यमाना भीमाभी राक्षसीभिर्वरानना।।5.24.17।। सबाष्पमप सर्पन्ती शिंशुपां तामुपागमत्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भीमाभिः राक्षसीभिः by fierce ogresses, भर्त्स्यमाना threatened, सा she, वरानना charming one, सबाष्पम् with tears, अपसर्पन्ती moved away, ताम् that, शिंशुपाम् Simsupa, उपागमत् reached.

#### Explanation

As the charming Sita was threatened by the fierce ogresses, she moved towards the simsupa tree with eyes full of tears.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

ततस्तां शिंशुपां सीता राक्षसीभिः समावृता।।5.24.18।। अभिगम्य विशालाक्षी तस्थौ शोकपरिप्लुता।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, विशालाक्षी largeeyed one, सीता Sita, तां शिंशुपाम् that Simsupa, अभिगम्य having reached, राक्षसीभिः by the ogresses, समावृता surrounded, शोकपरिप्लुता drowned in sorrow, तस्थौ stayed.

#### Explanation

Then the largeeyed Sita, drowned in sorrow surrounded by the ogresses went near the simsupa tree and stood there.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

तां कृशां दीनवदनां मलिनाम्बरधारिणीम्।।5.24.19।। भर्त्सयाञ्चक्रिरे सीतां राक्षस्यस्तां समन्ततः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ताः the, राक्षस्यः ogresses, कृशाम् shrunken, दीनवदनाम् dejected face, मलिनाम्बरधारिणीम् clad in soiled clothes, तां सीताम् that Sita, समन्ततः surrounded, भर्त्सयाञ्चक्रिरे started abusing.

#### Explanation

The ogresses started intimidating Sita who was dressed in soiled clothes and emaciated with a dejected look on her face.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

ततस्तां विनता नाम राक्षसी भीमदर्शना।।5.24.20।। अब्रवीत्कुपिताकारा कराला निर्णतोदरी।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, भीमदर्शना of terrifying looks, कुपिताकारा of loathsome appearance, कराला a dark one, निर्णतोदरी with a large sunken belly, विनतानाम called Vinata, राक्षसी ogress, ताम् her, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

Then a dark ogress called Vinata with a terryfying look, loathsome appearance and a large sunken belly said to Sita:

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

सीते पर्याप्तमेतावद्भर्तुः स्नेहो निदर्शितः।।5.24.21।। सर्वात्रातिकृतं भद्रे व्यसनायोपकल्पते

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीते O Sita, भर्तुः  your husband, स्नेहः love, निदर्शितः is shown, एतावत् to this extent, पर्याप्तम् it is enough, भद्रे auspicious lady, सर्वत्र everywhere, अतिकृतम् excess, व्यसनाय to adversity, उपकल्पते leads.

#### Explanation

'O Sita you have shown your love for your husband to this extent. It is enough. O auspicious lady anything carried to excess leads to adversity.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

परितुष्टास्मि भद्रं ते मानुषस्ते कृतो विधिः।।5.24.22।। ममापि तु वचः पथ्यं ब्रुवन्त्याः कुरु मैथिलि।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मैथिलि O Mythili, परितुष्टा beseech, अस्मि I am, ते your, मानुषः human, विधिः duty, कृतः is done, ते to you, भद्रम् be happy, ब्रुवन्त्याः मम my, पथ्यम् wishingwell, वच:words, अपि also, कुरु follow.

#### Explanation

"O Mythili I beseech you (to oblige Ravana). You have discharged your duty as a human being. Be happy. But you should also heed my advice which is, after all, salutary.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

रावणं भज भर्तारं भर्तारं सर्वरक्षसाम्।।5.24.23।। विक्रान्तं रूपवन्तं च सुरेशमिव वासवम्। दक्षिणं त्यागशीलं च सर्वस्य प्रियदर्शनम्।।5.24.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सर्वरक्षसाम् of all ogres, भर्तारम् lord विक्रान्तम् courageous, रूपवन्तं च handsome too, सुरेशम् the lord of gods, वासवमिव like Indra, दक्षिणम् a generous one, त्यागशीलं च sacrificing, सर्वस्य of all, प्रियदर्शनम् pleasing in appearance, रावणम् Ravana, भर्तारम् as husband, भज accept.

#### Explanation

'Accept as your husband the demon king Ravana, courageous and handsome like Indra, king of the gods generous, sacrificing and pleasing.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

रावणं भज भर्तारं भर्तारं सर्वरक्षसाम्।।5.24.23।। विक्रान्तं रूपवन्तं च सुरेशमिव वासवम्। दक्षिणं त्यागशीलं च सर्वस्य प्रियदर्शनम्।।5.24.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सर्वरक्षसाम् of all ogres, भर्तारम् lord विक्रान्तम् courageous, रूपवन्तं च handsome too, सुरेशम् the lord of gods, वासवमिव like Indra, दक्षिणम् a generous one, त्यागशीलं च sacrificing, सर्वस्य of all, प्रियदर्शनम् pleasing in appearance, रावणम् Ravana, भर्तारम् as husband, भज accept.

#### Explanation

'Accept as your husband the demon king Ravana, courageous and handsome like Indra, king of the gods generous, sacrificing and pleasing.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

मानुषं कृपणं रामं त्यक्त्वा, रावणमाश्रय। दिव्याङ्गरागा वैदेहि दिव्याभरणभूषिता।।5.24.25।। अद्यप्रभृति सर्वेषां लोकानामीश्वरी भव।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मानुषम् a human, कृपणम् helpless, रामम् Rama, त्यक्त्वा leave, रावणम् Ravana, आश्रय seek refuge, वैदेहि Vaidehi, दिव्याङ्गरागा have heavenly unguents, दिव्याभरणभूषिता wearing imposing ornaments, अद्यप्रभृति from today, सर्वेषाम् for all, लोकानाम् people, ईश्वरी queen,भव you become

#### Explanation

'Leave the helpless human Rama and seek Ravana's refuge. O Vaidehi, using the heavenly unguents and adorned with imposing ornaments from now on, be the queen of all people.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

अग्नेः स्वाहा यथा देवी शची वेन्द्रस्य शोभने।।5.24.26।। किं ते रामेण वैदेहि कृपणेन गतायुषा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शोभने O beautiful one, अग्नेः for fire, देवी queen, स्वाहा यथा like Swaha, इन्द्रस्य Indra's, शचीव like Sachi, वैदेहि Vaidehi, कृपणेन a pitiable, गतायुषा whose life span is to end soon, रामेण with Rama, किम् what is the use?

#### Explanation

O beautiful Sita Just like Swaha, queen to the Firegod and Sachi to Indra, accept Ravana. Why are you still thinking of Rama who is helpless and whose life span is coming to an end soon.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

एतदुक्तं च मे वाक्यं यदि त्वं न करिष्यसि।।5.24.27।। अस्मिन्मुहूर्ते सर्वास्त्वां भक्षयिष्यामहे वयम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

उक्तम् spoken, मे my, एतत् this, वाक्यम् word, त्वम् you, न करिष्यसि you do not accept, यदि if, वयम् we, सर्वाः all of us, अस्मिन् at this, मुहूर्ते moment, त्वाम् you, भक्षयिष्यामहे we will eat you.

#### Explanation

'If you do not heed our words of advice, all of us will eat you at this very moment.'

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

अन्या तु विकटा नाम लम्बमानपयोधरा।।5.24.28।। अब्रवीत्कुपिता सीतां मुष्टिमुद्यम्य गर्जती।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लम्बमानपयोधरा lady with hanging breasts, विकटानाम called Vikata, अन्या तु another woman also, मुष्टिम् fist, उद्यम्य raised, कुपिता angry one, गर्जती roaring, सीताम् at Sita, अब्रवीत् she said.

#### Explanation

Now Vikata another ogress with hanging breasts raised her fist up in anger and roared at Sita.

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

बहून्यप्रियरूपाणि वचनानि सुदुर्मते।।5.24.29।। अनुक्रोशान्मृदुत्वाच्च सोढानि तव मैथिलि।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सुदुर्मते O wicked, मैथिलि Mythili, अप्रियरूपाणि of unpleasant, बहूनि many, तव your वचनानि words, अनुक्रोशात् out of kindness, मृदुत्वाच्च of and due to mildness, सोढानि are put up with.

#### Explanation

O wicked Mythili! we have tolerated many unpleasant words uttered by you due to our kindness and mildness..

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

न च नः कुरुषे वाक्यं हितं कालपुरस्कृतम्।।5.24.30।। आनीतासि समुद्रस्य पारमन्यैर्दुरासदम्। रावणान्तःपुरं घोरं प्रविष्टा चासि मैथिलि।।5.24.31।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मैथिलि Mythili, कालपुरस्कृतम् timely, हितम् wishing you well, नः our, वाक्यम् word, न कुरुषे च you do not accept, अन्यैः by others, दुरासदम् not approachable, समुद्रस्य ocean's, पारम् to the shore, आनीता brought, असि you are, घोरम् dreadful, रावणान्तःपुरम् Ravana's inner chambers, प्रविष्टा entered, असि you are.

#### Explanation

'O Mythili you have heeded not our good and timely advice tendered in your interest. You have been brought to the other shore of the ocean and to the inner chambers of Ravana which is very difficult for others to access.

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

न च नः कुरुषे वाक्यं हितं कालपुरस्कृतम्।।5.24.30।। आनीतासि समुद्रस्य पारमन्यैर्दुरासदम्। रावणान्तःपुरं घोरं प्रविष्टा चासि मैथिलि।।5.24.31।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मैथिलि Mythili, कालपुरस्कृतम् timely, हितम् wishing you well, नः our, वाक्यम् word, न कुरुषे च you do not accept, अन्यैः by others, दुरासदम् not approachable, समुद्रस्य ocean's, पारम् to the shore, आनीता brought, असि you are, घोरम् dreadful, रावणान्तःपुरम् Ravana's inner chambers, प्रविष्टा entered, असि you are.

#### Explanation

'O Mythili you have heeded not our good and timely advice tendered in your interest. You have been brought to the other shore of the ocean and to the inner chambers of Ravana which is very difficult for others to access.

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

रावणस्य गृहे रुद्धामस्माभिस्तु सुरक्षिताम्। न त्वां शक्तः परित्रातुमपि साक्षात्पुरन्दरः।।5.24.32।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रावणस्य Ravana's, गृहे in home, रुद्धाम् arrested, अस्माभिः by us, सुरक्षिताम् very well guarded, त्वाम् you, परित्रातुम् to rescue, साक्षात् even if, पुरन्दरः अपि even the destroyer of citadels, (Indra) also, न शक्तः not able.

#### Explanation

'You are in Ravana's house, wellguarded by us. It is not possible even for Indra, destroyer of citadels, to come to your rescue (let alone Rama).

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

कुरुष्व हितवादिन्या वचनं मम मैथिलि। अलमश्रुप्रपातेन त्यज शोकमनर्थकम्।।5.24.33।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मैथिलि Mythili, हितवादिन्याः of a wellwisher, मम my, वचनम् word, कुरुष्व you may heed, अश्रुप्रपातेन by shedding tears, अलम् enough, अनर्थकम् useless, शोकम् sorrow, त्यज give up.

#### Explanation

'Mythili, act according to our advice. I am your wellwisher. Give up sorrow and stop shedding useless tears.

---

### Shloka 34

#### Shloka Text

भज प्रीतिं च हर्षं च त्यजैतां नित्यदैन्यताम्। सीते राक्षसराजेन सह क्रीड यथासुखम्।।5.24.34।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीते O Sita, प्रीतिं च and love, हर्षं च happiness, भज adopt, एताम् this, नित्यदैन्यताम् being dejected always, त्यज give up, राक्षसराजेन सह along with the demon king, यथासुखम् as you please, क्रीड you enjoy.

#### Explanation

'O Sita offer your love to Ravana, adore him and be happy. Give up this continuous dejection. Enjoy all pleasures along with the king of demons.

---

### Shloka 35

#### Shloka Text

जानासि हि यथा भीरु स्त्रीणां यौवनमध्रुवम्। यावन्न ते व्यतिक्रामेत्तावत्सुखमवाप्नुहि।।5.24.35।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भीरु O timid lady, स्त्रीणाम् for women, यौवनम् youth, यथा since, अध्रुवम् not stable, जानासि हि you know, ते you, यावत् until, न व्यतिक्रामेत् before it is over, तावत् सुखम् till then all pleasures, अवाप्नुहि enjoy.

#### Explanation

'O timid Sita know that youth of women is fleeting and transient. Before it is over, enjoy all kinds of pleasures.

---

### Shloka 36

#### Shloka Text

उद्यानानि च रम्याणि पर्वतोपवनानि च। सह राक्षसराजेन चर त्वं मदिरेक्षणे।।5.24.36।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मदिरेक्षणे O lady of intoxicating eyes, त्वम् you, राक्षसराजेन with the demon king, रम्याणि delightful, उद्यानानि gardens, पर्वतोपवनानि and gardens surrounding mountain ranges, चर range

#### Explanation

'O lady of intoxicating eyes range in the company of the demon king, the delightful gardens and mountain ranges filled with gardens.

---

### Shloka 37

#### Shloka Text

स्त्री सहस्राणि ते सप्त वशे स्थास्यन्ति सुन्दरि। रावणं भज भर्तारं भर्तारं सर्वरक्षसाम्।।5.24.37।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सुन्दरि O beautiful one, सप्त seven, स्त्री सहस्राणि a thousand women, ते your, वशे under your control, स्थास्यन्ति will remain, सर्वरक्षसाम् for all the ogres, भर्तारम् lord, रावणम् Ravana, भर्तारम् as your husband, भज accept.

#### Explanation

'O beautiful Sita seven thousand ogresses will be at your beck and call. (Therefore) accept Ravana, the lord of all ogres as your husband.

---

### Shloka 38

#### Shloka Text

उत्पाट्य वा ते हृदयं भक्षयिष्यामि मैथिलि। यदि मे व्याहृतं वाक्यं न यथावत्करिष्यसि।।5.24.38।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मैथिलि Mythili, व्याहृतम् spoken, मे me, वाक्यम् word, यथावत् faithfully, न करिष्यसि if you do not obey यदि if, ते हृदयम् your heart, उत्पाट्य वा pluck out, भक्षयिष्यामि eat up.

#### Explanation

'O Mythili if you heed not my words of advice, I will pluck out your heart and eat it up'.

---

### Shloka 39

#### Shloka Text

ततश्चण्डोदरी नाम राक्षसी क्रोधमूर्छिता। भ्रामयन्ती महच्छूलमिदं वचनमब्रवीत्।।5.24.39।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, चण्डोदरी नाम called Chandodari, राक्षसी ogress, क्रोधमूर्छिता overcome with anger, महत् huge, शूलम् trident, भ्रामयन्ती while rotating, इदम् this, वचनम् word, अब्रवीत् spoke.

#### Explanation

Next spoke a fiercelooking ogress called Chandodari, overcome with anger taking up a huge trident and rotating.

---

### Shloka 40

#### Shloka Text

इमां हरिणलोलाक्षीं त्रासोत्कम्पिपयोधराम्। रावणेन हृतां दृष्टवा दौहृदो मे महानभूत्।।5.24.40।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हरिणलोलाक्षीम् eyes flashing like doe's, त्रासोत्कम्पिपयोधराम् her breasts heaving in stress, रावणेन by Ravana, हृताम् abducted, इमाम् this lady, दृष्ट्वा seeing you, मे my, महान् great, दौहृदः desire in my heart, अभूत् developed.

#### Explanation

'I have developed a morbid desire to feast upon this woman with eyes flashing like doe's, whose breasts heave with stress and fear after she was abducted by Ravana.

---

### Shloka 41

#### Shloka Text

यकृत्प्लीहमथोत्पीडं हृदयं च सबन्धनम्। आन्त्राण्यपि तथा शीर्षं खादेयमिति मे मतिः।।5.24.41।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

यकृत् liver, प्लीहम् spleen, उत्पीडम् pressing out, सबन्धनम् including all joints, हृदयं च heart also, अन्त्राण्यपि च intestines, तथा similarly, शीर्षम् head, खादेयम् इति I may eat up, मे my, मतिः thought.

#### Explanation

'I thought of feasting on your spleen, liver, heart, all the joints, head and intestines.'

---

### Shloka 42

#### Shloka Text

ततस्तु प्रघसा नाम राक्षसी वाक्यमब्रवीत्। कण्ठमस्या नृशंसायाः पीडयाम किमास्यते।।5.24.42।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, प्रघसा नाम called Praghasa, राक्षसी ogress,, वाक्यम् these words, अब्रवीत् said, अस्याः her, नृशंसायाः of the heartless lady, कण्ठम् throat, पीडयाम we will twist, किम् why, आस्यते are you sitting idle.

#### Explanation

Next spoke an ogress called Praghasa, saying 'Why not we twist and squeeze the neck of this heartless lady. Why are you sitting idle still? Come on.'

---

### Shloka 43

#### Shloka Text

निवेद्यतां ततो राज्ञे मानुषी सा मृतेति ह। नात्र कश्चन संदेहः खादतेति स वक्ष्यति।।5.24.43।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सा she, मानुषी that human being, मृतेति is dead, ततः then, राज्ञे to the king, निवेद्यताम् let it be announced, खादत you may eat her, इति thus, सः he, वक्ष्यति he will say, अत्र here, कश्चन even a little, सन्देहः doubt, न not.

#### Explanation

'Thereafter we will announce that such a human being is dead. Then Ravana will simply say 'eat her up'. There is no doubt .'

---

### Shloka 44

#### Shloka Text

ततस्त्वजामुखी नाम राक्षसी वाक्यमब्रवीत्। विशस्येमां ततः सर्वाः समान् कुरुत पिण्डकान्।।5.24.44।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, अजामुखी नाम called Ajamukhi, राक्षसी ogress, वाक्यम् these words, अब्रवीत् said, सर्वाः all, इमाम् this Sita's, विशस्य  cut her body, ततः then, समान् equal  पिण्डकान् lumps, कुरुत do.

#### Explanation

Then an ogress called Ajamukhi said, 'Let her body be cut into lumps of equal pieces.

---

### Shloka 45

#### Shloka Text

विभजाम ततः सर्वा विवादो मे न रोचते। पेयमानीयतां क्षिप्रं लेह्यमुच्चावचं बहु।।5.24.45।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः thereafter, सर्वाः all, विभजाम we will share, मे to me, विवादः dispute, न रोचते it is disliked, क्षिप्रम् swiftly, पेयम् drink, उच्चावचम् of all types, बहु many, लेह्यम् licking food, आनीयताम् may be brought.

#### Explanation

'We shall share them. I do not like disputes in this regard. Go swiftly and get drinks and food to lick. Let the accompanying  dishes of all variety and quality be brought.'

---

### Shloka 46

#### Shloka Text

ततः शूर्पणखा नाम राक्षसी वाक्यमब्रवीत्। अजामुख्या यदुक्तं हि तदेव मम रोचते।।5.24.46।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, शूर्पणखा नाम called Soorpanakha, राक्षसी rakshasi, वाक्यम् these words, अब्रवीत् said, अजामुख्या Ajamukhi, यत्  what ever, उक्तम् was said, तदेव that alone, मम to me, रोचते is acceptable.

#### Explanation

Then an ogress called Surpanakha said, 'I too like the proposal of Ajamukhi.'

---

### Shloka 47

#### Shloka Text

सुरा चानीयतां क्षिप्रं सर्वशोकविनाशिनी। मानुषं मांसमास्वाद्य नृत्यामोऽथ निकुम्भिलाम्।।5.24.47।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

क्षिप्रम् quickly, सर्वशोकविनाशिनी destroyer of all sorrows, सुरा च wine also, आनीयताम् may be brought, मानुषम् humans, मांसम् flesh, आस्वाद्य after relishing, अथ then, निकुम्भिलाम् Nikumbhila, नृत्यामः we will dance.

#### Explanation

'Get the wine quickly, destroyer of all sorrows. After relishing the human flesh we will start Nikumbhila dance.'

---

### Shloka 48

#### Shloka Text

एवं संभर्त्स्यमाना सा सीता सुरसुतोपमा। राक्षसीभिः सुघोराभिर्दैर्यमुत्सृज्य रोदिति।।5.24.48।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सुघोराभिः by the highly dreadful ones, राक्षसीभिः by ogresses, एवम् in that manner, संभर्त्स्यमाना being threatened, सुरसुतोपमा like the daughter of a god, सा सीता that Sita, धैर्यम् courage, उत्सृज्य after giving up, रोदिति she cried aloud.

#### Explanation

Threatened by the ogresses in that manner, Sita who was like the daughter of a god, cried aloud. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये सुन्दरकाण्डे चतुर्विंशस्सर्गः। Thus ends the twentyfourth sarga of Sundarakanda of the holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 25

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

अथ तासां वदन्तीनां परुषं दारुणं बहु। राक्षसीनामासौम्यानां रुरोद जनकात्मजा।।5.25.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथ then, असौम्यानाम्  ugly, तासाम् of them, राक्षसीनाम् of the ogresses, परुषम् harsh, दारुणम् terrific, बहु much, वदन्तीनाम् they spoke, जनकात्मजा Janaka's daughter, रुरोद cried.

#### Explanation

On hearing the many harsh words of dire threat by the ugly ogresses, the daughter of Janaka wept.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्ता तु वैदेही राक्षसीभिर्मनस्विनी। उवाच परमत्रस्ता भाष्पग्द्गदया गिरा।।5.25.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसीभिः by the ogresses, एवम् in that way, उक्ता having spoken, मनस्विनी a noble lady, वैदेही Vaidehi, परमत्रस्ता terribly afraid, भाष्पगद्गदया throat choked, with tears, गिरा with a word, उवाच said.

#### Explanation

In reply to the ogresses who thus spoke, noble Vaidehi, terribly frightened, said in a voice choked with tears.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

न मानुषी राक्षसस्य भार्या भवितुमर्हति। कामं खादत मां सर्वा न करिष्यामि वो वचः।।5.25.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मानुषी a human, राक्षसस्य demon's, भार्या wife, भवितुम् to become, न अर्हति not fit, सर्वाः all, कामम् freely, माम् me, खादत you can eat, वः your, वचः word, न करिष्यामि I will not follow.

#### Explanation

'A human ought not to become the wife of a demon. You can eat me up. I will not follow your advice.'

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

सा राक्षसीमध्यगता सीता सुरसुतोपमा। न शर्म लेभे दुःखार्ता रावणेन च तर्जिता।।5.25.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसीमध्यगता surrounded by ogresses, सुरसुतोपमा like the daughter of a divine, रावणेन च by Ravana, तर्जिता threatened, सा सीता that Sita, दुःखार्ता distressed, शर्म solace, न लेभे could not get.

#### Explanation

Surrounded by the ogresses, distressed Sita, who was like the daughter of a divine, did not get any solace.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

वेपते स्माधिकं सीता विशन्ती वाङ्गमात्मनः। वने यूथपरिभ्रष्टा मृगी कोकैरिवार्दिता।।5.25.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वने in the forest, यूथपरिभ्रष्टा strayed from the herd, कोकैः by wolves, अर्दिता tormented, मृगीव like a doe, सीता Sita, आत्मनः herself, अङ्गम् her limbs, विशन्तिः withdrawing herself, अधिकम् excessively, वेपते स्म trembled.

#### Explanation

'Like a doe that strayed from the herd in the forest and tormented by  wolves around, Sita had withdrawn into herself all her limbs through fear, she was trembling excessively.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

सा त्वशोकस्य विपुलां शाखामालम्ब्य पुष्पिताम्। चिन्तयामास शोकेन भर्तारं भग्नमानसा।।5.25.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शोकेन with grief, भग्नमानसा with a broken heart, सा she, अशोकस्य Ashoka tree, पुष्पिताम् flowered, विपुलाम् large, शाखाम् branch, आलम्ब्य laying hold on, भर्तारम् of her husband, चिन्तयामास thought.

#### Explanation

Laying hold on a large blossoming branch of the Ashoka tree, Sita thought of her husband with a broken heart.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

सा स्नापयन्ती विपुलौ स्तनौ नेत्रजलस्रवैः। चिन्तयन्ती न शोकस्य तदान्तमधिगच्छति।।5.25.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, सा she, नेत्रजलस्रवैः with the flow of tears, विपुलौ large, स्तनौ breasts, स्नापयन्ती bathing them, चिन्तयन्ती brooding, शोकस्य of sorrow, अन्तम् end, न अधिगच्छति could not find.

#### Explanation

Her large breasts bathed with the flow of tears, she continued to brood over her plight  and found no end to it.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

सा वेपमाना पतिता प्रवाते कदली यथा। राक्षसीनां भयत्रस्ता विवर्णवदनाभवत्।।5.25.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सा she, प्रवाते to stormy wind, पतिता fallen, कदली यथा like a banana plant, वेपमाना trembling, राक्षसीनां of the ogresses, भयंत्रस्ता frightened, विवर्णवदना palefaced, अभवत् became.

#### Explanation

Trembling like a banana tree uprooted by the stormy wind, Sita who was seized with fear of the ogresses looked pale and desperate.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

तस्याः सा दीर्घविपुला वेपन्त्या सीतया तदा। ददृशे कम्पिनी वेणी व्यालीव परिसर्पती।।5.25.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वेपन्त्याः of the trembling Sita, तस्याः her, दीर्घविपुला long and luxuriant, कम्पिनी shaking, सा that, सीतया by Sita, वेणी braid, परिसर्पती moving, व्यालीव like a female serpent, ददृशे appeared.

#### Explanation

The long, luxuriant braid of hair of the trembling Sita waving (in the wind), appeared like a crawling female serpent.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

सा निःश्वसन्ती दुःखार्ता शोकोपहतचेतना। आर्ता व्यसृजदश्रूणि मैथिली विललाप च।।5.25.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दुःखार्ता distressed lady, शोकोपहतचेतना consciousness drowned in tears, आर्ता afflicted, सा she, मैथिली Mythili, निःश्वसन्ती while sighing, अश्रूणि tears, व्यसृजत् shedding, विललाप ह cried.

#### Explanation

The distressed Mythili, her consciousness drowned in grief, sighing, shed streams of tears.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

हा रामेति च दुःखार्ता हा पुनर्लक्ष्मणेति च। हा श्वश्रु मम कौसल्ये हा सुमित्रेति भामिनी।।5.25.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भामिनी beautiful lady, दुःखार्ता afflicted, हा रामेति alas, Rama, पुनः again, हा लक्ष्मणेति alas, Lakshmana, मम my, श्वश्रु motherinlaw, हा कौसल्ये alas, Kausalya, हा सुमित्रेति alas, Sumitra.

#### Explanation

'The bright, beautiful Sita began to wail, alas, "Rama alas, Lakshmana alas, my motherinlaw, Kausalya alas, Sumitra

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

लोकप्रवादः सत्योऽयं पण्डितैः समुदाहृतः। अकाले दुर्लभो मृत्युः स्त्रिया वा पुरुषस्य वा।।5.25.12।। यदहमेवं क्रूराभी राक्षसीभिरिहार्दिता। जीवामि हीना रामेण मुहूर्तमपि दुःखिता।।5.25.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I, एवम् in this way, क्रूराभिः by cruel, राक्षसीभिः by ogresses, अर्दिता tormented, इह here, रामेण from Rama, हीना separated from, दुःखार्ता afflicted, मुहूर्तमपि even a moment, यदा such being the fact, जीवामि I live, स्त्रिया वा by a woman or, पुरुषस्य वा or for a man, अकाले untimely, मृत्युः death, दुर्लभः is difficult, पण्डितैः by the learned, समुदाहृतः quoted, लोकप्रवादः among people, सत्यः truth.

#### Explanation

'There is a famous popular saying among the learned that untimely death is not possible  either for a woman or a man.It is true that I am living in this miserable condition, deprived of Rama's company and tormented by these cruel ogresses here. Living in such conditions even for a moment is impossible. But nobody dies an untimely death.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

लोकप्रवादः सत्योऽयं पण्डितैः समुदाहृतः। अकाले दुर्लभो मृत्युः स्त्रिया वा पुरुषस्य वा।।5.25.12।। यदहमेवं क्रूराभी राक्षसीभिरिहार्दिता। जीवामि हीना रामेण मुहूर्तमपि दुःखिता।।5.25.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I, एवम् in this way, क्रूराभिः by cruel, राक्षसीभिः by ogresses, अर्दिता tormented, इह here, रामेण from Rama, हीना separated from, दुःखार्ता afflicted, मुहूर्तमपि even a moment, यदा such being the fact, जीवामि I live, स्त्रिया वा by a woman or, पुरुषस्य वा or for a man, अकाले untimely, मृत्युः death, दुर्लभः is difficult, पण्डितैः by the learned, समुदाहृतः quoted, लोकप्रवादः among people, सत्यः truth.

#### Explanation

'There is a famous popular saying among the learned that untimely death is not possible  either for a woman or a man.It is true that I am living in this miserable condition, deprived of Rama's company and tormented by these cruel ogresses here. Living in such conditions even for a moment is impossible. But nobody dies an untimely death.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

एषाल्पपुण्या कृपणा विनशिष्याम्यनाथवत्। समुद्रमध्ये नौः पूर्णा वायुवेगैरिवाहता।।5.25.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अल्पपुण्या a lowly women, कृपणा a wretched, एषा here I am, अनाथवत् like an orphan, समुद्रमध्ये in the midst of ocean, वायुवेगैः by the speed of the wind, आहता hit, पूर्णा full, नौः इव like a boat, विनशिष्यामि will be ruined.

#### Explanation

'I am a woman of low merit, a wretched woman, an orphan. I will be ruined like the loaded boat that gets hit in the midst of the ocean by the stormy wind.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

भर्तारं तमपश्यन्ती राक्षसीवशमागता। सीदामि खलु शोकेन कूलं तोयहतं यथा।।5.25.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भर्तारम् husband, तम् him, अपश्यन्ती not finding, राक्षसीवशम् under control of the ogresses, आगता reached, तोयहतम् eroded by water, तीरं यथा like the bank of a river, शोकेन on account of grief, सीदामि खलु I am collapsing indeed.

#### Explanation

'Unable to find my husband and seized by these ogresses, I am collapsing on account of grief like a riverbank under the current of water.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

तं पद्मदलपत्राक्षं सिंहविक्रान्तगामिनम्। धन्याः पश्यन्ति मे नाथं कृतज्ञं प्रियवादिनम्।।5.25.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पद्मदलपत्राक्षं one who has eyes like lotus petals, सिंहविक्रान्तगामिनम् one who walks majestically like a lion, कृतज्ञम् grateful one, प्रियवादिनम् sweet in his talk, तम् him, मे my, नाथम् lord, धन्याः blessed, पश्यन्ति can see.

#### Explanation

'Blessed are those who are in touch with my husband with eyes like lotus petals, whose walk is majestic like a lion's, who has a sense of gratitude and is pleasing in words.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

सर्वथा तेन हीनाया रामेण विदितात्मना। तीक्ष्णं विषमिवास्वाद्य दुर्लभं मम जीवितम्।।5.25.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विदितात्मना endowed with selfknowledge, तेन by him, रामेण by Rama, हीनायाः separated, मम my, तीक्ष्णम् deadly, विषम् poison, आस्वाद्येव like one who has drunk, जीवितम् life, सर्वथा by all means, दुर्लभम् impossible.

#### Explanation

'Separated from Rama endowed with selfknowledge, it is impossible for me to live like one who has drunk venom.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

कीदृशं तु महापापं मया जन्मान्तरे कृतम्। यनेदं प्राप्यते दुःखं मया घोरं सुदारुणम्।।5.25.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

येन by such a manner, मया by me, घोरम् terrific, सुदारुणम् very cruel, इदम् this, दुःखम् sorrow, प्राप्यते experienced, कीदृशम् what type of, महापापम्  great sin, मया by me, जन्मान्तरे in my past life, कृतम् is done.

#### Explanation

'I do not know what great sin I have committed in my past life for which I experience this terrific, cruel affliction?

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

जीवितं त्यक्तुमिच्छामि शोकेन महता वृता। राक्षसीभिश्च रक्षन्त्या रामो नासाद्यते मया।।5.25.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महता with great, शोकेन by sorrow, अवृता filled, जीवितम् life, त्यक्तुम् to give up, इच्छामि I long, रामः Rama, मया by me, नासाद्यते reach, राक्षसीभिः by ogresses, सुरक्षिता I am wellguarded.

#### Explanation

'I long to give up my life, afflicted with great grief, as I, wellguarded by these ogresses, have no hope  to reach Rama.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

धिगस्तु खलु मानुष्यं धिगस्तु परवश्यताम्। न शक्यं यत्परित्यक्तुमात्मच्छन्देन जीवितम्।।5.25.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मानुष्यम् human, धिक् अस्तु what a pitiable situation, परवश्यताम् dependance, धिक् अस्तु fie upon, यत् since, आत्मच्छन्देन although I wish, जीवितम् life, परित्यक्तुम् to give up, न शक्यम् खलु not able.

#### Explanation

'Fie upon this human life. Fie upon this dependence. What a pitiable situation I am placed in. It is not possible for me, a human, to give up life at my free will.' इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये सुन्दरकाण्डे पञ्चविंशस्सर्गः। Thus ends the twentyfifth sarga of Sundarakanda of the holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 26

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

प्रसक्ताश्रुमुखीत्यवं ब्रुवन्ती जनकात्मजा। आधोगतमुखी बाला विलप्तुमुपचक्रमे।।5.26.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

प्रसक्ताश्रुमुखी shedding tears on this account, बाला young lady, जनकात्मजा Janaka's daughter, एवम् in this way, ब्रुवन्ती speaking, अधोगतमुखी with a face bending low, विलप्तुम् to cry, उपचक्रमे started.

#### Explanation

The young daughter of king Janaka thus speaking, began to cry, her face bent down.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

उन्मत्तेव प्रमत्तेव भ्रान्तचित्तेव शोचती। उपावृत्ता किशोरीव विवेष्टन्ती महीतले।।5.26.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

उन्मत्तेव like a bewildered person, प्रमत्तेव like an intoxicated one, भ्रान्तचित्तेव with distracted mind, शोचती worrying, उपावृत्ता wallowing, किशोरीव like an young horse, महीतले on the ground, विवेष्टन्ती coiled round.

#### Explanation

Bewildered or distracted or intoxicated, this young lady wailed, wallowing round on the ground like a young female horse:

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

राघवस्य प्रमत्तस्य रक्षसा कामरूपिणा। रावणेन प्रमथ्याहमानीता क्रोशती बलात्।।5.26.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राघवस्य Rama's, प्रमत्तस्य engaged, कामरूपिणा who can assume any form at his will, रक्षसा by a demon, रावणेन by Ravana, प्रमथ्य tormented, क्रोशती screaming, अहम् I, बलात् forcibly, आनीता I was brought.

#### Explanation

When Rama was engaged (in deer hunt) and I was screaming, I was brought forcibly by Ravana who can assume any form at his will.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

राक्षसीवशमापन्ना भर्त्स्यमाना सुदारुणम्। चिन्तयन्ती सुदुःखार्ता नाहं जीवितुमुत्सुहे।।5.26.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसीवशम् fallen under the clutches of the ogresses, आपन्ना I have been, सुदारुणम् very dreadful, भर्त्स्यमाना  threatened, चिन्तयन्ती worrying, सुदुःखार्ता overcome with terrible grief, अहम् I, जीवितुम् to live, न उत्सहे I have no interest.

#### Explanation

"Having fallen under the clutches of the dreadful demonesses and threatened by them, I am so full of woes and worries that I don't want to live.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

न हि मे जीवितेनार्थो नैवार्थेर्न च भूषणैः। वसन्त्या राक्षसीमध्ये विना रामं महारथम्।।5.26.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महारथम् great charioteer, रामं विना without Rama, राक्षसीमध्ये in the midst of ogresses, वसन्त्याः while living, मे my, जीवितेन with life, अर्थः use, न not, अर्थैः with wealth, न no, भूषणैश्च with ornaments, न no need.

#### Explanation

"In the absence of the great charioteer Rama, of what use is my living in the midst of ogresses? I need no wealth, no ornaments.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

अश्मसारमिदं नूनमथवाप्यजरामरम्। हृदयं मम येनेदं न दुःखेनावशीर्यते।।5.26.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथवा or, मम my, इदम् this, हृदयम् heart, नूनम् surely, अश्मसारम् made of  stone, अजरामरमपि with no age or death, येन since, दुःखेन grieving, नावशीर्यते it does not riven

#### Explanation

"It may be, that my heart is made of stone or it has no age or death since it does not riven even with grief.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

धिङ्मामनार्यामसतीं याहं तेना विना कृता। मुहूर्तमपि रक्षामि जीवितं पापजीविता।।5.26.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पापजीविता a woman of sinful life, या since, अहम् I, तेन विना कृता I am separated from Rama, मुहूर्तमपि even for a moment, जीवितम् life, रक्षामि I am preserving, अनार्याम् a wrteched, असतीम्  infidel, माम् me, धिक् fie upon

#### Explanation

"I am preserving this sinful life like this though separated from Rama. Fie on me, for this wretched, infidel life

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

का च मे जीविते श्रद्धा सुखे वा तं प्रियं विना। भर्तारं सागरान्ताया वसुधायाः प्रियंवदम्।।5.26.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सागरान्तायाः extending upto the ocean, वसुधायाः of the earth, भर्तारम् husband, प्रियंवदम् of pleasing words, तम् him, विना without, मे my, जीविते in life, सुखे वा in pleasure, श्रद्धा interest, का why?

#### Explanation

"How can I have interest in any kind of pleasure without my sweettongued husband, the lord of the whole earth extending up to the ocean.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

भिद्यतां भक्ष्यतां वापि शरीरं विसृजाम्यहम्। न चाप्यहं चिरं दुःखं सहेयं प्रियवर्जिता।।5.26.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I, शरीरम् my body, विसृजामि I will leave, भिद्यताम् you cut me to pieces, भक्ष्यतां वापि or you eat me up, प्रियवर्जिता separated from my dear lord, अहम् I, चिरम् for long, दुःखम् sorrow, न च सहेयम् I cannot bear.

#### Explanation

"I will give up my body. Break me  into pieces or eat me up. I cannot bear the sorrow of separation from my dear Rama any longer.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

चरणेनापि सव्येन न स्पृशेयं निशाचरम्। रावणं किं पुनरहं काममेयं विगर्हितम्।।5.26.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

निशाचरम् nightranger, विगर्हितम् vile, रावणम् Ravana, अहम् I, सव्येन with the left, चरेणेन अपि even with feet, न स्पृशेयम् will not touch, किं what, पुनः again, काममेयं can I love him.

#### Explanation

"I will not even touch that vile nightranger Ravana with my left foot. What to speak of loving him.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

प्रत्याख्यातं न जानाति नात्मानं नात्मनः कुलम्। यो नृशंसस्वभावेन मां प्रार्थयितुमिच्छति।।5.26.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

यः he who, नृशंसस्वभावेन due to cruel nature, माम् me, प्रार्थयितुम् for pleading, इच्छति hopes, आत्मानम् his own, प्रत्याख्यातम् loss of fame, न जानाति is not aware, आत्मनः of himself, कुलम् family, न not.

#### Explanation

'As Ravana has no heart, he pleads to gain me. He is aware of neither his own self nor his family.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

छिन्ना भिन्ना विभक्ता वा दीप्तेवाग्नौ प्रदीपिता। रावणं नोपतिष्ठेयं किं प्रलापेन वश्चिरम्।।5.26.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

छिन्ना वा whether cut, भिन्ना broken into pieces, दीप्ते as if burnt, अग्नौ by fire, प्रदीपिता वा or burnt by others, रावणम् Ravana, नोपतिष्ठेयम् I will not accept, चिरम् for long, विभक्ता: or else share, प्रलापेन by empty words, किम् what is the use.

#### Explanation

"Even if I am cut, broken to pieces or burnt in glowing fire, I will not accept Ravana. Why do you shout for so long?

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

ख्यातः प्राज्ञः कृतज्ञश्च सानुक्रोशश्च राघवः। सद्वृत्तो निरनुक्रोशश्शङ्के मद्भाग्यसङ्क्षयात्।।5.26.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राघवः Rama, ख्यातः renowned, प्राज्ञःwise, कृतज्ञ grateful, सानुक्रोशश्च a kind hearted one, सद्वृत्तः well behaved, मद्भाग्यसङ्क्षयात् due to my misfortune, निरनुक्रोशः turned pitiless, शङ्के I doubt.

#### Explanation

"Rama is renowned, wise, grateful and kindly. It is due to my misfortune that he has turned pitiless to me. There is no doubt.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

राक्षसानां सहस्राणि जनस्थाने चतुर्दश। येनैकेन निरस्तानि स मां किं नाभिपद्यते।।5.26.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एकेन by single one, येन by whom, जनस्थाने at Jansthana, राक्षसानाम् of demons, चतुर्दश fourteen, सहस्राणि thousand, निरस्तानि destroyed, सः he, माम् my, किम् why, नाभिपद्यते not protect me.

#### Explanation

"He who destroyed fourteen thousand demons singlehanded has not come to protect me. I do not know why.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

निरुद्धा रावणेनाहमल्पवीर्येण रक्षसा। समर्थः खलु मे भर्ता रावणं हन्तुमाहवे।।5.26.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I, अल्पवीर्येण of inferior valour, रक्षसा by the demon, रावणेन by Ravana, निरुद्धा I am held captive, मे भर्ता my husband, आहवे in battle, रावणम् Ravana, हन्तुम् killing, समर्थः खलु is surely capable.

#### Explanation

"Rama, my husband, for sure, is capable of killing in battle this Ravana who has held me captive and who is a demon of inferior valour.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

विराधो दण्डकारण्ये येन राक्षसपुङ्गवः। रणे रामेण निहतस्स मां किं नाभिपद्यते।।5.26.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

येन by whom, रामेण by Rama, दण्डकारण्ये in Dandaka forest, रणे in fight, राक्षसपुङ्गवः a bull among demons, विराध: Viradha, निहतः killed, सः that Rama, माम् me, किम् why, नाभिपद्यते not protecting me.

#### Explanation

"Why is Rama who killed Viradha, a bull among demons, in Dandaka forest in the fight not coming to rescue me?

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

कामं मध्ये समुद्रस्य लङ्केयं दुष्प्रधर्षणा। न तु राघवबाणानां गतिरोधो भविष्यति ।।5.26.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

समुद्रस्य ocean's, मध्ये in the middle, इयम् this, लङ्का Lanka, दुष्प्रधर्षणा unassailable, तु but, राघवबाणानाम् by the arrow of Rama, गतिरोध: resist, न भविष्यति will not be.

#### Explanation

"This Lanka in the middle of the ocean is unassailable. But there is nothing which can resist the arrows of Rama.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

किन्नु तत्कारणं येन रामो दृढपराक्रमः। रक्षसापहृतां भार्यामिष्टां नाभ्यवपद्यते।।5.26.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तत् such, कारणम् reason, किं नु what can be, येन by which, दृढपराक्रमः highly powerful, रामः Rama, रक्षसा by the demon, अपहृताम् abducted, इष्टाम् dear, भार्याम् wife, नाभ्यवपद्यते not reach.

#### Explanation

"What may be the reason that my loving husband, Rama who is highly powerful has not reached this place to which I was abducted by the demon.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

इहस्थां मां न जानीते शङ्के लक्ष्मणपूर्वजः। जानन्नपि हि तेजस्वी धर्षणं मर्षयिष्यति।।5.26.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मणपूर्वजः elder brother of Lakshmana, माम् me, इहस्थाम् that I am here, न जानीते he does not know, शङ्के I doubt, जानन्नपि even if he had known, तेजस्वी glorious, धर्षणम् such an outrageous act, मर्षयिष्यति he is tolerating.

#### Explanation

"The elder brother of Lakshamana does not know that I am here. Or, I'm afraid, the glorious Rama knows and yet tolerates such an outrageous act.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

हृतेति योऽधिगत्वा मां राघवाय निवेदयेत्। गृध्रराजोऽपि स रणे रावणेन निपातितः।।5.26.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

यः he who, अधिगत्वा after knowing, हृतेति  is borne away, राघवाय to Raghava, निवेदयेत् inform, सः he, गृध्रराजोऽपि even the king of vultures, रावणेन by Ravana, रणे in fight, निपातितः is thrashed down.

#### Explanation

"Even the king of vultures knows I was borne away. He could have informed Rama. But he has also been thrashed down on the earth by Ravana in the fight.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

कृतं कर्म महत्तेन मां तथाऽभ्यवपद्यता। तिष्ठता रावणद्वन्द्वे वृद्धेनापि जटायुषा।।5.26.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

माम् me, तथा then, अभ्यवपद्यता by advancing,  वृद्धेनापि  even being old, रावणद्वन्द्वे in the duel with Ravana, तिष्ठता stood, तेन जटायुषा by that Jatayu, महत् great, कर्म help, कृतम् did.

#### Explanation

"Though old, Jatayu did a great help to me by resisting Ravana in the duel and advancing (to confront him.).

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

यदि मामिह जानीयाद्वर्तमानां स राघवः। अद्य बाणैरभिक्रुद्धः कुर्याल्लोकमराक्षसम्।।5.26.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः he, राघवः Raghava, माम् me, इह here, वर्तमानाम् being here, जानीयाद्यदि if he knew, अभिक्रुद्धः angry, लोकम् this world, बाणैः with arrows, अद्य now, अराक्षसम् free of demons, कुर्यात् he would have made.

#### Explanation

"Had only Raghava known that I was here he would have freed this world of demons with his anger.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

विधमेच्च पुरीं लङ्कां शोषयेच्च महोदधिम्। रावणस्य च नीचस्य कीर्तिं नाम च नाशयेत्।।5.26.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लङ्कां पुरीम् city of Lanka, विधमेच्च he will blow away, महोदधिम् this great ocean, शोषयेच्च will drain it dry, नीचस्य of the mean fellow, रावणस्य Ravana's, कीर्तिम् fame, नाम च and name, नाशयेत् he would have destroyed.

#### Explanation

"Had Rama known that I am in Lanka he would have blown up this city, dried up the ocean and destroyed the name and fame of the mean Ravana.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

ततो निहतनाथानां राक्षसीनां गृहे गृहे। यथाहमेवं रुदती तथा भूयो न संशयः।।5.26.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, अहम् I, यथा like the way, एवम् in that way, रुदती while crying, तथा similarly, गृहे गृहे in every home, निहतनाथानाम् of those women who lost husbands, राक्षसीनाम् of the ogresses, भूयः again, संशयः no doubt.

#### Explanation

"Like me weeping, the cry of ogresses with lost husbands will be heard in every house in Lanka. There is no doubt.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

अन्विष्य रक्षसां लङ्कां कुर्याद्रामस्सलक्ष्मणः। न हि ताभ्यां रिपुर्दृष्टो मुहूर्तमपि जीवति।।5.26.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सलक्ष्मणः accompanied by Lakshmana, रामः Rama, रक्षसाम् of the demons, लङ्काम् Lanka, अन्विष्य after searching, कुर्यात् will do, ताभ्याम् by both of them, दृष्टः sight, रिपुः enemy, मुहूर्तमपि even for a moment, न जीवति हि will not be alive.

#### Explanation

"If only Lakshmana and Rama in their quest for Lanka reach here, it is  not possible within their sight for the enemy to survive  even a moment.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

चिताधूमाकुलपथा गृध्रमण्डलसङ्कुला। अचिरेण तु लङ्केयं श्मशानसदृशी भवेत्।।5.26.26।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इयम् this, लङ्का Lanka, अचिरेण without delay, चिताधूमकुलपथा with its atreets enveloped with smoke from funeral pyres, गृध्रमण्डलसङ्कुला thronged with flocks of vultures, श्मशानसदृशी like the burial ground, भवेत् will be.

#### Explanation

"Very soon this Lanka will be enveloped with smoke from funeral pyres and thronged with flocks of vultures moving in circles. It will be like a burial ground.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

अचिरेणैव कालेन प्राप्स्याम्येव मनोरथम्। दुष्प्रस्थानोऽयामाख्याति सर्वेषां वो विपर्ययम्।।5.26.27।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अचिरेण कालेनैव in a short time, मनोरथम् my desire, प्राप्स्याम्येव I will gain, अयम् this, दुष्प्रस्थानः behaving badly, सर्वेषाम् of all of you, वः you people, विपर्ययम् a reversal of fate, आख्याति  indicates

#### Explanation

"Your bad behaviour forebodes illluck for you.I shall have my desire fulfilled in a short time.

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

यादृशानीह दृश्यन्ते लङ्कायामशुभानि वै। अचिरेणैव कालेन भविष्यति हतप्रभा।।5.26.28।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इह here, लङ्कायाम् at Lanka, यादृशानि such, अशुभानि inauspicious signs, दृश्यन्ते are seen, अचिरेणैव कालेन in a short while, हतप्रभा lose its splendour, भविष्यति will be.

#### Explanation

"Inauspicious signs are seen here in Lanka. In a short time this city will lose its splendour.

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

नूनं लङ्का हते पापे रावणे राक्षसाधमे। शोषं यास्यति दुर्धर्षा प्रमदा विधवा यथा।।5.26.29।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पापे when the sinner, राक्षसाधमे mean demons, रावणे when Ravana, हते is killed, दुर्धर्षा impregnable, लङ्का Lanka, नूनम् surely, विधवा widow, प्रमदा यथा like a woman, शोषम् wither up, यास्यति truly.

#### Explanation

"Surely this impregnable Lanka will wither up (lose its splendour) and appear like a widow when this sinful Ravana, a mean demon is killed.

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

पुण्योत्सवसमुत्था च नष्टभर्त्री सराक्षसी। भविष्यति पुरी लङ्का नष्टभर्त्री यथाङ्गना।।5.26.30।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नष्टभर्त्री with the death of the king, लङ्कापुरी the city of Lanka, सराक्षसी along with the ogresses, नष्टभर्त्री with the death of her lord, अङ्गना यथा like a women, पुण्योत्सवसमुत्था च with several auspicious celebrations, भविष्यति will remain.

#### Explanation

"This city of Lanka with many auspicious celebrations will lose its king and all mean ogresses will remain like widows.

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

नूनं राक्षसकन्यानां रुदन्तीनां गृहे गृहे। श्रोष्यामि नचिरादेव दुःखार्तानामिह ध्वनिम्।।5.26.31।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नचिरादेव very soon, इह here, गृहे गृहे at every house, दुःखार्तानाम् distressful cry of women, रुदन्तीनाम् crying, राक्षरकन्यानाम् young ogresses, ध्वनिम् sound, नूनम् surely, श्रोष्यामि I will hear.

#### Explanation

"I shall surely hear very soon sounds of distressful cry of young ogresses in every household.

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

सान्थकारा हतद्योता हतराक्षसपुङ्गवा। भविष्यति पुरी लङ्का निर्दग्धा रामसायकैः।।5.26.32।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लङ्कापुरी the state of Lanka, रामसायकैः with Rama's arrows, निर्दग्धा burnt totally, सान्धकारा  filled with darkness, हतद्योता lost splendour, हतराक्षसपुङ्गवा with powerful demons killed, भविष्यति will be

#### Explanation

"Totally burnt by Rama's arrows, this Lanka will be filled with darkness, and its splendour lost and preeminent demons destroyed.

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

यदि नाम स शूरो मां रामो रक्तान्तलोचनः। जानीयाद्वर्तमानां हि रावणस्य निवेशने।।5.26.33।। अनेन तु नृशंसेन रावणेनाधमेन मे। समयो यस्तु निर्दिष्टस्तस्य कालोऽयमागतः।।5.26.34।। स च मे विहितो मृत्युरस्मिन् दुष्टे न वर्तते।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शूरः heroic, रक्तान्तलोचनः with bloodshot eyes, सः रामः that Rama, माम् me, रावणस्य Ravana's, निवेशने at home, वर्तमानाम् present, यदि that, नाम जानीयात् knows I am here.  नृशंसेन by the cruel one, अधमेन by the lowly person, अनेन by this one, रावणेन by Ravana, यः whichever, समयः time limit, मे to me, निर्दिष्टः is fixed, तस्य its, अयम् this, कालः time, आगतः has arrived, मे to me, विहितः fixed, सः that, मृत्युः death, अस्मिन् in him, दुष्टेन by this wicked fellow, वर्तते is fixed.

#### Explanation

"If the heroic Rama of bloodshot eyes comes to know that I am here at Ravana's, the expiry of time limit fixed by this cruel, lowly, foolish Ravana has arrived, this wicked fellow's death is inevitable.

---

### Shloka 34

#### Shloka Text

यदि नाम स शूरो मां रामो रक्तान्तलोचनः। जानीयाद्वर्तमानां हि रावणस्य निवेशने।।5.26.33।। अनेन तु नृशंसेन रावणेनाधमेन मे। समयो यस्तु निर्दिष्टस्तस्य कालोऽयमागतः।।5.26.34।। स च मे विहितो मृत्युरस्मिन् दुष्टे न वर्तते।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शूरः heroic, रक्तान्तलोचनः with bloodshot eyes, सः रामः that Rama, माम् me, रावणस्य Ravana's, निवेशने at home, वर्तमानाम् present, यदि that, नाम जानीयात् knows I am here.  नृशंसेन by the cruel one, अधमेन by the lowly person, अनेन by this one, रावणेन by Ravana, यः whichever, समयः time limit, मे to me, निर्दिष्टः is fixed, तस्य its, अयम् this, कालः time, आगतः has arrived, मे to me, विहितः fixed, सः that, मृत्युः death, अस्मिन् in him, दुष्टेन by this wicked fellow, वर्तते is fixed.

#### Explanation

"If the heroic Rama of bloodshot eyes comes to know that I am here at Ravana's, the expiry of time limit fixed by this cruel, lowly, foolish Ravana has arrived, this wicked fellow's death is inevitable.

---

### Shloka 35

#### Shloka Text

अकार्यं ये न जानन्ति नैर्ऋताः पापकारिणः।।5.26.35।। अधर्मात्तु महोत्पातो भविष्यति हि सांप्रतम्। नैते धर्मं विजानन्ति राक्षसाः पिशिताशनाः।।5.26.36।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पापकारिणः sinners, ये those, नैर्ऋताः demons, अकार्यम् a forbidden act, न जानन्ति they know not अधर्मात्तु due to adharma, सांप्रतम् presently, महोत्पातः calamity, भविष्यति will befall, पिशिताशनाः flesheating, एते these, राक्षसाः demons, धर्मम् dharma,न जानन्ति they are not aware.

#### Explanation

Nothing is forbidden for sinful demons. A calamity will befall now due to this adharma. The flesheating ogres are aware of neither dharma nor adharma.

---

### Shloka 36

#### Shloka Text

अकार्यं ये न जानन्ति नैर्ऋताः पापकारिणः।।5.26.35।। अधर्मात्तु महोत्पातो भविष्यति हि सांप्रतम्। नैते धर्मं विजानन्ति राक्षसाः पिशिताशनाः।।5.26.36।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पापकारिणः sinners, ये those, नैर्ऋताः demons, अकार्यम् a forbidden act, न जानन्ति they know not अधर्मात्तु due to adharma, सांप्रतम् presently, महोत्पातः calamity, भविष्यति will befall, पिशिताशनाः flesheating, एते these, राक्षसाः demons, धर्मम् dharma,न जानन्ति they are not aware.

#### Explanation

Nothing is forbidden for sinful demons. A calamity will befall now due to this adharma. The flesheating ogres are aware of neither dharma nor adharma.

---

### Shloka 37

#### Shloka Text

ध्रुवं मां प्रातराशार्थे राक्षसः कल्पयिष्यति। साहं कथं करिष्यामि तं विना प्रियदर्शनम्।।5.26.37।। रामं रक्तान्तनयनमपश्यन्ती सुदुःखिता।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसः ogre, ध्रुवम् determined, माम् me, प्रातराशार्थे for morning meal, कल्पयिष्यति will make it, सा such, अहम् I, प्रियदर्शनम्  who has pleasing countenance, तं विना without him, कथम् how, करिष्यामि I will  do, रक्तान्तनयनम् one whose eyes have a reddish tinge in the corners, रामम् Rama, अपश्यन्ती without seeing, सुदुःखिता  distressed very much.

#### Explanation

"This demon is determined to make me his morning meal. How can I do anything in the absence of my lord of pleasing countenance. I am distressed very much by not seeing Rama, possessed of eyes with a reddish tinge in the corners.

---

### Shloka 38

#### Shloka Text

यदि कश्चित् प्रदाता मे विषस्याद्य भवेदिह।।5.27.38।। क्षिप्रं वैवस्वतं देवं पश्येयं पतिना विना।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अद्य now, मे to me, विषस्य of poison, प्रदाता donor, कश्चित् some one, इह here, भवेद्यदि can give, पतिना  husband विना devoid, क्षिप्रम् soon, देवम् divine, वैवस्वतम् lord of death, पश्येयम् I will see.

#### Explanation

"If I can find some one who can give me poison now I shall soon see the lord of death without my lord.

---

### Shloka 39

#### Shloka Text

नाजानाज्जीवतीं रामस्स मां लक्ष्मणपूर्वजः।।5.26.39।। जानन्तौ तौ न कुर्यातां नोर्य्वां हि मम मार्गणम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मणपूर्वजः elder brother of Lakshmana, सः रामः that Rama, माम् me, जीवतीम् living, नाजानात् he is not aware तौ both of them, जानन्तौ if they had known, मम my, मार्गणम् search, उर्व्याम् on earth, न कुर्याताम् इति न not undertake.

#### Explanation

"Neither Lakshmana nor his elder brother knows that I am here alive. (Had they known) both of them would have ransacked this earth.

---

### Shloka 40

#### Shloka Text

नूनं ममैव शोकेन स वीरो लक्ष्मणाग्रजः।।5.26.40।। देवलोकमितो यातस्त्यक्त्वा देहं महीतले।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वीरः heroic one, लक्ष्मणाग्रजःLakshmna's elder brother, सः he, मम my, शोकेनैव suffering, महीतले on this earth, देहम् body, त्यक्त्वा  giving up, इतः from here, देवलोकम् to the abode of gods, यातः gone, नूनम् surely.

#### Explanation

"Would it be that the heroic elder brother of Lakshmana has gone to the abode of gods unable to find me on this earth?

---

### Shloka 41

#### Shloka Text

धन्या देवास्सगन्धर्वाः सिद्धाश्च परमर्षयः।।5.26.41।। मम पश्यन्ति ये नाथं रामं राजीवलोचनम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मम my, नाथम् husband, राजीवलोचनम् with eyes like lotus petals, रामम् Rama, ये those, पश्यन्ति will see, देवाः gods, सगन्धर्वाः along with gandharvas, सिद्धाश्च siddhas, परमर्षयः great sages, धन्याः blessed ones.

#### Explanation

"Blessed are the gods, gandharvas, and siddhas and great sages who are able to see my lotuseyed lord Rama.

---

### Shloka 42

#### Shloka Text

अथवा न हि तस्यार्थो धर्मकामस्य धीमतः।।5.26.42।। मया रामस्य राजर्षेर्भार्यया परमात्मनः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथवा or else, धर्मकामस्य one who is ever seeking dharma, धीमतः wise, राजर्षेः royal sage, परमात्मनः the supreme self, तस्य his, रामस्य Rama's, भार्यया by wife, मया by me, अर्थः interest, न हि dispassion.

#### Explanation

Or that royal sage, wise Rama who always seeks dharma having become one with the supreme spirit has no longer any interest in me?

---

### Shloka 43

#### Shloka Text

दृश्यमाने भवेत्प्रीति स्सौहृदं नास्त्यपश्यतः।।5.26.43।। नाशयन्ति कृतघ्नास्तु न रामो नाशयिष्यति।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दृश्यमाने remains before seeing, प्रीतिः love, भवेत् will be, अपश्यतः not see, सौहृदम् friendship, नास्ति does not last, कृतघ्नाः ungrateful one, नाशयन्ति will be destroyed, रामस्तु Rama too, न नाशयिष्यति will not persih.

#### Explanation

"Love attaches itself to one who remains before one's eyes.Friendship fades with separation. The ungrateful destroy love and friendship (as their love is conditioned by frequent meetings). But it cannot happen in case of Rama.

---

### Shloka 44

#### Shloka Text

किं नु मे न गुणाः केचित्किं वा भाग्यक्षयो मम।।5.26.44।।                                                                                                       याहं सीदामि रामेण हीना मुख्येन भामिनी।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भामिनी charming lady, या whoever, अहम् I, मुख्येन foremost, रामेण विना without Rama, सीदामि  sinking, मे for me, केचित्  some, गुणाः qualities, न किं नु are they not found, किं वा or else, मम my, भाग्यक्षयः fortune is diminished.

#### Explanation

"Do I, a charming lady, not have any good fortune because of my past deeds? I wonder how a foremost woman like me seperated from my husband is sinking in grief without Rama. Has my fortune diminished?

---

### Shloka 45

#### Shloka Text

श्रेयो मे जीवितान्मर्तुं विहीनाया महात्मनः।।5.26.45।। रामादक्लिष्टचारित्राच्छूराच्छत्रुनिबर्हणात्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अक्लिष्टचारित्रात् of blemishless character, शूरात् from the brave, शत्रुनिबर्हणात् who can destroy his enemy, महात्मनः great self, रामात् from Rama, विहीनायाः seperated, मे me, जीवितात् with life, मर्तुम् to die, श्रेयः it is better for me.

#### Explanation

Instead of living separated from noble and brave Rama, a blemishless character and a destroyer of enemies, it is better for me to die.

---

### Shloka 46

#### Shloka Text

अथवा न्यस्तशत्रौ तौ वने मूलफलाशिनौ।।5.26.46।। भ्रातरौ हि नरश्रेष्ठौ संवृत्तौ वनगोचरौ।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथवा or else, नरश्रेष्ठौ both foremost among men, तौ both, भ्रातरौ brothers, न्यतशत्रौ laid down their arms, वने in the forest, मूलफलाशनौ eating roots and fruits, वनगोचरौ roaming in the forest as ascetics, संवृत्तौ become.

#### Explanation

"May be both the brothers, foremost among men, have laid down their arms, and taken to asceticism, subsisting on roots and fruits.

---

### Shloka 47

#### Shloka Text

अथवा राक्षसेन्द्रेण रावणेन दुरात्मना।।5.26.47।। छद्मना घातितौ शूरौ भ्रातरौ रामलक्ष्मणौ।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथवा or else, शूरौ two heroes, भ्रातरौ both brothers, रामलक्ष्मणौ Rama and Lakshmana, दुरात्मना by the wicked one, राक्षसेन्द्रेण by the demon king, रावणेन by Ravana, छद्मना by some clever deceptive means, घातितौ are slain.

#### Explanation

"Or else, has this wicked demon king slain both the brothers Rama and Lakshmana by some deceptive means?

---

### Shloka 48

#### Shloka Text

साऽहमेवङ्गते काले मर्तुमिच्छामि सर्वथा।।5.26.48।। न च मे विहितो मृत्युरस्मिन् दुःखेऽपि वर्तते।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एवङ्गते in the circumstances, काले  at this time, सा such, अहम् I, सर्वथा by all means, मर्तुम् to die, इच्छामि I wish, अस्मिन् in this, दुःखेऽपि in sorrwful state, मे to me, मृत्युः death, विहितः ordained, न वर्तते does not exist.

#### Explanation

"By all means I wish to die in these circumstances. However, death is not ordained for me even in this sorrowful state.

---

### Shloka 49

#### Shloka Text

धन्याः खलु महात्मानो मुनयस्त्यक्तकिल्बिषाः।।5.26.49।। जितात्मनो महाभागा येषां न स्तः प्रियाप्रिये।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महात्मानः great men, त्यक्तकिल्बिषाः who have given up all sins, जितात्मानः those who have selfcontrol, महाभागाः exhalted ones, मुनयः sages, धन्याः खलु are surely blessed, येषाम् for whom, प्रियाप्रिये pleasure or displeasure, न स्तः none.

#### Explanation

Blessed are the exhalted sages who have not committed any sin. They have self control and have subdued their senses. There is no pleasure or displeasure for them.

---

### Shloka 50

#### Shloka Text

प्रियान्न संभवेद्दुःखमप्रियादधिकं भयम्।।5.26.50।। ताभ्यां हि ये वियुज्यन्ते नमस्तेषां महात्मनाम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

प्रियात् from a pleasing act, दुःखम् sorrow, अप्रियात् from an unpleasant act, अधिकम् excessive, भयम् fear, न संभवेत् may not result, ये those, ताभ्याम् from both of them, वियुज्यन्ते those who have distanced themselves, तेषाम् for them, महात्मानाम् of the great sages, नमः salutations to them.

#### Explanation

"Salutations to those exalted souls who have distanced themselves from pleasure and displeasure. Pleasures may not lead to suffering or displeasure to excessive, meaningless fear for such great souls.

---

### Shloka 51

#### Shloka Text

साहं त्यक्ता प्रियार्हेण रामेण विदितात्मना।।5.26.51।। प्राणांस्त्यक्ष्यामि पापस्य रावणस्य गता वशम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

प्रियेणैव who deserves pleasant things, विदितात्मना by the knower of the self, रामेण by Rama, त्यक्ता separated, पापस्य sinner's, रावणस्य Ravana's, वशम् clutches, गता reached, सा अहम् that I am, प्राणान् life, त्यक्ष्यामि I will give up.

#### Explanation

"Separated from the wise Rama who deserves pleasant things I will give up life being under the clutches of the sinful Ravana. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये सुन्दरकाण्डे षडिवंश स्सर्गः। Thus ends the twentysixth sarga of Sundarakanda of the holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 27

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

इत्युक्तास्सीतया घोरं राक्षस्यः क्रोधमूर्छिताः। काश्चिज्जग्मुस्तदाख्यातुं रावणस्य तरस्विनः।।5.27.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीतया by Sita, इति thus, उक्ताः spoken, राक्षस्यः rakshasis, घोरम् of terrible, क्रोधमूर्छिताः overwhelmed with anger, काश्चित् some, तत् that, तरस्विनः of the swift, रावणस्य to Ravana, आख्यातुम् to inform, जग्मुः went

#### Explanation

Hearing Sita's words, some terrifying demonesses, overwhelmed with anger, went to inform Ravana, who was quick to act.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

ततः सीतामुपागम्य राक्षस्यो घोरदर्शनाः। पुनः परुषमेकार्थमनर्थार्थमथाब्रुवन्।।5.27.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, घोरदर्शनाः dreadfullooking, राक्षस्यः demonesses, सीताम् Sita, उपागम्य having reached, अथ and, पुनः once again, अनर्थार्थम् a word leading to terrible consequences, एकार्थम् conveying one meaning, परुषम् harshly, अब्रुवन् spoke.

#### Explanation

The dreadfullooking demonesses once again approached Sita and conveyed her categorically in harsh words the terrible consequences (ahead)

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

अद्येदानीं तवानार्ये सीते पापविनिश्चये। राक्षस्यो भक्षयिष्यन्ति मांसमेतद्यथासुखम्।।5.27.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अनार्ये vile one, पापविनिश्चये O woman determined in evil ways, सीते O Sita, अद्य today, इदानीम् these, तव your, एतत मांसम् your flesh, राक्षस्यः rakshasa women, यथासुखम् heart's content, भक्षयिष्यन्ति will eat.

#### Explanation

"O vile one you are determined in your evil ways. Today these shedemons will eat away your flesh to their heart's content ".

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

सीतां ताभिरनार्याभिर्दृष्टवा सन्तर्जितां तदा। राक्षसी त्रिजटा वृद्धा शयाना वाक्यमब्रवीत्।।5.27.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, अनार्याभिः by ignoble women, ताभिः by them, सन्तर्जिताम् threatened, सीताम् Sita, दृष्ट्वा after seeing, शयाना while lying down, वृद्धा old, त्रिजटा Trijata, राक्षसी a demoness, वाक्यम् these words, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

Then seeing the ignoble shedemons threatening Sita, an old demoness called Trijata, who was lying down there, said:

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

आत्मानं खादतानार्या न सीतां भक्षयिष्यथ। जनकस्य सुतामिष्टां स्नुषां दशरथस्य च।।5.27.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अनार्याः O ignoble ones, आत्मानम् yourselves, खादत devour, जनकस्य Janaka's, इष्टाम् favourite, सुताम् daughter, दशरथस्य Dasaratha's, स्नुषां च and daughterinlaw, सीताम् Sita, न भक्षयिष्यथ do not eat.

#### Explanation

"Devour yourselves now, O ignoble ones, if you will. Do not eat Janaka's favourite daughter, the daughterinlaw of Dasaratha.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

स्वप्नो ह्यद्य मया दृष्टो दारुणो रोमहर्षणः। राक्षसानामभावाय भर्तुरस्या भवाय च।।5.27.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अद्य today, दारुणः terrific, रोमहर्षणः horripilating, स्वप्नः dream, राक्षसानाम् all the demons, अभावाय for the destruction, अस्याः her, भर्तुः husband's, भवाय  triumph, मया by myself, दृष्टः was seen.

#### Explanation

"Tonight I had a terrific, horriplating nightmare in which I saw the annihilation of all demons and the triumph of Sita's husband".

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्तास्त्रिजटया राक्षस्यः क्रोधमूर्छिताः। सर्वा एवाब्रुवन्भीतास्त्रिजटां तामिदं वचः।।5.27.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्रिजटया by Trijata, एवम् that way, उक्ताः having been spoken, क्रोधमूर्छिताः overcome by wrath, राक्षस्यः shedemons, सर्वाः एव all of them, भीताः terrified, ताम् her, त्रिजटाम् to Trijata, इदम् this, वचः word, अब्रुवन् spoke.

#### Explanation

Seized with fear, the shedemons, overcome with wrath on hearing Trijata's words said to her.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

कथयस्व त्वया दृष्टः स्वप्नोऽयं कीदृशो निशि। तासां श्रुत्वा तु वचनं राक्षसीनां मुखाच्युतम्।।5.27.8।। उवच वचनं काले त्रिजटा स्वप्नसंश्रितम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

निशि night, त्वया by you, दृष्टः seen, अयम् this, स्वप्नः dream, कीदृशः what did you see, कथयस्व tell, तासाम् of them, राक्षसीनाम् of shedemons, मुखात् from their mouth, च्युतम् slipped, वचनम् word, श्रुत्वा after hearing, त्रिजटा Trijata, काले early morning time, स्वप्नसंश्रितम् all about the dream, वचनम् words, उवाच spoke.

#### Explanation

"O Trijata, tell us the kind of dream you dreamt last night", they insisted. Hearing the words from the mouth of the shedemons, Trijata told them all about her dream.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

गजदन्तमयीं दिव्यां शिबिकामन्तरिक्षगाम्।।5.27.9।। युक्तां हंससहस्रेण स्वयमास्थाय राघवः। शुक्लमाल्याम्बरधरो लक्ष्मणेन सहागतः।।5.27.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राघवः Rama, शुक्लमाल्याम्बरधरः wearing a garland of white flowers and clad in white attire, गजदन्तमयीम् made of ivory, अन्तरिक्षगाम् coursing through the sky, हंससहस्रेण by a thousand swans, युक्ताम् yoked, शिबिकाम् palanquin, स्वयम् himself, आस्थाय having ascended, लक्ष्मणेन सह accompanied by Lakshmana, आगतः arrived.

#### Explanation

"Rama, in white attire, wearing a garland of white flowers, ascending a palanquin made of ivory drawn by a thousand swans and coursing through the sky with Lakshmana arrived.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

गजदन्तमयीं दिव्यां शिबिकामन्तरिक्षगाम्।।5.27.9।। युक्तां हंससहस्रेण स्वयमास्थाय राघवः। शुक्लमाल्याम्बरधरो लक्ष्मणेन सहागतः।।5.27.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राघवः Rama, शुक्लमाल्याम्बरधरः wearing a garland of white flowers and clad in white attire, गजदन्तमयीम् made of ivory, अन्तरिक्षगाम् coursing through the sky, हंससहस्रेण by a thousand swans, युक्ताम् yoked, शिबिकाम् palanquin, स्वयम् himself, आस्थाय having ascended, लक्ष्मणेन सह accompanied by Lakshmana, आगतः arrived.

#### Explanation

"Rama, in white attire, wearing a garland of white flowers, ascending a palanquin made of ivory drawn by a thousand swans and coursing through the sky with Lakshmana arrived.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

स्वप्ने चाद्य मया दृष्टा सीता शुक्लाम्बरावृता। सागरेण परिक्षिप्तं श्वेतं पर्वतमास्थिता।।5.27.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीता च Sita also, शुक्लाम्बरावृता wearing white robes, सागरेण by the ocean, परिक्षिप्तम् surrounded by, श्वेतम् white, पर्वतम् mountain, आस्थिता perched, मया by me, अद्य now, स्वप्ने in the dream, दृष्टा was seen.

#### Explanation

"I also saw Sita in my dream wearing white robes and perched on a white mountain surrounded by the ocean.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

रामेण सङ्गता सीता भास्करेण प्रभा यथा। राघवश्च मया दृष्टश्चतुर्दष्ट्रं महागजम्।।5.27.12।। आरूढ श्शैलसङ्काशं चचार सहलक्ष्मणः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीता Sita, रामेण with Rama, प्रभा  light, भास्करेण with the Sun, यथा like, सङ्गता united, रामश्च Rama also, चतुर्द्रंष्ट्रम् having four tusks, शैलसङ्काशम् resembling a mountain, महगजम् huge elephant, आरूढः ascended, मया by me, दृष्टः seen, सहलक्ष्मणः along with Lakshmana, चचार went.

#### Explanation

"Just as light is united with the Sun, I saw Sita united with Rama. I saw Rama with Lakshmana riding a huge, mountainlike elephant having four tusks.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

ततस्तौ नरशार्दूलौ दीप्यमानौ स्वतेजसा।।5.27.13।। शुक्लमाल्याम्बरधरौ जानकीं पर्युपस्थितौ।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, स्वतेजसा by their selfeffulgence, दीप्यमानौ glowing, शुक्लमाल्याम्बरधरौ wearing white garlands and clothes, तौ both, नरशार्दूलौ tigers among men, जानकीम् Janaki, पर्युपस्थितौ came near.

#### Explanation

"Dressed in white garlands and clothes, the two brothers, tigers among men, glowing in their effulgence approached Janaki.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

ततस्तस्य नगस्याग्रे ह्याकाशस्थस्य दन्तिनः।।5.27.14।। भर्त्रा परिगृहीतस्य जानकी स्कन्धमाश्रिता।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, जानकी Janaki, तस्य of that, नगस्य mountain's, अग्रे front, भर्त्रा by husband, परिगृहीतस्य held, आकाशस्थस्य waiting in the sky, दन्तिनः of the elephant, स्कन्धम् mounted, आश्रिता took seat.

#### Explanation

"Then from the front of the mountain Janaki shifted to the back of the elephant held by her husband waiting in the sky.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

भर्तुरङ्कात्समुत्पत्य ततः कमललोचना।।5.27.15।। चन्द्रसूर्यौ मया दृष्टा पाणिना परिमार्जती।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, कमललोचना lotus eyed Sita, भर्तुः husband's, अङ्कात् from his lap, समुत्पत्य springing up, पाणिना with palms, चन्द्रसूर्यौ Moon and Sun, परिमार्जती as she was caressing, मया me, दृष्टा was seen.

#### Explanation

"Then I saw the lotuseyed Sita springing up from the lap of her husband and playfully caressing the Moon and Sun with her palms.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

ततस्ताभ्यां कुमाराभ्यामास्थित: स गजोत्तमः।।5.27.16।। सीतया च विशालाक्ष्या लङ्काया उपरिस्थितः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, ताभ्याम् by both of them, कुमाराभ्याम् by both the princes, विशालाक्ष्या largeeyed, सीतया च by Sita, आस्थितः held, सः he, गजोत्तमः best of elephants, लङ्कायाः Lanka's, उपरि over, स्थितः stood.

#### Explanation

"Then the excellent elephant was seen halted in the city of Lanka with both princes and the largeeyed Sita mounted on the elephant.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

पाण्डुरर्षभयुक्तेन रथेनाष्टयुजा स्वयम्।।5.27.17।। इहोपयातः काकुत्स्थ स्सीतया सह भार्यया।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

काकुत्स्थ: Rama, भार्यया accompanied by wife, सीतया सह along with Sita, पाण्डुरर्षभयुक्तेन yoked by white oxen, अष्टयुजा eight of them, रथेन by a chariot, स्वयम् himself, इह here, उपयातः came.

#### Explanation

"Rama accompanied by his wife Sita came on a chariot yoked to eight white oxen.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

लक्ष्मणेन सह भ्रात्रा सीतया सह वीर्यवान्।।5.27.18।। आरुह्य पुष्पकं दिव्यं विमानं सूर्यसन्निभम्। उत्तरां दिशमालोक्य जगाम पुरुषोत्तमः।।5.27.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वीर्यवान् valiant, पुरुषोत्तमः foremost of men, भ्रात्रा with his brother, लक्ष्मणेन with Lakshmana, सीतया सह accompanied by Sita, दिव्यम् wonderful, सूर्यसन्निभम् resembling the Sun, पुष्पकं विमानम् Pushpaka chariot, आरुह्य after ascending, उत्तराम् north, दिशम् direction, आलोक्य seeing, जगाम went.

#### Explanation

"Valiant Rama, foremost among men, accompanied by his brother Lakshmana and wife Sita went towards the northerly direction in the wonderful Pushpaka chariot that resembled the Sun.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

लक्ष्मणेन सह भ्रात्रा सीतया सह वीर्यवान्।।5.27.18।। आरुह्य पुष्पकं दिव्यं विमानं सूर्यसन्निभम्। उत्तरां दिशमालोक्य जगाम पुरुषोत्तमः।।5.27.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वीर्यवान् valiant, पुरुषोत्तमः foremost of men, भ्रात्रा with his brother, लक्ष्मणेन with Lakshmana, सीतया सह accompanied by Sita, दिव्यम् wonderful, सूर्यसन्निभम् resembling the Sun, पुष्पकं विमानम् Pushpaka chariot, आरुह्य after ascending, उत्तराम् north, दिशम् direction, आलोक्य seeing, जगाम went.

#### Explanation

"Valiant Rama, foremost among men, accompanied by his brother Lakshmana and wife Sita went towards the northerly direction in the wonderful Pushpaka chariot that resembled the Sun.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

एवं स्वप्ने मया दृष्टो रामो विष्णुपराक्रमः। लक्ष्मणेन सह भ्रात्रा सीतया सह राघवः।।5.27.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विष्णुपराक्रमः valiant as Visnu, राघवः Raghava, रामः Rama, भ्रात्रा with his brother, लक्ष्मणेन also with Lakshmana, सीतया सह accompanied by Sita, स्वप्ने in dream, एवम् in that way, मया by myself, दृष्टः was seen.

#### Explanation

"In this way I saw in my dream Rama as valiant as Lord Visnu accompanied by his brother Lakshmana and his wife Sita.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text
