न हि रामो महातेजाश्शक्यो जेतुं सुरासुरैः। राक्षसैर्वापि चान्यैर्वा स्वर्गः पापजनैरिव।।5.27.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महातेजाः very brilliant, रामः Rama, सुरासुरैः by gods and demons, राक्षसैर्वापि and even by demons, अन्यैर्वा and others also, स्वर्गः heaven, पापजनैरिव like sinners, जेतुम् to win, न शक्यः not possible.

#### Explanation

"Just as a sinner cannot attain heaven Rama cannot be won by gods or demons, even rakshasas and others.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

रावणश्च मया दृष्टः क्षितौ तैलसमुक्षितः। रक्तवासाः पिबन्मत्तः करवीरकृतस्रजः।।5.27.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रावणश्च Ravana's, क्षितौ on the ground, तैलसमुक्षितः smeared with oil, रक्तवासाः clad in red clothes, करवीरकृतस्रजः wearing a garland of lilies, पिबन् drinking, मत्तः intoxicated, मया I, दृष्टः was seen.

#### Explanation

"I saw the body of Ravana lying on the ground smeared with oil, clad in red, wearing a garland of lilies and intoxicated with drinking.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

विमानात्पुष्पकादद्य रावणः पतितो भुवि। कृष्यमाणः स्त्रिया दृष्टो मुण्डः कृष्णाम्बरः पुनः।।5.27.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अद्य today, पुष्पकात् विमानात् from the Pushpaka chariot, भुवि on earth, पतितः fallen down, मुण्डः with shaven head, कृष्णाम्बरः clad in black clothes, रावणः Ravana, स्त्रिया by  a woman, कृष्यमाणः being dragged, पुन: again, दृष्टः saw.

#### Explanation

"Tonight I saw (in another dream) Ravana with shaven head, clad in black clothes, fallen down on the ground from the Pushpaka chariot, being dragged by a woman.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

रथेन खरयुक्तेन रक्तमाल्यानुलेपनः। पिपंस्तैलं हसन्नृत्यन् भ्रान्तचित्ताकुलेन्द्रियः।।5.27.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रक्तमाल्यानुलेपनः wearing red garlands and unguents, तैलम् oil, पिबन् drinking, हसन् laughing, नृत्यन् dancing, भ्रान्तचित्ताकुलेन्द्रियः with mind and snses perplexed, खरयुक्तेन yoked to donkeys, रथेन by the chariot.

#### Explanation

"He was wearing red garlands and unguents, his body smeared with oil. He was drinking, laughing and dancing, his mind and senses perplexed. He was driving on a chariot yoked to donkeys.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

गर्दभेन ययौ शीघ्रं दक्षिणां दिशमास्थितः। पुनरेव मया दृष्टो रावणो राक्षसेश्वरः।।5.27.25।। पतितोऽ वाक्चिरा रा भूमौ गर्दभाद्भयमोहितः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दक्षिणां दिशम् towards southerly direction, आस्थितः started, गर्दभेन after donkey, शीघ्रम् swiftly, ययौ went, राक्षसेश्वरः demon king, रावणः Ravana, भयमोहितः deluded by fear, गर्दभात् of  the donkey, अवाक्चिरा: head bent down, पतितः fallen, मया by me, पुनरेव also, दृष्टः saw.

#### Explanation

"I saw the demon king Ravana running behind the chariot driven by donkeys in the southerly direction. He was falling with his head bent down and deluded by the fear of the donkey.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

सहसोत्थाय संभ्रान्तो भयार्तो मदविह्वलः।।5.27.26।। उन्मत्त इव दिग्वासा दुर्वाक्यं प्रलपन्बहु। दुर्गन्धं दुस्सहं घोरं तिमिरं नरकोपमम्।।5.27.27।। मलपङ्कं प्रविश्याशु मग्नस्तत्र स रावणः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

स रावणः that Ravana, सहसा quickly, उत्थाय got up, संभ्रान्तः bewildered, भयार्तः terrorstricken, मदविह्वलः intoxicated with wine, दिग्वासाः naked (with sky as garment), उन्मत्त: mad, इव like, बहु many, दुर्वाक्यम् bad words, प्रलपन् uttering, दुर्गन्धम् emitting foul smell, दुस्सहम् words of abuse, घोरम् terrific, तिमिरम् dark,नरकोपमम् helllike, मलपङ्कम् pool of filth, प्रविश्य having disappeared, आशु he was, तत्र there, मग्नः drowned.

#### Explanation

"I saw Ravana, getting up quickly, in a bewildered state, terrorstricken, totally confused in intoxication. He had gone almost insane, naked uttering words of abuse. Plunged into a pool of filth and emitting foul smell he disappeared into a helllike dark chamber.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

सहसोत्थाय संभ्रान्तो भयार्तो मदविह्वलः।।5.27.26।। उन्मत्त इव दिग्वासा दुर्वाक्यं प्रलपन्बहु। दुर्गन्धं दुस्सहं घोरं तिमिरं नरकोपमम्।।5.27.27।। मलपङ्कं प्रविश्याशु मग्नस्तत्र स रावणः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

स रावणः that Ravana, सहसा quickly, उत्थाय got up, संभ्रान्तः bewildered, भयार्तः terrorstricken, मदविह्वलः intoxicated with wine, दिग्वासाः naked (with sky as garment), उन्मत्त: mad, इव like, बहु many, दुर्वाक्यम् bad words, प्रलपन् uttering, दुर्गन्धम् emitting foul smell, दुस्सहम् words of abuse, घोरम् terrific, तिमिरम् dark,नरकोपमम् helllike, मलपङ्कम् pool of filth, प्रविश्य having disappeared, आशु he was, तत्र there, मग्नः drowned.

#### Explanation

"I saw Ravana, getting up quickly, in a bewildered state, terrorstricken, totally confused in intoxication. He had gone almost insane, naked uttering words of abuse. Plunged into a pool of filth and emitting foul smell he disappeared into a helllike dark chamber.

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

कण्ठे बद्ध्वा दशग्रीवं प्रमदा रक्तवासिनी।।5.27.28।। काली कर्दमलिप्ताङ्गी दिशं याम्यां प्रकर्षति।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रक्तवासिनी a woman clad in red clothes, काली a dark one, कर्दमलिप्ताङ्गी a woman smeared with wet mud, प्रमदा woman, दशग्रीवम् tennecked Ravana, कण्ठे neck, बद्ध्वा having bound, याम्यां दिशम् towards the abode of Yama in the southerly direction, प्रकर्षति was dragging.

#### Explanation

A dark woman clad in red, body smeared with wet mud was seen dragging the ten-necked Ravana by his neck towards the abode of Yama in the southerly direction.

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

एवं तत्र मया दृष्टः कुम्भकर्णो निशाचरः।।5.27.29।। रावणस्य सुतास्सर्वे दृष्टास्तैलसमुक्षिताः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तत्र there, निशाचरः nightranger, कुम्भकर्णः Kumbhakarna, मया by me, एवम् in this way, दृष्टः was seen, रावणस्य Ravana's, सुताः sons, सर्वे all, तैलसमुक्षिताः body smeared with oil, दृष्टाः was seen.

#### Explanation

"There I saw the nightranger, Kumbhakarna and also Ravana's sons, their bodies smeared with oil.

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

वराहेण दशग्रीवश्शिंशुमारेण चेन्द्रजित्।।5.27.30।। उष्ट्रेण कुम्भकर्णश्च प्रयाता दक्षिणां दिशम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दशग्रीवः tennecked one, वराहेण by boar, इन्द्रजित् Indrajit, श्शिंशुमारेण च crocodile, कुम्भकर्ण Kumbhakarna, उष्ट्रेण च on a camel, दक्षिणां दिशम् towards the southern direction, प्रयाताः were travelling.

#### Explanation

"The tennecked Ravana was seen (this time) riding on a boar, Indrajit on a crocodile and Kumbhakarna on a camel all set towards the southerly direction.

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

एकस्तत्र मया दृष्टः श्वेतच्छत्रो विभीषणः।।5.27.31।। शुक्लमाल्याम्बरधरः शुक्लगन्धानुलेपनः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तत्र there, एकः only one, विभीषणः Vibhishana, श्वेतच्छत्रः with white umbrella, शुक्लमाल्याम्बरधरः wearing white garlands and clothes, शुक्लगन्धानुलेपनः with white sandal paste, मया by me, दृष्टः was seen.

#### Explanation

"Only Vibhishana was under a white umbrella clad in white, with white garlands and white sandal paste.

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

शङ्खदुन्धुभिनिर्घोषैर्नृत्तगीतैरलङ्कृतः।।5.27.32।। आरुह्य शैलसङ्काशं मेघस्तनितनिस्स्वनम्। चतुर्दन्तं गजं दिव्यमास्ते तत्र विभीषणः।।5.27.33।। चतुर्भिस्सचिवैः सार्थं वैहायसमुपस्थितः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विभीषणः Vibhishana, शङ्खदुन्दुभिनिर्घोषैः with sounds of conches and drums, नृत्तगीतैः by  dancers and musicians, अलङ्कृतः decorated, शैलसङ्काशम् like the mountain, मेघस्तनितनिस्स्वनम् trumpetting like a thundering cloud, चतुर्दन्तम् of four tusks, दिव्यम् wonderful, गजम् elephant, आरुह्य on ascending, तत्र there, आस्ते stood there, चतुर्भिः four, सचिवैः सार्थम् with ministers, वैहायसम् in the air, उपस्थितः reached.

#### Explanation

"(Hailed by) the sounds of conches and drums, and of musicians and dancers, Vibhishana was seen ascending on to a decorated fourtust elephant which was huge like a mountain and trumpting like a thundering cloud. He stood there along with four ministers and reached the aerial region.

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

शङ्खदुन्धुभिनिर्घोषैर्नृत्तगीतैरलङ्कृतः।।5.27.32।। आरुह्य शैलसङ्काशं मेघस्तनितनिस्स्वनम्। चतुर्दन्तं गजं दिव्यमास्ते तत्र विभीषणः।।5.27.33।। चतुर्भिस्सचिवैः सार्थं वैहायसमुपस्थितः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विभीषणः Vibhishana, शङ्खदुन्दुभिनिर्घोषैः with sounds of conches and drums, नृत्तगीतैः by  dancers and musicians, अलङ्कृतः decorated, शैलसङ्काशम् like the mountain, मेघस्तनितनिस्स्वनम् trumpetting like a thundering cloud, चतुर्दन्तम् of four tusks, दिव्यम् wonderful, गजम् elephant, आरुह्य on ascending, तत्र there, आस्ते stood there, चतुर्भिः four, सचिवैः सार्थम् with ministers, वैहायसम् in the air, उपस्थितः reached.

#### Explanation

"(Hailed by) the sounds of conches and drums, and of musicians and dancers, Vibhishana was seen ascending on to a decorated fourtust elephant which was huge like a mountain and trumpting like a thundering cloud. He stood there along with four ministers and reached the aerial region.

---

### Shloka 34

#### Shloka Text

समाजश्च मया दृष्टो गीतवादित्रनिःस्वनः।।5.27.34।। पिबतां रक्तमाल्यानां रक्षसां रक्तवाससाम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पिबताम् of those drinking, रक्तमाल्यानाम् of those putting on flower garlands, रक्तवाससाम् of those wearing red clothes, रक्षसाम् of rakshasas, गीतवादित्रनिःस्वनः sound of singing and playing musical instruments, समाजश्च a group, मया by me, दृष्टः was seen.

#### Explanation

" A large group of demons was seen collected together, drinking, wearing red garlands and red clothes, singing and playing musical instruments.

---

### Shloka 35

#### Shloka Text

लङ्का चेयं पुरी रम्या सवाजिरथकुञ्जराः।।5.27.35।। सागरे पतिता दृष्टा भग्नगोपुरतोरणा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रम्या delightful, इयम् this, लङ्का पुरी च city of Lanka, सवाजिरथकुञ्जरा with horses chariots and elephants, भग्नगोपुरतोरणा crumbling archways and towers, सागरे in the ocean, पतिता fallen, दृष्टा seen.

#### Explanation

"I saw this delightful Lanka teeming with horses, chariots and elephants, archways and towers crumbling and falling into the ocean.

---

### Shloka 36

#### Shloka Text

लङ्का दृष्टा मया स्वप्ने रावणेनाभिरक्षिता।।5.27.36।। दग्धा रामस्य दूतेन वानरेण तरस्विना।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रावणेन by Ravana, अभिरक्षिता protected, लङ्का Lanka, रामस्य Rama's, दूतेन by a messenger, तरस्विना by powerful, वानरेण by a vanara, दग्धा burnt, मया by me, स्वप्ने in the dream, दृष्टा seen.

#### Explanation

In my dream I saw this city of Lanka protected by Ravana burnt down by a powerful vanara , a messenger of Rama.

---

### Shloka 37

#### Shloka Text

पीत्वा तैलं प्रनृत्ताश्च प्रहसन्त्यो महास्वनाः।।5.27.37।। लङ्कायां भस्मरूक्षायां सर्वा राक्षसस्त्रियः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भस्मरूक्षायाम् in a dry place turned into ashes, लङ्कायाम् in this Lanka, सर्वाः all, राक्षसस्त्रियः demonesses, तैलम् oil, पीत्वा after drinking, प्रहसन्त्यः laughing away, महास्वनाः shouting in a loud voice, प्रनृत्ताः च danced.

#### Explanation

"All the demonesses in the city of Lanka which had turned dry, reduced to ashes, were dancing, laughing and shouting loudly drunk with oil..

---

### Shloka 38

#### Shloka Text

कुम्भकर्णादयश्चेमे सर्वे राक्षसपुङ्गवाः।।5.27.38।। रक्तं निवसनं गृह्य प्रविष्टा गोमयह्रदे।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कुम्भकर्णादयः Kumbhakarna and others, इमे these, सर्वे राक्षसपुङ्गवाः all demon leaders, रक्तम् red coloured, निवसनम् clothes, गृह्य wearing, गोमयह्रदे in a pool of cowdung, प्रविष्टाः entered.

#### Explanation

"Kumbhakarna and other demon leaders wearing red clothes entered into a pool of cow dung.

---

### Shloka 39

#### Shloka Text

अपगच्छत नश्यध्वं सीतामाप्नोति राघवः।।5.27.39।। घातयेत्परमामर्षी युष्मान्सार्थं हि राक्षसैः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अपगच्छत go away from here, नश्यध्वम् you will be destroyed, राघवः Raghava, सीताम् Sita, आप्नोति he will secure, परमामर्षी a very angry man, राक्षसैः सार्थम् along with the demons totally, युष्मान् you, घातयेत् will kill.

#### Explanation

"(Therefore) go away from here. You will be totally destroyed by Rama who will secure his wife and will kill  all of you along with others in great anger.

---

### Shloka 40

#### Shloka Text

प्रियां बहुमतां भार्यां वनवासमनुव्रताम्।।5.27.40।। भर्त्सितां तर्जितां वापि नानुमंस्यति राघवः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राघवः Rama, प्रियाम् his beloved, बहुमताम् highly respected one, वनवासम् exile in the forest, अनुव्रताम् followed, भार्याम् wife, भर्त्सिताम् abused, तर्जितां वापि or threatened, नानुमंस्यति he will not approve.

#### Explanation

"Rama will not approve of any one abusing or threatening his venerable, beloved wife, who had led a life of exile with him.

---

### Shloka 41

#### Shloka Text

तदलं क्रूरवाक्यैश्च सान्त्वमेवाभिधीयताम्।।5.27.41।। अभियाचाम वैदेहीमेतद्धि मम रोचते।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तत् then, क्रूरवाक्यैः cruel taunts, अलम् enough, सान्त्वमेव peacefully, अभिधीयताम् it may be said, वैदेहीम् Vaidehi, अभियाचाम we will beg, एतद्धि this itself, मे to me, रोचते is liked.

#### Explanation

"Give up these cruel taunts. Speak to her in a cordial manner. We will beg Vaidehi's forgiveness. I like this kind of action.

---

### Shloka 42

#### Shloka Text

यस्यामेवंविधः स्वप्नो दुःखितायां प्रदृश्यते।।5.27.42।। सा दुःखैर्विविधैर्मुक्ता प्रियं प्राप्नोत्यनुत्तमम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

यस्याम् when any woman, दुःखितायाम् when she is sorrowful, एवंविधः this kind of, स्वप्नः dream, प्रदृश्यते is seen, सा she, विविधैः with all kinds of, दुःखैः sorrows, मुक्ता she is relieved, अनुत्तमम् supreme, प्रियम् joy, प्राप्नोति will attain.

#### Explanation

"When any demoness in a sorrowful state dreams this kind of dream, she will be relieved of sorrow and will obtain supreme joy.

---

### Shloka 43

#### Shloka Text

भर्त्सितामपि याचध्वं राक्षस्यः किं विवक्षया।।5.27.43।। राघवाद्धि भयं घोरं राक्षसानामुपस्थितम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षस्यः O demonesses, भर्त्सितामपि even if she is threatened this way, याचध्वम् beg her, किम् विवक्षया where is the need to say something, राक्षसानाम् of demons, राघवात् from Rama, घोरम् terrific, भयम् fear, उपस्थितम् will come.

#### Explanation

"O demonesses beg her (pardon) now even though you had threatened her earlier. Where is the need to hesitate? There is cause for concern for demons from Rama.

---

### Shloka 44

#### Shloka Text

प्रणिपातप्रसन्ना हि मैथिली जनकात्मजा।।5.27.44।। अलमेषा परित्रातुं राक्षस्यो महतो भयात्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षस्यः O shedemons, जनकात्मजा Janaka's daughter, एषा this, मैथिली Mythili, प्रणिपातप्रसन्ना while pleased with your salutations, महतः great, भयात् fear, परित्रातुम् to save, अलम् able.

#### Explanation

"O shedemons, this Janaka's daughter from Mithila who is capable of protecting you against great dangers will be pleased by your submission.

---

### Shloka 45

#### Shloka Text

अपि चास्या विशालाक्ष्या न किंचिदुपलक्षये।।5.27.45।। विरूपमपि चाङ्गेषु सुसूक्ष्ममपि लक्षणम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अपि च and not only that, विशालाक्ष्याः of the largeeyed one, अस्याः her, अङ्गेषु in her limbs, सुसूक्ष्ममपि even a little, विरूपम् distortioninauspiciousness, लक्षणम् sign, किञ्चिदपि even a little, न उपलक्षये I do not see.

#### Explanation

"Look at the signs on her body. There is not a little inauspiciousness in the limbs of the largeeyed princess from Mithila.

---

### Shloka 46

#### Shloka Text

छायावैगुण्यमात्रं तु शङ्के दुःखमुपस्थितम्।।5.27.46।। अदुःखार्हामिमां देवीं वैहायसमुपस्थिताम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

छायावैगुण्यमात्रं तु only a change in her complexion, वैहायसम् is changed, उपस्थिताम् which she has, अदुःखार्हाम् not deserve to suffer, इमाम् this, देवीम् this queen, दुःखम् grief, उपस्थितम् has befallen, शङ्के I think.

#### Explanation

A great suffering has befallen a lady who does not deserve to suffer. Therefore, a change in her complexion has occured.

---

### Shloka 47

#### Shloka Text

अर्थसिद्धिं तु वैदेह्याः पश्याम्यहमुपस्थिताम्।।5.27.47।। राक्षसेन्द्रविनाशं च विजयं राघवस्य च।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I, वैदेह्याः for Vaidehi, अर्थसिद्धिं तु fulfilment, उपस्थिताम् approaching, पश्यामि I can see, राक्षसेन्द्रविनाशं च the destruction of the demon king, राघवस्य for Raghava, विजयं च success.

#### Explanation

"I see the day of fulfilment for Vaidehi has drawn near. I can also foresee the destruction of the demon king and the triumph of Rama.

---

### Shloka 48

#### Shloka Text

निमित्तभूतमेतत्तु श्रोतुमस्या महत्प्रियम्।।5.27.48।। दृश्यते च स्फुरच्चक्षुः पद्मपत्रमिवायतम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अस्याः her, महत् great, प्रियम् good news, श्रोतुम् to hear, निमित्तभूतम् has served as an augery, स्पुरत् throbbing, एतत् this, पद्मपत्रमिव like lotus petals, आयतम् large, चक्षुः eyes, दृश्यते is seen.

#### Explanation

"This dream indeed served as an augery for good news about her. Look, her (left) eye which is large like a lotus petal is throbbing.

---

### Shloka 49

#### Shloka Text

ईषच्छ हृषितो वास्या दक्षिणाया ह्यदक्षिणः।।5.27.49।। अकस्मादेव वैदेह्या बाहुरेकः प्रकम्पते।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दक्षिणायाः of a capable lady, अस्याः her, वैदेह्याः Vaidehi's, अदक्षिणः that left, एकः बाहुः that shoulder alone, अकस्मादेव suddenly, हृषितः it may be feeling, ईषत् a little bit, प्रकम्पते is throbbing.

#### Explanation

"The left shoulder of this great Vaidehi has started suddenly throbbing, indicating that she is feeling a bit happy.

---

### Shloka 50

#### Shloka Text

करेणुहस्तप्रतिम स्सव्यश्चोरुरनुत्तमः।।5.27.50।। वेपमानः सूचयति राघवं पुरतः स्थितम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

करेणुहस्तप्रतिमः comparable to a female elephant's trunk, अनुत्तमः excellent, सव्यः the left one, ऊरुः thigh, वेपमानः while trembling, राघवम् Ragahva, पुरतः city, स्थितम् standing, सूचयति as though indicating.

#### Explanation

"Her excellent left thigh, comparable to a female elephant's trunk is trembling, as though indicating that Rama is standing in her presence.

---

### Shloka 51

#### Shloka Text

पक्षी च शाखानिलयं प्रविष्टः पुनः पुनश्चोत्तमसान्त्ववादी। सुस्वागतां वाचमुदीरयानः पुनः पुनश्चोदयतीव हृष्टः।।5.27.51।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पक्षी च a bird, शाखानिलयम् on the branches, प्रविष्टः entered, पुनः once again, पुनश्च again and again, उत्तमसान्त्वादी the bird which utters excellent sweet notes, सुस्वागतम् वाचम् a happy tiding, उदीरयानः while expressing, हृष्टः joyous, पुनः पुनः again and again, चोदयतीव is as though prompting.

#### Explanation

"One can see the bird which utters excellent sweet notes indicating happy tidings, entering again and again the foliage of the tree, a delightful bird, as if prompting Sita again and again to rejoice. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये सुन्दरकाण्डे सप्तविंशस्सर्गः। Thus ends the twentyseventh sarga of Sundarakanda of the holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 28

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

सा राक्षसेन्द्रस्य वचो निशम्य तद्रावणस्याप्रियमप्रियार्ता। सीता वितत्रास यथा वनान्ते सिंहाभिपन्ना गजराजकन्या।।5.28.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सा सीता that Sita, राक्षसेन्द्रस्य demon king's, रावणस्य Ravana's, अप्रियम् unpleasant news, तत् that, वचः word, निशम्य after hearing, अप्रियार्ता restless, वनान्ते in the midst of the forest, सिंहाभिपन्ना caught by the lion, गजराजकन्या यथा like young calf of a lordly elephant, वितत्रास frightened.

#### Explanation

After hearing the unpleasant words spoken by the king of demons, which caused restlessness and sorrow to Sita, she was terrified like the young calf of a lordly elephant fallen into the clutches of a lion in the midst of the forest.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

सा राक्षसीमध्यगता च भीरु र्वाग्भिर्भृशं रावणतर्जिता च। कान्तारमध्ये विजने विसृष्टा बालेव कन्या विललाप सीता।।5.28.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसीमध्यगता encircled by the shedemons, भीरुः soft natured, भृशम् excessively, वाग्भि: with words, रावणतर्जिता च threatened by Ravana, सा that, सीता Sita, विजने desolate, कान्तारमध्ये in the midst of the forest, विसृष्टा deserted, बाला young, कन्या इव like a girl, विललाप wept.

#### Explanation

Encircled by the demonesses, Sita was repeatedly humiliated by Ravana's cruel words.She wept like a young girl deserted in a desolate forest.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

सत्यं बतेदं प्रवदन्ति लोके नाकालमृत्युर्भवतीति सन्तः। यत्राहमेवं परिभर्त्स्यमाना जीवामि दीना क्षणमप्यपुण्या।।5.28.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लोके in the world, अकालमृत्युः untimely death, न भवति does not occur, इति like this, इदम् thus, सत्यम् true, सन्तः elders, प्रवदन्ति say, यत्र wherever, एवम् this way, परिभर्त्स्यमाना being threatened, अपुण्या who lacks merits, अहम् I am, दीना pitiable, क्षणमपि even a second also, जीवामि I live, बत alas.

#### Explanation

"Even though I am threatened like this, I am living pitiably because of lack of merit. Indeed I regret my survival due to my bad luck. Elders say that death will not ocurr before its time. It seems to be true.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

सुखाद्विहीनं बहुदुःखपूर्ण मिदं तु नूनं हृदयं स्थिरं मे। विशीर्यते यन्न सहस्रधाऽद्य वज्राहतं शृङ्गमिवाचलस्य।।5.28.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सुखात् of joy, विहीनम् devoid, बहुदुःखपूर्णम् filled with agony, इदम् this, मे my, हृदयम् heart, नूनम् surely, स्थिरम् hard, यत् such, वज्राहतम् hit by a thunderbolt, अचलस्य of a mountain, शृङ्गमिव like the top, सहस्रधा into a thousand pieces, अद्य now, न विशीर्यते it is not breaking.

#### Explanation

"Even though I am devoid of joy, and living in deep agony, my heart bursts not into a thousand pieces like the summit of a mountain hit by thunderbolt (lightning).

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

नैवास्ति दोषो मम नूनमत्र वध्याहमस्याप्रियदर्शनस्य। भावं न चास्याहमनुप्रदातु मलं द्विजो मन्त्रमिवाद्विजाय।।5.28.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अत्र मम here my, दोषः fault, नैवास्ति is not there, अहम् I am, अप्रियदर्शनस्य ugly, अस्य his, वध्या to be killed, अस्मि I am, अहम् I,  अस्य his, भावम् (surrender) my heart, द्विजः brahmin, अद्विजाय to a nonbrahmin, मन्त्रमिव like the veda mantra, अनुप्रदातुम् to impart, न अलम् not proper.

#### Explanation

"I will not be blamed if I commit suicide now.I stand condemned to death in the hands of this ugly Ravana. I cannot bestow my affection on him or surender to his desire like a brahmin would not like to impart Vedic knowledge to a nonbrahmin. (Better die of my own accord than be killed by a sinful ogre).

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

नूनं ममाङ्गान्यचिरादनार्यः शस्त्रैश्शितैश्छेत्स्यति राक्षसेन्द्रः। तस्मिननागच्छति लोकनाथे गर्भस्थजन्तोरिव शल्यकृन्तः।।5.28.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लोकनाथे the lord of the world, तस्मिन् he, अनागच्छति before he comes here, अनार्यः vile lord, राक्षसेन्द्रः lord of demons, शल्यकृन्तः barber, गर्भस्थजन्तोरिव like the foetus from the womb,  मम my, अङ्गानि limbs, नूनम् surely, शितैः with sharp, शत्रै: with weapons, छेत्स्यति will cut into pieces.

#### Explanation

"If the lord of the world does not come here before that (time fixed by Ravana) the vile lord of demons will cut me into pieces with weapons just as a barber would cut to pieces the foetus with a sharp knife (in order to save a pregnant woman).

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

दुःखं बतेदं मम दुःखिताया मासौ चिरायाधिगमिष्यतो द्वौ। बद्धस्य वध्यस्य तथा निशान्ते राजापराधादिव तस्करस्य।।5.28.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राजापराधात् due to offending the king, बद्धस्य captured, निशान्ते by daybreak, वध्यस्य for execution, तस्करस्य इव like that of a thief, दुःखितायाः of a sorrowful, मम to me, द्वौ two, मासौ months, चिराय is a long duration, अधिगमिष्यतः will be spent, इदम् this, दुःखम् grief, बत alas.

#### Explanation

"Alas, two months is too long a time to wait in grief. I am like a thief captured for offending a king and waiting overnight for execution at day-break.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

हा राम हा लक्ष्मण हा सुमित्रे हा राममातः सह मे जनन्या। एषा विपद्याम्यहमल्पभाग्या महार्णवे नौरिव मूढवाता।।5.28.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हा राम Alas! Rama, हा लक्ष्मण Alas! Lakshmana, हा सुमित्रे Alas! Sumitra!, मे my, जनन्या सह own mother, हा राममातः Alas! Rama's mother, अल्पभाग्या an ill-starred woman, एषा here, अहम् I, महार्णवे in an ocean, मूढवाता by a whirl-wind, नौरिव like a boat, विपद्यामि I am perished.

#### Explanation

"Alas! Rama, Alas! Lakshmana, Alas! Sumitra, Alas! my mother, Alas! Rama's mother, I am an ill-starred woman going to perish in this way like a light boat capsized in the midst of an ocean when the whirl-wind blows.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

तरस्विनौ धारयता मृगस्य सत्त्वेन रूपं मनुजेन्द्रपुत्रौ। नूनं विशस्तौ मम कारणात्तौ सिंहर्षभौ द्वाविव वैद्युतेन।।5.28.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मृगस्य deer's, रूपम् form, धारयता by taking, सत्त्वेन by a creature, तरस्विनौ these pair of , तौ two, मनुजेन्द्रपुत्रौ princes, वैद्युतेन by bolt of lightning, द्वौ two, सिंहर्षभौ इव two mighty lions, मम कारणात् on my account, नूनम् verily, विशस्तौ are slain.

#### Explanation

"Just like two mighty lions are killed by a bolt of lightning, the two princes must have been killed by a creature in the guise of a deer on my account.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

नूनं स कालो मृगरूपधारी मामल्पभाग्यां लुलुभे तदानीम्। यत्रार्यपुत्रं विससर्ज मूढा रामानुजं लक्ष्मणपूर्वजं च।।5.28.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नूनम् verily, सः such, कालः the spirit of time, मृगरूपधारी in the guise of a deer, तदानीम् then, अल्पभाग्याम् an unfortunate woman, माम् me, लुलुभे tempted, यत्र wherever, मूढा foolish, रामानुजम् Rama's brother, लक्ष्मणपूर्वजम् Lakshmana's elder brother, आर्यपुत्रं च son of a noble king, विससर्ज sent away.

#### Explanation

"Verily, it is the spirit of the time that assumed the form of deer and tempted this unfortunate soul. It was foolish of me to send away, the sons of a noble king, Lakshmana and his elder brother.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

हा राम सत्यव्रत दीर्घबाहो हा पूर्णचन्द्रप्रतिमानवक्त्र। हा जीवलोकस्य हितः प्रियश्च वध्यां न मां वेत्सि हि राक्षसानाम्।।5.28.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सत्यव्रत O unfailing in vows, दीर्घबाहो O long-armed one, हा राम Alas! Rama, हा पूर्णचन्द्रप्रतिमानवक्त्र alas! one whose face is like the full moon, हा alas, जीवलोकस्य for all the worlds, हितः benefactor, प्रियश्च dear one, माम् me, राक्षसानाम् for demons, वध्याम् to be killed, न वेत्सि you are not aware.

#### Explanation

"Alas, O long-armed one unfailing in vows, whose face shines like the full moon. Alas, beloved of all the worlds! you are dear to every one. How is it that you are not aware that I am going to be killed by the demons?

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

अनन्य दैवत्वमियं क्षमा च भूमौ च शय्या नियमश्च धर्मे। पतिव्रतात्वं विफलं ममेदं कृतं कृतघ्नेष्विव मानुषाणाम्।।5.28.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अनन्यदैवत्वम् no god other than you, इयम् this, क्षमा च and tolerance, भूमौ on earth, शय्या sleep, धर्मे in righteousness, नियमश्च discipline, पतिव्रतात्वम् my chastity, मम my, इदम् this, कृतघ्नेषु in case of ungrateful man, मानुषाणाम् of human beings, कृतमिव like the help done, विफलम् is futile.

#### Explanation

"I am devoted to you and to no other god. My hardship in sleeping on the ground, my righteousness, my discipline and chastity have all proved futile like the devotion of an ungrateful person.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

मोघो हि धर्मश्चरितो मयायं तथैकपत्नीत्वामिदं निरर्थम्। या त्वां न पश्यामि कृशा विवर्णा हीना त्वया सङ्गमने निराशा।।5.28.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

या I who, त्वाम् you, न पश्यामि I do not see, त्वया by you, हीना deprived, सङ्गमने in reuniting, निराशा no hope, कृशा shrunk, विवर्णा pale, मया  by me, चरितः followed, अयम् this, धर्मः righteousness, मोघः हि is in vain, तथा likewise, इदम् this, एकपन्तीत्वम् devotion to you alone, निरर्थम् meaningless.

#### Explanation

"This righteousnes practised by me is in vain like my devotion to you, as I am unable to see you. I am a separated, emaciated, pale with no hope of reuniting with you.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

पितुर्निदेशं नियमेन कृत्वा  वनान्निवृत्तश्चरितव्रतश्च। स्त्रीभिस्तु मन्ये विपुलेक्षणाभिस्त्वं रंस्यसे वीतभयः कृतार्थः।।5.28.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्वम् you, पितुः father's, निदेशम् command, नियमेन truly, कृत्वा following, चरितव्रतश्च after the completion of the pledge, वनात् from the forest, निवृत्तः you return, वीतभयः rid of fear, कृतार्थः an accomplished one, विपुलेक्षणाभिः largeeyed, स्त्रीभिः with damsels, रंस्यसे be revelling, मन्ये I think.

#### Explanation

"Having truly fulfilled your pledge given to your father, you will return from the forest to Ayodhya, rid of all fear, as an accomplished person, will and revel in the company of largeeyed damsels, I think.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

अहं तु राम त्वयि जातकामा चिरं विनाशाय निबद्धभावा। मोघं चरित्वाथ तपो व्रतञ्च त्यक्ष्यामि धिग्जीवितमल्पभाग्याम्।।5.28.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राम Rama, त्वयि at you, जातकामा I loved, अहं तु I am also, विनाशाय for my doom, चिरम् for long, निबद्धभावा with my feeling of love fixed on you, तपः austerities, व्रतं च and vows, मोघम् in vain, चरित्वाथ after practising, जीवितम् life, त्यक्ष्यामि will be giving up, अल्पभाग्याम् I am unfortunate one, धिक् what a pity

#### Explanation

"O RamaI loved you and concentrated all my love on you for a long time only for my doom. I have observed vows and austerities in vain. I cannot continue it for long. Here I am giving up my life. Fie upon this luckless one (me).

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

सा जीवितं क्षिप्रमहं त्यजेयं विषेण शस्त्रेण शितेन वापि। विषस्य दाता न हि मेऽस्ति कश्चि च्छस्त्रस्य वा वेश्मनि राक्षसस्य।।5.28.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सा अहम् that I, क्षिप्रम् at once, जीवितम् life, विषेण by poisoning, शितेन by a sharp one, शस्त्रेण by a weapon, वापि or else, त्यजेयम् I give up, राक्षसस्य demon's, वेश्मनि at his residence, मे to me, विषस्य of poison, शस्त्रस्य वा or of weapon, दाता donor, कश्चित् any one, नास्ति is not found.

#### Explanation

"I will end my life at once by taking poison or else by a sharp weapon. Alas There is none to give get me poison or a weapon at this demon's residence".

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

इतीव देवी बहुधा विलप्य सर्वात्मना राममनुस्मरन्ती। प्रवेपमाना परिशुष्कवक्त्रा नगोत्तमं पुष्पितमाससाद।।5.28.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

देवी queen, इतीव in that way, बहुधा in many ways, विलप्य  wailing, सर्वात्मना self of all, रामम् Rama, अनुस्मरन्ती constantly remembering, प्रवेपमाना shivering, परिशुष्कवक्त्रा pale face, पुष्पितम् in bloom, नगोत्तमम् great tree, आससाद approached.

#### Explanation

Queen Sita was wailing in many ways, constantly remembering Rama, the soul of all. Shivering, her face pales as she approached the great Simsupa tree in bloom.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

सा शोकतप्ता बहुधा विचिन्त्य सीताऽथ वेण्युद्ग्रथनं गृहीत्वा। उद्बुध्य वेण्युद्ग्रथनेन शीघ्रमहं गमिष्यामि यमस्य मूलम्।।5.28.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शोकाभितप्ता burning with grief, सीता Sita, बहुधा in many ways, विचिन्त्य after worrying, अथ then, वेण्युद्ग्रथनम् braid of her hair, गृहीत्वा holding, अहम् I, वेण्युद्ग्रथनेन by the braid, उद्बुध्य tie, शीघ्रम् quickly, यमस्य lord of death's, मूलम् presence, गमिष्यामि I will reach.

#### Explanation

"Burning in grief, worrying in many ways, Sita took hold of her braid and said", by tying myself with this braid I shall quickly reach the presence of the lord of death".

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

उपस्थिता सा मृदुसर्वगात्री शाखां गृहत्वाऽध नगस्य तस्य। तस्यास्तु रामं प्रविचिन्तयन्त्या रामानुजं स्वं च कुलं शुभाङ्ग्या:।।5.28.19।। शेकानिमित्तानि तथा बहूनि धैर्यार्जितानि प्रवराणि लोके। प्रादुर्निमित्तानि तदा बभूवुः पुरापि सिद्धान्युपलक्षितानि।।5.28.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथ and then, मृदसर्वगात्री lady of delicate limbs, सा she, तस्य of that, नगस्य tree's, शाखाम् branch, गृहीत्वा holding, उपस्थिता stood, रामम् Rama, रामानुजम् Rama's brother, स्वं कुलं च and their family, प्रविचिन्तयन्त्या: was thinking, शुभाङ्ग्याः of charming body, तस्याः तु her, तदा then, शोकानिमित्तानि dispellers of sorrow, धैर्यार्जितानि harbingers of courage, लोके in the world, प्रवराणि foremost, तथा similarly, पुरापि ancient times also, सिद्धानि results, उपलक्षितानि  been proven, बहूनि many, निमित्तानि omens, प्रादुर्भभूवुः manifested.

#### Explanation

Sita of delicate limbs approached the Simsupa tree and held on to a branch and stood. While she was thinking of Rama and Lakshmana and their noble family many  omens of proven results appeared on her auspicious charming body. These omens were dispellers of grief, and harbingers of courage in the world as declared from ancient times. (Now) they manifested themselves. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये सुन्दरकाण्डे अष्टाविंशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the twentyeigth sarga of Sundarakanda of the holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 29

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

तथागतां तां व्यथितामनिन्दितां व्यपेतहर्षां परिदीनमानसाम्। शुभां निमित्तानि शुभानि भेजिरे नरं श्रिया जुष्टमिवोपजीविनः।।5.29.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तथागताम् as expected, व्यथिताम् agonized lady, अनिन्दिताम् faultless, व्यपेतहर्षाम् bereft of all joy, परिदीनमानसाम् distressed in mind, शुभाम् auspicious, ताम् her, श्रिया with benign favour of fortune, जुष्टम् pleased, नरम् man, उपजीविनः इव like well wishing, शुभानि auspicious, निमित्तानि for the sake, भेजिरे wealthy man.

#### Explanation

Like wellwishers flocking round a person on whom fortune is smiling, omens foretelling auspicious happenings in near future appeared to Sita. She was unimpeachable. She was in agony, distressed in mind, bereft of all joy (now) standing under that Simsupa tree.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

तस्याः शुभं वाममरालपक्ष्म राजीवृतं कृष्णविशालशुक्लम्। प्रास्पन्दतैकं नयनं सुकेश्या मीनाहतं पद्ममिवाभिताम्रम्।।5.29.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सुकेश्याः of the lady with beautiful hair, तस्याः her, शुभम् auspicious, अरालपक्ष्मराजीवृतम् surrounded by a row of curved eye lashes, कृष्णविशालशुक्लम् broad black pupils in white, वामनयनम् left eye, मीनाहतम् struck by fish, अभिताम्रम् reddish ring, एकम् one, पद्ममिव like lotus, प्रास्पन्दत throbbed.

#### Explanation

Her left eye with a row of curved eyelashes, the black pupils of the eye in white throbbed in an auspicious manner. The lady  with her long tresses, and throbbing eye appeared like a red lotus gently struck by a fish.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

भुजश्च चार्वञ्चितपीनवृत्तः परार्थ्यकालागरुचन्दनार्हः। अनुत्तमेनाध्युषितः प्रियेण चिरेण वामः समवेपताऽशु।।5.29.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

चार्वञ्चितपीनवृत्तः beautifuly curved round and stout, परार्थ्यकालागरुचन्दनार्हः deserving the application of excellent black agaru and sandal paste, अनुत्तमेन by the best, प्रियेण by the beloved, चिरेण for long, अध्युषितः  used to rest the head, वामः भुजश्च by the left arm, आशु suddenly, समवेपत throbbed.

#### Explanation

Her stout left arm curved beautifully, fit for excellent agaru and sandal paste once the pillow by her beloved lord for long, throbbed suddenly.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

गजेन्द्रहस्तप्रतिमश्च पीन स्तयोर्द्वयोः सम्हतयोः सुजातः। प्रस्पन्दमानः पुनरूरुरस्या रामं पुरस्तात् स्थितमाचचक्षे।।5.29.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

संहतयोः close to each other, तयोः द्वयोः of those two, गजेन्द्रहस्तप्रतिमः resembling an elephant's trunk, पीनः stout, सुजातः well shaped, अस्याः ऊरुः her thigh, प्रस्पन्दमानः throbbing, रामम् Rama, पुरस्तात् in front, स्थितम् stood, आचचक्षे indicated.

#### Explanation

Again her roundshaped left thigh resembling an elephant's trunk started throbbing, presaging union with Rama.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

शुभं पुनर्हेमसमानवर्ण मीषद्रजोध्वस्तमिवामलाक्ष्याः। वास स्स्थिताया श्शिखराग्रदन्त्याः किञ्चित्परिस्रंसत चारुगात्य्राः।।5.29.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पुनः again, अमलाक्ष्याः lady with pristine eyes, शिखराग्रदन्त्याः with round edged teeth, चारुगात्य्राः of the lady of beautiful limbs, स्थितायाः standing, शुभम् auspicious, हेमसमानवर्णम् of the colour of gold, ईषत् slightly, रजोध्वस्तमिव slightly dull on account of dust, वासः clothes, किञ्चित् a little, परिस्रंसत slipped.

#### Explanation

Her eyes were pristine, teeth wellshaped. The goldhued auspicious sari on her charming limbs was now a little soiled on account of dust. It slipped slightly as she stood up. This augured well for her.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

एतैर्निमित्तैरपरैश्च सुभ्रूः सम्बोधिता प्रागपि साधु सिद्धैः। वातातपक्लान्तमिव प्रणष्टं वर्षेण बीजं प्रतिसंजहर्ष।।5.29.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

प्रागपि in the past also, साधु well, सिद्धैः  by seers, एतैः by these, निमित्तै: by the presence of  omens, अपरैश्च and by others, सम्बोधिता addressed, सुभ्रूः a lady with lovely eyebrows, वातप्रक्लान्तम् blighted by wind and heat, प्रणष्टम् dried, बीजम् seed, वर्षेण इव with rain, प्रतिसञ्जहर्ष felt happy.

#### Explanation

Reassured by such omens which augured well as tested by time, Sita, with beautiful eyebrows, and deep foresight experienced great joy, like a seed blighted by the wind and the Sun comes back to joyful life through a pleasing shower. (A foreteller is addressed as siddha in sanskrit dramas)

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

तस्याः पुनर्बिम्बफलाधरोष्ठं स्वक्षिभ्रुकेशान्तमरालपक्ष्म। वक्त्रं बभासे सितशुक्लदंष्ट्रं राहोर्मुखाच्चन्द्र इव प्रमुक्तः।।5.29.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पुनः again, तस्याः her, बिम्बफलाधरोष्ठम् with lips like bimba fruit, स्वक्षिभ्रुकेशान्तम् beautiful eye brows, अरालपक्ष्म curved eyelashes extending up to her hair, सितचारुदन्तम् white and lovely teeth, वक्त्रम् mouth, राहोः from planet Rahu, मुखात् from face, प्रमुक्तः set free, चन्द्रः इव like the Moon, बभासे shone.

#### Explanation

Her face shining with beautiful red lips like bimba fruit, sparkling teeth, and curved eyelashes with lovely brows extending up to her hair, she appeared like a Moon released from the mouth of Rahu.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

सा वीतशोका व्यपनीततन्द्री शान्तज्वरा हर्षविबुद्धसत्त्वा। अशोभतार्या वदनेन शुक्ले शीतांशुना रात्रिरिवोदितेन।।5.29.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

आर्या revered lady, सा she, वीतशोका relieved from grief, व्यपनीततन्द्री a lady relieved from exhaustion, शान्तज्वरा fears allyed, हर्षविबुद्धसत्त्वा a lady with her mind illumined by joy, वदनेन with her countenance, शुक्ले in the bright fortnight, उदितेन risen, शीतांशुना with cool Moon, रात्रिरिव like the night, अशोभत shone.

#### Explanation

Revered Sita, completely relieved of grief, exhaustion gone, fears allayed, mind illumined with joy, looked charming with her countenance as a night with the Moon fully risen during the bright fortnight. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये सुन्दरकाण्डे एकोनत्रिंशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the twentyninth sarga of Sundarakanda of the holy Ramayana, the first epic compsed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 30

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

हनुमानपि विक्रान्तः सर्वं शुश्राव तत्त्वतः। सीतायास्त्रिजटायाश्च राक्षसीनां च तर्जनम्।।5.30.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विक्रान्तः valiant hero, हनुमानपि Hanuman also, सीतायाश्च Sita's, त्रिजटायाश्च Trijata's, सर्वम् everything, राक्षसीनाम् shedemons, तर्जनम् threatening, तत्त्वतः  in principle, शुश्राव heard.

#### Explanation

The valiant hero Hanuman heard everything including the lamentation of Sita and the dream of Trijata and the threats of the demonesses.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

अवेक्षमाणस्तां देवीं देवतामिव नन्दने। ततो बहुविधां चिन्तां चिन्तयामास वानरः।।5.30.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, वानरः vanara, नन्दने in the Nandana garden, देवतामिव like the goddess, तां देवीम् to that deitylike Sita, अवेक्षमाणः  looking, बहुविधाम् many ways, चिन्ताम् चिन्तयामास reflected.

#### Explanation

The sight of Sita who seemed a deity in the Nandana garden of Indra, set a chain of reflections in his mind :

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

यां कपीनां सहस्राणि सुबहून्ययुतानि च। दिक्षु सर्वासु मार्गन्ते सेयमासादिता मया।।5.30.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कपीनाम् of vanaras, सुबहूनि many, सहस्राणि thousands, अयुतानि च 10,000 soldiers सर्वासु all over, दिक्षु in directions, याम् whom, मार्गन्ते are searching, सा इयम् that Sita here, मया by myself, आसादिता she is found.

#### Explanation

"This is Sita whom thousands of vanaras are searching in all directions.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

चारेण तु सुयुक्तेन शत्रोश्शक्तिमवेक्षता। गूढेन चरता तावदवेक्षितमिदं मया।।5.30.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सुयुक्तेन very carefully, शत्रोः enemy's, शक्तिमवेक्षता while seeing the strength, गूढेन in disguise, चरता roaming, चारेण a spy, मया by myself, इदम् this place, अवेक्षितं तावत् seen everything.

#### Explanation

"Roaming everywhere incognito to ascertain carefully the strength of the enemy, I have seen everything.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

राक्षसानां विशेषश्च पुरी चेयमवेक्षिता। राक्षसाधिपतेरस्य प्रभावो रावणस्य च।।5.30.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसानाम् of the demons, विशेषश्च ability, इयम् this, पुरी च citadel also, अवेक्षिता had been seen, अस्य its, राक्षसाधिपतेः of the lord of demons, रावणस्य Ravana's, प्रभावश्च power also.

#### Explanation

"I have seen the ability of the demons and the citadel of Lanka. I have seen the power of Ravana the demon king.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

युक्तं तस्याप्रमेयस्य सर्वसत्त्वदयावतः। समाश्वासयितुं भार्यां पतिदर्शनकाङ्क्षिणीम्।।5.30.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सर्वसत्त्वदयावतः who is compassionate to all, अप्रमेयस्य man of immeasureable prowess, तस्य his, पतिदर्शनकाङ्क्षिणीम् longing to see her husband, भार्याम् wife, समाश्वासयितुम् to console युक्तम् is the right action.

#### Explanation

"It is the opportune time for me to comfort Sita eagerly waiting to see her husband who is of immeasurable prowess and who is compassionate to all beings.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

अहमाश्वासयाम्येनां पूर्णचन्द्रनिभाननाम्। अदृष्टदुःखां दुःखार्तां दुःखस्यान्तमगच्छतीम्।।5.30.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पूर्णचन्द्रनिभाननाम् who has the countenance of a fullmoon, अदृष्टदुःखाम् who has never seen suffering, दुःखार्ताम् experiencing sorrow, दुःखस्य from sorrrow, अन्तम् end, अगच्छतीम् not approaching, एनाम् her, अहम् I, आश्वासयामि will console.

#### Explanation

"The moonfaced Sita had never experienced sorrow before. She sees no end to her sorrow and suffering. I will console her.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

यद्यप्यहमिमां देवीं शोकोपहतचेतनाम्। अनाश्वास्य गमिष्यामि दोषवद्गमनं भवेत्।।5.30.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शोकोपहतचेतना whose mind is overwhelmed with grief, इमां देवीम् this Sita, अहम् I, अनाश्वस्य without reassuring, यदि गमिष्यामि if I go, गमनम् destination (Kishkinda), दोषवत् is blameworthy, भवेत् would be.

#### Explanation

"If I should return to Kishkindha without reassuring Sita, whose understanding has been clouded in grief I would be blamed.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

गते हि मयि तत्रेयं राजपुत्री यशस्विनी। परित्राणमविन्दन्ती जानकी जीवितं त्यजेत्।।5.30.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मयि when me, तत्र there, गते I go, यशस्विनी renowned, राजपुत्री princess, जानकी Janaki, परित्राणम् deliverance, अविन्दन्ती without getting, जीवितम् life, त्यजेत् she will give up.

#### Explanation

"If I so back, this illustrious princess, Janaki will give up her life, finding no means of deliverance (from this tragic situation).

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

मया च स महाबाहुः पूर्णचन्द्रनिभाननः। समाश्यासयितुं न्याय्यस्सीतादर्शनलालसः।।5.30.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाबाहुः strongarmed hero, पूर्णचन्द्रनिभाननः face like the fullmoon, सीतादर्शनलालसः yearning to see Sita, सः that Rama, मया by me, समाश्वसयितुम् to provide comfort, न्याय्यः it is proper.

#### Explanation

"I should provide some comfort to the strongarmed Rama with a face like the fullmoon yearning to see Sita.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

निशाचरीणां प्रत्यक्षमनर्हं चाभिभाषितम्। कथन्नु खलु कर्तव्यमिदं कृच्छ्रगतो ह्यहम्।।5.30.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

निशाचरीणाम् of nightrangers, प्रत्यक्षम् in their presence, अभिभाषितम्  talking, अनर्हम् is not proper, इदम् this, कथम् how, कर्तव्यं खलु नु should be fulfilled, अहम् I, कृच्छ्रगतो हि I am in a predicament.

#### Explanation

"I cannot talk to her in the presence of these nightroamers. How can I  discharge my duty in this predicament?

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

अनेन रात्रिशेषेण यदि नाश्वास्यते मया। सर्वथा नास्ति सन्देहः परित्यक्ष्यति जीवितम्।।5.30.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अनेन by this, रात्रिशेषेण when a part of the night is still left, मया by me, नाश्वास्यते यदि if I do not comfort her, जीवितम् life, परित्यक्ष्यति she will give up, सर्वथा by all means, सन्देहः doubt, नास्ति not.

#### Explanation

"If I do not comfort her before the remaining part of the night ends, she will give up her life. There is no doubt about it.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

रामश्च यदि पृच्छेन्मां किं मां सीताब्रवीद्वचः। किमहं तं प्रति ब्रूयामसंभाष्य सुमध्यमाम्।।5.30.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीता Sita, माम् to me, किम् what, वचः words, अब्रवीत् spoke, रामश्च Rama also , माम् I am, पृच्छेद्यदि if he enquires, सुमध्यमाम् lady with beautiful waist, असंभाष्य without talking, अहम् I, तं प्रति to him, किम् what, ब्रूयाम् I can say.

#### Explanation

"If Rama enquires about the message of Sita, this lady of beautiful waist, what can I speak without meeting and talking to her?

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

सीतासन्देशरहितं मामितस्त्वरयाऽगतम्। निर्दहेदपि काकुत्स्य क्रुद्धस्तीव्रेण चक्षुषा।।5.30.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीतासन्देशरहितम् without any message from Sita, इतः from here, त्वरया at once, गतम् gone, माम् me, काकुत्थ्सः Rama, क्रुद्धः angry, तीव्रेण with a sharp, चक्षुषा with eyes, निर्दहेदपि will burn me.

#### Explanation

"If I leave this place without any message from Sita, Rama will burn me with his eyes full of great anger.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

यदि चोद्योजयिष्यामि भर्तारं रामकारणात्। व्यर्थमागमनं तस्य ससैन्यस्य भविष्यति।।5.30.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामकारणात् for the cause of Rama, भर्तारम् lord of vanaras, यदि च उद्योजयिष्यामि if i motivate him, ससैन्यस्य of him along with his army, तस्य his, आगमनम् coming, व्यर्थम् useless, भविष्यति will become.

#### Explanation

"If I get the lord of vanaras and his army for the cause of Rama (without talking to Sita), their arrival will be useless (if Sita would have given up her life by then).

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

अन्तरं त्वहमासाद्य राक्षसीनामिह स्थितः। शनैराश्वासयिष्यामि सन्तापबहुळामिमाम्।।5.30.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I, इह here, स्थितः waiting, राक्षसीनाम् of the demonesses, अन्तरम् some chance, आसाद्य after finding, सन्तापबहुलाम् deeply distressed, इमाम् this lady, शनैः slowly, आश्वासयिष्यामि will comfort her.

#### Explanation

"I shall wait and speak to her slowly and console this deeply distressed lady when I get a chance to speak to her (when  these ogresses are asleep).

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

अहं त्वतितनुश्चैव वानरश्च विशेषतः। वाचं चोदाहरिष्यामि मानुषीमिह संस्कृताम्।।5.30.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहं तु I too, अतितनुश्च small body, विशेषतः more so, वानरश्च a vanara, इह here, सम्स्कृताम् sanskrit language, मानुषीम् of human, वाचं च and talk in, उदाहरिष्यामि I will adopt.

#### Explanation

I am indeed of small size and that too a monkey. Nevertheless I shall speak in Sanskrit, a language of the common people. (as the she-demons cannot understand that language).

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

यदि वाचं प्रदास्यामि द्विजातिरिव संस्कृताम्। रावणं मन्यमाना मां सीता भीता भविष्यति।।5.30.18।। वानरस्य विशेषेण कथं स्यादभिभाषणम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

द्विजातिरिव like a brahmin, संस्कृताम् sanskrit, वाचम् language, यदि if I प्रदास्यामि will speak, सीता Sita, माम् me, रावणम् Ravana, मन्यमाना thinking, भीता भविष्यति will become scared, विशेषेण specially, वानरस्य vanara's, अभिभाषणम् talking, कथम् how, स्यात् would be.

#### Explanation

"If I speak like a brahmin in sanskrit language she would be frightened thinking that I am Ravana and how would a vanara speak?

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

अवश्यमेव वक्तव्यं मानुषं वाक्यमर्थवत्।।5.30.19।। मया सान्त्वयितुं शक्या नान्यथेयमनिन्दिता।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अवश्यमेव certainly, मया by myself, अर्थवत् for that reason, मानुषम् common man's, वाक्यम् language, वक्तव्यम् should speak, अनिन्दिता siness lady, इयम् this, अन्यथा otherwise, सान्त्वयितुम् to pacify her, शक्या possible.

#### Explanation

"I will certainly speak to her meaningfully in the language of the people. Otherwise it is not possible to pacify the sinless Sita.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

सेयमालोक्य मे रूपं जानकी भाषितं तथा।।5.30.20।। रक्षोभिस्त्रासिता पूर्वं भूयस्त्रासं गमिष्यति।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पूर्वम् earlier, रक्षोभिः by the demons, त्रासिता frightened, सा इयम् this, जानकी Janaki, मे my, रूपम् form, तथा this way, भाषितम् speaking, आलोक्य on seeing, भूयः again, त्रासम् fear, गमिष्यति will experience.

#### Explanation

"Since Janaki has already been frightened by the demons, she will be even more terrified seeing me in this form and hearing me speaking this way.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

ततो जातपरित्रासा शब्दं कुर्यान्मनस्विनी।।5.30.21।। जानमाना  विशालाक्षी रावणं कामरूपिणम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, मनस्विनी sensitive woman, विशालाक्षी largeeyed, माम् me, कामरूपिणम् who can change form at will, रावणम् to Ravana, जानमाना she may think, जातपरित्रासा frightened, शब्दम् sound, कुर्यात् may make.

#### Explanation

By seeing me in this form, sensitive largeeyed Sita will scream loudly thinking that I am Ravana who can assume any form at his free will.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

सीतया च कृते शब्दे सहसा राक्षसीगणा:। नानाप्रहरणो घोर: समेयादन्तकोपम:।।5.30.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीतया by Sita's, शब्दे when sound, कृते is made, सहसा at once, नानाप्रहरण: holding different kinds of weapons, घोर: terrific ones, अन्तकोपम: comparable to the lord of death, राक्षसीगण: horde of ogresses, समेयात् would gather.

#### Explanation

"If Sita screams, the  horde of terrific ogresses will at once gather, armed with dreadful weapons like Yama, the god of death.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

ततो मां सम्परिक्षिप्य सर्वतो विकृताननाः।।5.30.23।। वधे च ग्रहणे चैव कुर्युर्यत्नं यथाबलम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, विकृताननाः women with hideous faces, माम् at me, सर्वतः from all directions, सम्परिक्षिप्य throwing down, वधे च to kill, ग्रहणे चैव and to capture me, यथाबलम् according to their strength, यत्नम् effort, कुर्युः will make.

#### Explanation

"Then all these hideous ogresses will swarm round me and make efforts to capture me with all their strength and kill me.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

गृह्य शाखाः प्रशाखाश्च स्कन्धांश्चोत्तमशाखिनाम्।।5.30.24।। दृष्ट्वा विपरिधावन्तं भवेयुर्भयशङ्किताः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

उत्तमशाखिनाम् of good trees, शाखाः branches, प्रशाखाश्च and side branches, स्कन्धांश्च and trunks, गृह्य by holding, विपरिधावन्तम् running away, दृष्ट्वा seeing, भयशङ्किताः get scared, भवेयुः will be.

#### Explanation

"Seeing me holding big and small branches of large trees and leaping from one place to the other, the ogresses will get scared.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

मम रूपं च सम्प्रेक्ष्य वने विचरतो महत्।।5.30.25।। राक्षस्यो भयवित्रस्ता भवेयुर्विकृताननाः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विकृताननाः with hideous faces, राक्षस्यः ogresses, वने in the grove, विचरतः roaming, मम me, महत् great, रूपम् form, सम्प्रेक्ष्य glancing, भयवित्रस्ताः very scared, भवेयुः will be.

#### Explanation

"These ogresses with their hideous faces will be frightened on looking at my huge form moving in this grove.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

ततः कुर्युस्समाह्वानं राक्षस्यो रक्षसामपि।।5.30.26।। राक्षसेन्द्रनियुक्तानां राक्षसेन्द्रनिवेशने।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः thereafter, राक्षस्यः ogresses, राक्षसेन्द्रनिवेशने in the residence of the king of demons, राक्षसेन्द्रनियुक्तानाम् engaged by the demon king to guard, रक्षसाम् अपि of the demons also, समाह्वानम् drawing their attention, कुर्युः they will do.

#### Explanation

"Thereafter the ogresses will draw the attention of demon guards engaged by the king of demons at his residence towards me.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

ते शूलशक्तिनिस्त्रिंशविविधायुधपाणयः।।5.30.27।। आपतेयुर्विमर्देऽस्मिन्वेगेनोद्वेगकारणात्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ते those, तस्मिन् in that, विमर्दे in war, शूलशक्तिनिस्त्रिंशविविधायुधपाणयः holding different kinds of weapons such as spears, tridents and swords, उद्वेगकारणात् due to fear, वेगेन speedily, आपतेयुः will collect.

#### Explanation

"Those ogresses due to fear will come speedily, armed with spears, tridents, swords and different kinds of weapons for a combat.

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

समृद्धस्तैस्तु परितो विधमन् रक्षसां बलम्।।5.30.28।। शक्नुयां न तु संप्राप्तुं परं पारं महोदधेः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तैः by them, परितः around me, समृद्धः filled, रक्षसाम् of demons, बलम् power, विधमन् while subduing, महोदधेः great sea, परं पारम् to the other side, सम्प्राप्तुम् to reach, न शक्नुयाम् not be possib.le

#### Explanation

"With them around me, I may not be able to  reach the other end of the ocean (my strength spent in the combat.)

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

मां वा गृह्णीयुराप्लुत्य बहवश्शीघ्रकारिणः।।5.30.29।। स्यादियं चागृहीतार्था मम च ग्रहणं भवेत्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शीघ्रकारिणः prompt, बहवः a large number, आप्लुत्य jumping into the sky, माम् me, गृह्णीयुः वा may catch me, इयं च this lady, अगृहीतार्था may not receive the message, स्यात् may be, मम my, ग्रहणं च arrest, भवेत् will be done.

#### Explanation

"The ogres who are in large numbers, here are very prompt to act. They will jump into the sky and bind me. Sita will not receive the message and I will be taken into custody by the demons.

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

हिंसाभिरुचयो हिंस्युरिमां वा जनकात्मजाम्।।5.30.30।। विपन्नं स्यात्ततः कार्यं रामसुग्रीवयोरिदम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वा or, हिंसाभिरुचयः keen on indulging in violence, इमाम् this lady, जनकात्मजाम् daughter of Janaka, हिंस्युः harm her, ततः then, रामसुग्रीवयोः of Rama and Sugriva, इदम् this, कार्यम् mission, विपन्नं स्यात् will fail.

#### Explanation

"These demons who are keen on indulging in violence will harm Janaka's daughter. Then the mission of Rama and Sugriva will also fail.

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

उद्देशे नष्टमार्गेऽस्मिन् राक्षसैः परिवारिते।।5.30.31।। सागरेण परिक्षिप्ते गुप्ते वसति जानकी।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

जानकी Janaki, नष्टमार्गे in a hidden location, राक्षसैः by the ogres, परिवारिते surrounded, सागरेण by the ocean, परिक्षिप्ते surrounded allover, गुप्ते in a secret place, अस्मिन् in this, उद्धेशे in this, वसति staying.

#### Explanation

"Janaki is staying in this untraceable location surrounded by demons. And this location is a secret place surrounded by the ocean .

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

विशस्ते वा गृहीते वा रक्षोभिर्मयि संयुगे।।5.30.32।। नान्यं पश्यामि रामस्य सहायं कार्यसाधने।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मयि me, संयुगे in a battle, रक्षोभिः by ogres, विशस्ते वा if I am killed, गृहीते वा or captured, रामस्य of Rama, कार्यसाधने to accomplish the task, अन्यम् another, सहायम् help, न पश्यामि I do not see.

#### Explanation

"If I am killed in the combat or captured by the demons, I do not see another person who can help in accomplishing this task.

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

विमृशंश्च न पश्यामि यो हते मयि वानरः।।5.30.33।। शतयोजनविस्तीर्णं लङ्घयेत महोदधिम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मयि when I am, हते killed, यः whosoever, वानरः vanara, शतयोजनविस्तीर्णम् extended over a distance of hundred yojanas, महोदधिम् the ocean, लङ्घयेत् can leap over, विमृशन् च even while reflecting carefully, न पश्यामि I cannot see.

#### Explanation

"When I am killed here, I cannot see any other vanara who can leap over a hundred yojanas of the ocean (to reach this place).

---

### Shloka 34

#### Shloka Text

कामं हन्तुं समर्थोऽस्मि सहस्राण्यपि रक्षसाम्।।5.30.34।। न तु शक्ष्यामि सम्प्राप्तुं परं पारं महोदधेः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रक्षसाम् of demons, सहस्राण्यपि even thousands of them, हन्तुम् to kill, समर्थः capable, अस्मि कामम् I am surely, तु also, महोदधेः of the ocean, परं पारम् to cross over, सम्प्राप्तुम् to reach, न शक्ष्यामि it may not be possible.

#### Explanation

"I may be capable of killing even thousands of demons but thereafter (thoroughly exhausted) reach the other side of the ocean.

---

### Shloka 35

#### Shloka Text

असत्यानि च युद्धानि संशयो मे न रोचते।।5.30.35।। कश्च निस्संशयं कार्यं कुर्यात्प्राज्ञः ससंशयम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

युद्धानि battles, असत्यानि च not true, संशयः doubtful, मे I, न रोचते it does not please me, कः who, प्राज्ञः wise man, निःसंशयम् without any doubt, कार्यम् task, ससंशयम् without any doubt, कुर्यात् will take up.

#### Explanation

"Battles, no doubt, are unpredictable. Which wise man with a sound intellect will take up a doubtful work unhesitatingly?

---

### Shloka 36

#### Shloka Text

प्राणत्यागश्च वैदेह्या भवेदनभिभाषणे।।5.30.36।। एष दोषो महान्हि स्यान्मम सीताभिभाषणे।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अनभिभाषणे if I do not speak, वैदेह्याः of Vaidehi, प्राणत्यागश्च abandonement of life, भवेत् may be, सीताभिभाषणे on talking to Sita, मम to me, एषः such, महान् great, दोषः drawback, स्यात् will be.

#### Explanation

"If I talk to Vaidehi it will be a great mistake on my part.If I do not speak to Vaidehi she may give up her life.

---

### Shloka 37

#### Shloka Text

भूताश्चार्था विनश्यन्ति देशकालविरोधिताः।।5.30.37।। विक्लबं दूतमासाद्य तमः सूर्योदये यथा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विक्लबम् a confused person, दूतम् messenger, आसाद्य by appointing, भूताः even sure ones, अर्थाः missions, देशकालविरोधिताः if place and time are not propitious, सूर्योदये at Sunrise, तमः यथा like the darkness, विनश्यन्ति will be destroyed.

#### Explanation

"Even well planned diplomatic missions fail like darkness dispelled at Sunrise if the messenger is confused and if the place and time are not propitious for them.

---

### Shloka 38

#### Shloka Text

अर्थानर्थान्तरे बुद्धिनिश्चितापि न शोभते।।5.30.38।। घातयन्ति हि कार्याणि दूताः पण्डितमानिनः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अर्थानर्थान्तरे swinging from a course of action or an action leading to calamity, बुद्धि intelligence, निश्चितापि though decided, न शोभते not proper, पण्डितमानिनः selfstyled scholars, दूताः messengers, कार्याणि their missions, घातयन्ति हि will be destroyed.

#### Explanation

"Swinging from a course of action or an action leading to calamity is not proper. Messengers who assume that they are scholars (blinded by ego) destroy their mission.

---

### Shloka 39

#### Shloka Text

न विनश्येत्कथं कार्यं वैक्लब्यं न कथं भवेत्।।5.30.39।। लङ्घनं च समुद्रस्य कथं नु न वृथा भवेत्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कार्यम् task, कथम् how, न विनश्येत् may not be spoilt, वैक्लब्यम् failure, कथम् how, न भवेत् not be allowed to take place, समुद्रस्य ocean's, लङ्घनं च crossing, कथं नु how indeed, वृथा wasteful, न भवेत् not to be.

#### Explanation

"How can the mission succeed? How can failure of mission be avoided? How can the crossing of the great ocean be not wasted?"

---

### Shloka 40

#### Shloka Text

कथं नु खलु वाक्यं मे शृणुयान्नोद्विजेत वा। इति सञ्चिन्त्य हनुमांश्चकार मतिमान्मतिम्।।5.30.40।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मे वाक्यम् my words, कथं नु how to, शृणुयायात् she may listen, न उद्विजेत वा and not get agitated, इति thus, सञ्चिन्त्य reflecting, मतिमान् wise, हनुमान् Hanuman, मतिम् in his mind, चकार exercised.

#### Explanation

Wise Hanuman came to a decision after reflecting in his mind about how not to create fear in Sita (while communicating with her.)

---

### Shloka 41

#### Shloka Text

राममक्लिष्टकर्माणं सुबन्धुमनुकीर्तयन्।।5.30.41।। नैनामुद्वेजयिष्यामि तद्बन्धुगतमानसाम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अक्लिष्टकर्माणम् one who is judicious in action, सुबन्धुम् very dear to her, रामम् Rama, अनुकीर्तयन् by praising him, तद्बन्धुगतमानसाम् since her mind is absorbed in him, एनाम् her, नोद्वेजयिष्यामि I will not create fear.

#### Explanation

"By praising Rama who is very dear, who is judicious in action and in whom her mind is absorbed, I will not create fear.

---

### Shloka 42

#### Shloka Text

इक्ष्वाकूणां वरिष्ठस्य रामस्य विदितात्मनः।।5.30.42।। शुभानि धर्मयुक्तानि वचनानि समर्पयन्। श्रावयिष्यामि सर्वाणि मधुरां प्रब्रुवन् गिरम्।।5.30.43।। श्रद्धास्यति यथाहीयं तथा सर्वं समादधे।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इक्ष्वाकूणाम् in Ikshvaku race, वरिष्ठस्य of the esteemed person, विदितात्मनः of the knower of the self, रामस्य Rama's, शुभानि auspicious, वचनानि words, समर्पयन् by offering, मधुराम् sweet, गिरम् word, प्रब्रुवन् while uttering, सर्वाणि everything, श्रावयिष्यामि will make her listen, इयम् this, यथा as, श्रद्धास्यति will trust me, सर्वम् everything, समाददे I will present.

#### Explanation

"I shall present in auspicious words the esteemed Rama of Ikshvaku family who is a knower of self and make her listen to everything in such a sweet manner that she will trust me.

---

### Shloka 43

#### Shloka Text

इक्ष्वाकूणां वरिष्ठस्य रामस्य विदितात्मनः।।5.30.42।। शुभानि धर्मयुक्तानि वचनानि समर्पयन्। श्रावयिष्यामि सर्वाणि मधुरां प्रब्रुवन् गिरम्।।5.30.43।। श्रद्धास्यति यथाहीयं तथा सर्वं समादधे।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इक्ष्वाकूणाम् in Ikshvaku race, वरिष्ठस्य of the esteemed person, विदितात्मनः of the knower of the self, रामस्य Rama's, शुभानि auspicious, वचनानि words, समर्पयन् by offering, मधुराम् sweet, गिरम् word, प्रब्रुवन् while uttering, सर्वाणि everything, श्रावयिष्यामि will make her listen, इयम् this, यथा as, श्रद्धास्यति will trust me, सर्वम् everything, समाददे I will present.

#### Explanation

"I shall present in auspicious words the esteemed Rama of Ikshvaku family who is a knower of self and make her listen to everything in such a sweet manner that she will trust me.

---

### Shloka 44

#### Shloka Text

इति स बहुविधं महानुभावो जगतिपतेः प्रमदामवेक्षमाणः। मधुरमवितथं जगाद वाक्यं द्रुमविटपान्तरमास्थितो हनूमान्।।5.30.44।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महानुभावः magnanimous, सः हनूमान् that Hanuman, द्रुमविटपान्तरम् behind the branch of the tree, आस्थितः seated, जगतिपतेः of the lord of the universe, प्रमदाम् wife, अवेक्षमाणः seeing, बहुविधम् in many ways, अवितथम् faithful, वाक्यम् words, इति thus, जगाद spoke

#### Explanation

Highsouled Hanuman seated on the branch of the tree without being seen began to speak faithfully in praise of the lord of the universe in many ways while looking at her. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये सुन्दरकाण्डे त्रिंशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the thirtieth sarga of Sundarakanda of the holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 31

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

एवं बहुविधां चिन्तां चिन्तयित्वा महाकपिः। संश्रवे मधुरं वाक्यं वैदेह्या व्याजहार ह।।5.31.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाकपिः great vanara, एवम् thus, बहुविधाम् in several ways, चिन्ताम् thought, चिन्तयित्वा deliberating in his mind, वैदेह्याः Vaidehi's, संश्रवे hearing, मधुरम् sweet, वाक्यम् words, व्याजहार ह  spoke.

#### Explanation

After deliberating in several ways the great Hanuman spoke these sweet words to Vaidehi:

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

राजा दशरथो नाम रथकुञ्जरवाजिमान्। पुण्यशीलो महाकीर्तिरिक्ष्वाकूणां महायशाः।।5.31.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रथकुञ्जरवाजिमान् with chariots, elephants and horses, पुण्यशीलः foremost in merits, महाकीर्तिः renowned, इक्ष्वाकूणाम् in Ikshvaku race, महायशाः illustrious, दशरथो नाम called Dasaratha, राजा king.

#### Explanation

"There was an illustrious king called Dasaratha in Ikshvaku race, a master of chariots, elephants and horses. He was highly meritorious and renowned.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

राजर्षीणां गुणश्रेष्ठस्तपसा चर्षिभि स्समः। चक्रवर्तिकुले जातः पुरन्दरसमो बले।।5.31.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राजर्षीणाम् among sagelike kings, गुणश्रेष्ठः most virtuous one, तपसा in austerity, ऋषिभिः with sages, समः equal, चक्रवर्तिकुले in a dynasty of emperors, जातः born, बले in might, पुरन्दरसमः crusher of citadels like Indra.

#### Explanation

"He was the most virtuous among royal sages, equal to sages in austerity, born in the race of emperors and equal to Indra in might.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

अहिंसारतिरक्षुद्रो घृणी सत्यपराक्रमः। मुख्यश्चेक्ष्वाकुवंशस्य लक्ष्मीवान् लक्ष्मिवर्धनः।।5.31.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहिंसारतिः committed to nonviolence, अक्षुद्रः magnanimous, घृणी compassionate, सत्यपराक्रमः one whose strength truth, इक्ष्वाकुवंशस्य of Ikshvaku family, मुख्यश्च chief, लक्ष्मीवान् prosperous, लक्ष्मिवर्धनः increaser of riches.

#### Explanation

"He was committed to nonviolence, magnanimous, compassionate and one whose strength was truth. He was not only a prosperous king of Ikshvaku family but also a promoter of prosperity of others too.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

पार्थिवव्यञ्जनैर्युक्तः पृथुश्रीः पार्थिवर्षभः। पृथिव्यां चतुरन्तायां विश्रुतस्सुखदस्सुखी।।5.31.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पार्थिवव्यञ्जनैः with signs of kingship, युक्तः endowed, पृथुश्रीः with vast wealth, पार्थिवर्षभः a bull among rulers, चतुरन्तायाम् surrounded by the sea, पृथिव्याम् on the earth, विश्रुतः well known, सुखदः provider of happiness (to people), सुखी happy man.

#### Explanation

"Endowed with signs of kingship and vast wealth, he was a bull among rulers of the vast earth, surrounded by the sea. A happy king himself, he was wellknown as a provider of happiness.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

तस्य पुत्रः प्रियो ज्येष्ठस्ताराधिपनिभाननः। रामो नाम विशेषज्ञः श्रेष्ठ स्सर्वधनुष्मताम्।।5.31.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तस्य his, प्रियः beloved, ताराधिपनिभाननः of Moon like face, विशेषज्ञः scholar of all knowledge, सर्वधनुष्मताम् among all those wielders of bow, श्रेष्ठः foremost, रामो नाम called Rama, ज्येष्ठः पुत्रः eldest son.

#### Explanation

"His beloved eldest son Rama, whose countenance is like the full moon, is a scholar of all branches of knowledge and is foremost among the bowmen on earth.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

रक्षिता स्वस्य धर्मस्य स्वजनस्य च रक्षिता। रक्षिता जीवलोकस्य धर्मस्य च परन्तपः।।5.31.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

परन्तपः scourage of foes, स्वस्य his, धर्मस्य of righteousness, रक्षिता a  protector, स्वजनस्य of his kins, रक्षिता a protector, जीवलोकस्य of all living creatures, धर्मस्य च and of righteousness, रक्षिता protector.

#### Explanation

"The scourage of enemies, he is a protector of righteousness and of his kins and all living creatures. He is an embodiment of righteousness.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

तस्य सत्याभिसन्धस्य वृद्धस्य वचनात्पितुः। सभार्यस्सह च भ्रात्रा वीरः प्रव्राजितो वनम्।।5.31.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सत्याभिसन्धस्य ever steadfast in truth, वृद्धस्य of the aged, पितुः father, तस्य his, वचनात्  honouring the word, वीरः hero, सभार्यः along with wife, सह च भ्रात्रा and accompanied by his brother, वनम् forest, प्रव्राजितः went as a wanderer.

#### Explanation

"Honouring the word of his aged father who was ever steadfast in truth, Rama left for the forest along with his wife and brother.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

तेन तत्र महारण्ये मृगयां परिधावता। राक्षसा निहताश्शूरा बहवः कामरूपिणः।।5.31.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तत्र there, महारण्ये in the thick forest, मृगयाम् hunting, परिधावता while chasing, तेन by him, शूराः warriors, कामरूपिणः those who can assume any form at their will, बहवः many, राक्षसाः demons, निहताः killed.

#### Explanation

"In the deep forest he went round hunting animals and killing the demon warriors who assumed any form at their free will.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

जनस्थानवधं श्रुत्वा हतौ च खरदूषणौ। ततस्त्वमर्षापहृता जानकी रावणेन तु।।5.31.10।। वञ्चयित्वा वने रामं मृगरूपेण मायया।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, जनस्थानवधम् killing at Janasthana, हतौ killed, खरदूषणौ च Khara and Dooshana, श्रुत्वा having heard, वने in the forest, रामम्  Rama, मृगरूपेण in the form of a deer, मायया by creating illusion, वञ्चयित्वा having deceived, रावणेन by Ravana, जानकी Janaki, अमर्षापहृता abducted out of anger.

#### Explanation

"Ravana having heard about the killing of all demons at Janasthana including Khara and Dooshana in the forest, deceived Rama in the guise of a deer and abducted Janaki in anger.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

स मार्गमाणस्तां देवीं रामस्सीतामनिन्दिताम्।।5.31.11।। आससाद वने मित्रं सुग्रीवं नाम वानरम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः रामः that Rama, अनिन्दिताम् sinless, देवीम् goddesslike lady, तां सीताम् that Sita, मार्गमाणः looking for, वने in the forest, सुग्रीवं नाम called Sugriva, वानरम् monkey, मित्रम् friendship, आससाद met.

#### Explanation

"While looking for the goddesslike lady, the sinless Sita in the forest, Rama met a monkey called Sugriva and made friendship with him

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

तत स्स वालिनं हत्वा रामः परपुरञ्जयः।।5.31.12।। प्रायच्छत्कपिराज्यं तत्सुग्रीवाय महाबलः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, परपुरञ्जयः one who can win over enemy forts, महाबलः powerful, सः रामः that Rama, वालिनम् that Vali, हत्वा having killed, तत् that, कपिराज्यम् kingdom of the monkeys, सुग्रीवाय to Sugriva, प्रायच्छत् offered.

#### Explanation

"Rama, a powerful king, who can win enemy forts killed Vali and offered the monkey kingdom to Sugriva.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

सुग्रीवेणापि सन्दिष्टा हरयः कामरूपिणः।।5.31.13।। दिक्षु सर्वासु तां देवीं विचिन्वन्ति सहस्रशः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सुग्रीवेण by Sugriva, सन्दिष्टाः received orders, कामरूपिणः those who can assume any form, कपयः monkeys, सहस्रशः in thousands, सर्वासु all over, दिक्षु in directions, तां देवीम् that divine queen, विचिन्वन्ति are searching.

#### Explanation

"Having received Sugriva's orders thousands of monkeys who can assume any form went searching for that divine queen in all directions.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

अहं सम्पातिवचनाच्छतयोजनमायतम्।।5.31.14।। अस्या हेतोर्विशालाक्ष्याः सागरं वेगवान्प्लुतः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I am, सम्पातिवचनात् by the words of Sampati, वेगवान् swiftly, अस्याः for her, विशालाक्ष्याः largeeyed, हेतोः for the cause, शतयोजनम् a hundred yojanas, आयतम् wide, सागरम् ocean, प्लुतः leaped.

#### Explanation

"On hearing the words of Sampati I leaped the wide ocean swiftly crossing a hundred yojanas in quest of the largeeyed Sita.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

यथारूपां यथावर्णां यथालक्ष्मीवतीं च निश्चिताम्।।5.31.15।। अश्रौषं राघवस्याहं सेयमासादिता मया।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I, राघवस्य Raghava's, ताम् her, यथारूपाम् of a similar form, यथावर्णाम् of the same complexion, यथालक्ष्मी of similar glow, अश्रौषम् I heard, सा she, इयम् the same one, मया by me, आसादिता has been found.

#### Explanation

"Surely I have seen here a lady of similar body, of similar complexion, of similar glow and beauty as described by Rama".

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

विररामैवमुक्त्वासौ वाचं वानरपुङ्गवः।।5.31.16।। जानकी चापि तच्छ्रुत्वा परं विस्मयमागता।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

असौ he, वानरपुङ्गवः chief of the vanaras, एवम् in that way, वाचम् word, उक्त्वा having spoken, विरराम stopped, जानकी चापि even Janaki, तत् that, श्रुत्वा having heard, परम् great, विस्मयम् wonder, आगता felt.

#### Explanation

Having spoken thus, the great vanara stopped speaking after that. Janaki was wonderstruck by the words of the vanara.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

ततस्सा वक्रकेशान्ता सुकेशी केशसंवृतम्।।5.31.17।। उन्नम्य वदनं भीरुश्शिंशुपावृक्षमैक्षत।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, वक्रकेशान्ता one who had hair curled up at the ends, भीरुः (timid) lady, क्लेशसंवृतम् covered with hanging hair, सा she, वदनम् face, उन्नम्य by raising, शिंशुपावृक्षम् Simsupa tree, ऐक्षत looked up.

#### Explanation

Then Sita looked up at the Simsupa tree timidly, her face covered with beautiful curly hair.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

निशम्य सीता वचनं कपेश्च दिशश्च सर्वाः प्रदिशश्च वीक्ष्य। स्वयं प्रहर्षं परमं जगाम सर्वात्मना राममनुस्मरन्ती ।।5.31.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीता Sita, कपेः Hanuman's, वचनम् word, निशम्य after hearing, सर्वाः all, दिशश्च directions, प्रदिशश्च and quarters, वीक्ष्य after observing, स्वयम् herself, सर्वात्मना self of all, रामम् Rama, अनुस्मरन्ती contemplating on, परमम् supreme, प्रहर्षम् joy, जगाम experienced.

#### Explanation

On hearing Hanuman's words Sita looked at all directions and quarters while quietly contemplating on Rama, the self of all and experienced supreme joy.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

सा तिर्यगूर्ध्वं च तथाप्यधस्तान्निरीक्षमाणा तमचिन्त्यबुद्धिम्। ददर्श पिङ्गाधिपतेरमात्यं वातात्मजं सूर्यमिवोदयस्थम्।।5.31.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सा she, तिर्यक् obliquely, ऊर्ध्वं च vertically, तथापि so also, अथस्तात् downward, निरीक्षमाणा observing, अचिन्त्यबुद्धिम् of unimaginable intelligence, पिङ्गाधिपतेः of king of monkeys, अमात्यम् minister, उदयस्थम् rising, सूर्यमिव like Sun, तं वातात्मजम् that son of the Windgod, ददर्श saw.

#### Explanation

She looked obliquely, up, and down and saw Hanuman of unimaginable intelligence, the minister of the monkey lord, son of the Windgod, who looked like the rising Sun. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये सुन्दरकाण्डे एकत्रिंशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the thirtyfirst sarga of Sundarakanda of the holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 32

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

ततश्शाखान्तरे लीनं दृष्ट्वा चलितमानसा। वेष्टितार्जुनवस्त्रं तं विद्युत्सङ्घातपिङ्गलम्।।5.32.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, शाखान्तरे in between the branches, लीनम् hidden, वेष्टितार्जुनवस्त्रम्  clad in white, विद्युत्सङ्घातपिङ्गलम् of tawny complexion like a cluster of lightnings, तम् him, दृष्ट्वा  seeing, चलितमानसा got perplexed.

#### Explanation

Sita got perplexed on seeing him (Hanuman), who was tawny in complexion and looked like a cluster of lightnings, clad in white  and hidden in between the branches.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

सा ददर्श कपिं तत्र प्रश्रितं प्रियवादिनम्। फुल्लाशोकोत्कराभासं तप्तचामीकरेक्षणम्।।5.32.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सा she, तत्र there, फुल्लाशोकोत्कराभासम् bright like fullyblossomed Ashoka flower, तप्तचामीकरेक्षणम् whose eyes are bright like molten gold, प्रश्रितम् was readily approaching, प्रियवादिनम् one who spoke softly and pleasingly, कपिम् monkey, ददर्श saw.

#### Explanation

There (on the tree) she noticed a monkey like a cluster of fully blossomed Ashoka flower, shining bright, whose eyes were glowing like pure molten gold, speaking softly and pleasingly.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

मैथिली चिन्तयामास विस्मयं परमं गता। अहो भीममिदं रूपं वानरस्य दुरासदम्।।5.32.3।। दुर्निरीक्षमिति ज्ञात्वा पुनरेव मुमोह सा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मैथिली Mythili, परमम् supreme, विस्मयम् astonishment, गता felt, चिन्तयामास began thinking, अहो Oh, वानरस्य vanara's, दुरासदम् inaccessible, इदम् this, रूपम् appearance, भीमम् frightening, दुर्निरीक्षम् impossible to look at, इति thus, ज्ञात्वा thinking, सा she, पुनरेव once again, मुमोह fainted.

#### Explanation

Astonisted Mythili began thinking. 'Oh this vanara's appearance is frightening. He is terrible to look at. He is inaccessible'. Thinking over this again and again, she fainted.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

विललाप भृशं सीता करुणं भयमोहिता।।5.32.4।। रामरामेति दुःखार्ता लक्ष्मणेति च भामिनी। रुरोद बहुधा सीता मन्दं मन्दस्वरा सती।।5.32.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भामिनी a noble lady, सीता Sita, भयमोहिता overcome with fear, दुःखार्ता sorrowful, रामरामेति saying Rama, Rama, लक्ष्मणेति च and Lakshmana also, भृशम् repeatedly, करुणम् pitiably, विललाप cried, सीता Sita, मन्दस्वरा सती in low voice, बहुधा many ways, रुरोद cried.

#### Explanation

Overcome with sorrow and fear, noble Sita sobbed pitiably muttering repeatedly. 'O Rama, O Rama, O Lakshmana'.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

विललाप भृशं सीता करुणं भयमोहिता।।5.32.4।। रामरामेति दुःखार्ता लक्ष्मणेति च भामिनी। रुरोद बहुधा सीता मन्दं मन्दस्वरा सती।।5.32.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भामिनी a noble lady, सीता Sita, भयमोहिता overcome with fear, दुःखार्ता sorrowful, रामरामेति saying Rama, Rama, लक्ष्मणेति च and Lakshmana also, भृशम् repeatedly, करुणम् pitiably, विललाप cried, सीता Sita, मन्दस्वरा सती in low voice, बहुधा many ways, रुरोद cried.

#### Explanation

Overcome with sorrow and fear, noble Sita sobbed pitiably muttering repeatedly. 'O Rama, O Rama, O Lakshmana'.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

सा तं दृष्ट्वा हरिश्रेष्ठं विनीतवदुपस्थितम्। मैथिली चिन्तयामास स्वप्नोऽयमिति भामिनी।।5.32.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भामिनी noble lady, सा मैथिली that Maithili, विनीतवत् humbly, उपस्थितम् stood near, तम् him, हरिश्रेष्ठम् best of vanaras, दृष्ट्वा after seeing, अयम् this, स्वप्नः dream, इति thus, चिन्तयामास began thinking.

#### Explanation

Noble Sita, seeing the best of vanaras who stood near humbly, began reflecting 'Is this a dream'?

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

सा वीक्षमाणा पृथुभुग्नवक्त्रं शाखामृगेन्द्रस्य यथोक्तकारम्। ददर्श पिङ्गाधिपते रमात्यं वातात्मजं बुद्धिमतां वरिष्ठम्।।5.32.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सा she, वीक्षमाणा was looking here and there, पृथुभुग्नवक्त्रम् with a large curved face, शाखामृगेन्द्रस्य of the lord of monkeys, यथोक्तकारम् obedient servant, पिङ्गाधिपते of the king of monkeys, अमात्यम् minister, बुद्धिमताम् among the intelligent, वरिष्ठम् foremost, वातात्मजम् son of the windgod, ददर्श saw.

#### Explanation

Looking here and there, she saw a monkey with a large, curved face, an obedient servant and a minister of the monkeylord, the foremost among the intelligentia and the son of the Windgod.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

सा तं समीक्ष्यैव भृशं विसंज्ञा गतासुकल्पेन बभूव सीता। चिरेण संज्ञां प्रतिलभ्य भूयो विचिन्तयामास विशालनेत्रा।।5.32.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation
