सा सीता that Sita, तम् him, समीक्ष्यैव after looking at, भृशम् greatly, विसंज्ञा lost senses, गतासुकल्पेव as if she was almost dead, बभूव remained, विशालनेत्रा largeeyed, चिरेण after a long time, संज्ञाम् senses, प्रतिलभ्य after getting back, भूयः again, विचिन्तयामास started thinking

#### Explanation

The largeeyed Sita lost her senses looking at him. She got back her senses after a long time and started thinking again:

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

स्वप्ने मयाऽयं विकृतोऽद्य दृष्टश्शाखामृगश्शास्त्रगणैर्निषिद्धः। स्वस्त्यस्तु रामाय स लक्ष्मणाय तथा पितुर्मे जनकस्य राज्ञः।।5.32.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अद्य today, मया by me, विकृतः an ugly form, शास्त्रगणैः by sastras, निषिद्धः prohibited, शाखामृगः monkey, स्वप्ने in a dream, दृष्टः is seen, सलक्ष्मणाय for Lakshmana, रामाय for Rama, तथा so also, मे पितुः my father, जनकस्य राज्ञः of the king Janaka, स्वस्ति auspicious, अस्तु be

#### Explanation

'Today I saw an ugly monkey in my dream. Its sight in a dream is inauspicious according to sastras. Let it be auspicious for Lakshmana and Rama for the sake of my father Janaka.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

स्वप्नोऽपि नायं नहि मेऽस्ति निद्रा शोकेन दुःखेन च पीडितायाः। सुखं हि मे नास्ति यतोऽस्मि हीना तेनेन्दुपूर्णप्रतिमाननेन।।5.32.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अयम् this, स्वप्नोऽपि even in a dream, न not, शोकेन with grief, दुःखेन च and with sorrow, पीडितायाः  a tormented woman, मे  to me, निद्रा sleep, नास्ति हि not there, मे to me, सुखम् pleasure, नास्ति हि not there, यतः therefore, इन्दुपूर्णप्रतिमाननेन whose face is like a fullmoon, तेन with him, हीना devoid, अस्मि I am

#### Explanation

"But no, it was not a dream for tormented by grief and sorrow I cannot have a dream. When separated from the moonfaced Rama I have no sleep or pleasure, how can I have dream?

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

रामेति रामेति सदैव बुद्ध्या विचिन्त्य वाचा ब्रुवती तमेव। तस्यानुरूपां च कथां तदर्थमेवं प्रपश्यामि तथा शृणोमि।।5.32.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामेति रामेति only Rama Rama, सदा ऐव always, बुद्ध्या with my brain, विचिन्त्य after thinking, तमेव him alone, ब्रुवती thinking of, तस्य his, अनुरूपाम् favourable, तदर्थम् for his sake, कथाम् talk, एवम् in this way, प्रपश्यामि I am seeing, तथा so  also, शृणोमि I am hearing.

#### Explanation

"I am always meditating upon Rama and thinking of him alone. I am talking about things related to him. Because of that I see him and hear him.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

अहं हि तस्याद्य मनोभवेन सम्पीडिता तद्गतसर्वभावा। विचिन्तयन्ती सततं तमेव तथैव पश्यामि तथा शृणोमि।।5.32.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अद्य now, अहम् I, तस्य his, मनोभवेन by love for him, सम्पीडिता tormented, तद्गतसर्वभावा all my thoughts immersed in him, सततम् always, तमेव about him only, विचिन्तयन्ती while I am thinking of, तथैव in a similar manner, पश्यामि I am seeing, तथैव only in such a way, शृणोमि I am listening.

#### Explanation

"I am tormented by intense love for Rama, with all my thoughts immersed in him. Since I am constantly thinking of him I see him and hear words about him. (She thinks it is all hallucination.)

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

मनोरथस्स्यादिति चिन्तयामि तथापि बुद्ध्या च वितर्कयामि। किं कारणं तस्य हि नास्ति रूपं सुव्यक्तरूपश्च वदत्ययं माम्।।5.32.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मनोरथः desire, स्यात् may be, इति thus, चिन्तयामि I think, बुद्ध्या च thinking, तथा like that, वितर्कयामि I am deliberating, तस्य its, रूपम् form, नास्ति हि is not seen, अयम् this, सुव्यक्तरूपः seen in a manifested form, माम् by me, वदति speaks, कारणम् cause, किम् what?

#### Explanation

"I feel it is only the desire in my mind. Desire has no form. But the one who is addressing me has a form. I cannot understand this.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

नमोऽस्तु वाचस्पतये सवज्रिणे स्वयंभुवे चैव हुताशनाय च। अनेन चोक्तं यदिदं ममाग्रतो वनौकसा तच्छ तथास्तु नान्यथा।।5.32.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सवज्रिणे along with Indra, वाचस्पतये for Brihaspati, स्वयंभुवे चैव even for the creator Brahma, हुताशनाय for god of fire, च also, नमः salutations, अस्तु may be, अनेन by him, वनौकसा by the resident of the forest, मम अग्रतः in front of me, यत् whatever, इदम् this, उक्तम् spoken, तत् that, तथा true, अस्तु may be, अन्यथा other than that, न not be.

#### Explanation

"My salutations to Indra, Brihaspati, Brahma the creator and also to the firegod. Let all those words spoken by the vanara here in front of me be true and not other than that." इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये सुन्दरकाण्डे द्वात्रिंशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the thirtysecond sarga of Sundarakanda of the holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 33

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

सोऽवतीर्य द्रुमात्तस्माद्विद्रुमप्रतिमाननः। विनीतवेषः कृपणः प्रणिपत्योपसृत्य च।।5.33.1।। तामब्रवीन्महातेजा हनूमान्मारुतात्मजः। शिरस्यञ्जलिमाधाय सीतां मधुरया गिरा।।5.33.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विद्रुमप्रतिमानन: whose face was like the colour of a coral, महातेजाः shining, मारुतात्मजः son of the Windgod, सः हनुमान् Hanuman, तस्मात् from that, द्रुमात् from the tree, अवतीर्य having climbed down, विनीतवेषः dressed in a sober manner, कृपणः piteous, प्रणिपत्य having offered salutations, उपसृत्य went near her, शिरसि on his head, अञ्चलिम् having folded palms, आधाय placed, तां सीताम् her, Sita, मधुरया in sweet, गिरा words, अब्रवीत् spoke.

#### Explanation

Hanuman, son of the Windgod whose face shone like coral, dressed in a sober manner climbed down the tree, went close to Sita, offered salutations with his palms on his head and spoke in sweet words:

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

सोऽवतीर्य द्रुमात्तस्माद्विद्रुमप्रतिमाननः। विनीतवेषः कृपणः प्रणिपत्योपसृत्य च।।5.33.1।। तामब्रवीन्महातेजा हनूमान्मारुतात्मजः। शिरस्यञ्जलिमाधाय सीतां मधुरया गिरा।।5.33.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विद्रुमप्रतिमानन: whose face was like the colour of a coral, महातेजाः shining, मारुतात्मजः son of the Windgod, सः हनुमान् Hanuman, तस्मात् from that, द्रुमात् from the tree, अवतीर्य having climbed down, विनीतवेषः dressed in a sober manner, कृपणः piteous, प्रणिपत्य having offered salutations, उपसृत्य went near her, शिरसि on his head, अञ्चलिम् having folded palms, आधाय placed, तां सीताम् her, Sita, मधुरया in sweet, गिरा words, अब्रवीत् spoke.

#### Explanation

Hanuman, son of the Windgod whose face shone like coral, dressed in a sober manner climbed down the tree, went close to Sita, offered salutations with his palms on his head and spoke in sweet words:

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

का नु पद्मपलाशाक्षि क्लिष्टकौशेयवासिनि। द्रुमस्य शाखामालम्ब्य तिष्ठसि त्वमनिन्दिते।।5.33.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पद्मपलाशाक्षि O one with eyes like lotus petals, क्लिष्टकौशेयवासिनि robed in crumpled silk, अनिन्दिते blameless lady, द्रुमस्य of the tree, शाखाम् branch, आलम्ब्य by holding, तिष्ठसि you are standing, का नु who are you?

#### Explanation

"O blameless lady with eyes like lotus petals, in crumpled silk, holding a branch, and standing, who are you?

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

किमर्थम् तव नेत्राभ्यां वारि स्रवति शोकजम्। पुण्डरीकपलाशाभ्यां विप्रकीर्णमिवोदकम्।।5.33.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पुण्डरीकपलाशाभ्याम् from two petals of white lotus, विप्रकीर्णम् trickled, उदकम् इव like water, तव your, नेत्राभ्याम् from your eyes, शोकजम् tears of grief, वारि water, किमर्थम् for what reason, स्रवति is flowing.

#### Explanation

"Why are tears of grief flowing from your eyes like water trickling down a pair of petals of white lotuses?

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

सुराणामसुराणां वा नागगन्धर्वरक्षसाम्। यक्षाणां किन्नराणां वा का त्वं भवसि शोभने।।5.33.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शोभने O auspicious one, त्वम् you, सुराणाम् among gods, असुराणां वा or demons, नागगन्धर्वरक्षसाम् of nagas, gandharvas, rakshasas, यक्षाणाम् of yakshas, किन्नराणां वा or kinneras, का who, भवसि you are.

#### Explanation

"O noble lady, are you a goddess or a demoness, a naga, a gandharva, a rakshasa, a yaksha or a kinnera?

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

का त्वं भवसि रुद्राणां मरुतां वा वरानने। वसूनां हि वरारोहे देवता प्रतिभासि मे।।5.33.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वरानने O beautiful lady, त्वम् you, रुद्राणाम् of the Rudras, मरुतां वा or even Marutas, वसूनाम् of Vasus, का who, भवसि you are, वरारोहे O fine woman, देवता goddess, मे to me, प्रतिभासि  you appear

#### Explanation

"O beautiful lady Do you belong to the race of Rudras, or Marutas, or Vasus?. O noble lady, you appear a goddess to me.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

कि नु चन्द्रमसा हीना पतिता विबुधालयात्। रोहिणी ज्योतिषां श्रेष्ठा श्रेष्ठसर्वगुणान्विता।।5.33.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

चन्द्रमसा from the Moon, हीना separated, विबुधालयात् from the abode of the gods, पतिता fallen down, ज्योतिषाम् among the luminaries, श्रेष्ठा best one, श्रेष्ठसर्वगुणान्विता who has all best qualities, रोहिणी Rohini किं नु you may be

#### Explanation

"are you Rohini, embellished with best virtues among the constellations? Have you come down from the abode of the luminaries separated from the Moon, her lord?

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

का त्वं भवसि कल्याणि त्वमनिन्दितलोचने। कोपाद्वा यदि वा मोहाद्भर्तारमसितेक्षणे।।5.33.8।। वसिष्ठं कोपयित्वा त्वं नासि कल्याण्यरुन्धती।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

असितेक्षणे O lady of lovely black eyes, त्वम् you, कोपाद्वा or out of anger, यदि वा or else, मोहात् out of delusion, भर्तारम् husband, वसिष्ठम् Vasistha, कोपयित्वा making him angry, कल्याणि O auspicious lady, अरुन्धती Arundhati, नासि are you not

#### Explanation

"O lady of lovely black eyes who are you? Have you by any chance offended your husband out of anger or out of delusion? Are you the auspicious Arundhati dislodged for making your lord Vasistha angry?

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

को नु पुत्रः पिता भ्राता भर्ता वा ते सुमध्यमे।।5.33.9।। अस्माल्लोकादमुं लोकं गतं त्वमनुशोचसि।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सुमध्यमे O woman of beautiful waist, अस्मात् from this, लोकात् from the world, अमुं लोकम् to this world, गतम् dead, त्वम् your, अनुशोचसि worrying about, ते your, पुत्रः son, पिता father, भ्राता brother, भर्ता वा or is it husband, को नु is he?

#### Explanation

"O woman of beautiful waist from which world have you come here? For whom are you worrying? Who is your son, father, brother or husband? Are you worrying about some one dead?.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

रोदनादतिनिश्श्वासाद्भूमिसंस्पर्शनादपि।।5.33.10।। न त्वां देवीमहं मन्ये राज्ञ स्सर्वज्ञावधारणात्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रोदनात् from your weeping, अतिनिश्श्वासात् breathing heavily, भूमिसंस्पर्शनादपि your touching the ground, राज्ञः royal, संज्ञावधारणात् having signs of sovereignty, त्वाम् you, देवीम् O goddess, अहम् I, न मन्ये I do not think.

#### Explanation

"From your weeping, your sighing and your touching the earth (gods do not touch the earth) and your signs of sovereignty, I do not think you are a goddess (meaning otherwise she posessed divine grace).

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

व्यञ्जनानि च ते यानि लक्षणानि च लक्षये।।5.33.11।। महिषी भूमिपालस्य राजकन्या च मे मता।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ते your, यानि those, व्यञ्जनानि signs, लक्षणानि च also qualities, लक्षये I see, भूमिपालस्य king's, महिषी consort, राजकन्या च and a princess, मे to me, मता it is felt.

#### Explanation

"On the basis of your royal signs and other qualities I infer that you are the daughter of a king or his consort.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

रावणेन जनस्थानाद्बलादपदहृता यदि।।5.33.12।। सीता त्वमसि भद्रं ते तन्ममाचक्ष्व पृच्छतः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्वम् you, जनस्थानात् from Janasthana, रावणेन by Ravana, बलात् forcibly, अपहृता borne away, सीता Sita, असि यदि if you are, तत् that, पृच्छतः  enquiring, मम to me, आचक्ष्व you tell, ते भद्रम् wish you well.

#### Explanation

"If you are Sita, who was forcefully borne away by Ravana from Janasthana you may kindly reveal to me freely. May god bless you.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

यथा हि तव वै दैन्यं रूपं चाप्यतिमानुषम्।।5.33.13।। तपसा चान्वितो वेषस्त्वं राममहिषी ध्रुवम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तव your, दैन्यम् plight, अतिमानुषम् superhuman, रूपं च and your form, तपसा with austerity, अन्वितः endowed, वेषः your dress, यथा as much, त्वम् you are, ध्रुवम् surely, राममहिषी Rama's queen.

#### Explanation

"Indeed your plight, the superhuman beauty and your robes marked with asceticism, surely tell me that you are Rama's queen."

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

सा तस्य वचनं श्रुत्वा रामकीर्तनहर्षिता।।5.33.14।। उवाच वाक्यं वैदेही हनुमन्तं द्रुमाश्रितम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सा वैदेही that Vaidehi, तस्य his, वचनम् word, श्रुत्वा having heard, रामकीर्तनहर्षिता happy to hear words of praise of Rama, द्रुमाश्रितम् on the tree, हनुमन्तम् Hanuman, वाक्यम् these words, उवाच spoke.

#### Explanation

Happy to have heard words of praise of Rama, Vaidehi spoke this to Hanuman, who got up the tree:

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

पृथिव्यां राजसिंहानां मुख्यस्य विदितात्मनः।।5.33.15।। स्नुषा दशरथस्याहं शत्रुसैन्यप्रतापिनः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I am, पृथिव्याम् on earth, राजसिंहानाम् among great kings, मुख्यस्य foremost, विदितात्मनः of the knower of self, शत्रुसैन्यप्रतापिनः of the slayer of enemy army, दशरथस्य Dasaratha's, स्नुषा daughterinlaw.

#### Explanation

"I am daughterinlaw of king Dasaratha, knower of the self, the foremost among kings on earth and a slayer of enemy army.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

दुहिता जनकस्याहं वैदेहस्य महात्मनः।।5.33.16।। सीतेति नाम नाम्नाऽहं भार्या रामस्य धीमतः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I am, वैदेहस्य Videha's, महात्मनः great soul, जनकस्य Janaka's, दुहिता daughter, नाम्ना सीतेति नाम Sita by name, अहम् I am, धीमतः of the sagacious, रामस्य Rama's, भार्या wife.

#### Explanation

"I am daughter of the great soul, Janaka, king of Videha, known as Sita. I am the wife of that sagacious Rama.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

समा द्वादश तत्राहं राघवस्य निवेशने।।5.33.17।। भुञ्जाना मानुषान्भोगान्सर्वकामसमृद्धिनी।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I am, तत्र there, राघवस्य Rama's, निवेशने the abode, मानुषान् mortal, भोगान् pleasures, भुञ्जाना experiencing, सर्वकामसमृद्धिनी all kinds of pleasures provided, द्वादश twelve, समाः years.

#### Explanation

"I enjoyed worldly pleasures in abundance for twelve years at Rama's abode.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

तत्र त्रयोदशे वर्षे राज्येनेक्ष्वाकुनन्दनम्।।5.33.18।। अभिषेचयितुं राजा सोपाध्यायः प्रचक्रमे।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, त्रयोदशे in the thirteenth, वर्षे in the year, सोपाध्यायः with his preceptor, राजा the king, इक्ष्वाकुनन्दनम् delight of the Ikshvakus, राज्येन in the kingdom, अभिषेचयितुम् to coronate, प्रचक्रमे arranged.

#### Explanation

"Then in the thirteenth year king Dasaratha along with the royal preceptor arranged to  coronate Rama, delight of the Ikshvaku family, in the kingdom.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

तस्मिन्सम्भ्रियमाणे तु राघवस्याभिषेचने।।5.33.19।। कैकयी नाम भर्तारं देवी वचनमब्रवीत्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तस्मिन् in that, राघवस्य Rama's, अभिषेचने in the coronation, सम्भ्रियमाणे while preparations were being made, कैकेयी नाम called Kaikeyi, देवी queen, भर्तारम् to her husband, वचनम् these words, अब्रवीत् spoke.

#### Explanation

"While preparations for the coronation were on, queen Kaikeyi said to her husband:

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

न पिबेयं न खादेयं प्रत्यहं मम भोजनम्।।5.33.20।। एष मे जीवितस्यान्तो रामो यद्यभिषिच्यते।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

न पिबेयम् I shall not drink, प्रत्यहम् daily, भोजनम् food, न खादेयम् I will not eat, रामः Rama, यदि if, अभिषिच्यते is coronated, एषः this is, मे my, जीवितस्य life's, अन्तः end.

#### Explanation

'I shall not drink or eat food, if Rama is coronated. I shall end my life this moment.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

यत्तदुक्तं त्वया वाक्यं प्रीत्या नृपतिसत्तम।।5.33.21।। तच्छेन्न वितथं कार्यं वनं गच्छतु राघवः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नृपतिसत्तम the foremost among kings, त्वया by you, प्रीत्या lovingly, यत् such, तत् वाक्यम् words, उक्तम् spoken, तत् the same, वितथम् false, न कार्यं that promise, यदि if you please, राघवः Rama, वनम् to the forest, गच्छतु  go.

#### Explanation

'O Dasaratha, the foremost among kings if the promise lovingly made by you earlier is not going to be false, Rama should go to the forest'.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

स राजा सत्यवाग्देव्या वरदानमनुस्मरन्।।5.33.22।। मुमोह वचनं श्रुत्वा कैकेय्याः क्रूरमप्रियम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सत्यवाक् ever truthful, सः राजा that king, देव्याः to the queen, वरदानम् boons, अनुस्मरन् remembering, कैकेय्याः of Kaikeyi, क्रूरम् cruel, अप्रियम् unpleasant, वचनम् word, श्रुत्वा having heard, मुमोह fainted.

#### Explanation

"Remembering the boons the ever truthful king had granted to Kaikeyi, he fainted after hearing her cruel and harsh words.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

ततस्तु स्थविरो राजा सत्ये धर्मे व्यवस्थितः।।5.33.23।। ज्येष्ठं यशस्विनं पुत्रं रुदन्राज्यमयाचत।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, सत्ये by truth, धर्मे by rigteousness, व्यवस्थितः one who held, स्थविरः aged, राजा king, रुदन् while crying, यशस्विनम् glorious one, ज्येष्ठम् eldest, पुत्रम् son, राज्यम् kingdom, अयाचत begged.

#### Explanation

"The aged king who held on to truthfulness and righteousness begged his famed eldest son Rama to return the kingdom.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

स पितुर्वचनं श्रीमानभिषेकात्परं प्रियम्।।5.33.24।। मनसा पूर्वमासाद्य वाचा प्रतिगृहीतवान्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

श्रीमान् illustrious one, सः he, पितुः father's, वचनम् word, अभिषेकात् more than coronation, परम् supreme, प्रियम् dear, मनसा by his mind, पूर्वम् first, आसाद्य having accepted, वाचा by word, प्रतिगृहीतवान् accepted

#### Explanation

"The illustrious Rama who valued his father's word more than sovereignty, bowed to the command of the father.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

दद्यान्न प्रतिगृह्णीयान्न ब्रूयात्किञ्चिदप्रियम्।।5.33.25।। अपि जीवितहेतोर्वा रामस्सत्यपराक्रमः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सत्यपराक्रमः one whose truthfulness was his strength, रामः Rama, दद्यात् he give,न प्रतिगृह्णीयात् he will not take, जीवितहेतोर्वा even for his life, किञ्चित् even a little, अप्रियम् harsh, न ब्रूयात् he will not speak .

#### Explanation

"Rama, whose strength was his truthfulness, gave whatever others sought but would not accept anything in return. He would not speak a harsh word.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

स विहायोत्तरीयाणि महार्हाणि महायशाः।।5.33.26।। विसृज्य मनसा राज्यं जनन्यै मां समादिशत्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महायशाः a highly renowned, सः he, महार्हाणि luxurious, उत्तरीयाणि upper garments, विहाय cast off, मनसा whole heartedly, राज्यम् kingdom, विसृज्य having given up, माम् me, जनन्यै to his mother, समादिशत् entrusted.

#### Explanation

"Having given up the kingdom  voluntarily, Rama, a highly renowned king cast off his luxurious upper garments and asked me to take care of his mother.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

साहं तस्याग्रतस्तूर्णं प्रस्थिता वनचारिणी।।5.33.27।। न हि मे तेन हीनाया वासस्स्वर्गेऽपि रोचते।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सा अहम् I myself, वनचारिणी a forestdweller, तूर्णम् quickly, तस्य his, अग्रतः ahead of him, प्रस्थिता started, तेन by him, हीनायाः separated, मे  me, स्वर्गेऽपि न रोचते did not prefer even heaven.

#### Explanation

"I, however resolved to live in the forest, marched ahead of him quickly as I did not prefer even heaven without him.

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

प्रागेव तु महाभागस्सौमित्रिर्मित्रनन्दनः।।5.33.28।। पूर्वजस्यानुयात्रार्थे द्रुमचीरैरलङ्कृतः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाभागः a noble one, मित्रनन्दनः a delight to friends, सौमित्रिः Saumitri, प्रागेव already, पूर्वजस्य eldest brother's, अनुयात्रार्थे to follow him, द्रुमचीरैः an bark, अलङ्कृतः dressed.

#### Explanation

None

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

ते वयं भर्तुरादेशं बहुमान्य दृढव्रताः।।5.33.29।। प्रविष्टास्स्म पुरादृष्टं वनं गम्भीरदर्शनम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ते then वयम् we, भर्तुः king's, आदेशम् order, बहुमान्य respecting, दृढव्रताः resolved firmly, पुरा in the past, अदृष्टम् not seen, गम्भीरदर्शनम् impenetrable, वनम् forest, प्रविष्टाः स्मः we entered.

#### Explanation

"Respecting the king's order and determined firmly, we entered the impenetrable forest.

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

वसतो दण्डकारण्ये तस्याहममितौजसः।।5.33.30।। रक्षसाऽपहृता भार्या रावणेन दुरात्मना।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दण्डकारण्ये in the Dandaka forest, वसतः while living, अमितौजसः the most valiant, तस्य his, भार्या wife, अहम् am, दुरात्मना by evilminded, रावणेन by Ravana, अपहृता was abducted.

#### Explanation

"While the most valiant Rama was living in the Dandaka forest, I, his wife was borne away by the evilminded Ravana.

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

द्वौ मासौ तेन मे कालो जीवितानुग्रहः कृतः।।5.33.31।। ऊर्ध्वं द्वाभ्यां तु मासाभ्यां ततस्तक्ष्यामि जीवितम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तेन by him, द्वौ two, मासौ months, मे to me, जीवितानुग्रहः grant of time to live, कृतः given, ततः then, द्वाभ्याम् after the two, मासाभ्याम् months, ऊर्ध्वम् after that, जीवितम् life, त्यक्ष्यामि I will give up.

#### Explanation

"He has allowed me two months time to live. After two months I will have to give up my life." इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये सुन्दरकाण्डे त्रयस्त्रिंशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the thirtythird sarga of Sundarakanda of the holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 34

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

तस्यास्तद्वचनं श्रुत्वा हनुमान्हरिपुङ्गवः। दुःखाद्दुःखाभिभूताया स्सान्त्वमुत्तरमब्रवीत्।।5.34.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हरिपुङ्गवः monkey leader, हनुमान् Hanuman, दुःखात् with sadness, दुःखाभिभूतायाः overwhelmed with grief, तस्याः to her, तत् those, वचनम् words, श्रुत्वा after hearing, सान्त्वम् comforting, उत्तरम् reply, अब्रवीत् spoke.

#### Explanation

Hearing the words of Sita who was overwhelmed with grief, the monkey leader Hanuman felt sad and thus spoke comforting her:

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

अहं रामस्य सन्देशाद्देवि दूतस्तवागतः। वैदेहि कुशली रामस्त्वां च कौशलमब्रवीत्।।5.34.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

देवि O queen, वैदेहि Vaidehi, अहम् I am, रामस्य Rama's, सन्देशात् by order, तव your, दूतः messenger, आगतः reached, कुशली is well, रामः Rama, त्वां च and you, कुशलम् welfare, अब्रवीत् wished you.

#### Explanation

"By the command of Rama I am here as a messenger. He is keeping well. He is well and wishes you well.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

यो ब्रह्ममस्त्रं वेदांश्च वेद वेदविदांवरः। स त्वां दाशरथी रामो देवि कौशलमब्रवीत्।।5.34.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

देवि O godlike lady, वेदविदाम् mastered the Vedas, वरः choicest, यः he is, ब्रह्मम् अस्त्रम् weapon of Brahma (Brahmastra), वेदांश्च and Vedas, वेद knows, सः he, दाशरथिः son of Dasaratha, रामः Rama, त्वाम् you, कौशलम् welfare, अब्रवीत् conveyed.

#### Explanation

"O godlike lady Rama, son of Dasaratha, master of the Vedas, wielder of Brahmastra wishes you well.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

लक्ष्मणश्च महातेजा भर्तुस्तेऽनुचरः प्रियः। कृतवान्शोकसन्तप्तश्शिरसा तेऽभिवादनम्।।5.34.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महातेजाः very mighty, ते भर्तुः your husband, प्रियः dear, अनुचरः follower, लक्ष्मणश्च Lakshmana also, शोकसन्तप्तः immersed in grief, शिरसा with his head in obeisance, ते to you, अभिवादनम् offers salutations, कृतवान् has done.

#### Explanation

"The mighty Lakshmana too, a dear brother and follower of your husband is immersed in grief, and sends salutations to you."

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

सा तयोः कुशलं देवी निशम्य नरसिंहयोः। प्रीतिसंहृष्टसर्वाङ्गी हनुमन्तमथाब्रवीत्।।5.34.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सा देवी that queen, तयोः of both of them, नरसिंहयोः of lions among men, कुशलम् wellbeing, निशम्य on hearing, प्रीतिसंहृष्टसर्वाङ्गी overtaken by joy experienced in all her limbs, अथ then, हनुमन्तम् Hanuman, अब्रवीत् spoke.

#### Explanation

On hearing the wellbeing of Rama and Lakshman, lions among men, Sita experienced thrill all over her body. She then  spoke to Hanuman:

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

कल्याणी बत गाथेयं लौकिकी प्रतिभाति मा। एति जीवन्तमानन्दो नरं वर्षशतादपि।।5.34.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

जीवन्तम् who lives, नरम् human being, वर्षशतादपि a hundred years, आनन्दः happiness, एति comes, इयम् this, लौकिकी popular, गाथा adage, कल्याणी is auspicious, मा to me, प्रतिभाति appears.

#### Explanation

"The adage that 'joy comes to a living being even though it be at the end of  a hundred years' is popular. It appears true in my case".

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

तया समागते तस्मिन्प्रीतिरुत्पादिताऽद्भुता। परस्परेण चालापं विश्वस्तौ तौ प्रचक्रतुः।।5.34.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

समागते when the meeting took place, तस्मिन् in him, तथा like that, अद्भुता wonderful, प्रीतिः pleasure, उत्पादिता was created, तौ in both, विश्वस्तौ confiding in the other, परस्परेण each to the other, आलापं च converse, चक्रतुः started.

#### Explanation

Hanuman thus created a sense of wonderful joy in her as they came together. Both of them started to converse restoring confidence in each other.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

तस्यास्तद्वचनं श्रुत्वा हनुमान्हरियूथपः। सीतायाश्शोकदीनायास्समीपमुपचक्रमे।।5.34.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हरियूथपः leader of the monkey troops, हनुमान् Hanuman, शोकदीनायाः of the piteous lady stricken with grief, तस्याः her, सीतायाः Sita's, तत् that, वचनम् word, श्रुत्वा after hearing, समीपम् close by, उपचक्रमे moved.

#### Explanation

Hearing the reply of Sita, who was crying piteously, stricken with grief, the monkey leader, Hanuman moved close to her.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

यथा यथा समीपं स हनुमानुपसर्पति। तथा तथा रावणं सा तं सीता परिशङ्कते।।5.34.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः हनुमान् Hanuman, यथा यथा slowly that way, समीपम् near, उपसर्पति started moving, तथा तथा in the same way, सा सीता that Sita, तम् him, रावणम् Ravana, परिशङ्कते suspected.

#### Explanation

As Hanuman started slowly drawing close, Sita suspected he might be Ravana.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

अहो धिग्दुष्कृतमिदं कथितं हि यदस्य मे। रूपान्तरमुपागम्य स एवायं हि रावणः।।5.34.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहो Oh, धिक् fie upon, मे to myself, इदम् this, अस्य to him, कथितम् story, दुष्कृतम् that which should not have been said, अयम् he, रूपान्तरम् in cognito, उपागम्य has come, सः रावणः हि verily he is Ravana.

#### Explanation

"Ohshame on me. I have told him my story which should not have been said. This is verily Ravana come in a disguise".

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

तामशोकस्य शाखां सा विमुक्त्वा शोककर्शिता। तस्यामेवानवद्याङ्गी धरण्यां समुपाविशत्।।5.34.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अनवद्याङ्गी who has flawless limbs, सा she, अशोकस्य Ashoka tree's, ताम् that, शाखाम् branch, विमुक्त्वा later leaving, शोककर्शिता emaciated out of sorrow, तस्याम् on that, धरण्यामेव on the ground, समुपाविशत् sat near.

#### Explanation

So saying Sita of flawless limbs, emaciated with grief, left the branch of Ashoka tree held by her and squatted on the ground.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

हनुमानपि दुःखार्तां तां दृष्ट्वा भयमोहिताम्। अवन्दत महाबाहुस्ततस्तां जनकात्मजाम्।।5.34.12।। सा चैनं भयवित्रस्ता भूयो नैवाभ्युदैक्षत।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाबाहुः longarmed one, हनुमानपि Hanuman too, दुःखार्ताम् sorrowful lady, भयमोहिताम् deluded with fear, ताम् her, दृष्ट्वा on seeing, ततः then, तां जनकात्मजाम् the daughter of Janaka, अवन्दत prostrated, सा च and she, भयवित्रस्ता trembling with fear, एनम् him, भूयः again, नाभ्युदैक्षत did not look at him.

#### Explanation

The longarmed Hanuman saw Sita, who was stricken with grief and deluded with fear. He prostrated to her. But out of fear she did not look at him.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

तं दृष्ट्वा वन्दमानं तु सीता शशिनिभानना।।5.34.13।। अब्रवीद्धीर्घमुच्छवस्य वानरं मधुरस्वरा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शशिनिभानना moonfaced lady, सीता Sita, तम् him, वन्दमानम् prostrating, दृष्ट्वा on seeing, दीर्घम् deep, उच्छवस्य after breathing out, मधुरस्वरा in a sweet voice, वानरम् to vanara, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

On seeing the vanara prostrated, the moonfaced lady, sighed deeply and spoke to him in a sweet voice:

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

मायां प्रविष्टो मायावी यदि त्वं रावणस्स्वयम्।।5.34.14।। उत्पादयसि मे भूयस्सन्तापं तन्न शोभनम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्वम् you, मायाम् disguised, प्रविष्टः entered, मायावी trickster, स्वयम् yourself, रावणः यदि if you are Ravana, मे to me, भूयः again, सन्तापम् grief, उत्पादयसि you have created, तत् that, शोभनम् न not good.

#### Explanation

"If you are that trickster Ravana who appears incognito before me, it is not good for you. You are causing grief again.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

स्वं परित्यज्य रूपं यः परिव्राजकरूपध्रुत्। जनस्थाने मया दृष्टस्त्वं स एवासि रावणः।।5.34.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

यः रावणः that Ravana, स्वं रूपम् his own form, परित्यज्य after giving up, परिव्राजकरूपवान् taking the form of a mendicant, जनस्थाने in Janasthana, मया by me, दृष्टः seen, त्वं you, स एव असि you are the same one.

#### Explanation

"You are the same Ravana who took the guise of a mendicant, giving up your real form and came to Janasthana.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

उपवासकृशां दीनां कामरूप निशाचर। सन्तापयसि मां भूयस्सन्तप्तां तन्न शोभनम्।।5.34.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कामरूप one who can assume any form, निशाचर nightstalker, उपवासकृशां an emaciated lady, सन्तप्तां a lady stricken with grief, दीनां pitiable, माम् me, भूयः again, सन्तापं grief, सन्तापयसि you are making me sorrowful, तत् that, शोभनं न not good for you.

#### Explanation

"Oh you are one who can assume any form at will. You are a nightstalker. It is not good for you to cause pain to me who is already emaciated through fasting, who is pitiable and who is already afflicted.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

अथवा नैतदेवं हि यन्मया परिशङ्कितम्।।5.34.17।। मनसो हि मम प्रीतिरुत्पन्ना तव दर्शनात्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथवा or may be, मया by me, यत् that which, परिशङ्कितम् that I am suspecting, एतत् because, एवम् all this, न हि not true, तव your, दर्शनात् by your presence, मम my, मनसः mind, प्रीतिः pleased, उत्पन्ना हि is generated.

#### Explanation

"May be my suspicion is not correct Because I am experiencing pleasure in my mind in your presence.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

यदि रामस्य दूतस्त्वमागतो भद्रमस्तु ते।।5.34.18।। पृच्छामि त्वां हरिश्रेष्ठ प्रिया रामकथा हि मे।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्वम् you, रामस्य Rama's, दूतः messenger, आगतः यदि if you have come here, ते to you, भद्रम् be auspicious, अस्तु be, हरिश्रेष्ठ O great vanara, मे to me, रामकथा Rama'story, प्रिया हि dear, त्वाम् you, पृच्छामि I ask you.

#### Explanation

"O great vanara, If on the other hand you have come here as Rama's messenger I wish you well. I love Rama's story. Tell me.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

गुणान्रामस्य कथय प्रियस्य मम वानर।।5.34.19।। चित्तं हरसि मे सौम्य नदीकूलं यथा रयः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वानर vanara, मम my, प्रियस्य dear lord's, रामस्य Rama's, गुणान् virtues, कथय you may tell me, सौम्य O gentle one, नदीकूलम् river bank, रयः यथा like the current of water, मे my, चित्तम् mind, हरसि you are luring.

#### Explanation

"O vanara Rama's virtues are dear to me to hear. O gentle one just as the current of the river wears away its banks, my mind is lured by your presence.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

अहो स्वप्नस्य सुखता याहमेवं चिराहृता।।5.34.20।। प्रेषितं नाम पश्यामि राघवेण वनौकसम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

स्वप्नस्य of a dream, सुखता pleasure, अहो Oh, चिराहृता kept dreaming for a long time, या that, राघवेण by Rama, प्रेषितं नाम  person sent, वनौकसम् vanara, एवम् that way, पश्यामि I am seeing.

#### Explanation

"Oh I have been seeing the person sent by Rama in my dream for a long time. This is a pleasant dream to me.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

स्वप्नेऽपि यद्यहं वीरं राघवं सहलक्ष्मणम्।।5.34.21।। पश्येयं नावसीदेयं स्वप्नोऽपि मम मत्सरी।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I, स्वप्नेऽपि even in a dream, सहलक्ष्मणम् along with Lakshmana, वीरम् heroic, राघवम् Rama, पश्येयं यदि if I can see, नावसीदेयम् I will not be despondent, मम my, स्वप्नोऽपि even the dream, मत्सरी inimical to me.

#### Explanation

"Even if I can see the heroic Rama together with Lakshmana in my dream my pain will be gone. Even the dream is inimical to me (I cannot sleep and thus cannot dream.)

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

नाहं स्वप्नमिमं मन्ये स्वप्ने दृष्ट्वा हि वानरम्।।5.34.22।। न शक्योऽभ्युदयः प्राप्तुं प्राप्तश्चाभ्युदयो मम।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I am, इमम् this, स्वप्नम् dream, न मन्ये I do not think so, स्वप्ने in a dream, वानरम् vanara, दृष्ट्वा having seen, अभ्युदयः welfare, प्राप्तुम् to attain, न शक्यः not possible, मम to me, अभ्युदयः pleasure, प्राप्तश्च is experienced.

#### Explanation

"I do not think it is a dream. Pleasure cannot be experienced by seeing a vanara in a dream. I experience pleasure now (seeing a monkey in a dream is a bad omen).

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

किन्नु स्याचित्तमोहोऽयं भवेद्वातगतिस्त्वियम्।।5.34.23।। उन्मादजो विकारो वा स्यादियं मृगतृष्णिका।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अयम् this, चित्तमोहः delusion in the mind, स्यात् किं नु why do I feel like this? इयम् this kind of, वातगतिः mental inbalance, भवेत् may be, उन्मादजः developed due to madness, विकारो वा or else is it a change?, इयम् this, मृगतृष्णिका a mirage, स्यात् may be.

#### Explanation

"Can this be a delusion of my mind or a mental imbalance (resulting in movement of the mind in the body). Is it a malady born of  my madness? Or is it a mirage?

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

अथवा नायमुन्मादो मोहोऽप्युन्मादलक्षणः।।5.34.24।। सम्बुध्ये चाहमात्मानमियं चापि वनौकसम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथवा if not, अयम् this, उन्मादः insanity, उन्मादलक्षणः sign of insanity, मोहोऽपि delusion also, न not, अहम् I, आत्मानम् myself, इमम् this, वनौकसम् vanara, सम्बुध्ये recognise.

#### Explanation

"No, this cannot be insanity. nor even delusion. But I recognise this vanara well"

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

इत्येवं बहुधा सीता सम्प्रधार्य बलाबलम्।।5.34.25।। रक्षसां कामरूपत्वान्मेने तं राक्षसाधिपम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीता Sita, इत्येवम् in this manner, बलाबलम् strength and weakness, बहुधा in many ways, सम्प्रधार्य after considering, रक्षसाम् of demons, कामरूपत्वात्  taking any form at will, तम् him, राक्षसाधिपम् king of demons, मेने he thought.

#### Explanation

Considering the strength and weakness of demons in that manner Sita thought that Hanuman was none other than the king of demons who can asume any form at will.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

एतां बुद्धिं तदा कृत्वा सीता सा तनुमध्यमा।।5.34.26।। न प्रतिव्याजहाराथ वानरं जनकात्मजा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, तनुमध्यमा lady with a fair waist, जनकात्मजा Janaka's daughter, सा सीता that Sita, एताम् in this way, बुद्धिम् thought, कृत्वा having entertained, अथ then, वानरम् to vanara, न प्रतिव्याजहार did not respond.

#### Explanation

Having entertained such thoughts, Sita, the lady with a fairwaist, daughter of Janaka,  did not respond to the vanara.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

सीतायाश्चिन्तितं बुद्ध्वा हनुमान्मारुतात्मजः।।5.34.27।। श्रोत्रानुकूलैर्वचनैस्तदा तां संप्रहर्षयत्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मारुतात्मजः son of the Windgod, हनुमान् Hanuman, सीतायाः Sita's, चिन्तितम् thought, बुद्ध्वा understood, तदा then, ताम् her, श्रोत्रानुकूलैः pleasing to hear, वचनैः by the words, सम्प्रहर्षयत् pleased.

#### Explanation

Hanuman, son of the Windgod understood what was going on in Sita's mind and spoke pleasing words that brought joy in her.

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

आदित्य इव तेजस्वी लोककान्तश्शशी यथा।।5.34.28।। राजा सर्वस्य लोकस्य देवो वैश्रवणो यथा। विक्रमेणोपपन्नश्च यथा विष्णुर्महायशाः।।5.34.29।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

आदित्य इव like the Sun, तेजस्वी glorious, शशी यथा like the Moon, लोककान्तः brings delight to the whole world, देवः god, वैश्रवणो यथा like Vaisravana, सर्वस्य of all, लोकस्य of the worlds, राजा king, महायशाः is renowned, विष्णुः यथा like Visnu, विक्रमेण in valour, उपपन्नश्च endowed .

#### Explanation

"Rama is glorious as the Sun, brings delight to the whole world like the Moon, is the king of kings like Vaisravana,  and renowned like Visnu endowed with valour.

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

आदित्य इव तेजस्वी लोककान्तश्शशी यथा।।5.34.28।। राजा सर्वस्य लोकस्य देवो वैश्रवणो यथा। विक्रमेणोपपन्नश्च यथा विष्णुर्महायशाः।।5.34.29।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

आदित्य इव like the Sun, तेजस्वी glorious, शशी यथा like the Moon, लोककान्तः brings delight to the whole world, देवः god, वैश्रवणो यथा like Vaisravana, सर्वस्य of all, लोकस्य of the worlds, राजा king, महायशाः is renowned, विष्णुः यथा like Visnu, विक्रमेण in valour, उपपन्नश्च endowed .

#### Explanation

"Rama is glorious as the Sun, brings delight to the whole world like the Moon, is the king of kings like Vaisravana,  and renowned like Visnu endowed with valour.

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

सत्यवादी मधुरवाग्देवो वाचस्पतिर्यथा। रूपवान्सुभग श्रीमान् कन्दर्प इव मूर्तिमान्।।5.34.30।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सत्यवादी truthful in speech, देवः god, वाचस्पतिर्यथा like Brihaspati, मधुरवाक् sweet tongued, रूपवान् handsome, सुभगः graceful, श्रीमान् prosperous, मूर्तिमान् a personification, कन्दर्पः इव like Kamadeva.

#### Explanation

"He is truthful in speech like Brihaspati, prosperous (like Kubera), sweettongued, handsome, graceful like Kamadeva.

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

स्थानक्रोधः प्रहर्ता च श्रेष्ठो लोके महारथः। बाहुच्छायामवष्टब्धो यस्य लोको महात्मनः।।5.34.31।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

स्थानक्रोधः who shows anger to the proper person, प्रहर्ता च who punishes, लोके the world, श्रेष्ठः supreme, महारथः great charioteer, लोकः in the world, यस्य whose, महात्मनः of the great self, बाहुच्छायामवष्टब्धो under the shadow of whose shoulders refuge is taken.

#### Explanation

"He shows anger to one who deserves it, he is the foremost charioteer of the world and he is a great self under the shadow of whose shoulders the whole world takes refuge.

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

अपकृष्याश्रमपदान्मृगरूपेण राघवम्। शून्ये येनापनीतासि तस्य द्रक्ष्यसि यत्फलम्।।5.34.32।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

येन since, राघवम् Rama, मृगरूपेण in the form of a deer, आश्रमपदात् from the hermitage, अपकृष्य were abducted, शून्ये isolated place, अपनीता you are borne away, असि you, तस्य its, यत्फलम् result, द्रक्ष्यसि you will see.

#### Explanation

"You will see the consequences of the action of Ravana who abducted you deceitfully in the form of deer when Rama was away from the hermitage.

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

न चिराद्रावणं संख्ये यो वधिष्यति वीर्यवान्। रोषप्रमुक्तैरिषुभिर्ज्वलद्भिरिव पावकैः।।5.34.33।। तेनाहं प्रेषितो दूत स्त्वत्सकाशमिहागतः। त्वद्वियोगेन दुःखार्त स्स त्वां कौशलमब्रवीत्।।5.34.34।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वीर्यवान् heroic, यः who, नचिरात् soon, रोषप्रमुक्तै: released in great anger, ज्वलद्भिः by burning, पावकैः इव like fire, इषुभि with arrows, संख्ये in war, रावणम् Ravana, वधिष्यति would slay, तेन therefore, दूतः envoy, प्रेषितः sent, इह here, त्वत्सकाशम् your presence, आगतः I have come, त्वद्वियोगेन due to separation from you, दुःखार्तः filled with sorrow, सः he, त्वाम् to you, कौशलम् welfare, अब्रवीत् asking.

#### Explanation

"Heroic Rama will soon release many scorching, fiery arrows in anger and kill Ravana. I seek your permission as an envoy of Rama. Filled with grief by your separation, he has made inquiries about your welfare.

---

### Shloka 34

#### Shloka Text

न चिराद्रावणं संख्ये यो वधिष्यति वीर्यवान्। रोषप्रमुक्तैरिषुभिर्ज्वलद्भिरिव पावकैः।।5.34.33।। तेनाहं प्रेषितो दूत स्त्वत्सकाशमिहागतः। त्वद्वियोगेन दुःखार्त स्स त्वां कौशलमब्रवीत्।।5.34.34।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वीर्यवान् heroic, यः who, नचिरात् soon, रोषप्रमुक्तै: released in great anger, ज्वलद्भिः by burning, पावकैः इव like fire, इषुभि with arrows, संख्ये in war, रावणम् Ravana, वधिष्यति would slay, तेन therefore, दूतः envoy, प्रेषितः sent, इह here, त्वत्सकाशम् your presence, आगतः I have come, त्वद्वियोगेन due to separation from you, दुःखार्तः filled with sorrow, सः he, त्वाम् to you, कौशलम् welfare, अब्रवीत् asking.

#### Explanation

"Heroic Rama will soon release many scorching, fiery arrows in anger and kill Ravana. I seek your permission as an envoy of Rama. Filled with grief by your separation, he has made inquiries about your welfare.

---

### Shloka 35

#### Shloka Text

लक्ष्मणश्च महातेजास्सुमित्रानन्दवर्धनः। अभिवाद्य महाबाहुस्स त्वां कौशलमब्रवीत्।।5.34.35।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महातेजाः brilliant, सुमित्रानन्दस्सवर्धनः delight of Sumitra, महाबाहुः longarmed one, सः लक्ष्मणश्च Lakshmna, त्वाम् to you, अभिवाद्य offering salutations, कौशलम् welfare, अब्रवीत् enquired.

#### Explanation

"The brilliant, longarmed Lakshmana, delight of Sumitra enquires about your welfare and offers salutations to you.

---

### Shloka 36

#### Shloka Text

रामस्य च सखा देवि सुग्रीवो नाम वानरः। राजा वानरमुख्यानां स त्वां कौशलमब्रवीत्।।5.34.36।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

देवि O goddesslike lady, रामस्य Rama's, सखा friend, वानरमुख्यानाम् of vanara chiefs, राजा king, सुग्रीवो नाम called Sugriva, सः वानरः  that vanara, त्वाम् you, कौशलम् wellbeing, अब्रवीत् asked.

#### Explanation

"O goddess like lady Rama's friend Sugriva, king of vanara chieftans inquires about your wellbeing.

---

### Shloka 37

#### Shloka Text

नित्यं स्मरति रामस्त्वां ससुग्रीव स्सलक्ष्मणः। दिष्ट्या जीवसि वैदेहि राक्षसीवशमागता।।5.34.37।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ससुग्रीवः together with Sugriva, सलक्ष्मणः and Lakshmna, रामः Rama, त्वाम् you, नित्यम् always, स्मरति remembers, वैदेहि Vaidehi, राक्षसीवशम् under the clutches of shedemons, आगता come, दिष्ट्या luckily, जीवसि you are alive.

#### Explanation

"O Vaidehi Rama, Lakshmana and Sugriva always remember you. Luckily you are alive even though you are under the clutches of shedemons.

---

### Shloka 38

#### Shloka Text

नचिराद्द्रक्ष्यसे रामं लक्ष्मणं च महाबलम्। मध्ये वानरकोटीनां सुग्रीवं चामितौजसम्।।5.34.38।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामम् Rama, महाबलम् mighty, लक्ष्मणं Lakshmana, वानरकोटीनाम् among crores of vanaras, मध्ये in the midst, अमितौजसम् of a hero of unlimited prowess, सुग्रीवं च also Sugriva, नचिरात् very soon, द्रक्ष्यसे you will see.

#### Explanation

"Very soon you will see mighty Rama, Lakshmana and Sugriva of unlimited prowess surrounded by crores of vanaras.

---

### Shloka 39

#### Shloka Text

अहं सुग्रीवसचिवो हनुमान्नाम वानरः। प्रविष्टो नगरीं लङ्कां लङ्घयित्वा महोदधिम्।।5.34.39।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I am, सुग्रीवसचिवः minister of Sugriva, हनुमान्नाम called Hanuman, वानरः a vanara, महोदधिम् great ocean, लङ्घयित्वा having leaped, लङ्कां नगरीम् city of Lanka, प्रविष्टः I have entered.

#### Explanation

"I am Sugriva's minister known as Hanuman, a vanara who entered the city of Lanka after leaping over the great ocean.

---

### Shloka 40

#### Shloka Text

कृत्वा मूर्ध्नि पदन्यासं रावणस्य दुरात्मनः। त्वां द्रष्टुमुपयातोऽहं समाश्रित्य पराक्रमम्।।5.34.40।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दुरात्मनः cruelminded, रावणस्य Ravana's, मूर्ध्नि: on the head, पदन्यासम् placing my foot, कृत्वा of having done, पराक्रमम् valour, समाश्रित्य using, अहम् I, त्वाम् you, द्रष्टुम् to see, उपयातः came here.

#### Explanation

"Setting my foot with my valour on the head of the cruelminded Ravana, using my valour,  I came here to see you.

---

### Shloka 41

#### Shloka Text

नाहमस्मि तथा देवि यथा मामवगच्छसि। विशङ्का त्यज्यतामेषा श्रद्धत्स्व वदतो मम।।5.34.41।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

देवि O goddesslike lady, माम् me, यथा as, अवगच्छसि you suppose, अहम् I, तथा like that, नास्मि I am not, एषा such, विशङ्का suspicion, त्यज्यताम् shake off, वदतः as I speak, मम my, श्रद्धत्स्व you may trust.

#### Explanation

"O goddesslike lady I am not  a deceitful person as you suppose me to be. Shake off your suspicion as I speak to you and trust me." इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये सुन्दरकाण्डे चतुस्त्रिंशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the thirtyfourth sarga of Sundarakanda of the holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 35

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

तां तु रामकथां श्रुत्वा वैदेही वानरर्षभात्। उवाच वचनं सान्त्वमिदं मधुरया गिरा।।5.35.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वैदेही Vaidehi, वानरर्षभात् from the bull among vanaras, ताम् that, रामकथाम् Rama's story, श्रुत्वा heard, मधुरया in sweet, गिरा word, सान्त्वम् polite, इदम् these, वचनम् words, उवाच spoke.

#### Explanation

On hearing about Rama from the bull among vanaras, thus spoke Vaidehi politely in a sweet voice:

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

क्व ते रामेण संसर्गः कथं जानासि लक्ष्मणम्। वानराणां नराणां च कथमासीत्समागमः।।5.35.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ते to you, रामेण with Rama, संसर्गः contact, क्व where, लक्ष्मणम् Lakshmana, कथम् how, जानासि do you assess, वानराणाम् of vanaras, नराणां च a human, समागमः union, कथम् how, आसीत् took place.

#### Explanation

"Where did you come in contact with Rama? How did you assess Lakshmana? How did an alliance of vanaras and humans take place?

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

यानि रामस्य लिङ्गानि लक्ष्मणस्य च वानर। तानि भूयस्समाचक्ष्व न मां शोकस्समाविशेत्।।5.35.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वानर O vanara, रामस्य Rama's, लक्ष्मणस्य च and Lakshmana's, यानि those, लिङ्गानि identification marks, तानि those, समाचक्ष्व you may tell, माम् to me, भूयः again, शोकः sorrow, न not, समाविशेत् entire account.

#### Explanation

"O vanara tell me all about the identification marks of Rama and Lakshmana again so that grief does not enter my mind.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

कीदृशं तस्य संस्थानं रूपं रामस्य कीदृशम्। कथमूरू कथं बाहू लक्ष्मणस्य च शंस मे।।5.35.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तस्य his, रामस्य Rama's, लक्ष्मणस्य Lakshmana's, संस्थानम् form, कीदृशम् how, रूपम् form, कीदृशम् how does his look, ऊरू thighs, कथम् how, बाहू arms, कथम् how, मे to me, शंस tell.

#### Explanation

"What does Rama look like? And Laksmana? How are his thighs, arms? Tell me".

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्तस्तु वैदेह्या हनुमान्मारुतात्मजः। ततो रामं यथातत्त्वमाख्यातुमुपचक्रमे।।5.35.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वैदेह्या by Vaidehi, एवम् in that manner, उक्तः spoken, मारुतात्मजः son of the Windgod, हनुमान् Hanuman, ततः thereafter, रामम् Rama, यथातत्त्वम् as it is, आख्यातुम् to describe, उपचक्रमे he started telling.

#### Explanation

Questioned thus by Vaidehi, Hanuman, son of the Windgod started describing Rama's physique as it is.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

जानन्ती बत दिष्ट्या मां वैदेहि परिपृच्छसि। भर्तुः कमलपत्त्राक्षि संस्थानं लक्ष्मणस्य च।।5.35.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कमलपत्त्राक्षि eyes like lotus petals, वैदेहि Vaidehi, जानन्ती even though you know, दिष्ट्या by good fortune, भर्तुः husband's, लक्ष्मणस्य च and Lakshmana's, संस्थानम् form and distinguishing marks, परिपृच्छसि have asked, बत indeed.

#### Explanation

"O Vaidehi with eyes like lotus petals although you know your husband's and Lakshmana's form and distinguishing marks, it is my good fortune that you asked me (to describe them).

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

यानि रामस्य चिह्नानि लक्ष्मणस्य च यानि वै। लक्षितानि विशालाक्षि वदतश्शृणु तानि मे।।5.35.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विशालाक्षि O largeeyed lady, रामस्य Rama's, यानि लक्षणानि those distinguishing marks, लक्ष्मणस्य च and Lakshmana's, यानि those, लक्षितानि signs, तानि them, वदतः as I describe, मे myself, शृणु listen.

#### Explanation

"O largeeyed lady I will describe those distinguishing marks of Rama and Lakshmana Listen.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

रामः कमलपत्त्राक्ष स्सर्वसत्त्वमनोहरः। रूपदाक्षिण्यसम्पन्नः प्रसूतो जनकात्मजे।।5.35.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

जनकात्मजे O Janaka's daughter, रामः Rama, कमलपत्त्राक्षः one who has eyes like lotus petals, सर्वसत्त्वमनोहरः one who delights all beings, रूपदाक्षिण्यसम्पन्नः endowed richly with charm and politeness, प्रसूतः born with.

#### Explanation

"O daughter of Janaka Rama with eyes like lotus petals is a person born with a delightful form, richly endowed with indescribable charm and politeness.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

तेजसाऽदित्य सङ्काशः क्षमया पृथिवीसमः। बृहस्पतिसमो बुद्ध्या यशसा वासवोपमः।।5.35.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तेजसा in effulgence, आदित्यसङ्काशः vies with the Sun, क्षमया in tolerance, पृथिवीसमः equals the earth, बुद्ध्या in intelligence, बृहस्पतिसमः equal to Brihaspathi, यशसा in fame, वासवोपमः equals Indra.

#### Explanation

"He vies with the Sun in effulgence, with earth in forbearance, with Brihaspati in intelligence, and in fame with Indra.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

रक्षिता जीवलोकस्य स्वजनस्याभिरक्षिता। रक्षिता स्वस्य वृत्तस्य धर्मस्य च परन्तपः।।5.35.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

परन्तपः scorcher of enemies, जीवलोकस्य of the world of living beings, रक्षिता protector, स्वजनस्य of his own people, अभिरक्षिता protector, स्वस्य of his own, वृत्तस्य of the race, धर्मस्य च and guards righteousness

#### Explanation

"Rama is a protector of the entire world of living beings not to speak of his own people. He is a protector of his own race. He guards righteousness. He is a scorcher of enemies.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

रामो भामिनि लोकस्य चातुर्वर्ण्यस्य रक्षिता। मर्यादानां च लोकस्य कर्ता कारयिता च सः।।5.35.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भामिनि O beautiful lady, रामः Rama, लोकस्य world's, चातुर्वर्ण्यस्य of the four classes of society, रक्षिता protector, सः he, लोकानाम् of all the worlds, मर्यादानाम् code and conduct, कर्ता maker, कारयिताचैव will make others follow.

#### Explanation

"O beautiful lady Rama is a protector of the four classes of society (Brahmana, Kshatriya, Vaisya and Sudra) in the world. He follows codes of conduct and makes others follow them.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

अर्चिष्मानर्चितोऽत्यर्थं ब्रह्मचर्यव्रते स्थितः। साधूनामुपकारज्ञः प्रचारज्ञश्च कर्मणाम्।।5.35.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अर्चिष्मान् luminous, अत्यर्थम् intensely, अर्चितः worshipped, ब्रह्मचर्यव्रते observing the vow of selfcontrol, स्थितः one who is steadfast, साधूनाम् for the holy men, उपकारज्ञः recognises right action, कर्मणाम् rites, प्रचारज्ञश्च  of the ways of  administration.

#### Explanation

"He is luminous and highly revered. He observes vow of selfcontrol. He is steadfast and recognises services rendered by sages. He is aware of the rites and the ways of administration.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

राजविद्याविनीतश्च ब्राह्मणानामुपासिता। श्रुतवान्शीलसम्पन्नो विनीतश्च परन्तपः।।5.35.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राजविद्याविनीतश्च welltrained in statecraft, ब्राह्मणानाम् of the brahmins, उपासिता a devotee, श्रुतवान् learned in scriptures, शीलसम्पन्नः endowed with excellent conduct, विनीतः is disciplined, परन्तपः  is a scorcher of enemies.

#### Explanation

"He is welltrained in statecraft. He is devoted to brahmins. Endowed with an excellent conduct, he is learned in scriptures and a scorcher of enemies.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

यजुर्वेदविनीतश्च वेदविद्भिस्सुपूजितः। धनुर्वेदे च वेदेषु वेदाङ्गेषु च निष्ठितः।।5.35.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

यजुर्वेदविनीतश्च versed in Yajurveda, वेदविद्भिः by the knowers of the Vedas, सुपूजितः respected, धनुर्वेदे च and in Dhanurveda also, वेदेषु in other Vedas also, वेदाङ्गेषु च Vedangas (auxillary Vedas) also, निष्ठितः is an expert.

#### Explanation

"He is versed in Yajurveda and is respected by Vedic scholars. He has mastered Dhanurveda and other Vedas and auxillary Vedas as well.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

विपुलांसो महाबाहुः कम्बुग्रीवश्शुभाननः। गूढजत्रुस्सुताम्राक्षो रामो देवि जनै श्श्रुतः।।5.35.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

देवि goddess like lady, रामः Rama, विपुलां सः broadshouldered, महाबाहुः strongarmed, कम्बुग्रीवः with conchshaped neck, शुभाननः with an auspicious countenance, गूढजत्रुः has round shoulders, सुताम्राक्षः copperyred eyes  जनैः in all worlds, श्रुतः renowned.

#### Explanation

"O godlike lady Rama has broad shoulders, strong arms, conchshaped neck and an auspicious countenance. With his fleshy round arms and copperyred eyes, he is renowned in all the worlds.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

दुन्दुभिस्वननिर्घोष स्स्निग्धवर्णः प्रतापवान्। सम स्समविभक्ताङ्गो वर्णं श्यामं समाश्रितः।।5.35.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दुन्दुभिस्वननिर्घोषः his tone is resonant like the drums, स्निग्धवर्णः has beautiful shining complexion, प्रतापवान् full of valour, समः not too tall or short, समविभक्ताङ्गः his limbs are symmetrical, श्यामम् dark, वर्णम् colour, समाश्रितः has proportionate limbs.

#### Explanation

"His tone is resonant like drums, he has a beautiful, dark, shining complexion. He has symmetrical, well proportioned limbs. He is neither too tall nor too short.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

त्रिस्थिरस्त्रिप्रलम्बश्च त्रिसमस्त्रिषु चोन्नतः। त्रिताम्रस्त्रिषु च स्निग्धो गम्भीरस्त्रिषु नित्यशः।।5.35.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्रिस्थिरः has the three stiff limbs (chest, fist and wrists), त्रिप्रलम्बश्च long in three (arms, eyebrows and scrotum), त्रिसमः has three even parts (locks of hair, knees and testicles), त्रिषु elevated in three parts (abdomen, navel and chest), उन्नतः raised, त्रिषु च all the three parts (soles, nails and eyes), स्निग्धः has coppery reddish colour, नित्यशः all over, त्रिषु in the three parts (the underlines of feet, the hair and the reproductive  organs), गम्भीरः is majestic.

#### Explanation

"He has three stiff parts, three long parts, three even parts, three shining parts, elevated in three parts, has three raised parts, his body has lines in three parts and is  of coppery reddish colour in three parts. He has a majestic look.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

त्रिवलीवांस्त्र्यवनतश्चतुर्व्यङ्गस्त्रिशीर्षवान्। चतुष्कलश्चतुर्लेखश्चतुष्किष्कुश्चतु स्समः।।5.35.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्रिवलीवान् has three folds on his neck, स्त्र्यवनतः his nipples and middle of his soles are depressed, चतुर्व्यङ्गः four parts of his body are depressedneck, penis, shanks and back, त्रिशीर्षवान् has three spirals in the head, चतुष्कलः has four lines under his thumb, चतुर्लेखः has four lines on the forehead, चतुष्किष्कुः he is four cubits in height, चतुस्समः he has arms, knees, cheeks and thighs well proportioned

#### Explanation

"He has three folds in the skin of his neck and belly, is depressed in the middle of his soles and the nipples, undersized at four placesthe neck, penis, shanks and back, has three spirals on his head, has four lines under his thumb, has four lines on his forehead, he is a person with four cubits in height and symmetrical arms, cheeks and thighs.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

चतुर्दशसमद्वन्द्वश्चतुर्दंष्ट्रश्चतुर्गतिः। महोष्ठहनुनासश्च पञ्चस्निग्धोऽष्टवंशवान्।।5.35.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

चतुर्दशसमद्वन्द्वः he is person with the fourteen parts of his body (eyebrows, nostrils, eyes, ears, lips, nipples, elbows, wrists, knees, testicles, loins, arms, legs and buttocks) in good proportion, चतुर्दंष्ट्रः he is one with four sharp teeth, चतुर्गतिः he moves like a lion, leopard, elephant and bull as occasion demands, महोष्ठहनुनासश्च he has sharp nose, charming lips and jaws, पञ्चस्निग्ध: he is one with five smooth parts (hair, eyes, skin, teeth and feet), अष्टवंशवान् his eight parts of the body (backbone, trunk, limbs, fingers, toes, nose, eyes and testicles) are long.

#### Explanation

"He is a person with his pairs of eyebrows, nostrils, eyes, ears, lips, nipples, elbows, wrists, knees, testicles, loins, arms, legs and buttocks in good symmetry he has four sharp teeth. He walks in different gaits like a lion, leopard, elephant and bull as occasion demands he has sharp nose, charming lips and jaws. He has smooth hair, eyes, skin, teeth and feet and his eight parts of his body namely back, trunk, limbs, fingers, toes, eyes and testicles are long.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

दशपद्मो दशबृहत्त्रिभिर्व्याप्तो द्विशुक्लवान्। षडुन्नतो नवतनुस्त्रिभिर्व्याप्नोति राघवः।।5.35.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राघवः Rama, दशपद्मः ten lotuslike limbs (eyes, face, mouth, tongue, lips, cheeks, nipples, nails, feet and hands), दशबृहत् he is one with ten wellproportioned limbs (head, forehead, ears, neck, chest, heart, belly, hands, legs, buttocks), त्रिभिः covered with three aspects (splendour, fame and glory), व्याप्तः well known, द्विशुक्लवान् two white limbs teeth and eyes, षडुन्नतः he has six elevated  limbs (nose, shoulders, forehead, chest and arm pits), नवतनुः has nine fine features (moustache, nails, knuckles, beard, skin, hair, penis, acumen, perception), त्रिभिः he pursues spiritual merit, worldly comforts and sensual delight in three periodsforenoon, middday and evening of the day, व्याप्नोति follows righteous principles.

#### Explanation

"Ten limbs of the body resemble lotuses. He has proportionate limbs. He is known by the virtue of his spledour, fame and glory. Both his teeth and eyes are fair. Six parts of his body are elevated, nine parts of his body are fine and sharp. He pursues spiritual merit, worldly comfort and sensual delight in three periods of the dayforenoon, midday and evening. He always adopts the righteous way of life.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

सत्यधर्मपरश्श्रीमान् सङ्ग्रहानुग्रहे रतः। देशकालविभागज्ञस्सर्वलोकप्रियंवदः।।5.35.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सत्यधर्मपरः established in truth and righteousness, श्रीमान् prosperous, सङ्ग्रहानुग्रहे रतः he is involved in both accumulation and favourable disposition towards others, देशकालविभागज्ञः who discharges his duties at proper time and place, सर्वलोकप्रियंवदः who is loved by all for his endearing words.

#### Explanation

"He is ever established in truth and righteousness. He is prosperous and desires to accumulate wealth in order to shower favour on others. He knows the right use of time and place in discharging his duties. He has endeared himself to all by his words.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

भ्राता तस्य च द्वैमात्रस्सौमित्रिरपराजितः। अनुरागेण रूपेण गुणैश्चैव तथाविधः।।5.35.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तस्य his, द्वैमात्रः born of a different mother, भ्राता brother, अपराजितः one who has never been defeated, सौमित्रिः Saumitri, अनुरागेण in affection, रूपेण in charm, गुणैश्चैव in virtues, तथाविधः similar to him.

#### Explanation

"His invincible brother Saumitri born of a different mother is a replica of Rama in affection, charm and virtues.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

तावुभौ नरशार्दूलौ त्वद्दर्शनसमुत्सुकौ। विचिन्वन्तौ महीं कृत्स्नामस्माभिरभिसङ्गतौ।।5.35.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नरशार्दूलौ tiger among men, तौ they, उभौ both, त्वद्दर्शनसमुत्सुकौ eager to see you, कृत्स्नाम् entire, महीम् earth, विचिन्वन्तौ both searching for, अस्माभिः by us, अभिसङ्गतौ they met us.

#### Explanation

"While earnestly searching for you all over the earth both tigers among men came in contact with us.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

त्वामेव मार्गमाणौ तौ विचरन्तौ वसुन्धराम्। ददर्शतुर्मृगपतिं पूर्वजेनावरोपितम्।।5.35.24।। ऋश्यमूकस्य पृष्ठे तु बहुपादपसङ्कुले। भ्रातुर्भयार्तमासीनं सुग्रीवं प्रियदर्शनम्।।5.35.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्वामेव for you, मार्गमाणौ while searching, वसुन्धराम् all over the earth, विचरन्तौ while both were wandering, तौ they both, बहुपादपसङ्कुले filled with many trees, ऋश्यमूकस्य पृष्ठे on the mountain Rshyamuka, आसीनम् seated, पूर्वजेन by the elder brother, अवरोपितम् one who has been thrown out, भ्रातुः brother's, भयार्तम् frightened, प्रियदर्शनम् one who is pleasing to see, मृगपतिम् lord of animals, सुग्रीवम् Sugriva, ददर्शतुः both of them saw.

#### Explanation

"While both of them were wandering, searching for you all over the earth they saw Sugriva, the lord of animals who had been thrown out of kingship by his elder brother and was living on the mountain Rshyamuka frightened of him.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

त्वामेव मार्गमाणौ तौ विचरन्तौ वसुन्धराम्। ददर्शतुर्मृगपतिं पूर्वजेनावरोपितम्।।5.35.24।। ऋश्यमूकस्य पृष्ठे तु बहुपादपसङ्कुले। भ्रातुर्भयार्तमासीनं सुग्रीवं प्रियदर्शनम्।।5.35.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्वामेव for you, मार्गमाणौ while searching, वसुन्धराम् all over the earth, विचरन्तौ while both were wandering, तौ they both, बहुपादपसङ्कुले filled with many trees, ऋश्यमूकस्य पृष्ठे on the mountain Rshyamuka, आसीनम् seated, पूर्वजेन by the elder brother, अवरोपितम् one who has been thrown out, भ्रातुः brother's, भयार्तम् frightened, प्रियदर्शनम् one who is pleasing to see, मृगपतिम् lord of animals, सुग्रीवम् Sugriva, ददर्शतुः both of them saw.

#### Explanation

"While both of them were wandering, searching for you all over the earth they saw Sugriva, the lord of animals who had been thrown out of kingship by his elder brother and was living on the mountain Rshyamuka frightened of him.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

वयं तु हरिराजं तं सुग्रीवं सत्यसङ्गरम्। परिचर्यामहे राज्यात्पूर्वजेनावरोपितम्।।5.35.26।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वयं तु we too, पूर्वजेन by his elder brother, राज्यात् from the kingdom, अवरोपितम् deposed, सत्यसङ्गरम् one who is committed to truth, हरिराजम् king of vanaras, तम् him, सुग्रीवम् Sugriva, परिचर्यामहे we serve him.

#### Explanation

"Sugriva, king of vanaras who is committed to defend truth has been deposed from the kingdom by his elder brother. We keep attending on him.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

ततस्तौ चीरवसनौ धनुः प्रवरपाणिनौ। ऋश्यमूकस्य शैलस्य रम्यं देशमुपागतौ।।5.35.27।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, चीरवसनौ clad in bark robes, धनुःप्रवरपाणिनौ armed with powerful bows, तौ both, ऋश्यमूकस्य शैलस्य on the Rshyamukha mountain, रम्यम् beautiful, देशम् location, उपागतौ both reached.

#### Explanation

"Both the princes clad in bark robes and armed with powerful bows reached the beautiful location of Rshyamukha mountain.

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

स तौ दृष्ट्वा नरव्याघ्रौ धन्विनौ वानरर्षभः। अवप्लुतो गिरेस्तस्य शिखरं भयमोहितः।।5.35.28।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः he, वानरर्षभः bull among vanaras, धन्विनौ armed with bows, तौ both of them, नरव्याघ्रौ tigers among men, दृष्ट्वा on seeing, भयमोहितः deluded with fear, तस्य from that, गिरेः mountain, शिखरम् top, अभिप्लुतः jumped.

#### Explanation

"On seeing the tigers among men armed with bows, Sugriva, the bull among vanaras jumped from the mountain deluded with fear and reached the peak.

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

ततस्स शिखरे तस्मिन्वानरेन्द्रो व्यवस्थितः। तयोस्समीपं मामेव प्रेषयामास सत्वरम्।।5.35.29।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः thereafter, सः he, वानरेन्द्रः lord of vanaras, तस्मिन् on that, शिखरे mountain top, व्यवस्थितः staying there, मामेव me only, तयोः of both of them, समीपम् towads, सत्वरम् quickly, प्रेषयामास sent.

#### Explanation

"Thereafter the lord of vanaras staying on the mountain top quickly sent me to meet both of them.

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

तावहं पुरुषव्याघ्रौ सुग्रीववचनात्प्रभू। रूपलक्षणसम्पन्नौ कृताञ्जलिरुपस्थितः।।5.35.30।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I, सुग्रीववचनात् according to the instruction of Sugriva, पुरुषव्याघ्रौ two tigers among men, प्रभू two lords, रूपलक्षणसम्पन्नौ both endowed with charming appearance and auspicious marks, तौ both, कृताञ्जलिः joined palms in salutation, उपस्थितः approached.

#### Explanation

None

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

तौ परिज्ञाततत्त्वार्थौ मया प्रीतिसमन्वितौ। पृष्ठमारोप्य तं देशं प्रापितौ पुरुषर्षभौ।।5.35.31।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

परिज्ञाततत्त्वर्थौ realised the true position, प्रीतिसमन्वितौ filled with pleasure, पुरुषर्षभौ two bulls among men, तौ both, मया by me, पृष्ठम् back, आरोप्य placing, तं देशम् to that place, प्रापितौ carried.

#### Explanation

"I recognised the true status of  both the mighty men who were filled with joy, placed them on my back, and reached the mountaintop.

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

निवेदितौ च तत्त्वेन सुग्रीवाय महात्मने। तयोरन्योन्यसल्लापाद्भृशं प्रीतिरजायत।।5.35.32।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महात्मने to the great self, सुग्रीवाय to Sugriva, तत्त्वेन faithfully, निवेदितौ reported by me, तयोः of both of them, अन्योन्यसल्लापात् got to know each other through conversation, भृशम् fond, प्रीतिः friendship, अजायत developed.

#### Explanation

"I reported faithfully about both the highsouled men to Sugriva. As they got to know each other through conversation a fast friendship developed between them.

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

ततस्तौ प्रीतिसम्पन्नौ हरीश्वरनरेश्वरौ। परस्परकृताश्वासौ कथया पूर्ववृत्तया।।5.35.33।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, प्रीतिसम्पन्नौ full of joy, तौ both, हरीश्वरनरेश्वरौ king of monkeys and the king of people, पूर्ववृत्तया with their past, कथया with account, परस्परकृताश्वासौ they consoled each other.

#### Explanation

None

---

### Shloka 34

#### Shloka Text

ततस्स सान्त्वयामास सुग्रीवं लक्ष्मणाग्रजः। स्त्रीहेतोर्वालिना भ्रात्रा निरस्तमुरुतेजसा।।5.35.34।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः thereafter, सः he, लक्ष्मणाग्रजः the elder brother of Lakshmana, उरुतेजसा with great prowess, भ्रात्रा by the brother, वालिना by Vali, स्त्रीहेतो on account of a woman, निरस्तम्  deposed, सुग्रीवम् Sugriva, सान्त्वयामास consoled.

#### Explanation

"Then Rama, consoled Sugriva who was dethroned and abandoned on acount of a woman by his brother Vali, endowed with great prowess.

---

### Shloka 35

#### Shloka Text

ततस्त्वन्नाशजं शोकं रामस्याक्लिष्टकर्मणः। लक्ष्मणो वानरेन्द्राय सुग्रीवाय न्यवेदयत्।।5.35.35।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, लक्ष्मणः Lakshmana, अक्लिष्टकर्मणः one who is adept in accomplishing tasks with ease, रामस्य Rama's, त्वन्नाशजम् on account of your loss, शोकम् grief, वानरेन्द्राय to the lord of vanaras, सुग्रीवाय to Sugriva, न्यवेदयत् revealed.

#### Explanation

"Then Lakshmana spoke to the lord of vanaras, Sugriva about your loss and the grief caused to Rama, who is adept in accomplishing difficult deeds with ease.

---

### Shloka 36

#### Shloka Text

स श्रुत्वा वानरेन्द्रस्तु लक्ष्मणेनेरितं वचः। तदासीन्निष्प्रभोऽत्यर्थं ग्रहग्रस्त इवांशुमान्।।5.35.36।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः he, वानरेन्द्रस्तु to the king of vanaras, लक्ष्मणेन by Lakshmana, ईरितम् spoken वचः words, श्रुत्वा having heard, तदा then, ग्रहग्रस्तः overshadowed by the planet, अंशुमानिव like the Sun, अत्यर्थम् extremely, निष्प्रभः lustreless, आसीत् became.

#### Explanation

"Having heard the words of Lakshmana, the king of vanaras became extremely lustreless like the Sun overshadowed by the planet Rahu.

---

### Shloka 37

#### Shloka Text

ततस्त्वद्गात्रशोभीनि रक्षसा ह्रियमाणया। यान्याभरणजालानि पातितानि महीतले।।5.35.37।। तानि सर्वाणि रामाय आनीय हरियूथपाः। संहृष्टा दर्शयामासुर्गतिं तु न विदुस्तव।।5.35.38।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः at that time, रक्षसा by the demon, ह्रियमाणया when you were borne away, त्वद्गात्रशोभिनी adorned your limbs, यानि those, आभरणजालानि bundle of ornaments, पातितानि dropped down, तानि सर्वाणि all of them, आनीय brought, हरियूथपाः vanara leaders, संहृष्टाः with great joy, रामाय to Rama, दर्शयामासुः showed, तव your, गतिं तु whereabouts, न विदुः did not know.

#### Explanation

None

---

### Shloka 38

#### Shloka Text

ततस्त्वद्गात्रशोभीनि रक्षसा ह्रियमाणया। यान्याभरणजालानि पातितानि महीतले।।5.35.37।। तानि सर्वाणि रामाय आनीय हरियूथपाः। संहृष्टा दर्शयामासुर्गतिं तु न विदुस्तव।।5.35.38।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः at that time, रक्षसा by the demon, ह्रियमाणया when you were borne away, त्वद्गात्रशोभिनी adorned your limbs, यानि those, आभरणजालानि bundle of ornaments, पातितानि dropped down, तानि सर्वाणि all of them, आनीय brought, हरियूथपाः vanara leaders, संहृष्टाः with great joy, रामाय to Rama, दर्शयामासुः showed, तव your, गतिं तु whereabouts, न विदुः did not know.

#### Explanation

None

---

### Shloka 39

#### Shloka Text

तानि रामाय दत्तानि मयैवोपहृतानि च। स्वनवन्त्यवकीर्णानि तस्मिन्विगतचेतसि।।5.35.39।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मयैव by me, उपहृतानि collected, तानि those ornaments, रामाय to Rama, दत्तानि gave, तस्मिन् them, विगतचेतसि lost senses, स्वनवन्ति making jingling sound, अवकीर्णानि having scattered.

#### Explanation

"I collected the ornaments and gave them to Rama. He lost his senses on seeing the jingling ornaments scattered.

---

### Shloka 40

#### Shloka Text

तान्यङ्के दर्शनीयानि कृत्वा बहुविधं तव। तेन देवप्रकाशेन देवेन परिदेवितम्।।5.35.40।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दर्शनीयानि beautiful ones, तव your, तानि those, अङ्के on his lap, कृत्वा having placed, देवप्रकाशेन by an effulgent one looking like the divine, तेन by him, देवेन divine self, बहुविधम् in many ways, परिदेवितम् wailed.

#### Explanation

"The divine self who is effulgent like god wailed in many ways placing the beautiful ornaments on his lap.

---

### Shloka 41

#### Shloka Text

पश्यतस्तानि रुदतस्ताम्यतश्च पुनः पुनः। प्रादीपयन्दाशरथेस्तानि शोकहुताशनम्।।5.35.41।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तानि them, पश्यतः as looked at, रुदतः while he wept, पुनः पुनः again and again, ताम्यतश्च  regretting, दाशरथेः Dasaratha's son Rama's, शोकहुताशनम् fire in the form of grief, तानि them, प्रादीपयन् inflamed.

#### Explanation

"Rama looked at them and wept clasping them again and again in grief which inflamed like fire.

---

### Shloka 42

#### Shloka Text

शयितं च चिरं तेन दुःखार्तेन महात्मना। मयापि विविधैर्वाक्यैः कृच्छ्रादुत्थापितः पुनः।।5.35.42।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दुःखार्तेन immersed in sorrow, तेन by him, महात्मना by the great self, शयितं च lay down, मयापि by me also, विविधैः by many, वाक्यैः by words, कृच्छ्रात् with great difficulty, पुनः again, उत्थापितः helped to get up.

#### Explanation

"Rama, the great self, immersed in sorrow lay down on the ground. I also consoled him with great difficulty and helped him to get up.

---

### Shloka 43

#### Shloka Text

तानि दृष्ट्वा महाबाहुर्दर्शयित्वा मुहुर्मुहुः। राघवस्सह सौमित्रिस्सुग्रीवे स न्यवेदयत्।।5.35.43।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाबाहुः longarmed one, सः राघवः that Rama, सहसौमित्रिः along with Lakshmana, तानि those, मुहुः मुहुः again and again, दर्शयित्वा after showing, सुग्रीवे to Sugriva, न्यवेदयत् presented.

#### Explanation

"Longarmed Rama along with Lakshmana looked at them again and again and gave them to the care of Sugriva.

---

### Shloka 44

#### Shloka Text

स तवादर्शनादार्ये राघवः परितप्यते। महता ज्वलता नित्यमग्निनेवाग्निपर्वतः।।5.35.44।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

आर्ये O divine lady, सः राघवः that Rama, तव your, अदर्शनात् separation from you, नित्यम् always, ज्वलता burning, महता great, अग्निना in fire, अग्निपर्वतः इव like a mountain of fire, परितप्यते  ever blazing.

#### Explanation

"O divine lady on account of your separation he is ever blazing like a mountain of fire (Samvartaka).

---

### Shloka 45

#### Shloka Text

त्वत्कृते तमनिद्रा च शोकश्चिन्ता च राघवम्। तापयन्ति महात्मानमग्न्यगारमिवाग्नयः।।5.35.45।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्वत्कृते on account of, महात्मानम् the great one, तं राघवम् that Rama, अनिद्रा च not sleeping, शोकः grief, चिन्ता च intense anxiety, अग्न्यगारम् like a furnace, अग्नयः इव like the three fires, तापयन्ति keep intensely heated.

#### Explanation

"Rama is burning like a furnace by (three fires) sleeplessness, grief and intense anxiety just as the three sacred fires keep a firesanctuary intensely heated.

---

### Shloka 46

#### Shloka Text

तवादर्शनशोकेन राघवः प्रविचाल्यते। महता भूमिकम्पेन महानिव शिलोच्चयः।।5.35.46।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तव your, अदर्शनशोकेन by the sorrow of not being able to see you, राघवः Rama of Raghu dynasty, महता with great, भूमिकम्पेन by earthquake, महान् great, शिलोच्छयः lofty mountain, प्रविचाल्यते is shaken.

#### Explanation

"On account of sorrow caused by not being able to see you, Rama of Raghu dynasty is shaken up like a lofty mountain is shaken by earthquake.

---

### Shloka 47

#### Shloka Text

काननानि सुरम्याणि नदीः प्रस्रवणानि च। चरन्न रतिमाप्नोति त्वामपश्यन्नृपात्मजे।।5.35.47।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नृपात्मजे daughter of a king, त्वाम् you, अपश्यन् unable to see, सुरम्याणि very delightful, काननानि forests, नदीः rivers, प्रस्रवणानि च and streams, चरन् while moving, रतिम् happiness, न आप्नोति not finding.

#### Explanation

"Oh princess unable to see you, Rama has found no joy in roaming delightful forests, rivers and streams.

---

### Shloka 48

#### Shloka Text

स त्वां मनुजशार्दूलः क्षिप्रं प्राप्स्यति राघवः। समित्रबान्धवं हत्वा रावणं जनकात्मजे।।5.35.48।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

जनकात्मजे Oh daughter of Janaka, मनुजशार्दूलः a tiger among men, सः राघवः that Rama, समित्रबान्धवम् along with his friends and relatives, रावणम् Ravana, हत्वा after killing, त्वाम् you, क्षिप्रम् soon, प्राप्स्यति will attain.

#### Explanation

"Oh daughter of Janaka after killing Ravana, along with his friends and relatives Rama, the tiger among men, will meet you.

---

### Shloka 49

#### Shloka Text

सहितौ रामसुग्रीवावुभावकुरुतां तदा। समयं वालिनं हन्तुं तव चान्वेषणं तथा।।5.35.49।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, रामसुग्रीवौ Rama and Sugriva, उभौ both, सहितौ together with, वालिनम् Vali, हन्तुम् to kill, तथा likewise, तव your, अन्वेषणम् search, समयम् agreement, अकुरुताम् reached.

#### Explanation

"(Having become friends) Rama and Sugriva together reached an agreement to kill Vali and search for you.

---

### Shloka 50

#### Shloka Text

ततस्ताभ्यां कुमाराभ्यां वीराभ्यां स हरीश्वरः। किष्किन्धां समुपागम्य वाली युद्धे निपातितः।।5.35.50।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः thereafter, वीराभ्याम् by both the heroes, ताभ्याम् by both of them, कुमाराभ्याम् by both princes, किष्किन्धाम् to Kishkinda, समुपागम्य after reaching, हरीश्वरः king of vanaras, सः that, वाली Vali, युद्धे in war, निपातितः killed.

#### Explanation

"Thereafter both the princes together arrived at Kishkinda and killed Vali, king of vanaras in a combat.

---

### Shloka 51

#### Shloka Text

ततो निहत्य तरसा रामो वालिनमाहवे। सर्वर्क्षहरिसङ्घानां सुग्रीवमकरोत्पतिम्।।5.35.51।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, रामः Rama, आहवे in a combat, तरसा quickly, वालिनम् Vali, निहत्य killed, सुग्रीवम् Sugriva, सर्वर्क्षहरिसङ्घानाम् for all troops of vanaras, पतिम् king, अकरोत् made.

#### Explanation

"Then Rama quickly killed Vali in a combat and made Sugriva the lord of all troops of vanaras and bears.

---

### Shloka 52

#### Shloka Text

रामसुग्रीवयोरैक्यं देव्येवं समजायत। हनुमन्तं च मां विद्धि तयोर्दूतमिहागतम्।।5.35.52।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

देवि Oh Illustrious lady, रामसुग्रीवयोः of both Rama and Sugriva, ऐक्यम् alliance, एवम् in that way, समजायत established, माम् me, तयोः their, दूतम् messenger, आगतम् came here, हनुमन्तम् Hanuman, विद्धि you may know.

#### Explanation

"Oh Illustrious lady In that manner alliance was established between Rama and Sugriva. Know me to be Hanuman, arrived here as their messenger.

---

### Shloka 53

#### Shloka Text

स्वराज्यं प्राप्य सुग्रीवस्समानीय हरीश्वरान्। त्वदर्थं प्रेषयामास दिशो दश महाबलान्।।5.35.53।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सुग्रीवः Sugriva, स्वराज्यम् his kingdom, प्राप्य having obtained, महाबलान् great army, हरीश्वरान् monkey leaders, समानीय after collecting, त्वदर्थम् for your sake, दश ten, दिशः directions, प्रेषयामास sent.

#### Explanation

"Having obtained his kingdom Sugriva despatched the vanara leaders to all ten directions (to search for you).

---

### Shloka 54

#### Shloka Text

आदिष्टा वानरेन्द्रेण सुग्रीवेण महौजसा। अद्रिराजप्रतीकाशास्सर्वतः प्रस्थितौ महीम्।।5.35.54।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वानरेन्द्रेण by the monkey leaders, महौजसा very powerful one, सुग्रीवेण by Sugriva, आदिष्टाः ordered, अद्रिराजप्रतीकाशाः huge as the Himalayas, king of mountains, सर्वतः all over, महीम् the earth, प्रस्थिता started moving.

#### Explanation

"Following Sugriva's orders, the powerful monkey leaders huge as the Himalayas, king of mountains went all over the earth (searching).

---

### Shloka 55

#### Shloka Text

ततस्ते मार्गमाणा वै सुग्रीववचनातुराः। चरन्ति वसुधां कृत्स्नां वयमन्ये च वानराः।।5.35.55।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, ते they, वयम् we, अन्ये others, वानराः च and the vanaras, सुग्रीववचनातुराः eager to carry out Sugriva's order, मार्गमाणाः while searching, कृत्स्नाम् entire, वसुधाम् the earth, चरन्ति we are moving about.

#### Explanation

"Following Sugriva's orders, we, the monkeys, since then are moving about searching the entire earth eager to find you or to carry out Sugriva's orders.

---

### Shloka 56

#### Shloka Text

अङ्गदो नाम लक्ष्मीवान्वालिसूनुर्महाबलः। प्रस्थितः कपिशार्दूलस्त्रिभागबलसंवृतः।।5.35.56।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मीवान् prosperous, वालिसूनुः Vali's son, महाबलः mighty, अङ्गदो नाम called Angada, हरिशार्दूलः a tiger among vanaras, त्रिभागबलसंवृतः together with one third of the army, प्रस्थितः departed.

#### Explanation

None

---

### Shloka 57

#### Shloka Text

तेषां नो विप्रणष्टानां विन्ध्ये पर्वतसत्तमे। भृशं शोकपरीतानामहोरात्रगणा गताः।।5.35.57।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विन्ध्ये in Vindhya, पर्वतसत्तमे in the great mountain, विप्रणष्टानाम् of those who were lost, भृशम् greatly, शोकपरीतानाम् of those immersed in sorrow, तेषाम् of all of them, नः of us, अहोरात्रगणाः many nights and days, गताःpassed

#### Explanation

"We wasted many nights and days immersed in disappointment (for not being able to find you) and we missed our path in the great Vindhya mountain.

---

### Shloka 58

#### Shloka Text

ते वयं कार्यनैराश्यात्कालस्यातिक्रमेण च। भयाच्च कपिराजस्य प्राणांस्त्यक्तुं व्यवस्थिताः।।5.35.58।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ते वयम् we were, कार्यनैराश्यात् disappointed, कालस्य of the time limit, अतिक्रमेण च exceeding, कपिराजस्य of monkey king's, भयाच्च out of fear also, प्राणान् life also, त्यक्तुम् to give up, व्यवस्थिताः decided.

#### Explanation
