"We decided to give up our lives due to disappointment, transgression of the time limit  fixed for us and fear of the monkey lord (fear of facing the king without finding you).

---

### Shloka 59

#### Shloka Text

विचित्य वनदुर्गाणि गिरिप्रस्रवणानि च। अनासाद्य पदं देव्याः प्राणांस्त्यक्तुं समुद्यताः।।5.35.59।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वनदुर्गाणि impenetrable forest forts, गिरिप्रस्रवणानि च mountain streams, विचित्य having searched, देव्याः the queen's, पदम् feet, अनासाद्य by not getting, प्राणान् life, त्यक्तुम् to give up, समुद्यताः we decided.

#### Explanation

"We decided to give up our lives since we were unable to see the feet of the queen (Sita) even after searching the forest fortresses and the (banks of) mountain streams.

---

### Shloka 60

#### Shloka Text

दृष्ट्वा प्रायोपविष्टांश्च सर्वान्वानरपुङ्गवान्। भृशं शोकार्णवे मग्नः पर्यदेवयदङ्गदः।।5.35.60।। तव नाशं च वैदेहि वालिनश्च वधं तथा। प्रायोपवेशमस्माकं मरणं च जटायुषः।।5.35.61।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वैदेहि oh Vaidehi, प्रायोपविष्टान् when we decided to die, सर्वान् all, वानरपुङ्गवान् vanara leaders, दृष्ट्वा on seeing, अङ्गदः Angada, भृशम् intensely, शोकार्णवे in the sea of sorrow, मग्नः plunged, तव yourself, नाशं च not found, तथा वालिनः similarly Vali's, वधं च and his killing, जटायुषः Jatayu's, मरणं च death also, अस्माकम् all of us, प्रायोपवेशम् decision to die, पर्यदेवयत् he was grieved.

#### Explanation

None

---

### Shloka 61

#### Shloka Text

दृष्ट्वा प्रायोपविष्टांश्च सर्वान्वानरपुङ्गवान्। भृशं शोकार्णवे मग्नः पर्यदेवयदङ्गदः।।5.35.60।। तव नाशं च वैदेहि वालिनश्च वधं तथा। प्रायोपवेशमस्माकं मरणं च जटायुषः।।5.35.61।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वैदेहि oh Vaidehi, प्रायोपविष्टान् when we decided to die, सर्वान् all, वानरपुङ्गवान् vanara leaders, दृष्ट्वा on seeing, अङ्गदः Angada, भृशम् intensely, शोकार्णवे in the sea of sorrow, मग्नः plunged, तव yourself, नाशं च not found, तथा वालिनः similarly Vali's, वधं च and his killing, जटायुषः Jatayu's, मरणं च death also, अस्माकम् all of us, प्रायोपवेशम् decision to die, पर्यदेवयत् he was grieved.

#### Explanation

None

---

### Shloka 62

#### Shloka Text

तेषां नस्वामिसन्देशान्निराशानां मुमूर्षताम्। कार्यहेतोरिवायातश्शकुनिर्वीर्यवान्महान्।।5.35.62।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

स्वामिसन्देशात् according to the king's cmmand (that who ever overstays would be killed), निराशानाम् disappointed, मुमूर्षताम् when we were desiring to die, तेषाम् for such of us, नः to us, कार्यहेतोरिव as if to fulfil the task, वीर्यवान् powerful, महान् gigantic, शकुनिः bird, आयातः came.

#### Explanation

"When we were disappointed (in tracing you) and were at the point of death having been commanded by the king (that whosoever overstays would be killed) a gigantic powerful bird appeared to fulfil our task.

---

### Shloka 63

#### Shloka Text

गृध्रराजस्य सोदर्यः सम्पातिर्नाम गृध्रराट्। श्रुत्वा भ्रातृवधं कोपादिदं वचनमब्रवीत्।।5.35.63।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

गृध्रराजस्य of the king of vultures, सोदर्यः brother, सम्पातिर्नाम called Sampati, गृध्रराट् king of birds, भ्रातृवधम् death of Jatayu, श्रुत्वा on hearing, कोपात् with anger, इदम् this, वचनम् word, अब्रवीत् spoke.

#### Explanation

"That king of vultures, Sampati was the brother of king of birds Jatayu. Hearing about the death of his younger brother Sampati felt distresed and spoke in anger:

---

### Shloka 64

#### Shloka Text

यवीयान्केन मे भ्राता हतः क्व च निपातितः। एतदाख्यातुमिच्छामि भवद्भिर्वानरोत्तमाः।।5.35.64।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मे my, यवीयान् younger, भ्राता brother, केन by whom, हतः was killed, क्व where, निपातितः was he dropped, वानरोत्तमाः of best of vanaras, भवद्भिः by you, एतत् this, आख्यातुम् to tell, इच्छामि I desire.

#### Explanation

'O best of vanaras by whom was my younger brother killed? Where was he dropped down? I wish you to tell me all about that.

---

### Shloka 65

#### Shloka Text

अङ्गदोऽकथयत्तस्य जनस्थाने महद्वधम्। रक्षसा भीमरूपेण त्वामुद्दिश्य यथातथम्।।5.35.65।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्वाम् you, उद्दिश्य aiming at, भीमरूपेण by a dreadful person, रक्षसा by the demon, जनस्थाने in Janasthana, महद्वधम् great killing, यथातथम् as it happened faithfully, अङ्गदः Angada, तस्य to him, अकथयत् told.

#### Explanation

None

---

### Shloka 66

#### Shloka Text

जटायुषो वधं श्रुत्वा दुःखितस्सोऽरुणात्मजः। त्वां शशंस वरारोहे वसन्तीं रावणालये।।5.35.66।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वरारोहे oh charming lady, सः he, अरुणात्मजः Aruna's son, जटायुषः Jatayu's, वधं death, श्रुत्वा having heard, दुःखितः became sad, त्वाम् yourself, रावणालये in Ravana's palace, वसन्तीम् living, शशंस narrated to us.

#### Explanation

"Having heard about the death of Jatayu, O charming lady that Sampati Aruna's son felt sad and told us that you are at Ravana's palace.

---

### Shloka 67

#### Shloka Text

तस्य तद्वचनं श्रुत्वा सम्पातेः प्रीतिवर्धनम्। अङ्गदप्रमुखास्तूर्णं ततस्सम्प्रस्थिता वयम्।।5.35.67।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तस्य his, सम्पातेः Samapati's, प्रीतिवर्धनम् that which was pleasing, तत् that, वचनम् word, श्रुत्वा having heard, अङ्गदप्रमुखाः Angada and others, वयम् all of us, तूर्णम् quickly, ततः from there, प्रस्थिताः departed.

#### Explanation

"Having heard Sampati's encouraging words all of us led by Angada departed immediately.

---

### Shloka 68

#### Shloka Text

विन्ध्यादुत्थाय सम्प्राप्ता स्सागरस्यान्तमुत्तरम्। त्वद्धर्शनकृतोत्साहा हृष्टास्तुष्टाः प्लवङ्गमाः।।5.35.68।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

प्लवङ्गमाः vanaras, त्वद्दर्शनकृतोत्साहा enthused to see you, हृष्टाः joyfully, तुष्टाः satisfied, विन्ध्यात् from Vindhya, उत्थाय having got up, सागरस्य towards the sea, उत्तरम् अन्तम् the limit of the northern direction, सम्प्राप्ताः reached.

#### Explanation

"The happy monkeys were satisfied. Enthusiastic about seeing you, they rose from the Vindhya mountain and reached the limit of the sea  shore in the north.

---

### Shloka 69

#### Shloka Text

अङ्गदप्रमुखास्सर्वे वेलोपान्तमुपस्थिताः। चिन्तां जग्मुः पुनर्भीतास्त्वद्दर्शनसमुत्सुकाः।।5.35.69।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अङ्गदप्रमुखाः Angada and other leaders, सर्वे all, त्वद्दर्शनमुत्सुकाः enthusiastic to see you, वेलोपान्तम् sea shore, उपस्थिताः reached, भीताः they were frightened, पुनः again, चिन्ताम् worry, जग्मुः felt.

#### Explanation

None

---

### Shloka 70

#### Shloka Text

अथाहं हरिसैन्यस्य सागरं प्रेक्ष्य सीदतः। व्यवधूय भयं तीव्रं योजनानां शतं प्लुतः।।5.35.70।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथ then, अहम् I, सागरम् ocean, प्रेक्ष्य after looking at, सीदतः in desperate mood, हरिसैन्यस्य Vanara army's, तीव्रम् intense, भयम् fear, व्यवधूय after pushing aside, योजनानाम् of yojanas, शतम् one hundred, प्लुतः leaped.

#### Explanation

"When the vanara army was feeling despondent at the sight of the ocean, I leaped across the sea, a hundred yojanas, pushing aside  all my fear.

---

### Shloka 71

#### Shloka Text

लङ्का चापि मया रात्रौ प्रविष्टा राक्षसाकुला। रावणश्च मया दृष्टस्त्वं च शोकपरिप्लुता।।5.35.71।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसाकुला filled with demons, लङ्का चापि Lanka also, मया by me, रात्रौ at night, प्रविष्टा entered, मया by me, रावणश्च Ravana also, दृष्टः is seen, शोकपरिप्लुता immersed in sorrow, त्वं च you also.

#### Explanation

"I entered at night the city of Lanka teeming with demons and saw Ravana and saw you immersed in sorrow.

---

### Shloka 72

#### Shloka Text

एतत्ते सर्वमाख्यातं यथावृत्तमनन्दिते। अभिभाषस्व मां देवि दूतो दाशरथेरहम्।।5.35.72।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अनिन्दिते O sinless lady, देवि O divine lady, एतत् this, सर्वम् everything, यथावृत्तम् as it happened, ते to you, आख्यातम् narrated, माम् me, अभिभाषस्व you may speak, अहम् I, दाशरथेः Dasaratha's son, दूतः messenger.

#### Explanation

"O sinless lady I have given you a faithful account. I am a messenger of Dasaratha's son. And you may speak now, O divine lady

---

### Shloka 73

#### Shloka Text

तं मां रामकृतोद्योगं त्वन्निमित्तमिहागतम्। सुग्रीवसचिवं देवि बुद्ध्यस्व पवनात्मजम्।।5.35.73।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

देवि O divine lady, तं माम् such me, रामकृतोद्योगम् have taken up Rama's mission, त्वन्निमित्तम् on your account, इह here, आगतम् came, सुग्रीवसचिवम् minister of Sugriva, पवनात्मजम् son of Vayu, बुद्ध्यस्व you may know.

#### Explanation

"O divine lady Know that I have come here for your cause carrying out the mission of Rama. I am Sugriva'a minister and son of the Windgod.

---

### Shloka 74

#### Shloka Text

कुशली तव काकुत्स्थस्सर्वशस्त्रभृतां वरः। गुरोराराधने युक्तो लक्ष्मणश्च सुलक्षणः।।5.35.74।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सर्वशस्त्रभृताम् among all wielders of weapons, वरः supreme, तव your, काकुत्स्थ Rama of the Kakutstha race, कुशली he is keeping well, गुरोः of his venerable teacher, आराधने in service, युक्तः engaged, सुलक्षणः a man of auspicious signs, लक्ष्मणश्च Lakshmana also.

#### Explanation

"Your lord Rama of Kakutstha race, supreme among wielders of weapons is doing well. So is venerable Lakshmana endowed with auspicious signs engaged in serving him.

---

### Shloka 75

#### Shloka Text

तस्य वीर्यवतो देवि भर्तुस्तव हिते रतः। अहमेकस्तु सम्प्राप्त स्सुग्रीववचनादिह।।5.35.75।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

देवि O queen, वीर्यवतः of valourous, तव your, भर्तुः husband's, तस्य his, हिते seeking welfare, रतः engaged, अहम् I, सुग्रीववचनात् at the behest of Sugriva, इह here, एकः alone, प्राप्तः reached.

#### Explanation

"O queen seeking the welfare of your Valiant husband I came here at the behest of Sugriva all alone.

---

### Shloka 76

#### Shloka Text

मयेयमसहायेन चरता कामरूपिणा। दक्षिणा दिगनुक्रान्ता त्वन्मार्गविचयैषिणा।।5.35.76।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्वन्मार्गविचयैषिणा while searching for your location, कामरूपिणा by a person who can take any form at will, असहायेन without any help, चरता while moving, मया by myself, इयम् this, दक्षिणा दिक् southern direction, अनुक्रान्ता reached.

#### Explanation

"I can assume any form at my will. I searched for you without any help moving in the southern direction and reached this place .

---

### Shloka 77

#### Shloka Text

दिष्ट्याहं हरिसैन्यानां त्वन्नाशमनुशोचताम्। अपनेष्यामि सन्तापं तवाभिगमशंसनात्।।5.35.77।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दिष्ट्या fortunately for me, अहम् I, त्वन्नाशम् your loss, अनुशोचताम् regretting, हरिसैन्यानाम् of the vanara army, सन्तापम् grief, तव your, अभिगमशंसनात् by letting them know, अपनेष्यामि I will dispel their sorrow.

#### Explanation

"Fortunately for me I can finally dispel the fear of the vanara chiefs who are regretting your loss, by letting them know that you have been found.

---

### Shloka 78

#### Shloka Text

दिष्ट्या हि मम न व्यर्थं देवि सागरलङ्घनम्। प्राप्स्याम्यहमिदं दिष्ट्या त्वद्दर्शनकृतं यशः।।5.35.78।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

देवि oh queen, दिष्ट्या luckily, मम my, सागरलङ्घनम् crossing the ocean, न व्यर्थम् is not wasted, दिष्ट्या fortunately, अहम् I, त्वद्दर्शनकृतम् by the act of seeing you, इदम् thus, यशः fame, प्राप्स्यामि I will attain.

#### Explanation

"Oh queen luckily for me, crossing the ocean has not gone in vain. I am fortunate because I will attain fame of finding you.

---

### Shloka 79

#### Shloka Text

राघवश्च महावीर्यः क्षिप्रं त्वामभिपत्स्यते। समित्रबान्धवं हत्वा रावणं राक्षसाधिपम्।।5.35.79।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महावीर्यः mighty hero, राघवश्च Raghava, समित्रबान्धवम् accompanied by friends and relatives, राक्षसाधिपम् king of demons, रावणम् Ravana, हत्वा after killing, क्षिप्रम् very soon, त्वाम् yourself, अभिपत्स्यते will reach you.

#### Explanation

"Mighty Raghava will take you after killing Ravana, the king of demons along with his kith and kin.

---

### Shloka 80

#### Shloka Text

माल्यवान्नाम वैदेहि गिरीणामुत्तमो गिरिः। ततो गच्छति गोकर्णं पर्वतं केसरी हरिः।।5.35.80।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वैदेहि Vaidehi, गिरीणाम् among the mountains, उत्तमः the best one, माल्यवान्नाम Malyavan, गिरिः mountain, ततः from there, केसरी Kesari, हरिः monkey, गोकर्णम् Gokarna, पर्वतम् mountain, गच्छति went.

#### Explanation

"Oh Vaidehi there is a famous mountain called Malyavan. From there a monkey called Kesari went to mount Gokarna.

---

### Shloka 81

#### Shloka Text

स च देवर्षिभिर्दिष्टः पिता मम महाकपिः। तीर्थे नदीपतेः पुण्ये शम्बसादनमुद्धरत्।।5.35.81।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

देवर्षिभिः by the divine sages, दिष्टः predicted, मम my, पिता father, सः महाकपिः that great monkey, नदीपतेः of lord of rivers, ocean, पुण्ये holy, तीर्थे in a place, शम्बसादनम् Sambasadanam, उद्धरत् raised.

#### Explanation

"He (Kesari) is said to be my father by the divine sages. He retrieved a piece of land in a holy spot of that sea shore and killed Sambasadana, a demon who was oppressing righteous people.

---

### Shloka 82

#### Shloka Text

तस्याहं हरिणः क्षेत्रे जातो वातेन मैथिलि। हनुमानिति विख्यातो लोके स्वेनैव कर्मणा।।5.35.82।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मैथिलि Mythili, अहम् I, तस्य his, हरिणः monkey's, क्षेत्रे in the land, वातेन by the Windgod, जातः born, स्वेन कर्मणा एव by my own act, लोके by people, हनुमानिति Hanuman, विख्यातः came to be known.

#### Explanation

"O Mythili I was born in that land retrieved by the Windgod. I am known as Hanuman by virtue of my own action.

---

### Shloka 83

#### Shloka Text

विश्वासार्थं तु वैदेहि भर्तुरुक्ता मया गुणाः। अचिराद्राघवो देवि त्वामितो नयिताऽनघे।।5.35.83।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वैदेहि Vaidehi, विश्वासार्थम् for inspiring confidence, मया by me, भर्तुः  your husband's, गुणाः qualities, उक्ताः are described, देवि O queen, राघवः Raghava, अचिरात् very soon, त्वाम् you, इतः from here, नयिता he will take you away, अनघे lady free from sins.

#### Explanation

"In order to inspire confidence in you about me I have narrated the virtues of your husband. O sinless queen Rama will take you from here very soon".

---

### Shloka 84

#### Shloka Text

एवं विश्वासिता सीता हेतुभिश्शोककर्शिता। उपपन्नैरभिज्ञानैर्दूतं तमवगच्छति।।5.35.84।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शोककर्शिता tormented by grief, सीता Sita, एवम् in that way, हेतुभिः with possible reasons, विश्वासिता having trusted, उपपन्नैः by the evidences, अभिज्ञानैः with identification, तम् him, दूतम् as a messenger, अवगच्छति she understood.

#### Explanation

The griefstricken Sita trusted him by his logical presentation of reasons as well as identifications. She believed that he was a messenger (of Rama).

---

### Shloka 85

#### Shloka Text

अतुलं च गता हर्षं प्रहर्षेण च जानकी। नेत्राभ्यां वक्रपक्ष्माभ्यां मुमोचानन्दजं जलम्।।5.35.85।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

जानकी Janaki, अतुलम् immeasurable, हर्षम् happiness, गता च experienced, प्रहर्षेण in her joy, वक्रपक्ष्माभ्याम् through her curved eyelashes, नेत्राभ्याम् from both eyes, आनन्दजम् arising out of joy, जलम् tears, मुमोच shed.

#### Explanation

Janaki experienced immeasurable happiness and shed tears of joy through her curved eyes.

---

### Shloka 86

#### Shloka Text

चारु तद्वदनं तस्यास्ताम्रशुक्लायतेक्षणम्। अशोभत विशालाक्ष्या राहुमुक्त इवोडुराट्।।5.35.86।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विशालाक्ष्याः of largeeyed lady, तस्याः her, चारु lovely, ताम्रशुक्लायतेक्षणम् having wide white eyes with tinge of red corners, तत् that, वदनम् face, राहुमुक्तः released from Rahu, उडुराडिव like the Moon, अशोभत shone.

#### Explanation

Her face with large eyes with a tinge of red corners shone like the Moon released from Rahu.

---

### Shloka 87

#### Shloka Text

हनुमन्तं कपिं व्यक्तं मन्यते नान्यथेति सा। अथोवाच हनूमांस्तामुत्तरं प्रियदर्शनाम्।।5.35.87।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सा she, हनुमन्तम् Hanuman, व्यक्तम् clearly, कपिम् the monkey, मन्यते realised, अन्यथा इति none other than, अथ then, हनूमान् Hanuman, प्रियदर्शनाम् a lady pleasing to see, ताम् her, उत्तराम् later, उवाच spoke.

#### Explanation

She realised that the monkey was none other than Hanuman. Then he looked at her who was pleasing to see and spoke again:

---

### Shloka 88

#### Shloka Text

एतत्ते सर्वमाख्यातं समाश्वसिहि मैथिलि। किं करोमि कथं वा ते रोचते प्रतियाम्यहम्।।5.35.88।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मैथिलि Mythili, ते to you, एतत् सर्वम् everything, आख्यातम् is narrated, समाश्वसिहि calm down, किम् what, करोमि shall I do, ते to you, कथं वा or how, रोचते it pleases, अहम् I, प्रतियामि I will go back.

#### Explanation

"Mythili, everything I intended to say has been narrated to you. Calm yourself down. What shall I do? Or else, tell me what pleases you. I shall go back.

---

### Shloka 89

#### Shloka Text

हतेऽसुरे संयति शम्बसादने कपिप्रवीरेण महर्षिचोदनात्। ततोऽस्मि वायुप्रभवो हि मैथिलि प्रभावतस्तत्प्रतिमश्च वानरः।।5.35.89।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मैथिलि Maithili, संयति in a fight, शम्बसादने Sambasadana, असुरे when the demon, कपिप्रवरेण mighty monkey, महर्षिचोदनात् by the command of great sage, हते सति where he was slain, अथ then, वायुप्रभवः by the spread of wind, प्रभावतः by his prowess, तत्प्रतिमः equal to him, वानरः vanara, अस्मि I am.

#### Explanation

"(My father) a mighty monkey killed Sambasadana in a battle on the command of a great seer. I am a vanara (born to a monkey) by the impact of the Windgod. I am equal to the Windgod in power." इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये सुन्दरकाण्डे पञ्चत्रिंशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the thirtyfifth sarga of Sundarakanda of the holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 36

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

भूय एव महातेजा हनूमान्पवनात्मजः। अब्रवीत्प्रश्रितं वाक्यं सीताप्रत्ययकारणात्।।5.36.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महातेजाः mighty, पवनात्मजः son of the Windgod, हनुमान् Hanuman, सीताप्रत्ययकारणात् in order to inspire trust in Sita, भूय एव again this way, प्रश्रितम् humbly, वाक्यम् these words, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

Mighty Hanuman, the son of the Windgod again spoke to Sita humbly to inspire confidence in her.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

वानरोऽहं महाभागे दूतो रामस्य धीमतः। रामनामाङ्कितं चेदं पश्य देव्यङ्गुलीयकम्।।5.36.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाभागे O noble lady, अहम् I am, धीमतः learned, रामस्य Rama's, दूतः envoy, वानरः vanara, देवि O godlike lady, रामनामाङ्कितम् which has the name of Rama inscribed, इदम् this, अङ्गुलीयकं च ring also, पश्य you see.

#### Explanation

"O noble lady I am a vanara, an envoy of learned Rama. You see this ring with Rama's name inscribed on it, O godlike lady

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

प्रत्ययार्थं तवाऽनीतं तेन दत्तं महात्मना। समाश्वसिहि भद्रं ते क्षीणदुःखफला ह्यसि।।5.36.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महात्मना greatself, तेन by him, दत्तम् given, तव your, प्रत्ययार्थम् to make you trust, अनीतम् is brought, समाश्वसिहि be calm, ते to you, भद्रम् let you be auspicious, क्षीणदुःखफला your sorows ended, असि हि indeed.

#### Explanation

"Be calm. Be blessed. Your sorrows have come to an end. This (the ring) is given by the great self, Rama, to make you trust me.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

गृहीत्वा प्रेक्षमाणा सा भर्तुः करविभूषणम्। भर्तारमिव सम्प्राप्ता जानकी मुदिताऽभवत्।।5.36.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भर्तुः husband, करविभूषणम् ornament that adorned his hand, गृहीत्वा having received, प्रेक्षमाणा gazing, सा जानकी Janaki, भर्तारम् husband, सम्प्राप्ता इव as if reached, मुदिता delighted, अभवत् became.

#### Explanation

She received the ornament that adorned her husband's hand and gazed at it and felt delighted as though her husband had arrived.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

चारु तद्वदनं तस्यास्ताम्रशुक्लायतेक्षणम्। अशोभत विशालाक्ष्या राहुमुक्त इवोडुराट्।।5.36.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तस्यास्ताम्रशुक्लायतेक्षणम् her white eyes with reddish tinge at the corners of the eyes, चारु beautiful, तद्वदनं her countenance, विशालाक्ष्या largeeyed, राहुमुक्त released by Rahu, इवोडुराट् like the Moon अशोभत shone

#### Explanation

Her beautiful countenance with large white eyes with a reddish tinge in the corners shone like the Moon released from Rahu.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

ततस्सा ह्रीमती बाला भर्तृसन्देशहर्षिता। परितुष्टा प्रियं कृत्वा प्रशशंस महाकपिम्।।5.36.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, बाला young lady, सा that Sita, भर्तृसन्देशहर्षिता delighted at the news of her husband, ह्रीमती blushing lady, परितुष्टा felt happy, प्रियम् pleasing, कृत्वा action, महाकपिम् by the great monkey, प्रशशंस praised.

#### Explanation

Young Sita felt delighted about her husband's news and blushed. By praising him for his action, she pleased the great monkey.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

विक्रान्तस्त्वं समर्थस्त्वं प्राज्ञस्त्वं वानरोत्तम। येनेदं राक्षसपदं त्वयैकेन प्रधर्षितम्।।5.36.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वानरोत्तम O foremost among the vanaras, एकेन single handed, येन by which, त्वया by you, इदम् this, राक्षसपदम् demon's abode, प्रधर्षितम् assailed, त्वम् you, विक्रान्तः you are very brave, त्वम् you, समर्थः are able, त्वम् you, प्राज्ञः are wise.

#### Explanation

"O foremost among the vanaras you have trespassed the abodes of demons all alone. You are mighty and capable and wise.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

शतयोजनविस्तीर्ण स्सागरो मकरालयः। विक्रमश्लाघनीयेन क्रमता गोष्पदीकृतः।।5.36.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विक्रमश्लाघनीयेन by your admirable prowess, क्रमता advancing, शतयोजनविस्तीर्णः extended over a hundred yojanas, मकरालयः abode of crocodiles, सागरः ocean, गोष्पदीकृतः is reduced to calf's hoof mark.

#### Explanation

"You with your admirable prowess have reduced this ocean which is an abode of crocodiles stretching over a hundred yojanas to a cow's hoof mark.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

न हि त्वां प्राकृतं मन्ये वानरं वानरर्षभ। यस्य ते नास्ति सन्त्रासो रावणान्नापि सम्भ्रमः।।5.36.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वानरर्षभ O bull among vanaras, यस्य of whom, ते to you, रावणात् from Ravana, सन्त्रासः fear, नास्ति not there, सम्भ्रमः अपि even excitement, न not, त्वाम् for you, प्राकृतम् ordinary, वानरम् monkey, न मन्ये I do not think.

#### Explanation

"I do not think you are an ordinary monkey. You have no fear or excitement about Ravana. You are a bull among vanaras.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

अर्हसे च कपिश्रेष्ठ मया समभिभाषितुम्। यद्यसि प्रेषितस्तेन रामेण विदितात्मना।।5.36.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कपिश्रेष्ठ O best among monkeys, विदितात्मना by a knower of self, तेन by him, रामेण by Rama, प्रेषितः sent, असि यदि you are, मया by me, समभिभाषितुम् to converse, अर्हसे you deserve.

#### Explanation

"O best of monkeys As you have been sent by Rama, the knower of the self, it is proper for me to converse with you.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

प्रेषयिष्यति दुर्धर्षो रामो न ह्यपरीक्षितम्। पराक्रममविज्ञाय मत्सकाशं विशेषतः।।5.36.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दुर्धर्षः formidable, रामः Rama, पराक्रमम् valour, अविज्ञाय without knowing, अपरीक्षितम् without testing, न प्रेषयिष्यति हि would not send any body, विशेषतः specially, मत्सकाशम् to me.

#### Explanation

"The formidable Rama will not send any one specially to me without testing him or knowing his valour.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

दिष्ट्या च कुशली रामो धर्मात्मा सत्यसङ्गरः। लक्ष्मणश्च महातेजास्सुमित्रानन्दवर्धनः।।5.36.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दिष्ट्या luckily, धर्मात्मा righteous, सत्यसङ्गरः one who follows truth, रामः Rama, महातेजाः glorious, सुमित्रानन्दवर्धनः enhancer of Sumitra's joy, लक्ष्मणश्च Lakshmana also, कुशली well.

#### Explanation

"To my luck, righteous Rama who abides in truth and glorious Lakshmana who enhances the joy of Sumitra are keeping well.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

कुशली यदि काकुत्स्थः किं नु सागरमेखलाम्। महीं दहति कोपेन युगान्ताग्निरिवोत्थितः।।5.36.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

काकुत्स्थः Kakutsthas, कुशली यदि if he is keeping well, उत्थितः generated, युगान्ताग्निरिव at the time of dissolution of the era, सागरमेखलाम् oceangirdled, महीम् earth, कोपेन with his rage, किम् why, न दहति not burning?

#### Explanation

"Indeed if Kakutstha is well wherefore does he not burn the oceangirdled earth in his rage as (the earth is burnt) at the time of dissolution of the era?

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

अथवा शक्तिमन्तौ तौ सुराणामपि निग्रहे। ममैव तु न दुःखानामस्ति मन्ये विपर्ययः।।5.36.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथवा or else, तौ both, सुराणामपि even gods, निग्रहे in subduing, शक्तिमन्तौ both are capable, तु but, मम my, दुःखानामेव for my sorrow, विपर्ययः disaster, नास्ति not there, मन्ये I think.

#### Explanation

"Both of them are capable of subduing even the gods. I think the reversal of  my fate is reason for their inaction.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

कच्चिन्न व्यथितो रामः कच्चिन्न परितप्यते। उत्तराणि च कार्याणि कुरुते पुरुषोत्तमः।।5.36.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामः Rama, न व्यथितः कच्चित् whether not depressed, न परितप्यते कच्चित् indeed not suffering, पुरुषोत्तमः great among men, उत्तराणि necessary steps, कार्याणि action, कुरुते will be carrying out perhaps?

#### Explanation

"Is Rama the greatest among men not depressed? Does he not feel helpless? He is (perhaps) taking necessary steps (for my recovery).

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

कच्चिन्न दीन स्सम्भ्रान्तः कार्येषु न च मुह्यति। कच्चित्पुरुषकार्याणि कुरुते नृपतेस्सुतः।।5.36.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नृपतेः सुतः king's son, दीनः depressed, सम्भ्रान्तः perplexed, न कच्चित् not indeed, कार्येषु at work, न च मुह्यति is not spoiling, पुरुषकार्याणि the actions of men of achievement, कच्चित् कुरुते surely he is doing I trust

#### Explanation

"I suppose prince Rama is not too depressed or perplexed to attend to his work. He will (perhaps) be doing the duties expected of a manly person.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

द्विविधं त्रिविधोपायमुपायमपि सेवते। विजिगीषुस्सुहृत्कच्चिन्मित्रेषु च परन्तपः।।5.36.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

परन्तपः scorcher of enemies, द्विविधम् two kinds, उपायम् strategies, त्रिविधोपायमपि even three kinds of trategies, सेवते following, विजगीषुः he who desires to be victorious, मित्रेषु with friends, सुहृत् कच्चित् has a good team indeed

#### Explanation

"Tell me if Rama, scorcher of enemies, desirous of victory is following a two-fold policy (confrontation and conciliation) A tough enemy cannot be defeated by following this method. A three-fold strategy has to be adopted in this case (confrontation, conciliation and creation of dissension among his foes) to be victorious. I hope he is well-disposed to his friends and has a good team!

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

कच्चिन्मित्राणि लभते मित्रैश्चाप्यभिगम्यते। कच्चित्कल्याणमित्त्रश्च मित्रत्त्रैश्चापि पुरस्कृतः।।5.36.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मित्राणि allies, कच्चित् लभते hope he will be making, मित्रैः with allies, अपि अभिगम्यते will want his friendship, कल्याणमित्रश्च कच्चित् hope he has good allies, मित्रैश्च by his allies, अपि पुरस्कृतः honoured

#### Explanation

"I hope he is making new friends, who desire his friendship. Hopefully, Rama is well honoured  by his allies.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

कच्चिदाशास्ति देवानां प्रसादं पार्थिवात्मजः। कच्चित्पुरुषकारं च दैवं च प्रतिपद्यते।।5.36.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पार्थिवात्मजः prince, देवानाम् of gods, प्रसादम् blessings, आशास्ति कच्चित् is there any hope, पुरुषकारं च his personal efforts, दैवं च providence, प्रतिपद्यते he obeys

#### Explanation

"Does the prince ever seek the blessings of gods or fall back upon personal effort as well as providence?

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

कच्चिन्न विगतस्नेहः प्रवासान्मयि राघवः। कच्चिन्मां व्यसनादस्मान्मोक्षयिष्यति वानर।।5.36.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राघवः Rama, प्रवासात् due to separation, मयि in me विगतस्नेहः devoid of love, न कच्चित् suppose not, वानर vanara, माम् me, अस्मात् from this, व्यसनात् worry, मोक्षयिष्यति कच्चित् will liberate me from this

#### Explanation

"I hope Rama is not devoid of love towards me by  my separation. O Vanara, hope he will liberate me from this misery.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

सुखानामुचितो नित्यमसुखानामनौचितः। दुःखमुत्तरमासाद्य कच्चिद्रामो न सीदति।।5.36.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नित्यम् always, सुखानाम् delight, उचितः one who deserves, असुखानाम् of difficulties, अनौचितः he does not deserve, राघवः Rama, उत्तरम् later, दुःखम् sorrow, आसाद्य having experienced, न सीदति कच्चित्  hope he is not depressed

#### Explanation

"I hope Rama who deserves happiness and not suffering has not become depressed now , having experienced a spate of sorrows.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

कौसल्यायास्तथा कच्चित्सुमित्रायास्तथैव च। अभीक्ष्णं श्रूयते कच्चित्कुशलं भरतस्य च।।5.36.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कौसल्यायाः Kausalya's, तथा in the same manner, सुमित्रायाः Sumitra's, तथैव च in the same way, भरतस्य Bharata's, कुशलम् welfare, अभीक्ष्णम् constantly, श्रूयते कच्चित् hope he is hearing

#### Explanation

"I hope he regularly receives news about the welfare of Kausalya, Sumitra and Bharata.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

मन्निमित्तेन मानार्हः कच्चिच्छोकेन राघवः। कच्चिन्नान्यमना रामः कच्चिन्मां तारयिष्यति।।5.36.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मानार्हः respectable, राघवः Rama, मन्निमित्तेन on my account, शोकेन because of sorrow, कच्चित् I hope, रामः Rama, नअन्यमनाः he is not absent minded, न कच्चित् may be, माम् me, कच्चित् तारयिष्यति will he save me?

#### Explanation

"I hope respectable Rama is not too absentminded for being immersed in sorrow on my account. Hope he will save me.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

कच्चिदक्षौहिणीं भीमां भरतो भ्रातृवत्सलः। ध्वजिनीं मन्त्रिभिर्गुप्तां प्रेषयिष्यति मत्कृते।।5.36.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भ्रातृवत्सलः devoted to brother, भरतः Bharata, मत्कृते for my sake, मन्त्रिभिः with his ministers, गुप्ताम् protected, भीमाम् formidable, अक्षौहिणीम् ध्वजिनीम् akshauhini army, कच्चित् प्रेषयिष्यति  I trust he will send

#### Explanation

"Will Bharata, the devoted brother, send his formidable army of one akshavhini protected by ministers for my cause? I trust he will. (Akshauhini  109350 foot soldiers  65610 horse soldiers  21860 elephants soldiers on chariot)

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

वानराधिपतिश्शीमान्सुग्रीवः कच्चिदेष्यति। मत्कृते हरिभिर्वीरैर्वृतो दन्तनखायुधैः।।5.36.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मत्कृते for my sake, श्रीमान् illustrious, वानराधिपतिः king of vanaras, सुग्रीवः Sugriva, दन्तनखायुधै: those who use their teeth and nails as weapons, वीरैः with valiant ones, हरिभिः with vanaras, वृतः surrounded, एष्वति कच्चित् trust he will reach.

#### Explanation

"I trust that the illustrious Sugriva surrounded  by valiant vanaras who use their teeth and nails as weapons will reach this place.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

कच्चिच्छ लक्ष्मणश्शूरस्सुमित्रानन्दवर्धनः। अस्त्रविच्छरजालेन राक्षसान्विधमिष्यति।।5.36.26।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शूरः hero, सुमित्रानन्दवर्धनः who enhances Sumitra's delight, अस्त्रवित् a master of weapons, लक्ष्मणः Lakshmana, शरजालेन by network of arrows, राक्षसान् demons, कच्चित् विधमिष्यति trust he will blow away.

#### Explanation

"Hope heroic Lakshmana, enhancer of the joy of Sumitra, who has mastered the use of weapons will blow away the demons through a network of arrows.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

रौद्रेण कच्चिदस्त्रेण ज्वलता निहतं रणे। द्रक्ष्याम्यल्पेन कालेन रावणं ससुहृज्जनम्।।5.36.27।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ज्वलता burning, रौद्रेण with anger, अस्त्रेण by weapon, रणे in the battle, ससुहृज्जनम् along with his relatives, निहतम् slain, रावणम् Ravana, अल्पेन कालेन  in a short time, द्रक्ष्यामि कच्चित् hope to see.

#### Explanation

"Can I hope to see Ravana and his relatives slain in the battle by fiery weapons in a short time?

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

कच्चिन्न तद्धेमसमानवर्णं तस्याननं पद्मसमानगन्धि। मया विना शुष्यति शोकदीनं जलक्षये पद्ममिवातपेन।।5.36.28।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तस्य his, हेमसमानवर्णम् complexion like gold, पद्मसमानगन्धि fragrance  like  lotus, तत् that, आननम् face, मया विना without me, शोकदीनम् pale with grief, जलक्षये when water dries up, आतपेन due to heat, पद्ममिव like a lotus, न शुष्यति कच्चित् not scorched

#### Explanation

"Hope, Rama's moonlike face without me with its golden complexion and lotus fragrance must not have been pale and withered with tears like the lotus in a pond scorched by the heat.

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

धर्मापदेशात्त्यजतश्च राज्यं मां चाप्यरण्यं नयतः पदातिम्। नासीद्व्यथा यस्य न भीर्न शोकः कच्चित्स धैर्यं हृदये करोति।।5.36.29।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

धर्मापदेशात् for the cause of establishing righteousness, राज्यम् sovereignty, त्यजतः given up, मां चापि including me, पदातिम् on foot, अरण्यम् to the forest, नयतः conducted, यस्य whose, व्यथा agony, नासीत् not shown, भीः fear, न not, शोकः grief, न not, सः he, हृदये at heart, धैर्यम् courage, करोति कच्चित् will sustain I hope.

#### Explanation

"Rama has given up sovereignty for the sake of establishing righteousness and led me into the forest on foot.He had not then felt any fear, anxiety or grief at heart.Rama showed patience, instead. Hope he retains them.

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

न चास्य माता न पिता च नान्यः स्नेहाद्विशिष्टोऽस्ति मया समो वा। तावत्त्वहं दूत जिजीविषेयं यावत्प्रवृत्तिं शृणुयां प्रियस्य।।5.36.30।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अस्य his, स्नेहात् due to love, मया with me, समो वा equal or, विशिष्टः superior, माता mother, न not, पिता father, न not, अन्यः any one else, नास्ति not  there, दूत messenger, प्रियस्य beloved's, प्रवृत्तिम् news, यावत् until, शृणुयाम् I may hear, तावत्तु so long, अहम् I, जिजीविषेयम् I wish to survive.

#### Explanation

"His love for me is superior to his love for his father, mother or any one else. O messenger, I wish to survive till I hear any  news from my dear lord."

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

इतीव देवी वचनं महार्थं तं वानरेन्द्रं मधुरार्थमुक्त्वा। श्रोतुं पुनस्तस्य वचोऽभिरामं रामार्थयुक्तं विरराम रामा।।5.36.31।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

देवी queen, तं वानरेन्द्रम् that vanara leader, महार्थम् very meaningful, मधुरार्थम् sweet, वचनम् word, उक्त्वा having spoken, तस्य his, अभिरामम्  loving Rama, रामार्थयुक्तम् said for Rama's cause, वचः word, पुनः again, श्रोतुम् to hear, विरराम stopped.

#### Explanation

The godlike lady, having spoken to the vanara leader in a very meaningful and sweet manner about her beloved Rama, she remained silent, waiting to hear Hanuman's attractive words of description of Rama once again.

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

सीताया वचनं श्रुत्वा मारुतिर्भीमविक्रमः। शिरस्यञ्जलिमाधाय वाक्यमुत्तरमब्रवीत्।।5.36.32।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भीमविक्रमः fearsome power, मारुतिः Maruti, सीतायाः Sita's, वचनम् word, श्रुत्वा on hearing, शिरसि on his head, अञ्जलिम् both his hands, आधाय placing, उत्तरम् reply, वाक्यम् words, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

Having heard Sita, Hanuman endowed with fearsome power placed his folded hands on his head and said this:

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

न त्वामिहस्थां जानीते रामः कमललोचने। तेन त्वां नानयत्याशु शचीमिव पुरन्दरः।।5.36.33।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कमललोचने lotuseyed, त्वाम् you, इहस्थाम् being here, रामः Rama, न जानीते he does not know, तेन therefore, पुरन्दरः Indra, शचीमिव like Sachi, त्वाम् you, आशु immediately, नानयति he is not taking away.

#### Explanation

"O lotuseyed one Rama knows not that you are here. Therefore he has not taken you away immediately as did Indra to rescue his wife Sachi.

---

### Shloka 34

#### Shloka Text

श्रुत्वैव तु वचो मह्यं क्षिप्रमेष्यति राघवः। चमूं प्रकर्षन्महतीं हर्यृक्षगणसङ्कुलाम्।।5.36.34।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मह्यं my, वचः word, श्रुत्वैव तु after hearing, राघवः Rama, हर्यृक्षगणसङ्कुलाम् troops of vanaras and bears, महतीम् huge, चमूम् army, प्रकर्षन् leading, क्षिप्रम् at once, एष्यति will approach.

#### Explanation

"As soon as he hears your words from me, Rama will reach (here), leading a huge army of powerful troops of vanaras and bears.

---

### Shloka 35

#### Shloka Text

विष्टम्भयित्वा बाणौघैरक्षोभ्यं वरुणालयम्। करिष्यति पुरीं लङ्कां काकुत्स्थः शान्तराक्षसाम्।।5.36.35।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

काकुत्स्थः Kakutstha, अक्षोभ्यम् imperturbable, वरुणालयम् abode of Varuna, बाणौघैः by torrent of arrows, विष्टम्भयित्वा make still, लङ्कां पुरीम् city of Lanka, शान्तराक्षसाम् all the demons, करिष्यति will make.

#### Explanation

"Kakutstha Rama will still the imperturbable abode of Varuna (ocean) by the torrent of his arrows and rid the city of Lanka of all the demons.

---

### Shloka 36

#### Shloka Text

तत्र यद्यन्तरा मृत्युर्यदि देवास्सहासुराः। स्थास्यन्ति पथि रामस्य स तानपि वधिष्यति।।5.36.36।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तत्र there, रामस्य Rama's, पथि on his way, अन्तरा in between, मृत्युः death, सहासुराः including all demons, देवाः gods, स्थास्यन्ति यदि if they obstruct, सः he, तानपि even them, वधिष्यति will slay.

#### Explanation

"Thereafter, whoever comes Rama's way,  be it  gods or demons or even death or any one else he will destroy them.

---

### Shloka 37

#### Shloka Text

तवादर्शनजेनार्ये शोकेन स परिप्लुतः। न शर्म लभते रामस्सिंहार्दित इव द्विपः।।5.36.37।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

आर्ये O noble lady, सः रामः that Rama, तव your, अदर्शनजेन on account of not seeing you, शोकेन in sorrow, परिप्लुतः immersed, सिंहार्दितः tormented by a lion, द्विपः इव like an elephant, शर्म न लभते is not happy.

#### Explanation

"O noble lady not being able to see you, Rama is immersed in an ocean of sorrow, he has no solace like an elephant tormented by a lion.

---

### Shloka 38

#### Shloka Text

मलयेन च विन्ध्येन मेरुणा मन्दरेण च। दर्दुरेण च ते देवि शपे मूलफलेन च।।5.36.38।। यथा सुनयनं वल्गु बिम्बोष्ठं चारु कुण्डलम् । मुखं द्रक्ष्यसि रामस्य पूर्णचन्द्रमिवोदितम्।।5.36.39।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

देवि O Sita, मलयेन च by Malaya mountain, विन्ध्येन by Vindhya, मेरुणा by Meru, दर्दुरेण by Dardura, मूलफलेन by roots and fruits, ते to you, शपे I swear, यथा as, सुनयनम्  with beautiful eyes, वल्गु बिम्बोष्ठम् lips like bimba fruit, चारु कुण्डलम् with lovely earrings, उदितम् risen, पूर्णचन्द्रमिव like the fullmoon, रामस्य Rama's, मुखम् face, द्रक्ष्यसि you will see.

#### Explanation

"O Sita I swear in the name of mountains like Malaya, Vindhya, Meru, and Dardura (the abodes of monkeys and bears) and  by the roots and fruits as well (which are the food of vanaras). you will soon behold Rama with beautiful eyes, red lips like bimba fruit, lovely earrings and a countenance resembling the full moon.

---

### Shloka 39

#### Shloka Text

मलयेन च विन्ध्येन मेरुणा मन्दरेण च। दर्दुरेण च ते देवि शपे मूलफलेन च।।5.36.38।। यथा सुनयनं वल्गु बिम्बोष्ठं चारु कुण्डलम् । मुखं द्रक्ष्यसि रामस्य पूर्णचन्द्रमिवोदितम्।।5.36.39।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

देवि O Sita, मलयेन च by Malaya mountain, विन्ध्येन by Vindhya, मेरुणा by Meru, दर्दुरेण by Dardura, मूलफलेन by roots and fruits, ते to you, शपे I swear, यथा as, सुनयनम्  with beautiful eyes, वल्गु बिम्बोष्ठम् lips like bimba fruit, चारु कुण्डलम् with lovely earrings, उदितम् risen, पूर्णचन्द्रमिव like the fullmoon, रामस्य Rama's, मुखम् face, द्रक्ष्यसि you will see.

#### Explanation

"O Sita I swear in the name of mountains like Malaya, Vindhya, Meru, and Dardura (the abodes of monkeys and bears) and  by the roots and fruits as well (which are the food of vanaras). you will soon behold Rama with beautiful eyes, red lips like bimba fruit, lovely earrings and a countenance resembling the full moon.

---

### Shloka 40

#### Shloka Text

क्षिप्रं द्रक्ष्यसि वैदेहि रामं प्रस्रवणे गिरौ। शतक्रतुमिवासीनं नागराजस्य मूर्धनि।।5.36.40।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वैदेहि Vaidehi, प्रस्रवणे गिरौ on mount Prasravana, रामम् Rama, नागराजस्य elephant's, मूर्धनि on the back, आसीनम् seated, शतक्रतुम् इव like Indra who has performed a hundred sacrifices, क्षिप्रम् soon, द्रक्ष्यसि you will see.

#### Explanation

"O Vaidehi, you will soon see Rama seated on the Prasravana mountain just as Indra (who performed a hundred sacrifices) seated on the back of the king of elephants.

---

### Shloka 41

#### Shloka Text

न मांसं राघवो भुङक्ते न चाऽपि मधु सेवते। वन्यं सुविहितं नित्यं भक्तमश्नाति पञ्चमम्।।5.36.41।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राघवः Rama, मांसम् meat, न भुङक्ते not eating, मधुचापि even wine, न सेवते he is not taking, नित्यम् always, पञ्चमम् fifth meal, सुविहितम् prescribed for, वन्यम् available in the forest, भुक्तम् food, अश्नाति eats.

#### Explanation

"Rama is not eating meat, nor drinking wine. He takes only the one fifth of a meal (sanctioned for an ascetic) available in the forest.

---

### Shloka 42

#### Shloka Text

नैव दंशान्न मशकान्न कीटान्न सरीसृपान्। राघवोऽपनयेद्गात्रात्त्वद्गतेनान्तरात्मना।।5.36.42।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राघवः Rama, त्वद्गतेन thinking of you, अन्तरात्मना with heart, गात्रात् from his limbs, दंशान् flies, नैव अपनयेत् will not drive away, मशकान् mosquitoes, न not, कीटान् insects, न not, सरीसृपान् serpents, न not.

#### Explanation

"Since you left, Rama has been always thinking sincerely of you, no longer drives away flies or mosquitoes nor insects or serpents from his body (his mind entirely fixed on you).

---

### Shloka 43

#### Shloka Text

नित्यं ध्यानपरो रामो नित्यं शोकपरायणः। नान्यच्चिन्तयते किञ्चित्स तु कामवशं गतः।।5.36.43।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामः Rama, नित्यम् ever, ध्यानपरः reflecting on you, नित्यम् ever, शोकपरायणः totally given to grief, सः he, कामवशं गतः absorbed in love for you, अन्यत् no other thoughts, न चिन्तयते he is not thinking.

#### Explanation

"Rama is always lost in thought. He is totally given to grief. Absorbed in love for you, he thinks of none else৷৷

---

### Shloka 44

#### Shloka Text

अनिद्रस्सततं रामस्सुप्तोऽपि च नरोत्तमः। सीतेति मधुरां वाणीं व्याहरन्प्रतिबुध्यते।।5.36.44।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामः Rama, सततम् always, अनिद्रः he does not sleep, नरोत्तमः best of men, सुप्तोऽपि even if he sleeps, सीतेति calling out 'Sita', मधुराम् sweet, वाणीम् voice, व्याहरन् uttering, प्रतिबुध्यते he wakes up.

#### Explanation

"Rama, the best of men never goes to sleep and even if he drops off to sleep he mutters 'Sita, Sita' in a sweet voice in his sleep.

---

### Shloka 45

#### Shloka Text

दृष्ट्वा फलं वा पुष्पं वा यद्वाऽन्यत्सुमनोहरम्। बहुशो हा प्रियेत्येवं श्वसंस्त्वामभिभाषते।।5.36.45।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

फलं वा fruit or, पुष्पं वा or flowers, यद्वा such similar, सुमनोहरम् delightful, अन्यत् any other thing, दृष्ट्वा seeing, श्वसन् while, हा प्रियेत्येवम् alas, darling, त्वाम् you, बहुशः in many, अभिभाषते speaks.

#### Explanation

"Sighing deeply whenever Rama sees a fruit or a flower or any such delightful thing, he remembers you and calls you out again and again, saying, 'alas, my darling '

---

### Shloka 46

#### Shloka Text

स देवि नित्यं परितप्यमान स्त्वामेव सीतेत्यभिभाषमाणः। धृतव्रतो राजसुतो महात्मा तवैव लाभाय कृतप्रयत्नः।।5.36.46।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

देवि godlike lady, महात्मा great self, सः Rama, राजसुतः son of a king, नित्यम् always, परितप्यमानः suffering, सीतेति saying Sita, त्वामेव you only, अभिभाषमाणः keeps talking in the air, धृतव्रतः firm in his decision, तव your, लाभाय to secure you only, कृतप्रयत्नः makes efforts.

#### Explanation

"O divine lady prince Rama is ceaselessly suffering. He keeps talking to you in air. The determined Rama is only thinking of your recovery ".

---

### Shloka 47

#### Shloka Text

सा रामसङ्कीर्तनवीतशोका रामस्य शोकेन समानशोका। शरन्मुखे साम्बुदशेषचन्द्रा निशेव वैदेहसुता बभूव।।5.36.47।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामस्य Rama's, शोकेन because of  his sorrow, समानशोका equally sad, रामसङ्कीर्तनवीतशोका devoid of sorrow on hearing about Rama, सा she, वैदेहसुता the daughter of Videha, शरन्मुखे at the advent of autumn, साम्बुदशेषचन्द्रा Moon muffled by clouds, निशेव like the night, बभूव appeared.

#### Explanation

Her sorrow was mitigated by references made by Hanuman about Rama.She felt equally sad when she heard about Rama's agony. The princess from Videha appeared like a night at the advent of autumn, with the Moon looking partly muffled by clouds and partly bright. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये सुन्दरकाण्डे षट्त्रिंशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the thirtysixth sarga of Sundarakanda of the holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 37

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

सीता तद्वचनं श्रुत्वा पूर्णचन्द्रनिभानना। हनूमन्तमुवाचेदं धर्मार्थसहितं वचः।।5.37.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पूर्णचन्द्रनिभानना face like the full moon, सीता Sita, तद्वचनम् that word, श्रुत्वा having heard, हनूमन्तम् Hanuman, इदम् this, धर्मार्थसहितम् full of righteousness, वचः word, उवाच spoke.

#### Explanation

To the righteous words (about Rama's grief) of Hanuman, Sita with a face like the full moon replied.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

अमृतं विषसंसृष्टं त्वया वानर भाषितम्। यच्च नान्यमना रामो यच्च शोकपरायणः ।।5.37.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वानर O vanara, रामः Rama, अन्यमनाः न not thinking of others,  इति यत् that way, इति च यत् and this way also (that he is sad), त्वया by you, भाषितम् spoken, विषसंसृष्टम् mixed with poison, अमृतम् nectar.

#### Explanation

"O vanara you have told me that Rama has his mind always fixed on me, not on others and you have also said that he is sad. It is like offering me poison mixed with nectar.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

ऐश्वर्ये वा सुविस्तीर्णे व्यसने वा सुदारुणे। रज्ज्वेव पुरुषं बद्ध्वा कृतान्तः परिकर्षति।।5.37.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कृतान्तः death, पुरुषम् a man, रज्ज्वा with a cord, बद्ध्वा इव like tying, सुविस्त्रीर्णे in the great, ऐश्वर्ये वा in fortune, सुदारुणे deeply sunk, व्यसने वा  in adversity, परिकर्षति will snatch.

#### Explanation

"Death snatches a man tied with a cord, whether he is rolling in luxury or sinking in adversity.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

विधिर्नूनमसंहार्यः प्राणिनां प्लवगोत्तम। सौमित्रिं मां च रामं च व्यसनै: पश्य मोहितान्।।5.37.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

प्लवगोत्तम O best of vanaras, प्राणिनाम् for living beings, नूनम् surely, विधिः destiny, असंहार्यः it cannot be overcome, व्यसनैः with sorrows, मोहितान् afflicted, सौमित्रिम् Saumitri, मां च me too, रामं च Rama also, पश्य you see.

#### Explanation

"O best of vanaras surely it is not possible for living beings to overcome destiny. You see how Sri Rama, Saumitri, and my own self are all   afflicted with grief.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

शोकस्यास्य कदा पारं राघवोऽधिगमिष्यति। प्लवमानः परिश्रान्तो हतनौ स्सागरे यथा।।5.37.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सागरे in the ocean, हतनौः in shipwreck, प्लवमानः while getting drowned, परिश्रान्तः यथा  who has suffered, राघवः Rama, अस्य of this, शोकस्य from grief, पारम् the shore, कदा when, अधिगमिष्यति will he reach.

#### Explanation

"When will Raghava reach the shore of this ocean of grief? He is like a man who has suffered a ship wreck, trying to swim?

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

राक्षसानां वधं कृत्वा सूदयित्वा च रावणम्। लङ्कामुन्मूलितां कृत्वा कदा द्रक्ष्यति मां पतिः।।5.37.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पतिः husband, राक्षसानाम् of all the demons, वधम् killing, कृत्वा after doing, रावणं च and Ravana, सूदयित्वा after killing, लङ्काम् Lanka, उन्मूलिताम् uprooting, कृत्वा after doing, माम् कदा when me, द्रक्ष्यति he will see.

#### Explanation

"When will my husband see me after killing the demons including Ravana and uprooting Lanka?

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

स वाच्यस्संत्वरस्वेति यावदेव न पूर्यते। अयं संवत्सरः कालस्तावद्धि मम जीवितम्।।5.37.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अयम् this, संवत्सरः कालः one year's time, यावदेव until then, न पूर्यते before it is completed, संत्वरस्वेति you may be quick, सः he, वाच्यः he should be informed, मम I, जीवितम् life, तावद्धि till then only.

#### Explanation

"One year's time has been given (by Ravana) to me.  Rama should be informed to be quick in reaching before the completion of the time limit and that I will survive till then only.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

वर्तते दशमो मासो द्वौ तु शेषौ प्लवङ्गम। रावणेन नृशंसेन समयो यः कृतो मम।।5.37.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

प्लवङ्गम O vanara, नृशंसेन रावणेन by the wicked Ravana, मम to me, यः such, समयः time, कृतः given, दशमः tenth, मासः month, वर्तते is running, द्वौ तु two only, शेषौ remain.

#### Explanation

"O vanara this is the end of the tenth month. Only two more months are left. This is the condition laid by the wicked Ravana in my case.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

विभीषणेन च भ्रात्रा मम निर्यातनं प्रति। अनुनीतः प्रयत्नेन न च तत्कुरुते मतिम्।।5.37.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भ्रात्रा by the brother, विभीषणेन by Vibhisana, मम my, निर्यातनं प्रति for my restoration, प्रयत्नेन made efforts, अनुनीतः he was advised gently, तत् such, मतिम् thought, न कुरुते he has not agreed.

#### Explanation

"Vibhisana made efforts to advise Ravana for my restoration. But Ravana has not agreed to that.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

मम प्रतिप्रदानं हि रावणस्य न रोचते। रावणं मार्गते संख्ये मृत्युः कालवशं गतम्।।5.37.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मम my, प्रतिप्रदानम् returning, रावणस्य to Ravana, न रोचते is not liked, कालवशं गतम् fallen into the trap of time, रावणम् Ravana, संख्ये in a battle, मृत्युः death, मार्गते  is searching.

#### Explanation

"Ravana did not like to return me having fallen into the death trap. Death lies in wait for him in battle.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

ज्येष्ठा कन्या नला नाम विभीषणसुता कपे। तया ममेदमाख्यातं मात्रा प्रहितया स्वयम्।।5.37.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कपे O vanara, नला Nala, ज्येष्ठा eldest daughter, विभीषणसुता daughter of Vibhisana, मात्रा प्रहितया sent by her mother, तया by her, स्वयम् personally, इदम् here, मम to me, आख्यातम् reported.

#### Explanation

"O vanara Nala the eldest daughter of Vibhisana sent by her mother came here personally and reported this to me.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

असंशयं हरिश्रेष्ठ क्षिप्रं मां प्राप्स्यते पतिः। अन्तरात्मा च मे शुद्धस्तस्मिंश्च बहवो गुणाः।।5.37.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हरिश्रेष्ठ O eminent vanara, पतिः my lord, क्षिप्रम् soon, माम् me, असंशयम् no doubt, प्राप्स्यति he will reach, मे my, अन्तरात्मा च inner self also, शुद्धः pure, तस्मिंश्च in that, बहवः many, गुणाः are virtues.

#### Explanation

"O eminent vanara no doubt my lord will soon reach this place. My pure conscience tells me that Rama has many virtues.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

उत्साहः पौरुषं सत्त्वमानृशंस्यं कृतज्ञता। विक्रमश्च प्रभावश्च सन्ति वानर राघवे।।5.37.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वानरvanara, राघवे in Rama, उत्साहः perseverance, पौरुषम् manliness, सत्त्वम् prowess, अनृशंस्यम् kindness, कृतज्ञता gratitude, विक्रमश्च competence, प्रभावश्च power, सन्ति endowed.

#### Explanation

"O vanara Rama is endowed with perseverance, manliness, prowess, kindness, gratitude, competence and power.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

चतुर्दशसहस्राणि राक्षसानां जघान यः। जनस्थाने विना भ्रात्रा शत्रुः कस्तस्य नोद्विजेत् ।।5.37.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

यः whoever, जनस्थाने in Janasthana, विना भ्रात्रा without his brother also, राक्षसानाम् demons, चतुर्दशसहस्राणि fourteen thousand, जघान slew, तस्य his, कः who, शत्रुः enemy, नोद्विजेत् will not tremble.

#### Explanation

"At Janasthana without the help of his brother also he slew fourteen thousand demons. Which enemy will not tremble before him?

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

न स शक्यस्तुलयितुं व्यसनैः पुरुषर्षभः। अहं तस्य प्रभावज्ञा शक्रस्येव पुलोमजा ।।5.37.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पुरुषर्षभः bull among men, सः he, व्यसनैः in calamities, तुलयितुम् shaken, न शक्यः not possible, पुलोमजा daughter of Puloma, शक्रस्येव just as Indra, अहम् I, तस्य his, प्रभावज्ञा know his might.

#### Explanation

"O bull among men he will not be shaken by calamities. Just as Sachi, the daughter of Puloma knows Indra's might I know Rama's.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

शरजालांशुमान्शूरः कपे रामदिवाकरः। शत्रुरक्षोमयं तोयमुपशोषं नयिष्यति।।5.37.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कपे O monkey, शूरः valiant, रामदिवाकर: Rama the Sun, शरजालांशुमान् with his volley of dazzling arrows, शत्रुरक्षोमयम् enemy forces of the demons, तोयम् water, उपशोषम् will dry up, नयिष्यति will effect.

#### Explanation

"O monkey the valiant Rama is like the Sungod shoofing a dazzling volley of arrows which will dry up the sea of enemy forces."

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

इति सञ्जल्पमानां तां रामार्थे शोककर्शिताम्। आश्रुसम्पूर्णनयनामुवाच वचनं कपिः।।5.37.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इति thus, सञ्जल्पमानाम् while she was speaking freely, रामार्थे on account of Rama, शोककर्शिताम् emaciated with sorrow, अश्रु सम्पूर्णनयनाम् her eyes filled with tears, ताम् her, कपिः vanara, वचनम् word, उवाच spoke.

#### Explanation

While Sita, tormented with grief on account of Rama was thus speaking, her eyes filled with tears, the monkey said:

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

श्रुत्वैव तु वचो मह्यं क्षिप्रमेष्यति राघवः। चमूं प्रकर्षन्महतीं हर्यृक्षगणसङ्कुलाम्।।5.37.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राघवः Rama, श्रुत्वैव तु after hearing this, वचः word, मह्यं by me, क्षिप्रम् soon, ऐष्यति he will reach, चमूं an army, प्रकर्षन् while leading, महतीं huge, हर्यृक्षगणसङ्कुलाम् with an army of vanaras and bears.

#### Explanation

"On hearing me Rama will reach soon with a huge army of vanaras and bears.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

अथवा मोचयिष्यामि त्वामद्यैव वरानने। अस्माद्धुःखादुपारोह मम पृष्ठमनिन्दिते।।5.37.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथवा or, वरानने O lady with a beautiful face, त्वाम् you, अद्यैव now itself, अस्मात् from this, दुःखात् from grief, मोचयिष्यामि I shall redeem you, अनिन्दिते O blameles one, मम my, पृष्ठम् back, उपारोह you may  ascend.

#### Explanation

"O beautiful lady, if you please, I shall redeem you today itself from grief. You may ride on my back, O blameless lady.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

त्वां हि पृष्ठगतां कृत्वा सन्तरिष्यामि सागरम्। शक्तिरस्ति हि मे वोढुं लङ्कामपि सरावणाम् ।।5.37.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्वाम् you, पृष्ठगताम् placing you on my back, कृत्वा on doing so, सागरम् ocean, सन्तरिष्यामि I can cross over, सरावणाम् along with Ravana, लङ्कामपि even Lanka, वोढुम् to carry, मे to me, शक्ति capacity, अस्ति हि indeed I have.

#### Explanation

"Placing you on my back I shall cross the ocean. Indeed, I have the capacity to carry the entire city of Lanka along with Ravana.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

अहं प्रस्रवणस्थाय राघवायाद्य मैथिलि। प्रापयिष्यामि शक्राय हव्यं हुतमिवानलः।।5.37.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मैथिलि O Mythili, अनलः sacred fire, हुतम्  put into fire, हव्यम् oblations, शक्राय by Indra, अहम् I, प्रस्रवणस्थाय to a person staying on Prasravan mountain, राघवाय to Rama, अद्यैव प्रापयिष्यामि I will take you today itself.

#### Explanation

"O Mythili Just as oblations offered to the sacred fire goes to Indra, I shall offer you today to Rama waiting on the Prasravana mountain.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

द्रक्ष्यस्यद्वैव वैदेहि राघवं सहलक्ष्मणम्। व्यवसायसमायुक्तं विष्णुं दैत्यवधे यथा।।5.37.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वैदेहि Vaidehi, दैत्यवधे in the killing of demons, व्यवसायसमायुक्तम् making all out efforts, विष्णुं यथा like Visnu, सहलक्ष्मणम् together with Lakshmana, राघवम् Rama, अद्यैव right now, द्रक्ष्यसि you will see.

#### Explanation

"O Vaidehi you will see Rama and Lakshmana making all out efforts like Lord Visnu to kill the demons.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

त्वद्दर्शनकृतोत्साहमाश्रमस्थं महाबलम्। पुरन्दरमिवासीनं नागराजस्य मूर्धनि।।5.37.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्वद्दर्शनकृतोत्साहम् enthused in seeing you, आश्रमस्थम् staying in his asram, महाबलम् mighty, नागराजस्य of the king of elephants Airavatam, मूर्धनि on the back, आसीनम् seated, पुरन्दरमिव like the destroyer of citadels (Indra).

#### Explanation

Mighty Rama staying in asram ill be enthused to see you seated on my back like Lord Purandara (Indra) seated on the back of the lord of elephants (Airavata)

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

पृष्ठमारोह मे देवि मा विकाङ्क्षस्व शोभने। योगमन्विच्छ रामेण शशाङ्केनेव रोहिणी।।5.37.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

देवि O god like lady, मम my, पृष्ठम् back, आरोह climb up, शोभने O beautiful one, मा विकाङ्क्षस्व do not be reluctant, रोहिणी Rohini, शशाङ्केनेव like joining with the Moon, रामेण with Rama, योगम् union, अन्विच्छ you may become.

#### Explanation

"O god like lady climb up my back. Do not be reluctant. Join Rama just as Rohini unites with the Moon, O beautiful one

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

कथयन्तीव चन्द्रेण सूर्येण च महार्चिषा। मत्पृष्ठमधिरुह्य त्वं तराकाशमहार्णवौ।।5.37.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्वम् you, मत्पृष्ठम् my back, अधिरुह्य after climbing up, चन्द्रेण with the Moon, महार्चिषा  flaming light, सूर्येण with the Sun, कथयन्तीव conversing, आकाशमहार्णवौ sky and ocean, तर cross.

#### Explanation

"Climb on my back and cross the ocean through space in the flaming light, conversing with Moon and Sun.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

न हि मे सम्प्रयातस्य त्वामितो नयतोऽङ्गने। अनुगन्तुं गतिं शक्तास्सर्वे लङ्कानिवासिनः।।5.37.26।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अङ्गने O beautiful lady, त्वाम् you, इतः from the place, नयतः while carrying, सम्प्रयातस्य joined together, मे my, गतिम् speed, अनुगन्तुम् to follow me, सर्वे all, लङ्कानिवासिनः the inhabitants of Lanka, न शक्ताः not possible for any one.

#### Explanation

"O beautiful lady it is not possible for all the inhabitants of Lanka together to follow me while I carry you from this place.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

यथैवाहमिह प्राप्तस्तथैवाहमसंशयः। यास्यामि पश्य वैदेहि त्वामुद्यम्य विहायसम्।।5.37.27।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वैदेहि Vaidehi, यथैवाहमिह just as I reached here, प्राप्तस्तथैव in the same way I reached, अहम I, त्वामुद्यम्य taking you on my back, यास्यामि will go, विहायसम् across the sky, नसंशयः no doubt, पश्य you may see.

#### Explanation

"O Vaidehi just as I reached here I shall beyond doubt take you on my back in the same way across the sky without any difficulty. See.

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

मैथिली तु हरिश्रेष्ठाच्छ्रुत्वा वचनमद्भुतम्। हर्षविस्मितसर्वाङ्गी हनुमन्तमथाब्रवीत्।।5.37.28।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मैथिली तु Mythili also, हरिश्रेष्ठात् from the best of vanaras, अद्भुतम् wonderful, वचनम् word, श्रुत्वा on hearing, हर्षविस्मितसर्वाङ्गी experienced immense joy in every limb of her body, अथ then, हनुमन्तम् Hanuman, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

When Mythili heard the wonderful proposal from Hanuman, the best of vanaras, she experienced immense joy in every limb of her body and said:

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

हनुमन्दूरमध्वानं कथं मां वोढुमिच्छसि। तदेव खलु ते मन्ये कपित्वं हरियूथप।।5.37.29।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हनुमन् Hanuman, दूरम् distant, अध्वानम् path, माम् me, वोढुम् carry, कथम् how, इच्छसि you intend, हरियूथप O leader of vanaras, ते you, कपित्वम् monkey nature, तदेव खलु indeed that is, मन्ये I think.

#### Explanation

"Hanuman how do you intend to carry me to such a distant place? Indeed, it is your monkey nature, I suppose.

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

कथं वाल्पशरीरस्त्वं मामितो नेतुमिच्छसि। सकाशं मानवेन्द्रस्य भर्तुर्मे प्लवगर्षभ।।5.37.30।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

प्लवगर्षभ O bull among monkeys, अल्पशरीरः small body, त्वम् you, इतः from here, मे my, भर्तुः to my husband, मानवेन्द्रस्य lord of men, सकाशम् presence of, नेतुम् to carry, कथं वा how is it, इच्छसि you intend.

#### Explanation

"O bull among monkeys you have  such a small body. How can you intend to carry me from here to my husband, the lord of men"?

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

सीताया वचनं श्रुत्वा हनुमान्मारुतात्मजः। चिन्तयामास लक्ष्मीवान्नवं परिभवं कृतम्।।5.37.31।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मारुतात्मजः son of the Windgod, लक्ष्मीवान् illustrious one, हनुमान् Hanuman, सीतायाः Sita's, वचनम् word, श्रुत्वा on hearing, नवम् new, परिभवम् कृतम् insult hurled, चिन्तयामास he thought.

#### Explanation

On hearing Sita's new belittling statement about him, Hanuman, the illustrious son of the Windgod reflected:

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

न मे जानाति सत्त्वं वा प्रभावं वाऽसितेक्षणा। तस्मात्पश्यतु वैदेही यद्रूपं मम कामतः।।5.37.32।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

असितेक्षणा blackeyed, वैदेही Vaidehi, मे my, सत्त्वं वा strength, प्रभावं वा my power, न जानाति she is not aware, तस्मात् therefore, कामतः freely, मम my, यत् that real, रूपम् form, पश्यतु see.

#### Explanation

'Blackeyed Sita is not aware of my strength or my power. Therefore let her see the real (huge) form that I can assume at my free will'.

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

इति सञ्चिन्त्य हनुमांस्तदा प्लवगसत्तमः। दर्शयामास वैदेह्यास्स्वरूपमरिमर्दनः।।5.37.33।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

प्लवगसत्तमः the noble monkey, अरिमर्दनः crusher of enemies,हनुमान् Hanuman, इति in that way, सञ्चिन्त्य thinking that way, तदा then, स्वरूपम् his real form, वैदेह्याः to Vaidehi, दर्शयामास he revealed.

#### Explanation

Thinking thus, Hanuman, the noble monkey, crusher of enemies, started revealing his real form to Vaidehi.

---

### Shloka 34

#### Shloka Text

स तस्मात्पादपाद्धीमानाप्लुत्य प्लवगर्षभः। ततो वर्धितुमारेभे सीताप्रत्ययकारणात्।।5.37.34।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

धीमान् wise, प्लवगर्षभः bull among monkeys, सः he, तस्मात् पादपात् from the tree, अप्लुत्य jumping down, ततः from there, सीताप्रत्ययकारणात् in order to instil confidence in Sita, वर्धितुम् to grow in size, आरेभे started.

#### Explanation

The wise bull among monkeys, jumped down from the tree and started growing in size in order to instil confidence in Sita.

---

### Shloka 35

#### Shloka Text

मेरुमन्दरसङ्काशो बभौ दीप्तानलप्रभः। अग्रतो व्यवतस्थे च सीताया वानरोत्तमः।।5.37.35।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वानरोत्तमः foremost among vanaras, मेरुमन्दरसङ्काशः like mount Meru or Mandara, दीप्तानलप्रभः glowing like blazing fire, बभौ appeared, सीतायाः Sita's, अग्रतः in front, व्यवतस्थे च and stood there.

#### Explanation

Hanuman, the foremost of the vanaras resembling mount Meru or Mandara stood there glowing like blazing fire in front of Sita.

---

### Shloka 36

#### Shloka Text

हरिः पर्वतसङ्काशस्ताम्रवक्त्रो महाबलः। वज्रदंष्ट्रनखो भीमो वैदेहीमिदमब्रवीत्।।5.37.36।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पर्वतसङ्काशः resembling a mountain, ताम्रवक्त्रः one who has a red face, महाबलः very strong, वज्रदंष्ट्रनखः teeth and nails as strong as diamonds, भीम: dreadful, हरिः vanara, वैदेहीम् to Vaidehi, इदम् this, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

Resembling a mountain in size, with a red face, with teeth and nails strong like  diamond, the vanara in dreadful form said to Vaidehi:

---

### Shloka 37

#### Shloka Text

सपर्वतवनोद्देशां साट्टप्राकारतोरणाम्। लङ्कामिमां सनाथां वा नयितुं शक्तिरस्ति मे।।5.37.37।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सपर्वतवनोद्देशाम् filled with mountains and gardens, साट्टप्राकारतोरणाम् forts,  ramparts and gateways, सनाथाम् along with its king, इमाम् this, लङ्कां वा Lanka itself, नयितुम्  carried away, मे my, शक्ति: capacity, अस्ति is there.

#### Explanation

"I have the capacity to carry away this entire Lanka uprooted with its mountains, gardens, forts, ramparts and gateways along with the king.

---

### Shloka 38

#### Shloka Text

तदवस्थाप्यतां बुद्धिरलं देवि विकाङ्क्षया। विशोकं कुरु वैदेहि राघवं सहलक्ष्मणम्।।5.37.38।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

देवि queen, तत् so, विकाङ्क्ष्या with unworthy attitude, अलम् enough, बुद्धि: intellect, अवस्थाप्यताम् be stable, वैदेहि Vaidehi, सहलक्ष्मणम् with Lakshmana, राघवम् Rama, विशोकम् rid of sorrow, कुरु do.

#### Explanation

"O queen it is enough. Give up this unworthy attitude. Be stable. O Vaidehi, rid Lakshmana and Rama of grief".

---

### Shloka 39

#### Shloka Text

तं दृष्ट्वा भीमसङ्काशमुवाच जनकात्मजा। पद्मपत्रविशालाक्षी मारुतस्यौरसं सुतम्।।5.37.39।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पद्मपत्रविशालाक्षी with eyes large like lotus petals, जनकात्मा daughter of Janaka, भीमसङ्काशम् dreadful to viewers, मारुतस्य Maruta's, औरसं सुतम् own son, तम् him, दृष्ट्वा on seeing, उवाच said.

#### Explanation

Seeing the son of Maruta in dreadful form, the broad lotus petaleyed daughter of Janaka with eyes as large as lotus petals replied :

---

### Shloka 40

#### Shloka Text

तव सत्त्वं बलं चैव विजानामि महाकपे। वायोरिव गतिं चैव तेजश्चाग्नेरिवाद्भुतम्।।5.37.40।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाकपे O great monkey, तव your, सत्त्वम् strength, बलं चैव and power, वायोरिव like the Windgod's, गतिम् speed, आग्नेरिव like fire's, अद्भुतम् astonishing, तेजश्च splendour, विजानामि I have understood

#### Explanation

"O great monkey I have understood your strength and power. Your speed is like the Windgod's and your power is astonishing and splendid.

---

### Shloka 41

#### Shloka Text

प्राकृतोऽन्यः कथं चेमां भूमिमागन्तुमर्हति। उदधेरप्रमेयस्य पारं वानरपुङ्गव।।5.37.41।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वानरपुङ्गव O chief of vanaras, प्राकृतः an ordinary one, अन्यः other, अप्रमेयस्य of an immeasurable one, उदधेः ocean's, पारम् far off shore, इमाम् this, भूमिम् this land, आगन्तुम् reach, कथम् how, अर्हति fit.

#### Explanation

"O chief of vanaras how could an ordinary person be capable of reaching this land on far off shore of the immeasurable ocean?

---

### Shloka 42

#### Shloka Text

जानामि गमने शक्तिं नयने चापि ते मम। अवश्यं सम्प्रधार्याशु कार्यसिद्धिर्महात्मनः।।5.37.42।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

गमने in making the journey, मम me, नयने in  taking, शक्तिम् power, जानामि I know, महात्मनः of the great soul, कार्यसिददि success of the mission, अवश्यम् surely, आशु immediately, सम्प्रधार्या after planning properly

#### Explanation

"I know you are capable of carrying me on your journey. I am sure about the quick success of your mission which has been carried out after careful planning.

---

### Shloka 43

#### Shloka Text

अयुक्तं तु कपिश्रेष्ठ मम गन्तुं त्वयाऽनघ। वायुवेगसवेगस्य वेगो मां मोहयेत्तव।।5.37.43।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अनघ sinless one, कपिश्रेष्ठ foremost monkey, त्वया by you, मम my, गन्तुम् to go with, अयुक्तम् not proper, वायुवेगसवेगस्य of a person with the speed of the wind, तव your, वेगः speed, माम् me, मोहयेत् will render me unconscious.

#### Explanation

"O sinless and foremost monkey going with you is not proper for me. Your windlike speed will render me unconscious.

---

### Shloka 44

#### Shloka Text

अहमाकाशमापन्ना ह्युपर्युपरि सागरम्। प्रपतेयं हि ते पृष्ठाद्भयाद्वेगेव गच्छतः।।5.37.44।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सागरम् उपर्युपरि above the ocean, आकाशम् sky, अहम् I, वेगेन speedily, गच्छतः when you go, ते your, पृष्ठात् from the back, भयात् out of fear, आपन्ना when I reach, प्रपतेयम् I may tumble down.

#### Explanation

"When you fly over the ocean in the sky  speedily I may tumble down from your back by your speed out of fear.

---

### Shloka 45

#### Shloka Text

पतिता सागरे चाहं तिमिनक्रझषाकुले। भवेयमाशु विवशा यादसामन्नमुत्तमम्।।5.37.45।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तिमिनक्रझषाकुले filled with whales, crocodiles and fish, सागरे in the ocean, पतिता fallen in, अहम् I, विवशा helpless, आशु immediately, यादसाम् of aquatic animals, उत्तमम् very good, अन्नम् food, भवेयम् I will be.

#### Explanation

None

---

### Shloka 46

#### Shloka Text

न च शक्ष्ये त्वया सार्धं गन्तुं शत्रुविनाशन। कलत्रवति सन्देहस्त्वय्यपि स्यादसंशयः।।5.37.46।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शत्रुविनाशन O destroyer of foes, त्वया सार्थम् along with you, गन्तुम् to go, न शक्ष्ये च I have no power, कलत्रवति when burdened with a woman, त्वय्यपि with you also, संदेहः doubt, स्यात् may be, असंशयः there is no doubt.

#### Explanation

"O destroyer of foes, it is not possible for me to go with you. Further, when you are burdened with a woman you will also fall into danger (because of me), no doubt.

---

### Shloka 47

#### Shloka Text

ह्रियमाणां तु मां दृष्ट्वा राक्षसा भीमविक्रमाः। अनुगच्छेयुरादिष्टा रावणेन दुरात्मना।।5.37.47।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ह्रियमाणाम् while I am, माम् me, दृष्ट्वा after seeing, भीमविक्रमाः of fearsome valour, राक्षसाः demons, दुरात्मना by evilminded, रावणेन by Ravana, आदिष्टाः ordered, अनुगच्छेयुः they will pursue

#### Explanation

"When I am seen (while being carried away) by the demons of fearsome valour they will pursue you, ordered by the wicked Ravana.

---

### Shloka 48

#### Shloka Text

तैस्त्वं परिवृतश्शूरैश्शूलमुद्गरपाणिभिः। भवेस्त्वं संशयं प्राप्तो मया वीर कलत्रवान्।।5.37.48।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्वम् you, शूलमुद्गरपाणिभिः by those wielding spears and mace, तैः by them, शूरैः by warriors, परिवृतः surrounded, वीर O hero, मया by me, कलत्रवान् one who has a wife, त्वम् you, संशयम् doubt, प्राप्तः have been, भवेः  you.

#### Explanation

"O heroic monkey as the warriors armed with spears and maces surround you and attack, your life will be in danger, because of me.

---

### Shloka 49

#### Shloka Text

सायुधा बहवो व्योम्नि राक्षसास्त्वं निरायुधः। कथं शक्ष्यसि संयातुं मां चैव परिरक्षितुम्।।5.37.49।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

व्योम्नि in the sky, सायुधाः will be armed with weapons, राक्षसाः demons, बहवः many, त्वम् you, निरायुधः unarmed, संयातुम् to fight, माम् me, परिरक्षितुं च and safeguard, कथम् how, शक्ष्यसि you will be capable?

#### Explanation

"The demons are in large number and they will be armed with weapons. You are unarmed. How can you fight and protect me?

---

### Shloka 50

#### Shloka Text

युध्यमानस्य रक्षोभिस्तव तैः क्रूरकर्मभिः। प्रपतेयं हि ते पृष्ठाद्भयार्ता कपिसत्तम।।5.37.50।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कपिसत्तम O noble monkey, तव your, क्रूरकर्मभिः by those of wicked deeds, तैः by them, रक्षोभिः by the demons, युध्यमानस्य for you while engaged in fighting, भयार्ता out of fear, ते your, पृष्ठात् from the back, प्रपतेयं हि I will drop down.

#### Explanation

"O noble monkey while you are engaged in fighting with those demons of wicked deeds, I may drop down out of fear.

---

### Shloka 51

#### Shloka Text

अथ रक्षांसि भीमानि महान्ति बलवन्ति च। कथञ्चित्सांपराये त्वां जयेयुः कपिसत्तम।।5.37.51।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कपिसत्तम O noble monkey, अथ then, भीमानि fearsome, महान्ति massive, बलवन्ति च powerful, रक्षांसि demons, सांपराये in the fight, कथञ्चित् in any case, त्वाम् you, जयेयुः win over.

#### Explanation

"O noble monkey the massive, fearsome and powerful demons may defeat you in the fight in any way.

---

### Shloka 52

#### Shloka Text

अथवा युध्यमानस्य पतेयं विमुखस्य ते। पतितां च गृहीत्वा मां नयेयुः पापराक्षसाः।।5.37.52।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथवा or else, युध्यमानस्य for you while engaged in the fighting, ते for you, विमुखस्य while you turn your attention other way, पतेयम् I may fall down, पतिताम् when I have fallen down, माम् me, गृहीत्वा taking hold, पापराक्षसाः wretched rakshasas, नयेयुः may take me away.

#### Explanation

"Or else, while you are engaged in fighting and turn your attention the other way I might fall down and the sinful ogres may bear me away.

---

### Shloka 53

#### Shloka Text

मां वा हरेयुस्त्वद्धस्ताद्विशसेयुरथापि वा। अव्यवस्थौ हि दृश्येते युद्धे जयपराजयौ।।5.37.53।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्वत् हस्तात् from your hand, माम् me, हरेयुः they may take one away, अथापि वा or rather, विशसेयुः may cut me, युद्धे in the fight, जयपराजयौ success or failure, अव्यवस्थौ not sure, दृश्येते हि they appear.

#### Explanation

"They may snatch me away from your hands or cut me to pieces. Success or defeat is uncertain in a war.

---

### Shloka 54

#### Shloka Text

अहं वापि विपद्येयं रक्षोभिरभितर्जिता। त्वत्प्रयत्नो हरिश्रेष्ठ भवेन्निष्फल एव तु।।5.37.54।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रक्षोभिः caught by the ogres, अभितर्जिता very much threatened, अहम् I, विपद्येयं वापि or else I may die, हरिश्रेष्ठ best of monkeys, त्वत्प्रयत्नः your effort, निष्फल एव will be wasteful, तु indeed, भवेत् may be.

#### Explanation

"In such a case, caught and intimidated by the demons I may die. Then your efforts will be wasted.

---

### Shloka 55

#### Shloka Text

कामं त्वमसि पर्याप्तो निहन्तुं सर्वराक्षसान्। राघवस्य यशो हीयेत्त्वया शस्तैस्तु राक्षसैः।।5.37.55।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्वम् you, सर्वराक्षसान् all the ogres, निहन्तुम् to slay, पर्याप्तः अपि कामम् it may be you can do the task, त्वया by you, शस्तै by those killed, राक्षसैः by demons, राघवस्य Rama's, यशः fame, हीयेत् will diminish.

#### Explanation

"It may be that you can kill  all the demons but by such an act the fame of Rama will be diminished. (He cannot give his strength a trial)

---

### Shloka 56

#### Shloka Text

अथवाऽदाय रक्षांसि न्यसेयुस्सम्वृते हि माम्। यत्र ते नाभिजानीयुर्हरयो नापि राघवौ।।5.37.56।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथवा or else, रक्षांसि the ogres, माम् me, आदाय after taking, यत्र where, ते हरयः those of you vanaras, राघवौ अपि and even Rama and Lakshmana, नाभिजानीयुः may not know, संवृते in a secret place, न्यसेयु: they may hide.

#### Explanation

"Or else, the ogres will carry me  and conceal me in a secret place which the vanaras or Rama and Lakshmana may not know.

---

### Shloka 57

#### Shloka Text

आरम्भस्तु मदर्थोऽयं ततस्तव निरर्थकः। त्वया हि सह रामस्य महानागमने गुणः।।5.37.57।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, मदर्थः for my sake, तव your, आरम्भस्तु effort, निरर्थकः futile, त्वया सह along with you, रामस्य Rama, आगमने coming here, महान् great, गुणः merit.

#### Explanation

"All your effort for my sake would be futile. Hence Rama's arrival along with you will be of great benefit.

---

### Shloka 58

#### Shloka Text

मयि जीवितमायत्तं राघवस्य महात्मनः। भ्रात्रूणां च महाबाहो तव राजकुलस्य च।।5.37.58।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाबाहो strongarmed one, महात्मनः of the great self, राघवस्य Rama's, भ्रात्रूणाम् of his brothers, तव your, राजकुलस्य of the relatives of your king, जीवितम् life, मयि in me, आयत्तम् is dependent.

#### Explanation

"O strongarmed Hanuman your life and the life of the great self Rama, of his brother, and relatives of your king is dependent upon my survival.

---

### Shloka 59

#### Shloka Text

तौ निराशौ मदर्थं तु शोकसन्तापकर्शितौ। सह सर्वर्क्षहरिभिस्त्यक्ष्यतः प्राणसङ्ग्रहम्।।5.37.59।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मदर्थम् for my sake, शोकसन्तापकर्शितौ emaciated with sorrow and suffering, तौ they both, निराशौ with no hope of finding me, सर्वर्क्षहरिभिः सह along with all vanaras and bears, प्राणसङ्ग्रहम् the five elements of which life is made, त्यक्ष्यतः will give up.

#### Explanation

"Both Rama and Lakshmana who are emaciated with sorrow and suffering on account of me will give up their lives along with vanaras and bears.

---

### Shloka 60

#### Shloka Text

भर्तुर्भक्तिं पुरस्कृत्य रामादन्यस्य वानर। न स्पृशामि शरीरं तु पुंसो वानरपुङ्गव।।5.37.60।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वानरपुङ्गव O chief of vanaras, वानर vanara, भर्तुः husband's, भक्तिम् devotion, पुरस्कृत्य after cherishing, रामात् from Rama, अन्यस्य other, पुंसः man's, शरीरं तु body, न स्पृशामि I do not touch.

#### Explanation

"O chief of vanaras I cherish my devotion towards Rama alone and not touch the body of any other man.

---

### Shloka 61

#### Shloka Text

यदहं गात्रसंस्पर्शं रावणस्य बलाद्गता। अनीशा किं करिष्यामि विनाथा विवशा सती।।5.37.61।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I, बलात् by force, रावणस्य Ravana's, गात्रसंस्पर्शम् touch of his body, गता यत् that I had to experience, अनीशा having no control over my self, विवशा सती a helpless woman, विनाथा without my husband, किं करिष्यामि what can I do?

#### Explanation

"I had no control over myself. I was helpless and my husband was not present when I had to bear the touch of Ravana's limbs. What can I do?

---

### Shloka 62

#### Shloka Text

यदि रामो दशग्रीवमिह हत्त्वा सबान्धवम्। मामितो गृह्य गच्छेत तत्तस्य सदृशं भवेत्।।5.37.62।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामः Rama, सबान्धवम् along with his relations, दशग्रीवम् Ravana, इह here, हत्त्वा after killing, माम् me, इतः from here, गृह्य taking, गच्छेत यदि and if he goes, तत् then, तस्य his, सदृशम् proper, भवेत् it will be.

#### Explanation

"It will be proper if Rama comes here, kills Ravana along with all his relations and takes me away.

---

### Shloka 63

#### Shloka Text

श्रुता हि दृष्टाश्च मया पराक्रमा महात्मनस्तस्य रणावमर्दिनः। न देवगन्धर्वभुजङ्गराक्षसा भवन्ति रामेण समा हि संयुगे।।5.37.63।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रणावमर्दिनः of the crusher of enemies, महात्मनः great self, तस्य his, पराक्रमाः valiant deeds, मया by me, श्रुताः heard, दृष्टाश्च witnessed, संयुगे in war, देवगन्धर्वभुजङ्गराक्षसाः whether gods, gandharvas, nagas or demons, रामेण by Rama, समाः equal, नहि not indeed.

#### Explanation

"I have seen and heard about Rama's conquest of enemies and his valiant deeds in war. Indeed, gods gandharvas, nagas or even demons cannot match him in war.

---

### Shloka 64

#### Shloka Text

समीक्ष्य तं संयति चित्रकार्मुकम् महाबलं वासवतुल्यविक्रमम्। सलक्ष्मणं को विषहेत राघवं हुताशनं दीप्तमिवानिलेरितम्।।5.37.64।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

चित्रकार्मुकम् wielder of a powerful bow, महाबलम् endowed with great strength, वासवतुल्यविक्रमम् equal to Vasava in valour, सलक्ष्मणम् along with Lakshmana, अनिलेरितम् whipped by wind, दीप्तम् blazing, हुताशनमिव like the fire, तं राघवम् him, Rama, सयति in war, समीक्ष्य facing, कः who, विषहेतः can withstand.

#### Explanation

"After seeing Rama who is equal to lord Indra in valour. He is like blazing fire whipped by wind, wielding a wonderful bow, who can withstand the power of strong Rama accompanied by Lakshmana in war?

---

### Shloka 65

#### Shloka Text

सलक्ष्मणं राघवमाजिमर्दनं दिशागजं मत्तमिव व्यवस्थितम्। सहेत को वानरमुख्य संयुगे युगान्तसूर्यप्रतिमं शरार्चिषम्।।5.37.65।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वानरमुख्य chief of vanaras, सलक्ष्मणम् with Lakshmana, आजिमर्दनम् crusher of enemies in war, मत्तम् intoxicated, दिशागजमिव like the elephant supporting quarter of the earth, व्यवस्थितम् steady, शरार्चिषम् arrows of rays, युगान्तसूर्यप्रतिमम् resembling the Sun at the at the time of the deluge, राघवम् Rama, संयुगे in a war, कः who, सहेत can withstand.

#### Explanation

"O chief of vanaras who can withstand Rama accompanied by Lakshmana, the conqueror of enemies? His arrows resemble the rays of the Sun at the time of the deluge, steady like the elephants who support the quarters of the earth.

---

## Sarga 38

### Shloka 1
