#### Shloka Text

ततस्स कपिशार्दूलस्तेन वाक्येन तोषितः। सीतामुवाच तच्छृत्वा वाक्यं वाक्यविशारदः।।5.38.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, वाक्यविशारदः eloquent in speech, सः कपिशार्दूलः tiger among monkeys, तत् वाक्यम् those words, श्रुत्वा hearing, तेन वाक्येन by those words, तोषितः contented with, सीताम् to Sita, उवाच said.

#### Explanation

Satisfied with the words of Sita, the tiger among monkeys, who was eloquent in speech said:

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

युक्तरूपं त्वया देवि भाषितं शुभदर्शने। सदृशं स्त्रीस्वभावस्य साध्वीनां विनयस्य च।।5.38.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शुभदर्शने whose appearance is auspicious, देवि queen, त्वया you, भाषितम् spoken, युक्तरूपम् is in accordance with your nature, स्त्रीस्वभावस्य of feminine nature, साध्वीनाम् of virtuous women, विनयस्य च and of the modesty, सदृशम् appropriate.

#### Explanation

"O queen O lady of auspicious appearance your words are most appropriate to and characteristic of a modest virtuous woman.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

स्त्रीत्वं न तु समर्थं हि सागरं व्यतिवर्तितुम्। मामधिष्ठाय विस्तीर्णं शतयोजनमायतम्।।5.38.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

माम् me, अधिष्ठाय after climbing up, विस्तीर्णम् vast, शतयोजनम् of hundred yojanas, आयतम् wide, सागरम् the ocean, व्यतिवर्तितुम् to cross, स्त्रीत्वम् for a woman, न समर्थं हि is not possible.

#### Explanation

"It may not be possible for a woman to cross on my back the vast ocean stretching over a hundred yojanas. It is not possible for a woman.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

द्वितीयं कारणं यच्च ब्रवीषि विनयान्विते। रामादन्यस्य नार्हामि संस्पर्शमिति जानकि ।।5.38.4।। एतत्ते सदृशं देवि पत्न्यास्तस्य महात्मनः। का ह्यन्या त्वामृते देवि ब्रूयाद्वचनमीदृशम्।।5.38.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विनयान्विते O lady with humility etc., जानकि Janaki, रामात् from Rama, अन्यस्य of others, संस्पर्शम् touch, नार्हिमि I do not want, यत् that which, द्वितीयम् second, कारणम् reason, ब्रवीषि you said, एतत् that, तस्य his, महात्मनः of the great soul, पत्न्याः of the wife, ते to you, सदृशम् appropriate, देवि O queen, त्वाम् your, ऋते excepting, अन्या other than, का who else, ईदृशम् such, वचनम् word, ब्रूयात् will speak.

#### Explanation

"O humble lady the second reason given by you, 'I ought not to touch a person other than Rama' is well said and worthy of you, being the wife of the great soul. Who else can speak such words?

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

द्वितीयं कारणं यच्च ब्रवीषि विनयान्विते। रामादन्यस्य नार्हामि संस्पर्शमिति जानकि ।।5.38.4।। एतत्ते सदृशं देवि पत्न्यास्तस्य महात्मनः। का ह्यन्या त्वामृते देवि ब्रूयाद्वचनमीदृशम्।।5.38.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विनयान्विते O lady with humility etc., जानकि Janaki, रामात् from Rama, अन्यस्य of others, संस्पर्शम् touch, नार्हिमि I do not want, यत् that which, द्वितीयम् second, कारणम् reason, ब्रवीषि you said, एतत् that, तस्य his, महात्मनः of the great soul, पत्न्याः of the wife, ते to you, सदृशम् appropriate, देवि O queen, त्वाम् your, ऋते excepting, अन्या other than, का who else, ईदृशम् such, वचनम् word, ब्रूयात् will speak.

#### Explanation

"O humble lady the second reason given by you, 'I ought not to touch a person other than Rama' is well said and worthy of you, being the wife of the great soul. Who else can speak such words?

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

श्रोष्यते चैव काकुत्स्थ: सर्वं निरवशेषतः। चेष्टितं यत्त्वया देवि भाषितं मम चाग्रतः।।5.38.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

काकुत्स्थ: Rama the scion of the Kakutstha family, सर्वं all, देवि queen, त्वया your, यत् what ever, चेष्टितम् is done, मम my, अग्रतः presence, भाषितं च you have spoken, सर्वम् all, निरवशेषतः without omission, श्रोष्यते च will hear.

#### Explanation

"O queen Kakutstha Rama will hear from me fully all that has been said and done by you in my presence without any omission.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

कारणैर्बहुभिर्देवि रामप्रियचिकीर्षया। स्नेहप्रस्कन्नमनसा मयैतत्समुदीरितम्।।5.38.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

देवि O queen, बहुभिः with many, कारणैः with reasons, रामप्रियचिकीर्षया desiring to please Rama, स्नेहप्रस्कन्नमनसा mind is sprinkled with love, मया by me, एतत् this, समुदीरितम् uttered.

#### Explanation

"O queen my heart is drenched with love for Rama and desires to please him for many reasons. Therefore these words have been spoken by me.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

लङ्काया दुष्प्रवेशत्वाद्दुस्तरत्वान्महोदधेः। सामर्थ्यादात्मनश्चैव मयैतत्समुदीरितम्।।5.38.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लङ्कायाः of Lanka, दुष्प्रवेशत्वात् difficult to enter, महोदधेः of the ocean, दुस्तरत्वात् difficult to cross, आत्मनः of my self, सामर्थ्याश्चैव because of my capacity, मया by me, एतत् all this, समुदीरितम् pronounced.

#### Explanation

"Crossing the ocean and entering Lanka are  difficult. But I accomplished it. Hence my proposal.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

इच्छामि त्वां समानेतुमद्यैव रघुबन्धुना। गुरुस्नेहेन भक्त्या च नान्यथैतदुदाहृतम्।।5.38.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

गुरुस्नेहेन out of love for my master, भक्त्या च and devotion, त्वाम् you, रघुबन्धुना with Rama, अद्यैव today itself, समानेतुम् to take, इच्छामि desirous of, एतत् all this, अन्यथा any other, न उदाहृतम् not mentioned otherwise.

#### Explanation

"I offered to take you today itself out of my love and devotion to Rama with a desire to unite you with him and not with any other intention.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

यदि नोत्सहसे यातुं मया सार्थमनिन्दिते। अभिज्ञानं प्रयच्छ त्वं जानीयाद्राघवो हि यत्।।5.38.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अनिन्दिते O blameless lady, मया सार्थम् along with me, यातुम् to go, नोत्सहसे यदि if not desiring, यत् that, राघवः Rama, जानीयात् coming and seeing you, अभिज्ञानम् to let him know, त्वम् you, प्रयच्छ you may give me a token of identity.

#### Explanation

"O blameless lady If you wish not to accompany me you may give me a token of identification to let Rama know about my coming here and seeing you".

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्ता हनुमता सीता सुरसुतोपमा। उवाच वचनं मन्दं बाष्पप्रग्रथिताक्षरम्।।5.38.11।। इदं श्रेष्ठमभिज्ञानं ब्रूयास्त्वं तु मम प्रियम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हनुमता by Hanuman, एवम् in that way, उक्ता having been addressed, सुरसुतोपमा resembling a god's daughter, सीता Sita, बाष्पप्रग्रथिताक्षरम् words choked with tears, वचनम् word, मन्दम् in low tone, उवाच said, त्वं तु you also, मम my, प्रियम् dear, श्रेष्ठम् best, इदम् thus, अभिज्ञानम् token of identity, ब्रूयाः said.

#### Explanation

Having heard Hanuman, Sita, a child of god, said these words in a low tone. Choked with tears, 'Carry this token of identity to my dear husband':

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

शैलस्य चित्रकूटस्य पादे पूर्वोत्तरे पुरा।।5.38.12।। तापसाश्रमवासिन्याः प्राज्यमूलफलोदके। तस्मिन्सिद्धाश्रमे देशे मन्दाकिन्या विदूरतः।।5.38.13।। तस्योपवनषण्डेषु नानापुष्पसुगन्धिषु। विहृत्य सलिले क्लिन्ना ममाङ्के समुपाविशमः।।5.38.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पुरा earlier, चित्रकूटस्य शैलस्य on the mountain of Chitrakuta, पूर्वोत्तरे in the northeast, पादे on foot, प्राज्यमूलफलोदके with abundant roots fruits and water, तस्मिन् in that, सिद्धाश्रमे Siddhasrama, देशे in a region, मन्दाकिन्याध्यविदूरतः not far from Mandakini river, तस्य of him, नानापुष्पसुगन्धिषु many kinds of fragrant flowers, उपवनषण्डेषु in gardens, सलिले in water, विहृत्य  after sporting, क्लिन्नः wet, तापसाश्रमवासिन्याः hermitages of ascetics, मम me, अङ्के the body, समुपाविशः you were seated.

#### Explanation

'Long ago we were staying at Siddhasrama. This hermitage was situated to the northeast at the foot of Chitrakuta mountain which was not far from the river Mandakini. It had abundant roots, fruits and water. You sported with me in the gardens which were fragrant with many types of flowers and ponds. You sat down on my lap with wet clothes in the hermitage'.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

शैलस्य चित्रकूटस्य पादे पूर्वोत्तरे पुरा।।5.38.12।। तापसाश्रमवासिन्याः प्राज्यमूलफलोदके। तस्मिन्सिद्धाश्रमे देशे मन्दाकिन्या विदूरतः।।5.38.13।। तस्योपवनषण्डेषु नानापुष्पसुगन्धिषु। विहृत्य सलिले क्लिन्ना ममाङ्के समुपाविशमः।।5.38.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पुरा earlier, चित्रकूटस्य शैलस्य on the mountain of Chitrakuta, पूर्वोत्तरे in the northeast, पादे on foot, प्राज्यमूलफलोदके with abundant roots fruits and water, तस्मिन् in that, सिद्धाश्रमे Siddhasrama, देशे in a region, मन्दाकिन्याध्यविदूरतः not far from Mandakini river, तस्य of him, नानापुष्पसुगन्धिषु many kinds of fragrant flowers, उपवनषण्डेषु in gardens, सलिले in water, विहृत्य  after sporting, क्लिन्नः wet, तापसाश्रमवासिन्याः hermitages of ascetics, मम me, अङ्के the body, समुपाविशः you were seated.

#### Explanation

'Long ago we were staying at Siddhasrama. This hermitage was situated to the northeast at the foot of Chitrakuta mountain which was not far from the river Mandakini. It had abundant roots, fruits and water. You sported with me in the gardens which were fragrant with many types of flowers and ponds. You sat down on my lap with wet clothes in the hermitage'.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

शैलस्य चित्रकूटस्य पादे पूर्वोत्तरे पुरा।।5.38.12।। तापसाश्रमवासिन्याः प्राज्यमूलफलोदके। तस्मिन्सिद्धाश्रमे देशे मन्दाकिन्या विदूरतः।।5.38.13।। तस्योपवनषण्डेषु नानापुष्पसुगन्धिषु। विहृत्य सलिले क्लिन्ना ममाङ्के समुपाविशमः।।5.38.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पुरा earlier, चित्रकूटस्य शैलस्य on the mountain of Chitrakuta, पूर्वोत्तरे in the northeast, पादे on foot, प्राज्यमूलफलोदके with abundant roots fruits and water, तस्मिन् in that, सिद्धाश्रमे Siddhasrama, देशे in a region, मन्दाकिन्याध्यविदूरतः not far from Mandakini river, तस्य of him, नानापुष्पसुगन्धिषु many kinds of fragrant flowers, उपवनषण्डेषु in gardens, सलिले in water, विहृत्य  after sporting, क्लिन्नः wet, तापसाश्रमवासिन्याः hermitages of ascetics, मम me, अङ्के the body, समुपाविशः you were seated.

#### Explanation

'Long ago we were staying at Siddhasrama. This hermitage was situated to the northeast at the foot of Chitrakuta mountain which was not far from the river Mandakini. It had abundant roots, fruits and water. You sported with me in the gardens which were fragrant with many types of flowers and ponds. You sat down on my lap with wet clothes in the hermitage'.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

ततो मांससमायुक्तो वायसः पर्यतुण्डयत्। तमहं लोष्टमुद्यम्य वारयामिस्म वायसम्।।5.38.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, मांससमायुक्तः it brought meat, वायसः crow, पर्यतुण्डयत् went round with its beak and pecked, अहम् I, लोष्टम् clod of earth, उद्यम्य by lifting, तम् him, वायसम् that crow, वारयामि स्म I presented him.

#### Explanation

'Then a crow greedy of flesh began to peck my bossom with its beak. I took a clod of earth and warded him off.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

दारयन्स च मां काकस्तत्त्रैव परिलीयते। न चाप्युपारमन्मांसाद्भक्षार्थि बलिभोजनः।।5.38.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः काकः that crow, माम् me, दारयन् while pecking, तत्रैव there itself, परिलीयते waited, भक्षार्थि hungry for food, सः he, बलिभोजनः eater of offerings, मांसात् flesh, न उपारमच्चापि was not giving up.

#### Explanation

'The crow was an eater of offerings, hungry for food began to peck me again and again for flesh.He did not stop.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

उत्कर्षन्त्यां च रशनां क्रुद्धायां मयि पक्षिणि। स्रस्यमाने च वसने ततो दृष्टा त्वया ह्यहम्।।5.38.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मयि I, पक्षिणि by the crow, क्रुद्धायाम् when I was angry, वसने when the cloth, स्रस्यमाने च was slipping, रशनाम् waist string, उत्कर्षन्त्यां च as I was pulling up, ततः then, त्वया by you, अहम् I, दृष्टा seen.

#### Explanation

'My upper cloth was slipping. I was trying to pull up my waist string angrily when you saw me.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

त्वयाऽपहसिता चाहं क्रुद्धा संलज्जिता तदा। भक्षगृध्नेन काकेन दारिता त्वामुपागता।।5.38.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, क्रुद्धा enraged, अहम् I, त्वया your, अपहसिता made fun of me, संलज्जिता  embarassed, भक्षगृध्नेन by the voracious bird, काकेन by the crow, दारिता torn, त्वाम् you, उपागता  sought.

#### Explanation

'Then you made fun of me, and I became angry. I was abashed. Torn by the voracious bird I sought your shelter.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

आसीनस्य च ते श्रान्ता पुनरुत्सङ्गमाविशम्। क्रुध्यन्ती च प्रहृष्टेन त्वयाऽहं परिसान्त्विता।।5.38.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

श्रान्ता exhausted, आसीनस्य when you sat, ते your, उत्सङ्गम् lap, पुनः again, आविशम् settled again, क्रुध्यन्ती seeing me angry, अहम् I, प्रहृष्टेन happily, त्वया by you, परिसान्त्विता you pacified me.

#### Explanation

'Exhausted, I sat on your lap again. Seeing my angry face you pacified me. You were happy (to see me draw close).

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

बाष्पपूर्णमुखी मन्दं चक्षुषी परिमार्जती। लक्षिताऽहं त्वया नाथ वायसेन प्रकोपिता।।5.38.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नाथ O lord, वायसेन by the crow, प्रकोपिता angered, बाष्पपूर्णमुखी face filled with tears, मन्दम् slowly, चक्षुषी eyes, परिमार्जती while wiping, अहम् I was, त्वया you, लक्षिता marked.

#### Explanation

'O lord when I was angered by the crow and my face was filled with tears, you marked me wiping my eyes.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

परिश्रमात्प्रसुप्ता च राघवाङ्केऽप्यहं चिरम्। पर्यायेण प्रसुप्तश्च ममाङ्के भरताग्रजः।।5.38.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहमपि I too, परिश्रमात् due to exhaustion, राघवाङ्के on Rama's lap, चिरम् for long time, प्रसुप्ता च I slept, पर्यायेण in turn, भरताग्रजः Bharata's elder brother, मम my, अङ्के on the lap, प्रसुप्तः slept.

#### Explanation

'Totally exhausted I slept on your lap for a long time, and in turn you slept on my lap later.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

स तत्र पुनरेवाथ वायसस्समुपागमत्। ततस्सुप्तप्रबुद्धां मां रामस्याङ्कात्समुत्थिताम्।।5.38.22।। वायसस्सहसागम्य विददार स्तनान्तरे। पुनः पुनरथोत्पत्य विददार स मां भृशम्।।5.38.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथ and then, सः वायसः that crow, पुनरेव again, तत्र there, समुपागमत् came back, ततः then, सः he, वायसः crow, सहसा atonce, आगम्य having come, सुप्तप्रबुद्धाम् awakened from sleep, रामस्य Rama's, अङ्कात् from the lap, समुत्थिताम् risen, माम् myself, स्तनान्तरे between my breasts, विददार clawed, अथ and, पुनः again, उत्पत्य flying, माम् myself, भृशम् repeatedly, विददार clawed down.

#### Explanation

'The crow came back again and clawed me in the space between my breasts even after I woke up after slumber from Rama's lap. Flying, it tore my bosom again and again.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

स तत्र पुनरेवाथ वायसस्समुपागमत्। ततस्सुप्तप्रबुद्धां मां रामस्याङ्कात्समुत्थिताम्।।5.38.22।। वायसस्सहसागम्य विददार स्तनान्तरे। पुनः पुनरथोत्पत्य विददार स मां भृशम्।।5.38.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथ and then, सः वायसः that crow, पुनरेव again, तत्र there, समुपागमत् came back, ततः then, सः he, वायसः crow, सहसा atonce, आगम्य having come, सुप्तप्रबुद्धाम् awakened from sleep, रामस्य Rama's, अङ्कात् from the lap, समुत्थिताम् risen, माम् myself, स्तनान्तरे between my breasts, विददार clawed, अथ and, पुनः again, उत्पत्य flying, माम् myself, भृशम् repeatedly, विददार clawed down.

#### Explanation

'The crow came back again and clawed me in the space between my breasts even after I woke up after slumber from Rama's lap. Flying, it tore my bosom again and again.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

ततस्समुक्षितो रामो मुक्तैश्शोणितबिन्दुभिः। वायसेन ततस्तेन बलवत्क्लिश्यमानया।।5.38.24।। स मया बोधितश्श्रीमान्सुखसुप्तः परन्तपः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, रामः Rama, मुक्तै: dripping, शोणितबिन्दुभिः drops of blood, समुक्षितः seeing, ततः then, तेन वायसेन by that crow, बलवत् forcibly, क्लिश्यमानया tormented, मया by me, श्रीमान् illustrious, परन्तपः scorcher of foes, सुखसुप्तः happily sleeping, सः he, बोधितः he was awakened.

#### Explanation

'At the touch of the drops of blood dripping from my body, tormented by seeing me distressed with the dripping blood, you were awakened, you who were an illustrious scorcher of foes.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

स मां दृष्ट्वा महाबाहुर्वितुन्नां स्तनयोस्तदा।।5.38.25।। आशीविष इव क्रुद्धश्वसन्वाक्यमभाषत।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाबाहुः strong armed, सः Rama, तदा then, स्तनयोः in the breasts, वितुन्नाम् wounded, माम् me, दृष्ट्वा on seeing, क्रुद्धः angered, आशीविष इव like a (hissing) snake, श्वसन् breathing hard, वाक्यम् these words, अभाषत spoke.

#### Explanation

'O strongarmed Rama, seeing my torn breasts, you became angry like a hissing serpent  and hissing said :

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

केन ते नागनासोरु विक्षतं वै स्तनान्तरम्।।5.38.26।। कः क्रीडति सरोषेण पञ्चवक्त्रेण भोगिना।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नागनासोरु lady whose thighs look like elephant's trunk, सीता Sita, स्तनान्तरम् your breasts, केन by whom, विक्षतं वै wounded, सरोषेण with the angry, पञ्चवक्त्रेण fivehooded, भोगिना by the serpent, कः who, क्रीडति sporting.

#### Explanation

'O Sita with a beautiful lap like an elephant trunk by whom is your bosom wounded? Who is sporting with an angry fivehooded snake?

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

वीक्षमाणस्ततस्तं वै वायसं समुदैक्षत।।5.38.27।। नखैस्सरुधिरैस्तीक्ष्णैर्मामेवाभिमुखं स्थितम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, वीक्षमाणः while glancing, सरुधिरैः with blood stained, तीक्ष्णैः with sharp ones, नखैः with nails, मामेव me, अभिमुखम् facing me, स्थितम् standing, तं वायसम् that crow, समुदैक्षत वै he saw.

#### Explanation

'Then looking around, you saw the crow facing me with sharp, bloodstained claws.

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

पुत्त्रः किल स शक्रस्य वायसः पततां वरः।।5.38.28।। धरान्तरगतश्शीघ्रं पवनस्य गतौ समः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पतताम् of the birds, वरः eminent, सः वायसः that crow, शक्रस्य Indra's, पुत्रः किल it is said that he is the son, धरान्तरगतः he covered a long distance, शीघ्रम् in great speed, गतौ in movement, पवनस्य Windgod's, समः matching.

#### Explanation

'This crow was the best of birds. He was surely Indra's son who had covered a long distance with great speed and his speed was like the Windgod's.

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

ततस्तस्मिन्महाबाहुः कोपसंवर्तितेक्षणः।।5.38.29।। वायसे कृतवान्क्रूरां मतिं मतिमतां वरः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, मतिमताम् among the wise, वरः best, महाबाहुः strongarmed, कोपसंवर्तितेक्षणः with eye balls rolling in anger, तस्मिन् him, वायसे at the crow, क्रूराम् cruel, मतिम् mind, कृतवान् made up.

#### Explanation

'Then you, the strongarmed and best among the wise, your eyeballs rolling in fury, made up your mind about the cruel crow.

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

स दर्भं संस्तराद्गृह्य ब्राह्मेणास्त्रेण योजयत्।।5.38.30।। स दीप्त इव कालाग्निर्जज्वालाभिमुखो द्विजम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः he, Rama, संस्तरात् from his darbha mat, दर्भम् darbha grass, गृह्य took out, ब्राह्मेण अस्त्रेण invoked the weapon of Brahma into it by reciting the mantra, योजयत् discharged, सः that, दीप्तः blazed, कालाग्निरिव like the fire of death, द्विजम् the bird, अभिमुखः towards, जज्वाल glowed.

#### Explanation

'Taking out a blade of darbha grass from your mat, you invoked the weapon of Brahma by reciting the mantra. Then you discharged it which blazed like the fire of death and went spearheading towards the crow.

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

स तं प्रदीप्तं चिक्षेप दर्भं तं वायसं प्रति।।5.38.31।। ततस्तं वायसं दर्भस्सोम्बरेऽनुजगाम ह।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः he, प्रदीप्तम् glowing, तं दर्भम् that darbha, तं वायसं प्रति him, the crow in turn, चिक्षेप threw, ततः then, सः दर्भः that darbha, तं वायसम् that crow, अम्बरे in the sky, अनुजगाम ह followed.

#### Explanation

'Then you threw the burning darbha grass at the crow and it chased him in the sky.

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

अनुसृष्टस्तदा काको जगाम विविधां गतिम्।।5.38.32।। लोककाम इमं लोकं सर्वं वै विचचार ह।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, काकः that crow, अनुसृष्टः one who was followed, विविधाम् many, गतिम् places, जगाम went, लोककामः the entire universe desiring safety, इमम् this, सर्वम् all, लोकम् worlds, विचचार ह went round.

#### Explanation

'Then that crow flew to many places seeking asylum all over the universe chased by the blade of grass.

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

स पित्रा च परित्यक्तस्सुरैश्च समहर्षिभिः।।5.38.33।। त्रीन्लोकान्सम्परिक्रम्य तमेव शरणं गतः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः he, त्रीन् three, लोकान् worlds, सम्परिक्रम्य having gone round, पित्रा च and to his father also, समहर्षिभिः including the sages, सुरैश्च by gods, परित्यक्तः rejected, तमेव him alone, शरणं गतः went seeking refuge.

#### Explanation

'Then the crow went round all the three worlds, having been rejected by his father Indra, including all the sages and gods. Refused by all he came back to Rama seeking shelter.

---

### Shloka 34

#### Shloka Text

स तं निपतितं भूमौ शरण्यश्शरणागतम्।।5.38.34।। वधार्हमपि काकुत्स्थ: कृपया पर्यपालयत्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शरण्यः saviour, सः काकुत्स्थ: that Kakutstha, शरणागतम् one who sought refuge, भूमौ on the ground, निपतितम् fallen on the ground, तम् him, वधार्हमपि even though deserves to be killed, कृपया out of compassion, पर्यपालयत् saved.

#### Explanation

'Seeing the crow fallen on the ground, O Kakutstha, you who are a saviour of those who seek refuge saved him out of compassion, even though he deserved to be killed.

---

### Shloka 35

#### Shloka Text

परिद्यूनं विषण्णं च स तमायान्तमब्रवीत्।।5.38.35।। मोघं कर्तुं न शक्यं तु ब्राह्ममस्त्रं तदुच्यताम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः Rama, परिद्यूनम् pained, विषण्णम् despondent, आयान्तम् he reached, तम् him, अब्रवीत् said, ब्राह्मम् अस्त्रम् Brahma weapon, मोघम् in vain, कर्तुम् to do, न शक्यं तु not possible, तत् that, उच्यताम् tell.

#### Explanation

'Rama, you saw the crow in pain and despondency who returned to you and said that it is not possible to make Brahma's weapon ineffectual.You may tell now. (what should be done.)

---

### Shloka 36

#### Shloka Text

हिनस्तु दक्षिणाक्षि त्वच्छर इत्यथ सोऽब्रवीत्।।5.38.36।। ततस्तस्याक्षि काकस्य हिनस्ति स्म स दक्षिणम्। दत्त्वा स दक्षिणं नेत्रं प्राणेभ्यः परिरक्षितः।।5.38.37।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथ and then, सः that, त्वच्छरः your arrow, दक्षिणाक्षि right eye, हिनस्तु may be blinded, इति thus, अब्रवीत् said, ततः then, सः he, तस्य काकस्य that crow's, दक्षिणम् right, अक्षि eye, हिनस्ति स्म blinded by hitting, सः that, दक्षिणम् right, दत्त्वा having offered, प्राणेभ्यः from life, परिरक्षितः protected.

#### Explanation

'Then that crow said, 'let my right eye be blinded'. you then hit the right eye of the crow'. Thus the crow offered his right eye and saved himself.

---

### Shloka 37

#### Shloka Text

हिनस्तु दक्षिणाक्षि त्वच्छर इत्यथ सोऽब्रवीत्।।5.38.36।। ततस्तस्याक्षि काकस्य हिनस्ति स्म स दक्षिणम्। दत्त्वा स दक्षिणं नेत्रं प्राणेभ्यः परिरक्षितः।।5.38.37।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथ and then, सः that, त्वच्छरः your arrow, दक्षिणाक्षि right eye, हिनस्तु may be blinded, इति thus, अब्रवीत् said, ततः then, सः he, तस्य काकस्य that crow's, दक्षिणम् right, अक्षि eye, हिनस्ति स्म blinded by hitting, सः that, दक्षिणम् right, दत्त्वा having offered, प्राणेभ्यः from life, परिरक्षितः protected.

#### Explanation

'Then that crow said, 'let my right eye be blinded'. you then hit the right eye of the crow'. Thus the crow offered his right eye and saved himself.

---

### Shloka 38

#### Shloka Text

स रामाय नमस्कृत्य राज्ञे दशरथाय च। विसृष्टस्तेन वीरेण प्रतिपेदे स्वमालयम्।।5.38.38।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः that crow, रामाय to Rama, राज्ञे to the king, दशरथाय च and to Dasaratha, नमस्कृत्य having offered salutations, तेन by him, वीरेण by the warrior, विसृष्टः permitted to leave, स्वम् his own, आलयम् dwelling, प्रतिपेदे went reached.

#### Explanation

'That crow offered salutations to you Rama and king Dasaratha, thereafter took permission and went to his own dwelling.

---

### Shloka 39

#### Shloka Text

मत्कृते काकमात्रे तु ब्रह्मास्त्रं समुदीरितम्। कस्माद्यो मां हरेत्त्वत्तः क्षमसे तं महीपते।।5.38.39।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महीपते lord of the earth, मत्कृते for the sake of, काकमात्रे on a mere crow, ब्रह्मास्त्रम् weapon of Brahma, समुदीरितम् released, त्वत्तः from you, माम् me, यः he who, अहरत् abducted, तम् him, कस्मात् why, क्षमसे you excuse him.

#### Explanation

'(Addressing Rama as though he was present before her, Sita said) 'O lord of the earth for my sake you released the weapon of Brahma on a crow. What makes you excuse him who has abducted me?

---

### Shloka 40

#### Shloka Text

स कुरुष्व महोत्साहः कृपां मयि नरर्षभ। त्वया नाथवती नाथ ह्यनाथा इव दृश्यते।।5.38.40।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नरर्षभ bull among men, सः Rama, महोत्साहः one with great love, मयि in me, कृपाम् compassion, कुरुष्व bestow, नाथ O lord, त्वया your, नाथवती I have been protected, अनाथा इव like an orphan, दृश्यते  appears.

#### Explanation

'O bull among men you are so loving to me. I am like an orphan. Be kind to me. She who has found her lord in you stays without a protector.

---

### Shloka 41

#### Shloka Text

आनृशंस्यं परो धर्मस्तवत्त्त ऐव मया श्रुतः। जानामि त्वां महावीर्यं महोत्साहं महाबलम्।।5.38.41।। अपारपारमक्षोभ्यं गाम्भीर्यात्सागरोपमम्। भर्तारं ससमुद्राया धरण्या वासवोपमम्।।5.38.42।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अनृशंस्यम् kindness, परः supreme, धर्मः is righteous, मया by me, त्वत्तः एव from you alone,श्रुतः heard, त्वाम् you, महावीर्यम् heroic, महोत्साहम् vigour, महाबलम् mighty, अपारपारम् boundless, अक्षोभ्यम् never to be stirred, गाम्भीर्यात् with depth, सागरोपमम् like the ocean, ससमुद्रायाः inclusive of seas, धरण्याः of the earth, भर्तारम् lord, वासवोपमम् like Vasava, जानामि I know.

#### Explanation

'O my supreme lord I know you are kind, righteous, valiant, vigroous, mighty. You are boundless, calm and deep like the ocean. You are lord of earth and the seas and compeer of Vasava. I have heard from you that motiveless compassion is the best way to make one righteous.

---

### Shloka 42

#### Shloka Text

आनृशंस्यं परो धर्मस्तवत्त्त ऐव मया श्रुतः। जानामि त्वां महावीर्यं महोत्साहं महाबलम्।।5.38.41।। अपारपारमक्षोभ्यं गाम्भीर्यात्सागरोपमम्। भर्तारं ससमुद्राया धरण्या वासवोपमम्।।5.38.42।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अनृशंस्यम् kindness, परः supreme, धर्मः is righteous, मया by me, त्वत्तः एव from you alone,श्रुतः heard, त्वाम् you, महावीर्यम् heroic, महोत्साहम् vigour, महाबलम् mighty, अपारपारम् boundless, अक्षोभ्यम् never to be stirred, गाम्भीर्यात् with depth, सागरोपमम् like the ocean, ससमुद्रायाः inclusive of seas, धरण्याः of the earth, भर्तारम् lord, वासवोपमम् like Vasava, जानामि I know.

#### Explanation

'O my supreme lord I know you are kind, righteous, valiant, vigroous, mighty. You are boundless, calm and deep like the ocean. You are lord of earth and the seas and compeer of Vasava. I have heard from you that motiveless compassion is the best way to make one righteous.

---

### Shloka 43

#### Shloka Text

एवमस्त्रविदां श्रेष्ठस्सत्यवान्बलवानपि। किमर्थमस्त्रं रक्षस्सु न योजयसि राघव।।5.38.43।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राघव Rama, एवम् in that way, अस्त्रविदाम् among the users of weapons, श्रेष्ठः expert, सत्यवान् truthful, बलवानपि even strong, रक्षस्सु demons, अस्त्रम् weapon, किमर्थम् why, न योजयसि you are not using?

#### Explanation

'Even though you are an expert in the use of weapons, truthful and strong. (Yet) why are you not using weapons aganst the demons?'

---

### Shloka 44

#### Shloka Text

न नागा नाऽपि गन्धर्वा नासुरा न मरुद्गणाः। रामस्य समरे वेगं शक्ताः प्रतिसमाधितुं।।5.38.44।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

समरे in war, रामस्य Rama's, वेगम् speed, प्रतिसमाधितुम् to hit back, नागाः serpents, न शक्ताः not able, गन्धर्वा अपि even gandharvas, न not, असुराः demons, न not, मरुद्गणाः marutas, न not.

#### Explanation

(Now Sita turns to Hanuman and says) "In war serpents, gandharvas, demons or Marutas cannot withstand the speed of Rama's arrows and hit back.

---

### Shloka 45

#### Shloka Text

तस्य वीर्यवतः कश्चिद्यद्यस्ति मयि सम्भ्रमः। किमर्थं न शरैस्तीक्ष्णै: क्षयं नयति राक्षसान्।।5.38.45।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वीर्यवतः hero of great discipline, तस्य his, मयि in me, कश्चित् even a little, सम्भ्रमः hurry, अस्ति यदि if he has, तीक्ष्णैः with sharp, शरैः with arrows, राक्षसान् at the demons, किमर्थम् why, क्षयम् destroy, न नयति not killing.

#### Explanation

"Does Rama have a little anxiety about me? If he has, why does he, a disciplined hero, not  put an end to the demons with his sharp arrows?

---

### Shloka 46

#### Shloka Text

भ्रातुरादेशमादाय लक्ष्मणो वा परन्तपः। कस्य हेतोर्न मां वीरः परित्राति महाबलः।।5.38.46।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

परन्तपः scorcher of enemies, महाबलः mighty, वीरः hero, लक्ष्मणो वा even Lakshmana, भ्रातुः brother, आदेशम् order, आदाय after taking, कस्य हेतोः for what reason, माम् me, न परित्राति not rescue.

#### Explanation

"Or, why does the mighty hero, Lakshmana, who is a scorcher of enemies, not come to my rescue taking the orders from his brother?

---

### Shloka 47

#### Shloka Text

यदि तौ पुरुषव्याघ्रौ वाय्वग्निसमतेजसौ। सुराणामपि दुर्धर्षौ किमर्थं मामुपेक्षतः।।5.38.47।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वाय्वग्निसमतेजसौ resembling wind and fire in power, पुरुषव्याघ्रौ two tigers among men, तौ they both, सुराणाम् even the gods, दुर्धर्षौ यदि अपि even though unassailable also, माम् me, किमर्थम् how is it, उपेक्षतः disregarding.

#### Explanation

"Both Rama and Lakshmana are tigers among men who resemble wind and fire in power and have the unassailability of the gods. Wherefore are they disregarding me?.

---

### Shloka 48

#### Shloka Text

ममैव दुष्कृतं किञ्चिन्महदस्ति न संशयः। समर्थावपि तौ यन्मां नावेक्षेते परन्तपौ।।5.38.48।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ममैव mine alone, महत् atrocious, किञ्चित् some, दुष्कृतम् sin, अस्ति is committed, संशयः doubt, न no, यत् because, समर्थावपि although capable, परन्तपौ both scorchers of enemies, तौ both, माम् me, न आवेक्षेते not deliver.

#### Explanation

"Perhaps I must have committed an atrocious sin. There is no doubt. For although both of them are scorchers of enemies, they deliver me not."

---

### Shloka 49

#### Shloka Text

वैदेह्या वचनं श्रुत्वा करुणं साश्रुभाषितम्। अथाब्रवीन्महातेजा हनुमान्मारुतात्मजः।।5.38.49।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथ and then, महातेजाः lustrous, मारुतात्मजः Windgod's son, हनुमान् Hanuman, वैदेह्या Vaidehi's, साश्रु with tears, करुणम् piteous, भाषितम् spoken, वचनम् words, श्रुत्वा having heard, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

Hearing the piteous words of Vaidehi and beholding her eyes filled with tears, the Windgod's son, lustrous Hanuman replied thus:.

---

### Shloka 50

#### Shloka Text

त्वच्छोकविमुखो रामो देवि सत्येन ते शपे। रामे दुःखाभिपन्ने च लक्ष्मणः परितप्यते।।5.38.50।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

देवि Devi, रामः Rama, त्वच्छोकविमुखः he is averse to your misery, सत्येन truly, ते your, शपे I vow, रामे when Rama, दुःखाभिपन्ने immersed in sorrow, लक्ष्मणः च even Lakshmana, परितप्यते wails.

#### Explanation

"O godlike lady Rama is not averse to your misery. On my word this is true. Rama is immersed in sorrow. Even Lakshmana wails.

---

### Shloka 51

#### Shloka Text

कथञ्चिद्भवती दृष्टा न कालः परिदेवितुम्। इमं मुहूर्तं दुःखानां द्रक्ष्यस्यन्तमनिन्दिते।।5.38.51।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कथञ्चित् with difficulty, भवती your self, दृष्टा could be found, परिदेवितुम् wailing, कालः time, न not, अनिन्दिते blameless lady, इमम् this, मुहूर्तम् moment, दुःखानाम् all sorrrow, अन्तम् end, द्रक्ष्यसि you will see.

#### Explanation

"I could find you with difficulty. This is not the time to wail. O blameless lady take it, your sorrow has come to an end at this moment.

---

### Shloka 52

#### Shloka Text

तावुभौ पुरुषव्याघ्रौ राजपुत्रौ महाबलौ। त्वद्दर्शनकृतोत्साहौ लङ्कां भस्मीकरिष्यतः।।5.38.52।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पुरुषव्याघ्रौ two tigers among men, महाबलौ mighty, त्वद्दर्शनकृतोत्साहौ eager to see you, उभौ both, तौ राजपुत्रौ those two princes, लङ्काम् this Lanka, भस्मीकरिष्यतः will reduce to ashes.

#### Explanation

"Both the mighty brothers, tigers among men, are eager to see you. Both the princes will reduce this Lanka  into ashes.

---

### Shloka 53

#### Shloka Text

हत्त्वा च समरे क्रूरं रावणं सहबान्धवम्। राघवस्त्वां विशालाक्षि नेष्यति स्वां पुरीं प्रति।।5.38.53।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विशालाक्षि O largeeyed lady, राघवः Rama, समरे in war, सहबान्धवम् along with relatives, क्रूरम् cruel, रावणम् Ravana, हत्वा after killing, स्वां पुरीं प्रति towards his city, त्वाम् you, नेष्यति he will be taking you.

#### Explanation

"O largeeyed lady cruel Ravana along with his relatives will be killed by Rama in war and Rama will return to his city with you.

---

### Shloka 54

#### Shloka Text

ब्रूहि यद्राघवो वाच्यो लक्ष्मणश्च महाबलः। सुग्रीवो वापि तेजस्वी हरयोऽपि समागताः।।5.38.54।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राघवः Rama, यत् whichever, वाच्यः you may like to convey, महाबलः mighty Rama, लक्ष्मणश्च and Lakshmana, तेजस्वी brilliant, सुग्रीवो वापि and Sugriva also, समागताः those who collect there, हरयोऽपि even the vanaras, ब्रूहि say.

#### Explanation

"Communicate your message to mighty Rama and Lakshamana, to brilliant Sugriva and even the vanaras who gather there.

---

### Shloka 55

#### Shloka Text

इत्युक्तवति तस्मिंस्तु सीता सुरसुतोपमा। उवाच शोकसन्तप्ता हनुमन्तं प्लवङ्गमम्।।5.38.55।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तस्मिन् when he, इति in that way, उक्तवति when he said, सुरसुतोपमा lady resembling a god's daughter, सीता Sita, शोकसन्तप्ता filled with grief, प्लवङ्गमम् to vanara, हनुमन्तम् Hanuman, उवाच said

#### Explanation

"Hanuman having spoken this way, Sita, resembling a god's daughter, grieving, thus addressed Hanuman:

---

### Shloka 56

#### Shloka Text

कौसल्या लोकभर्तारं सुषुवे यं मनस्विनी। तं ममार्थे सुखं पृच्छ शिरसा चाभिवादय।।5.38.56।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लोकभर्तारम् lord of the world, यम् whom, मनस्विनी sensitive woman, कौसल्या Kausalya, सुषुवे bore, तम् him, मदर्थे on my behalf, सुखम् welfare, पृच्छ you may inquire into, शिरसा by bowing, अभिवादय च offer salutations.

#### Explanation

"Bow to Rama and offer salutations on my behalf. Inquiries concerning his welfare should be addressed to Rama, whom virtuous Kausalya bore as a son.

---

### Shloka 57

#### Shloka Text

स्रजश्च सर्वरत्नानि प्रिया याश्च वराङ्गनाः। ऐश्वर्यं च विशालायां पृथिव्यामपि दुर्लभम्।।5.38.57।। पितरं मातरं चैव सम्मान्याभिप्रसाद्य च। अनुप्रव्रजितो रामं सुमित्रा येन सुप्रजाः।।5.38.58।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

येन by whom, सुमित्रा Sumitra, सुप्रजाः has attained the title of blessed mother, स्रजश्च garlands, सर्वरत्नानि all jewels, प्रियाः dear, याः those, वराङ्गनाः best of women, विशालायाम् in the vast, पृथिव्याम् in the whole earth, दुर्लभम् most difficult to attain, ऐश्वर्यं चापि prosperity also, पितरम् father, मातरं चापि even mother, सम्मान्य having respected, अभिप्रसाद्य  pleased by adopting the life of an ascetic, च and, रामम् with Rama, अनुप्रव्रजितः accompanied.

#### Explanation

None

---

### Shloka 58

#### Shloka Text

स्रजश्च सर्वरत्नानि प्रिया याश्च वराङ्गनाः। ऐश्वर्यं च विशालायां पृथिव्यामपि दुर्लभम्।।5.38.57।। पितरं मातरं चैव सम्मान्याभिप्रसाद्य च। अनुप्रव्रजितो रामं सुमित्रा येन सुप्रजाः।।5.38.58।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

येन by whom, सुमित्रा Sumitra, सुप्रजाः has attained the title of blessed mother, स्रजश्च garlands, सर्वरत्नानि all jewels, प्रियाः dear, याः those, वराङ्गनाः best of women, विशालायाम् in the vast, पृथिव्याम् in the whole earth, दुर्लभम् most difficult to attain, ऐश्वर्यं चापि prosperity also, पितरम् father, मातरं चापि even mother, सम्मान्य having respected, अभिप्रसाद्य  pleased by adopting the life of an ascetic, च and, रामम् with Rama, अनुप्रव्रजितः accompanied.

#### Explanation

None

---

### Shloka 59

#### Shloka Text

आनुकूल्येन धर्मात्मा त्यक्त्वा सुखमनुत्तमम्। अनुगच्छति काकुत्स्थं भ्रातरं पालयन्वने।।5.38.59।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

धर्मात्मा righteous self, अनुत्तमम् great, सुखम् pleasures, त्यक्त्वा having given up, भ्रातरम् his brother, काकुत्स्थम् Kakustha Rama, वने to the forest, आनुकूल्येन in his favour, पालयन् while serving, अनुगच्छति followed.

#### Explanation

"Great, righteous Lakshmana had given up his pleasures and followed his brother Rama in his favour into the forest to be able to serve him.

---

### Shloka 60

#### Shloka Text

सिंहस्कन्धो महाबाहुर्मनस्वी प्रियदर्शिनः। पितृवद्वर्तते रामे मातृवन्मां समाचरन्।।5.38.60।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सिंहस्कन्ध: lionshoulders, महाबाहुः strongarmed one, मनस्वी highsouled, प्रियदर्शनः a handsome person, माम् me, मातृवत् like his mother, समाचरन् while treating, रामे with Rama, पितृवत् as his father, वर्तते conducts.

#### Explanation

"Lakshmana, who has lionshoulders and strong arms, who is handsome, who takes Rama as his father and treats me as his own mother.

---

### Shloka 61

#### Shloka Text

ह्रियमाणां तदा वीरो न तु मां वेद लक्ष्मणः। वृद्धोपसेवी लक्ष्मीवान् शक्तो न बहुभाषिता।।5.38.61।। राजपुत्रः प्रियः श्रेष्ठः सदृशः श्वशुरस्य मे।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वीरः hero, लक्ष्मणः Lakshmana, तदा then, माम् me, ह्रियमाणाम् while I was borne away, न वेद not know, वृद्धोपसेवी serving elders, लक्ष्मीवान् prosperous, शक्तः energetic, न बहुभाषिता limited in speech, मे my, श्वशुरस्य of my fatherinlaw, सदृशः resembles, प्रियः dear, श्रेष्ठः foremost, राजपुत्रः prince.

#### Explanation

"Heroic Lakshmana, did not know while I was borne away. He is prosperous, foremost in serving elders. He is energetic and reserved in his speech like my fatherinlaw. He is a prince dear (to his brother).

---

### Shloka 62

#### Shloka Text

ममः प्रियतरो नित्यं भ्राता रामस्य लक्ष्मणः।।5.38.62।। नियुक्तो धुरि यस्यां तु तामुद्वहति वीर्यवान्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामस्य Rama's, भ्राता brother, लक्ष्मणः Lakshmana, नित्यम् always, मम to me, प्रियतरः loving, वीर्यवान् heroic, यस्याम् in whichever, धुरि task, नियुक्तः he is entrusted, ताम् such a job, उद्वहति executes.

#### Explanation

"Rama's brother Lakshmana is always dear to me. Heroic Lakshmana will discharge whichever responsibility is entrusted to him.

---

### Shloka 63

#### Shloka Text

यं दृष्ट्वा राघवो नैव वृत्तमार्यमनुस्मरेत्।।5.38.63।। स ममार्थाय कुशलं वक्तव्यो वचनान्मम।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

यम् who, दृष्ट्वा after seeing, राघवः Rama, वृत्तम् support, आर्यम् noble, न अनुस्मरेत् not remembering, सः he, मम my, अर्थाय sake, मम on me, वचनात् words, कुशलम् welfare, वक्तव्यः that I have enquired.

#### Explanation

"He is Lakshmana for whose care and support Rama did not miss the noble king Dasaratha (during exile). Convey him my words of enquiry about his wellbeing.

---

### Shloka 64

#### Shloka Text

मृदुर्नित्यं शुचिर्दक्षः प्रियो रामस्य लक्ष्मणः।।5.38.64।। यथा हि वानरश्रेष्ठ दुःखक्षयकरो भवेत्। त्वमस्मिन्कार्यनिर्योगे प्रमाणं हरिसत्तमः।।5.38.65।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वानरश्रेष्ठः excellent vanara, हरिसत्तम: esteemed vanaras, मृदुः gentle, नित्यम् always, शुचिः pure, दक्षः competent, रामस्य Rama's, प्रियः beloved, लक्ष्मणः Lakshmana, यथा as such, दुःखक्षयकरः who mitigates suffering, भवेत् will be, अस्मिन् in that, कार्यनिर्योगे in a task, त्वम् you, प्रमाणम् authority.

#### Explanation

"Lakshmana is soft, pure, competent and dear to Rama. O great vanara, act in such a manner that Lakshmana can mitigate my suffering. O great monkey, the best of vanaras you are competent in accomplishing this task.

---

### Shloka 65

#### Shloka Text

मृदुर्नित्यं शुचिर्दक्षः प्रियो रामस्य लक्ष्मणः।।5.38.64।। यथा हि वानरश्रेष्ठ दुःखक्षयकरो भवेत्। त्वमस्मिन्कार्यनिर्योगे प्रमाणं हरिसत्तमः।।5.38.65।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वानरश्रेष्ठः excellent vanara, हरिसत्तम: esteemed vanaras, मृदुः gentle, नित्यम् always, शुचिः pure, दक्षः competent, रामस्य Rama's, प्रियः beloved, लक्ष्मणः Lakshmana, यथा as such, दुःखक्षयकरः who mitigates suffering, भवेत् will be, अस्मिन् in that, कार्यनिर्योगे in a task, त्वम् you, प्रमाणम् authority.

#### Explanation

"Lakshmana is soft, pure, competent and dear to Rama. O great vanara, act in such a manner that Lakshmana can mitigate my suffering. O great monkey, the best of vanaras you are competent in accomplishing this task.

---

### Shloka 66

#### Shloka Text

राघवस्त्वत्समारम्भान्मयि यत्नपरो भवेत्। इदं ब्रूयाश्च मे नाथं शूरं रामं पुनः पुनः।।5.38.66।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राघवः Rama, त्वत्समारम्भात् by your efforts alone, मयि regarding me, यत्नपरः one who makes efforts, भवेत् will be, मे my, नाथम् husband, शूरम् powerful hero, रामम् Rama, पुनः पुनः again and again, इदम् this, ब्रूयाः may tell.

#### Explanation

"By your efforts alone my husband will try for my release. You may tell these words to my heroic husband, Rama, again and again.

---

### Shloka 67

#### Shloka Text

जीवितं धारयिष्यामि मासं दशरथात्मज। ऊर्ध्वं मासान्न जीवेयं सत्येनाहं ब्रवीमि ते।।5.38.67।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दशरथात्मज Dasaratha's son, Rama, मासम् a month, जीवितम् life, धारयिष्यामि will hold on to life, मासात् after a month, ऊर्ध्वम् beyond that, न जीवेयम् I will not live, अहम् I, सत्येन on truth, ते to you, ब्रवीमि I am telling.

#### Explanation

"I will hold on to life  only for a month. Beyond, I will not survive. I swear by truth. You may tell this to Rama.

---

### Shloka 68

#### Shloka Text

रावणेनोपरुद्धां मां निकृत्य पापकर्मणा। त्रातुमर्हसि वीर त्वं पातालादिव कौशिकीम्।।5.38.68।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वीर hero, पापकर्मणा by the wicked, रावणेन by Ravana, निकृत्य after insulting, उपरुद्धाम् imprisonment, माम् me, त्वम् you, पातालात् from the underworld or Pataala, कौशिकीमिव like Kausik, त्रातुम् rescue me, अर्हसि is proper.

#### Explanation

"O heroic Rama just as Kausik was rescued from the underworld it behoves you to deliver me from imprisonment of wicked Ravana".

---

### Shloka 69

#### Shloka Text

ततो वस्त्रगतं मुक्त्वा दिव्यं चूडामणिं शुभम्। प्रदेयो राघवायेति सीता हनुमते ददौ।।5.38.69।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, सीता Sita, वस्त्रगतम् tied up in clothes, शुभम् auspicious, दिव्यम् exquisite, चूडामणिम् Chudamani, an ornament worn on the head, मुक्त्वा took out, राघवाय to Rama, प्रदेयः to be given, इति thus, हनुमते to Hanuman, ददौ gave.

#### Explanation

Then Sita took out the exquisite and auspicious Chudamani worn on the head, untied from her clothes and gave it to Hanuman to be presented to Rama.

---

### Shloka 70

#### Shloka Text

प्रतिगृह्य ततो वीरो मणिरत्नमनुत्तमम्। अङ्गुल्या योजयामास न ह्यस्य प्राभवद्भुजः।।5.38.70।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, वीरः hero, अनुत्तमम् very great, मणिरत्नम् jewel, प्रतिगृह्य after taking, अङ्गुल्या on his finger, योजयामास placed अस्य to his, भुजः arm, न प्राभवत् हि was not fitting.

#### Explanation

Then Hanuman, taking the extraordinary jewel and placed it on his finger as it was not fitting to his arm.

---

### Shloka 71

#### Shloka Text

मणिरत्नं कपिवरः प्रतिगृह्याभिवाद्य च। सीतां प्रदक्षिणं कृत्वा प्रणतः पार्व्शतः स्थितः।।5.38.71।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कपिवरः best of vanaras, मणिरत्नम् jewel, प्रतिगृह्य after taking, सीताम् from Sita, अभिवाद्य च having saluted, प्रदक्षिणं कृत्वा having circumambulated, प्रणतः humbly, पार्श्वतः by the side, स्थितः stood.

#### Explanation

Having recieved the jewel from Sita and circumambulated her, Hanuman offered salutations to  her and stood by her.

---

### Shloka 72

#### Shloka Text

हर्षेण महता युक्तः सीतादर्शनजेन सः। हृदयेन गतो रामं शरीरेण तु विष्ठितः।।5.38.72।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः he, सीतादर्शनजेन by seeing Sita, महता with a great, हर्षेण joy, युक्तः endowed, हृदयेन his mind, रामम् to Rama, गतः went, शरीरेण तु but body, विष्ठितः stood there.

#### Explanation

Hanuman stood there physically, with great joy gazing at Sita, but mentally he was with Rama.

---

### Shloka 73

#### Shloka Text

मणिवरमुपगृह्य तं महार्हं जनकनृपात्मजया धृतं प्रभावात्। गिरिरिव पवनावधूतमुक्तः सुखितमनाः प्रतिसङ्क्रमं प्रपेदे।।5.38.73।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महार्हम् precious, जनकनृपात्मजया by Janaka's daughter, धृतम् borne, तं मणिवरम् that great jewel, उपगृह्य taking, प्रभावात् by its glow, पवनावधूतमुक्तः shaken by wind and released, गिरिरिव like a mountain, सुखितमनाः pleased, प्रतिसङ्क्रमम् to return, प्रपेदे prepared.

#### Explanation

Having received the most precious jewel from the daughter of Janaka, Hanuman prepared to return like a mountain released from the impact of the wind. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये सुन्दरकाण्डे अष्टत्रिंशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the thirtyeighth sarga of Sundarakanda of the holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 39

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

मणिं दत्त्वा ततः सीता हनूमन्तमथाब्रवीत्। अभिज्ञानमभिज्ञातमेतद्रामस्य तत्त्वतः।।5.39.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीता Sita, मणिं Jewel, दत्त्वा having given, ततः that way, हनूमन्तम् to Hanuman, अब्रवीत् said, एतत् this, रामस्य to Rama, अभिज्ञात known, तत्त्वतः truly, अभिज्ञानम् signet.

#### Explanation

Sita gave the jewel to Hanuman and said, "This signet ornament is known to Rama very well".

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

मणिं तु दृष्ट्वा रामो वै त्रयाणां संस्मरिष्यति। वीरो जनन्या मम च राज्ञो दशरथस्य च।।5.39.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मणिम् jewel, दृष्ट्वा after seeing, रामः Rama, त्रयाणाम् of three of us, जनन्याः of the mother, मम च me and, राज्ञः king, दशरथस्य च Dasaratha and, संस्मरिष्यति will recall.

#### Explanation

"Seeing this jewel,Rama will recall three of us, my mother, myself and the king Dasaratha" (as this was given by her mother at her marriage.)

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

स भूयस्त्वं समुत्साहे चोदितो हरिसत्तम। अस्मिन्कार्यसमारम्भे प्रचिन्तय यदुत्तरम्।।5.39.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हरिसत्तम great Hanuman, सः such Rama, त्वम् you, भूयः again, समुत्साहचोदितः prompted by enthusiasm, अस्मिन् in this, कार्यसमारम्भे in this effort, यत् that, उत्तरम् now, प्रचिन्तय think over.

#### Explanation

"Great Hanuman you are enthused now. You may think over the future course of action.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

त्वमस्मिन्कार्यनिर्योगे प्रमाणं हरिसत्तम। हनुमन्यत्नमास्थाय दुःखक्षयकरो भव।।5.39.4।। तस्य चिन्तयतो यत्नो दुःखक्षयकरो भवेत्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हरिसत्तम O best of monkeys, अस्मिन् in this, कार्यनिर्योगे in carrying out this task, त्वम् you, प्रमाणम् are responsible, हनुमान् Hanuman, यत्नम् initiative, आस्थाय after making, दुःखक्षयकरः reducer of suffering, भव you are, तस्य for him, चिन्तयतः  thinking, यत्नः effort, दुःखक्षयकरः reducer of grief, भवेत् become.

#### Explanation

"O best of monkeys it is your  responsibility to carry out this task. O Hanuman think carefully of that. Take the initiative to reduce my suffering. I am confident that you can do it."

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

स तथेति प्रतिज्ञाय मारुतिर्भीमविक्रमः।।5.39.5।। शिरसा वन्ध्य वैदेहीं गमनायोपचक्रमे।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भीमविक्रमः possessed with fierceful valour, सः मारुतिः that Maruti, तथेति so be it, प्रतिज्ञाय promised, वैदेहीम् to Vaidehi, शिरसा with his head, वन्ध्य having offered salutation, गमनाय to depart, उपचक्रमे started.

#### Explanation

Hanuman possessed with fierce valour said to Vaidehi, 'so be it' and bowed his head, and offered salutations before he departed.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

ज्ञात्वा सम्प्रस्थितं देवी वानरं मारुतात्मजम्।।5.39.6।। बाष्पगद्गदया वाचा मैथिली वाक्यमब्रवीत्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

देवी noble lady, मैथिली Mythili, मारुतात्मजम् Maruti's son, वानरम् vanara, सम्प्रस्थितम् who had started, ज्ञात्वा having realised, बाष्पगद्गदया with voice choked with tears, वाचा with speech, वाक्यम् these words, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

The noble lady, Sita, having been fully convinced about Hanuman and realising that he is about to start, her voice was choked with tears. She said these words:

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

कुशलं हनुमन्ब्रूयाः सहितौ रामलक्ष्मणौ।।5.39.7।। सुग्रीवं च सहामात्यं वृद्धान् सर्वांश्च वानरान्। ब्रूयास्त्वं वानरश्रेष्ठ कुशलं धर्मसंहितम्।।5.39.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वानरश्रेष्ठः O great vanara, हनुमन् O Hanuman, सहितौ both, रामलक्ष्मणौ Rama and Lakshmana, कुशलम् welfare, ब्रूयाः you may communicate, सहामात्यम् along with his ministers, सुग्रीवं च and Sugriva, वृद्धान् elders, सर्वान् all, वानरान् vanaras, त्वम् you, धर्मसंहितम् righteous manner, कुशलम् welfare, ब्रूयाः you may communicate

#### Explanation

"O Hanuman the great communicate my welfare  to both Rama and Lakshmana, to Sugriva, his ministers, elderly vanaras in righteous order.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

कुशलं हनुमन्ब्रूयाः सहितौ रामलक्ष्मणौ।।5.39.7।। सुग्रीवं च सहामात्यं वृद्धान् सर्वांश्च वानरान्। ब्रूयास्त्वं वानरश्रेष्ठ कुशलं धर्मसंहितम्।।5.39.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वानरश्रेष्ठः O great vanara, हनुमन् O Hanuman, सहितौ both, रामलक्ष्मणौ Rama and Lakshmana, कुशलम् welfare, ब्रूयाः you may communicate, सहामात्यम् along with his ministers, सुग्रीवं च and Sugriva, वृद्धान् elders, सर्वान् all, वानरान् vanaras, त्वम् you, धर्मसंहितम् righteous manner, कुशलम् welfare, ब्रूयाः you may communicate

#### Explanation

"O Hanuman the great communicate my welfare  to both Rama and Lakshmana, to Sugriva, his ministers, elderly vanaras in righteous order.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

यथा स च महाबाहुर्मां तारयति राघवः। अस्माद्धुःखाम्बुसंरोधात्त्वं समाधातुमर्हसि।।5.39.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाबाहुः strongarmed, सः राघवः that Rama, अस्मात् from this, दुःखाम्बुसंरोधात् relieve me from this ocean of grief, यथा that way, तारयति he helps me to cross, त्वम् you, समाधातुम् to pacify, अर्हसि you may.

#### Explanation

"You must pacify the strongarmed Rama in a manner by which he will help me to cross the ocean of grief and rlieve me.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

जीवन्तीं मां यथा रामः सम्भावयति कीर्तिमान्। तत्तथा हनुमन्वाच्यं वाचा धर्ममवाप्नुहि।।5.39.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हनुमान् Hanuman, कीर्तिमान् illustrious one, रामः Rama, माम् me, जीवन्तीम् while I am alove, यथा in such way, सम्भावयति will take me, तत् that, तथा that way, वाच्यम् words, वाचा tell, धर्मम् righteous, अवाप्नुहि you will acquire.

#### Explanation

"Let the illustrious Rama take me alive. Help me with your (persuasive) words and you will acquire merit.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

नित्यमुत्साहयुक्ताश्च वाचश्रुत्वा त्वयेरिताः। वर्धिष्यते दाशरथेः पौरुषं मदवाप्तये।।5.39.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्वया you, ईरिताः spoken, उत्साहयुक्ताः full of determination for action, वाचः words, नित्यम् ever, श्रुत्वा after hearing, मदवाप्तये for acquiring me, दाशरथेः son of Dasaratha, पौरुषम् manliness, वर्धिष्यते will increase.

#### Explanation

"On hearing your ever spirited words his manliness to get me back will be charged.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

मत्सन्देशयुता वाचस्त्वत्तश्श्रुत्वा च राघवः। पराक्रमविधिं वीरो विधिवत्संविधास्यति।।5.39.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वीरः hero, राघवः Rama, त्वत्तः from you, मत्सन्देशयुताः with repetition of my message, वाचः words, श्रुत्वा after hearing, विधिवत् arrange duly, पराक्रमविधम् following his valour, संविधास्यति will make efforts.

#### Explanation

None

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

सीताया वचनं श्रुत्वा हनुमान्मारुतात्मजः। शिरस्यञ्जलिमाधाय वाक्यमुत्तरमब्रवीत्।।5.39.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मारुतात्मजः son of Maruta, हनुमान् Hanuman, सीतायाः Sita's, वचनम् words, श्रुत्वा after listening, शिरसि on his head, अञ्जलिम् folded palms, आधाय placed, उत्तरम् replied, वाक्यम् these words, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

"Having heard Sita's words, Hanuman, son of Maruta, placed his folded palms over his head in reverence and replied:

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

क्षिप्रमेष्यति काकुत्स्थो हार्यृक्षप्रवरैर्वृतः। यस्ते युधि विजित्यारीन्शोकं व्यपनयिष्यति।।5.39.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

काकुत्स्थ: Kakutstha, हर्यृक्षप्रवरैः with vanaras and bears, क्षिप्रम् at once, एष्यति he will hasten, यः he, युधि in war, अरीन् enemies, विजित्य winning over, ते your, शोकम् grief, व्यपनयिष्यति will relieve.

#### Explanation

"Kakutstha Rama, accompanied by vanaras and bears will reach here soon and conquer the enemies in war and relieve your grief.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

न हि पश्यामि मर्त्येषु नासुरेषु सुरेषु वा। यस्तस्य क्षिपतो बाणान्स्थातुमुत्सहतेऽग्रतः।।5.39.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

यः when he, बाणान् arrows, क्षिपतः while he releases, तस्य  his, अग्रतः in front, स्थातुम् to stand, उत्सहते will have, मर्त्येषु among humans, नासुरेषु or among demons, सुरेषु वा or among gods, न हि पश्यामि I do not see.

#### Explanation

"I do not see any one among humans or demons or even among gods who can face his arrows when released.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

अप्यर्कमपि पर्जन्यमपि वैवस्वतं यमम्। स हि सोढुं रणे शक्तस्तव हेतोर्विशेषतः।।5.39.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः he, रणे in war, अर्कमपि even the Sun, पर्जन्यमपि or the raingod, वैवस्वतम् son of the Sun, यमम् Yama the lord of death, सोढुम् to hear, शक्तः capable, तव हेतोः for you, विशेषतः specially.

#### Explanation

"Indeed, Rama is capable of conquering even the Sungod, or the raingod or Yama (the son of the Sun) specially for your sake.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

स हि सागरपर्यन्तां महीं शासितुमीहति। त्वन्निमित्तो हि रामस्य जयो जनकनन्दिनि।।5.39.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः he, सागरपर्यन्ताम् surrounded by the entire ocean, महीम् earth, शासितुम् to rule, अर्हति हि is fit, जनकनन्दिनि O Janaka's delight, रामस्य Rama's, जयः victory, त्वन्निमित्तो हि for your sake only.

#### Explanation

"Rama is fit to rule the entire earth surrounded by the ocean. O delight of Janaka he will succeed in serving your cause."

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

तस्य तद्वचनं श्रुत्वा सम्यक्सत्यं सुभाषितम्। जानकी बहुमेनेऽथ वचनं चेदमब्रवीत्।।5.39.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

जानकी Janaki, तस्य his, सत्यम् truth, सम्यक् proper, सुभाषितम् good words, तत् that, वचनम् words, श्रुत्वा on hearing, बहुमेने held in great esteem, अथ then, इदम् thus, वचनं च and the word, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

On hearing the truthful, proper, pleasing words by Hanuman whom she held in great esteem, Sita said this:

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

ततस्तं प्रस्थितं सीता वीक्षमाणा पुनः पुनः। भर्तृस्नेहान्वितं वाक्यं सौहार्दादनुमानयत्।।5.39.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, सीता Sita, प्रस्थितम् ready to start, तम् him, पुनः पुनः again and again, वीक्षमाणा looking at, भर्तृस्नेहान्वितम् conveying her love to Rama, वाक्यम् word, सौहार्दात् goodhearted, अनुमानयत् she made him understand.

#### Explanation

Sita again and again looked at Hanuman who was about to start. She made him understand her words conveying her love for Rama.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

यदि वा मन्यसे वीर वसैकाहमरिन्दम। कस्मिंश्चित्संवृते देशे विश्रान्तःश्वो गमिष्यसि।।5.39.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अरिन्दम O destroyer of enemies, वीर hero, मन्यसे यदि if you  think so, कस्मिंश्चित् some where, संवृते in a closed space, देशे in location, एकाहम् for one day, वस you may stay, विश्रान्तः rest, श्वः tomorrow, गमिष्यसि you may go.

#### Explanation

"O destroyer of enemies if you think of halting for a day you may do so in a solitary location and leave tomorrow.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

मम चेदल्पभाग्याया सान्निध्यात्तव वानर। अस्य शोकस्य महतो मुहूर्तं मोक्षणं भवेत्।।5.39.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वानर vanara, तव your, सान्निध्यात् by the presence, अल्पभाग्यायाः of an unfortunate woman, मम to me, महतः great, अस्य शोकस्य from the suffering, मुहूर्तम् for a short while, मोक्षणम् relief, भवेत् चेत् will be.

#### Explanation

"Your presence will relieve me of my suffering, if you stay here for a while.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

गते हि हरिशार्दूल पुनरागमनाय तु। प्राणानामपि सन्देहो मम स्यान्नात्र संशयः।।5.39.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हरिशार्दूल O tiger among vanaras, पुनरागमनाय for your visit once again, गते when you go, मम for me, प्राणानामपि and for my life, सन्देहः doubt, स्यात् will be, अत्र here, सन्देहः doubt, न no.

#### Explanation

"O tiger among vanaras if you leave this place to come again, my life will be uncertain, (if your coming is uncertain). There is no doubt about it.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

तवादर्शनजः शोको भूयो मां परितापयेत्। दुःखाद्धुःखपरामृष्टां दीपयन्निव वानर।।5.39.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वानर vanara, दुःखात् agonised, दुःखपरामृष्टाम् agony increased further, माम् me, तव your, अदर्शनजः not seeing you, शोकः sorrow, दीपयन्निव as if inflamming my agony, भूयः again, परितापयेत् it will trouble me.

#### Explanation

"My agony would be increased further by not seeing you. It would be inflamming my agony and I would be even more pained.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

अयं च वीर सन्देहस्तिष्ठतीव ममाग्रतः। सुमहांस्त्वत्सहायेषु हर्यृक्षेषु हरीश्वरः।।5.39.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वीर hero, हरीश्वर chief of vanaras, त्वत्सहायेषु your helpless, हर्यृक्षेषु among vanaras and bears, अयम् this, सुमहान् great, सन्देहः doubtful, मम अग्रतः in front of me, तिष्ठतीव dwells in my mind.

#### Explanation

"O heroic chief of vanaras I am doubtful as to whether vanaras and bears can extend help?

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

कथं नु खलु दुष्पारं तरिष्यन्ति महोदधिम्। तानि हर्यृक्षसैन्यानि तौ वा नरवरात्मजौ।।5.39.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तानि those, हर्यृक्षसैन्यानि army of vanaras and bears, तौ they both, Rama and Lakshmana, नरवरात्मजौ princes, दुष्पारम् difficult to cross, महोदधिम्  ocean, कथं नु how indeed, तरिष्यन्ति they can cross.

#### Explanation

"How can the army of vanaras and bears, for that matter, princes Rama and Lakshmana negotiate the ocean, which is difficult to cross?

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

त्रयाणामेव भूतानां सागरस्यास्य लङ्घने। शक्तिस्स्याद्वैनतेयस्य तव वा मारुतस्य वा।।5.39.26।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अस्य of this, सागरस्य ocean's, लङ्घने to leap, वैनतेयस्य वा for Bainateya (Garuda) or, तव वा or you, मारुतस्य or Windgod वा or, त्रयाणाम् for these three, भूतानाम् living beings, शक्तिः energy, स्यात् have.

#### Explanation

"It is only three beings who have the capacity to leap over this ocean. Garuda, or you or the Windgod.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

तदस्मिन्कार्यनिर्योगे वीरैवं दुरतिक्रमे। किं पश्यसि समाधानं त्वं हि कार्यविदां वरः।।5.39.27।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वीर hero, तत् such, एवम् in that way, दुरतिक्रमे in a very difficult task, अस्मिन् in this, कार्यनिर्योगे the task, किम् what, समाधानम् means, पश्यसि you foresee, त्वम् you, कार्यविदाम् among those who are capable, वरः हि foremost.

#### Explanation

"What means do you, being the foremost hero among the three who are capable performing this difficult task, foresee?

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

काममस्य त्वमेवैकः कार्यस्य परिसाधने। पर्याप्तः परवीरघ्न यशस्यस्ते फलोदयः।।5.39.28।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

परवीरघ्न slayer of enemy heroes, अस्य कार्यस्य this task, परिसाधने to accomplish, त्वम् you, एक एव alone are, पर्याप्तः कामम् are competent, ते to you, फलोदयः fruit, यशस्यः of the accomplishment.

#### Explanation

"O slayer of enemy heroes if you alone can accomplish this task the fame of doing this will be yours.

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

बलैस्समग्रैर्यदि मां रावणं जित्य संयुगे। विजयी स्वपुरं यायात्तत्तस्य सदृशं भवेत्।।5.39.29।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

संयुगे in war, समग्रैः with  the entire, बलैः with army, रावणम् Ravana, जित्य after winning, माम् me, विजयी victorious , स्वपुरम् his own city, यायात् यदि if he can reach, तत् that, तस्य his, सदृशम् worthy of him, भवेत् will be.

#### Explanation

"It would be worthy of Rama if he with his entire army emerges victorious over Ravana in a battle and takes me to his city.

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

शरैस्तु सङ्कुलां कृत्वा लङ्कां परबलार्दनः। मां नयेद्यदि काकुत्स्थः तत्तस्य सदृशं भवेत्।।5.39.30।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

परबलार्दनः slayer of enemy army, काकुत्स्थः Kakutstha, लङ्काम् Lanka, शरैः with arrows, सङ्कुलाम् an agitated place, कृत्वा does, माम् me, नयेद्यदि if he takes, तत् that, तस्य his action, सदृशम् agreeable, भवेत् will be.

#### Explanation

"If Kakutstha (Rama), slayer of the enemy army fills the entire Lanka with his arrows and takes me, then his action will be worthy of him.

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

तद्यथा तस्य विक्रान्तमनुरूपं महात्मनः। भवेदाहवशूरस्य तथा त्वमुपपादय।।5.39.31।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तत् that, महात्मनः of the great self, आहवशूरस्य of the hero in war, तस्य his, अनुरूपम् worthy of him, विक्रान्तम् victorious, यथा in such a way, भवेत् will be, तथा in that way, त्वम् you, उपपादय you may make arrangement.

#### Explanation

"You may make arrangement in such a manner that, that great self, the hero in war makes his victorious march which is worthy of him."

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

तदर्थोपहितं वाक्यं प्रश्रितं हेतुसंहितम्। निशम्य हनुमान्शेषं वाक्यमुत्तरमब्रवीत्।।5.39.32।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हनुमान् Hanuman, अर्थोपहितम् meaningful, प्रश्रितम् courteous, हेतुसंहितम् logical, तत् those, वाक्यम् words, निशम्य having heard, शेषम् the rest, वाक्यम् word, अब्रवीत् said, उत्तरं in reply.

#### Explanation

Having heard the meaningful, courteous and logical words of Sita, Hanuman replied:

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

देवि हर्यृक्षसैन्यानामीश्वरः प्लवतां वरः। सुग्रीवस्सत्त्वसम्पन्नस्तवार्थे कृतनिश्चयः।।5.39.33।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

देवि Devi, हर्यृक्षसैन्यानाम् of the army of vanaras and bears, ईश्वरः lord, प्लवतां वरः foremost among monkeys, सत्त्वसम्पन्नः richly endowed with satvaguna, सुग्रीवः Sugriva, तव अर्धे for your sake, कृतनिश्चयः detemined to accomplish.

#### Explanation

"O Devi the lord of monkeys, Sugriva heads the army of vanaras and bears. He is richly endowed with satvaguna and is determined to accomplish your cause and free you from sorrow.

---

### Shloka 34

#### Shloka Text

स वानरसहस्राणां कोटीभिरभिसंवृतः। क्षिप्रमेष्यति वैदेहि राक्षसानां निबर्हणः।।5.39.34।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वैदेहि Vaidehi, सः he, the Sugriva, वानरसहस्राणाम् of thousands of vanaras, कोटीभिः with crores of them, अभिसंवृतः is surrounded, राक्षसानाम् of all the ogres, निबर्हणः will destroy, क्षिप्रम् swiftly, एष्यति he will reach.

#### Explanation

"O Vaidehi Sugriva will reach this place with thousands of crores of vanaras very soon and would kill all the ogres.

---

### Shloka 35

#### Shloka Text

तस्य विक्रमसम्पन्नास्सत्त्ववन्तो महाबलाः। मनस्सङ्कल्पसम्पाता निदेशे हरयः स्थिताः।।5.39.35।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विक्रमसम्पन्नाः powerful, सत्त्ववन्तः virtuous, महाबलाः mighty, मनस्सङ्कल्पसम्पाताः who can leap with the speed of mind, हरयः vanaras, तस्य his, निदेशे at his command, स्थिताः awaiting.

#### Explanation

"Valiant, virtuous and powerful vanaras who can leap with the speed of mind are awaiting orders (of Sugriva).

---

### Shloka 36

#### Shloka Text

येषां नोपरि नाधस्तान्न तिर्यक्सज्जते गतिः। न च कर्मसु सीदन्ति महत्स्वमिततेजसः।।5.39.36।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

येषाम् for those monkeys, गतिः movement, उपरि upward, न सज्जते  do not get impeded, अधस्तात्  downward, न not, तिर्यक्  horizontally, न not, अमिततेजसः very brilliant ones, महत्सु any kind, कर्मसु of task, न सीदन्ति do not care

#### Explanation

"Their course cannot be impeded either upward, or downward or right or left. They  are indeed very brilliant and would never fail in any task.

---

### Shloka 37

#### Shloka Text

असकृत्तैर्महोत्साहैस्ससागरधराधरा। प्रदक्षिणीकृता भूमिर्वायुमार्गानुसारिभिः।।5.39.37।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महोत्साहैः by the enthusiastic, वायुमार्गानुसारिभिः following aerial path, तैः by them, असकृत् repeatedly, ससागरधराधरा with the oceans and mountains, भूमिः earth, प्रदक्षिणीकृता has been circumambulated.

#### Explanation

"They have often circumambulated the earth including its oceans and mountains through the aerial path with great enthusiasm.

---

### Shloka 38

#### Shloka Text

मद्विशिष्टाश्च तुल्याश्च सन्ति तत्र वनौकसः। मत्तः प्रत्यवरः कश्चिन्नास्ति सुग्रीवसन्निधौ।।5.39.38।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तत्र there, मद्विशिष्टाश्च more powerful than me, तुल्याश्च those equal to me, वनौकसः vanaras who move in the forest, सन्ति are, मत्तः to me, प्रत्यवरः inferior, कश्चित् not even one, सुग्रीवसन्निधौ under Sugriva, नास्ति not there.

#### Explanation

"The vanaras under Sugriva who move in the forest are more powerful than me or equal to me. None is inferior to me.

---

### Shloka 39

#### Shloka Text

अहं तावदिह प्राप्तः किं पुनस्ते महाबलाः। न हि प्रकृष्टाः प्रेष्यन्ते प्रेष्यन्ते हीतरे जनाः।।5.39.39।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहं तावत् I have, इह here, अनुप्राप्तः reached, महाबलाः very powerful, ते to you, किं पुनः why not others, प्रकृष्टाः the best ones, न प्रेष्यन्ते हि will not be sent, इतरे others, जनाः ordinary ones, प्रेष्यन्ते हि will be sent.

#### Explanation

"If I have managed to come here, why not the other powerful ones? The superior leaders are not sent (as messengers). Only ordinary ones like me are.

---

### Shloka 40

#### Shloka Text

तदलं परितापेन देवि शोको व्यपैतु ते। एकोत्पातेन ते लङ्कामेष्यन्ति हरियूथपाः।।5.39.40।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

देवि noble lady, तत् therefore, परितापेन with lamentation, अलम् enough, ते your, शोकः agony, व्यपैतु may be given up, ते those, हरियूथपाः monkey troops, एकोत्पातेन in one stride, लङ्काम् to Lanka, एष्यन्ति will reach.

#### Explanation

"O noble lady therefore, give up your agony. Enough of your lamentation. The monkey troops will reach this place in one stride.

---

### Shloka 41

#### Shloka Text

मम पृष्ठगतौ तौ च चन्द्रसूर्याविवोदितौ। त्वत्सकाशं महासत्त्वौ नृसिंहावागमिष्यतः।।5.39.41।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महासत्त्वौ both great men, नृसिंहौ both lions among men, तौ च and both, मम my, पृष्ठगतौ on my back, उदितौ just risen, चन्द्रसूर्याविव like Sun and Moon, त्वत्सकाशम् to your presence, आगमिष्यतः will come.

#### Explanation

"Both the valiant Rama and Lakshmana, lions among men, looking brilliant like Sun and Moon will come to you riding on my back.

---

### Shloka 42

#### Shloka Text

ततो वीरौ नरवरौ सहितौ रामलक्ष्मणौ। आगम्य नगरीं लङ्कां सायकैर्विधमिष्यतः।।5.39.42।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, वीरौ two heroes, नरवरौ both the princes, रामलक्ष्मणौ Rama and Lakshmana, सहितौ together, आगम्य on coming, लङ्कां नगरीम् to the city of Lanka, सायकैः with their arrows, विधमिष्यतः will blow away.

#### Explanation

"Both the heroes, Rama and Lakshmana will come and blow up the city of Lanka with their unfailing arrows.

---

### Shloka 43

#### Shloka Text

सगणं रावणं हत्त्वा राघवो रघुनन्दनः। त्वामादाय वरारोहे स्वपुरीं प्रतियास्यति।।5.39.43।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वरारोहे O beautiful lady, रघुनन्दनः the delight of the Raghu race, राघवः Rama, सगणम् along with his kins, रावणम् Ravana, हत्त्वा on killing, त्वाम् you, आदाय after taking you, स्वपुरीम् their city, प्रतियास्यति would go back.

#### Explanation

"O beautiful lady Raghava, the delight of the Raghu race would kill Ravana and his kins and return with you to his city৷৷

---

### Shloka 44

#### Shloka Text

तदाश्वसिहि भद्रं ते भव त्वं कालकाङ्क्षिणी। नचिराद्द्रक्ष्यसे रामं प्रज्वलन्तमिवानलम्।।5.39.44।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तत् therefore, आश्वसिहि rest assured, ते भद्रम् let it be auspicious to you, त्वम् you, कालकाङ्क्षिणी count your time, भव be, प्रज्वलन्तम् burning, अनलम् इव like the fire, रामम् Rama, नचिरात् shortly, द्रक्ष्यसे would see.

#### Explanation

"Therefore, count the days and rest assured. Let it be auspicious to you. You will shortly see Rama burning like fire.

---

### Shloka 45

#### Shloka Text

निहते राक्षसेन्द्रेऽस्मिन्सपुत्रामात्यबान्धवे। त्वं समेष्वसि रामेण शशाङ्केनेव रोहिणी।।5.39.45।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सपुत्रामात्यबान्धवे along with his sons, ministers and relatives, अस्मिन् when, राक्षसेन्द्रे the king of demons, निहते killed, त्वम् you, रोहिणी Rohini, शशाङ्केनेव like the Moon, रामेण with Rama, समेष्यसि you will reunite.

#### Explanation

"When Ravana is slain, with his sons, ministers and kith and kin, you will reunite with Rama as Rohini unites with the Moon.

---

### Shloka 46

#### Shloka Text

क्षिप्रं त्वं देवि शोकस्य पारं यास्यसि मैथिलि। रावणं चैव रामेण निहतं द्रक्ष्यसेऽचिरात्।।5.39.46।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

देवि queen, मैथिलि Mythili, त्वम् you, क्षिप्रम् soon, शोकस्य grief, पारम् to the other shore, यास्यसि will go, अचिरात् very shortly, रामेण by Rama, रावणम् Ravana, निहतं slain, द्रक्ष्यसे चैव you will see.

#### Explanation

"O queen Mythili you come to the end of your sorrow and will soon see Ravana killed by Rama".

---

### Shloka 47

#### Shloka Text

एवमाश्वास्य वैदेहीं हनुमान्मारुतात्मजः। गमनाय मतिं कृत्वा वैदेहीं पुनरब्रवीत्।।5.39.47।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मारुतात्मजः son of the Windgod, हनुमान् Hanuman, वैदेहीम् to Vaidehi, एवम् in that manner, आश्वास्य having consoled, गमनाय to depart, मतिम् thought, कृत्वा after resolving, पुनः again, वैदेहीम् to Vaidehi, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

Having thus consoled Vaidehi, Hanuman, son of the Windgod resolved to depart. Once again he spoke to Vadehi:

---

### Shloka 48

#### Shloka Text

तमरिघ्नं कृतात्मानं क्षिप्रं द्रक्ष्यसि राघवम्। लक्ष्मणं च धनुष्पाणिं लङ्काद्वारमुपागतम्।।5.39.48।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लङ्काद्वारम् at the entrance of Lanka, उपागतम् reached, अरिघ्नम् slayer of foes, कृतात्मानम् an accomplished man, तं राघवम् that Rama, धनुष्पाणिम् wielder of bow, लक्ष्मणं च and Lakshmana, क्षिप्रम् very soon, द्रक्ष्यसि you will see.

#### Explanation

"Very soon you would see the slayer of foes, who is an accomplished wielder of the bow (Rama) along with Lakshmana at the entrance of Lanka.

---

### Shloka 49

#### Shloka Text

नखदंष्ट्रायुधान्वीरान्सिम्हशार्दूलविक्रमान्। वानरान्वारणेन्द्राभान्क्षिप्रं द्रक्ष्यसि सङ्गतान्।।5.39.49।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नखदंष्ट्रायुधान् whose nails and teeth are their weapons, वीरान् heroes, सिंहशार्दूलविक्रमान् resembling lion and tiger in valour, वारणेन्द्राभान् who resemble wellbred elephants, सङ्गतान् reach, वानरान् vanaras, क्षिप्रम् soon, द्रक्ष्यसि you will see.

#### Explanation

"You will soon see heroic vanaras whose weapons are their teeth, who are like lion and tiger in their valour. They are (mighty) like wellbred elephants.

---

### Shloka 50

#### Shloka Text

शैलाम्बुदनिकाशानां लङ्कामलयसानुषु। नर्दतां कपिमुख्यानामार्ये यूधान्यनेकशः।।5.39.50।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

आर्ये respectable lady, शैलाम्बुदनिकाशानाम् whose body is like clouds on mountain, नर्दताम् of the roaring, कपिमुख्यानाम् of the chiefs of vanaras, अनेकशः  in many ways, यूधानि troops, लङ्कामलयसानुषु on the  mountain of Lanka.

#### Explanation

"O respectable lady you would see many troops of vanara chiefs who resemble clouds hovering on the mountain peaks of Lankaroaring.

---

### Shloka 51

#### Shloka Text

स तु मर्मणि घोरेण ताडितो मन्मथेषुणा। न शर्म लभते रामस्सिंहार्धित इव द्विपः।।5.39.51।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः रामः that Rama, घोरेण by the dreadful, मन्मथेषुणा arrows of god of love, मर्मणि pierced into vitals, ताडितः hit, सिंहार्दितः hit by a lion, द्विपः इव like an elephant, न शर्म लभते is not happy

#### Explanation

"Rama is tormented by the dreadful arrows of the god of love pierced into vitals, like an elephant hit by a lion. He has no happiness in life.

---

### Shloka 52

#### Shloka Text

मा रुदो देवि शोकेन माभूत्ते मनसोऽप्रियम्। शचीव पत्या शक्रेण भर्त्रा नाथवती ह्यसि।।5.39.52।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

देवि devi, शोकेन with sorrow, मा रुदः do not cry, ते your, मनसः in mind, अप्रियम् unpleasant, माभूत् no thought be entertained, पत्या with a husband शक्रेण to Indra, शचीव like Sachi, भर्त्रा by your husband, नाथवती you have a great husband, असि हि you are.

#### Explanation

"O devi weep no more.Entertain no unpleasant thoughts.You have a great husband, just as Sachi has her lord Indra.

---

### Shloka 53

#### Shloka Text

रामाद्विशिष्टः कोऽन्योऽस्ति कश्चित्सौमित्रिणा समः। अग्निमारुतकल्पौ तौ भ्रातरौ तव संश्रयौ।।5.39.53।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामात् than Rama, विशिष्टः more distinguished, अन्यः others, कः who is there, अस्ति is, सौमित्रिणा to Lakshmana, समः equal, कश्चित् who is there, अग्निमारुतकल्पौ equal to fire and wind, तौ भ्रातरौ both brothers, तव your, संश्रयौ they are your refuge.

#### Explanation

"Who is superior to Rama? Who is equal to Lakshmana? Both the brothers are like Fire and Wind. They are your refuge.

---

### Shloka 54

#### Shloka Text

नास्मिंश्चिरं वत्स्यसि देवि देशे रक्षोगणैरध्युषितेऽतिरौद्रे। न ते चिरादामगमनं प्रियस्य क्षमस्व मत्सङ्गमकालमात्रम्।।5.39.54।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

देवि noble lady, रक्षोगणैः by the hosts of ogres, अध्युषिते in a stronghold, अतिरौद्रे in a dreadful place, अस्मिन् देशे country, चिरम् not very long, न वत्स्यसि will not stay, ते प्रियस्य with your beloved, आगमनम् coming, न चिरात् not very long, मत्सङ्गमकालमात्रम् for this period, क्षमस्व pardon me.

#### Explanation

"O noble lady You will not stay longer in this country, which is a strong hold of hosts of demons and a dreadful place It will not be very long before you unite with your beloved. Pray, have  patience till then. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये सुन्दरकाण्डे एकोनचत्वारिंशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the thirtyninth sarga of Sundarakanda of the holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 40

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

श्रुत्वा तु वचनं तस्य वायुसूनोर्महात्मनः। उवाचात्महितं वाक्यं सीता सुरसुतोपमा।।5.40.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महात्मनः great soul, तस्य his, वायुसूनोः son of the Windgod, वचनम् words, श्रुत्वा having heard, सुरसुतोपमा like the daughter of gods, सीता Sita, आत्महितम् for her benefit, वाक्यम् words, उवाच spoke.

#### Explanation

Sita who was like the daughter of the gods heard the son of the Windgod for her benefit and replied:

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

त्वां दृष्ट्वा प्रियवक्तारं सम्प्रहृष्यामि वानरः। अर्धसञ्जातसस्येव वृष्टिं प्राप्य वसुन्धरा।।5.40.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वानर vanara, प्रियवक्तारम् who has been speaking pleasing words, त्वाम् you दृष्ट्वा after seeing, अर्धसञ्जातसस्या as half grown, वसुन्धरा earth, वृष्टिम् rain, प्राप्येव as if begotten, सम्प्रहृष्यामि I feel happy.

#### Explanation

"O vanara just as the earth feels happy when the half grown crops receive rain, I feel happy after seeing you. You have been speaking pleasing words

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

यथा तं पुरुषव्याघ्रं गात्रैश्शोकाभिकर्शितैः। संस्पृशेयं सकामाहं तथा कुरु दयां मयि।।5.40.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सकामम् a passionate lady, अहम् I, शोकाभिकर्शितैः emaciated with suffering, गात्रैः with limbs, तं पुरुषव्याघ्रम् him that tiger among men, यथा as, संस्पृशेयम् I may touch, तथा in that way, मयि in me, दयाम् kindness, कुरु do.

#### Explanation

"Be kind to me and act in such a manner that I may embrace my lord passionately with my body emaciated with grief.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

अभिज्ञानं च रामस्य दद्या हरिगणोत्तम। क्षिप्तामिषीकां काकस्य कोपादेकाक्षिशातनीम्।।5.40.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हरिगणोत्तम best leader of the vanaras, कोपात् with anger, क्षिप्ताम् released, काकस्य crow's, एकाक्षिशातनीम् blinding one eye, इषीकाम् by a blade of grass, रामस्य to Rama, अभिज्ञानम् as a token, दद्याः give.

#### Explanation

"O best leader of vanaras remind Rama about the incident of the release of a blade of grass in anger blinding one of the eyes of the crow narrated to you.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

मनश्शिलायास्तिलको गण्डपार्श्वे निवेशितः। त्वया प्रणष्टे तिलके तं किल स्मर्तुमर्हसि।।5.40.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तिलके when tilaka mark, प्रणष्टे got erased, त्वया by you, मनश्शिलायाः with a grind stone, तिलकः tilaka (mark on forehead), गण्डपार्श्वे stone near by, निवेशितः किल painted indeed, तम् that tilaka, स्मर्तुम् to remember, अर्हसि you will.

#### Explanation

'(Further remind him), 'When my tilaka mark was erased, you painted tilaka with a stone (manassita pigment) nearby. O Rama you should remember that (incident).

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

स वीर्यवान्कथं सीतां हृतां समनुमन्यसे। वसन्तीं रक्षसां मध्ये महेन्द्रवरुणोपमः।।5.40.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वीर्यवान् valiant, महेन्द्रवरुणोपमः comparable to Varuna and Indra, सः Rama, हृताम् seized, रक्षसाम् of rakshasas, मध्ये in the midst of, वसन्तीम् dwelling, सीताम् Sita, कथम् how, समनुमन्यसे he will agree.

#### Explanation

'Rama you are like Indra and Varuna in valiance. How are you tolerating Sita seized, dwelling among the demons?

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

एष चूडामणिर्दिव्यो मया सुपरिरक्षितः। एतं दृष्ट्वा प्रहृष्यामि व्यसने त्वामिवानघ।।5.40.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अनघ sinless, दिव्यः wonderful, एषः this, चूडामणिः Chudamani, मया of mine, सुपरिरक्षितः carefully safe guared, व्यसने in distress, एतम् this one, दृष्ट्वा seeing, त्वामिव like you here, प्रहृष्यामि look at it.

#### Explanation

"O sinless Rama this wonderful Chudamani of mine has been carefully safeguarded. Seeing this is like seeing you when I am in distress.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

एष निर्यातितश्श्रीमान्मया ते वारिसम्भवः। अतः परं न शक्ष्यामि जीवितुं शोकलालसा।।5.40.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

श्रीमान् glorious one, वारिसम्भवः born of the sea, एषः this, ते to you, निर्यातितः is returned, शोकलालसा engrossed in grief, अतः परम् beyond this, जीवितुम् to live, न शक्ष्यामि it is not possible for me.

#### Explanation

"I am sending you this (Chudamani) born of the sea. O glorious Rama and now engrossed in grief it is not possible for me to live.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

असह्यानि च दुःखानि वाचश्च हृदयच्छिदः। राक्षसीनां सुघोराणां त्वत्कृते मर्षयाम्यहम्।।5.40.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I, असह्यानि unbearable, दुःखानि grief, सुघोराणाम् of dreadful ones, राक्षसीनाम् of ogresses, हृदयच्छिदः piercing my heart, वाचश्च utterings, त्वत्कृते your sake, मर्षयामि I am enduring.

#### Explanation

"I am tolerating this unbearable grief, and the words of the dreadful ogresses piercing my heart. Only for you.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text
