धारयिष्यामि मासं तु जीवितं शत्रुसूदन। ऊर्ध्वं मासान्न जीविष्ये त्वया हीना नृपात्मज।।5.40.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शत्रुसूदन O subduer of enemies, नृपात्मज O prince, मासं तु for one month only, जीवितम् life, धारयिष्यामि will survive, त्वया you, हीना separated, मासात् for one month only, ऊर्ध्वम् beyond that, न जीविष्ये will not live.

#### Explanation

"O subduer of enemies O prince I will hold on to life only for a month. I will not survive beyond a month in your absence.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

घोरो राक्षसराजोऽयं दृष्टिश्च न सुखा मयि। त्वां च श्रुत्वा विपद्यन्तं न जीवेयमहं क्षणम्।।5.40.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अयम् this, राक्षसराजः demon king, घोरः fearsome, मयि at me, दृष्टिः look, सुखा moral, न no, त्वाम् you, विपद्यन्तम् facing calamities, श्रुत्वा hearing, अहम् I, क्षणम् even a moment, न जीवेयम् I will not survive.

#### Explanation

"The demon king is frightful. He looks at me with immoral (lusty) eyes. I do not wish to live even for a moment after hearing about the adversities (you have faced)."

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

वैदेह्या वचनं श्रुत्वा करुणं साश्रु भाषितम्। अथाब्रवीन्महातेजा हनुमान्मारुतात्मजः।।5.40.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथ then, महातेजाः a brilliant one, मारुतात्मजः son of the Windgod, हनुमान् Hanuman, साश्रु weeping, भाषितम् words spoken, करुणम् piteously, वैदेह्याः Vaidehi's, वचनम् words, श्रुत्वा on hearing, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

Hearing the words of Sita who was weeping piteously, brilliant Hanuman said this:

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

त्वच्छोकविमुखो रामो देवि सत्येन ते शपे। रामे दुःखाभिभूते तु लक्ष्मणः परितप्यते।।5.40.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

देवि O irreproachable lady, रामः Rama, त्वच्छोकविमुखः out of grief he is not showing any  interest in life, सत्येन truly, ते to you, शपे I promise, रामे when Rama, दुःखाभिभूते he is in grief, लक्ष्मणः च even Lakshmana, परितप्यते is immersed in sorrow.

#### Explanation

"O irreproachable lady I swear by you that Rama has grown averse to everything out of grief of separation from you. When Rama is in grief even Lakshmana gets immersed in sorrow.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

कथञ्चिद्भवती दृष्टा न कालः परिशोचितुम्। इमं मुहूर्तं दुःखानामन्तं द्रक्ष्यसि भामिनि।।5.40.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कथञ्चित् somehow, भवती you, दृष्टा seen, परिशोचितम् to worry, कालः time, न not, भामिनि a beautiful lady, इमम् this, मुहूर्तम् moment, दुःखानाम् for grief, अन्तम् end, द्रक्ष्यसि you can see.

#### Explanation

"O beautiful lady somehow I could meet you. This is not the time to worry about. You will see the end of suffering at this very moment.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

तावुभौ पुरुषव्याघ्रौ राजपुत्रावरिन्दमौ। त्वद्दर्शनकृतोत्साहौ लङ्कां भस्मीकरिष्यतः।।5.40.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पुरुषव्याघ्रौ tigers among men, अरिन्दमौ crusher of enemies, त्वद्दर्शनकृतोत्साहौ very determined to see you, उभौ both, तौ राजपुत्रौ both the princes, लङ्काम् Lanka, भस्मीकरिष्यतः will reduce to ashes.

#### Explanation

"Both the princes are tigers among men and crushers of enemies. They are determined to see you, and will reduce Lanka to ashes.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

हत्त्वा च समरे क्रूरं रावणं सहबान्धवम्। राघवौ त्वां विशालाक्षि स्वां पुरीं प्रापयिष्यतः।।5.40.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विशालाक्षि largeeyed one, राघवः Rama, समरे in war, सहबान्धवम् along with relatives, क्रूरम् vile, रावणम् Ravana, हत्वा after killing, स्वां पुरीं प्रति to his own city, त्वाम् you, प्रापयिष्यतः will take you back.

#### Explanation

"O largeeyed lady Killing in war the vile Ravana, the demon king and all his relatives, Rama would take you back to his own city৷৷

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

यत्तु रामो विजानीयादभिज्ञानमनिन्दिते। प्रीतिसञ्जननं तस्य भूयस्त्वं दातुमर्हसि।।5.40.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अनिन्दिते blameless lady, रामः Rama, यत् that which, अभिज्ञानम् knows, विजानीयात् to identify you, तस्य your, प्रीतिसञ्जननम् that which makes him more happy, भूयः one more, त्वम् you, दातुम् may give, अर्हसि is proper.

#### Explanation

"O unimpeachable lady give one more identification which would make him more happy".

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

साब्रवीद्दत्तमेवेति मयाभिज्ञानमुत्तमम्। एतदेव हि रामस्य दृष्ट्वा मत्केशभूषणम्।।5.40.18।। श्रद्धेयं हनुमन्वाक्यं तव वीर भविष्यति।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मया mine, उत्तमम् best, अभिज्ञानम् identification, दत्तमेव already given, इति this, सा she, अब्रवीत् said, वीर hero, हनुमान् Hanuman, एतत् all this, मत्केशभूषणम् my jewel for the hair, दृष्ट्वा seeing, तव his, वाक्यम् words, रामस्य Rama's, श्रद्धेयम् what you tell, भविष्यति will believe.

#### Explanation

"I have already given the best identification. When you hand this jewel used on my hair to him,  he will believe what you tell him".

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

स तं मणिवरं गृह्य श्रीमान्प्लवगसत्तमः।।5.40.19।। प्रणम्य शिरसा देवीं गमनायोपचक्रमे।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

श्रीमान् illustrious one, सः he, प्लवगसत्तमः best of vanaras, मणिवरम् best jewel, गृह्य holding, देवीम् to Sita, शिरसा by his head, प्रणम्य prostrated, गमनाय to depart, उपचक्रमे started.

#### Explanation

The illustrious vanara held the ornament, prostrated to Sita and prepared to depart.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

तमुत्पातकृतोत्साहमवेक्ष्य हरिपुङ्गवम्।।5.40.20।। वर्धमानं महावेगमुवाच जनकात्मजा। अश्रुपूर्णमुखी दीना बाष्पगद्गदया गिरा।।5.40.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

जनकात्मजा Janaka's daughter, उत्पातकृतोत्साहम् risen up eager to leap, वर्धमानम् enlarging, महावेगम् very swiftly, तं हरिपुङ्गवम् him the chief of vanaras, आवेक्ष्य looking at him, अश्रुपूर्णमुखी her face filled with tears, दीना pitiably, बाष्पगद्गदया throat choked with tears, गिरा with these words, उवाच spoke.

#### Explanation

Seeing Hanuman eager to leap, enlarging his body quickly, she appeared pitiable with her face filled with tears and throat choked.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

तमुत्पातकृतोत्साहमवेक्ष्य हरिपुङ्गवम्।।5.40.20।। वर्धमानं महावेगमुवाच जनकात्मजा। अश्रुपूर्णमुखी दीना बाष्पगद्गदया गिरा।।5.40.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

जनकात्मजा Janaka's daughter, उत्पातकृतोत्साहम् risen up eager to leap, वर्धमानम् enlarging, महावेगम् very swiftly, तं हरिपुङ्गवम् him the chief of vanaras, आवेक्ष्य looking at him, अश्रुपूर्णमुखी her face filled with tears, दीना pitiably, बाष्पगद्गदया throat choked with tears, गिरा with these words, उवाच spoke.

#### Explanation

Seeing Hanuman eager to leap, enlarging his body quickly, she appeared pitiable with her face filled with tears and throat choked.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

हनुमन्सिंहसङ्काशौ भ्रातरौ रामलक्ष्मणौ। सुग्रीवं च सहामात्यं सर्वान् ब्रूयाह्यनामयम्।।5.40.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हनुमान् Hanuman, भ्रातरौ brothers, सिंहसङ्काशौ both comparable to lion, रामलक्ष्मणौ to Rama and Lakshmana, सहामात्यम् along with ministers, सुग्रीवं च and Sugriva, सर्वान् to all, अनामयम् wellbeing, ब्रूयाः convey.

#### Explanation

"O Hanuman to the lionlike brothers Rama and Lakshmana, to Sugriva  including his ministers and to every one convey my good wishes.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

यथा च स महाबाहुर्मां तारयति राघवः। अस्माद्दु:खाम्बुसम्रोधात्त्वं समाधातुमर्हसि।।5.40.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाबाहुः mightyarmed, सः राघवः that Rama, अस्मात् from this, दुःखाम्बुसंशेधात् from this ocean of sorrow, यथा in such a way, तारयति will help to cross, त्वम् you, समाधातुम् to make efforts, अर्हसि it is proper.

#### Explanation

"You should make efforts in such a manner that the mightyarmed Rama will help me in crossing the cean of sorrow.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

इमं च तीव्रं मम शोकवेगं रक्षोभिरेभिः परिभर्त्सनं च। ब्रूयास्तु रामस्य गतस्समीपम् शिवश्च तेऽध्वाऽस्तु हरिप्रवीर।।5.40.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हरिप्रवीर foremost vanara, रामस्य to Rama, समीपम् near, गतः after going, मम my, इमम् this, तीव्रम् intense, शोकवेगम् of grief, एभिः by these, रक्षोभिः by the rakshasas, परिभर्त्सनं च threats also, ब्रूयाः you nararte, ते to you, अध्वा journey, शिवः happy, अस्तु let it be.

#### Explanation

"Foremost vanara tell Rama about my intense grief as well as the threats of the demons. May your journey be happy.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

स राजपुत्र्य्राप्रतिवेदितार्थः कपिः कृतार्थः परिहृष्टचेताः। अल्पावशेषं प्रसमीक्ष्य कार्यं दिशं ह्युदीचीं मनसा जगाम।।5.40.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः that, कपिः vanara, राजपुत्र्या by the princess, प्रतिवेदितार्थः understood the message, कृतार्थः accomplished, परिहृष्टचेताः delighted at heart, कार्यम् task, अल्पावशेषम् a small left over, प्रसमीक्ष्य after considering, उदीचीम् north, दिशम् direction, मनसा mentally, जगम reached.

#### Explanation

Hanuman understood the message of princess Sita and felt delighted at heart at his accomplishment. Considering the small task yet to be accomplished he reached the northern shore mentally (He thought of Rama who waited in Kishkinda which is in the northern direction). इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये सुन्दरकाण्डे चत्वारिंशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the fortieth sarga of Sundarakanda of the holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 41

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

स च वाग्भिः प्रशस्ताभिर्गमिष्यन्पूजितस्तया। तस्माद्देशादपक्रम्य चिन्तयामास वानरः।।5.41.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

गमिष्यन् to depart, सः वानरः that vanara, तया by her, प्रशस्ताभिः with choicest, वाग्भिः with words, पूजितः honoured, तस्मात् from that, देशात् from location, अपक्रम्य moved, चिन्तयामास started thinking.

#### Explanation

Having been honoured and praised by Sita's words, Hanuman prepared to depart from Sita's presence.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

अल्पशेषमिदं कार्यं दृष्टेयमसितेक्षणा। त्रीनुपायानतिक्रम्य चतुर्थ: इह विद्यते।।5.41.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इदं कार्यम् this work, अल्पशेषम् little more remains, इयम् this, असितेक्षणा blackeyed lady, दृष्टा seen, त्रीन् three expedientsnegotiation, presentation and, dissension (Sama, Dana, Bheda), उपायान् strategies, अतिक्रम्य crossing, इह here, चतुर्थ fourth (expedient) punishment (Danda), विद्यते remains.

#### Explanation

None

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

न साम रक्षस्सु गुणाय कल्पते न दानमर्थोपचितेषु युज्यते। न भेदसाध्या बलदर्पिता जनाः पराक्रमस्त्वेव ममेह रोचते।।5.41.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रक्षस्सु with demons, साम negotiation, गुणाय for any merit, न कल्पते does not lead, अर्थोपचितेषु being wealthy, दानम् gifts, न युज्यते not proper, बलदर्पिताः proud of their strength, जनाः people, भेदसाध्याः will not yield by sowing seeds of dissension, न not, इह here, मम my, पराक्रमस्त्वेव only by valour, रोचते  pleases me.

#### Explanation

'With demons, negotiation is of no use. They are wealthy, they need no gifts. Proud of their stregth, the demons will not yield to dissension. I think only by using power it can be done.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

न चास्य कार्यस्य पराक्रमादृते विनिश्चयः कश्चिदिहोपपद्यते। हतप्रवीरा हि रणे हि राक्षसाः कथञ्चिदीयुर्यदिहाद्य मार्दवम्।।5.41.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इह here, अस्य of this, कार्यस्य of the task, पराक्रमात् ऋते other than power, विनिश्चयः a decision, कश्चित् even one, इह here, न उपपद्यते not hold good, यत् that, रणे in fight, हतप्रवीराः slay a few heroes, राक्षसाः ogres, अद्य to day, इह here, कथञ्चित् somehow, मार्दवम् soften, ईयुः will yield.

#### Explanation

'Other than power, there is no way to accomplish the task of ascertaining their strength. If I slay a few strong ogres somehow they may soften a little and yield.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

कार्ये कर्मणि निर्दिष्टे यो बहून्यपि साधयेत्। पूर्वकार्याविरोधेन स कार्यं कर्तुमर्हति।।5.41.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कार्ये in a work to be accomplished, कर्मणि in a work, निर्दिष्टे when the work is entrusted, यः he who, पूर्वकार्याविरोधेन without affecting the earlier work, बहून्यपि many, साधयेत् will achieve, सः he, कार्यम् task, कर्तुम् to do, अर्हति is fit.

#### Explanation

'He, who can perform tasks in addition to the objective accomplished without affecting it, is really worthy.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

न ह्येकस्साधको हेतुस्स्वल्पस्यापीह कर्मणः। यो ह्यर्थं बहुधा वेद स समर्थोऽर्थसाधने।।5.41.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इह here, स्वल्पस्य of a trivial task, अपि also, कर्मणः of the task, साधकः effective, हेतुः cause, एकः one, न हि is not, यः he who, अर्थम् as an object, बहुधा in many ways (means of doing), वेद knows, सः he, अर्थसाधने in achieving the task, समर्थः capable.

#### Explanation

'There are ways more than one even to perform a small work. And the capable know it.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

इहैव तावत्कृतनिश्चयो ह्यहं यदि व्रजेयं प्लवगेश्वरालयम्। परात्मसम्मर्दविशेषतत्त्ववित्ततः कृतं स्यान्मम भर्तृशासनम्।।5.41.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I, इहैव here itself, तावत् therefore, परात्मसम्मर्दविशेषतत्त्ववित् by understanding the difference between the strength of enemy forces and our strength in war, प्लवगेश्वरालयम् abode of vanaras, व्रजेयं यदि if I go, ततः then, मम my, भर्तृशासनम् the orders of my lord, कृतम् executed, स्यात् well.

#### Explanation

'If I ascertain the difference in the strength of enemies and vanaras in war here itself and return to Kishkinda, the abode of vanaras, then I would have executed the orders of my lord properly.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

कथं नु खल्वद्य भवेत्सुखागतं प्रसह्य युद्धं मम राक्षसैः सह। तथैव खल्वात्मबलं च सारवत्सम्मानयेन्मां च रणे दशाननः।।5.41.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अद्य today, मम for me, राक्षसैः सह with ogres, प्रसह्य enduring, युद्धम् war, कथं नु how to, सुखागतम् happy coming, भवेत् may be, तथैव in that way, आत्मबलं च our strength, सारवत् substantial, सः he, दशाननः च tenheaded Ravana's, माम् me, रणे in fight, मानयेत्  measure.

#### Explanation

'How can I have enduring war with the ogres today? How will my visit to this place end in happy return? Only in a combat this tenheaded Ravana will guage his strength and mine

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

ततस्समासाद्य रणे दशाननं समन्त्रिवर्गं सबलप्रयायिनम्। हृदि स्थितं तस्य मतं बलं च वै सुखेन मत्त्वाऽहमितः पुनर्व्रजे।।5.41.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः thereafter, समन्त्रिवर्गम् along with the ministers, सबलप्रयायिनम् and army chiefs marching forward, दशाननम् tenheaded Ravana, रणे in war, समासाद्य after finding out, तस्य his, हृदि in his mind, स्थितम् established, मतम् feeling, बलं च वै strength, मत्त्वा knowing, अहम् I, इतः from here, सुखेन happily, पुनः again, व्रजे can go.

#### Explanation

'I can return happily only after I gauge the tenheaded Ravana's army in war, his strength, his companions including the ministers and his mind.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

इदमस्य नृशंसस्य नन्दनोपममुत्तमम्। वनं नेत्रमनःकान्तं नानाद्रुमलतायुतम्।।5.41.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नृशंसस्य wicked Ravana's, अस्य his, इदम् this (Ashoka garden), नेत्रमनःकान्तम् feast to the eyes and mind, नानाद्रुमलतायुतम् filled with different kinds of trees and creepers, उत्तमम् excellent, वनम् garden, नन्दनोपमम् like the Nandana garden of Indra.

#### Explanation

'This Ashoka garden of Ravana filled with different kinds of trees and creepers is a feast to the eyes and mind. It is an excellent garden like the Nandana  garden of Indra.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

इदं विध्वंसयिष्यामि शुष्कं वनमिवानलः। अस्मिन्भग्ने ततः कोपं करिष्यति दशाननः।।5.41.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शुष्कम् dried up, वनम् forest, अनलः इव like fire, इदम् this, विध्वंसयिष्यामि will destroy, अस्मिन् when this, भग्ने devastate, ततः then, दशाननः Ravana, कोपम्  anger, करिष्यति will exhibit.

#### Explanation

'I will devastate this garden, just as fire destroys a dried up forest. This will enrage Ravana.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

ततो महत्साश्वमहारथद्विपं बलं समादेक्ष्यति राक्षसाधिपः। त्रिशूलकालायसपट्टिसायुधं ततो महद्युद्धमिदं भविष्यति।।5.41.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, राक्षसाधिपः king of ogres, साश्वमहारथद्विपम् with horses, chariots and elephants, महत् great, त्रिशूलकालायसपट्टिसायुधम् armed with tridents, black iron spears, बलम् army, समादेक्ष्यति will order, ततः then, इदम् this, महत् great, युद्धम् war, भविष्यति will take place.

#### Explanation

'Then the demon king will order the horses, chariots and elephants and army equipped with tridents and spears to march against me. A great war will take place.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

अहं तु तैः संयति चण्डविक्रमै स्समेत्य रक्षोभिरसह्य विक्रमः। निहत्य तद्रावणचोदितं बलं सुखं गमिष्यामि कपीश्वरालयम्।।5.41.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहं तु I on my part, चण्डविक्रमैः with those endowed with fierce strength, तैः with them, रक्षोभिः by demons, संयति in fight, समेत्य colliding, असह्यविक्रमः irresistible valour, रावणचोदितम् sent by Ravana, तत् that, बलम् army, निहत्य after destroying, सुखम् happily, कपीश्वरालयम् to the abode of vanaras, गमिष्यामि I will go.

#### Explanation

'I will fight with the ogres sent by Ravana, endowed with fierce strength and irresistible valour. After destroying their army I will go to the abode of vanaras happily'.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

ततो मारुतवत्कृद्धो मारुतिर्भीमविक्रमः। ऊरुवेगेन महता द्रुमान्क्षेप्तुमथारभत्।।5.41.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, भीमविक्रमः a vanara of fierce valour, मारुतिः Maruti, क्रुद्धः furious one, अथ and then, महता with great, ऊरुवेगेन with speed sprung from his thighs, मारुतवत् like the Windgod, द्रुमान् trees, क्षेप्तुम् to uproot, आरभत् started.

#### Explanation

Furious Maruti endowed with fierce valour started uprooting the trees with the speed sprung from his thighs like the Windgod.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

ततस्तु हनुमान्वीरो बभञ्ज प्रमदावनम्। मत्तद्विजसमाघुष्टं नानाद्रुमलतायुतम्।।5.41.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, वीरः hero, हनुमान् Hanuman, मत्तद्विजसमाघुष्टम् filled with intoxicated birds, नानाद्रुमलतायुतम् with variety of trees and vines, प्रमदावनम् garden grown for women folk, बभञ्ज felled.

#### Explanation

Then Hanuman felled a variety of trees and vines inhabited by intoxicated birds in the beautiful garden meant for womenfolk.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

तद्वनं मथितैर्वृक्षैर्भिन्नैश्च सलिलाशयैः। चूर्णितैः पर्वताग्रैश्च बभूवाप्रियदर्शनम्।।5.41.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तत् वनम् that garden, मथितैः with destroyed, वृक्षैः trees, भिन्नैः with breached, सलिलाशयैः ponds, चूर्णितैः powdered, पर्वताग्रैश्च mountain peaks, अप्रियदर्शनम् unpleasant appearance, बभूव remained.

#### Explanation

That garden looked ugly with uprooted trees, breached ponds and powdered mountain peaks.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

नानाशकुन्तविरुतैः प्रभिन्नैस्सलिलाशयैः। ताम्रैः किसलयैः क्लान्तै: क्लान्तद्रुमलतायुतम्।।5.41.17।। न बभौ तद्वनं तत्र दावानलहतं यथा। व्याकुलावरणा रेजुर्विह्वला इव ता लताः।।5.41.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कलान्तद्रुमलतायुतम् with wilted vines and trees, तत् वनम् that garden, नानाशकुन्तविरुतैः with birds shrieking with different kinds of sounds, प्रभिन्नैः with smashed, सलिलाशयैः with ponds of water, क्लान्तै: withered, ताम्रैः with red ones only, किसलयैः with tender shoots, दावानलहतंरणाः like burntin forest fire, ताः लताः those climbers, विह्वलाः इव like women with their robes disarrayed, रेजुः trembling in fear.

#### Explanation

Vines and trees wilted, birds shrieking, the embankments of ponds destroyed, its tender coppery red shoots withered, the garden looked as though it is burnt by forest fire and the climbers looked like women shivering in fear with their robes disarrayed.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

नानाशकुन्तविरुतैः प्रभिन्नैस्सलिलाशयैः। ताम्रैः किसलयैः क्लान्तै: क्लान्तद्रुमलतायुतम्।।5.41.17।। न बभौ तद्वनं तत्र दावानलहतं यथा। व्याकुलावरणा रेजुर्विह्वला इव ता लताः।।5.41.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कलान्तद्रुमलतायुतम् with wilted vines and trees, तत् वनम् that garden, नानाशकुन्तविरुतैः with birds shrieking with different kinds of sounds, प्रभिन्नैः with smashed, सलिलाशयैः with ponds of water, क्लान्तै: withered, ताम्रैः with red ones only, किसलयैः with tender shoots, दावानलहतंरणाः like burntin forest fire, ताः लताः those climbers, विह्वलाः इव like women with their robes disarrayed, रेजुः trembling in fear.

#### Explanation

Vines and trees wilted, birds shrieking, the embankments of ponds destroyed, its tender coppery red shoots withered, the garden looked as though it is burnt by forest fire and the climbers looked like women shivering in fear with their robes disarrayed.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

लतागृहैश्चित्रगृहैश्च नाशितैर्महोरगैर्व्यालमृगैश्च निर्धुतैः। शिलागृहैरुन्मथितैस्तथा गृहैः प्रणष्टरूपं तदभून्महद्वनम्।।5.41.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महत् great, तत् वनम् that garden, नाशितैः ruined, लतागृहैः arbours, चित्रगृहैः picture galleries, निर्धुतैः destroyed, महोरगैः huge serpents, व्यालमृगैश्च and even with wild animals, तथा in the same manner, उन्मथितैः scattered, शिलागृहैः stone houses, गृहैः with houses, प्रणष्टरूपम् disfigured, अभूत् became.

#### Explanation

The great garden lay disfigured, with the arbours and the picture galleries ruined, huge serpents and wild animals scattered, with stone houses and sheds destroyed.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

सा विह्वलाऽशोकलताप्रताना वनस्थली शोकलताप्रताना। जाता दशास्यप्रमदावनस्य कपेर्बलाद्धि प्रमदावनस्य।।5.41.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दशास्यप्रमदावनस्य of that pleasure garden of the tenheaded Ravana, प्रमदावनस्य कपेः with the strength of the monkey who had set out to protect a lady (Sita), सा वनस्थली that garden, बलात् by force, विह्वला destroyed, शोकलताप्रताना was spreading out creepers of sorrow, जाता appeared.

#### Explanation

The pleasuregarden of Ravana appeared as though it was spreading the creepers of sorrow, since it was totally destroyed by the monkey who had set out to protect a woman. प्रमदावनस्य  of the pleasure garden, प्रमदाअवनस्य of the hero who came to protect a lady.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

स तस्य कृत्वार्थपतेर्महाकपिर्महद्व्यलीकं मनसो महात्मनः। युयुत्सुरेको बहुभिर्महाबलैश्शिया ज्वलंस्तोरणमास्थितः कपिः।।5.41.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः महाकपिः that great vanara, महात्मनः of the great self, तस्य his, अर्थपतेः of the lord of wealth, मनसः in mind, महत् great, व्यलीकम् unpleasantness, कृत्वा having caused, महाबलैः mighty, बहुभिः many, एकः singlehanded, युयुत्सुः ready to fight, श्रिया blazing, ज्वलन् glowing, तोरणम् exit doorway, आस्थितः stood.

#### Explanation

The great vanara having perpetrated mischief, rousing anger in the mind of the great lord of wealth, stood at the exit doorway ready to combat singlehanded with many warriors in that  mighty army blazing in glory. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये सुन्दरकाण्डे एकचत्वारिंशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the fortyfirst sarga of Sundarakanda of the holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 42

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

ततः पक्षिनिनादेन वृक्षभङ्गस्वनेन च। बभूवुस्त्राससम्भ्रान्तास्सर्वे लङ्कानिवासिनः।।5.42.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः thereafter, पक्षिनिनादेन by the sounds of birds, वृक्षभङ्गस्वनेन by the cracking sound of trees, सर्वे all, लङ्कानिवासिनः the residents of Lanka, त्राससम्भ्रान्ताः were panicked, बभूवुः became.

#### Explanation

The residents of Lanka panicked at the shrieking sounds of birds and cracking sounds of trees.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

विद्रुताश्च भयत्रस्ता विनेदुर्मृगपक्षिणः। रक्षसां च निमित्तानि क्रूराणि प्रतिपेदिरे।।5.42.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मृगपक्षिणः beasts and birds, विद्रुता: ran and flown away, भयत्रस्ताः became scared, विनेदुः screeching in all directions, रक्षसां च for the ogres, क्रूराणि fierce, निमित्तानि portents, प्रतिपेदिरे appeared.

#### Explanation

The frightened beasts and birds ran and flew, screeching in all directions. Fierce portents appeared to the demons.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

ततो गतायां निद्रायां राक्षस्यो विकृताननाः। तद्वनं ददृशुर्भग्नं तं च वीरं महाकपिम्।।5.42.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, विकृताननाः women of hideous faces, राक्षस्यः ogressses, निद्रायाम् in sleep, गतायाम् was awakened, भग्नम् devastated, तत् that, वनम् garden, वीरम् hero, तं महाकपिं च that great vanara, ददृशुः saw.

#### Explanation

Hideouslooking ogresses, awakened from sleep, saw the garden devastated by the heroic Vanara.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

स ता दृष्ट्वा महाबाहुर्महासत्त्वो महाबलः। चकार सुमहद्रूपं राक्षसीनां भयावहम्।।5.42.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाबाहुः strong armed one, महासत्त्वः a great hero, महाबलः mighty, सः he, Hanuman, ताः the women, दृष्ट्वा seeing, राक्षसीनाम् for the ogresses, भयावहम् to create fear, सुमहत् very huge, रूपम् form, चकार assumed.

#### Explanation

When the powerful, mighty and strongarmed Hanuman saw the ogresses he assumed a very huge form in order to strike terror in them.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

ततस्तं गिरिसङ्काशमतिकायं महाबलम्। राक्षस्यो वानरं दृष्ट्वा पप्रच्छुर्जनकात्मजाम्।।5.42.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, गिरिसङ्काशम् looking like a mountain, अतिकायम् of huge body, महाबलम्  mighty, तं वानरम् that vanara, दृष्ट्वा after seeing, राक्षस्यः ogresses, जनकात्मजाम् Janaki, पप्रच्छुः questioned.

#### Explanation

Then, seeing the huge body of the mighty vanara resembling a mountain the ogresses questioned Janaki about him.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

कोऽयं कस्य कुतो वायं किं निमित्तमिहागतः। कथं त्वया सहानेन संवादः कृत इत्युत।।5.42.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अयम् this person, कः who, कस्य whose, अयम् he, कुतः from where, किं निमित्तम् for what purpose, इह here, आगतः he has come, उत or, अनेन by him, त्वया सह with you, संवादः talk, कृतः  made.

#### Explanation

"Who is he? By whom has he been sent? From where and why did he come here? What dialogue  did you have with him?

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

आचक्ष्व नो विशालाक्षि मा भूत्ते सुभगे भयम्। संवादमसितापाङ्गे त्वया किं कृतवानयम्।।5.42.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विशालाक्षि largeeyed lady, नः to us, आचक्ष्व O you may tell, सुभगे O auspicious one, ते to you, भयम् fear, मा भूत् let there be none, असितापाङ्गे O lady with dark sideglances, अयम् this, त्वया with you, किम् what, संवादम् dialogue, कृतवान् he had.

#### Explanation

"O lovely, largeeyed lady O auspicious one have no fear. What conversation did he have with you? Tell us".

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

अथाब्रवीन्महासाध्वी सीता सर्वाङ्गसुन्दरी। रक्षसां भीमरूपाणां विज्ञाने मम का गतिः।।5.42.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथ then, महासाध्वी a chaste lady, सर्वाङ्गसुन्दरी a lady of beautiful limbs, सीता Sita, तदा then, अब्रवीत् said, भीमरूपाणाम् of fierce form, रक्षसाम् of ogresses, विज्ञाने in knowing, गतिः direction, मम to me, का what?

#### Explanation

Chaste and beautiful Sita said, "How do I know about the ogresses who assume many fierce forms?"

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

यूयमेवाभिजानीत योऽयं यद्वा करिष्यति। अहिरेव ह्यहेः पादान्विजानाति न संशयः।।5.42.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अयम् this, यः whoever, यद्वा what, करिष्यति he is about, यूयमेव you alone, अभिजानीत you may find out, अहेः of a serpent, पादान् feet, अहिरेव a serpent alone, विजानाति  knows, संशयः doubt, न no.

#### Explanation

"You alone can know about who he is and what he is about. A serpent alone knows the movement (footprints) of another serpent. There is no doubt about it.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

अहमप्यस्य भीतास्मि नैनं जानामि को न्वयं। वेद्मि राक्षसमेवैनं कामरूपिणमागतम्।।5.42.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहमपि I am also, अस्य at him, भीता अस्मि I am scared of,  एनम् him, को नु who he is, अयम् this person, न जानामि I do not know, एनम् him, आगतम् came, कामरूपिणम् who has assumed his desired form in various ways, राक्षसमेव demon only, वेद्मि I think.

#### Explanation

"I am also scared of him. I do not know who he is. I think he is a demon, who came here assuming a form of his choice.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

वैदेह्या वचनं श्रुत्वा राक्षस्यो विद्रुता दिशः। स्थिताः काश्चिद्गताः काश्चिद्रावणाय निवेदितुम्।।5.42.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वैदेह्याः Vaidehi's, वचनम् words, श्रुत्वा after listening to, राक्षस्यः ogresses, दिशः in all directions, विद्रुताः ran, काश्चित् some, स्थिताः stood there, काश्चित् some, रावणाय to Ravana, निवेदितुम् to report, गताः fled.

#### Explanation

LIstening to Vaidehi's words the ogresses divided themselves and fled in all directions. While some stood there, others went to report to Ravana.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

रावणस्य समीपे तु राक्षस्यो विकृताननाः। विरूपं वानरं भीममाख्यातुमुपचक्रमुः।।5.42.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विकृताननाः uglyfaced, राक्षस्यः shedemons, रावणस्य to Ravana, समीपे presence, विरूपम्  disfigured, भीमम् frightening, वानरम् vanara, आख्यातुम् to report, उपचक्रमुः started.

#### Explanation

The uglyfaced ogresses went to the presence of Ravana and reported about the frightening form of the vanara.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

अशोकवनिकामध्ये राजन्भीमवपुः कपिः। सीतया कृतसंवादस्तिष्ठत्यमितविक्रमः।।5.42.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राजन् O king, सीतया with Sita, कृतसंवादः had a dialogue, भीमवपुः fearsome figure, अमितविक्रमः very powerful, कपिः vanara, अशोकवनिकामध्ये in the midst of Ashoka garden, तिष्ठति is stationed.

#### Explanation

"Your majesty an extremely powerful and frightening Vanara had a dialogue with Sita. He is stationed in the midst of Ashoka garden.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

न च तं जानकी सीता हरिं हरिणलोचना। अस्माभिर्बहुधा पृष्टा निवेदयितुमिच्छति।।5.42.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हरिणलोचना deereyed, जानकी Janaki, सीता Sita, अस्माभिः by us, पृष्टा questioned, तम् about him, निवेदयितुम् to disclose, न इच्छति not willing.

#### Explanation

"The deereyed Sita is not willing to disclose to us his identity even when we questioned her.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

वासवस्य भवेद्दूतो दूतो वैश्रवणस्य वा। प्रेषितो वापि रामेण सीतान्वेषणकाङ्क्षया।।5.42.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वासवस्य Vasava's, दूतः envoy, वैश्रवणस्य Vaisravana's, दूतो वा is he an envoy, सीतान्वेषणकाङ्क्षया or one who has come to trace Sita, रामेण by Rama, प्रेषितो वापि or sent, भवेत् may be.

#### Explanation

"He may be an envoy of Indra or Kubera or of Rama sent with an intent to trace Sita.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

तेन त्वद्भुतरूपेण यत्तत्तव मनोहरम्। नानामृगगणाकीर्णं प्रमृष्टं प्रमदावनम्।।5.42.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अद्भुतरूपेण by a person of wonderful form, तेन  by him, मनोहरम् delightful, नानामृगगणाकीर्णम् filled with various kinds of animals, यत् such, तव your, प्रमदावनम् beautiful pleasure garden, प्रमृष्टम् ruined.

#### Explanation

"Your beautiful pleasure garden filled with a variety of beasts has been ruined by this wonderful figure.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

न तत्र कश्चिदुद्देशो यस्तेन न विनाशितः। यत्र सा जानकी सीता स तेन न विनाशितः।।5.42.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तेन by him, यः whichever, न विनाशितः not ruined, उद्देशः place, तत्र there, कश्चित् even one, न not, सा that, जानकी Janaki, सीता Sita, यत्र wherever, सः that place, तेन by him, न विनाशितः not ruined.

#### Explanation

There is not a single place that has not been ruined by him there. Only the place where Janaka's daughter, Sita is resting has not been touched.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

जानकीरक्षणार्थं वा श्रमाद्वा नोपलभ्यते। अथवा कश्श्रमस्तस्य सैव तेनाभिरक्षिता।।5.42.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

जानकीरक्षणार्थं वा is it to protect Janaki, श्रमाद्वा or is it because he was tired, नोपलभ्यते we are not able to understand, अथवा or else, तस्य his, श्रमः tiresomeness, कः what, तेन by him, सैव she alone, अभिरक्षिता is spared.

#### Explanation

"It is not clear whether he spared that place for protecting Janakai or he stopped due to exhaustion. What is fatigue for him? It must be to save her that she is spared by him.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

चारुपल्लवपुष्पाढ्यं यं सीता स्वयमास्थिता। प्रवृद्धश्शिंशुपावृक्ष स्स च तेनाभिरक्षितः।।5.42.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीता Sita, चारुपल्लवपुष्पाढ्यम् a tree with lovely tender leaves and flowers, यम् whom, स्वयम् personally, आस्थिता she resorted, सः that, प्रवृद्धः large, शिंशुपावृक्षः simsupa tree, तेन by him, अभिरक्षितः is saved from destruction.

#### Explanation

"He has spared the simsupa tree with lovely, tender leaves and flowers which is a chosen  resort by Sita.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

तस्योग्ररूपस्योग्र त्वं दण्डमाज्ञातुमर्हसि। सीता सम्भाषिता येन तद्वनं च विनाशितम्।।5.42.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

येन whomsoever, सीता Sita, सम्भाषिता had dialogue, तत् वनं च that garden also, विनाशितम् has been ruined, तस्य his, उग्ररूपस्य of a fierce form, त्वम् you, उग्रम् severe, दण्डम् punishment, आज्ञातुम् to order, अर्हसि is proper

#### Explanation

"Your highness should take severe action on that person of fierce appearance with whom Sita dared to speak, and by whom the garden is ruined.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

मनः परिगृहीतां तां तव रक्षोगणेश्वर। कस्सीतामभिभाषेत यो न स्यात्त्यक्तजीवितः।।5.42.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रक्षोगणेश्वर O lord of the demon clan, मनः परिगृहीताम् one who is desired by you, तां सीताम् that Sita, यः whoever, त्यक्तजीवितः given up hope of life, न स्यात् if not, कः who, अभिभाषेत he will dare to talk.

#### Explanation

"O lord of the demon clan Who dares to talk to Sita if he has not given up all hope of life, since she is desired by you".

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

राक्षसीनां वचश्त्रुत्वा रावणो राक्षसेश्वरः। हुताग्निरिव जज्वाल कोपसंवर्तितेक्षणः।।5.42.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसेश्वरः lord of ogres, रावणः Ravana, राक्षसीनाम् shedemons, वचः words, श्रुत्वा after hearing, कोपसंवर्तितेक्षणः his eyeballs rolling in anger, हुताग्निरिव like sacrificial fire into which offering is made,जज्वाल flared.

#### Explanation

On listening to the report of the ogresses, Ravana, the lord of demons flared up like blazing flame of a sacrificial fire, his eyeballs rolling in rage.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

तस्य क्रुद्धस्य नेत्राभ्यां प्रापतन्नास्रबिन्दवः। दीप्ताभ्यामिव दीपाभ्यां सार्चिष स्स्नेहबिन्दवः।।5.42.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

क्रुद्धस्य of the angry, तस्य his, नेत्राभ्याम् from both the eyes, दीप्ताभ्याम् burning ones, दीपाभ्याम् from two lamps, सार्चिषः with flames, स्नेहबिन्दवः इव like drops of oil, अस्रबिन्दवः drops of tears, प्रापतन् fell down.

#### Explanation

From his angry eyes fell down drops of tears just as burning drops of oil drip from two burning lamps.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

आत्मनस्सदृशान्शूरान्किङ्करान्नाम राक्षसान्। व्यादिदेश महातेजा निग्रहार्थं हनूमतः।।5.42.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महातेजाः powerful, हनूमतः Hanuman's, निग्रहार्थम् to catch, आत्मनः his, सदृशान् equals, शूरान् heroes, किङ्करान्नाम called kinkaras, राक्षसान् demons, व्यादिदेश commanded.

#### Explanation

Then powerful Ravana commanded heroic demons called kinkaras who vied in strength to catch Hanuman.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

तेषामशीतिसाहस्रं किङ्कराणां तरस्विनाम्। निर्ययुर्भवनात्तस्मात्कूटमुद्गरपाणयः।।5.42.25।। महोदरा महादंष्ट्रा घोररूपा महाबलाः। युद्धाभिमनसस्सर्वे हनुमद्ग्रहणोद्यताः।।5.42.26।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तरस्विनाम् of swiftmoving ones, तेषां किङ्कराणाम् of the kinkaras, अशीतिसाहस्रम् eighty thousand, महोदराः largebellied, महादंष्ट्राः largetoothed, घोररूपाः dreadfullooking, महाबलाः powerful, युद्धाभिमनसः ready to fight in war, सर्वे all, कूटमुद्गरपाणयः hammers and clubs, हनुमद्ग्रहणोद्यताः resolved to capture Hanuman, तस्मात् from that, भवनात् building, निर्ययुः came out.

#### Explanation

Eighty thousand powerful  kinkaras (a clan of demons), largebellied, with large teeth and of dreadful appearance, swift in action, armed with hammers and clubs marched from there, resolved to capture Hanuman.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

तेषामशीतिसाहस्रं किङ्कराणां तरस्विनाम्। निर्ययुर्भवनात्तस्मात्कूटमुद्गरपाणयः।।5.42.25।। महोदरा महादंष्ट्रा घोररूपा महाबलाः। युद्धाभिमनसस्सर्वे हनुमद्ग्रहणोद्यताः।।5.42.26।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तरस्विनाम् of swiftmoving ones, तेषां किङ्कराणाम् of the kinkaras, अशीतिसाहस्रम् eighty thousand, महोदराः largebellied, महादंष्ट्राः largetoothed, घोररूपाः dreadfullooking, महाबलाः powerful, युद्धाभिमनसः ready to fight in war, सर्वे all, कूटमुद्गरपाणयः hammers and clubs, हनुमद्ग्रहणोद्यताः resolved to capture Hanuman, तस्मात् from that, भवनात् building, निर्ययुः came out.

#### Explanation

Eighty thousand powerful  kinkaras (a clan of demons), largebellied, with large teeth and of dreadful appearance, swift in action, armed with hammers and clubs marched from there, resolved to capture Hanuman.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

ते कपीन्द्रं समासाद्य तोरणस्थमवस्थितम्। अभिपेतुर्महावेगाः पतङ्गा इव पावकम्।।5.42.27।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ते they, तोरणस्थम् near the archway, अवस्थितम् stood ready, कपीन्द्रम् leader of vanaras, समासाद्य having approached, महावेगाः swift ones, पतङ्गाः moths, पावकम् इव like rushing into the flame, अभिपेतुः rushed.

#### Explanation

The kinkaras rushed towards the leader of vanaras who stood near the archway like the moths rushing towards the flame.

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

ते गदाभिर्विचित्राभिः परिघैः काञ्चनाङ्गदैः। आजघ्नुर्वानरश्रेष्ठं शरैश्चादित्यसन्निभैः।।5.42.28।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ते they, विचित्राभिः with wonderful ones, गदाभिः with iron maces, काञ्चनाङ्गदैः edged with gold, परिघैः with crowbars, आदित्यसन्निभैः resembling the Sun, शरैश्च with arrows, वानरश्रेष्ठम् best of vanaras, आजघ्नुः assailed.

#### Explanation

They assailed Hanuman with iron maces, crowbars edged with gold and shining arrows resembling the Sun.

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

मुद्गरैः पट्टिशैश्शूलैः प्रासतोमरशक्तिभिः। परिवार्य हनूमन्तं सहसा तस्थुरग्रतः।।5.42.29।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मुद्गरैः with hammers, पट्टि with sharpedged spears, शूलैः with tridents, प्रासतोमरशक्तिभिः with barbed missiles and powerful javelins, सहसा at once, हनूमन्तम् Hanuman, परिवार्य surrounded, अग्रतः in front of him, तस्थुः stood.

#### Explanation

They quickly surrounded Hanuman and stood with hammers, sharpedged spears, tridents, barbed missiles and powerful javelins.

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

हनुमानपि तेजस्वी श्रीमान्पर्वतसन्निभः। क्षितावाविध्य लाङ्गूलं ननाद च महास्वनम्।।5.42.30।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तेजस्वी brilliant, श्रीमान् illustrious one, पर्वतसन्निभः resembling a mountain, हनुमानपि Hanuman also, लाङ्गूलम् tail, क्षितौ on the ground, आविध्य waved, महास्वनम् causing loud sound, ननाद च roared.

#### Explanation

Gigantic Hanuman, illustrious and brilliant, roared and waved his tail striking it with force on the ground causing loud sound.

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

स भूत्वा सुमहाकायो हनुमान्मारुतात्मजः। धृष्टमास्फोटयामास लङ्कां शब्देन पूरयन्।।5.42.31।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मारुतात्मजः Maruti's son, सः हनुमान् that Hanuman, सुमहाकायः with a huge body, भूत्वा assuming, शब्देन by the sound, लङ्काम् entire Lanka, पूरयन् echoed, धृष्टम् wildly, अस्फोटयामास patted.

#### Explanation

Hanuman, son of Maruti, enlarged his body to a huge size and patted himself wildly  making loud noise that echoed the entire Lanka.

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

तस्यास्फोटितशब्देन महता सानुनादिना। पेतुर्विहङ्गा गगनादुच्चैश्चेदमघोषयत्।।5.42.32।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सानुनादिना by the frightening echoing sound, महता with great, तस्य his, अस्फोटितशब्देन by the sound of the patting, विहङ्गाः birds, गगनात् from the sky, पेतुः fell down, उच्चैः with a loud noise, इदम् thus, अघोषयत् च proclaimed.

#### Explanation

By the highly frightening sound caused by Hanuman patting, the birds from the sky fell down making a loud noise and Hanuman proclaimed in a loud voice as follows:

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

जयत्यतिबलो रामो लक्ष्मणश्च महाबलः। राजा जयति सुग्रीवो राघवेणाभिपालितः।।5.42.33।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अतिबलः mighty, रामः Rama, जयति is victorious, महाबलः powerful, लक्ष्मणश्च Lakshmana also, राघवेण by Rama, अभिपालितः ruled, राजा king, सुग्रीवः Sugriva, जयति is victorious.

#### Explanation

"Victory to Rama, the mighty and powerful, Victory to Lakshmana. Victory to king Sugriva who is ruled by Rama."

---

### Shloka 34

#### Shloka Text

दासोऽहं कोसलेन्द्रस्य रामस्याक्लिष्टकर्मणः। हनुमान्शत्रुसैन्यानां निहन्ता मारुतात्मजः।।5.42.34।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शत्रुसैन्यानाम् of the enemy army, निहन्ता killer, मारुतात्मजः son of the Windgod, हनुमान् Hanuman, अहम् I am, कोसलेन्द्रस्य of the lord of Kosala kingdom, अक्लिष्टकर्मणः a man of unwearied action, रामस्य Rama's, दासः servant.

#### Explanation

"I, son of the Windgod, destroyer of hostile armies, am a servant of Rama, the lord of Kosala kingdom, a man of unwearied action.

---

### Shloka 35

#### Shloka Text

न रावणसहस्रं मे युद्धे प्रतिबलं भवेत्। शिलाभिस्तु प्रहरतः पादपैश्च सहस्रशः।।5.42.35।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सहस्रशः a thousand ways, शिलाभिः with stones, पादपैश्च with even trees, प्रहरतः while I pound, मे to me, युद्धे in war, रावणसहस्रम् a thousand Ravanas, प्रतिबलम् match in strength, न भवेत् will not.

#### Explanation

Even a thousand Ravanas will not match me in strength when I pound stones and trees in a thousand ways.

---

### Shloka 36

#### Shloka Text

अर्दयित्वा पुरीं लङ्कामभिवाद्य च मैथिलीम्। समृद्धार्थो गमिष्यामि मिषतां सर्वरक्षसाम्।।5.42.36।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सर्वरक्षसाम् all ogres, मिषताम् as they gaze, लङ्कां पुरीम् city of Lanka, अर्दयित्वा shall destroy, मैथिलीम् to Mythili, अभिवाद्य च and pay respects, समृद्धार्थः having accomplished my purpose, गमिष्यामि I will return.

#### Explanation

"I shall destroy the city of Lanka and pay my respects to Mythili right under the nose of all demons. I will return with my purpose accomplished.

---

### Shloka 37

#### Shloka Text

तस्य सन्नादशब्देन तेऽभवन्भयशङ्किताः। ददृशुश्च हनूमन्तं सन्ध्यामेघमिवोन्नतम्।।5.42.37।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ते they, तस्य his, सन्नादशब्देन with the sound of hearing, भयशङ्किता: errified, अभवन् seemed, सन्ध्यामेघमिव like a cloud in twilight, उन्नतम् towering, हनूमन्तम् Hanuman, ददृशुश्च saw.

#### Explanation

Terrified on hearing the roar, the kinkaras looked at the towering Hanuman who seemed like a cloud in twilight (red in colour).

---

### Shloka 38

#### Shloka Text

स्वामिसन्देशनिश्शङ्कास्ततस्ते राक्षसाः कपिम्। चित्रैः प्रहरणैर्भीमैरभिपेतुस्ततस्ततः।।5.42.38।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, ते they, राक्षसाः demons, स्वामिसन्देशनिःशङ्का: on account the king's command putting aside their fear, चित्रैः with dreadful, प्रहरणैः with weapons, कपिम् the vanara, अभिपेतुः attacked him.

#### Explanation

Obeying their king's commnad, putting aside their fear, the kinkaras freely attacked the vanara  from all directions with dreadful weapons.

---

### Shloka 39

#### Shloka Text

स तैः परिवृतश्शूरैस्सर्वतस्सुमहाबलः। आससादाऽयसं भीमं परिघं तोरणाश्रितम्।।5.42.39।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सुमहाबलः exceesively powerful one, सः he, Hanuman, शूरैः with heroes, तैः with those, सर्वतः all over, परिवृतः surrounded, तोरणाश्रितम् near the  archway, भीमम् terrific, आयसम् a weapon made of iron, परिघम् iron beam, आससाद picked up.

#### Explanation

Extremely powerful Hanuman surrounded by heroes all over, on his part went to the archway and picked up a terrific iron beam.

---

### Shloka 40

#### Shloka Text

स तं परिघमादाय जघान रजनीचरान्। स पन्नगमिवादाय स्फुरन्तं विनतासुतः।।5.42.40।। विचचाराम्बरे वीरः परिगृह्य च मारुतिः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः he, तं परिघम् that iron beam, आदाय took hold, रजनीचरान् nightrangers, जघान struck them, वीरः hero, सः मारुतिः that Maruti, विनतासुतः just as Vinatha's son, स्फुरन्तम् shoots, पन्नगम् serpent, आदाय seized, परिगृह्य took hold, अम्बरे in the sky, विचचार started moving.

#### Explanation

Hanuman, the mighty hero, seized the iron beam and struck the kinkaras, just as Garuda, the son of Vinata shoots up a struggling serpent and starts taking strides in the sky with the weapon in his hand (like Indra with his thunderbolt did with demons).

---

### Shloka 41

#### Shloka Text

स हत्वा राक्षसान्वीरान्किङ्करान्मारुतात्मजः।।5.42.41।। युद्धाकाङ्क्षी पुनर्वीरस्तोरणं समुपाश्रितः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वीरः hero, सः मारुतात्मजः that Windgod's son, वीरान् heroes, किङ्करान् kinkaras, राक्षसान् ogres, हत्वा having killed, पुनः again, युद्धाकाङ्क्षी desiring further combat, तोरणम् archway, समुपाश्रितः reached.

#### Explanation

The great Windgod's son killed the kinkaras, and returned to the archway desiring further combat.

---

### Shloka 42

#### Shloka Text

ततस्तस्माद्भयान्मुक्ताः कतिचित्तत्र राक्षसाः।।5.42.42।। निहतान्किङ्करान्सर्वान्रावणायन्यवेदयन्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, तत्र that, तस्मात् from fear, भयात् out of fear, मुक्ताः relieved, कतिचित् some, राक्षसाः ogres, सर्वान् all, किङ्करान् kinkaras, निहतान् killed, रावणाय to Ravana, न्यवेदयन् reported.

#### Explanation

Then a few surviving ogres took to their heels after getting over that shock and reported to Ravana of the destruction of the kinkaras in the combat

---

### Shloka 43

#### Shloka Text

स राक्षसानां निहतं महद्बलं निशम्य राजा परिवृत्तलोचनः। समादिदेशाप्रतिमं पराक्रमे प्रहस्तपुत्रं समरे सुदुर्जयम्।।5.42.43।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

स he, राजा king, राक्षसानाम् of ogres, महत् mighty, बलम् army, निहतम् killed, निशम्य after hearing, परिवृत्तलोचनः with his eyes rolling, पराक्रमे in valour, अप्रतिमम् matchless, समरे in war, सुदुर्जयम् difficult to conquer, प्रहस्तपुत्रम् son of Prahasta, समादिदेश commanded.

#### Explanation

The mighty demon king having heard about the killing of the formidable army of ogres, his eyes rolling in rage, commanded the son of Prahasta, who is difficult to conquer and matchless in war. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये सुन्दरकाण्डे द्विचत्वारिंशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the fortysecond sarga of Sundarakanda of the holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 43

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

ततस्स किङ्करान्हत्वा हनुमान्ध्यानमास्थितः। वनं भग्नं मया चैत्यप्रासादो न विनाशितः।।5.43.1।। तस्मात्प्रासादमप्येवं भीमं विध्वंसयाम्यहं। इति सञ्चिन्त्य मनसा हनुमान्दर्शयन्बलम्।।5.43.2।। चैत्यप्रासादमाप्लुत्य मेरुशृङ्गमिवोन्नतम्। आरुरोह कपिश्रेष्ठो हनुमान्मारुतात्मजः।।5.43.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, सः हनुमान् that Hanuman, किङ्करान् kinkaras, हत्त्वा having slain, ध्यानम् reflected, आस्थितः resumed, मया by me, वनम् garden, भग्नम् destroyed, चैत्यप्रासादः the Chaitya palace, न विनाशितः not ruined, तस्मात् therefore, अद्य now, अहम् I, भीमम् fearsome, प्रासादम् building, एवम् in this way, विध्वंसयामि I will destroy, कपिश्रेष्ठः foremost of vanaras, मारुतात्मजः son of the Windgod, हनुमान् Hanuman, मनसा in his mind, इति thus, सञ्चिन्त्य thinking, बलम् strength, दर्शयन्  showed, मेरुशृङ्गमिव like the peak of mount Meru, उन्नतम् tall, चैत्यप्रासादम् Chaitya palace, उत्ल्पुत्य jumped, आरुरोह climbed

#### Explanation

Having slain the kinkaras, Hanuman stood and reflected, ' I have ruined the garden and not the palatial buildings that are like temples (Chaityas)'. Then Hanuman, son of the Windgod, decided in his mind to show his strength and jumped on to the top of the palatial building that appeared like the peak of mount Meru.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

ततस्स किङ्करान्हत्वा हनुमान्ध्यानमास्थितः। वनं भग्नं मया चैत्यप्रासादो न विनाशितः।।5.43.1।। तस्मात्प्रासादमप्येवं भीमं विध्वंसयाम्यहं। इति सञ्चिन्त्य मनसा हनुमान्दर्शयन्बलम्।।5.43.2।। चैत्यप्रासादमाप्लुत्य मेरुशृङ्गमिवोन्नतम्। आरुरोह कपिश्रेष्ठो हनुमान्मारुतात्मजः।।5.43.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, सः हनुमान् that Hanuman, किङ्करान् kinkaras, हत्त्वा having slain, ध्यानम् reflected, आस्थितः resumed, मया by me, वनम् garden, भग्नम् destroyed, चैत्यप्रासादः the Chaitya palace, न विनाशितः not ruined, तस्मात् therefore, अद्य now, अहम् I, भीमम् fearsome, प्रासादम् building, एवम् in this way, विध्वंसयामि I will destroy, कपिश्रेष्ठः foremost of vanaras, मारुतात्मजः son of the Windgod, हनुमान् Hanuman, मनसा in his mind, इति thus, सञ्चिन्त्य thinking, बलम् strength, दर्शयन्  showed, मेरुशृङ्गमिव like the peak of mount Meru, उन्नतम् tall, चैत्यप्रासादम् Chaitya palace, उत्ल्पुत्य jumped, आरुरोह climbed

#### Explanation

Having slain the kinkaras, Hanuman stood and reflected, ' I have ruined the garden and not the palatial buildings that are like temples (Chaityas)'. Then Hanuman, son of the Windgod, decided in his mind to show his strength and jumped on to the top of the palatial building that appeared like the peak of mount Meru.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

ततस्स किङ्करान्हत्वा हनुमान्ध्यानमास्थितः। वनं भग्नं मया चैत्यप्रासादो न विनाशितः।।5.43.1।। तस्मात्प्रासादमप्येवं भीमं विध्वंसयाम्यहं। इति सञ्चिन्त्य मनसा हनुमान्दर्शयन्बलम्।।5.43.2।। चैत्यप्रासादमाप्लुत्य मेरुशृङ्गमिवोन्नतम्। आरुरोह कपिश्रेष्ठो हनुमान्मारुतात्मजः।।5.43.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, सः हनुमान् that Hanuman, किङ्करान् kinkaras, हत्त्वा having slain, ध्यानम् reflected, आस्थितः resumed, मया by me, वनम् garden, भग्नम् destroyed, चैत्यप्रासादः the Chaitya palace, न विनाशितः not ruined, तस्मात् therefore, अद्य now, अहम् I, भीमम् fearsome, प्रासादम् building, एवम् in this way, विध्वंसयामि I will destroy, कपिश्रेष्ठः foremost of vanaras, मारुतात्मजः son of the Windgod, हनुमान् Hanuman, मनसा in his mind, इति thus, सञ्चिन्त्य thinking, बलम् strength, दर्शयन्  showed, मेरुशृङ्गमिव like the peak of mount Meru, उन्नतम् tall, चैत्यप्रासादम् Chaitya palace, उत्ल्पुत्य jumped, आरुरोह climbed

#### Explanation

Having slain the kinkaras, Hanuman stood and reflected, ' I have ruined the garden and not the palatial buildings that are like temples (Chaityas)'. Then Hanuman, son of the Windgod, decided in his mind to show his strength and jumped on to the top of the palatial building that appeared like the peak of mount Meru.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

आरुह्य गिरिसङ्काशं प्रासादं हरियूथपः। बभौ स सुमहातेजाः प्रतिसूर्य इवोदितः।।5.43.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हरियूथपः vanara leader, सुमहातेजाः brilliant, सः he, गिरिसङ्काशम् resembling a mountain, प्रासादम् palace, आरुह्य after climbing, उदितः rising, प्रतिसूर्यः इव like another Sun, बभौ looked

#### Explanation

The powerful vanara leader climbed the tall, palatial building which resembled a mountain. He looked like another Sun, just risen.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

संप्रधृष्य च दुर्धर्षं चैत्यप्रासादमुत्तमम्। हनुमान्प्रज्वलन्लक्ष्म्या पारियात्रोपमोऽभवत्।।5.43.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हनुमान् Hanuman, दुर्धर्षम् indomitable, उत्तमम् fine, चैत्यप्रासादम् palace building, सम्प्रधृष्य च  having surrounded लक्ष्म्या with glory, प्रज्वलन् blazing, पारियात्रोपमः like mountain Pariyatra, अभवत् appeared

#### Explanation

Having destroyed the fine palace building, the indomitable Hanuman was blazing with glory like the mountain Pariyatra. (one of the seven Kula mountains viz., Mahendra, Malaya, Sahya, Shuktiman, Riksha, Vindhya and Pariyatra)

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

स भूत्वा सुमहाकायः प्रभावान्मारुतात्मजः। धृष्टमास्फोटयामास लङ्कां शब्देन पूरयन्।।5.43.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः मारुतात्मजः that son of the Wind god, प्रभावात् by his prowess, सुमहाकायः of a huge body, भूत्वा being, शब्देन by the sound, लङ्काम् Lanka, पूरयन् while filling, धृष्टम् roughly, अस्फोटयामास started patting (his arms)

#### Explanation

Hanuman heaved his body  huge  with his prowess and patted his arms loudly filling the city of Lanka with that terrible sound.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

तस्यास्फोटितशब्देन महता श्रोत्रघातिना। पेतुर्विहङ्गमास्तत्र चैत्यपालाश्च मोहिताः।।5.43.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तस्य by the, श्रोत्रघातिना by the deafening sound, महता with a loud, अस्फोटितशब्देन with the sound of patting, तत्र there, विहङ्गमाः birds, पेतुः fell down, चैत्यपालाश्च guards of the palace, मोहिताः lost consciousness.

#### Explanation

By the deafening sound caused by the patting, the birds fell down and the guards of the palace lost consciousness.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

अस्त्रविज्जयतां रामो लक्ष्मणश्च महाबलः। राजा जयति सुग्रीवो राघवेणाभिपालितः।।5.43.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अस्त्रवित् knowledgeable about all missiles, रामः Rama, जयताम् be victorious, महाबलः matchless warrior, लक्ष्मणश्च Lakshmana also, राघवेण by Rama, अभिपालितः ruled, राजा king, सुग्रीवः Sugriva, जयति be victorious.

#### Explanation

'May Sri Rama, unsurpassed in the use of missiles be victorious May his brother, mighty Lakshmana, a matchless warrior be victorious. May King Sugriva ruled by Rama be victorious.'

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

दासोऽहं कोसलेन्द्रस्य रामस्याक्लिष्टकर्मणः। हनुमान्शत्रुसैन्यानां निहन्ता मारुतात्मजः।।5.43.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शत्रुसैन्यानाम् of enemy army, निहन्ता killer, मारुतात्मजः son of the Windgod, हनुमान् Hanuman, अहम् I, कोसलेन्द्रस्य king of Kosala's, अक्लिष्टकर्मणः one who can  acomplish any task with ease, रामस्य Rama's, दासः servant.

#### Explanation

'I am the son of the Windgod, destroyer of enemy army. I am a servant of Rama, the king of Kosala who can accomplish any task with ease.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

न रावणसहस्रं मे युद्धे प्रतिबलं भवेत्। शिलाभिस्तु प्रहरतः पादपैश्च सहस्रशः।।5.43.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सहस्रशः in a thousand ways, शिलाभिः with rocks, पादपैश्च or even with trees, प्रहरतः hurling, मे to me, युद्धे in war, रावणसहस्रम् a thousand Ravanas, प्रतिबलम् match in might, तु also, न भवेत् not be.

#### Explanation

'Not even a thousand Ravanas can stand my might  in combat as I start assailing them with thousands of  rocks or even trees.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

अर्दयित्वा पुरीं लङ्कामभिवाद्य च मैथिलीम्। समृद्धार्थो गमिष्यामि मिषतां सर्वरक्षसाम्।।5.43.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सर्वरक्षसाम् of all ogres, मिषताम् they keep looking, लङ्कां पुरीम् city of Lanka, अर्थयित्वा destroying, मैथिलीम् Mythili, अभिवाद्य च offering obeisance, समृद्धार्थः having accomplished the task, गमिष्यामि return to.

#### Explanation

'I shall return only after accomplishing the destruction of the city of Lanka and offering obeisance to Sita, while all demons keep looking at me'.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्त्वा विमानस्थश्चैत्यस्थान्हरियूथपः। ननाद भीमनिर्ह्रादो रक्षसां जनयन्भयम्।।5.43.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हरियूथपः monkey leader, विमानस्थः standing on the vimana, चैत्यस्थान् of the palace, एवम् that way, उक्त्वा having said, भीमनिर्ह्रादः making terrific sound, रक्षसाम् to the ogres, भयम् fear, जनयन् while creating, ननाद roared.

#### Explanation

Having made proclamations that way from the top of the Vimana (palace) the monkey leader roared making terrific sound frightening the ogres.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

तेन शब्देन महता चैत्यपालाश्शतं ययुः। गृहीत्वा विविधानस्त्रान्प्रासान्खङ्गान्परश्वथान्।।5.43.13।। विसृजन्तो महाकाया मारुतिं पर्यवारयन्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महता with terrific, तेन शब्देन by that sound, शतम् hundred, चैत्यपालाः guards of the palace, विविधान् many, अस्त्रान् weapons, प्रासान् darts, खङ्गान् swords, परश्वथान् axes, गृहीत्वा after picking, ययुः marked, महाकायाः those of huge bodies, विसृजन्तः hurling, मारुतिम् Maruti, पर्यवारयन् surrounded.

#### Explanation

Hearing the terrific sound, a hundred gigantic guards of the palace set forth, holding different kinds of weapons like darts, swords and axes and hurled at Maruti surrounding him.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

ते गदाभिर्विचित्राभिः परिघैः काञ्चनाङ्गदैः। आजघ्नुर्वानरश्रेष्ठं बाणैश्चादित्यसन्निभैः।।5.43.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ते they, विचित्राभिः with astonishing, गदाभिः with maces, काञ्चनाङ्गदैः maces decorated with gold, परिघैः with crowbars, आदित्यसन्निभैः sharp like the Sun's rays, बाणैश्च with arrows, वानरश्रेष्ठम् foremost of vanaras, अजघ्नुः hit.

#### Explanation

Holding astonishing maces decorated with gold, iron crowbars and sharp arrows resembling the rays of the Sun the ogres hit Hanuman, the foremost of vanaras.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

आवर्त इव गङ्गायास्तोयस्य विपुलो महान्। परिक्षिप्य हरिश्रेष्ठं स बभौ रक्षसां गणः।।5.43.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः that, रक्षसां गणः horde of ogres, हरिश्रेष्ठम् best of vanaras, परिक्षिप्य having surrounded, गङ्गायाः of the river Ganga, तोयस्य of the water, महान् huge, विपुलः big, आवर्त इव like a whirlpool, बभौ looked.

#### Explanation

The demons surrounding the great monkey looked like a big whirlpool of the water of the river Ganga.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

ततो वातात्मजः क्रुद्धो भीम रूपं समास्थितः।।5.43.16।। प्रासादस्य महन्तस्य स्तम्बं हेमपरिष्कृतम्। उत्पाटयित्वा वेगेन हनुमान्पवनात्मजः।।5.43.17।। ततस्तं भ्रामयामास शतधारं महाबलः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, वातात्मज son of the Windgod, क्रुद्धः infuriated, भीमम् fearsome, रूपम् form, समास्थितः assumed, महान् great, पवनात्मजः son of the Windgod, महाबलः mighty, हनुमान् Hanuman, तस्य प्रासादस्य of that palace, हेमपरिष्कृतम् decked with gold, स्तम्भं pillar, उत्पाटयित्वा uprooted, ततः then, शतधारम् holding a hundred edged, तम् that, वेगेन swiftly, भ्रामयामास whirled round.

#### Explanation

Thereupon the infuriated son of the Windgod, assumed a fearsome form, uprooted a pillar of the palace decked with gold and holding that hundred edged pillar whirled it round swiftly.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

ततो वातात्मजः क्रुद्धो भीम रूपं समास्थितः।।5.43.16।। प्रासादस्य महन्तस्य स्तम्बं हेमपरिष्कृतम्। उत्पाटयित्वा वेगेन हनुमान्पवनात्मजः।।5.43.17।। ततस्तं भ्रामयामास शतधारं महाबलः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, वातात्मज son of the Windgod, क्रुद्धः infuriated, भीमम् fearsome, रूपम् form, समास्थितः assumed, महान् great, पवनात्मजः son of the Windgod, महाबलः mighty, हनुमान् Hanuman, तस्य प्रासादस्य of that palace, हेमपरिष्कृतम् decked with gold, स्तम्भं pillar, उत्पाटयित्वा uprooted, ततः then, शतधारम् holding a hundred edged, तम् that, वेगेन swiftly, भ्रामयामास whirled round.

#### Explanation

Thereupon the infuriated son of the Windgod, assumed a fearsome form, uprooted a pillar of the palace decked with gold and holding that hundred edged pillar whirled it round swiftly.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

तत्र चाग्निस्समभवत्प्रासादश्चाप्यदह्यत।।5.43.18।। दह्यमानं ततो दृष्ट्वा प्रासादं हरियूथपः। स राक्षसशतं हत्त्वा वज्रेणेन्द्र इवासुरान्।।5.43.19।। अन्तरिक्षे स्थितश्श्तीमानिदं वचनमब्रवीत्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तत्र thereafter, अग्नि: च and fire, समभवत् was created, प्रासादश्च the palace, अपि also, अदह्यत was burnt, ततः then, सः हरियूथपः Hanuman, दृष्ट्वा after seeing, इन्द्रः Indra, वज्रेण with a thunderbolt, असुरानिव like the ogres, राक्षसशतम् a hundred ogres, हत्त्वा having killed, श्रीमान् illustrous, अन्तरिक्षे in the aerial region, स्थितः stood, इदम् this, वचनम् word, अब्रवीत् proclaimed.

#### Explanation

Thereafter fire was generated (through friction) and with if the palace blazed. Hanuman saw the palace burning. He killed a hundred demons there as Indra killed demons with his thunderbolt. (The whirling pillar that generated fire apeared like a thunderbolt) Later he stood in the air and proclaimed this:

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

तत्र चाग्निस्समभवत्प्रासादश्चाप्यदह्यत।।5.43.18।। दह्यमानं ततो दृष्ट्वा प्रासादं हरियूथपः। स राक्षसशतं हत्त्वा वज्रेणेन्द्र इवासुरान्।।5.43.19।। अन्तरिक्षे स्थितश्श्तीमानिदं वचनमब्रवीत्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तत्र thereafter, अग्नि: च and fire, समभवत् was created, प्रासादश्च the palace, अपि also, अदह्यत was burnt, ततः then, सः हरियूथपः Hanuman, दृष्ट्वा after seeing, इन्द्रः Indra, वज्रेण with a thunderbolt, असुरानिव like the ogres, राक्षसशतम् a hundred ogres, हत्त्वा having killed, श्रीमान् illustrous, अन्तरिक्षे in the aerial region, स्थितः stood, इदम् this, वचनम् word, अब्रवीत् proclaimed.

#### Explanation

Thereafter fire was generated (through friction) and with if the palace blazed. Hanuman saw the palace burning. He killed a hundred demons there as Indra killed demons with his thunderbolt. (The whirling pillar that generated fire apeared like a thunderbolt) Later he stood in the air and proclaimed this:

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

मादृशानां सहस्राणि विसृष्टानि महात्मनाम्।।5.43.20।। बलिनां वानरेन्द्राणां सुग्रीववशवर्तिनाम्। अटन्ति वसुधां कृत्स्नां वयमन्ये च वानराः।।5.43.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महात्मनाम् huge, बलिनाम् of mighty, सुग्रीववशवर्तिनाम् of those loyal to Sugriva, मादृशानाम् of people like me, वानरेन्द्राणाम् of the best of  vanara leaders, सहस्राणि in thousands, विसृष्टानि are despatched, वयम् we, अन्ये others, वानराश्च and vanaras, कृत्स्नाम् entire, वसुधाम् the earth, अटन्ति we are wandering.

#### Explanation

"Thousands of huge and mighty monkeys, equal to me, loyal to king Sugriva, are coming under the orders and have been recruited from the entire earth. They  are the best among monkeys.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

मादृशानां सहस्राणि विसृष्टानि महात्मनाम्।।5.43.20।। बलिनां वानरेन्द्राणां सुग्रीववशवर्तिनाम्। अटन्ति वसुधां कृत्स्नां वयमन्ये च वानराः।।5.43.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महात्मनाम् huge, बलिनाम् of mighty, सुग्रीववशवर्तिनाम् of those loyal to Sugriva, मादृशानाम् of people like me, वानरेन्द्राणाम् of the best of  vanara leaders, सहस्राणि in thousands, विसृष्टानि are despatched, वयम् we, अन्ये others, वानराश्च and vanaras, कृत्स्नाम् entire, वसुधाम् the earth, अटन्ति we are wandering.

#### Explanation

"Thousands of huge and mighty monkeys, equal to me, loyal to king Sugriva, are coming under the orders and have been recruited from the entire earth. They  are the best among monkeys.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

दशनागबलाः केचित्केचिद्दशगुणोत्तराः। केचिन्नागसहस्रस्य बभूवुस्तुल्यविक्रमाः।।5.43.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

केचित् some, दशनागबलाः have ten elephants strength, केचित् some, दशगुणोत्तराः ten hundred elephants strength, केचित् some, नागसहस्रस्य thousand elephants strength, तुल्यविक्रमाः equal in power, बभूवुः will be.

#### Explanation

"Of them some have the strength equal to ten elephants, others are ten times stronger, and still others have a thousand elephants' strength.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

सन्ति चौघबलाः केचित्केचिद्वायुबलोपमाः। अप्रमेयबलाश्चान्ये तत्रासन्हरियूथपाः।।5.43.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

केचित् some, ओघबलाः strong as flood, सन्ति there are, केचित् some, वायुबलोपमाः have the power of wind, तत्र there, अन्ये others, हरियूथपाः leaders of monkeys, अप्रमेयबलाश्च of immeasureable strength, आसन् there are.

#### Explanation

"Some have the strength of flood waters, some are powerful as wind and yet others have strength beyond measure.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

ईदृग्विधैस्तु हरिभिर्वृतो दन्तनखायुधैः। शतैश्शतसहस्रैश्च कोटीभिरयुतैरपि।।5.43.24।। आगमिष्यति सुग्रीवः सर्वेषां वो निषूदनः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ईद्दृग्विधैः endowed with such warriors, दन्त अपितु नखायुधै: by those using teeth and nails as weapons, शतैः in hundreds, अयुतैः in ayuta (ten thousand), शतसहस्रैः a hundred thousand, कोटीभिः in crores, हरिभिः with vanaras also, वृतः surrounded, सुग्रीवः Sugriva, सर्वेषाम् of all of them, वः you, निषूदनः killer, आगमिष्यति would come here.

#### Explanation

"Sugriva will come here with hundreds of thousands of crores of vanaras who would be fighting with their teeth and nails as weapons.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

नेयमस्ति पुरी लङ्का न यूयं न च रावणः।5.43.25।। यस्मादिक्ष्वाकुनाथेन बद्धं वैरं महात्मना।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

यस्मात् since, महात्मना with great self, इक्ष्वाकुनाथेन with the lord of Ikshvaku dynasty, वैरम् enmity, बद्धम् is bound, इयम् this, लङ्कापुरी city of Lanka, नास्ति will not be found, यूयम् even you, न not, रावणः च and Ravana, न also.

#### Explanation

"You have established great enmity with the noble lord of Ikshvaku dynasty. Neither this Lanka will endure nor you, nor even Ravana will be traced. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये सुन्दरकाण्डे त्रिचत्वारिशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the fortythird sarga of Sundarakanda of the holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 44

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

सन्दिष्टो राक्षसेन्द्रेण प्रहस्तस्य सुतो बली। जम्बुमाली महादंष्ट्रो निर्जगाम धनुर्धरः।।5.44.1।। रक्तमाल्याम्बरधरस्स्रग्वी रुचिरकुण्डलः। महान्विवृत्तनयनश्चण्डस्समरदुर्जयः।।5.44.2।। धनुश्शक्रधनुः प्रख्यं महद्रुचिरसायकम्। विष्फारयाणो वेगेन वज्राशनिसमस्वनम्।।5.44.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महादंष्ट्रः one with big front teeth, धनुर्धरः bow in hand, रक्तमाल्याम्बरधरः adorned with a garland of red flowers and red clothes, स्रग्वी wearing a chaplet, रुचिरकुण्डलः with beautiful earrings, महान् great, विवृत्तनयनः who had big round eyes, चण्डः fierce, समरदुर्जयः  invincible in war, बली mighty, प्रहस्तस्य सुतः son of Prahasta,जम्बुमाली Jambumali,राक्षसेन्द्रेण by the demon king, सन्दिष्टः commanded, महत् huge, रुचिरसायकम् who had radiant arrows, वज्राशनिसमस्वनम् making sounds like thunderbolt, शक्रधनुःप्रख्यम् like Indra's splendid bow (a rainbow), धनुः bow, वेगेन very swiftly, विष्फारयाणः as if thundering, निर्जगाम went out.

#### Explanation

Commanded  by the demon king, Jambumali the invincible son of Prahasta, who had large teeth in front, big rolling eyes, red flower garland, red robes and a chaplet with beautiful earrings. He went round twanging his producing thunderous sound. His arrows were huge, shining and beautiful.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

सन्दिष्टो राक्षसेन्द्रेण प्रहस्तस्य सुतो बली। जम्बुमाली महादंष्ट्रो निर्जगाम धनुर्धरः।।5.44.1।। रक्तमाल्याम्बरधरस्स्रग्वी रुचिरकुण्डलः। महान्विवृत्तनयनश्चण्डस्समरदुर्जयः।।5.44.2।। धनुश्शक्रधनुः प्रख्यं महद्रुचिरसायकम्। विष्फारयाणो वेगेन वज्राशनिसमस्वनम्।।5.44.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महादंष्ट्रः one with big front teeth, धनुर्धरः bow in hand, रक्तमाल्याम्बरधरः adorned with a garland of red flowers and red clothes, स्रग्वी wearing a chaplet, रुचिरकुण्डलः with beautiful earrings, महान् great, विवृत्तनयनः who had big round eyes, चण्डः fierce, समरदुर्जयः  invincible in war, बली mighty, प्रहस्तस्य सुतः son of Prahasta,जम्बुमाली Jambumali,राक्षसेन्द्रेण by the demon king, सन्दिष्टः commanded, महत् huge, रुचिरसायकम् who had radiant arrows, वज्राशनिसमस्वनम् making sounds like thunderbolt, शक्रधनुःप्रख्यम् like Indra's splendid bow (a rainbow), धनुः bow, वेगेन very swiftly, विष्फारयाणः as if thundering, निर्जगाम went out.

#### Explanation

Commanded  by the demon king, Jambumali the invincible son of Prahasta, who had large teeth in front, big rolling eyes, red flower garland, red robes and a chaplet with beautiful earrings. He went round twanging his producing thunderous sound. His arrows were huge, shining and beautiful.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

सन्दिष्टो राक्षसेन्द्रेण प्रहस्तस्य सुतो बली। जम्बुमाली महादंष्ट्रो निर्जगाम धनुर्धरः।।5.44.1।। रक्तमाल्याम्बरधरस्स्रग्वी रुचिरकुण्डलः। महान्विवृत्तनयनश्चण्डस्समरदुर्जयः।।5.44.2।। धनुश्शक्रधनुः प्रख्यं महद्रुचिरसायकम्। विष्फारयाणो वेगेन वज्राशनिसमस्वनम्।।5.44.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महादंष्ट्रः one with big front teeth, धनुर्धरः bow in hand, रक्तमाल्याम्बरधरः adorned with a garland of red flowers and red clothes, स्रग्वी wearing a chaplet, रुचिरकुण्डलः with beautiful earrings, महान् great, विवृत्तनयनः who had big round eyes, चण्डः fierce, समरदुर्जयः  invincible in war, बली mighty, प्रहस्तस्य सुतः son of Prahasta,जम्बुमाली Jambumali,राक्षसेन्द्रेण by the demon king, सन्दिष्टः commanded, महत् huge, रुचिरसायकम् who had radiant arrows, वज्राशनिसमस्वनम् making sounds like thunderbolt, शक्रधनुःप्रख्यम् like Indra's splendid bow (a rainbow), धनुः bow, वेगेन very swiftly, विष्फारयाणः as if thundering, निर्जगाम went out.

#### Explanation

Commanded  by the demon king, Jambumali the invincible son of Prahasta, who had large teeth in front, big rolling eyes, red flower garland, red robes and a chaplet with beautiful earrings. He went round twanging his producing thunderous sound. His arrows were huge, shining and beautiful.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

तस्य विष्फारघोषेण धनुषो महता दिशः। प्रदिशश्च नभश्चैव सहसा समपूर्यत।।5.44.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तस्य his, धनुषः bow's, महता by the huge, विष्फारघोषेण by the twang, दिशः all directions, प्रदिशश्चैव and intermediate directions, नभश्चैव heavens also, सहसा at once, समपूर्यत were filled.

#### Explanation

All directions including the intermediate directions and the sky were filled with that loud sound produced by his twang.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

रथेन खरयुक्तेन तमागतमुदीक्ष्य सः। हनुमान्वेगसम्पन्नो जहर्ष च ननाद च।।5.44.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

खरयुक्तेन drawn by donkeys, रथेन by the chariot, आगतम् reached, तम् him, उदीक्षय after seeing, सः हनुमान् that Hanuman, वेगसम्पन्नः who was endowed with speed, जहर्ष च felt glad, ननाद च and made a loud noise.

#### Explanation

Seeing him coming in a chariot drawn by donkeys, Hanuman who was swift to act was glad (to have an opportunity to fight) and made a loud noise.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

तं तोरणविटङ्कस्थं हनुमन्तं महाकपिम्। जम्बुमाली महाबाहुर्विव्याध निशितैश्शरैः।।5.44.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाबाहुः strongarmed one, जम्बुमाली Jambumali, तोरणविटङ्कस्थम् who stood on top of the exit archway, महाकपिम् great monkey, तं हनुमन्तम् that Hanuman, निशितैः with sharp, शरैः with arrows, विव्याध hit hard.

#### Explanation

Strongarmed Jambumali with his sharp arrows hit great Hanuman who stood on top of the exit archway.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

अर्धचन्द्रेण वदने शिरस्येकेन कर्णिना। बाह्वोर्विव्याध नाराचैर्दशभिस्तं कपीश्वरम्।।5.44.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अर्धचन्द्रेण with a crescentshaped arrow, वदने on the face, एकेन with one, कर्णिना ear shaped arrow, शिरसि on the head, दशभिः ten, नाराचैः iron arrows, बाह्वोः into the arms, तं कपीश्वरम् that lord of monkeys, विव्याध hit.

#### Explanation

Jambumali released a crescentshaped arrow on the face, one earshaped (barbed) arrow on the head and ten iron arrows into the arms of the lord of monkeys.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

तस्य तच्छुशुभे ताम्रं शरेणाभिहतं मुखम्। शरदीवाम्बुजं फुल्लं विद्धं भास्कररश्मिना।।5.44.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शरेण by an arrow, अभिहतम् struck, ताम्रम् reddish, तत् his, मुखम् face, शरदि in autumn, भास्करश्मिना with Sun's radiance, विद्धम् hit, फुल्लम् blown, अम्बुजमिव like a lotus, शुशुभे appeared.

#### Explanation

His red face struck by an arrow appeared like a fullblown lotus in autumn season hit  by  the rays of the Sun.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

तत्तस्य रक्तं रक्तेन रञ्जितं शुशुभे मुखम्। यथाकाशे महापद्मं सिक्तं चन्दनबिन्दुभिः।।5.44.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रक्तेन by blood, रञ्जितम् glowing, रक्तम् red, तस्य his, तत् that, मुखम् face, चन्दनबिन्दुभिः with sandal drops, सिक्तम् sprinkled, आकाशे in the sky, महापद्मं यथा like a great lotus, शुशुभे looked bright.

#### Explanation

His red face  stained with blood was glowing bright like red lotus in the sky sprinkled with drops of red sandal.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

चुकोप बाणाभिहतो राक्षसस्य महाकपिः। ततः पार्श्वेऽतिविपुलां ददर्श महतीं शिलाम्।।5.44.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

बाणाभिहतः struck by the shafts, महाकपिः great monkey, राक्षसस्य of the demon, चुकोप enraged, ततः then, पार्श्वे lying beside, अतिविपुलाम् huge, महतीम् on the ground, शिलाम् rock, ददर्श he saw.

#### Explanation

Struck by the shafts of the ogre, the great monkey was enraged and happened to see a huge,  heavy rock lying beside him on the ground.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

तरसा तां समुत्पाट्य चिक्षेप बलवद्भली। तां शरैर्दशभिः क्रुद्धस्ताडयामास राक्षसः।।5.44.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

बली a powerful one, ताम् that boulder, तरसा with vehemence, समुत्पाट्य having lifted, बलवत् with his strength, चिक्षेप hurled, राक्षसः giant, क्रुद्धः angrily, ताम् that rock, दशभिः with ten, शरैः with arrows, ताडयामास smashed.

#### Explanation

Powerful Hanuman with all vehemence lifted the rock and hurled it at the giant which he angrily smashed with ten arrows.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

विपन्नं कर्म तद्दृष्ट्वा हनुमांश्चण्डविक्रमः। सालं विपुलमुत्पाट्य भ्रामयामास वीर्यवान्।।5.44.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

चण्डविक्रमः who had fierce power, वीर्यवान् a warrior, हनुमान् Hanuman, तत् that कर्म action, विपन्नम् failed, दृष्ट्वा seeing, विपुलम् huge, सालम् sala tree, उत्पाट्य pulled out, भ्रामयामास whirled.

#### Explanation

Hanuman, a strong, powerful and fierce warrior pulled out a sala tree and whirled it. Seeing that his attempt has failed.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

भ्रामयन्तं कपिं दृष्ट्वा सालवृक्षं महाबलम्। चिक्षेप सुबहून्बाणान्जम्बुमाली महाबलः।।5.44.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाबलः mighty, जम्बुमाली Jambumali, सालवृक्षम् sala tree, भ्रामयन्तम् after whirling, महाबलम् mighty, कपिम् monkey, दृष्ट्वा after seeing, सुबहून् many, बाणान् arrows, चिक्षेप fired.

#### Explanation

Gigantic Jambumali seeing the mighty Hanuman whirling the sala tree fired many arrows at him.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

सालं चतुर्भिश्चिच्छेद वानरं पञ्चभिर्भुजे। उरस्येकेन बाणेन दशभिस्तु स्तनान्तरे।।5.44.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सालम् sala tree, चतुर्भिः with four, चिच्छेद cut off, वानरम् vanara, पञ्चभिः with five, भुजे on his arm, उरसि on his chest, एकेन बाणेन with one arrow, स्तनान्तरे in the hollow of the chest, दशभिः with ten.

#### Explanation

He struck the sala tree with four arows, the arm of Hanuman with five arrows, chest with one, the hollow of the chest with ten more arrows.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

स शरैः पूरिततनु: क्रोधेन महता वृतः। तमेव परिघं गृह्य भ्रामयामास वेगितः।।5.44.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शरैः arrows, पूरिततनु: the body was filled, सः that, महता intense, क्रोधेन with anger, वृतः endowed, वेगितः speedily, तं परिघमेव the same iron spear, गृह्य seized, भ्रामयामास started whirling.

#### Explanation

Hanuman filled with arrows all over his body, seized an iron spear (which he made use of in the initial battle) and impetuously spun it in  intense anger.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

अतिवेगोऽतिवेगेन भ्रामयित्वा बलोत्कटः। परिघं पातयामास जम्बुमालेर्महोरसि।।5.44.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

बलोत्कटः of immeasurable strength, अतिवेगः a hero of great speed, परिघम् iron spear, अतिवेगेन with great speed, भ्रामयित्वा after turning round and round, जम्बुमालेः on Jambumali, महोरसि on the broad chest, पातयामास hurled.

#### Explanation

Hanuman of immeasurable strength hurled the iron spear after turning it round and round at high speed on the broad chest of Jambumali.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

तस्य चैव शिरो नास्ति न बाहू न च जानुनी। न धनुर्न रथो नाश्वास्तत्रादृश्यन्त नेषवः।।5.44.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तत्र there, तस्य his, शिरः चैव even head, नास्ति not there, बाहू shoulders, न not, जानुनी knees, न not, धनुः bow, न not, रथः chariot, न not, अश्वाः donkeys, horses, नादृश्यन्तः not there to discern, इषवः न nothing was seen.

#### Explanation

Neither his head could be discerned nor his arms, knees, nor bow, chariot nor donkeys. Nothing was seen.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

स हतस्सहसा तेन जम्बुमाली महाबलः। पपात निहतो भूमौ चूर्णिताङ्गविभूषणः।।5.44.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तेन by that blow, हतः killed, महाबलः mighty, जम्बुमाली Jambumali, सहसा inconsiderately, निहतः having been killed, चूर्णिताङ्गविभूषणः with his ornaments reduced to powder, भूमौ on the floor, पपात fell down.

#### Explanation

Mighty Jambumali killed  by that blow dropped down dead at once with his ornaments reduced to powder.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

जम्बुमालिं च निहतं किङ्करांश्च महाबलान्। चुक्रोध रावणश्शुत्वा कोपसंरक्तलोचनः।।5.44.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रावणः Ravana, जम्बुमालिं च even Jambumali, निहतम् being klled, महाबलान् of great strength, किंकरांश्च and kinkaras also, श्रुत्वा hearing, कोपसंरक्तलोचनः bloodshot eyes, चुक्रोध got enraged.

#### Explanation

Ravana's eyes became bloodshot in anger when he heard about the death of Jambumali and of the strong army of kinkaras.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

स रोषसंवर्तितताम्रलोचनः प्रहस्तपुत्त्रे निहते महाबले। अमात्यपुत्त्रानतिवीर्यविक्रमान् समादिदेशाशु निशाचरेश्वरः।।5.44.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाबले who had great strength, प्रहस्तपुत्त्रे Prahasta's son, निहते killed, सः निशाचरेश्वरः demon king, रोषसंवर्तितताम्रलोचनः his eyes red and rolling in anger, आशु quickly, अतिवीर्यविक्रमान् highly valiant wariors, अमात्यपुत्रान् sons of ministers, समादिदेश commanded.

#### Explanation

When the mighty son of Prahasta was killed, infuriated Ravana, his eyes red and rolling, commanded the highly valiant sons of ministers. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये सुन्दरकाण्डे चतुश्चत्वारिंशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the fortyfourth sarga of Sundarakanda of the holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 45

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

ततस्ते राक्षसेन्द्रेण चोदिता मन्त्रिणस्सुताः। निर्ययुर्भवनात्तस्मात्सप्तसप्तार्चिवर्चसः।।5.45.1।। महाबलपरीवारा धनुष्मन्तो महाबलाः। कृतास्त्रास्त्रविदां श्रेष्ठाः परस्परजयैषिणः।।5.45.2।। हेमजालपरिक्षिप्तैर्ध्वजवद्भिः पताकिभिः। तोयदस्वननिर्घोषैर्वाजियुक्तैर्महारथैः।।5.45.3।। तप्तकाञ्चनचित्राणि चापान्यमितविक्रमाः। विस्फारयन्तस्संहृष्टास्तटित्वन्त इवाम्बुदाः।।5.45.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation
