"O hero take no large army as it is futile with this monkey endowed with extraordinary vigour. Even a thunderbolt can do nothing. He is like the wind and has no match in speed. He is like the sacrificial fire who cannot be destroyed by weapons.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

तमेवमर्थं प्रसमीक्ष्य सम्यक् स्वकर्मसाम्याद्धि समाहितात्मा। स्मरंश्च दिव्यं धनुषोऽस्त्रवीर्यं व्रजाक्षतं कर्म समारभस्व।।5.48.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तम् him, एवम् in that way, अर्थम् matter, सम्यक् properly, प्रसमीक्ष्य assess, स्वकर्मसाम्यात् person of good judgement, समाहितात्मा with singleminded attention, धनुषः of the bow, दिव्यम् divine, अस्त्रवीर्यम् strength of your astras, स्मरंश्च recollecting, व्रज move, कर्म the act, अक्षतम् without being destroyed in the middle, समारभस्व start.

#### Explanation

"Assess the situation properly. You are a person of sound judgement. You are a master in weaponry. Recollect the strength of your bow and divine astras and march forward with a singleminded attention. Act in a manner that does not bring your doom.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

न खल्वियं मतिश्श्रेष्ठा यत्त्वां सम्प्रेषयाम्यहम्। इयं च राजधर्माणां क्षत्रियस्य मतिर्मता।।5.48.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I, त्वाम् you, सम्प्रेषयामि इति यत् this way sending you to war is not good, इयं मतिः this I think, श्रेष्ठा the best, न खलु not certainly, इयम् this, राजधर्माणाम् in accordance with state craft, क्षत्रियस्य for a kshatriya, मतिः thought, मता approved.

#### Explanation

"I am reluctant to send you to war in the circumstances. But you are the best choice for me. However, certainly this is the duty of a kshatriya who follows the science of polity. Hence I am sending you.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

नानाशस्त्रेषु सङ्ग्रामे वैशारद्यमरिन्दम। अवश्यमेव बोद्धव्यं काम्यश्च विजयो रणे।।5.48.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अरिंदम subduer of foes, सङ्ग्रामे in battle, नानाशस्त्रेषु with many kinds of weapons, वैशारद्यम् efficient, अवश्यमेव ultimately, बोद्धव्यम् aware, रणे in war, विजयश्च only victory, काम्यः seek.

#### Explanation

"O subduer of enemies you are capable of handling different kinds of weapons. Therefore, understand that I seek only your ultimate triumph in war.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

ततः पितुस्तद्वचनं निशम्य प्रदक्षिणं दक्षसुतप्रभावः। चकार भर्तारमदीनसत्त्वो रणाय वीरः प्रतिपन्नबुद्धिः।।5.48.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, दक्षसुतप्रभावः powerful  like the son of Daksha, वीरः warrior, पितुः father's, तत् वचनम् those words, निशम्य having heard, अदीनसत्त्वः who is never distressed, रणाय in war, प्रतिपन्नबुद्धिः prepared his mind, भर्तारम् by the king, प्रदक्षिणं चकार went round with respect.

#### Explanation

Hearing the father's words (of exhortation), he, who is never distressed at the prospect of war decided to seek the battle as he was powerful like the son of Daksha, and went round his father with respect.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

ततस्तै स्स्वगणैरिष्टैरिन्द्रजित् प्रतिपूजितः। युद्धोद्धतः कृतोत्साहस्सङ्ग्रामं प्रत्यपद्यत।।5.48.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, यद्धोद्धतः rushed forth for war, इन्द्रजित् Indrajit, इष्टैः  by people who loved him, तैः स्वगणैः by his own kith and kin, प्रतिपूजितः was honoured with reverence, कृतोत्साहः became vigorous, सङ्ग्रामम् for war, प्रत्यपद्यत went ahead.

#### Explanation

Having been honoured by his own kith and kin who loved him, Indrajit rushed forth for war with martial vigour.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

श्रीमान्पद्मपलाशाक्षो राक्षसाधिपतेस्सुतः। निर्जगाम महातेजास्समुद्र इव पर्वसु।।5.48.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

श्रीमान् illustrious, पद्मपलाशाक्षः eyes of lotus petals, महातेजाः highly resplendent, राक्षसाधिपतेः सुतः son of the lord of demons, पर्वसु on a fullmoon day, समुद्रः इव like the turbulent ocean, निर्जगाम came out.

#### Explanation

When the illustrious son of Ravana, the highly Indrajit resplendent with eyes of lotus petals marched ahead he looked like the turbulent ocean on a fullmoon day.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

स पक्षिराजोपमतुल्यवेगैर्व्याळैश्चतुर्भिः सिततीक्षणदंष्ट्रैः। रथं समायुक्तमसहयवेगं समारुरोहेन्द्रजिदिन्द्रकल्पः।।5.48.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इन्द्रकल्पः like Indra, सः इन्द्रजित् that Indrajit, पक्षिराजोपमतुल्यवेगैः equal to Garuda the king of birds in speed, सिततीक्षणदंष्ट्रैः that had sharp big white teeth, चतुर्भिः four, व्याघै: with tigers, समायुक्तम् yoked, असहयवेगम् very high speed, रथम् chariot, समारुरोह ascended.

#### Explanation

Like Indra, Indrajit ascended the chariot (which had the standard of Indra as it was seized from him as a token of his having conquered Indra) yoked to four sharptoothed tigers. It moved in swiftness vying with Garuda, the king of birds in speed.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

स रथी धन्विनां श्रेष्ठः शस्त्रज्ञोस्त्रविदां वरः। रथेनाभिययौ क्षिप्रं हनुमान्यत्र सोऽभवत्।।5.48.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रथी charioteer, धन्विनाम् among wielders of bows, श्रेष्ठः best, शस्त्रज्ञः in the knowledge of archery, अस्त्रविदाम् among exports in weaponry, वरः great, सः that, सः हनुमान् that Hanuman, यत्र whichever, अभवत् stationed, शीघ्रम् quickly, रथेन by his chariot, अभिययौ marched.

#### Explanation

Mounted on the chariot, Indrajit, the best in archery and an expert in weaponry went quickly to the place where that great Hanuman was stationed.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

स तस्य रथनिर्घोषं ज्यास्वनं कार्मुकस्य च। निशम्य हरिवीरोऽसौ संप्रहृष्टतरोऽभवत्।।5.48.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः असौ he also, हरिवीरः leader of monkeys, तस्य his, रधनिर्घोषम् rumbling of the chariot, कार्मुकस्य of his bow, ज्यास्वनं च sound of his bow, निशम्य after hearing, संप्रहृष्टः तर: more happy, अभवत् became.

#### Explanation

The leader of the monkey also became happier on hearing the rumbling (of the chariot) and the twang of Indrajit's bowstring.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

सुमहच्चापमादाय शितशल्यांश्च सायकान्। हनुमन्तमभिप्रेत्य जगाम रणपण्डितः।।5.48.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रणपण्डितः adept in warfare, सुमहत् highly powerful, चापम् bow, शितशल्यान् sharpedged ones, सायकान् arrows, आदाय picking, हनुमन्तम् Hanuman, अभिप्रेत्य having aimed at, जगाम went forward

#### Explanation

Indrajit who was an adept in warfare, picked his highly powerful bow and aimed his sharpedged arrow at Hanuman.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

तस्मिंस्ततः संयति जातहर्षे रणाय निर्गच्छति चापपाणौ। दिशश्च सर्वाः कलुषा बभूवु र्मृगाश्च रौद्रा बहुधा विनेदुः।।5.48.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, संयति for war, जातहर्षे feeling happy, तस्मिन् with him, चापपाणौ when he held bow in his hand, रणाय for war, निर्गच्छति was going forth, सर्वाः all, दिशः quarters, कलुषाः gloomy, बभूवुः, appeared रौद्राः wild, मृगाश्च animals, बहुधा in  various ways, विनेदुः howled

#### Explanation

When he sallied forth happily holding a bow full of passion for war, darkness prevailed in all quarters and it became gloomy. Beasts began to howl in various frightful ways.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

समागतास्तत्र तु नागयक्षा महर्षयश्चक्रचराश्च सिद्धाः। नभस्समावृत्य च पक्षिसङ्घा विनेदुरुच्चैः परमप्रहृष्टाः।।5.48.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तत्र there, समागताः having assembled, नागयक्षाः Nagas and Yakshas, चक्रचराः those who go round the orbits, महर्षयः seers, सिद्धाश्च Siddhas, पक्षिसङ्घाः flocks of birds, नभः in the sky, समावृत्य  collected, परमप्रहृष्टाः very happy, उच्चैः loudly, विनेदुः screeched

#### Explanation

(To see the combat) the Nagas, Yakshas, seers and Siddhas who move round the heavenly orbits assembled in the sky. The birds collected  in flocks in the sky and screeched loudly and happily.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

आयान्तं सरथं दृष्ट्वा तूर्णमिन्द्रजितं कपिः। विननाद महानादं व्यवर्धत च वेगवान्।।5.48.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कपिः monkey, तूर्णम् speedily, आयान्तम् coming, सरथम् that chariot, इन्द्रजितम् Indrajit, दृष्टवा after seeing, महानादम् great sound, विननाद made, वेगवान् who was endowed with speed, व्यवर्धत च enlarged his body

#### Explanation

Hanuman of very high speed enlarged his body and roared, perceiving Indrajit's chariot coming swiftly.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

इन्द्रजित्तु रथं दिव्यमास्थितश्चित्रकार्मुकः। धनुर्विष्फारयामास तटिदूर्जितन्निस्स्वनम्।।5.48.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इन्द्रजित्तु Indrajit on his part, दिव्यम् divine, रथम् chariot, आस्थितः seated, चित्रकार्मुकः wonderful bow, तटिदूर्जितन्निस्स्वनम् rumbling with lightning speed, धनुः bow, विष्फारयामास twanged

#### Explanation

Indrajit on his part sat in his divine chariot holding a wonderful bow and in a lightning speed twanged it.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

ततस्समेतावतितीक्ष्णवेगौ महाबलौ तौ रणनिर्विशङ्कौ। कपिश्च रक्षोधिपतेस्तनूजः सुरासुरेन्द्राविव बद्धवैरौ।।5.48.26।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, अतितीक्ष्णवेगौ very fast in speed, महाबलौ mighty, रणनिर्विशङ्कौ fearless in war, तौ those two, कपिश्च vanara and, रक्षोधिपतेः of the demon king, तनूजः son, बद्धवैरौ inimical to each other, सुरासुरेन्द्राविव like sura and asura, समेतौ met

#### Explanation

Then the son of the demon king and Hanuman, both very fast in speed, mighty and powerful, fearless in war and like sura and asura closed in upon each other.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

स तस्य वीरस्य महारथस्य धनुष्मतः संयति सम्मतस्य। शरप्रवेगं व्यहनत्प्रवृद्ध श्चचार मार्गे पितुरप्रमेयः।।5.48.27।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अप्रमेयः immeasurable, सः he, प्रवृद्धः increased, महारथस्य magnificent chariotwarrior, धनुष्मतः bowman, संयति in battle, संमतस्य efficient, तस्य his वीरस्य hero's, शरप्रवेगम् the speeed of arrows, व्यहनत् escaped, पितुः father's, मार्गे path, चचार went

#### Explanation

Hanuman of immeasurable strength heaved himself to a huge proprtion and went moving on the path of his father (windgod) rendering useless the extraordinary speed of the arrows of Indrajit, who was great in chariot warfare, and an efficient archer.

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

ततश्शरानायततीक्ष्णशल्यान् सुपत्रिणः काञ्चनचित्रपुङ्खान्। मुमोच वीरः परवीरहन्ता सुसन्नतान् वज्रनिपातवेगान्।।5.48.28।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, परवीरहन्ता a slayer of enemy warriors, वीरः hero, आयततीक्षणशल्यान् long and sharppointed arrows, सुपत्रिणः with fine feathers, काञ्चनचित्रपुङ्स्वा with gold tips touching the bowstring, सुसन्नतान् a little bent at the tips, वज्रनिपातवेगान् which had the speed of lightning, शरान् arrows, मुमोच discharged

#### Explanation

Then the heroic Indrajit, slayer of enemy warriors, discharged long arrows that were fixed with fine feathers with sharppointed gold tips, bent a little at the tip touching the bow string, as swift as lightning.

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

ततस्तु तत्स्यन्दननिस्स्वनं च मृदङ्गभेरीपटहस्वनं च। विकृष्यमाणस्य च कार्मुकस्य निशम्य घोषं पुनरुत्पपात।।5.48.29।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, सः he, तत्स्यन्दन्निःस्वनं च rumbling of the chariot and, मृदङ्गभेरीपटहस्वनं च beating sound of mrudangas, bheris and patahas, विकृष्यमाणस्य drawn, कार्मुकस्य of the bow, घोषम् loud sound, निशम्य percieving, पुनः again, उत्पपात rose up

#### Explanation

Thereupon, hearing the rumbling of the chariot, the sounds of mrudangas, bheris, and patahas drawing towards him, and percieving the loud twang of the bow (of Indrajit) Hanuman sprang up again.

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

शराणामन्तरेष्वाशु व्यवर्तत महाकपिः। हरिस्तस्याभिलक्ष्यस्य मोघयन्लक्ष्यसंग्रहम्।।5.48.30।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाकपिः great monkey, हरिः tawny one, आशु quickly, अभिलक्ष्यस्य aiming at target, तस्य his, लक्ष्यसंग्रहम् hosts of aims, मोघयन् while rendering futile, शराणाम् arrows,अन्तरेषु between spaces, व्यवर्तत moved

#### Explanation

The great tawny monkey moved quickly between spaces of arrows that were aimed at him rendering hosts of Indrajit's targets futile.

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

शराणामग्रतस्तस्य पुनः समभिवर्तत। प्रसार्य हस्तौ हनुमानुत्पपातानिलात्मजः।।5.48.31।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अनिलात्मजः son of the Windgod, हनुमान् Hanuman, पुनः again, तस्य his, शराणाम् by his arrows, अग्रतः in front, समभिवर्तत stationed, हस्तौ hands, प्रसार्य having stretched, उत्पपात jumped

#### Explanation

Hanuman, son of the Windgod again stood facing the arrows directly stretching his hands and jumped (into the air).

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

तावुभौ वेगसम्पन्नौ रणकर्मविशारदौ। सर्वभूतमनोग्राहि चक्रतुर्युद्धमुत्तमम्।।5.48.32।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वेगसम्पन्नौ both endowed with speed, रणकर्मविशारदौ two experts in warfare, तौ उभौ both, सर्वभूतमनोग्राहिf  captivated the minds of all creatures,उत्तमम् best, युद्धम् war, चक्रतुः fought

#### Explanation

Both the warriors endowed with speed and skill in warfare carried on the fight which captivated the minds of all creatures.

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

हनुमतो वेद न राक्षसोऽन्तरं न मारुतिस्तस्य महात्मनोऽन्तरम्। परस्परं निर्विषहौ बभूवतुः समेत्य तौ देवसमानविक्रमौ।।5.48.33।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसः giant, हनूमतः of Hanuman, अन्तरम् way to hit, न वेद did not know, मारुतिः Maruti, महात्मनः of the great self, तस्य his, न not, देवसमानविक्रमौ equal to gods in valour, तौ both, समेत्य met, परस्परम् with each other, निर्विषहौ unable to bear with each other, बभूवतुः became.

#### Explanation

The demon did not find a way to hit Hanuman. Maruti also found no opportunity to hit great Indrajit. Both of them being equal to gods in valour became too much to each other in war. (Both of them moved at meteoric speed making it difficult to aim at the enemy.)

---

### Shloka 34

#### Shloka Text

ततस्तु लक्ष्ये स विहन्यमाने शरेष्वमोघेषु च संपतत्सु। जगाम चिन्तां महतीं महात्मा समाधिसंयोगसमाहितात्मा।।5.48.34।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, लक्ष्ये when aim, विहन्यमाने missing, अमोघेषु infallible, शरेषु arrows, संपतत्सु  released, महात्मा great, समाधिसंयोगसमाहितात्मा the great warrior entered a meditative mood with great concentration सः that, महतीम् very, चिन्ताम् thought, जगाम felt

#### Explanation

Indrajit's infallible arrows missed Hanuman. Then the great warrior became perplexed and started thinking seriously within himself entering into a reflective mood.

---

### Shloka 35

#### Shloka Text

ततो मतिं राक्षसराजसूनु श्चकार तस्मिन् हरिवीरमुख्ये। अवध्यतां तस्य कपेस्समीक्ष्य कथं निगच्छेदिति निग्रहार्थम्।।5.48.35।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, राक्षसराजसूनुः son of the demon king, तस्य of that कपेः of vanara, अवध्यताम् that should not be killed, समीक्ष्य observing, निग्रहार्थम् in order to catch him, कथम् how, निगच्छेत् he may not come, इति thus, तस्मिन् in him, हरिवीरमुख्ये at the monkey, मतिम् mind, चकार resolved

#### Explanation

Then Indrajit, son of the demon king, thinking that a vanara should not be killed started considering other means of catching hm.

---

### Shloka 36

#### Shloka Text

ततः पैतामहं वीरः सोऽस्त्रमस्त्रविदां वरः। संदधे सुमहत्तेजा: तं हरिप्रवरं प्रति।।5.48.36।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, वीरः  hero, अस्त्रविदाम् among masters of weapons, वरः excellent, सुमहातेजाः glorious, सः he, तं him, हरिप्रवरं प्रति towards foremost of monkeys, पैतामहम् Grandfather Brahma's, अस्त्रम् weapon, संदधे directed

#### Explanation

Then the heroic master of weapons, an excellent and glorious warrior directed the  Brahmastram towards the foremost of monkeys.

---

### Shloka 37

#### Shloka Text

अवध्योऽयमिति ज्ञात्वा तमस्त्रेणास्त्रतत्त्ववित्। निजग्राह महाबाहुर्मारुतात्मजमिन्द्रजित्।।5.48.37।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अस्त्रतत्त्ववित् a knower of the secrets of all missiles, महाबाहुः strongarmed, इन्द्रजित् Indrajit, अयम् this, अवध्यः not be killed, इति this, ज्ञात्वा realising, तम् him, मारुतात्मजम् son of the Windgod, अस्त्रेण by the divine weaponBrahmastram, निजग्राह caught

#### Explanation

Indrajit, the strongarmed hero, who knew the secrets of all missiles, realising that the son of the Windgod cannot be killed caught him by the divine weapon of Brahma (Brahmastram).

---

### Shloka 38

#### Shloka Text

तेन बद्धस्ततोऽस्त्रेण राक्षसेन स वानरः। अभवन्निर्विचेष्टश्च पपात स महीतले ।।5.48.38।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, राक्षसेन by the demon, अस्त्रेण by his weapon, बद्धः bound, सः वानरः that Hanuman, निर्विचेष्टः unable to move, अभवत् became, सः he, महीतले on the ground, पपात fell

#### Explanation

Then Hanuman bound by the weapon of the demon was unable to move and fell on the ground.

---

### Shloka 39

#### Shloka Text

ततोऽथ बुद्ध्वा स तदस्त्रबन्धं प्रभोः प्रभावाद्विगतात्मवेगः। पितामहानुग्रहमात्मनश्च विचिन्तयामास हरिप्रवीरः।।5.48.39।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, अथ and, सः हरिप्रवीरः that monkey leader, तदस्त्रबन्धंम् bond of that astram, बुद्ध्वा after realising, प्रभोः of the Lord, प्रभावात् by the grace, विगतात्मवेगः finding his speed arrested, आत्मनः for me, पितामहानुग्रहम् the boon of lord Brahma, विचिन्तयामास started thinking

#### Explanation

The monkey leader realised that his speed was arrested by the power of the lord Brahma, by the strength of the bond of Brahmastram. He then recalled lord Brahma's boon to him.

---

### Shloka 40

#### Shloka Text

तत स्स्वायम्बुवैर्मन्त्रैर्ब्रह्मास्त्रमभिमन्त्रितम्। हनुमांश्चिन्तयामास वरदानं पितामहात्।।5.48.40।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, हनुमान् Hanuman, स्वायंभुवैः by Brahma the creator, मन्त्रै mantra, अभिमन्त्रितम् appiled mantra, ब्रह्मास्त्रम् Brahmastram, पितामहात् grand sire, वरदानम् boon, चिन्तयामास started thinking

#### Explanation

Hanuman realised that it was by invoking creator Brahma and applying the mantra that he was caught by Brahmastram. On that occasion he started thinking of the boon given to him  by Grandsire Brahma :

---

### Shloka 41

#### Shloka Text

न मेऽस्त्रबन्धस्य च शक्तिरस्ति विमोक्षणे लोकगुरोः प्रभावात्। इत्येव मत्वा विहितोऽस्त्रबन्धो मयात्मयोनेरनुवर्तितव्यः।।5.48.41।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लोकगुरोः of the Guru of the universe, प्रभावात् by the effect, अस्त्रबन्धस्य of the bondage with astra, विमोक्षणे to release, मे to me, शक्तिः power, नास्ति not possible, इत्येव thus only, मत्वा  thinking, विहितः ordered, आत्मयोनेः by himself, अस्त्रबन्ध: bondage of astra, मया by me, अनुवर्तितव्यः should be obeyed

#### Explanation

'I deem it an effect of the power of Brahma. It is not possiblle for me to free myself due to the power of the great lord presiding over the universe through the astra. It should be obeyed'.

---

### Shloka 42

#### Shloka Text

स वीर्यमस्त्रस्य कपिर्विचार्य पितामहानुग्रहमात्मनश्च। विमोक्षशक्तिं परिचिन्तयित्वा पितामहाज्ञामनुवर्तते स्म।।5.48.42।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः कपिः  that Hanuman, अस्त्रस्य astra's, वीर्यम् power, विचार्य reflecting, आत्मनः in himself, पितामहानुग्रहं च by the favour of Brahma, विमोक्षशक्तिम् the power of liberation from bondage, परिचिन्तयित्वा recalling about, पितामहाज्ञाम् by the order of Brahma, अनुवर्तते स्म he obeyed

#### Explanation

Reflecting within himself about the power of the astra, Hanuman recalled Braham's favour to him. He recalled his own power to liberate himself from the Brahmastra and resolved to abide by the order of Brahma.

---

### Shloka 43

#### Shloka Text

अस्त्रेणापि हि बद्धस्य भयं मम न जायते। पितामहमहेन्द्राभ्यां रक्षितस्यानिलेन च।।5.48.43।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पितामहमहेन्द्राभ्याम् both by Brahma and Indra, अनिलेन च and by the Windgod, रक्षितस्य for me being protected, मे to me, अस्त्रेण by astra, बद्धस्यापि even though bound, भयम् fear, न जायते not having

#### Explanation

'Even though I am bound by the astra, I have the protection of both Brahma and Indra as well as the Windgod. Hence there is no cause for apprension.

---

### Shloka 44

#### Shloka Text

ग्रहणे चापि रक्षोभिर्महन्मे गुणदर्शनम्। राक्षसेन्द्रेण संवादस्तस्माद्गृह्णन्तु मां परे।।5.48.44।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रक्षोभिः by the demon, ग्रहणे चापि if I am caught, मे me, महत् great, गुणदर्शनम् will have the opportunity to see, राक्षसेन्द्रेण of the demon lord, संवादः to discuss, तस्मात् therefore, माम् to me, परे enemies, गृह्णन्तु take.

#### Explanation

'If I am caught by the demon I will have the opportunity to see the demon lord. Therefore, let me be caught by my enemies'.

---

### Shloka 45

#### Shloka Text

स निश्चितार्थः परवीरहन्ता समीक्ष्यकारी विनिवृत्तचेष्टः। परैः प्रसह्याभिगतैर्निगृह्य ननाद तैस्त्रै: परिभर्त्स्यमानः।।5.48.45।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

परवीरहन्ता killer of enemy warriors, समीक्ष्यकारी one who assesses before he acts, सः he, निश्चितारः resolved, विनिवृत्तचेष्टः his power of movement arrested, अभिगतैः went, तैस्त्रै: slowly and slowly, परैः by enemies, प्रसह्य forcibly, निगृह्य siezed, परिभर्त्समानः abused, ननाद roared

#### Explanation

Hanuman, a destroyer of enemies, who assesses before he acts, resolved (to submit to the ordinance of Brahma), to be silent (the power of his movement affected). When the enemy forces came to him slowly and slowly, siezed him forcibly and abused him, he roared.

---

### Shloka 46

#### Shloka Text

ततस्तं राक्षसा दृष्ट्वा निर्विचेष्टमरिंदमम्। बबन्धुश्शणवल्कैश्च द्रुमचीरैश्च संहतैः।।5.48.46।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, राक्षसाः the ogres, अरिंदमम् subduer of enemies, तम् him, निर्विचेष्टम् refrained from moving, दृष्टवा seeing, शणवल्कैश्च with hemp rope, संहतैः firmly knit together, द्रुमचीरैश्च  by bark robes, बबन्धु  tied

#### Explanation

Then the ogres had Hanuman, the subduer of enemies, who made no effort to move, bound with hemp rope and bark robes.

---

### Shloka 47

#### Shloka Text

स रोचयामास परैश्च बन्धनं प्रसह्य वीरैरभिनिग्रहं च। कौतूहलान्मां यदि राक्षसेन्द्रो द्रष्टुं व्यवस्येदिति निश्चितार्थः।।5.48.47।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसेन्द्रः demon king, माम् me, कौतूहलात् out of curiosity, द्रष्टुम् to see, व्यवस्येद्यदि if he decides, इति this way, निश्चितार्थः decided, सः he परै: by the enemies, बन्धनम bondage, वीरैः by warriors, प्रसह्य by force, अभिनिग्रहं च even capture also, रोचयामास enjoyed

#### Explanation

Bound by the enemy warriors, Hanuman, decided to bear all humiliations, thinking, 'May be the demon king might come to see me out of curiosity if he is so disposed'.

---

### Shloka 48

#### Shloka Text

स बद्धस्तेन वल्केन विमुक्तोऽस्त्रेण वीर्यवान्। अस्त्रबन्धः स चान्यं हि न बन्धमनुवर्तते।।5.48.48।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तेन by that, वल्केन with bark, बद्धः tied, वीर्यवान् valiant, सः he, अस्त्रेण by (Brahm) astra, विमुक्तः set free, सः that, अस्त्रबन्धः bondage of astra, अन्यम् other, बन्धम् bondage, न अनुवर्तते हि not tolerate

#### Explanation

On being bound by bark ropes Hanuman was set free from the bond of Brahmastra, since  the bondage of Brahmastra does not tolerate another ordinary bondage.

---

### Shloka 49

#### Shloka Text

अथेन्द्रजित्तु द्रुमचीरबद्धं विचार्य वीरः कपिसत्तमं तम्। विमुक्तमस्त्रेण जगाम चिन्तां नान्येन बद्धो ह्यनुवर्ततेऽस्त्रम्।।5.48.49।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथ and then, वीरः hero, इन्द्रजित्तु Indrajit, द्रुमचीरबद्धम् bound by  bark rope, तम् him, कपिसत्तमम् foremost of vanaras, अस्त्रेण by astra, विमुक्तम् released, विचार्य thinking, चिन्ताम् thought, जगाम started, अन्येन by other, बद्धः bound, अस्त्रम् astra, नानु वर्तते हि indeed does not follow.

#### Explanation

Then the heroic Indrajit coming to know that the foremost of the monkeys bound by bark ropes was released from the bondage of Brahmastra, thought, 'indeed the divine weapon does not tolerate other bonds, so the vanara is released'.

---

### Shloka 50

#### Shloka Text

अहो महत्कर्म कृतं निरर्थकम्कं न राक्षसैर्मन्त्रगतिर्विमृष्टा। पुनश्च मन्त्रे विहतेऽस्त्रमन्यत्प्रवर्तते संशयिता स्स्मसर्वे।।5.48.50।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहो Alas, महत् great, कर्म action, निरर्थकम् wasted, कृतम् done, राक्षसैः by the ogres, मन्त्रगतिः impact of mantra, न विमृष्टाः is not considered, मन्त्रे when mantra, विहते is ineffective, अन्यत् other, अस्त्रम् astra, न प्रवर्तते works, सर्वे all, संशयिताः our lives will be at risk, स्म us

#### Explanation

'Alas! my achievement has been wasted. The ogres had not considered the impact of the mantra of astra . Once the Brahmastra is ineffective another astra cannot be operative again. We are all running a risk.'

---

### Shloka 51

#### Shloka Text

अस्त्रेण हनुमान्मुक्तो नात्मानमवबुध्यत। कृष्यमाणस्तु रक्षोभि स्तौश्च बन्धैर्निपीडितः।।5.48.51।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अस्त्रेण by Astra, मुक्तः was released, हनुमान् Hanuman, तैः those, रक्षोभिः by the ogres, कृष्यमाणः dragged, बन्धै: was tied, निपीडितः hurting him, आत्मानम् himself, न अवबुध्यत he did not know

#### Explanation

Though binding and dragging by the ogres had hurt Hanuman, he on his part did not know that he had been released from Brahmastra.

---

### Shloka 52

#### Shloka Text

हन्यमानस्ततः क्रूरै राक्षसैः काष्ठमुष्टिभिः। समीपं राक्षसेन्द्रस्य प्राकृष्यत स वानरः।।5.48.52।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, सः वानरः that vanara, क्रूरैः by the cruel ones, राक्षसैः by rakshasas, काष्ठमुष्टिभिः  with sticks and fists, हन्यमानः  beaten, राक्षसेन्द्रस्य to the lord of rakshasas, समीपम् near, प्राकृष्यत was dragged

#### Explanation

The cruel ogres hit Hanuman with sticks and fists and dragged him to the presence of the demon lord.

---

### Shloka 53

#### Shloka Text

अथेन्द्रजित्तं प्रसमीक्ष्य मुक्तमस्त्रेण बद्धं द्रुमचीरसूत्रैः। न्यदर्शयत्तत्र महाबलं तं हरिप्रवीरं सगणाय राज्ञे।।5.48.53।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथ and then, द्रुमचीरसूत्रैः with bark ropes, बद्धम् bound, तम् him, अस्त्रेण by the astra, मुक्तम् released, प्रसमीक्ष्य seeing, इन्द्रजित् Indrajit, महाबलम् who had great power, तं हरिप्रवीरम् foremost monkey, तत्र there, सगणाय including the courtiers, राज्ञे king in the royal assembly, न्यदर्शयत् presented

#### Explanation

Then Indarjit, seeing the powerful Hanuman, the foremost of monkeys who was released from the Brahmastra, still bound by bark ropes, presented him to the ogres and their master in the royal assembly.

---

### Shloka 54

#### Shloka Text

तं मत्तमिव मातङ्गं बद्धं कपिवरोत्तमम्। राक्षसा राक्षसेन्द्राय रावणाय न्यवेदयन्।।5.48.54।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मत्तम् in rut, मातङ्गमिव elephant like, बद्धम् bound, कपिवरोत्तमम् best of monkeys, तम् him, राक्षसाः ogres, राक्षसेन्द्राय to the demon king, रावणाय to Ravana, न्यवेदयन् presented

#### Explanation

The ogres presented the best of monkeys, Hanuman looking like an elephant in rut to Ravana, the demon king.

---

### Shloka 55

#### Shloka Text

कोऽयं कस्य कुतोवात्र किं कार्यं को व्यपाश्रयः। इति राक्षसवीराणां तत्र सञ्जज्ञिरे कथाः।।5.48.55।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अयम् this, कः who is, कस्य his, कुतो वा  whose son is he, अत्र here, किम् why, कार्यम् task, व्यपाश्रयः to whom, कः what, इति this, तत्र there, राक्षसवीराणाम् heroic demons, कथाः talked about him, सञ्जज्ञिरे talked among themselves

#### Explanation

"Who is he? Whose son is he? Why has he come here? Where has he come from? What business does he have here?" Thus the heroic demons talked among themselves.

---

### Shloka 56

#### Shloka Text

हन्यतां दह्यतां वापि भक्ष्यतामिति चापरे। राक्षसास्तत्र सङ्क्रुद्धा: परस्परमथाब्रुवन्।।5.48.56।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथ and then, अपरे others, राक्षसाः rakshasas, सङ्कृद्धाः very angry, हन्यताम् kill him, दह्यतां चापि he may be burnt also, भक्ष्यताम् devoure, इति this way, परस्परम् one another, अब्रुवन् spoke

#### Explanation

Thereafter other ogres angrily said 'Let us kill him, burn him or otherwise devour him,' thus they spoke to one another.

---

### Shloka 57

#### Shloka Text

अतीत्य मार्गं सहसा महात्मा स तत्र रक्षोधिपपादमूले। ददर्श राज्ञः परिचारवृद्धान् गृहं महारत्नविभूषितं च।।5.48.57।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महात्मा great self, सः he, सहसा quickly, मार्गम् the path, अतीत्य after crossing, तत्र there, राज्ञः of the king, महारत्नविभूषितम् adorned with precious gems, गृहम् palace, रक्षोधिपपादमूले near the feet of the king, परिचारवृद्धान् the aged and experienced ministers of the king, ददर्श saw

#### Explanation

The great Hanuman moving quickly and crossing the path came near the feet of the king. He saw the aged and experienced ministers of the king and the palace of the king adorned with precious gems.

---

### Shloka 58

#### Shloka Text

स ददर्श महातेजा रावणः कपिसत्तमम्। रक्षोभिर्विकृताकारैः कृष्यमाणमितस्ततः।।5.48.58।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महातेजाः resplendent one, सः रावणः that Ravana, विकृताकारैः by ugly looking ones, रक्षोभिः by the rakshasas, इतस्ततः here and there, कृष्यमाणम् being dragged, कपिसत्तमम्  foremost monkey, ददर्श saw

#### Explanation

Resplendent Ravana saw  the ogres of ugly appearance dragging the foremost of the monkeys here and there.

---

### Shloka 59

#### Shloka Text

राक्षसाधिपतिं चापि ददर्श कपिसत्तमः। तेजोबलसमायुक्तं तपन्तमिव भास्करम्।।5.48.59।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कपिसत्तमः च and the best of monkeys, तेजोबलसमायुक्तम् one who had splendour and strength, तपन्तम् radiating brilliance, भास्करम् इव like the Sun, राक्षसाधिपतिम् king of demons, ददर्श saw

#### Explanation

Hanuman the best of monkeys also saw the demon king who had both splendour and strength, radiating brilliance like the Sun.

---

### Shloka 60

#### Shloka Text

सरोषसम्वर्तितताम्रदृष्टिर्दशाननस्तं कपिमन्ववेक्ष्य। अथोपविष्टान् कुलशीलवृद्धान् समादिशत्तं प्रति मन्त्रिमुख्यान्।।5.48.60।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः दशाननः that tenheaded one, रोषसम्वर्तितताम्रदृष्टिः  his red eyes rolling in  rage, तं कपिम् him the monkey, अन्ववेक्ष्य observing unwinkingly, अथ and then, उपविष्टान् seated, कुलशीलवृद्धान् noble and aged members of his clan, मन्त्रिमुख्यान् important ministers, तं प्रति with regard to him, समादिशत् ordered

#### Explanation

That tenheaded rakshasa king, rolling his red eyes rapidly in rage and observing the monkey unwinkingly ordered the noble and aged ministers of his clan to interrogate Hanuman.

---

### Shloka 61

#### Shloka Text

यथाक्रमं तैस्स कपिर्विपृष्टः कार्यार्थमर्धस्य च मूलमादौ। निवेदयामास हरीश्वरस्य दूतः सकाशादहमागतोऽस्मि।।5.48.61।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तैः by those, यथाक्रमम् in a proper order, कार्यार्थम् purpose, अर्धस्य of the purpose, मूलम् origin, विपृष्टः  questioned, सः कपिः that monkey, हरीश्वरस्य सकाशात्  a messsenger of the king of monkeys, सकाशात from his place, आगतः अस्मि I came,  निवेदयामास he reported

#### Explanation

At the very outset they questioned Hanuman manner in the usual about the place he came from and the purpose. Hanuman reported, 'I am Hanuman. I came as a messenger by the order of the king of monkeys'. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये सुन्दरकाण्डे अष्टचत्वारिंशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the fortyeigth sarga of Sundarakanda of the holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 49

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

ततस्स कर्मणा तस्य विस्मितो भीमविक्रमः। हनुमान्रोषताम्राक्षो रक्षोधिपमवैक्षत।।5.49.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, भीमविक्रमः  of fierce valour, सः हनुमान् that Hanuman, तस्य his, तेन कर्मणा by that act, विस्मितः astonished, रोषताम्राक्षः eyes red with anger, रक्षोधिपम् demon king, अवैक्षत beheld.

#### Explanation

The highly courageous Hanuman, astonished at  the  action (of binding and dragging him to the court) by the demon king looked at him with eyes turned red in anger.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

भ्राजमानं महार्हेण काञ्चनेन विराजता। मुक्ताजालावृतेनाथ मकुटेन महाद्युतिम्।।5.49.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महार्हेण of innate splendour, काञ्चनेन with a golden, विराजता shining, अथ and, मुक्ताजालावृतेन covered with strings of pearls, मकुटेन with crown, भ्राजमानम् shining, महाद्युतिम् glittering.

#### Explanation

(Hanuman looked at Ravana) of innate splendour, who shone with a glittering crown of gold glowing, encircled with strings of pearls.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

वज्रसंयोगसंयुक्तैर्महार्हमणिविग्रहैः। हैमैराभरणैश्चित्रैर्मनसेव प्रकल्पितैः।।5.49.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वज्रसंयोगसंयुक्तै: studded with diamonds, महार्हमणिविग्रहैः adorned with small motifs made of precious gems, मनसा by imagination, प्रकल्पितैरिव as if created, चित्रैः wonderful, हैमैः golden, आभरणैः with ornaments.

#### Explanation

He was decked with wonderful golden ornaments studded with diamonds and had small motifs of precious gems fixed to them. They were as though designed by imagination. (An ornament made with the hand connot be so fine and delicate.)

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

महार्हक्षौमसंवीतं रक्तचन्दनरूषितम्। स्वानुलिप्तं विचित्राभिर्विविधाभिश्च भक्तिभिः।।5.49.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महार्हक्षौमसंवीतम् dressed in exquisite silk, रक्तचन्दनरूषितम् smeared with red sandal paste, विचित्राभिः with wonderful, विविधाभिश्च with many kinds, भक्तिभिः with ornamental designs drawn, स्वानुलिप्तम् smeared with unguents.

#### Explanation

He was dressed in exquisite silk and smeared with red sandal paste and had many ornamental designs drawn on his body with fragrant unguents.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

विचित्रैर्दर्शनीयैश्च रक्ताक्षैर्भीमदर्शनैः। दीप्ततीक्ष्णमहादंष्ट्रैः प्रलम्बदशनच्छदैः।।5.49.5।। शिरोभिर्दशभिर्वीरं भ्राजमानं महौजसम्। नानाव्यालसमाकीर्णैश्शिखरैरिव मन्दरम्।।5.49.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दर्शनीयैः appeared, रक्ताक्षैः with bloodred eyes, भीमदर्शनैः with terrible looks, दीप्ततीक्ष्णमहादंष्ट्रैः with shining sharp big teeth, प्रलम्बदशनच्छदैः with drooping lips, दशभिः with ten, शिरोभिः with heads, विचित्रैः wonderful, नानाव्यालसमाकीर्णैः with different kinds of beasts, शिखरैः with peaks, मन्दरम् इव like Mandara mountain, भ्राजमानं splendid, महौजसम् lustrous, वीरम् hero.

#### Explanation

He appeared terrible with bloodred eyes, shining sharp big teeth, drooping lips and ten heads, which looked like ten peaks of mountain Mandara inhabited by various beasts. He was heroic, powerful and splendid.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

विचित्रैर्दर्शनीयैश्च रक्ताक्षैर्भीमदर्शनैः। दीप्ततीक्ष्णमहादंष्ट्रैः प्रलम्बदशनच्छदैः।।5.49.5।। शिरोभिर्दशभिर्वीरं भ्राजमानं महौजसम्। नानाव्यालसमाकीर्णैश्शिखरैरिव मन्दरम्।।5.49.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दर्शनीयैः appeared, रक्ताक्षैः with bloodred eyes, भीमदर्शनैः with terrible looks, दीप्ततीक्ष्णमहादंष्ट्रैः with shining sharp big teeth, प्रलम्बदशनच्छदैः with drooping lips, दशभिः with ten, शिरोभिः with heads, विचित्रैः wonderful, नानाव्यालसमाकीर्णैः with different kinds of beasts, शिखरैः with peaks, मन्दरम् इव like Mandara mountain, भ्राजमानं splendid, महौजसम् lustrous, वीरम् hero.

#### Explanation

He appeared terrible with bloodred eyes, shining sharp big teeth, drooping lips and ten heads, which looked like ten peaks of mountain Mandara inhabited by various beasts. He was heroic, powerful and splendid.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

नीलाञ्जनचयप्रख्यं हारेणोरसि राजता। पूर्णचन्द्राभवक्त्रेण सबलाकमिवाम्बुदम्।।5.49.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नीलाञ्जनचयप्रख्यम् like a black mountain of collyrium, उरसि on the chest, राजता by the illumining, हारेण with a pearl necklace, पूर्णचन्द्राभवक्त्रेण with a face like the fullmoon, बलाकम् with cranes around, अम्बुदमिव like a cloud.

#### Explanation

He was like a black mountain of collyrium, and with a face like the fullmoon. With a pearl necklace illumining his chest he appeared like a cloud lit up by the fullmoon with white cranes flying across. (The white pearlnecklace moving on his chest appeared like a row of cranes flying through the black cloud.)

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

बाहुभिर्बद्धकेयूरैश्चन्दनोत्तमरूषितैः। भ्राजमानाङ्गदैः पीनैः पञ्चशीर्षैरिवोरगैः।।5.49.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

बद्धकेयूरैः wearing armlets, चन्दनोत्तमरूषितैः smeared with choicest fragrants of sandal, भ्राजमानाङ्गदैः with shining, पीनैः stout ones, पञ्चशीर्षैः five headed, उरगैरिव like serpents, बाहुभिः with arms.

#### Explanation

Adorned with armlets (keyura), smeared with choicest sandal paste, his shining, stout armlets appeared like many fivehooded serpents (fingers appearing like hoods).

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

महतिस्फाटिके चित्रे रत्नसंयोगसंस्कृते। उत्तमास्तरणास्तीर्णे सूपविष्टं वरासने।।5.49.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रत्नसंयोगसंस्कृते encrusted with precious stones, चित्रे a wonderful one, उत्तमास्तरणास्तीर्णे covered with beautiful carpets, स्फाटिके with crystals, महति huge one, वरासने a magnificent throne, सूपविष्टम् well seated.

#### Explanation

(He was) seated on a huge magnificent throne of crystal encrusted with precious stones and overspread with a wonderful carpet.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

अलङ्कृताभिरत्यर्थं प्रमदाभिः समन्ततः। वालव्यजनहस्ताभिरारात्समुपसेवितम्।।5.49.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अत्यर्थम् exceedingly well, अलङ्कृताभिः with decorated ones, वालव्यजनहस्ताभिः holding whisks in their hands, प्रमदाभिः with beautiful girls, समन्ततः all over, आरात् the vicinity, समुपसेवितम्  attended on him.

#### Explanation

Young and beautiful girls decorated exceedingly well holding whisks all over the vicinity attended on him.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

दुर्धरेण प्रहस्तेन महापार्श्वेन रक्षसा। मन्त्रिभिर्मन्त्रतत्त्वज्ञैर्निकुम्भेन च मन्त्रिणा।।5.49.11।। सुखोपविष्टं रक्षोभिश्चतुर्भिर्बलदर्पितैः। कृत्स्नं परिवृतं लोकं चतुर्भिरिव सागरैः।।5.49.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दुर्धरेण by Durdhara, प्रहस्तेन by Prahasta, रक्षसा by the ogres, महापार्श्वेन by Mahaparsva, मन्त्रिणा by the ministers, निकुम्भेन by Nikumbha, मन्त्रतत्त्वज्ञैः with learned ones, मन्त्रिभिः with ministers, बलदर्पितैः with arrogant ones, चतुर्भिः with four, रक्षोभिः with demons, सुखोपविष्टम् comfortably seated, चतुर्भिः with four, सागरैः with oceans, परिवृतम् surrounded, कृत्स्नम् entire, लोकम् इव like the world.

#### Explanation

He was attended by Durdhara, Prahasta, Mahaparsva and Nikumbha who were his learned ministers. Seated comfortably and attended by the four arrogant ministers, he was puffed with pride of their strength and appeared like the entire world surrounded by four oceans.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

दुर्धरेण प्रहस्तेन महापार्श्वेन रक्षसा। मन्त्रिभिर्मन्त्रतत्त्वज्ञैर्निकुम्भेन च मन्त्रिणा।।5.49.11।। सुखोपविष्टं रक्षोभिश्चतुर्भिर्बलदर्पितैः। कृत्स्नं परिवृतं लोकं चतुर्भिरिव सागरैः।।5.49.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दुर्धरेण by Durdhara, प्रहस्तेन by Prahasta, रक्षसा by the ogres, महापार्श्वेन by Mahaparsva, मन्त्रिणा by the ministers, निकुम्भेन by Nikumbha, मन्त्रतत्त्वज्ञैः with learned ones, मन्त्रिभिः with ministers, बलदर्पितैः with arrogant ones, चतुर्भिः with four, रक्षोभिः with demons, सुखोपविष्टम् comfortably seated, चतुर्भिः with four, सागरैः with oceans, परिवृतम् surrounded, कृत्स्नम् entire, लोकम् इव like the world.

#### Explanation

He was attended by Durdhara, Prahasta, Mahaparsva and Nikumbha who were his learned ministers. Seated comfortably and attended by the four arrogant ministers, he was puffed with pride of their strength and appeared like the entire world surrounded by four oceans.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

मन्त्रिभिर्मन्त्रतत्त्वज्ञैरन्यैश्च शुभबुद्धिभिः। अन्वास्यमानं रक्षोभिः सुरैरिव सुरेश्वरम्।।5.49.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मन्त्रतत्त्वज्ञैः by learned ones, शुभबुद्धिभिः by the intellectuals, मन्त्रिभिः with ministers, अन्यैः with others, रक्षोभिः with ogres, सुरैः with suras, सुरेश्वरम् इव like Indra, अन्वास्यमानम् attended him.

#### Explanation

He was attended by ogres including ministers who were intellectuals and adept in counselling. He appeared like lord Indra attended by the gods.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

अपश्यद्राक्षसपतिं हनुमानतितेजसम्। विष्ठितं मेरुशिखरे सतोयमिव तोयदम्।।5.49.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हनुमान् Hanuman, अतितेजसम् highly resplendent, मेरुशिखरे on the peak of mount Meru, विष्ठितम् seated, सतोयम् laden with water, तोयदम् इव like cloud, राक्षसपतिम् demon king, अपश्यत् saw.

#### Explanation

Hanuman saw the highly resplendent giant king (seated on the throne) who appeared like a cloud laden with water appearing on the peak of mount Meru.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

स तैस्सम्पीड्यमानोऽपि रक्षोभिर्भीमविक्रमैः। विस्मयं परमं गत्वा रक्षोधिपमवैक्षत।।5.49.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः he, भीमविक्रमैः by those of fierce valour, रक्षोभिः by ogres, सम्पीड्यमानोऽपि even though pressed hard, परमम् great, विस्मयम् wonder, गत्वा experiencing, रक्षोधिपम् king of demons, अवैक्षत saw.

#### Explanation

Hanuman looked at the demon king with great amazement even while he was dealt harshly by the demons of fierce valour.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

भ्राजमानं ततो दृष्ट्वा हनुमान्राक्षसेश्वरम्। मनसा चिन्तयामास तेजसा तस्य मोहितः।।5.49.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, हनुमान् Hanuman, भ्राजमानम् splendid, राक्षसेश्वरम् lord of ogres, दृष्ट्वा after seeing, तस्य his, तेजसा by splendour, मोहितः dismayed, मनसा in mind, चिन्तयामास started thinking.

#### Explanation

Seeing the splendour of the lord of demons, Hanuman was dimayed and started thinking thus:

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

अहो रूपमहो धैर्यमहो सत्त्वमहो द्युतिः। अहो राक्षसराजस्य सर्वलक्षणयुक्तता।।5.49.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसराजस्य Ravana's, रूपम् form, अहो oh, wonderful, धैर्यम् courage, अहो amazing, सत्त्वम् power, अहो is great, द्युतिः glow, अहो great, सर्वलक्षणयुक्तता endowment of all merits, अहो how great.

#### Explanation

'Oh what charm what courage what strength what splendour How great is this demonking, an endowment of all merits.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

यद्यधर्मो न बलवान् स्यादयं राक्षसेश्वरः। स्यादयं सुरलोकस्य सशक्रस्यापि रक्षिता।।5.49.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अयम् this, अधर्मः unrighteousness, बलवान् powerful, न स्याद्यदि if it was not, अयम् this, राक्षसेश्वरः lord of demons, सशक्रस्य including Indra, सुरलोकस्यापि even of the world of gods, रक्षिता lord, स्यात् would become.

#### Explanation

'If only this lord of demons was not unrighteous, he could have become even the lord (protector) of gods including Indra.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

अस्य क्रूरैर्नृशंसैश्च कर्मभिर्लोककुत्सितैः। सर्वे बिभ्यति खल्वस्माल्लोकास्सामरदानवाः।।5.49.19।। अयं ह्युत्सहते क्रुद्धः कर्तुमेकार्णवं जगत्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

क्रूरैः with cruel, नृशंसैश्च wicked, लोककुत्सितैः contemptible to the people, अस्य कर्मभिः by his deeds, सामरदानवाः even the demons and gods, सर्वे all, लोकाः people of the world, अस्मात् from his, बिभ्यति हि get sacred, अयम् this person, क्रुद्धः becomes angry, जगत् the world, एकार्णवम् one ocean, कर्तुम् to render, उत्सहते हि indeed he will intend.

#### Explanation

'This Ravana has done many cruel, fierce and contemptible deeds. That is why even the demons, gods and all the people of this world get scared of him. He will render the entire world into a single ocean when he turns angry'.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

इति चिन्तां बहुविधामकरोन्मतिमान् हरिः।।5.49.20।। दृष्ट्वा राक्षसराजस्य प्रभावममितौजसः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अमितौजसः brilliant, राक्षसराजस्य Ravana's, प्रभावम् power, दृष्ट्वा after seeing, मतिमान् intelligent, हरिः Hanuman, इति in this way, बहुविधाम् many ways, चिन्ताम् thought, अकरोत् entertained.

#### Explanation

Many such thoughts crossed Hanuman's intelligent mind on seeing Ravana's brilliance and power. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये सुन्दरकाण्डे एकोनपञ्चाशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the fortyninth sarga of Sundarakanda of the holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 50

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

तमुद्वीक्ष्य महाबाहुः पिङ्गाक्षं पुरत स्थितम्। कोपेन महताऽविष्टो रावणो लोकरावणः।।5.50.1।। शङ्काहृतात्मा दध्यौ स कपीन्द्रं तेजसावृतम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाबाहुः mightyarmed, लोकरावणः who torments all worlds, सः रावणः that Ravana, पुरतः in front, स्थितम् stood, तं पिङ्गाक्षम् that tawnyeyed one, उद्वीक्ष्य after looking at, महता with great, कोपेन in anger, आविष्टः overpowered, शङ्काहृतात्मा doubting at heart, तेजसा with brilliance, आवृतम् surrounded, कपीन्द्रम् lord of monkeys, दध्यौ thought over.

#### Explanation

The mighty armed Ravana, a tormentor of all worlds, overcome with anger beheld the tawnyeyed brilliant lord of monkeys who stood before him and started thinking in his mind full of apprehensions.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

किमेष भगवान्नन्दी भवेत्साक्षादिहागतः।।5.50.2।। येन शप्तोऽस्मि कैलासे मया सञ्चालिते पुरा। सोऽयं वानरमूर्तिस्स्यात्किंस्विद्बाणो महाऽसुरः।।5.50.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एषः he who, पुरा earlier, मया by me, कैलासे when Kailasa, सञ्चालिते when shaken, येन by that, शप्तः अस्मि I was, भगवान् lord, साक्षात् personally, इह here, आगतः has come, नन्दी Nandi, किं भवेत् could he be?, सः अयम् ths same, वानरमूर्तिः in vanara form, महासुरः great demon, बाणः Bana, स्यात् किं स्वित् could be himself.

#### Explanation

'Is he Lord Nandi who came here in person? I was earlier cursed by him when I shook the mount Kailasa in the past. Could he have assumed a monkeyform or may be he is the great demon Bana. (Nandi is the bull, the vehicle of Lord Siva).

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

किमेष भगवान्नन्दी भवेत्साक्षादिहागतः।।5.50.2।। येन शप्तोऽस्मि कैलासे मया सञ्चालिते पुरा। सोऽयं वानरमूर्तिस्स्यात्किंस्विद्बाणो महाऽसुरः।।5.50.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एषः he who, पुरा earlier, मया by me, कैलासे when Kailasa, सञ्चालिते when shaken, येन by that, शप्तः अस्मि I was, भगवान् lord, साक्षात् personally, इह here, आगतः has come, नन्दी Nandi, किं भवेत् could he be?, सः अयम् ths same, वानरमूर्तिः in vanara form, महासुरः great demon, बाणः Bana, स्यात् किं स्वित् could be himself.

#### Explanation

'Is he Lord Nandi who came here in person? I was earlier cursed by him when I shook the mount Kailasa in the past. Could he have assumed a monkeyform or may be he is the great demon Bana. (Nandi is the bull, the vehicle of Lord Siva).

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

स राजा रोषताम्राक्षः प्रहस्तं मन्त्रिसत्तमम्। कालयुक्तमुवाचेदं वचोऽविपुलमर्थवत्।।5.50.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः राजा that king, रोषताम्राक्षः eyes red in anger, मन्त्रिसत्तमम् esteemed minister, प्रहस्तम् Prahasta, कालयुक्तम् a timely word, अर्थवत् profound, अविपुलम् brief, इदं वचः this word, उवाच spoke.

#### Explanation

The infuriated demon king told the great minister Prahasta, these  profound words in a brief manner appropriate to the hour.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

दुरात्मा पृच्छ्यतामेष कुतः किं वात्र कारणम्। वनभङ्गे च कोऽस्यार्थो राक्षसीनां च तर्जने।।5.50.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एषः he, दुरात्मा wicked one, पृच्छ्यताम् enquire, कुतः from which place, अत्र here, किं कारणम् for what reason, वनभङ्गे च in destroyed the garden, राक्षसीनाम् of shedemons, तर्जने threatened, अस्य its, अर्थः purpose, कः what?

#### Explanation

"Enquire from this wicked one, why he has come and from where, Why he destroyed the garden and for what purpose he threatened the demonesses?

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

मत्पुरीमप्रधृष्यां वाऽगमने किं प्रयोजनम्। आयोधने वा किं कार्यं पृच्छयतामेष दुर्मतिः।।5.50.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अप्रधृष्याम् inviolable, मत्पुरीम् my state, आगमने in arriving, किं प्रयोजनम् for what purpose, आयोधने वा or to wage war, किं कार्यम् for what purpose, एषः दुर्मतिः this wicked one, पृच्छ्यताम् enquire.

#### Explanation

"Enquire why this wicked one entered this inviolable city and for what purpose did he wage a war"

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

रावणस्य वचश्श्रुत्वा प्रहस्तो वाक्यमब्रवीत्। समाश्वसिहि भद्रं ते न भीः कार्या त्वया कपे।।5.50.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

प्रहस्तः Prahasta, रावणस्य Ravana's, वचः words, श्रुत्वा on hearing, वाक्यम् words, अब्रवीत् spoke, कपे O monkey, समाश्वसिहि be relaxed, ते भद्रम् feel safe, त्वया you, भीः न कार्या need not entertain fear.

#### Explanation

On hearing Ravana, Prahasta spoke these words boosting the monkey's morale 'O monkey Be relaxed. Feel safe. You need not entertain any fear.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

यदि तावत्त्वमिन्द्रेण प्रेषितो रावणालयम्। तत्त्वमाख्याहि मा भूत्ते भयं वानर मोक्ष्यसे।।5.50.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वानर O vanara, त्वम् you, इन्द्रेण by Indra, रावणालयम् to the abode of Ravana, प्रेषितः यदि तावत् if you are sent, तत्त्वम् truth, आख्याहि you may tell, ते to you, भयम् माभूत् be not afraid, मोक्ष्यसे you will be released.

#### Explanation

"Have you came to the abode of Ravana sent by Indra? Speak the truth. Be not afraid.You will be released".

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

यदि वैश्रवणस्य त्वं यमस्य वरुणस्य च। चाररूपमिदं कृत्वा प्रविष्टो नः पुरीमिमाम्।।5.50.9।। विष्णुना प्रेषितो वाऽपि दूतो विजयकाङ्क्षिणा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इदम् this, चाररूपम् charming appearance, कृत्वा after assuming, नः our, इमां पुरीम् this city, प्रविष्टः entered, त्वम् you, वैश्रवणस्य Vaisravana's (Kubera's), यमस्य Yama's, वरुणस्य च Varuna's and, विजयकाङ्क्षिणा with a desire to win, विष्णुना  by Visnu, दूतः messenger, प्रेषितो वापि sent perhaps.

#### Explanation

None

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

न हि ते वानरं तेजो रूपमात्रं तु वानरम्।।5.50.10।। तत्त्वत: कथयस्वाद्य ततो वानर मोक्ष्यसे।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वानर vanara, ते your, रूपमात्रं तु only your appearance, वानरम् is of a vanara, तेजः glow, वानरम् of a vanara, न हि not indeed, अद्य this day, तत्त्वतः truth, कथयस्व you may speak, ततः then, मोक्ष्यसे you will be set free.

#### Explanation

"Your appearance is only of that of a vanara. But your glow is not of a vanara indeed. Tell the truth today. You will be set free.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

अनृतं वदतश्चापि दुर्लभं तव जीवितम्।।5.50.11।। अथवा यन्निमित्तस्ते प्रवेशो रावणालये।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तव your, अनृतम् false, वदतः while you speak, जीवितम् life, दुर्लभम् difficult, अथवा or else, रावणालये in the abode of Ravana, ते प्रवेशः your entry, यन्निमित्तः for waht reason.

#### Explanation

"If you tell a lie, it will be difficult for you to live. Or else, tell me for what reason have you entered the abode of Ravana.'

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्तो हरिश्रेष्ठस्तदा रक्षोगणेश्वरम्।।5.50.12।। अब्रवीन्नास्मि शक्रस्य यमस्य वरुणस्य वा। धनदेन न मे सख्यं विष्णुना नास्मि चोदितः।।5.50.13।। जातिरेव मम त्वेषा वानरोऽहमिहागतः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एवम् that way, उक्तः having been addressed, हरिश्रेष्ठः foremost of monkeys, तदा then, रक्षोगणेश्वरम् to the lord of the demon clan, अब्रवीत् said, शक्रस्य of Indra, यमस्य Yama's, वरुणस्य Varuna's, नास्मि I am not (a messenger), मे to me, धनदेन with Kubera, सख्यम् friendship, न not, विष्णुना by Visnu, चोदितः sent (prompted), न not, एषा this way, मम my, जातिरेव birth itself, अहम् I am, वानरः vanara, इह here, आगतः come.

#### Explanation

The foremost of the vanaras spoke to the lord of ogres in response to the equiries made to him: "I have not come from Indra or Yama or Varuna. I have no friendship with Kubera. I have not been sent by Visnu. By birth I am vanara and I have come here.'

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्तो हरिश्रेष्ठस्तदा रक्षोगणेश्वरम्।।5.50.12।। अब्रवीन्नास्मि शक्रस्य यमस्य वरुणस्य वा। धनदेन न मे सख्यं विष्णुना नास्मि चोदितः।।5.50.13।। जातिरेव मम त्वेषा वानरोऽहमिहागतः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एवम् that way, उक्तः having been addressed, हरिश्रेष्ठः foremost of monkeys, तदा then, रक्षोगणेश्वरम् to the lord of the demon clan, अब्रवीत् said, शक्रस्य of Indra, यमस्य Yama's, वरुणस्य Varuna's, नास्मि I am not (a messenger), मे to me, धनदेन with Kubera, सख्यम् friendship, न not, विष्णुना by Visnu, चोदितः sent (prompted), न not, एषा this way, मम my, जातिरेव birth itself, अहम् I am, वानरः vanara, इह here, आगतः come.

#### Explanation

The foremost of the vanaras spoke to the lord of ogres in response to the equiries made to him: "I have not come from Indra or Yama or Varuna. I have no friendship with Kubera. I have not been sent by Visnu. By birth I am vanara and I have come here.'

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

दर्शने राक्षसेन्द्रस्य दुर्लभे तदिदं मया।।5.50.14।। वनं राक्षसराजस्य दर्शनार्थे विनाशितम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसेन्द्रस्य of the lord of ogres, दर्शने to see, दुर्लभे which is difficult, मया by me, तत् that, इदम् this, वनम् garden, राक्षसराजस्य demon king's, दर्शनार्थे in order to see, विनाशितम् to destroy.

#### Explanation

None

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

ततस्ते राक्षसाः प्राप्ता बलिनो युद्धकाङ्क्षिणः। रक्षणार्थं तु देहस्य प्रतियुद्धा मया रणे।।5.50.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, बलिनः powerful, ते राक्षसाः those ogres, युद्धकाङ्क्षिणः desiring to fight, प्राप्ताः came, देहस्य of body, रक्षणार्थं तु to protect, मया by me, रणे in the combat, प्रतियुद्धाः in return I fought.

#### Explanation

"Desiring to fight with me the powerful ogres came. Then I fought in self defence.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

अस्त्रपाशैर्न शक्योऽहं बद्धुं देवासुरैरपि।।5.50.16।। पितामहादेष वरो मामाप्येषोऽभ्युपागतः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I, देवसुरैरपि even by suras and asuras, अस्त्रपाशैः by bondage of astras, बद्धुम् to bind, न शक्यः not possible, एषः वरः such a boon, ममापि to me also, पितामहादेव only from grand father Brahma, अभ्युपागतः it has been granted me.

#### Explanation

"It is not possible to bind me with any bondage of astras even by devas or danavas. Such a boon has been granted to me by Grandfather Brahma.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

राजानं द्रष्टुकामेन मयास्त्रमनुवर्तितम्।।5.50.17।। विमुक्तो ह्यहमस्त्रेण राक्षसैस्त्वभिपीडितः। केनचिद्राजकार्येण सम्प्राप्तोऽस्मि तवान्तिकम्।।5.50.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राजानम् king, द्रष्टुकामेन to see, मया by me, अस्त्रम् this astra, अनुवर्तितम् is honoured, राक्षसैः by ogres, अभिपीडितः तु even pressed, अहम् I, अस्त्रेण by astra, विमुक्तो हि though released, केनचित् by some mission, राजकार्येण pertaining to the king, तव to your, अन्तिकम् presence, सम्प्राप्तः अस्मि have come.

#### Explanation

"Wishing to see the king I honoured this astra and not otherwise. Even though I was tormented by the ogres and released by the astra I submitted to you as I have a small work assigned by the king.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

राजानं द्रष्टुकामेन मयास्त्रमनुवर्तितम्।।5.50.17।। विमुक्तो ह्यहमस्त्रेण राक्षसैस्त्वभिपीडितः। केनचिद्राजकार्येण सम्प्राप्तोऽस्मि तवान्तिकम्।।5.50.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राजानम् king, द्रष्टुकामेन to see, मया by me, अस्त्रम् this astra, अनुवर्तितम् is honoured, राक्षसैः by ogres, अभिपीडितः तु even pressed, अहम् I, अस्त्रेण by astra, विमुक्तो हि though released, केनचित् by some mission, राजकार्येण pertaining to the king, तव to your, अन्तिकम् presence, सम्प्राप्तः अस्मि have come.

#### Explanation

"Wishing to see the king I honoured this astra and not otherwise. Even though I was tormented by the ogres and released by the astra I submitted to you as I have a small work assigned by the king.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

दूतोऽहमिति विज्ञेयो राघवस्यामितौजसः। श्रूयतां चापि वचनं मम पथ्यमिदं प्रभो।।5.50.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I, अमितौजसः of highly powerful, राघवस्य Rama's, दूतः इति messenger thus, विज्ञेयः it may be known, प्रभो O lord, पथ्यम् good, इदम् this, मम वचनम् my word, श्रूयतां चापि listen.

#### Explanation

Know that I am a messenger of the highly powerful Rama. O lord please listen to these beneficial words of mine meant for your welfare. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये सुन्दरकाण्डे पञ्चाशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the fiftieth sarga of Sundarakanda of the holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 51

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

तं समीक्ष्य महासत्त्वं सत्त्ववान्हरिसत्तमः। वाक्यमर्थवदव्यग्रस्तमुवाच दशाननम्।।5.51.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सत्त्ववान् courageous, हरिसत्तमः best of monkeys, महासत्त्वम् very powerful, तं दशाननम् the tenheaded, समीक्ष्य after observing, अव्यग्रम् slowly, अर्थवत् meaningful, वाक्यम् these words, तत् such, उवाच spoke.

#### Explanation

Hanuman, the best of monkeys, looking at the very powerful Ravana spoke slowly  these highly meaningful words.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

अहं सुग्रीवसंदेशादिह प्राप्तस्तवालयम्। राक्षसेन्द्र हरीशस्त्वां भ्राता कुशलमब्रवीत्।।5.51.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसेन्द्र O lord of demons, अहम् I am, सुग्रीवसंदेशात् by the orders of Sugriva, इह here, तव आलयम् your realm, प्राप्तः reached, भ्राता brotherlike, हरीशः lord of monkeys, त्वाम् to you, कुशलम् good wishes, अब्रवीत् spoke.

#### Explanation

"O lord of demons I reached your realm here by the orders of Sugriva, lord of monkeys who is like your brother. He conveys his good wishes to you.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

भ्रातुश्शृणु समादेशं सुग्रीवस्य महात्मनः। धर्मार्थोपहितं वाक्यमिह चामुत्र च क्षमम्।।5.51.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महात्मनः the great self, भ्रातुः brother, सुग्रीवस्य Sugriva's, समादेशम् message, इह च pertinent to this world and, अमुत्र च in the other world, क्षमम् that which is beneficial, धर्मार्थोपहितम् consistent with righteousness and propriety, वाक्यम् words, शृणु you may listen.

#### Explanation

"The great Sugriva who is like your brother sends you a message pertinent to this world and the other world, that which is beneficial and consistent with righteousness and propriety (धर्म and अर्थ). Listen:

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

राजा दशरथो नाम रथकुञ्जरवाजिमान्। पितेव बन्धुर्लोकस्य सुरेश्वरसमद्युतिः।।5.51.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रथकुञ्जरवाजिमान् who was richly endowed with chariots, horses and elephants, बन्धु: friend, लोकस्य of this world, पितेव like a father, सुरेश्वरसमद्युतिः equal to Indra in splendour, दशरथो नाम named Dasaratha, राजा emperor.

#### Explanation

"There was an emperor named Dasaratha richly endowed with chariots, horses and elephants. He was a friend to the world and was like a father and equal to Indra in splendour.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

ज्येष्ठस्तस्य महाबाहुः पुत्रः प्रियकरः प्रभुः। पितुर्निदेशान्निष्क्रान्तः प्रविष्टो दण्डकावनम्।।5.51.5।। लक्ष्मणेन सह भ्रात्रा सीतया चापि भार्यया। रामो नाम महातेजा धर्म्यं पन्थानमास्थितः।।5.51.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तस्य his, ज्येष्ठः पुत्रः eldest son, महाबाहुः strongarmed, प्रियकरः very dear, प्रभुः a lord, महातेजाः highly effulgent one, रामो नाम named Rama, धर्म्यम् righteous, पन्थानम् आस्थितः has entered a path, पितुः father's, निर्देशात् by order, निष्क्रान्तः went in exile, भ्रात्रा by his brother, लक्ष्मणेन with Lakshmana, भार्यया with his wife, सीतया चापि by Sita also, दण्डकावनम् Dandaka forest, प्रविष्टः entered.

#### Explanation

"His eldest son, a highly effulgent one, named Rama who was dear to the king, accepting his father's orders went into exile with brother Lakshmana and wife Sita also accepting a righteous path and entered Dandaka forest.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

ज्येष्ठस्तस्य महाबाहुः पुत्रः प्रियकरः प्रभुः। पितुर्निदेशान्निष्क्रान्तः प्रविष्टो दण्डकावनम्।।5.51.5।। लक्ष्मणेन सह भ्रात्रा सीतया चापि भार्यया। रामो नाम महातेजा धर्म्यं पन्थानमास्थितः।।5.51.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तस्य his, ज्येष्ठः पुत्रः eldest son, महाबाहुः strongarmed, प्रियकरः very dear, प्रभुः a lord, महातेजाः highly effulgent one, रामो नाम named Rama, धर्म्यम् righteous, पन्थानम् आस्थितः has entered a path, पितुः father's, निर्देशात् by order, निष्क्रान्तः went in exile, भ्रात्रा by his brother, लक्ष्मणेन with Lakshmana, भार्यया with his wife, सीतया चापि by Sita also, दण्डकावनम् Dandaka forest, प्रविष्टः entered.

#### Explanation

"His eldest son, a highly effulgent one, named Rama who was dear to the king, accepting his father's orders went into exile with brother Lakshmana and wife Sita also accepting a righteous path and entered Dandaka forest.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

तस्य भार्या वने नष्टा सीता पतिमनुव्रता। वैदेहस्य सुता राज्ञो जनकस्य महात्मनः।।5.51.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वैदेहस्य of Videha, महात्मनः  of the great self, जनकस्य Janka's, राज्ञः  king's, सुता daughter, पतिम् husband, अनुव्रता follwing, तस्य his, भार्या wife, सीता Sita, वने in the forest, नष्टा lost.

#### Explanation

"Sita, daughter of king Janaka of Videha kingdom, loyally followed her husband, Rama. She was lost in the forest.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

स मार्गमाणस्तां देवीं राजपुत्रः सहानुजः। ऋश्यमूकमनुप्राप्त: सुग्रीवेण समागतः।।5.51.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तां देवीम् that divine lady, मार्गमाणः searching, सहानुजः along with his brother, सः राजपुत्रः that prince, ऋश्यमूकम् to Rshyamuka, अनुप्राप्तः reached, सुग्रीवेण with Sugriva, समागतः united.

#### Explanation

"Searching for the divine lady the prince reached mount Rshyamuka along with his brother and there he met Sugriva.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

तस्य तेन प्रतिज्ञातं सीतायाः परिमार्गणम्। सुग्रीवस्यापि रामेण हरिराज्यं निवेदितम्।।5.51.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तेन by him, तस्य his, सीतायाः Sita's, परिमार्गणम् to search, प्रतिज्ञातम् is promised, रामेणापि even by Rama also, सुग्रीवस्य Sugriva's, हरिराज्यम् kingdom of monkeys, निवेदितम् assured.

#### Explanation

"Sugriva promised Rama to undertake search for Sita and Rama assured Sugriva of securing him the kingdom of the monkeys.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

ततस्तेन मृधे हत्वा राजपुत्रेण वालिनम्। सुग्रीवः स्थापितो राज्ये हर्यृक्षाणां गणेश्वरः।।5.51.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, तेन by him, राजपुत्रेण by the prince, वालिनम् Vali, मृधे in battle, हत्वा after killing, सुग्रीवः Sugriva, हर्यृऋक्षाणाम् kingdom of apes and bears, गणेश्वरः lord of the troops, राज्ये in kingdom, स्थापितः was established.

#### Explanation

"Then prince Rama killed Vali in battle and made Sugriva, king of the vanaras and bears and restored the kingship of Sugriva.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

त्वया विज्ञातपूर्वश्च वाली वानरपुङ्गवः। रामेण निहतस्सङ्ख्ये शरेणैकेन वानरः।।5.51.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वानरपुङ्गवः chief of vanaras, वाली Vali, त्वया by you, विज्ञातपूर्वश्च is known earlier, वानरः vanara, रामेण by Rama, सङ्ख्ये in a combat, एकेन by only one, शरेण with an arrow, निहतः is killed.

#### Explanation

"You know of Vali, chief of the vanaras, in the past. Rama killed him with a single arrow in the combat.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

स सीतामार्गणे व्यग्रस्सुग्रीवः सत्यसङ्गरः। हरीन् सम्प्रेषयामास दिशस्सर्वा हरीश्वरः।।5.51.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सत्यसङ्गरः one who is a faithful warrior, हरीश्वरः  king of monkeys, सः सुग्रीवः that Sugriva, सीतामार्गणे in searching Sita, व्यग्रः was anxious, सर्वाः all, दिशः directions, हरीन् vanaras, सम्प्रेषयामास sent.

#### Explanation

"Sugriva, who is true to his word and a faithful warrior, king of monkeys anxiously sent vanaras in all directions for searching Sita.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

तां हरीणां सहस्राणि शतानि नियुतानि च। दिक्षु सर्वासु मार्गन्ते ह्यधश्चोपरि चाम्बरे।।5.51.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हरीणाम् of monkeys, सहस्राणि in thousands, शतानि hundreds, नियुतानि च of unlimited numbers, ताम् her, सर्वासु in all, दिक्षु in directions, अधश्च underworld, उपरि in heavens, अम्बरे च in the sky, मार्गन्ते are searching.

#### Explanation

"Hundreds, thousands and unlimited number of monkeys are searching (for Sita) in all directions, in the underworld and heavens and the sky.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

वैनतेयसमाः केचित्केचित्तत्रानिलोपमाः। असङ्गगतयशशीघ्रा हरिवीरा महाबलाः।।5.51.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तत्र there, महाबलाः very powerful, केचित् some, हरिवीराः heroic vanaras, वैनतेयसमाः like Vainateya (Garuda), केचित् a few, अनिलोपमाः are like the Windgod, शीघ्राः swiftfooted warriors, असङ्गगतयः went without touching the earth.

#### Explanation

"Some of the very powerful heroes equal to Garuda, equal to the Windgod (in swiftness) have gone (in quest of Sita) without touching the earth.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

अहं तु हनुमान्नाम मारुतस्यौरसस्सुतः। सीतायास्तु कृते तूर्णं शतयोजनमायतम्।।5.51.15।। समुद्रं लङ्घयित्वैव तां दिदृक्षुरिहागतः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मारुतस्य Maruta's, औरसः सुतः own son, हनुमान्नाम Hanuman by name, अहं तु I too, सीतायाः कृते for Sita, तूर्णम् swiftly, शतयोजनम् a hundred yojanas, आयतम् broad, समुद्रम् ocean, लङ्घयित्वैव  leaping, ताम् her, दिदृक्षुः  desirous of seeing, इह here, आगतः came.

#### Explanation

"I, Maruta's son, Hanuman by name too came swiftly leaping across a hundred yojanas across the ocean searching for Sita.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

भ्रमता च मया दृष्टा गृहे ते जनकात्मजा।।5.51.16।। तद्भवान् दृष्टधर्मार्थस्तपःकृतपरिग्रहः। परदारान् महाप्राज्ञ नोपरोद्धुं त्वमर्हसि।।5.51.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भ्रमता while roaming, मया by myself, ते your, गृहे in house, जनकात्मजा Janaka's daughter, दृष्टा saw, भवान् you, दृष्टधर्मार्थः knower of the truth of righteousness, तपः कृतपरिग्रहः carried out great austerities, तत् such, महाप्राज्ञ very wise person, त्वम् you, परदारान् another man's wife, उपरोद्धुम् to abduct, न अर्हसि  it does not behove you.

#### Explanation

"While I was roaming I saw Janaka's daughter Sita at your house.You are a knower of the truth of righteousness. You are very wise and carrried out great austerities. It does not behove you to abduct another man's wife.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

भ्रमता च मया दृष्टा गृहे ते जनकात्मजा।।5.51.16।। तद्भवान् दृष्टधर्मार्थस्तपःकृतपरिग्रहः। परदारान् महाप्राज्ञ नोपरोद्धुं त्वमर्हसि।।5.51.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भ्रमता while roaming, मया by myself, ते your, गृहे in house, जनकात्मजा Janaka's daughter, दृष्टा saw, भवान् you, दृष्टधर्मार्थः knower of the truth of righteousness, तपः कृतपरिग्रहः carried out great austerities, तत् such, महाप्राज्ञ very wise person, त्वम् you, परदारान् another man's wife, उपरोद्धुम् to abduct, न अर्हसि  it does not behove you.

#### Explanation

"While I was roaming I saw Janaka's daughter Sita at your house.You are a knower of the truth of righteousness. You are very wise and carrried out great austerities. It does not behove you to abduct another man's wife.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

न हि धर्मविरुद्धेषु बह्वपायेषु कर्मसु। मूलघातिषु सज्जन्ते बुद्धिमन्तो भवद्विधाः।।5.51.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भवद्विधाः respectable people like you, बुद्धिमन्तः wise men, धर्मविरुद्धेषु acts which are unrighteous, बह्वपायेषु indulge in such actions, मूलघातिषु  striking at the very root, कर्मसु in actions, न सज्जन्ते हि do not involve indeed.

#### Explanation

"Indeed, respectable people and wise men like you should not indulge in actions opposed to righteousness as it will strike at the very root of your existence.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

कश्च लक्ष्मणमुक्तानां रामकोपानुवर्तिनाम्। शराणामग्रतः स्थातुं शक्तो देवासुरेष्वपि।।5.51.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लक्ष्मणमुक्तानाम् released by Lakshmana, रामकोपानुवर्तिनाम् following Rama's wrath, शराणाम् of those arrows, अग्रतः in front, स्थातुम् to stand, देवासुरेष्वपि even among gods and asuras, कः who, शक्त: has power.

#### Explanation

"Who, even among gods and asuras can withstand the arrows released by Lakshmana in obedience to the infuriated Rama's orders?

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

न चापि त्रिषु लोकेषु राजन्विद्येत कश्चन। राघवस्य व्यलीकं यः कृत्वा सुखमवाप्नुयात्।।5.51.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राजन् O king, यः whoever, राघवस्य Rama's, व्यलीकम् displeasure, कृत्वा after doing, सुखम् happiness, अवाप्नुयात् can experience, त्रिषु in the three, लोकेषु in worlds, कश्चन who, न विद्येत may not be there.

#### Explanation

"O king  there is none in these three worlds who can enjoy happiness by displeasing Rama.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

तत् त्रिकालहितं वाक्यं धर्म्यमर्थानुबन्धि च। मन्यस्व नरदेवाय जानकी प्रतिदीयताम्।।5.51.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तत् that, त्रिकालहितम् good for all three times, past present and future, धर्म्यम् is righteous, अर्थानुबन्धि च and provide you material wealth, वाक्यम् these words, मन्यस्व think, नरदेवाय to the lord of the people, जानकी Janaki, प्रतिदीयताम् return.

#### Explanation

"Think that my advice is beneficial to you for you present, past and future. It is righteous. It will provide you material wealth too. Hence return Sita to the lord of the people.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

दृष्टा हीयं मया देवी लब्धं यदिह दुर्लभम्। उत्तरं कर्म यच्छेषं निमित्तं तत्र राघवः।।5.51.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मया by me, इयं देवी this divine lady, दृष्टा हि indeed seen, यत् such, दुर्लभम् difficult, इह here, लब्धम् attained, उत्तरम् in future, यत् such, कर्म action, शेषम् remaining, तत्र there, राघवः Rama, निमित्तम् may plan.

#### Explanation

"I have actually spotted the divine lady here which was most difficult. Rama will plan the remaining course of action.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

लक्षितेयं मया सीता तथा शोकपरायणा। गृह्य यां नाभिजानासि पञ्चास्यामिव पन्नगीम्।।5.51.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तथा like that, शोकपरायणा immersed in deep sorrow, इयं सीता this Sita, मया by me, लक्षिता is seen, पञ्चास्याम् five hooded, पन्नगीमिव like a female serpent, याम् whom, गृह्य after abducting, नाभिजानासि you do not realise.

#### Explanation

"I have seen Sita immersed in deep sorrow. You do not realise that by abducting her you are keeping a fivehooded female serpent in your house.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

नेयं जरयितुं शक्या सासुरैरमरैरपि। विषसंसृष्टमत्यर्थं भुक्तमन्नमिवौजसा ।।5.51.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अत्यर्थम् excessive, विषसंसृष्टम् mixed with poison, भुक्तम् eaten, अन्नमिव like food, इयम् this, सासुरैः including asuras, अमरैरपि even gods, ओजसा absorb, जरयितुम् to weaken, न शक्या not possible.

#### Explanation

"Just as food mixed with venom cannot be digested by the great fire (fire of hunger), it is not possible to keep Sita even by gods and demons. (Sita is like food mixed with poison. Ravana cannot digest her with all his power.)

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

तपस्सन्तापलब्धस्ते योऽयं धर्मपरिग्रहः। न स नाशयितुं न्याय्य आत्मप्राणपरिग्रहः।।5.51.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तपस्सन्तापलब्ध: obtained by the the virtue of austerity, ते to you, यः that, अयम् this, धर्मपरिग्रहः accumlation of righteousness, आत्मप्राणपरिग्रहः at the cost of your life, सः that, नाशयितुम् to destroy, न न्याय्यः not proper.

#### Explanation

"You have acquired the merit of righteousness by prolonged hardships experienced during your penance. It is not proper to forfeit your own life in this way.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

अवध्यतां तपोभिर्यां भवान् समनुपश्यति। आत्मनः सासुरैर्देवैर्हेतुस्तत्राप्ययं महान्।।5.51.26।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भवान् your self, तपोभिः with austerities, आत्मनः of yourself, सासुरैः including asuras, देवैः by devas, याम् whom, अवध्यताम् not to be killed, समनुपश्यसि you see, तत्रापि even then, अयम् this, महान् great, हेतुः cause.

#### Explanation

"You know you will not be killed by asuras or gods. In that case also this (keeping Sita with you) may be the prime cause of your death.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

सुग्रीवो न हि देवोऽयं नासुरो न च राक्षसः। न दानवो न गन्धर्वो न यक्षो न च पन्नगः।।5.51.27।। तस्मात्प्राणपरित्राणं कथं राजन्करिष्यसि।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अयं सुग्रीवः this Sugriva, न हि not indeed, असुरः च an asura, राक्षसः च न not a demon, दानवः न not danava, गन्धर्वः न not a gandharva, यक्षः न not a yaksha, पन्नगश्च न not a pannga, राजन् O king, तस्मात् therefore, प्राणपरित्राणम् to protect your life, कथम् how, करिष्यसि you will do.

#### Explanation

"Sugriva is not an demon, nor a danava nor gandharva, nor a yaksha nor even a pannaga. Therefore how can you save your life from Sugriva?

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

न तु धर्मोपसंहारमधर्मफलसंहितम्।।5.51.28।। तदेव फलमन्वेति धर्मश्चाधर्मनाशन:।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

धर्मोपसंहारम् exceeding rigteousness, अधर्मफलसंहितम् yields results of unrighteousness, न तु not, तत् that, फलमेव result itself, अन्वेति follows, धर्मश्च righteousness, अधर्मनाशनः will destroy unrighteousness.

#### Explanation

"The fruit of dharma does not accrue to one who has reached the culmination of  adharma. Unrighteousness will destroy  the fruits of righteousness.

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

प्राप्तं धर्मफलं तावद्भवता नात्र संशयः। फलमस्याप्यधर्मस्य क्षिप्रमेव प्रपत्स्यसे।।5.51.29।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भवता by you, धर्मफलम् merits earned through righteousness, प्राप्तं तावत् you reaped, अत्र here, संशयः no doubt, न not, अस्य of this, अधर्मस्य of unrighteousness, फलमपि result also, क्षिप्रमेव quickly, प्रपत्स्यसे reap

#### Explanation

"There is no doubt you have earned merit through righteousness (dharma destroys adharma and yields sweet fruit ultimately). But now you will quickly reap the result of your unrighteousness also.

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

जनस्थानवधं बुद्ध्वा बुद्ध्वा वालिवधं तथा।।5.51.30।। रामसुग्रीवसख्यं च बुध्यस्व हितमात्मनः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

जनस्थानवधम् killing of all ogres at Janasthana, बुद्ध्वा after realising, तथा that way, वालिवधम् killing of Vali, बुद्ध्वा after realising, रामसुग्रीवसख्यं च alliance of Rama and Sugriva also, आत्मनः your own, हितम् benefit, बुध्यस्व know (Judge and adopt suitable course of action.)

#### Explanation

"You know about the killing of all ogres at Janasthana and of Vali also. You know about the alliance of Rama and Sugriva. You may  decide on a proper course of action for the sake of your own welfare.

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

कामं खल्वहमप्येकस्सवाजिरथकुञ्जराम्।।5.51.31।। लङ्कां नाशयितुं शक्तस्तस्यैष तु न निश्चयः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I, एकोऽपि all alone also, सवाजिरथकुञ्जराम् full of horses, chariots and elephants, लङ्काम् Lanka, नाशयितुम् to destroy, कामम् indeed, शक्तः खलु I have the energy, एषः this, तु indeed, निश्चयः resolution, न not.

#### Explanation

" Alone, I can destroy this entire Lanka teeming with horses, chariots and elephants. Indeed I have the energy but not the mandate of Sri Rama.

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

रामेण हि प्रतिज्ञातं हर्यृक्षगणसन्निधौ।।5.51.32।। उत्सादनममित्राणां सीता यैस्तु प्रधर्षिता।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामेण by Rama, हर्यृक्षगणसन्निधौ in the presence of bears and vanaras, यैः by those, सीता Sita, प्रधर्षिता is troubled, अमित्राणाम् of enemy troops, उत्सादनम् exterminate, प्रतिज्ञातं हि indeed made a vow.

#### Explanation

"Rama made a vow in the presence of bears and vanaras that he would exterminate the arrogant enemy troops  by whom Sita is abducted and assaulted.

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

अपकुर्वन् हि रामस्य साक्षादपि पुरन्दरः।।5.51.33।। न सुखं प्राप्नुयादन्यः किं पुनस्त्वद्विधो जनः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामस्य to Rama, अपकुर्वन् while offending, साक्षात् personally, पुरन्दरः अपि even the destroyer of enemy citadel, Indra, सुखम् happiness, नप्राप्नुयात् will not have, त्वद्विधः people like you, किं पुनः what to say again.

#### Explanation

"Indra, the destroyer of enemy citadels cannot live happily if he offends Rama. What to speak of persons like you?

---

### Shloka 34

#### Shloka Text

यां सीतेत्यभिजानासि येयं तिष्ठति ते वशे।।5.51.34।। कालरात्रीति तां विद्धि सर्वलङ्काविनाशिनीम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

याम् whom, सीता इति as Sita, अभिजानासि know, या इयम् whosoever, ते वशे under your custody, तिष्ठति stays, ताम् her, सर्वलङ्काविनाशिनीम् destroyer of the entire Lanka, कालरात्रीति the dark night of dissolution, विद्धि you may know.

#### Explanation

"Sita who is under your custody is no ordinary lady. She is indeed the dark night of dissolution, destroyer of entire Lanka. Know this.

---

### Shloka 35

#### Shloka Text

तदलं कालपाशेन सीताविग्रहरूपिणा।।5.51.35।। स्वयं स्कन्धावसक्तेन क्षेममात्मनि चिन्त्यताम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तत् therefore, सीताविग्रहरूपिणा in the form of Sita, स्वयम् personally, स्कन्धावसक्तेन on your shoulder, कालपाशेन by the noose of death, अलम् enough, आत्मनि for your sake, क्षेमम् welfare, चिन्त्यताम् think over.

#### Explanation

"Stop holding the noose of death in Sita's form whom you yourself have placed on your shoulders. Think of your own welfare.

---

### Shloka 36

#### Shloka Text

सीतायास्तेजसा दग्धां रामकोपप्रपीडिताम्।।5.51.36।। दह्यमानामिमां पश्य पुरीं साट्टप्रतोलिकाम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

Sita's, तेजसा by the glowing fire, दग्धाम् burnt, रामकोपप्रपीडिताम् pressed by Rama's wrath, दह्यमानाम् being burnt, साट्टप्रतोलिकाम् including its marketplaces and streets, इमाम् of this, पुरीम् city, पश्य see.

#### Explanation

"Visualize this city of Lanka including its marketplaces and main streets as  burnt by Sita's power of chastity and  Rama's anger.

---

### Shloka 37

#### Shloka Text

स्वानि मित्राणि मन्त्रींश्च ज्ञातीन् भ्रात्रून् सुतान् हितान्।।5.51.37।। भोगान्दारांश्च लङ्कां च मा विनाशमुपानय।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

स्वानि your, मित्राणि friends, मन्त्रींश्च ministers, ज्ञातीन् clan, भ्रात्रून् brothers and sisters, सुतान् sons, हितान् wellwishers, भोगान् pleasures, दारांश्च and wives, लङ्कां च and Lanka, विनाशम् destruction, मा उपानय do not lead.

#### Explanation

"Do not pave the path of your friends, ministers, clan, brothers and sisters, sons, wellwishers, wives and luxuries and Lanka to destruction.

---

### Shloka 38

#### Shloka Text

सत्यं राक्षसराजेन्द्र शृणुष्व वचनं मम।।5.51.38।। रामदासस्य दूतस्य वानरस्य विशेषतः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसराजेन्द्र O king of demons, रामदासस्य of Rama's servant, दूतस्य of a messenger's, विशेषतः specially, वानरस्य of a vanara, मम my, सत्यम् true, वचनम् word, शृणुष्व listen.

#### Explanation

"O king of demon listen to the words of truth of the servant of Rama, his messenger and specially a vanara.

---

### Shloka 39

#### Shloka Text

सर्वान् लोकान् सुसंहृत्य सभूतान् सचराचरान्।।5.51.39।। पुनरेव तथा स्रष्टुं शक्तो रामो महायशाः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महायशाः illustrious, रामः Rama, सभूतान् including all creatures, सचराचरान् including movables and immovable, सर्वान् all, लोकान् worlds, सुसंहृत्य after putting an end, पुनरेव once again, तथा in the same way, स्रष्टुम् to recreate, शक्तः has the power.

#### Explanation

"Illustrious Rama can put an end  to and dissolve all creatures including both movable and immovables of all worlds. He can recreate all of them in the same way. He has such superhuman powers.

---

### Shloka 40

#### Shloka Text
