भङ्क्त्वा वनं पादपरत्नसङ्कुलं हत्वा तु रक्षांसि महान्ति संयुगे। दग्ध्वा पुरीं तां गृहरत्नमालिनीं तस्थौ हनूमान्पवनात्मजः कपिः।।5.54.43।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पवनात्मजः son of the Windgod, हनूमान् Hanuman, पादपरत्नसङ्कुलम् that which was full of excellent trees, वनम् garden, भङ्क्त्वा after breaking, संयुगे in war, महान्ति great, रक्षांसि demons, हत्वा after killing, गृहरत्नमालिनीम् with rows of beautiful houses, तां पुरीम् that city, दग्ध्वा after burning, तस्थौ stood.

#### Explanation

Hanuman, son of the Windgod stood at ease having shattered the garden that was full of excellent trees, and killed the demons and burnt down rows of beautiful houses in the city.

---

### Shloka 44

#### Shloka Text

त्रिकूटशृङ्गाग्रतले विचित्रे प्रतिष्ठितो वानरराजसिंहः। प्रदीप्तलाङ्गूलकृतार्चिमाली व्यराजताऽऽदित्य इवांशुमाली।।5.54.44।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विचित्रे on a wonderful, त्रिकूटशृङ्गाग्रतले on the peak of Trikuta mountain, प्रतिष्ठितः stationed, प्रदीप्तलाङ्गूलकृतार्चिमाली enriched by the flames emerging from his burning tail, वानरराजसिंहः a lion among vanara chiefs, अंशुमाली surrounded by rays of light, आदित्यः इव like the Sun, व्यराजत was resplendent.

#### Explanation

Standing on the peak of Trikuta mountain with his tail burning like the aura of the Sun, the lion among vanaras looked resplendent like the Sun surrounded by his rays.

---

### Shloka 45

#### Shloka Text

स राक्षसांस्तान्सुबहूंश्च हत्वा वनं च भङ्क्त्वा बहुपादपं तत्। विसृज्य रक्षोभवनेषु चाग्निं जगाम रामं मनसा महात्मा।।5.54.45।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महात्मा a great self, सः he, सुबहून् many of them, तान् राक्षसान् the demons, हत्वा having killed, बहुपादपम् having many trees, तत् वनं च that garden also, भङ्क्त्वा after breaking, रक्षोभवनेषु in the mansions of demons, अग्निम् fire, विसृज्य after setting, मनसा in his mind, रामम् Rama, जगाम reached.

#### Explanation

Great Hanuman, sought Rama's presence in his mind after killing many demons, breaking down many trees of the garden, and setting fire to the mansions of demons.

---

### Shloka 46

#### Shloka Text

ततस्तु तं वानरवीरमुख्यं महाबलं मारुततुल्यवेगम्। महामतिं वायुसुतं वरिष्ठं प्रतुष्टुवुर्देवगणाश्च सर्वे।।5.54.46।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, वानरवीरमुख्यम् chief of heroic vanaras, महाबलम् of great strength, मारुततुल्यवेगम् who had the speed of wind, महामतिम् very wise, वरिष्ठम् efficient, तं वायुसुतम् son of the Windgod, सर्वे all, देवगणाः gods, प्रतुष्टुवुः praised.

#### Explanation

All the gods praised the vanara chief, son of the Windgod, who was powerful, wise and efficient and had the speed of the Wind.

---

### Shloka 47

#### Shloka Text

भङ्क्त्वा वनं महातेजा हत्वा रक्षांसि संयुगे। दग्ध्वा लङ्कापुरीं रम्यां रराज स महाकपिः।।5.54.47।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महातेजाः splendid, महाकपिः great monkey, वनम् garden, भङ्क्त्वा having destroyed, संयुगे in the war, रक्षांसि demons, हत्वा having killed, रम्याम् delightful, लङ्कापुरीम् Lanka city, दग्ध्वा having burnt, रराज shone.

#### Explanation

Hanuman destroyed the garden, killed the demons, burnt the city of Lanka and was shining with delight

---

### Shloka 48

#### Shloka Text

तत्र देवास्सगन्धर्वास्सिद्धाश्च परमर्षयः। दृष्ट्वा लङ्कां प्रदग्धां तां विस्मयं परमं गताः।।5.54.48।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तत्र there, सगन्धर्वाः including the gandharvas, देवाः gods, सिद्धाश्च siddhas, परमर्षयः great sages, दग्धाम् burnt, तां लङ्काम् पुरीम् that Lanka city, दृष्ट्वा after seeing, परमम् supreme, विस्मयम् wonder, गताः experienced.

#### Explanation

The gandharvas, gods, siddhas and great sages were amazed at seeing the burning city of Lanka৷৷

---

### Shloka 49

#### Shloka Text

तं दृष्ट्वा वानरश्रेष्ठं हनुमन्तं महाकपिम्। कालाग्निरिति सञ्चिन्त्य सर्वभूतानि तत्रसुः।।5.54.49।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वानरश्रेष्ठम् best of vanaras, महाकपिम् great monkey, तं हनुमन्तम् that Hanuman, दृष्ट्वा after seeing, कालाग्निरिति that it is the fire of dissolution, सञ्चिन्त्य thinking, सर्वभूतानि all beings, तत्रसुः feared.

#### Explanation

All beings were afraid of Hanuman thinking that he was the fatal fire, consuming worlds at the hour of dissolution .

---

### Shloka 50

#### Shloka Text

देवाश्च सर्वे मुनिपुङ्गवाश्च गन्धर्वविद्याधरनागयक्षाः। भूतानि सर्वाणि महान्ति तत्र जग्मुः परां प्रीतिमतुल्यरूपाम्।।5.54.50।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तत्र there,सर्वे all,देवाश्च gods, मुनिपुङ्गवाश्च great sages,गन्धर्वविद्याधरनागयक्षाः gandharvas, vidyadharas, nagas and yakshas, महान्ति great, सर्वाणि all, भूतानि beings, अतुल्यरूपाम् of a matchless form, पराम् supreme, प्रीतिम् pleasure, जग्मुः felt.

#### Explanation

All gods, great sages, gandharvas, vidyadharas, nagas and yakshas and all great beings experienced supreme joy. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये सुन्दरकाण्डे चतुःपञ्चाशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the fiftyfourth sarga of Sundarakanda of the holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 55

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

लङ्कां समस्तां सन्दीप्य लाङ्गूलाग्निं महाबलः। निर्वापयामास तदा समुद्रे हरिसत्तमः।। सन्दीप्यमानां विध्वस्तां त्रस्तरक्षोगणां पुरीम्। आवेक्ष्य हनुमान् लङ्कां चिन्तयामास वानरः।।5.55.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लङ्कां समस्तां the entire city of Lanka, सन्दीप्य having burnt, महाबलः powerful, तदा then, लाङ्गूलाग्निं fire on his tail, समुद्रे in the ocean, निर्वापयामास put off, वानरः vanara, हनुमान् Hanuman, सन्दीप्यमानाम् having been lit, विध्वस्ताम् destroyed, त्रस्तरक्षोगणाम् panic stricken ogres, लङ्कां पुरीम् Lanka city, आवेक्ष्य  seeing, चिन्तयामास began to think.

#### Explanation

Powerful Hanuman, having burnt the entire city of Lanka, put out the fire on his tail in the ocean. Beholding the burning city, the destroyed gardens and the panic stricken ogres, he began to think:

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

तस्याभूत्सुमहांस्त्रासः कुत्सा चात्मन्यजायत। लङ्कां प्रदहता कर्म किंस्वित्कृतमिदं मया।।5.55.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तस्य to him, सुमहान् very great, त्रासः fear, अभूत् overtook, आत्मनि in himself, कुत्सा च self reproach, अजायत was felt, लङ्काम् Lanka, प्रदहता while burning, मया by me, इदम् this, किंस्वित् what a heinous, कर्म action, कृतम् perpetrated.

#### Explanation

Overtaken by great fear, Hanuman felt a sense of selfreproach seeing Lanka reduced to ashes, and said, ' Lanka is burnt. What a heinous action has been perpetrated by me'.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

धन्यास्ते पुरुषश्रेष्ठा ये बुद्ध्या कोपमुत्थितम्। निरुन्धन्ति महात्मानो दीप्तमग्निमिवाम्भसा।।5.55.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

उत्थितम् rising, कोपम् anger, बुद्ध्या by their wisdom,दीप्तम् burning अग्निम् fire, अम्भसा इव with water, ये those, निरुन्धन्ति will restrain, महात्मानः highsouled men, ते they, पुरुषश्रेष्ठाः best among men, धन्याः blessed.

#### Explanation

'Blessed indeed are the highsouled men, who in their wisdom restrain their own anger as one would put off  burning fire with water.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

क्रुद्धः पापं न कुर्यात्कः क्रुद्धो हन्याद्गुरूनपि। क्रुद्धः परुषया वाचा नरस्साधूनधिक्षिपेत्।।5.55.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

क्रुद्धः angry man, कः who, पापम् sin, न कुर्यात् will not comit, क्रुद्धः angry man, गुरूनपि even his elders, हन्यात् will kill, क्रुद्धः angry, नरः human being, परुषया with a harsh, वाचा with word, साधून् sages, अधिक्षिपेत् will accuse.

#### Explanation

'Which sinful act an angry man will not commit? He will even kill his respectable elders or insult sages with his harsh tongue.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

वाच्यावाच्यं प्रकुपितो न विजानाति कर्हिचित्। नाकार्यमस्ति क्रुद्धस्य नावाच्यं विद्यते क्वचित्।।5.55.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

प्रकुपितः one who is angry, कर्हिचित् at any time, वाच्यावाच्यम् a word worthy of speaking or not, न विजानाति knows not, क्रुद्धस्य angry man's, अकार्यम् misdeed, न अस्ति exists not, अवाच्यम् unutterable, क्वचित् anywhere, न विद्यते exists not.

#### Explanation

'An angry person knows not what he should utter and what he should not. No act appears wrong for an angry man. Nothing is unutterable for him at any time, anywhere.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

यस्समुत्पतितं क्रोधं क्षमयैव निरस्यति। यथोरगस्त्वचं जीर्णां स वै पुरुष उच्यते।।5.55.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

उरगः snake, जीर्णाम् worn out, त्वचं यथा as its slough, यः he who, समुत्पतितम् rising, क्रोधम् anger, क्षमया एव with tolerance alone, निरस्यति drives away, सः वै he alone, पुरुषः man, उच्यते is said to be.

#### Explanation

'A man who drives away his rising anger with tolerance just as a snake casts off its slough is alone called a truly wise man.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

धिगस्तु मां सुदुर्बुद्धिं निर्लज्जं पापकृत्तमम्। अचिन्तयित्वा तां सीतामग्निदं स्वामिघातुकम्।।5.55.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तां सीताम् that Sita, अचिन्तयित्वा not thinking of, अग्निदम् set fire, स्वामिघातुकम् betrayer of the master, सुदुर्बुद्धिम् having of a wicked mind, निर्लज्जम् shameless, पापकृत्तमम् greatest sinner, माम् me, धिक् अस्तु fie upon.

#### Explanation

'Fie upon me, a wicked, shameless person and the greatest sinner for betraying the  master and setting fire to the city without thinking of Sita.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

यदि दग्धा त्वियं लङ्का नूनमार्याऽपि जानकी। दग्धा तेन मया भर्तुर्हितं कार्यमजानता।।5.55.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इयम् this, लङ्का Lanka, दग्धा यदि if it burnt, आर्या noble lady, जानक्यपि even Janaki, दग्धा is burnt, तेन therefore, अजानता unknowingly, मया by me, भर्तुः master's, कार्यम् mission, हतम् spoilt.

#### Explanation

'If this Lanka is burnt, even noble Janaki is burnt, my master's work is spoilt by me unknowingly.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

यदर्थमयमारम्भस्तत्कार्यमवसादितम्। मया हि दहता लङ्कां न सीता परिरक्षिता।।5.55.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

यदर्थम् on account of what, अयम् this, आरम्भ: effort, तत् कार्यम् that work, अवसादितम् is foiled, लङ्काम् Lanka, दहता while burning, मया by me, सीता Sita, न परिरक्षिता हि not protected.

#### Explanation

'My very mission is foiled. Sita has not been protected by me for no part of Lanka has escaped unburnt.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

ईषत्कार्यमिदं कार्यं कृतमासीन्न संशयः। तस्य क्रोधाभिभूतेन मया मूलक्षयः कृतः।।5.55.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इदं कार्यम् this work, ईषत्कार्यम् a small work, कृतम् is done, आसीन्न was, क्रोधाभिभूतेन over powered by anger, मया by me, तस्य its, मूलक्षयः the very root of my achievement is defeated, कृतः done, संशयः doubt, न no.

#### Explanation

'A small work is done, no doubt. But the very root of my achievement has been destroyed in my anger.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

विनष्टा जानकी नूनं न ह्यदग्धः प्रदृश्यते। लङ्कायां कश्चिदुद्धेशस्सर्वा भस्मीकृता पुरी।।5.55.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नूनम् surely, जानकी Janaki, विनष्टा lost, लङ्कायाम् in Lanka, कश्चित् even a little, उद्धेशः place, अदग्धः unburnt, न प्रदृश्यते हि indeed not seen, सर्वाः entire, पुरी city, भस्मीकृता is reduced to ashes.

#### Explanation

'Surely Janaki has perished in Lanka, for not even a little place is left unburnt. The entire city is reduced to ashes.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

यदि तद्विहतं कार्यं मम प्रज्ञाविपर्ययात्। इहैव प्राणसंन्यासो ममापि ह्यद्य रोचते।।5.55.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मम my, प्रज्ञाविपर्ययात् stupidity, तत् this, कार्यम् work, विहतं यदि if it has been foiled, ममापि foil me also, इहैव here itself, प्राणसन्यासः give up life, अद्य now, रोचते is proper.

#### Explanation

'If my work has been destroyed by my perversity of judgement, I will give up my life now  and here itself'.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

किमग्नौ निपताम्यद्य आहोस्विद्बडबामुखे। शरीरमाहो सत्त्वानां दद्मि सागरवासिनाम्।।5.55.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अद्य today, अग्नौ in fire, निपतामि shall I jump, आहोस्वित् alas बडबामुखे in the mouth of submarine fire, आहो alas शरीरम् body, सागरवासिनाम् सत्त्वानाम् to those aquatic creatures living in the ocean दद्मि I would offer.

#### Explanation

'Shall I end my life by jumping into fire just now, or else into the mouth of submarine fire? Should I offer my body to the living creatures of the ocean as their food?

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

कथं हि जीवता शक्यो मया द्रष्टुं हरीश्वरः। तौ वा पुरुषशार्दूलौ कार्यसर्वस्वघातिना।।5.55.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कार्यसर्वस्वघातिना after completely spoiling the work, मया by me, जीवता with life, हरीश्वरः king of monkeys, द्रष्टुम् to see, कथं वा how to, शक्यः possible, पुरुषशार्दूलौ two tigers among men, तौ वा or both those (Rama and Lakshmana.)

#### Explanation

'Having spoilt the entire work how can I show my face to the king of monkeys? How is it possible to see both the tigers among men?

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

मया खलु तदेवेदं रोषदोषात्प्रदर्शितम्। प्रथितं त्रिषु लोकेषु कपित्वमनवस्थितम्।।5.55.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मया by me, रोषदोषात् due to the mistake committed angerily, त्रिषु लोकेषु in all the three worlds, प्रथितम् wellknown, अनवस्थितम् Instability, तत् that, इदम् this, कपित्वम् monkeynature, प्रदर्शितं खलु indeed exhibited.

#### Explanation

'On account of my mistake of yielding to anger, I have exhibited the unstable monkeynature and made it well established in the three worlds.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

धिगस्तु राजसं भावमनीशमनवस्थितम्। ईश्वरेणापि यद्रागान्मया सीता न रक्षिता।।5.55.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अनीशम् having no control, अनवस्थितम् unstable, राजसम् excitable, भावम् nature, धिक् अस्तु fie upon, यत् that which, ईश्वरेणापि even though I am powerful, मया by me, रागात् out of passion, सीता Sita, न रक्षिता is not protected.

#### Explanation

'Fie upon my hasty action commited due to lack of selfcontrol. I did not save Sita eventhough the entire situation was under my control. (I had the  capacity to save her. But I failed due to anger.)

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

विनष्टायां तु सीतायां तावुभौ विनशिष्यतः। तयोर्विनाशे सुग्रीवः सबन्धुर्विनशिष्यति।।5.55.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सीतायाम् When Sita, विनष्टायाम् is lost, तौ उभौ those two, विनशिष्यतः will die, तयोः of those two, विनाशे when they are dead, सबन्धुः including all relatives, सुग्रीवः Sugriva, विनशिष्यति will perish.

#### Explanation

'If the two princes know about Sita's loss they will die and when they are dead Sugriva would perish along with all his relatives.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

एतदेव वचश्श्रुत्वा भरतो भ्रातृवत्सलः। धर्मात्मा सहशत्रुघ्नः कथं शक्ष्यति जीवितुम्।।5.55.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एतदेव this alone, वचः word, श्रुत्वा having heard, भ्रातृवत्सलः a loving brother, धर्मात्मा righteous one, सहशत्रुघ्नः along with Satrughna, भरतः Bharata, जीवितुम् to live, कथम् how, शक्ष्यति he can.

#### Explanation

'How will the loving righteous brother, Bharata along with Satrughna survive after hearing this?

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

इक्ष्वाकुवंशे धर्मिष्ठे गते नाशमसंशयम्। भविष्यन्ति प्रजास्सर्वाश्शोकसन्तापपीडिताः।।5.55.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

धर्मिष्ठे when the righteous, इक्ष्वाकुवंशे Ikshvaku race, नाशम् will perish, गते when it happens, सर्वाः all, प्रजाः people, असंशयम् there is no doubt, शोकसन्तापपीडिताः tormented by grief and remorse, भविष्यन्ति would be.

#### Explanation

'When the righteous Ikshvaku race perishes the people would be tormented by grief and remorse. There is no doubt.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

तदहं भाग्यरहितो लुप्तधर्मार्थसङ्ग्रहः।।5.55.20।। रोषदोषपरीतात्मा व्यक्तं लोकविनाशनः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तत् then, भाग्यरहितः unfortunate, लुप्तधर्मार्थसङ्ग्रहः failed to secure dharma and artha, रोषदोषपरीतात्मा overwhelmed with anger, अहम् I, व्यक्तम् this is evident, लोकनाशनः  destroyer of the whole world.

#### Explanation

'I have failed in seeking dharma and artha. It is evident is that I am a destroyer of the world because I was overtaken by anger. How unfortunate I am'

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

इति चिन्तयतस्तस्य निमित्तान्युपपेदिरे। पूर्वमप्युपलब्धानि साक्षात्पुनरचिन्तयत्।।5.55.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तस्य when he, इति thus, चिन्तयतः while he was bemoaning, साक्षात् directly witnessed, पूर्वमपि earlier, उपलब्धानि experienced, निमित्तानि omens, उपपेदिरे पुनः once again  appeared, अचिन्तयत् he reflected upon them again.

#### Explanation

When Hanuman was thus bemoaning, good omens as in the past appeared before him. He started reflecting once again:

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

अथवा चारुसर्वाङ्गी रक्षिता स्वेन तेजसा। न नशिष्यति कल्याणी नाग्निरग्नौ प्रवर्तते।।5.55.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथवा or else, चारुसर्वाङ्गी lady of beautiful limbs, स्वेन by her own, तेजसा by splendour, रक्षिता is protected, कल्याणी auspicious lady, न नशिष्यति will not perish, अग्निः fire, अग्नौ at fire, न प्रवर्तते cannot actbe effective.

#### Explanation

'On the other hand a beautiful and auspicious lady like Sita will be protected by her own splendour and will not perish as fire cannot extinguish fire. (Sita is pure like fire. Here the great sage Valmiki is suggesting the future incident in which Sita emerged safe from 'Agnipariksha' or fireordeal.)

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

न हि धर्मात्मनस्तस्य भार्याममिततेजसः। स्वचारित्राभिगुप्तां तां स्प्रष्टुमर्हति पावकः।।5.55.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

धर्मात्मनः of the righteous one, अमिततेजसः of a man of immeasureable glory, तस्य his, भार्याम् wife, स्वचारित्राभिगुप्ताम् protected by her own chastity, ताम् her, पावकः fire, स्प्रष्टुम् to touch, नार्हति हि cannot.

#### Explanation

'Fire cannot touch that lady who is protected by her own chastity and who is wedded to the righteous man of immasureable glory.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

नूनं रामप्रभावेण वैदेह्यास्सुकृतेन च। यन्मां दहनकर्मायं नादहद्धव्यवाहनः।।5.55.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दहनकर्मा fire who consumes, अयम् this, हन्यवाहनः the consumer of oblations, माम् me, नादहत् did not burn, इति यत् and this is, नूनम् surely, रामप्रभावेण by Rama's power, वैदेह्याः Vaidehi's, सुकृतेन च by her merits also.

#### Explanation

'Fire, the consumer of oblations who has the property of burning has not burnt me and this is surely on account of Rama's power and Vaidehi's merits.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

त्रयाणां भरतादीनां भ्रात्रूणां देवता च या। रामस्य च मन: कान्ता सा कथं विनशिष्यति।।5.55.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

या such lady, भरतादीनाम् of Bharata and others, त्रयाणाम् of all the three, देवताः च is treated as a goddess, रामस्य Rama's, मनः कान्ता dear to his heart, सा she, कथम् how, विनशिष्यति will she be destroyed?

#### Explanation

'To Bharata, Lakshmana and Satrughna she is a goddess and she is dear to Rama's heart, how would she perish?

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

यद्वा दहनकर्मायं सर्वत्र प्रभुरव्ययः। न मे दहति लाङ्गूलं कथमार्यां प्रधक्ष्यति।।5.55.26।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

यद्वा or if, सर्वत्र everywhere, प्रभुः lord, अव्ययः that which never fails, अयम् this, दहनकर्मा burning firegod, मे my, लङ्गूलम् tail, न दहति has not burnt, आर्याम् noble Sita, कथम् how, प्रधक्ष्यति will he burn.

#### Explanation

'If not so, the firegod who never fails in burning has not burnt my tail. How will he burn noble Sita?'

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

पुनश्चाचिन्तयत्तत्र हनुमान्विस्मितस्तदा। हिरण्यनाभस्य गिरेर्जलमध्ये प्रदर्शनम्।।5.55.27।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, हनुमान् Hanuman, विस्मितः wondered, जलमध्ये in the midst of water, हिरण्यनाभस्य of Hiranyanabha's, गिरेः mountain, प्रदर्शनम् appearing, तत्र there, पुनः again, अचिन्तयत् thought.

#### Explanation

Then Hanuman thought of the appearance of mountain Hiranyanabha in the midst of water, a wonderful phenomenon.

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

तपसा सत्यवाक्येन अनन्यत्वाच्च भर्तरि। अपि सा निर्दहेदग्निं न तामग्निः प्रधक्ष्यति।।5.55.28।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तपसा by asceticism, सत्यवाक्येन by adhering to truth, भर्तरि towards her husband, अनन्यत्वाच्च completeexclusive devotion, सा she, अग्निम् fire, निर्दहेत् अपि may even burn, ताम् her, अग्निः fire, न प्रधक्ष्यति is not capable of burning.

#### Explanation

'By virtue of her power of asceticism, power of truthfulness and exclusive devotion towards her husband, she may consume the fire. Fire will not burn her'.

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

स तथा चिन्तयंस्तत्र देव्या धर्मपरिग्रहम्। शुश्राव हनुमान्वाक्यं चारणानां महात्मनाम्।।5.55.29।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तत्र there, तथा so, देव्याः queen's, धर्मपरिग्रहम् power of righteousness, चिन्तयन् while thinking, सः हनुमान् that Hanuman, महात्मनाम् of the great ones, चारणानाम् of charanas (heavenly birds moving in the sky), वाक्यम् word, शुश्राव heard.

#### Explanation

As Hanuman was thinking so, he heard the words of the great charanas who were praising the queen's power of righteousness.

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

अहो खलु कृतं कर्म दुष्करं हि हनूमता। अग्निं विसृजताऽभीक्ष्णं भीमं राक्षसवेश्मनि।।5.55.30।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसवेश्मनि in the demons buildings, आभीक्ष्णम् excessively, भीमम् terrific, अग्निम् fire, विसृजता spreading, हनूमता by Hanuman, दुष्करम् marvellous, कर्म task, कृतं खलु has been done verily, अहो Oh

#### Explanation

'Oh Hanuman has indeed accomplished the marvellous task of spreading the terrific fire on the demons buildings.

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

प्रपलायितरक्षः स्त्रीबालवृद्धसमाकुला। जनकोलाहलाध्माता क्रन्दन्तीवाद्रिकन्दरै: ।।5.55.31।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

प्रपलायितरक्षस्त्रीबालवृद्धसमाकुला crowded with children, women and old demons running (in a bid to save their lives), जनकोलाहलाध्माता loud with the wails of demons people, अद्रिकन्दरे with the cave like houses, क्रन्दन्ती इव as if  wailing loudly.

#### Explanation

The city is crowded with women, children and old demons running (in a bid to save themselves). The loud wails of demons resounded in the mountaincaves. It was as if the city was shrieking loudly.

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

दग्धेयं नगरी सर्वा साट्टप्राकारतोरणा। जानकी न च दग्धेति विस्मयोऽद्भुत एव नः।।5.55.32।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

साट्टप्राकारतोरणा with towering markets ramparts and portals, इयम् this, नगरी city, सर्वा entirely, दग्धा is burnt, जानकी च and Janaki, न दग्धा is not burnt, इति thus, नः for us, अद्भुतः wonderful, विस्मयः एव It is surprising.

#### Explanation

'The city with its towering markets, ramparts and portals is burnt, but Janaki is not. It is really wonderful, strange

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

स निमित्तैश्च दृष्टार्थैः कारणैश्च महागुणैः। ऋषिवाक्यैश्च हनुमानभवत्प्रीतमानसः।।5.55.33।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः हनूमान् that Hanuman, दृष्ठार्थैः with proofs having seen, निमित्तै: with omens, महागुणैः  good tidings, कारणैश्च and by reasons, ऋषिवाक्यैश्च by the sages words, प्रीतमानसः happy at heart, अभवत् became.

#### Explanation

With omens that proved to be right and for good reasons and from the words of the sages (charanas), Hanuman felt happy.

---

### Shloka 34

#### Shloka Text

ततः कपिः प्राप्तमनोरथार्थस्तामक्षतां राजसुतां विदित्वा। प्रत्यक्षतस्तां पुनरेव दृष्टवा प्रतिप्रयाणाय मतिं चकार।।5.55.34।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, कपिः Hanuman, प्राप्तमनोरथार्थः with his desires fulfilled, तां राजसुताम् the princess, अक्षताम् not hurt, विदित्वा after knowing, ताम् her, पुनरेव once again, प्रत्यक्षतः directly, दृष्ट्वा after seeing, प्रतिप्रयाणाय for departing, मतिम् thought, चकार entertained.

#### Explanation

Hanuman, having fulfilled his desires and knowing that Sita is not hurt, thought of departing after meeting Sita once again (to testify that she is really safe). इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये सुन्दरकाण्डे पञ्चपञ्चाशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the fiftyfifth sarga of Sundarakanda  of the holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 56

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

ततस्तु शिंशुपामूले जानकीं पर्युपस्थिताम्। अभिवाद्याब्रवीद्दिष्ट्या पश्यामि त्वामिहाक्षताम्।।5.56.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, शिंशुपामूले under the simsupa tree, पर्युपस्थिताम् stationed, जानकीम् Janakai, अभिवाद्य offered salutation, अब्रवीत् said, दिष्ट्या luckily, अक्षताम् unscathed, त्वाम् you, इह here, पश्यामि I see you.

#### Explanation

Then Hanuman offered salutations to Janaki, stationed at the foot of the simsupa tree, and said, "Luckily I see you unscathed."

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

ततस्तं प्रस्थितं सीता वीक्षमाणा पुनः पुनः। भर्तृस्नेहान्वितं वाक्यं हनुमन्तमभाषत।।5.56.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, प्रस्थितम् him who was ready to start his journey, तं हनुमन्तम् that Hanuman, पुनः पुनः again and again, वीक्षमाणा looking at him, सीता Sita, भर्तृस्नेहान्वितम् showing her love for her husband, वाक्यम् this word, अभाषत spoke.

#### Explanation

Looking at Hanuman again and again, as he was ready to start on his return journey, thus spoke Sita expressing her love for her husband:

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

काममस्य त्वमेवैकः कार्यस्य परिसाधने। पर्याप्तः परवीरघ्न यशस्यस्ते बलोदयः।।5.56.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

परवीरघ्नः destroyer of foes, अस्य कार्यस्य of this task, परिसाधने in this task (of rescuing), त्वम् you, एकः एव singlehanded, कामम् indeed, पर्याप्तः capable, ते to you, बलोदयः rise of strength, यशस्यः will bring glory.

#### Explanation

"O destroyer of foes, you are capable of rescuing me singlehanded. Your success will  raise your strength and bring glory.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

शरैस्तु सङ्कुलां कृत्वा लङ्कां परबलार्दनः। मां नयेद्यदि काकुत्स्थस्तत्तस्य सदृशं भवेत्।।5.56.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

परबलार्दनः slayer of enemy forces,काकुत्स्थः Kakutstha,लङ्काम् to Lanka, शरैः तु with arrows indeed, सङ्कुलाम् spreading, कृत्वा all over, माम् me, नयेद्यदि if he takes me, तत् that, तस्य for him, सदृशम् worthy, भवेत् will be.

#### Explanation

"If Kakutstha Rama, the slayer of enemy forces comes to Lanka, fills it with his arrows and takes me back, it will be worthy of him.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

तद्यथा तस्य विक्रान्तमनुरूपं महात्मनः। भवत्याहवशूरस्य तथा त्वमुपपादय।।5.56.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तत् that, महात्मनः the exalted, आहवशूरस्य who is chivalrous in the battle, तस्य his, विक्रान्तम् heroic advance, अनुरूपम् worthy of his stature, यथा as such, भवति will be, तथा that manner, त्वम् you, उपपादय act.

#### Explanation

"You should act in such a manner that the exalted Rama, who is chivalrous in the battlefield, exhibits his valour worthy of his stature".

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

तदर्थोपहितं वाक्यं प्रश्रितं हेतुसंहितम्। निशम्य हनुमांस्तस्या वाक्यमुत्तरमब्रवीत्।।5.56.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हनुमान् Hanuman, प्रश्रितम् properly inclined, हेतुसंहितम् support with reason, अर्थोपहितम् meaningful, तस्याः  her, तत् that, वाक्यम् word, निशम्य after hearing, उत्तरम् reply, वाक्यम् these words, अब्रवीत् spoke.

#### Explanation

Hearing her meaningful reply which was welldisposed and reasonable, Hanuman replied:

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

क्षिप्रमेष्यति काकुत्स्थः हर्यृक्षप्रवरैर्वृतः। यस्ते युधि विजित्यारीन् शोकं व्यपनयिष्यति।।5.56.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

काकुत्स्थः Kakutstha, हर्यृक्षप्रवरैः with the foremost among vanaras and bears, वृतः surrounded, क्षिप्रम् very soon, एष्यति he will come, यः he who, युधि in battle, अरीन् enemies, विजित्य having won, ते your, शोकम् sorrow, व्यपनयिष्यति will remove.

#### Explanation

"Rama will come soon with the best of vanaras and bears. He would win the battle and remove your sorrrow.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

एवमाश्वास्य वैदेहीं हनुमान् मारुतात्मजः। गमनाय मतिं कृत्वा वैदेहीमभ्यवादयत्।।5.56.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मारुतात्मजः son of the Windgod, हनुमान् Hanuman, वैदेहीम् to Vaidehi, एवम् in that way, आश्वास्य having consoled, गमनाय to depart, मतिम् his mind, कृत्वा after deciding, वैदेहीम् to Vaidehi, अभ्यवादयत् respectfully saluted.

#### Explanation

Hanuman, son of the Windgod having consoled Vaidehi, saluted her respectfully and decided to depart.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

ततस्स कपिशार्दूलः स्वामिसन्दर्शनोत्सुकः। आरुरोह गिरिश्रेष्ठमरिष्टमरिमर्दनः।।5.56.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, अरिमर्दनः crusher of foes, सः he, कपिशार्दूलः a tiger among vanaras, स्वामिसन्दर्शनोत्सुकः anxious to see his lord, अरिष्टम् Arishta, गिरिश्रेष्ठम् excellent mountain, आरुरोह ascended.

#### Explanation

Then Hanuman, the crusher of enemies, a tiger among vanaras anxious to see his lord ascended the mount Arishta.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

तुङ्गपद्मकजुष्टाभिर्नीलाभिर्वनराजिभिः। सोत्तरीयमिवाम्भोदैः शृंगान्तरविलम्बिभिः।।5.56.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तुङ्गपद्मकजुष्टाभि: by those choicest tall padmaka trees, नीलाभिः by dark ones, वनराजिभिः with forest ranges, शृङ्गान्तरविलम्बिभिः hanging down from the spaces between the peaks of mountain, अम्भोदैः the rainy clouds, सोत्तरीयमिव as if covered with an upper garment.

#### Explanation

Mount Arishta was full of blue forestranges with choicest tall padmaka trees. With clouds hanging on the slopes of mountain peaks, it appeared as though the mountain was wrapped in an upper garment.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

बोध्यमानमिव प्रीत्या दिवाकरकरैश्शुभैः। उन्मिषन्तमिवोद्धूतैर्लोचनैरिव धातुभिः।।5.56.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शुभैः with auspicious ones, दिवाकरकरैः with Sun's rays, प्रीत्या affectionately, बोध्यमानमिव as if he was being awakened, उद्धूतैः with those pushed up (by wind), लोचनैरिव like with the eyes, धातुभिः with the minerals, उन्मिषन्तमिव as if he opening.

#### Explanation

As if the mountain was awakened from sleep by the rays of the blessed Sun. With the colour of glowing minerals exuding, it was as if the mountain was looking out with his eyes open. (The minerals underneath the mud appear when wind blows removing the dust covering them. The glowing minerals are fancied to be eyes of the mountain.)

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

तोयौघननिस्स्वनैर्मन्द्रैः प्राधीतमिव पर्वतम्। प्रगीतमिव विस्पष्टैर्नानाप्रस्रवणस्वनैः।।5.56.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मन्द्रैः with loud, तोयौघनिस्वनैः sounds of gurgling masses of waters, प्राधीतमिव as if reciting (loudly), विस्पष्टैः with clear sounds, नानाप्रस्रवणस्वनैः with different kinds of sounds of waterfalls, प्रगीतमिव as if singing, पर्वतम् mountain.

#### Explanation

With the loud sounds of masses of gurgling waters, the mountain appeared as though it was standing and reciting (the Vedas) loudly. With murmuring sounds of different waterfalls, it appeared as though the mountain was singing. (The sage differentiates the steadily trickling watersound from the rhythmic musical sounds of waterfalls.)

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

देवदारुभिरत्युच्चैरूर्ध्वबाहुमिव स्थितम्। प्रपातजलनिर्घोषैः प्राक्रुष्टमिव सर्वतः।।5.56.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अत्युच्चैः by the tall ones, देवदारुभिः with devadaru trees, ऊर्ध्वबाहुमिव as if with raised hands, स्थितम् standing, प्रपातजलनिर्घोषैः with the sounds of waterfalls, सर्वतः all over, प्राक्रुष्टमिव like the reverberations

#### Explanation

With the tall devadaru trees on the mountain, it appeared as if the sages were standing with shoulders lifted up and performing penance. The sounds of waterfalls filled all over with reverberations.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

वेपमानमिव श्यामैः कम्पमानैश्शरद्वनैः। वेणुभिर्मारुतोद्धूतैः कूजन्तमिव कीचकैः।।5.56.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

श्यामैः with dark ones, कम्पमानैः with shaking, शरद्वनैः clouds of autumn, वेपमानमिव as if trembling, वेणुभिः कीचकैः with hollow bamboos, मारुतोद्धूतैः hit by the wind, कीचकैः hollow bamboos rattling in the wind, कूजन्तमिव appearing like the warbling sound.

#### Explanation

The mountain appeared as if trembling with the dark clouds of autumn. The shrill sounds pasing through the hollow bamboos filled with air appeared as if it was producing sweet musical notes.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

निश्श्वसन्तमिवामर्षाद्घोरैराशीविषोत्तमैः। नीहारकृतगम्भीरैर्ध्यायन्तमिव गह्वरैः।।5.56.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

घोरैः with dreadful ones, आशीविषोत्तमैः with poisonous snakes, निःश्वसन्तमिव as if hissing, नीहारकृतगम्भीरैः with majestically covered heavy mist, गह्वरैः with caves, ध्यायन्तमिव as though meditating, controlling all senses.

#### Explanation

The mountain was as though hissing like a dreadful, poisonous snake. With the caves standing majestically covered with mist, the mountain appeared as if it was meditating.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

मेघपादनिभैः पादैः प्रक्रान्तमिव सर्वतः। जृंम्भमाणमिवाऽकाशे शिखरैरभ्रमालिभिः।।5.56.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मेघपादनिभैः appearing like the feet of clouds, पादैः with its feet, सर्वत: all over, प्रक्रान्तमिव as if traversing, अभ्रमालिभिः with ranges of clouds, शिखरैः with its peaks, आकाशे in the sky, जृम्भमाणमिव as if yawning.

#### Explanation

With the ranges of foothills appearing like the feet of clouds traversing, with its peaks like garlands of clouds traversing over the mountain, it made one feel it was yawning.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

कूटैश्च बहुधा कीर्णैश्शोभितं बहुकन्दरैः। सालतालाश्वकर्णैश्च वंशैश्च बहुभिर्वृतम्।।5.56.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

बहुधा many kinds, कीर्णैः scattered with, बहुकन्दरैः with many caves, कूटैश्च with mountain peaks, शोभितम् delighting, बहुभिः with many, सालतालाश्वकर्णैश्च with Sal, palms, Aswakarna trees and, वंशैश्च with bamboos and reeds, वृतम् canopied with.

#### Explanation

With many kinds of caves and peaks scattered all over, the mountain looked delightful. It was canopied with numerous trees like Sal, palms, Aswakarnas and bamboos.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

लतावितानैर्विततैः पुष्पवद्भिरलङ्कृतम्। नानामृगगणाकीर्णं धातुनिष्यन्दभूषितम्।।5.56.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विततैः with those spread out, पुष्पवद्भिः with those filled with flowers, लतावितानैः with creepers stretched all over, अलङ्कृतम् decorated, नानामृगगणाकीर्णम् crowded with herds of animals, धातुनिष्यन्दभूषितम् decorated with colourful minerals dropping down.

#### Explanation

It was spread with creepers filled with beautiful flowers and herds of animals. The colourful minerals flowing down decorated the mountain.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

बहुप्रस्रवणोपेतं शिलासञ्चयसङ्कटम्। महर्षियक्षगन्धर्वकिन्नरोरगसेवितम्।।5.56.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

बहुप्रस्रवणोपेतम् full of waterfalls, शिलासञ्चयसङ्कटम् with heaps of rocks making (the streams' flow difficult), महर्षियक्षगन्धर्वकिन्नरोरगसेवितम् attended with sages, yakshas, gandharvas, kinnaras and nagas.

#### Explanation

There were many streams of water flowing and rocks making it difficult for the streams to flow. The mountain was inhabited by sages, yakshas, gandharvas, kinnaras and nagas, and uragas.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

लतापादपसङ्घातं सिंहाध्युषितकन्दरम्। व्याघ्रसङ्घसमाकीर्णं स्वादुमूलफलद्रुमम्।।5.56.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लतापादपसङ्घातम् with clusters of creepers and trees, सिंहाध्युषितकन्दरम् caves inhabited by lions, व्याघ्रसङ्घसमाकीर्णम् herds of tigers scattered all over, स्वादुमूलफलद्रुमम् full of trees bearing tasty roots and fruits.

#### Explanation

The clusters of creepers, and trees bearing tasty fruits and roots were uprooted. There were caves inhabited by lions. Herds of tigers were scattered all over (by the impetus of Hanuman's pressure on the mountain).

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

तमारुरोह हनुमान् पर्वतं पवनात्मजः। रामदर्शनशीघ्रेण प्रहर्षेणाभिचोदितः।।5.56.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पवनात्मजः son of the Windgod, हनुमान् Hanuman, रामदर्शनशीघ्रेण anxious to see Rama soon, प्रहर्षेण with joy, अभिचोदितः  prompted, तं पर्वतम् that mountain, आरुरोह ascended.

#### Explanation

Anxious to see Rama soon, the son of the Windgod ascended the mountain happily.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

तेन पादतलाक्रान्ता रम्येषु गिरिसानुषु। सघोषास्समशीर्यन्त शिलाश्चूर्णीकृतास्ततः।।5.56.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, रम्येषु in delightful ones, गिरिसानुषु on mountain ranges, तेन by him, पादतलाक्रान्ताः crushed by his feet, शिलाः rocks, सघोषाः making loud sound, चूर्णीकृताः reduced to powder, समशीर्यन्त rolled down.

#### Explanation

Crushed under Hanuman's feet the rocks making a loud noise on the beautiful mountain ranges rolled down, reduced to powder.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

स तमारुह्य शैलेन्द्रं व्यवर्धत महाकपिः। दक्षिणादुत्तरं पारं प्रार्थयन् लवणाम्भसः।।5.56.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः महाकपिः that great Hanuman, लवणाम्भसः of the saltwater ocean, दक्षिणात् from the South, उत्तरं पारम् towards northern shore, प्रार्थयन् desiring, तं शैलेन्द्रम् that king of mountains, आरुह्य having ascended, व्यवर्धत started enlarging body.

#### Explanation

On ascending the king of mountains, desiring to reach the northern shore of the saltwater ocean from the Southern shore, he enlarged his body (in preparation to leap).

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

अधिरुह्य ततो वीरः पर्वतं पवनात्मजः। ददर्श सागरं भीमं मीनोरगनिषेवितम्।।5.56.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, वीरः hero, पवनात्मजः son of the Windgod, पर्वतम् mountain, अधिरुह्य having ascended, मीनोरगनिषेवितम् inhabited by fishes and sankes, भीमम् dreadful, सागरम् ocean, ददर्श saw.

#### Explanation

Then the heroic son of the Windgod, who had ascended the mountain saw the dreadful ocean inhabited by fishes and snakes.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

स मारुत इवाऽकाशं मारुतस्यात्मसम्भवः। प्रपेदे हरिशार्दूलो दक्षिणादुत्तरां दिशम्।।5.56.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मारुतस्य Windgod's, आत्मसम्भवः son, सः हरिशार्दूलः that tiger among monkeys, दक्षिणात् from the south, उत्तरांदिशम् towards northern side, मारुतः इव like the windgod, आकाशम् sky, प्रपेदे leaped.

#### Explanation

Hanuman, the tiger among monkeys leaped into the sky like the Windgod from the south to the north shore.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

स तदा पीडितस्तेन कपिना पर्वतोत्तमः। ररास सह तैर्भूतैः प्रविशन्वसुधातलम्।।5.56.26।। कम्पमानैश्च शिखरैः पतद्भिरपि च द्रुमैः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, तेन कपिना by that vanara, पीडितः pressed, सः पर्वतोत्तमः that great mountain, तैः those, भूतैः सह along with the creatures, वसुधातलम् to the earth, प्रविशन् while entering, कम्पमानैः with shaking, शिखरैः with peaks, पतद्भिः with dropped, द्रुमैः with trees, ररास crushed down making great sound.

#### Explanation

Pressed under the feet of Hanuman, the great mountain made loud sound sinking into the earth along with all its creatures, with its shaken mountain peaks and uprooted trees dropping down.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

तस्योरुवेगोन्मथिताः पादपाः पुष्पशालिनः।।5.56.27।। निपेतुर्भूतले रुग्णाश्शक्रायुधहता इव।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तस्य his, ऊरुवेगात् with the force of his thighs, मथिताः shaken, पुष्पशालिनः trees with blossoms, पादपाः trees, रुग्णाः men suffering with diseases, शक्रायुधहता इव as if struck by Indra's thunderbolt, भूतले on the ground, निपेतुः fell.

#### Explanation

Shaken by the violent force of Hanuman's thighs, the blossoming trees fell on the ground like sickmen hit by Indra's thunderbolt.

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

कन्दरान्तरसंस्थानां पीडितानां महौजसाम्।।5.56.28।। सिंहानां निनदो भीमो नभो भिन्दन् स शुश्रुवे।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कन्दरान्तरसंस्थानाम् of those residing in mountain caves, पीडितानाम् of those crushed, महौजसाम् of the powerful ones, सिंहानाम् of lions, भीमः terrific, सः निनदः that noise, नभः sky, भिन्दन् as if breaking, शुश्रुवे was heard.

#### Explanation

The dreadful roar of strong lions, lying in mountaincaves was heard cracking the sky when they were crushed in the turmoil.

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

त्रस्तव्यावृत्तवसना व्याकुलीकृतभूषणाः।।5.56.29।। विद्याधर्यः समुत्पेतुस्सहसा धरणीधरात्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विद्याधर्यः Vidyadharis, त्रस्तव्यावृत्तवसनाः with their clothes slipped with fear, व्याकुलीकृतभूषणाः with ornaments disarrayed, सहसा at ones, धरणीधरात् from the mountain, समुत्पेतुः flew.

#### Explanation

The Vidyadharis with their clothes slipped and ornaments disarrayed in fear, at once flew from the mountain.

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

अतिप्रमाणा बलिनो दीप्तजिह्वा महाविषाः।।5.56.30।। निपीडितशिरोग्रीवा व्यचेष्टन्त महाहयः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अतिप्रमाणाः very big ones in size, बलिनः strong ones, दीप्तजिह्वाः with glowing tongues, महाविषाः highly poisonous, महाहयः huge snakes, निपीडितशिरोग्रीवाः pressed on their heads and necks, व्यचेष्टन्त started protruding.

#### Explanation

Highly powerful, huge and poisonous snakes with their glowing tongues protruded, pressed on their heads and necks (by Hanuman).

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

किन्नरोरगन्धर्वयक्षविद्याधरास्तदा।।5.56.31।। पीडितं तं नगवरं त्यक्त्वा गगनमास्थिता:।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, किन्नरोरगगन्धर्वयक्षविद्याधराः kinnaras, uragas, gandharvas, yakshas and vidyadharas, पीडितम् tormented, तं नगवरम् that mountain, त्यक्त्वा having left, गगनम् to the sky, आस्थिताः occupied.

#### Explanation

Then the kinnaras, uragas, gandhravas, yakshas and vidyadharas left the tortured mountain and occupied the sky.

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

स च भूमिधरः श्रीमान्बलिना तेन पीडितः।।5.56.32।। सवृक्षशिखरोदग्रः प्रविवेश रसातलम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

बलिना by the powerful one, तेन by him, पीडितः crushed, सवृक्षशिखरोदग्रः along with its trees and standing tall peaks, श्रीमान् prosperous, सः भूमिधरश्च that mountain, रसातलम् underworld, प्रविवेश entered.

#### Explanation

Crushed by the powerful Hanuman, that prosperous mountain with its trees standing and tall peaks sank into the underworld.

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

दशयोजनविस्तारस्त्रिंशद्योजनमुच्छ्रितः।।5.56.33।। धरण्यां समतां यातः स बभूव धराधरः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दशयोजनविस्तारः ten yojanas in width, त्रिंशद्योजनम् thirty yojanas, उच्छ्रितः in height, सः धराधरः that mountain, धरण्याम् along with, समताम् equal, यातः reduced, बभूव became.

#### Explanation

That mountain of ten yojanas in width and thirty yojanas in height got levelled with the earth.

---

### Shloka 34

#### Shloka Text

स लिलङ्घयिषुर्भीमं सलीलं लवणार्णवम्।।5.56.34।। कल्लोलास्फालवेलान्तमुत्पपात नभो हरिः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः हरिः that vanara, भीमम् terrific, कल्लोलास्फालवेलान्तम् with huge waves dashing towards the shore, लवणार्णवम् ocean of saltwater, सलीलम् easily, लिलङ्घयिषुः desiring to cross, नभः sky, उत्पपात leaped.

#### Explanation

Eager to leap the terrific ocean lashed by huge waves hitting the shore Hanuman rose up effortlessly and jumped into the sky. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये सुन्दरकाण्डे षट्पञ्चाशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the  fiftysixth sarga of Sundarakanda of the holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 57

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

स चन्द्रकुमुदं रम्यं सार्ककारण्डवं शुभम्। तिष्यश्रवणकादम्बमभ्रशैवालशाद्वलम्।।5.57.1।। पुनर्वसुमहामीनं लोहिताङ्गमहाग्रहम्। ऐरावतमहाद्वीपं स्वातिहंसविलोलितम्।।5.57.2।। वातसङ्घातजातोर्मि चन्द्रांशुशिशिराम्बुमत्। भुजङ्गयक्षगन्धर्वप्रबुद्धकमलोत्पलम्।।5.57.3।। हनुमान्मारुतगतिर्महानौरिव सागरम्। अपारमपरिश्रान्तः पुप्लुवे गगनार्णवम् ।।5.57.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मारुतगतिः like the Windgod, हनुमान् Hanuman, अपरिश्रान्तः nwearied, सचन्द्रकुमुदम् Moon for a white lotus, सार्ककारण्डवम् Sun for a waterfowl, शुभम् auspicious, तिष्यश्रवणकादम्बम् Tishya and Shravana for sweetvoiced swans, अभ्रशैवालशाद्वलम् clouds for duckweeds and grassy spots on the shores, पुनर्वसुमहामीनम् Punarvasu constellation for large fish, लोहिताङ्गमहाग्रहम् the planet Mars for a crocodile, ऐरावतमहाद्वीपम् Airavata for a large island, स्वातिहंसविलोलितम् Swati for swan in water, वातसङ्घातजातोर्मि waves produced by wind for its billows, चन्द्रांशुश्शिराम्बुमत् cool Moonbeams for cold waters, भुजङ्गयक्षगन्धर्वप्रबुद्धकमलोत्पलम् nagas, yakshas, gandharvas together as fully blossomed lotuses, अपारम् गगनार्णवम् another ocean like, महानौ: huge boat, सागरम् इव like the ocean, पुप्लुवे flew across.

#### Explanation

Hanuman leaped ike the Windgod, unwearied across the sky, as a huge boat crosses an ocean. The ocean was graced with a swan, the sky with constellation of Swati with fullblown lotuses and water lilies, like the nagas, yakshas, and gandharvas gathered together. The Moon in the sky was like a white lotus, the Sun like a waterfowl, the auspicious Tishya and Shravana like the sweetvoiced swans, and the clouds like duckweeds and green grassy spots on the shore. The Punarvasu star was like a large fish and the planet Mars was like a crocodile. Airavata was like the great island. The waves produced by the wind were like cool moonlight in the cold water.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

स चन्द्रकुमुदं रम्यं सार्ककारण्डवं शुभम्। तिष्यश्रवणकादम्बमभ्रशैवालशाद्वलम्।।5.57.1।। पुनर्वसुमहामीनं लोहिताङ्गमहाग्रहम्। ऐरावतमहाद्वीपं स्वातिहंसविलोलितम्।।5.57.2।। वातसङ्घातजातोर्मि चन्द्रांशुशिशिराम्बुमत्। भुजङ्गयक्षगन्धर्वप्रबुद्धकमलोत्पलम्।।5.57.3।। हनुमान्मारुतगतिर्महानौरिव सागरम्। अपारमपरिश्रान्तः पुप्लुवे गगनार्णवम् ।।5.57.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मारुतगतिः like the Windgod, हनुमान् Hanuman, अपरिश्रान्तः nwearied, सचन्द्रकुमुदम् Moon for a white lotus, सार्ककारण्डवम् Sun for a waterfowl, शुभम् auspicious, तिष्यश्रवणकादम्बम् Tishya and Shravana for sweetvoiced swans, अभ्रशैवालशाद्वलम् clouds for duckweeds and grassy spots on the shores, पुनर्वसुमहामीनम् Punarvasu constellation for large fish, लोहिताङ्गमहाग्रहम् the planet Mars for a crocodile, ऐरावतमहाद्वीपम् Airavata for a large island, स्वातिहंसविलोलितम् Swati for swan in water, वातसङ्घातजातोर्मि waves produced by wind for its billows, चन्द्रांशुश्शिराम्बुमत् cool Moonbeams for cold waters, भुजङ्गयक्षगन्धर्वप्रबुद्धकमलोत्पलम् nagas, yakshas, gandharvas together as fully blossomed lotuses, अपारम् गगनार्णवम् another ocean like, महानौ: huge boat, सागरम् इव like the ocean, पुप्लुवे flew across.

#### Explanation

Hanuman leaped ike the Windgod, unwearied across the sky, as a huge boat crosses an ocean. The ocean was graced with a swan, the sky with constellation of Swati with fullblown lotuses and water lilies, like the nagas, yakshas, and gandharvas gathered together. The Moon in the sky was like a white lotus, the Sun like a waterfowl, the auspicious Tishya and Shravana like the sweetvoiced swans, and the clouds like duckweeds and green grassy spots on the shore. The Punarvasu star was like a large fish and the planet Mars was like a crocodile. Airavata was like the great island. The waves produced by the wind were like cool moonlight in the cold water.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

स चन्द्रकुमुदं रम्यं सार्ककारण्डवं शुभम्। तिष्यश्रवणकादम्बमभ्रशैवालशाद्वलम्।।5.57.1।। पुनर्वसुमहामीनं लोहिताङ्गमहाग्रहम्। ऐरावतमहाद्वीपं स्वातिहंसविलोलितम्।।5.57.2।। वातसङ्घातजातोर्मि चन्द्रांशुशिशिराम्बुमत्। भुजङ्गयक्षगन्धर्वप्रबुद्धकमलोत्पलम्।।5.57.3।। हनुमान्मारुतगतिर्महानौरिव सागरम्। अपारमपरिश्रान्तः पुप्लुवे गगनार्णवम् ।।5.57.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मारुतगतिः like the Windgod, हनुमान् Hanuman, अपरिश्रान्तः nwearied, सचन्द्रकुमुदम् Moon for a white lotus, सार्ककारण्डवम् Sun for a waterfowl, शुभम् auspicious, तिष्यश्रवणकादम्बम् Tishya and Shravana for sweetvoiced swans, अभ्रशैवालशाद्वलम् clouds for duckweeds and grassy spots on the shores, पुनर्वसुमहामीनम् Punarvasu constellation for large fish, लोहिताङ्गमहाग्रहम् the planet Mars for a crocodile, ऐरावतमहाद्वीपम् Airavata for a large island, स्वातिहंसविलोलितम् Swati for swan in water, वातसङ्घातजातोर्मि waves produced by wind for its billows, चन्द्रांशुश्शिराम्बुमत् cool Moonbeams for cold waters, भुजङ्गयक्षगन्धर्वप्रबुद्धकमलोत्पलम् nagas, yakshas, gandharvas together as fully blossomed lotuses, अपारम् गगनार्णवम् another ocean like, महानौ: huge boat, सागरम् इव like the ocean, पुप्लुवे flew across.

#### Explanation

Hanuman leaped ike the Windgod, unwearied across the sky, as a huge boat crosses an ocean. The ocean was graced with a swan, the sky with constellation of Swati with fullblown lotuses and water lilies, like the nagas, yakshas, and gandharvas gathered together. The Moon in the sky was like a white lotus, the Sun like a waterfowl, the auspicious Tishya and Shravana like the sweetvoiced swans, and the clouds like duckweeds and green grassy spots on the shore. The Punarvasu star was like a large fish and the planet Mars was like a crocodile. Airavata was like the great island. The waves produced by the wind were like cool moonlight in the cold water.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

स चन्द्रकुमुदं रम्यं सार्ककारण्डवं शुभम्। तिष्यश्रवणकादम्बमभ्रशैवालशाद्वलम्।।5.57.1।। पुनर्वसुमहामीनं लोहिताङ्गमहाग्रहम्। ऐरावतमहाद्वीपं स्वातिहंसविलोलितम्।।5.57.2।। वातसङ्घातजातोर्मि चन्द्रांशुशिशिराम्बुमत्। भुजङ्गयक्षगन्धर्वप्रबुद्धकमलोत्पलम्।।5.57.3।। हनुमान्मारुतगतिर्महानौरिव सागरम्। अपारमपरिश्रान्तः पुप्लुवे गगनार्णवम् ।।5.57.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मारुतगतिः like the Windgod, हनुमान् Hanuman, अपरिश्रान्तः nwearied, सचन्द्रकुमुदम् Moon for a white lotus, सार्ककारण्डवम् Sun for a waterfowl, शुभम् auspicious, तिष्यश्रवणकादम्बम् Tishya and Shravana for sweetvoiced swans, अभ्रशैवालशाद्वलम् clouds for duckweeds and grassy spots on the shores, पुनर्वसुमहामीनम् Punarvasu constellation for large fish, लोहिताङ्गमहाग्रहम् the planet Mars for a crocodile, ऐरावतमहाद्वीपम् Airavata for a large island, स्वातिहंसविलोलितम् Swati for swan in water, वातसङ्घातजातोर्मि waves produced by wind for its billows, चन्द्रांशुश्शिराम्बुमत् cool Moonbeams for cold waters, भुजङ्गयक्षगन्धर्वप्रबुद्धकमलोत्पलम् nagas, yakshas, gandharvas together as fully blossomed lotuses, अपारम् गगनार्णवम् another ocean like, महानौ: huge boat, सागरम् इव like the ocean, पुप्लुवे flew across.

#### Explanation

Hanuman leaped ike the Windgod, unwearied across the sky, as a huge boat crosses an ocean. The ocean was graced with a swan, the sky with constellation of Swati with fullblown lotuses and water lilies, like the nagas, yakshas, and gandharvas gathered together. The Moon in the sky was like a white lotus, the Sun like a waterfowl, the auspicious Tishya and Shravana like the sweetvoiced swans, and the clouds like duckweeds and green grassy spots on the shore. The Punarvasu star was like a large fish and the planet Mars was like a crocodile. Airavata was like the great island. The waves produced by the wind were like cool moonlight in the cold water.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

ग्रसमान इवाकाशं ताराधिपमिवोल्लिखन्। हरन्निव सनक्षत्रं गगनं सार्कमण्डलम्।।5.57.5।। मारुतस्यात्मजः श्रीमान्कपिर्व्योमचरो महान्। हनुमान्मेघजालानि विकर्षन्निव गच्छति।।5.57.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मारुतस्य Windgod's, आत्मजः son, श्रीमान् prosperous, महान् great, कपिः monkey, हनुमान् Hanuman, व्योमचरः who moves through the wind, आकाशम् sky, ग्रसमानः इव as if swallowing, ताराधिपम् lord of stars, उल्लिखन्निव as if scratching, सनक्षत्रम् with the stars, सार्कमण्डलम् constellation of Sun, गगनम् sky, हरन्निव as if seizing, मेघजालानि विकर्षन्निव drawing the clusters of clouds, गच्छति flew.

#### Explanation

Great Hanuman, son of the Windgod, flew through the abode of the Windgod as if he was swallowing the space, scratching the  Moon, lord of the stars, as if seizing the heavens along with the constellation of Sun and stars and as though he was drawing the clusters of clouds.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

ग्रसमान इवाकाशं ताराधिपमिवोल्लिखन्। हरन्निव सनक्षत्रं गगनं सार्कमण्डलम्।।5.57.5।। मारुतस्यात्मजः श्रीमान्कपिर्व्योमचरो महान्। हनुमान्मेघजालानि विकर्षन्निव गच्छति।।5.57.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मारुतस्य Windgod's, आत्मजः son, श्रीमान् prosperous, महान् great, कपिः monkey, हनुमान् Hanuman, व्योमचरः who moves through the wind, आकाशम् sky, ग्रसमानः इव as if swallowing, ताराधिपम् lord of stars, उल्लिखन्निव as if scratching, सनक्षत्रम् with the stars, सार्कमण्डलम् constellation of Sun, गगनम् sky, हरन्निव as if seizing, मेघजालानि विकर्षन्निव drawing the clusters of clouds, गच्छति flew.

#### Explanation

Great Hanuman, son of the Windgod, flew through the abode of the Windgod as if he was swallowing the space, scratching the  Moon, lord of the stars, as if seizing the heavens along with the constellation of Sun and stars and as though he was drawing the clusters of clouds.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

पाण्डुरारुणवर्णानि नीलमाञ्जिष्ठकानि च। हरितारुणवर्णानि महाभ्राणि चकाशिरे।।5.57.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पाण्डरारुणवर्णानि of white and black colour, नीलमाञ्जिष्ठकानि च and of blue yellow colour, हरितारुणवर्णानि of greenish red colour, महाभ्राणि shone briliantly, चकाशिरे made their appearance.

#### Explanation

(In contact with Hanuman) the clouds shone forth brilliantly in white and black, in blue and yellow and in greenish red colour.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

प्रविशन्नभ्रजालानि निष्पतंश्च पुनः पुनः। प्रच्छन्नश्च प्रकाशश्च चन्द्रमा इव लक्ष्यते।।5.57.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अभ्रजालानि into the mass of clouds, प्रविशन् entering, पुनः पुनः again and again, निष्पतंश्च disappearing, प्रच्छन्नश्च covered by, प्रकाशश्च shining again, चन्द्रमाः like the Moon, लक्ष्यते was appearing.

#### Explanation

With his exist and entrances into the mass of clouds, his appearances and disappearances among the clouds, he looked like the Moon.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

विविधाभ्रघनापन्नगोचरो धवलाम्बरः। दृश्यादृश्यतनुर्वीरस्तदा चन्द्रायतेऽम्बरे।।5.57.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, विविधाभ्रघनासन्नगोचरः passing through the clouds clad in white, धवळाम्बरः white sky, दृश्यादृश्यतनुः appearing and disappearing, वीरः hero, अम्बरे in the sky, चन्द्रायते like the Moon.

#### Explanation

Passing through the clouds, appearing and disappearing, the heroic Hanuman clad in white was like the Moon in the white sky.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

तार्क्ष्यायमाणो गगने बभासे वायुनन्दनः। दारयन्मेघबृन्दानि निष्पतंश्च पुनः पुनः।।5.57.10।। नदन्नादेन महता मेघस्वनमहास्वनः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मेघबृन्दानि through flakes of clouds, दारयन् making way, पुनः पुनः again and again, निष्पतंश्च disappearing, महता great, नादेन in loud voice, नदन् noise, मेघस्वनमहास्वनः roaring like thundering clouds, वायुनन्दनः the delight of the Windgod, गगने in the sky, तार्क्ष्यायमाणः like Garuda, बभासे shone.

#### Explanation

As he made his way through flakes of clouds, again and again appearing and disappearing, Hanuman the delight of the Windgod shone in the sky roaring like a thundering cloud. He looked like Garuda.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

प्रवरान्राक्षसान् हत्वा नाम विश्राव्य चात्मनः।।5.57.11।। आकुलां नगरीं कृत्वा व्यथयित्वा च रावणम्। अर्दयित्वा बलं घोरं वैदेहीमभिवाद्य च।।5.57.12।। आजगाम महातेजाः पुनर्मध्येन सागरम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महातेजाः glorious, प्रवरान् eminent, राक्षसान् ogres हत्वा having killed, आत्मनः himself, नाम name, विश्राव्य widely known, नगरीम् in the city, आकुलाम् having caused havoc, कृत्वा  done, रावणम् Ravana, व्यथयित्वा having caused sorrow, घोरम् terrific, बलम् army, अर्दयित्वा having tormented, वैदेहीम् to Vaidehi, अभिवाद्य च having offered salutation, पुनः again, सागरं मध्येन to cross the ocean, आजगाम departed.

#### Explanation

The glorious Hanuman, having killed eminent ogres and thereby making himself widely known, having caused havoc to the city of Lanka and  terrific sorrow to Ravana by tormenting the army, offered salutations to Vaidehi from midocean and continued his journey.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

प्रवरान्राक्षसान् हत्वा नाम विश्राव्य चात्मनः।।5.57.11।। आकुलां नगरीं कृत्वा व्यथयित्वा च रावणम्। अर्दयित्वा बलं घोरं वैदेहीमभिवाद्य च।।5.57.12।। आजगाम महातेजाः पुनर्मध्येन सागरम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महातेजाः glorious, प्रवरान् eminent, राक्षसान् ogres हत्वा having killed, आत्मनः himself, नाम name, विश्राव्य widely known, नगरीम् in the city, आकुलाम् having caused havoc, कृत्वा  done, रावणम् Ravana, व्यथयित्वा having caused sorrow, घोरम् terrific, बलम् army, अर्दयित्वा having tormented, वैदेहीम् to Vaidehi, अभिवाद्य च having offered salutation, पुनः again, सागरं मध्येन to cross the ocean, आजगाम departed.

#### Explanation

The glorious Hanuman, having killed eminent ogres and thereby making himself widely known, having caused havoc to the city of Lanka and  terrific sorrow to Ravana by tormenting the army, offered salutations to Vaidehi from midocean and continued his journey.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

पर्वतेन्द्रं सुनाभं च समुपस्पृश्य वीर्यवान्।।5.57.13।। ज्यामुक्त इव नाराचो महावेगोऽभ्युपागतः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वीर्यवान् hero, पर्वतेन्द्रम् lord of mountains,सुनाभं च from the centre, समुपस्पृश्य touched fondly, ज्यामुक्तः telling everything, नाराचः इव like the arrow from a powerful bowstring, महावेगः with great speed, अभ्युपागतः went.

#### Explanation

The valiant Hanuman touched the lord of mountains fondly and went with great speed just as an arrow released from the centre of a powerful bowstring.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

स किञ्चिदनुसम्प्राप्तः समालोक्य महागिरिम्।।5.57.14।। महेन्द्रं मेघसङ्काशं ननाद हरिपुङ्गवः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः he, हरिपुङ्गवः chief of monkeys, महागिरिम् great mountain, मेघसङ्काशम् resembling the mass of cloud, महेन्द्रम् Mahendra, किञ्चित् a little, अनुसम्प्राप्तः coming near, समालोक्य beholding, ननाद thundered.

#### Explanation

The chief of the monkeys arrived near, and beholding the face of the Mahendra mountain, which resembled a mass of clouds, thundered.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

स पूरयामास कपिर्दिशो दश समन्ततः।।5.57.15।। नदन्नादेन महता मेघस्वनमहास्वनः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मेघस्वनमहास्वनः one who makes a thundering noise, सः कपिः that Hanuman, नदन् noise, महता great, नादेन by the noise, दश दिशः all the ten directions, समन्ततः all over, पूरयामास filled.

#### Explanation

The thundering noise of Hanuman filled all the ten directions with great sound.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

स तं देशमनुप्राप्तः सुहृद्धर्शनलालसः।।5.57.16।। ननाद हरिशार्दूलो लाङ्गूलं चाप्यकम्पयत्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तं देशम् that place, अनुप्राप्तः having sighted, सुहृद्धर्शनलालसः waiting to see his companions, सः हरिशार्दूलः that tiger among monkeys, ननाद made loud noise, लाङ्गूलम् his tail, अकम्पयच्च shook in joy.

#### Explanation

Having sighted the place of his companions and waiting to see them for long that tiger among monkeys made a loud noise and shook his tail in joy.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

तस्य नानद्यमानस्य सुपर्णचरिते पथि।।5.57.17।। फलतीवास्य घोषेण गगनं सार्कमण्डलम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सुपर्णचरिते the path way of Suparna, पथि lord, नानद्यमानस्य making terrific sound, अस्य by his, घोषेण sound, सार्कमण्डलम् the Sun's orbit, गगनम् the sky, फलति इव as if splitting.

#### Explanation

By the terrific sounds made by Hanuman in joy, it seemed as though the pathway of Garuda, the sky along with the Sun's orbit was splitting.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

ये तु तत्रोत्तरे तीरे समुद्रस्य महाबलाः।।5.57.18।। पूर्वं संविष्ठिताश्शूरा वायुपुत्रदिदृक्षवः। महतो वातनुन्नस्य तोयदस्येव गर्जितम्।।5.57.19।। शुश्रुवुस्ते तदा घोषमूरुवेगं हनूमतः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तत्र thereafter, समुद्रस्य ocean's, उत्तरे तीरे northern shore, पूर्वम् earlier, संविष्ठिताः waiting, महाबलाः powerful (monkeys), शूराः brave, वायुपुत्रदिदृक्षवः awaiting the arrival of the son of the Windgod, ये those, ते what, तदा then, वातनुन्नस्य by the impact of sweeping motion of his thighs, महतः great, तोयदस्य stormy cloud's, गर्जितम् इव like the thundering noise, हनूमतः of Hanuman, घोषम् roaring, ऊरुवेगम् by the speed, शुश्रुवुः heard.

#### Explanation

Thereafter the brave and powerful monkeys waiting on the northern shore of the ocean for the arrival of the son of the Windgod, heard the roar and thundering sound of the stormy cloud produced by the sweeping motion of Hanuman's thighs propelled by the speed of his movement.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

ये तु तत्रोत्तरे तीरे समुद्रस्य महाबलाः।।5.57.18।। पूर्वं संविष्ठिताश्शूरा वायुपुत्रदिदृक्षवः। महतो वातनुन्नस्य तोयदस्येव गर्जितम्।।5.57.19।। शुश्रुवुस्ते तदा घोषमूरुवेगं हनूमतः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तत्र thereafter, समुद्रस्य ocean's, उत्तरे तीरे northern shore, पूर्वम् earlier, संविष्ठिताः waiting, महाबलाः powerful (monkeys), शूराः brave, वायुपुत्रदिदृक्षवः awaiting the arrival of the son of the Windgod, ये those, ते what, तदा then, वातनुन्नस्य by the impact of sweeping motion of his thighs, महतः great, तोयदस्य stormy cloud's, गर्जितम् इव like the thundering noise, हनूमतः of Hanuman, घोषम् roaring, ऊरुवेगम् by the speed, शुश्रुवुः heard.

#### Explanation

Thereafter the brave and powerful monkeys waiting on the northern shore of the ocean for the arrival of the son of the Windgod, heard the roar and thundering sound of the stormy cloud produced by the sweeping motion of Hanuman's thighs propelled by the speed of his movement.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

ते दीनमनसस्सर्वे शुश्रुवुः काननौकसः।।5.57.20।। वानरेन्द्रस्य निर्घोषं पर्जन्यनिनदोपमम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दीनमनसः dejected at heart, ते they, सर्वे all, काननौकसः with eagerness, वानरेन्द्रस्य chief of vanara's, पर्जन्यनिनदोपमम् like the thundering of clouds, निर्घोषम् noise, शुश्रुवुः heard.

#### Explanation

All the vanaras who were feeling dejected and waiting anxiously heard the noise of Hanuman that sounded like a thundering cloud.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

निशम्य नदतो नादं वानरास्ते समन्ततः।।5.57.21।। बभूवुरुत्सुकास्सर्वे सुहृद्धर्शनकाङ्क्षिणः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

समन्ततः together, सर्वे all of them, ते वानराः those vanaras, नदतः chattering, नादम् voice, निशम्य hearing, सुहृद्धर्शनकाङ्क्षिणः waiting to see their friend, उत्सुकाः eager, बभूवुः became.

#### Explanation

On hearing the loud voice of Hanuman, all the vanaras were longing to see their friend.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

जाम्बवान् स हरिश्रेष्ठः प्रीतिसंहृष्टमानसः।।5.57.22।। उपामन्त्र्य हरीन् सर्वानिदं वचनमब्रवीत्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हरिश्रेष्ठः chief of vanaras, सः जाम्बवान् that Jambavan, प्रीतिसंहृष्टमानसः very happy at heart, सर्वान् all, हरीन् monkeys, उपामन्त्य्र called, इदम् these, वचनम् words, अब्रवीत् spoke.

#### Explanation

Jambavan the chief of vanaras became very happy at heart and called all the vanaras and spoke:

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

सर्वथा कृतकार्योऽसौ हनुमान्नात्र संशयः।।5.57.23।। न ह्यस्याकृतकार्यस्य नाद एवंविधो भवेत्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

असौ Oh that, हनुमान् Hanuman, सर्वथा always, कृतकार्यः successful, अकृतकार्यस्य if he was not successsful, अस्य his, नादः sound, एवंविधः in this manner, न भवेत् हि will not be so.

#### Explanation

"Oh Hanuman is always successful. If he has not, he would not make sounds in this manner".

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

तस्य बाहूरुवेगं च निनादं च महात्मनः।।5.57.24।। निशम्य हरयो हृष्टाः समुत्पेतुस्ततस्ततः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महात्मनः great self, तस्य his, बहूरुवेगं च speed of his arms and thighs, निनादं च sounds, निशम्य rejoiced, हृष्टाः joyful, हरयः vanaras, ततस्ततः then and there, समुत्पेतुः jumped in joy.

#### Explanation

Hearing the sound of his dashing movements of arms and thighs the vanaras jumped about in joy, then and there.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

ते नगाग्रान्नगाग्राणि शिखराच्छिखराणि च।।5.57.25।। प्रहृष्टाः समपद्यन्त हनूमन्तं दिदृक्षवः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ते those vanaras, प्रहृष्टाः very happy, हनूमन्तम् Hanuman, दिदृक्षवः longing to see him, नगाग्रात् from the top of a tree, नगाग्राणि to another tree top, शिखरात् from the peak of mountain, शिखराणि च to another peak of a mountain, समपद्यन्त jumped.

#### Explanation

The vanaras were very happy. Longing to see Hanuman, they jumped from one tree top to another and from one peak of a mountain to the other.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

ते प्रीताः पादपाग्रेषु गृह्य शाखाः सुविष्ठिताः।।5.57.26।। वासांसीव प्रशाखाश्च समाविध्यन्त वानराः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ते वानराः those vanaras, प्रीताः happily, पादपाग्रेषु from the tree top, शाखाः branches, गृह्य holding, सुविष्ठिताः waiting, प्रशाखाः the branches, वासांसीव like clothes, समाविध्यन्त shook them.

#### Explanation

Waiting for Hanuman, the vanaras jumped from the tree tops holding the branches. They shook the branches just like clothes to welcome friends.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

गिरिगह्वरसंलीनो यथा गर्जति मारुतः।।5.57.27।। एवं जगर्ज बलवान् हनुमान्मारुतात्मजः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

गिरिगह्वरसंलीनः like the wind in the mountain caves, मारुतः wind, यथा similarly, गर्जति roared, बलवान् powerful, मारुतात्मजः son of the Windgod, हनुमान् Hanuman, एवम् in that way, जगर्ज roared.

#### Explanation

Hanuman, the powerful son of the Windgod roared like the wind captured in a mountain cave would.

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

तमभ्रघनसङ्काशमापतन्तं महाकपिम्।।5.57.28।। दृष्ट्वा ते वानरास्सर्वे तस्थुः प्राञ्जलयस्तदा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, अभ्रघनसङ्काशम् resembling heavy cloud, आपतन्तम् approaching, तं महाकपिम् that great vanara, दृष्ट्वा having seen, ते those, वानराः vanaras, प्राञ्जलयः with folded hands, तस्थुः stood.

#### Explanation

(On seeing) Hanuman, who was resembling a heavy cloud approaching, the vanaras stood with folded hands.

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

ततस्तु वेगवांस्तस्य गिरेर्गिरिनिभः कपिः।।5.57.29।। निपपात महेन्द्रस्य शिखरे पादपाकुले।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः thereafter, गिरिनिभः of mountain size, वेगवान् with great speed, कपिः monkey, तस्य on that, महेन्द्रस्य Mahendra's,  गिरेः mountain, पादपाकुले full of trees, शिखरे summit, निपपात descended.

#### Explanation

Thereafter the vanara, who resembled a mountain, descended with great speed on the summit of mount Mahendra full of trees.

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

हर्षेणापूर्यमाणोऽसौ रम्ये पर्वतनिर्झरे।।5.57.30।। छिन्नपक्ष इवाऽकाशात्पपात धरणीधरः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हर्षेण आपूर्यमाणः full of immeasurable joy, असौ that, छिन्नपक्षः with wings cut off, धरणीधरः इव like a mountain, रम्ये delighted, पर्वन्तिर्झरे on the bank of the mountain stream, आकाशात् from the sky, पपात landed.

#### Explanation

Overwhelmed with immeasurable joy, Hanuman descended from the sky like the mountain with its wings cut off and dropped on the bank of a mountain stream.

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

ततस्ते प्रीतमनसस्सर्वे वानरपुङ्गवाः।।5.57.31।। हनुमन्तं महात्मानं परिवार्योपतस्थिरे। परिवार्य च ते सर्वे परां प्रीतिमुपागताः।।5.57.32।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, सर्वे all, ते of them, वानरपुङ्गवाः vanara leaders, प्रीतमनसः happy at heart, महात्मानम् that great self, हनुमन्तम् to Hanuman, परिवार्य surrounded, उपतस्थिरे without moving, ते सर्वे all of them, परिवार्य around, पराम् extreme, प्रीतिम् joyful, उपागताः became.

#### Explanation

Feeling very happy at heart all the vanara leaders gathered around Hanuman. They  stood motionless with extreme happiness.

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

ततस्ते प्रीतमनसस्सर्वे वानरपुङ्गवाः।।5.57.31।। हनुमन्तं महात्मानं परिवार्योपतस्थिरे। परिवार्य च ते सर्वे परां प्रीतिमुपागताः।।5.57.32।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, सर्वे all, ते of them, वानरपुङ्गवाः vanara leaders, प्रीतमनसः happy at heart, महात्मानम् that great self, हनुमन्तम् to Hanuman, परिवार्य surrounded, उपतस्थिरे without moving, ते सर्वे all of them, परिवार्य around, पराम् extreme, प्रीतिम् joyful, उपागताः became.

#### Explanation

Feeling very happy at heart all the vanara leaders gathered around Hanuman. They  stood motionless with extreme happiness.

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

प्रहृष्टवदना स्सर्वे तमरोगमुपागतम्। उपायनानि चादाय मूलानि च फलानि च।।5.57.33।। प्रत्यर्चयन् हरिश्रेष्ठं हरयो मारुतात्मजम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सर्वे हरयः the vanaras, प्रहृष्टवदनाः face filled with joy, मूलानि च even roots, फलानि च and fruits, उपायनानि gifts, आदाय offered, आरोगम् without being hurt, उपागतम् returned, हरिश्रेष्ठम् foremost of monkeys, मारुतात्मजम् son of the Windgod, प्रत्यर्पयन् offered salutations.

#### Explanation

The vanaras were filled with joy seeing Hanuman who returned unhurt. They offered roots and fruits to him. Hanuman on his part offered salutations to the foremost of the monkeys.

---

### Shloka 34

#### Shloka Text

हनुमांस्तु गुरून् वृद्धाञ्जाम्बवत्प्रमुखांस्तदा।।5.57.34।। कुमारमङ्गदं चैव सोऽवन्दत महाकपिः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, महाकपिः great monkey, हनुमांस्तु Hanuman also, गुरून् preceptors, वृद्धान् elders, जाम्बवत्प्रमुखान् Jambavan and other leaders, कुमारम् to the young, अङ्गदं चैव Angada also, अवन्दत  saluted.

#### Explanation

The great Hanuman also offered salutations to elders, preceptors such as Jambavan and other leaders. He also saluted the young Angada.

---

### Shloka 35

#### Shloka Text

स ताभ्यां पूजितः पूज्यः कपिभिश्च प्रसादितः।।5.57.35।। दृष्टा सीतेति विक्रान्त स्संक्षेपेण न्यवेदयत्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विक्रान्तः valiant one, पूज्यः worthy of worship, सः he, ताभ्याम् both, पूजितः having been honoured, कपिभिः by vanaras also, प्रसादितः pleased, सीता Sita, दृष्टा saw, इति this, सङ्क्षेपेण briefly, न्यवेदयत् revealed.

#### Explanation

The valiant Hanuman worthy of worship having been honoured by both Jambavan and Angada was pleased. He responded briefly, saying 'Saw Sita'.

---

### Shloka 36

#### Shloka Text

निषसाद च हस्तेन गृहीत्वा वालिनस्सुतम्।।5.57.36।। रमणीये वनोद्देशे महेन्द्रस्य गिरेस्तदा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, वालिनः Vali's, सुतम् son, हस्तेन with hand, गृहीत्वा holding, महेन्द्रस्य गिरेः Mahendra mountain, रमणीये beautiful, वनोद्धेशे place of the garden, निषसाद च sat down.

#### Explanation

Then holding the hand of Vali's son he sat down at a beautiful location of the garden of mountain Mahendra.

---

### Shloka 37

#### Shloka Text

हनुमानब्रवीद्धृष्टस्तदा तान्वानरर्षभान्।।5.57.37।। अशोकवनिकासंस्था दृष्टा सा जनकात्मजा। रक्षमाणा सुघोराभी राक्षसीभिरनिन्दिता।।5.57.38।। एकवेणीधरा बाला रामदर्शनलालसा। उपवासपरिश्रान्ता जटिला मलिना कृशा।।5.57.39।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, हृष्टः became happy, हनुमान् Hanuman, तान् them, वानरर्षभान् bull among vanaras, अब्रवीत् said, अशोकवनिकासंस्था in the groves of Ashoka trees, सुघोराभिः very dreadful, राक्षसीभिः demons, रक्ष्यमाणा guarded by, अनिन्दिता blameless lady, एकवेणीधरा having one braid, बाला young, रामदर्शनलालसा with eagerness to see Rama, उपवासपरिश्रान्ता emaciated due to fasting, जटिला hair matted, मलिना soiled, कृशा weakened, सा जनकात्मजा that Janaka's daughter, दृष्टा saw.

#### Explanation

Hanuman felt happy and told Angada, the bull of vanaras, "I saw Sita in the groves of Ashoka guarded by dreadful ogres. She is blameless, eager to see Rama. She is wearing a single braid of hair. She is young and emaciated due to fasting. Her hair is matted and soiled".

---

### Shloka 38

#### Shloka Text

हनुमानब्रवीद्धृष्टस्तदा तान्वानरर्षभान्।।5.57.37।। अशोकवनिकासंस्था दृष्टा सा जनकात्मजा। रक्षमाणा सुघोराभी राक्षसीभिरनिन्दिता।।5.57.38।। एकवेणीधरा बाला रामदर्शनलालसा। उपवासपरिश्रान्ता जटिला मलिना कृशा।।5.57.39।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, हृष्टः became happy, हनुमान् Hanuman, तान् them, वानरर्षभान् bull among vanaras, अब्रवीत् said, अशोकवनिकासंस्था in the groves of Ashoka trees, सुघोराभिः very dreadful, राक्षसीभिः demons, रक्ष्यमाणा guarded by, अनिन्दिता blameless lady, एकवेणीधरा having one braid, बाला young, रामदर्शनलालसा with eagerness to see Rama, उपवासपरिश्रान्ता emaciated due to fasting, जटिला hair matted, मलिना soiled, कृशा weakened, सा जनकात्मजा that Janaka's daughter, दृष्टा saw.

#### Explanation

Hanuman felt happy and told Angada, the bull of vanaras, "I saw Sita in the groves of Ashoka guarded by dreadful ogres. She is blameless, eager to see Rama. She is wearing a single braid of hair. She is young and emaciated due to fasting. Her hair is matted and soiled".

---

### Shloka 39

#### Shloka Text

हनुमानब्रवीद्धृष्टस्तदा तान्वानरर्षभान्।।5.57.37।। अशोकवनिकासंस्था दृष्टा सा जनकात्मजा। रक्षमाणा सुघोराभी राक्षसीभिरनिन्दिता।।5.57.38।। एकवेणीधरा बाला रामदर्शनलालसा। उपवासपरिश्रान्ता जटिला मलिना कृशा।।5.57.39।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, हृष्टः became happy, हनुमान् Hanuman, तान् them, वानरर्षभान् bull among vanaras, अब्रवीत् said, अशोकवनिकासंस्था in the groves of Ashoka trees, सुघोराभिः very dreadful, राक्षसीभिः demons, रक्ष्यमाणा guarded by, अनिन्दिता blameless lady, एकवेणीधरा having one braid, बाला young, रामदर्शनलालसा with eagerness to see Rama, उपवासपरिश्रान्ता emaciated due to fasting, जटिला hair matted, मलिना soiled, कृशा weakened, सा जनकात्मजा that Janaka's daughter, दृष्टा saw.

#### Explanation

Hanuman felt happy and told Angada, the bull of vanaras, "I saw Sita in the groves of Ashoka guarded by dreadful ogres. She is blameless, eager to see Rama. She is wearing a single braid of hair. She is young and emaciated due to fasting. Her hair is matted and soiled".

---

### Shloka 40

#### Shloka Text

ततो दृष्टेति वचनं महार्थममृतोपमम्। निशम्य मारुतेस्सर्वे मुदिता वानराभवन्।।5.57.40।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, दृष्टा seeing, इति thus, मारुतेः Hanuman, महार्थम् most meaningful, अमृतोपमम् like nectar, वचनम् these words, निशम्य hearing, सर्वे all, वानराः vanara, मुदिताः joyful, अभवन् remained.

#### Explanation

Then hearing Hanuman's meaningful and nectarlike words all the vanaras rejoiced.

---

### Shloka 41

#### Shloka Text

क्ष्वेलन्त्यन्ये नदन्त्यन्ये गर्जन्त्यन्ये महाबलाः। चक्रुः किलकिलामन्ये प्रतिगर्जन्ति चापरे।।5.57.41।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाबलाः powerful, अन्ये others, क्ष्वेलन्ति howled, अन्ये some others, नदन्ति chattered, अन्ये some others, गर्जन्ति roared like lion, अन्ये some, किलकिलाम् screamed, चक्रुः round and round, आपरे went, प्रतिगर्जन्ति echoed the roaring.

#### Explanation

Some powerful vanaras howled, some chattered, some roared and some screamed, going round and round and others echoed the sounds.

---

### Shloka 42

#### Shloka Text

केचिदुच्छ्रितलाङ्गूलाः प्रहृष्टाः कपिकुञ्जराः। आयताञ्चितदीर्घाणि लाङ्गूलानि प्रविव्यधुः।।5.57.42।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

प्रहृष्टाः very exited with joy, केचित् a few, कपिकुञ्जराः elephants among monkeys, उच्छ्रितलाङ्गूलाः lifted their tails, आयताञ्चितदीर्घाणि beautiful and long ones, लाङ्गूलानि tails, प्रविव्यधुः hit on the ground.

#### Explanation

Some elephantlike vanaras who were excited and felt overjoyed lifted their long and beautiful tails and hit them on the ground.

---

### Shloka 43

#### Shloka Text

अपरे च हनूमन्तं वानरा वारणोपमम्। आप्लुत्य गिरिशृङ्गेभ्यस्संस्पृशन्ति स्म हर्षिताः।।5.57.43।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

आपरे a few, वानराः vanaras, हर्षिताः in happiness, गिरिशृङ्गेभ्यः from the top of the mountain, आप्लुत्य jumped down, वारणोपमम् elephantlike, हनूमन्तम् Hanuman, संस्पृशन्ति स्म hugged.

#### Explanation

A few vanaras jumped down from the mountain top and hugged the elephantlike Hanuman.

---

### Shloka 44

#### Shloka Text

उक्तवाक्यं हनूमन्तमङ्गदस्तमथाब्रवीत्। सर्वेषां हरिवीराणां मध्ये वचनमुत्तमम्।।5.57.44।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथ and then, अङ्गदः Angada, उक्तवाक्यम् words spoken, हनूमन्तम् by Hanuman, सर्वेषाम् all of them, हरिवीराणाम् vanara heroes, मध्ये midst, उत्तमम् best, वचनम् words, अब्रवीत् spoke.

#### Explanation

And hearing Hanuman's sweetest words, Angada addressed the heroic vanaras

---

### Shloka 45

#### Shloka Text

सत्त्वे वीर्ये न ते कश्चित्समो वानर विद्यते। यदवप्लुत्य विस्तीर्णं सागरं पुनरागतः।।5.57.45।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वानर vanara, यत् such, विस्तीर्णम् wide, सागरम् ocean, अवप्लुत्य leaping, पुनः again, आगतः returned, सत्त्वे in strength, वीर्ये in valour, ते to you, समः equal, कश्चित् none, न विद्यते not known.

#### Explanation

"O vanara you have leaped such a wide ocean and have returned. In strength and valour. There is none equal to you.

---

### Shloka 46

#### Shloka Text

अहो स्वामिनि ते भक्तिरहो वीर्यमहो धृतिः। दिष्ट्या दृष्टा त्वया देवी रामपत्नी यशस्विनी।।5.57.46।। दिष्ट्या त्यक्ष्यति काकुत्स्थ श्शोकं सीतावियोगजम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

स्वामिनि to the king, ते to you, भक्तिः devotion, अहो Oh, वीर्यम् valour, अहो Oh, धृतिः fortitude, अहो Oh, दिष्ट्या courage, त्वया of yours, रामपत्नी Rama's wife, यशस्विनी illustrious, देवी wife, दृष्टा saw, दिष्ट्या our luck, काकुत्स्थः Rama, सीतावियोगजम् born of separation from Sita, शोकम् grief, त्यक्ष्यति will be removed.

#### Explanation

"O your devotion to the king, O your valour, fortitude  and courage is admirable. You could see Rama's wife, an illustrious lady. This is a matter of our good luck. Rama will (now) shed the sorrow due to separation from Sita.

---

### Shloka 47

#### Shloka Text

ततोऽङ्गदं हनूमन्तं जाम्बवन्तं च वानराः।।5.57.47।। परिवार्य प्रमुदिता भेजिरे विपुलाश्शिलाः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, वानराः vanaras, प्रमुदिताः very happily, अङ्गदम् Angada's, हनूमन्तम् Hanuman, जाम्बवन्तं च and Jambavan, परिवार्य and going round, विपुलाः extensive, शिलाः rocks, भेजिरे betook to.

#### Explanation

Then the vanaras went round all the three Angada, Hanuman and Jambavan. Out of joy they lay down on the huge rocks.

---

### Shloka 48

#### Shloka Text

श्रोतुकामास्समुद्रस्य लङ्घनं वानरोत्तमाः।।5.57.48।। दर्शनं चापि लङ्कायास्सीताया रावणस्य च। तस्थुः प्राञ्जलयस्सर्वे हनुमद्वचनोन्मुखाः।।5.57.49।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सर्वे all, वानरोत्तमाः best of vanaras, समुद्रस्य at the ocean, लङ्घनम् crossing, लङ्कायाः of Lanka, सीतायाः about Sita, रावणस्य च and Ravana, दर्शनं चापि and seeing, श्रोतुकामाः longing to listen, प्राञ्जलयः with folded palms, हनुमद्वचनोन्मुखाः from the mouth of Hanuman, तस्थुः kept watching.

#### Explanation

All the vanaras longing to listen to Hanuman about his crossing the ocean, seeing Lanka, Sita and Ravana kept looking at the face of Hanuman.

---

### Shloka 49

#### Shloka Text

श्रोतुकामास्समुद्रस्य लङ्घनं वानरोत्तमाः।।5.57.48।। दर्शनं चापि लङ्कायास्सीताया रावणस्य च। तस्थुः प्राञ्जलयस्सर्वे हनुमद्वचनोन्मुखाः।।5.57.49।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation
