मया च कथितं तस्मै वालिनश्च वधं प्रति। तत्र साहाय्यहेतोर्मे समयं कर्तुमर्हसि।।5.58.137।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मया च I also, वालिनः Vali, वधं प्रति will kill in turn, तस्मै your, कथितम् story, तत्र thereupon, मे to me, सहाय्यहेतोः will help, समयम् agreement, कर्तुम् will do (said), अर्हसि is proper.

#### Explanation

'Sugriva told the story of Vali in turn and asked for his help and to make agreement to kill Vali.

---

### Shloka 138

#### Shloka Text

वालिना हृतराज्येन सुग्रीवेण महाप्रभुः। चक्रेऽग्निसाक्षिकं सख्यं राघवस्सह लक्ष्मणः।।5.58.138।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाप्रभुः that great lord, सह लक्ष्मणः accompanied by Lakshmana, राघवः Rama, वालिना Vali, हृतराज्येन kingdom usurped, सुग्रीवेण by Sugriva, अग्निसाक्षिकम् in the presence of fire as witness, सख्यम् ally, चक्रे made.

#### Explanation

With fire as witness the great lord, Rama along with Lakshmana made friendship with Sugriva whose kingdom had been usurped.

---

### Shloka 139

#### Shloka Text

तेन वालिनमुत्पाट्य शरेणैकेन संयुगे। वानराणां महाराजः कृतस्स प्लवतां प्रभुः।।5.58.139।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तेन and then, संयुगे in the combat, एकेन with a single, शरेण arrow, वालिनम् Vali, उत्पाट्य slayed, प्लवताम् for vanaras, प्रभुः king, सः he, वानराणाम् vanaras, महाराजः lord, कृतः did.

#### Explanation

'In the combat Rama killed Vali with a single arrow and made Sugriva the king of vanaras.

---

### Shloka 140

#### Shloka Text

तस्य साहाय्यमस्माभिः कार्यं सर्वात्मना त्विह। तेन प्रस्थापितस्तुभ्यं समीपमिह धर्मतः।।5.58.140।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इह here, अस्माभिः we also, सर्वात्मना all of us, तस्य to him, सहाय्यम् help, कार्यम् in this task, तेन so, तुभ्यम् surely, समीपम् to you, धर्मतः righteously, प्रस्थापितः sent to convey the message.

#### Explanation

'We too have to help him in this task. So a message is sent to you on righteous grounds.

---

### Shloka 141

#### Shloka Text

क्षिप्रमानीयतां सीता दीयतां राघवाय च। यावन्न हरयो वीरा विधमन्ति बलं तव।।5.58.141।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वीराः heroic, हरयः vanaras, तव your, बलम् army, यावत् entire, न विधमन्ति before exterminating your army, सीता Sita, क्षिप्रम् at once, आनीयताम् get, राघवाय to Rama, दीयतां च you may give.

#### Explanation

'Before the heroic vanaras exterminate your entire army, return Sita at once to Rama.

---

### Shloka 142

#### Shloka Text

वानराणां प्रभावो हि न केन विदितः पुरा। देवतानां सङ्काशं च ये गच्छन्ति निमन्त्रिताः।।5.58.142।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ये who, निमन्त्रिताः summoned to, देवतानाम् even devatas, सङ्काशम् for the help, गच्छन्ति पुरा earlier, वानराणाम् by the vanaras, प्रभावः power, केन who, न विदितः does not know.

#### Explanation

'Who knows not the power of Vanaras in the past? Even gods had sought their help.

---

### Shloka 143

#### Shloka Text

इति वानरराजस्त्वामाहेत्यभिहितो मया। मामैक्षत ततः क्रुद्धश्चक्षुषा प्रदहन्निव।।5.58.143।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इति this, वानरराजः king of vanaras, त्वाम् to you, आह to tell, इति this, मया my, अभिहितः wishing your welfare, ततः that, क्रुद्धः angrily, चक्षुषा eyes, प्रदहन्निव as if to burn, माम् at me, ऐक्षत looked.

#### Explanation

'This I was asked to tell you by the king of vanaras wishing your welfare'. 'Ravana then angrily looked at me as if to burn me.

---

### Shloka 144

#### Shloka Text

तेन वध्योऽहमाज्ञप्तो रक्षसा रौद्रकर्मणा। मत्प्रभावमविज्ञाय रावणेन दुरात्मना।।5.58.144।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रौद्रकर्मणा one who performs dreadful actions, रक्षसा by the demon, दुरात्मना evilminded one, तेन रावणेन by that Ravana, मत्प्रभावम् my power, अविज्ञाय not knowing, अहम् I, वध्यः to kill, आज्ञप्तः ordered.

#### Explanation

"The evilminded demon, Ravana, who perpetrates dreadful acts ordered my slaughter, not knowing my power.

---

### Shloka 145

#### Shloka Text

ततो विभीषणो नाम तस्य भ्राता महामतिः। तेन राक्षसराजोऽसौ याचितो मम कारणात्।।5.58.145।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विभीषणो नाम named Vibhishana, महामतिः very pious, तस्य his, भ्राता brother, ततः then, तेन him, असौ was there, राक्षसराजः demon king, मम कारणात् on account of me, याचितः  solicited.

#### Explanation

"Vibhisana, a very pious brother of the demon king, interceded on my behalf.

---

### Shloka 146

#### Shloka Text

नैवं राक्षसशार्दूल त्यज्यतामेष निश्चयः। राजशास्त्रव्यपेतो हि मार्गः संसेव्यते त्वया।।5.58.146।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षसशार्दूल tiger among demons, एवम् in that way, न not, एषः that way, निश्चयः decision, त्यज्यताम् give up, राजशास्त्रव्यपेतः according to science of royal polity, मार्गः course, त्वया by you, संसेव्यते हि indeed not permitted.

#### Explanation

"Vibhishana, a tiger among demons, asked Ravana to give up his decision as it was a forbidden course according to the science of royal polity.

---

### Shloka 147

#### Shloka Text

दूतवध्या न दृष्टा हि राजशास्त्रेषु राक्षस। दूतेन वेदितव्यं च यथार्थं हितवादिना।।5.58.147।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राक्षस demon, राजशास्त्रेषु by the royal code, दूतवध्या killing of emissary, न दृष्टा हि not seen indeed, हितवादिना wellwisher, दूतेन messenger, यथार्थम् truth, वेदितव्यम् has to convey.

#### Explanation

'The royal code prohibits the murder of an emissary. A wellwishing emissary should convey the truth.

---

### Shloka 148

#### Shloka Text

सुमहत्यपराधेऽपि दूतस्यातुलविक्रमः। विरूपकरणं दृष्टं न वधोऽस्तीति शास्त्रतः।।5.58.148।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अतुलविक्रमः one of immeasureable courage, सुमहति any kind, अपराधेऽपि harm done, दूतस्य at emissary, विरूपकरणम् may be mutilated, दृष्टम् seen, शास्त्रतः in the sastras,वधः killing,नास्ति not there.

#### Explanation

'O Ravana of immeasurable courage even if the emissary has done any kind of harm he may be mutilated but not murdered. Nowhere do the sastras allow killing (of an ambassador).

---

### Shloka 149

#### Shloka Text

विभीषणेनैवमुक्तो रावणस्सन्दिदेश तान्। राक्षसानेतदेवास्य लाङ्गूलं दह्यतामिति।।5.58.149।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विभीषणेन by Vibhishana, एवम् in that way, उक्तः having spoken, रावणः Ravana, अस्य his, एतत् all that, लाङ्गूलम् tail, दह्यतामिति to burn, तान् राक्षसान् to the demons, सन्दिदेश instructed.

#### Explanation

'On hearing Vibhishana, Ravana instructed the demons to burn my tail.

---

### Shloka 150

#### Shloka Text

ततस्तस्य वचः श्रुत्वा मम पुच्छं समन्ततः। वेष्टितं शणवल्कैश्च जीर्णैः कार्पासजैः पटैः।।5.58.150।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, तस्य those, वचः words, श्रुत्वा having heard, मम my, पुच्छम् tail, समन्ततः all of them together, शणवल्कैः saris made of fibre, जीर्णैः tattered, कार्पासजैः of cotton, पटैः rags, वेष्टितम् wrapped.

#### Explanation

"Having heard the king's words, all the demons collected and held my tail, wrapped it with saris of fibres and tattered rags of cotton.

---

### Shloka 151

#### Shloka Text

राक्षसाः सिद्धसन्नाहास्ततस्ते चण्डविक्रमाः। तदाऽदह्यन्त मे पुच्छं निघ्नन्त: काष्ठमुष्टिभिः।।5.58.151।। बद्धस्य बहुभिः पाशैर्यन्त्रितस्य च राक्षसैः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, सिद्धसन्नाहाः got ready, चण्डविक्रमाः who had terrific courage, राक्षसाः demons, काष्ठमुष्टिभिः with firesticks and fists, निघ्नन्तः hit, बहुभिः many, पाशैः ropes, बद्धस्य tied, राक्षसैः by the demons, यन्त्रितस्य fastened, मे me, पुच्छम् tail, तदा then, अदह्यन्त set fire.

#### Explanation

"Many ferociously courageous demons got ready. They bound me and hit me with firesticks and fists. They fastened my tail with ropes and set in on fire.

---

### Shloka 152

#### Shloka Text

ततस्ते राक्षसाश्शूरा बद्धं मामग्निसंवृतम्।।5.58.152।। अघोषयन्राजमार्गे नगरद्वारमागताः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, शूराः courageous, राक्षसाः by demons, नगरद्वारम् gate of the city, आगताः took, बद्धम् bound, अग्निसंवृतम् set on fire, माम् me, राजमार्गे in the royal street, अघोषयन् announced loudly.

#### Explanation

"The brave demons took me round the city through the royal streets and announced loudly to let every one know that I am bound and that my tail has been set on fire.

---

### Shloka 153

#### Shloka Text

ततोऽहं सुमहद्रूपं संक्षिप्य पुनरात्मनः।।5.58.153।। विमोचयित्वा तं बन्धं प्रकृतिस्थः स्थितः पुनः। आयसं परिघं गृह्य तानि रक्षांस्यसूदयम्।।5.58.154।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, अहम् I, आत्मनः my own, सुमहत् very huge, रूपम् form, पुनः again, संक्षिप्य assumed small form, तं बन्धम् that bondage, विमोचयित्वा got rid off, पुनः again, प्रकृतिस्थः into my natural huge form, स्थितः stayed, आयसम् near by, परिघम् iron beam, गृह्य took up, तानि रक्षांसि that rakshasas, असूदयम् killed.

#### Explanation

"I reduced my form and rid of the bondage. Then again I assumed my original huge form and took up an iron bar lying near and killed all the demons (present).

---

### Shloka 154

#### Shloka Text

ततोऽहं सुमहद्रूपं संक्षिप्य पुनरात्मनः।।5.58.153।। विमोचयित्वा तं बन्धं प्रकृतिस्थः स्थितः पुनः। आयसं परिघं गृह्य तानि रक्षांस्यसूदयम्।।5.58.154।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, अहम् I, आत्मनः my own, सुमहत् very huge, रूपम् form, पुनः again, संक्षिप्य assumed small form, तं बन्धम् that bondage, विमोचयित्वा got rid off, पुनः again, प्रकृतिस्थः into my natural huge form, स्थितः stayed, आयसम् near by, परिघम् iron beam, गृह्य took up, तानि रक्षांसि that rakshasas, असूदयम् killed.

#### Explanation

"I reduced my form and rid of the bondage. Then again I assumed my original huge form and took up an iron bar lying near and killed all the demons (present).

---

### Shloka 155

#### Shloka Text

ततस्तन्नगरद्वारं वेगेनाप्लुतवानहम्। पुच्छेन च प्रदीप्तेन तां पुरीं साट्टगोपुराम्।।5.58.155।। दहाम्यहमसंभ्रान्तो युगान्ताग्निरिव प्रजाः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, अहम् I, वेगेन quickly, तत् that, नगरद्वारम् gate of the city, आप्लुतवान् leapt, अहम् I, असंभ्रान्तः not perplexed, युगान्ताग्निः fire at the end of the dissolution, प्रजाः इव like the world, साट्टप्राकारगोपुराम् boundaries and towering mansions, तां पुरीम् of the city, प्रदीप्तेन by fire, पुच्चेन by the tail, दहामि burnt away.

#### Explanation

"Then I leaped to the entrance gate of the city without being perplexed. With my burning tail I set fire to the boundaries and towering mansions which looked like the world at the end of dissolution.

---

### Shloka 156

#### Shloka Text

विनष्टा जानकी व्यक्तं न ह्यदग्धः प्रदृश्यते।।5.58.156।। लङ्कायां कश्चिदुद्धेशः सर्वा भस्मीकृता पुरी।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

व्यक्तम् after doing so, जानकी Janaki, विनष्टा may be burnt, लङ्कायाम् with Lanka, कश्चित् perhaps, उद्धेशः thinking so, अदग्धः rose up, न प्रदृश्यते हि not even one place was left unburnt, सर्वा completely, पुरी city, भस्मीकृता reduced to ashes

#### Explanation

None

---

### Shloka 157

#### Shloka Text

दहता च मया लङ्कां दग्धा सीता न संशयः।।5.58.157।। रामस्य हि महत्कार्यं मयेदं वितथीकृतम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लङ्काम् Lanka, दहता burnt, मया by me, सीता Sita, दग्धा has burnt, संशयः doubt, न not, मया to me, रामस्य of Rama, इदम् this, महत् great, कार्यम् purpose, वितथीकृतम् has been spoilt.

#### Explanation

"Lanka has been burnt by me and Sita might have also been burnt. There is no doubt. The great purpose of Rama has been spoilt by me'.

---

### Shloka 158

#### Shloka Text

इति शोकसमाविष्टश्चिन्तामहमुपागतः।।5.58.158।। अथाहं वाचमश्रौषं चारणानां शुभाक्षराम्। जानकी न च दग्धेति विस्मयोदन्तभाषिणाम्।।5.58.159।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इति thus, शोकसमाविष्टः overtaken by sorrow, अहम् I, चिन्ताम् worry, उपागतः became, अथ and then, अहम् I, जानकी Janaki, न च दग्धा not been burn, इति this, विस्मयोदन्तभाषिणाम् wonderful utterances, चारणानाम् from charanas, शुभाक्षराम् propitious, वाचम् words, अश्रौषम् heard.

#### Explanation

"Thus I was overtaken by sorrow and worry. Just then I heard the wonderful propitious utterances of charanas announcing that Sita was not burnt.

---

### Shloka 159

#### Shloka Text

इति शोकसमाविष्टश्चिन्तामहमुपागतः।।5.58.158।। अथाहं वाचमश्रौषं चारणानां शुभाक्षराम्। जानकी न च दग्धेति विस्मयोदन्तभाषिणाम्।।5.58.159।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इति thus, शोकसमाविष्टः overtaken by sorrow, अहम् I, चिन्ताम् worry, उपागतः became, अथ and then, अहम् I, जानकी Janaki, न च दग्धा not been burn, इति this, विस्मयोदन्तभाषिणाम् wonderful utterances, चारणानाम् from charanas, शुभाक्षराम् propitious, वाचम् words, अश्रौषम् heard.

#### Explanation

"Thus I was overtaken by sorrow and worry. Just then I heard the wonderful propitious utterances of charanas announcing that Sita was not burnt.

---

### Shloka 160

#### Shloka Text

ततो मे बुद्धिरुत्पन्ना श्रुत्वा तामद्भुतां गिरम्। अदग्धा जानकीत्येवं निमित्तैश्चोपलक्षिता।।5.58.160।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अद्भुताम् amazing, तां गिरम् those words, श्रुत्वा after hearing, ततः then, जानकी Janaki, अदग्धा not been burnt, इत्येवम् this way, मे my, बुद्धिः knew, उत्पन्ना arose, निमित्तैश्च on account of her, उपलक्षिता I understood.

#### Explanation

"It was amazing to hear the words of the charanas. On hearing that Jankai had not been burnt, I understood that it is on her account (that he is also not burnt  although his tail was ignited.)

---

### Shloka 161

#### Shloka Text

दीप्यमाने तु लाङ्गूले न मां दहति पावकः। हृदयं च प्रहृष्टं मे वातास्सुरभिगन्धिनः।।5.58.161।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लाङ्गूले tail, दीप्यमाने was burning, पावकः by fire, माम् but I, न दहति was not burnt, मे my, हृदयं च from my heart, प्रहृष्टम् joy, वाताः wind, सुरभिगन्धिनः very sweet fragrance.

#### Explanation

"My tail was burning but I was not burnt by fire. There was joy in my heart. The wind carried sweet fragrance.

---

### Shloka 162

#### Shloka Text

तैर्निमित्तैश्च दृष्टाथै: कारणैश्च महागुणैः। ऋषिवाक्यैश्च सिद्धार्थैरभवं हृष्टमानसः।।5.58.162।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दृष्टार्थैः by such signs, तैः those two, निमित्तै: reasons, महागुणैः great virtues, कारणैश्च and for these reasons, सिद्धार्थैः will accomplish, ऋषिवाक्यैश्च uttered by the words of sages, हृष्टमानसः felt very happy, अभवम् attained.

#### Explanation

"For these two auspicious signs and the highly propitious words uttered by the sages I felt glad at heart.

---

### Shloka 163

#### Shloka Text

पुनर्दृष्ट्वा च वैदेहीं विसृष्टश्च तया पुनः। ततः पर्वतमासाद्य तत्रारिष्टमहं पुनः।।5.58.163।। प्रतिप्लवनमारेभे युष्मद्धर्शनकांक्षया।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वैदेहीम् to Vaidehi, पुनः again, दृष्ट्वा च and to see, तया like that, पुनः again, विसृष्टः seeking permission, ततः then, अरिष्टपर्वतम् mountain Arishta, पुनः again, आसाद्य ascended, अहम् I, युष्मद्धर्शनकांक्षया to see you all, प्रतिप्लवनम् leapt again, आरेभे started.

#### Explanation

None

---

### Shloka 164

#### Shloka Text

ततः पवनचन्द्रार्कसिद्धगन्धर्वसेवितम्।।5.58.164।। पन्थानमहमाक्रम्य भवतो दृष्टवानिह।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, अहम् I, पवनचन्द्रार्कसिद्धगन्धर्वसेवितम् by Windgod, the Moon, the Sun, the siddhas and the gandharvas, पन्थानम् through the path travelled by, आश्रित्य came, इह here, भवतः to you, दृष्टवान् to see.

#### Explanation

"Passing along the path of the Windgod, the Moon, the Sun, the siddhas and the gandharvas  I came to see you.

---

### Shloka 165

#### Shloka Text

राघवस्य प्रभावेण भवतां चैव तेजसा।।5.58.165।। सुग्रीवस्य च कार्यार्थं मया सर्वमनुष्ठितम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राघवस्य Rama's, प्रभावेण by the power, भवताम् your, तेजसा चैव grace, सुग्रीवस्य Sugriva's, कार्यार्थं च on his account, मया for me, सर्वम् all, अनुष्ठितम् was favourable.

#### Explanation

"Everything was favourable to me by the power of Rama, by your goodwill and for the great purpose of Sugriva.

---

### Shloka 166

#### Shloka Text

एतत्सर्वं मया तत्र यथावदुपपादितम्।।5.58.166।। अत्र यन्न कृतं शेषं तत्सर्वं क्रियतामिति।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एतत् all that, सर्वम् entire, तत्र there, मया by me, यथावत् as it is, उपपादितम् presented, अत्र there, यत् whatever, न कृतम् not done, शेषम् remaining task, तत् that, सर्वम् everything, क्रियताम् will do.

#### Explanation

"Let all that could not be done there and still remains to be done, be accomplished by you. I have presented the entire thing". इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये सुन्दरकाण्डे अष्टपञ्चाशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the fiftyeighth sarga of Sundarakanda of the holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 59

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

एतदाख्याय तत्सर्वं हनुमान्मारुतात्मजः। भूयस्समुपचक्राम वचनं वक्तुमुत्तरम्।।5.59.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मारुतात्मजः son of the Windgod, हनुमान् Hanuman, सर्वम् entire, तत् एतत् all that, आख्याय having narrated, भूयः whatever happened, उत्तरं वचनम् the good words, वक्तुम् told, समुपचक्राम again started telling.

#### Explanation

Hanuman, son of the Windgod having narrated in detail all that had happened again started disclosing further details৷৷

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

सफलो राघवोद्योग स्सुग्रीवस्य च सम्भ्रमः। शीलमासाद्य सीताया मम च प्रवणं मनः।।5.59.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राघवोद्योगः Rama's exertions, सुग्रीवस्य Sugriva's, सम्भ्रमः enthusiasm, सीतायाः Sita's, शीलम् conduct, आसाद्य attained, सफलः successl, मम I am, मनश्च in my mind, प्रवणम् developed devotion.

#### Explanation

"It is due to Rama's exertions, Sugriva's endeavour and Sita's conduct that I have been successful. My devotion to Sita has further deepened.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

तपसा निर्दहेल्लोकान्क्रुद्धो वा निर्दहेदपि। सर्वधातिप्रवृद्धोऽसौ रावणो राक्षसाधिपः ॥ 5.59.3 ॥

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सर्वधा ever, अतिप्रवृद्ध: rich in asceticism, असौ he is, राक्षसाधिपः demon king, तपसा his penance, लोकान् in the world, निर्दहेत् can burn, कुद्धो वापि even though angry, निर्दहेदपि not burnt

#### Explanation

"The demon king is rich in asceticism. He can burn the whole world with the power of his penance. In spite of Sita's anger he was not burnt(Hanuman gives the reason for Ravana's survival).

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

तस्य तां स्पृशतो गात्रं तपसा न विनाशितम्। न तदग्निशिखा कुर्यात्संस्पृष्टा पाणिना सती।।5.59.4।। जनकस्यात्मजा कुर्याद्यत्क्रोधकलुषीकृता।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ताम् her, स्पृशतः with mere touch, तस्य of her, गात्रम् body, तपसा by penance, न विनाशितम् not destroyed, क्रोधकलुषीकृता if enraged (she) can destroy, जनकस्य Janaka's, आत्मजा daughter, यत् such, कुर्यात् she can do, तत् that, पाणिना by hand, संस्पृष्टासती touched with hand, अग्निशिखा like flame of fire, न कुर्यात् not done so.

#### Explanation

"Sita can burn Ravana with the mere touch of her body but because of the power of his penance, he was not burnt even though he touched her hand. If enraged, Janaki can burn the whole world (by virtue of her chastity) in a way that even the flame of fire can not do. (But she desired that her husband should kill him.)

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

जाम्बवत्प्रमुखान् सर्वाननुज्ञाप्य महाहरीन्।।5.59.5।। अस्मिन्नेवं गते कार्ये भवतां च निवेदिते। न्याय्यं स्म सह वैदेह्या द्रष्टुं तौ पार्थिवात्मजौ।।5.59.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भवताम् to you, निवेदिते relating to you, अस्मिन् your, कार्ये task, एवं गते to go there, जाम्बवत्प्रमुखान् with Jambavan and other important people, महाहरीन् great vanara heroes, समनुज्ञाप्य with their permission, वैदेह्या सह along with Vaidehi, तौ both, पार्थिवात्मजौ king's sons, द्रष्टुम् to see, न्याय्यं स्म is proper.

#### Explanation

None

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

जाम्बवत्प्रमुखान् सर्वाननुज्ञाप्य महाहरीन्।।5.59.5।। अस्मिन्नेवं गते कार्ये भवतां च निवेदिते। न्याय्यं स्म सह वैदेह्या द्रष्टुं तौ पार्थिवात्मजौ।।5.59.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भवताम् to you, निवेदिते relating to you, अस्मिन् your, कार्ये task, एवं गते to go there, जाम्बवत्प्रमुखान् with Jambavan and other important people, महाहरीन् great vanara heroes, समनुज्ञाप्य with their permission, वैदेह्या सह along with Vaidehi, तौ both, पार्थिवात्मजौ king's sons, द्रष्टुम् to see, न्याय्यं स्म is proper.

#### Explanation

None

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

अहमेकोऽपि पर्याप्तस्सराक्षसगणां पुरीम्। तां लङ्कां तरसा हन्तुं रावणं च महाबलम्।।5.59.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I, एकोऽपि alone, सराक्षसगणाम् all the demon troops, तां लङ्कां पुरीम् and that city of Lanka, महाबलम् the great army, रावणं च and Ravana, तरसा quickly, हन्तुम् can kill, पर्याप्तः is enough.

#### Explanation

None

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

किं पुन स्सहितो वीरैर्बलवद्भिः कृतात्मभिः। कृतास्त्रै: प्लवगैश्शूरैर्भवद्भिविजयैषिभिः।।5.59.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

बलवद्भिः strong, कृतात्मभिः wise, कृतास्त्रै: accomplished, शूरैः heroic, विजयैषिभिः those desiring victory, प्लवगैः monkeys, भवद्भिः are with you, सहितः together, किं पुनः why speak again?

#### Explanation

"When strong, wise and accomplished persons are there with me desiring victory  why speak again?

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

अहं तु रावणं युद्धे ससैन्यं सपुरस्सरम्। सहपुत्त्रं वधिष्यामि सहोदरयुतं युधि।।5.59.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहं तु I also, युद्धे in war, ससैन्यम् all the army, सपुरःसरम् his followers, सपुत्त्रम् his sons, सहोदरयुतम् his brothers, रावणम् of Ravana, युधि in war, वधिRष्यामि can kill.

#### Explanation

"I can also kill all the army,  his followers, sons and brothers of Ravana in war.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

ब्राह्ममैन्द्रं च रौद्रं च वायव्यं वारणं तथा। यदि शक्रजितोऽस्त्राणि दुर्निरीक्षाणि संयुगे।।5.59.10।। तान्यहं वधिष्यामि हनिष्यामि च राक्षसान्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ब्राह्मम् Brahma's, ऐन्द्रं च even Indra's, रौद्रं च Rudra's, वायव्यम् or Windgod's, तथा similarly, वारुणम् Varuna's, शक्रजितः Indrajit, अस्त्राणि missiles, दुर्निरीक्षाणि though difficult to see, यदि such, तानि of them, संयुगे in war, वधिष्यामि will kill, राक्षसान् demon, हनिष्यामि च and destroy them.

#### Explanation

"Even if the missiles used by Indrajit are Brahmastra, Indrastra, Rudrastra, Vayavastra, Varunastra which are difficult to see. I will destroy them in war and kill them.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

भवतामभ्यनुज्ञातो विक्रमो मे रुणद्धि तम्।।5.59.11।। मयातुला विसृष्टा हि शैलवृष्टिर्निरन्तरा। देवानपि रणे हन्यात्किं पुनस्तान्निशाचरान्।।5.59.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भवताम् by you, अभ्यनुज्ञातः now permit me, मे I, विक्रमः with my valour, तम् them, रुणद्धि  shattered,मया by me, विसृष्टा sent forth, अतुला matchless, निरन्तरा ceaseless, शैलवृष्टिः shower of rocks, रणे in war, देवानपि even gods, हन्यात् will die, तान् those, निशाचरान् nightrangers, किं पुनः what to say ?

#### Explanation

Now if I am permitted, I shall repulse them and destroy them with my valour. A  ceaseless shower of rocks sent forth by me in war is enough to destroy even gods what to say about the nightrangers?

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

भवतामभ्यनुज्ञातो विक्रमो मे रुणद्धि तम्।।5.59.11।। मयातुला विसृष्टा हि शैलवृष्टिर्निरन्तरा। देवानपि रणे हन्यात्किं पुनस्तान्निशाचरान्।।5.59.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भवताम् by you, अभ्यनुज्ञातः now permit me, मे I, विक्रमः with my valour, तम् them, रुणद्धि  shattered,मया by me, विसृष्टा sent forth, अतुला matchless, निरन्तरा ceaseless, शैलवृष्टिः shower of rocks, रणे in war, देवानपि even gods, हन्यात् will die, तान् those, निशाचरान् nightrangers, किं पुनः what to say ?

#### Explanation

Now if I am permitted, I shall repulse them and destroy them with my valour. A  ceaseless shower of rocks sent forth by me in war is enough to destroy even gods what to say about the nightrangers?

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

सागरोऽप्यतियाद्वेलां मन्दरः प्रचलेदपि। न जाम्बवन्तं समरे कम्पयेदरिवाहिनी।।5.59.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सागरः ocean, वेलाम् shore, अतियादपि exceeds also, मन्दरः mountain Mandara, प्रचलेदपि may shake up, समरे in war, अरिवाहिनी flow of enemies, जाम्बवन्तम् Jambavan, न कम्पयेत् not move.

#### Explanation

"The ocean may exceed its limits, the mountain Mandara may be shaken from its position but  no hostile army can move Jambavan in war.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

सर्वराक्षससङ्घानां राक्षसा ये च पूर्वकाः। अलमेको विनाशाय वीरो वालिसुतः कपिः।।5.59.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वीरः hero, वालिसुतः son of Vali, कपिः vanara, एकः alone, सर्वराक्षससङ्घानाम् all the demon hordes, पूर्वकाः led, ये by him, विनाशाय to destroy, अलम् enough.

#### Explanation

None

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

पनसस्योरुवेगेन नीलस्य च महात्मनः। मन्दरोऽप्यवशीर्येत किंपुनर्युधि राक्षसाः।।5.59.15।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पनसस्य Panasa's, महात्मनः great soul, नीलस्य च even Nila, उरुवेगेन by the speed of his thighs, मन्दरोऽपि even Mandara mountain, अवशीर्येत would be shattered, युधि in battle, राक्षसाः demons, किं पुनः what to say again.

#### Explanation

"Even the mountain Mandara would be shattered by great Panasa or Nila by the speed of their thighs. What to say in a battle against ogres?

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

सदेवासुरयक्षेषु गन्धर्वोरगपक्षिषु। मैन्दस्य प्रतियोद्धारं शंसत द्विविदस्य वा।।5.59.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सदेवासुरयक्षेषु gods, and yakshas, गन्दर्वोरगपक्षिषु gandharvas, nagas and birds, मैन्दस्य Mainda's, द्विविदस्य वा or Dvivida, प्रतियोद्दारम् who can oppose in battle, शंसत tell me.

#### Explanation

"Who among gods, yakshas, gandharvas, nagas and birds can contend Mainda or Dvivida in battle?Tell me.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

अश्विपुत्रौ महाभागावेतौ प्लवगसत्तमौ। एतयोः प्रतियोद्दारं न पश्यामि रणाजिरे।।5.59.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अश्विपुत्रौ sons of Aswini, एतौ so also, महाभागौ  outstanding, प्लवगसत्तमौ foremost fighters, रणाजिरे  proud of fighting war, एतयोः these people, प्रतियोद्दारम् to oppose, न पश्यामि I do not see any.

#### Explanation

"The (two) sons of Aswini are outstanding and foremost fighters and proud of waging war. Who can oppose them? I do not see any one.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

पितामहवरोत्सेकात्परमं दर्पमास्थितौ। अमृतप्राशिनावेतौ सर्ववानरसत्तमौ।।5.59.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पितामहवरोत्सेकात् by virtue of the boons received from Brahma, परमम् very, दर्पम् proud, आस्थितौ filled with, एतौ both of them that way, वानरसत्तमौ foremost of vanaras, अमृतप्राशिनौ have consumed nectar of immortality.

#### Explanation

"By virtue of the boons received from Brahma, they are very proud. They have also consumed nectar of immortality and are foremost among vanaras.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

अश्विनोर्माननार्थं हि सर्वलोकपितामहः। सर्वावध्यत्वमतुलमनयोर्दत्तवान्पुरा।।5.59.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पुरा earlier, सर्वलोकपितामहः Brahma the grandfather of all worlds, अश्विनोः for Aswini sons, माननार्थम् to honour, अनयोः given, अतुलम् immeasureable, सर्वावध्यत्वम् invulnerability, दत्तवान् given.

#### Explanation

"Earlier Brahma, the grandsire of all worlds has given the Aswini sons immeasurable invulnerability to honour them.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

वरोत्सेकेन मत्तौ च प्रमथ्य महतीं चमूम्। सुराणाममृतं वीरौ पीतवन्तौ प्लवङ्गमौ।।5.59.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वरोत्सेकेन armed with the pride of boons, मत्तौ च and intoxicated, वीरौ heroic, प्लवङ्गमौ monkeys, सुराणाम् of suras, महतीम् vast, चमूम् प्रमथ्य army slain, अमृतम् nectar of immortality, पीतवन्तौ drank.

#### Explanation

"The heroic monkeys, proud of their boons became intoxicated it and slaughtered vast army and drank the nectar of immortality.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

एतावेव हि सङ्कृद्धौ सवाजिरथकुञ्जराम्। लङ्कां नाशयितुं शक्तौ सर्वे तिष्ठन्तु वानराः।।5.59.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सङ्कृद्धौ if they become angry, एतावेव these two, सवाजिरथकुञ्जराम् the cavalry, chariots and elephants, लङ्काम् Lanka also, नाशयितुम् to destroy, शक्तौ have the power, सर्वे all, वानराः vanaras, तिष्ठन्तु even if they stay away.

#### Explanation

"If these two become angry they have the power to destroy Lanka, the cavalry, chariots and also the elephants even if the vanaras stay away.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

मयैव निहता लङ्का दग्धा भस्मीकृता पुनः। राजमार्गेषु सर्वत्र नाम विश्रावितं मया।।5.59.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

लङ्का Lanka, मयैव myself, निहता burn, राजमार्गेषु in the royal path, सर्वत्र everywhere, मया by me, नाम name, विश्रावितम् become popular.

#### Explanation

"I have burnt Lanka and have made my name popular even on the highways.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

जयत्यतिबलो रामो लक्ष्मणश्च महाबलः। राजा जयति सुग्रीवो राघवेणाभिपालितः।।5.59.23।। अहं कोसलराजस्य दासः पवनसम्भवः। हनुमानिति सर्वत्र नाम विश्रावितं मया।।5.59.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अतिबलः mighty, रामः Rama, जयति will triumph, महाबलः mighty, लक्ष्मणश्च and Lakshmana, राघवेण by Rama, अभिपालितः protected, राजा king, सुग्रीवः Sugriva, जयति will triumph, पवनसम्भवः son of the Windgod, अहम् I, कोसलराजस्य king of Kosala kingdom, दासः servant, हनुमान् Hanuman, इति this, मया by me, नाम name, विश्रावितम् announced.

#### Explanation

"Mighty Rama and Lakshmana and King Sugriva protected by Rama are renowned for their valour. I am Hanuman, son of the Windgod, a servant of Rama. This I announced.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

जयत्यतिबलो रामो लक्ष्मणश्च महाबलः। राजा जयति सुग्रीवो राघवेणाभिपालितः।।5.59.23।। अहं कोसलराजस्य दासः पवनसम्भवः। हनुमानिति सर्वत्र नाम विश्रावितं मया।।5.59.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अतिबलः mighty, रामः Rama, जयति will triumph, महाबलः mighty, लक्ष्मणश्च and Lakshmana, राघवेण by Rama, अभिपालितः protected, राजा king, सुग्रीवः Sugriva, जयति will triumph, पवनसम्भवः son of the Windgod, अहम् I, कोसलराजस्य king of Kosala kingdom, दासः servant, हनुमान् Hanuman, इति this, मया by me, नाम name, विश्रावितम् announced.

#### Explanation

"Mighty Rama and Lakshmana and King Sugriva protected by Rama are renowned for their valour. I am Hanuman, son of the Windgod, a servant of Rama. This I announced.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

अशोकवनिकामध्ये रावणस्य दुरात्मनः। अध स्ताच्छिंशुपावृक्षे साध्वी करुणमास्थिता।।5.59.25।। राक्षसीभिः परिवृता शोकसंतापकर्शिता। मेघलेखापरिवृता चन्द्रलेखेव निष्प्रभा।।5.59.26।।। अचिन्तयन्ती वैदेही रावणं बलदर्पितम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दुरात्मनः wicked, रावणस्य Ravana's, अशोकवनिकामध्ये in the midst of Ashoka garden, शिंशुपावृक्षे Simsupa tree, अधस्तात् under, साध्वी noble lady, राक्षसीभिः by ogresses, परिवृता surrounded, शोकसन्तापकर्शिता tormented by tears, मेघलेखापरिवृता veiled by clouds, चन्द्रलेखेव like the Moonrays, निष्प्रभा without  brightness, बलदर्पितम्  glory, रावणम् Ravana, अचिन्तयन्ती not even thinking, वैदेही Vaidehi, करुणम् piteous, आस्थिता remained.

#### Explanation

"In the wicked, Ravana's Ashoka garden under the Simsupa tree the noble lady Sita surrounded by ogresses, is sitting, tormented by tears. Her brightness is like the Moon's, veiled by flakes of clouds. She does not care for Ravana's glory. She is brooding over Rama only.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

अशोकवनिकामध्ये रावणस्य दुरात्मनः। अध स्ताच्छिंशुपावृक्षे साध्वी करुणमास्थिता।।5.59.25।। राक्षसीभिः परिवृता शोकसंतापकर्शिता। मेघलेखापरिवृता चन्द्रलेखेव निष्प्रभा।।5.59.26।।। अचिन्तयन्ती वैदेही रावणं बलदर्पितम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दुरात्मनः wicked, रावणस्य Ravana's, अशोकवनिकामध्ये in the midst of Ashoka garden, शिंशुपावृक्षे Simsupa tree, अधस्तात् under, साध्वी noble lady, राक्षसीभिः by ogresses, परिवृता surrounded, शोकसन्तापकर्शिता tormented by tears, मेघलेखापरिवृता veiled by clouds, चन्द्रलेखेव like the Moonrays, निष्प्रभा without  brightness, बलदर्पितम्  glory, रावणम् Ravana, अचिन्तयन्ती not even thinking, वैदेही Vaidehi, करुणम् piteous, आस्थिता remained.

#### Explanation

"In the wicked, Ravana's Ashoka garden under the Simsupa tree the noble lady Sita surrounded by ogresses, is sitting, tormented by tears. Her brightness is like the Moon's, veiled by flakes of clouds. She does not care for Ravana's glory. She is brooding over Rama only.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

पतिव्रता च सुश्रोणी अवष्टब्धा च जानकी।।5.59.27।। अनुरक्ता हि वैदेही रामं सर्वात्मना शुभा। अनन्यचित्ता रामे च पौलोमीव पुरन्दरे।।5.59.28।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पतिव्रता chaste woman, सुश्रोणी who has beautiful hips, जानकी Janaki, अवष्टब्धा though bound, शुभा auspicious, वैदेही Vaidehi, सर्वात्मना all her self, रामम् Rama only, अनुरक्ता devoted, पुरन्दरे Indra, पौलोमीव like Sachi the wife of Indra, रामे only on Rama, अनन्यचित्ता always thinking.

#### Explanation

"Chaste Sita of beautiful hips, an auspicious lady though bound is wholly devoted to Rama alone like Sachi, the wife of Indra who was bound by Nahusha.

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

पतिव्रता च सुश्रोणी अवष्टब्धा च जानकी।।5.59.27।। अनुरक्ता हि वैदेही रामं सर्वात्मना शुभा। अनन्यचित्ता रामे च पौलोमीव पुरन्दरे।।5.59.28।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पतिव्रता chaste woman, सुश्रोणी who has beautiful hips, जानकी Janaki, अवष्टब्धा though bound, शुभा auspicious, वैदेही Vaidehi, सर्वात्मना all her self, रामम् Rama only, अनुरक्ता devoted, पुरन्दरे Indra, पौलोमीव like Sachi the wife of Indra, रामे only on Rama, अनन्यचित्ता always thinking.

#### Explanation

"Chaste Sita of beautiful hips, an auspicious lady though bound is wholly devoted to Rama alone like Sachi, the wife of Indra who was bound by Nahusha.

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

तदेकवासस्संवीता रजोध्वस्ता तथैव च। शोकसन्तापदीनाङ्गी सीता भर्तृहिते रता।।5.59.29।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदेकवासस्संवीता wearing only one cloth, तथैव च with that only, रजोध्वस्ता filled with dust, शोकसन्तापदीनाङ्गी very sorrowful and piteous, सीता Sita, भर्तृहिते wishing the welfare of her husband, रता remained.

#### Explanation

"Wearing the same single cloth which she had worn before, filled with dust, Sita remained sorrowful and piteous wishing her husband's welfare.

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

सा मया राक्षसीमध्ये तर्ज्यमाना मुहुर्मुहुः। राक्षसीभिर्विरूपाभिर्दृष्टा हि प्रमदावने।।5.59.30।। एकवेणीधरा दीना भर्तृचिन्तापरायणा। अधश्शय्या विवर्णाङ्गी पद्मिनीव हिमागमे।।5.59.31।। रावणाद्विनिवृत्तार्था मर्तव्यकृतनिश्चया।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विरूपाभिः uglylooking, राक्षसीभिः ogresses, मुहुर्मुहुः again and again, तर्ज्यमाना threatened, एकवेणीधरा with a single braid, दीना pathetic, भर्तृचिन्तापरायणा thinking of her husband all the time, अधः now, शय्या lying on floor, हिमागमे in winter season, पद्मिनिव like the lotus, विवर्णाङ्गी devoid of lustre, रावणात् by Ravana, विनिवृत्तार्था turned away, मर्तव्यकृतनिश्चया decided to die, सा that she, मया by me, राक्षसीमध्ये in the midst of ogresses, प्रमदावने in the pleasure garden, दृष्टा saw her.

#### Explanation

"I saw Sita often threatened by uglylooking ogresses. Wearing a single braid, she looked pathetic, thinking always about her husband, lying on the bare ground. She was like a lustreless lotus in winter. Spurning the advances by Ravana, she is rather determined to commit suicide.

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

सा मया राक्षसीमध्ये तर्ज्यमाना मुहुर्मुहुः। राक्षसीभिर्विरूपाभिर्दृष्टा हि प्रमदावने।।5.59.30।। एकवेणीधरा दीना भर्तृचिन्तापरायणा। अधश्शय्या विवर्णाङ्गी पद्मिनीव हिमागमे।।5.59.31।। रावणाद्विनिवृत्तार्था मर्तव्यकृतनिश्चया।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

विरूपाभिः uglylooking, राक्षसीभिः ogresses, मुहुर्मुहुः again and again, तर्ज्यमाना threatened, एकवेणीधरा with a single braid, दीना pathetic, भर्तृचिन्तापरायणा thinking of her husband all the time, अधः now, शय्या lying on floor, हिमागमे in winter season, पद्मिनिव like the lotus, विवर्णाङ्गी devoid of lustre, रावणात् by Ravana, विनिवृत्तार्था turned away, मर्तव्यकृतनिश्चया decided to die, सा that she, मया by me, राक्षसीमध्ये in the midst of ogresses, प्रमदावने in the pleasure garden, दृष्टा saw her.

#### Explanation

"I saw Sita often threatened by uglylooking ogresses. Wearing a single braid, she looked pathetic, thinking always about her husband, lying on the bare ground. She was like a lustreless lotus in winter. Spurning the advances by Ravana, she is rather determined to commit suicide.

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

कथञ्चिन्मृगशाबाक्षी विश्वासमुपपादिता।।5.59.32।। ततः संभाषिता चैव सर्वमर्थं च दर्शिता। रामसुग्रीवसख्यं च श्रुत्वा प्रीतिमुपागता।।5.59.33।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मृगशाबाक्षी fawneyed woman, कथञ्चित् little, विश्वासम् confidence, उपपादिता pacified, ततः then, संभाषिता चैव after conversing only, सर्वम् everything, अर्थं च with difficulty, दर्शिता presented, रामसुग्रीवसख्यं च alliance of Rama and Sugriva, श्रुत्वा after hearing, प्रीतिम् happy, उपागता became.

#### Explanation

"I created a little confidence in the fawneyed lady by pacifying her and explaining her with great difficulty the alliance of Rama and Sugriva on hearing which she became happy.

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

कथञ्चिन्मृगशाबाक्षी विश्वासमुपपादिता।।5.59.32।। ततः संभाषिता चैव सर्वमर्थं च दर्शिता। रामसुग्रीवसख्यं च श्रुत्वा प्रीतिमुपागता।।5.59.33।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मृगशाबाक्षी fawneyed woman, कथञ्चित् little, विश्वासम् confidence, उपपादिता pacified, ततः then, संभाषिता चैव after conversing only, सर्वम् everything, अर्थं च with difficulty, दर्शिता presented, रामसुग्रीवसख्यं च alliance of Rama and Sugriva, श्रुत्वा after hearing, प्रीतिम् happy, उपागता became.

#### Explanation

"I created a little confidence in the fawneyed lady by pacifying her and explaining her with great difficulty the alliance of Rama and Sugriva on hearing which she became happy.

---

### Shloka 34

#### Shloka Text

नियतस्समुदाचारो भक्तिर्भर्तरि चोत्तमा। यन्न हन्ति दशग्रीवं सा महात्मा कृतागसम्।।5.59.34।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महात्मा great soul, सा that, कृतागसम् has done harm, दशग्रीवं to Ravana,न हन्ति not killed, इति यत् this being so, नियत: of good conduct, समुदाचारः chaste, भर्तरि towards husband, उत्तमा high, भक्तिः devotion

#### Explanation

"Ravana is a great soul because, even though he has done harm to her, he is not killed. This is because Sita being a chaste woman of good conduct and deep devotion towards her husband (she refrained from killing Ravana as she wanted her husband to do that.)

---

### Shloka 35

#### Shloka Text

निमित्तमात्रं रामस्तु वधे तस्य भविष्यति। सा प्रकृत्यैव तन्वङ्गी तद्वियोगाच्च कर्शिता।।5.59.35।। प्रतिपत्पाठशीलस्य विद्येव तनुमतां गता।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामस्तु Rama also, तस्य his, वधे killing, निमित्तमात्रं instrumental, भविष्यति will be, प्रकृत्यैव naturally, तन्वङ्गी thin body, तद्वियोगात् by the separation from her husband, कर्शिता च and also emaciated, सा she, प्रतिपत्पाठशीलस्य just as a student on the new Moon day, विद्येव shrunken, तनुमताम्  slender in body, गता has become.

#### Explanation

"To Rama, killing Ravana is easy (since Ravana's power of penance has already declined by his abduction of Sita). Separated from her husband she is naturally emaciated. She has become slender just like a student on the new Moon day (on the first day of study).

---

### Shloka 36

#### Shloka Text

एवमास्ते महाभागा सीता शोकपरायणा।।5.59.36।। यदत्र प्रतिकर्तव्यं तत्सर्वमुपपाद्यताम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाभागा noble lady, सीता Sita, अस्ते being, एवम् in this way, शोकपरायणा absorbed in grief, अत्र there, यत् that, प्रतिकर्तव्यम् what is to be done, तत् that, सर्वम् all, उपपाद्यताम् you may do.

#### Explanation

"Noble Sita is absorbed in grief. Let us do all that has to be done now." इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये सुन्दरकाण्डे एकोनषष्टितमस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the fiftyninth sarga of Sundarakanda of the holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 60

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

तस्य तद्वचनं श्रुत्वा वालिसूनुरभाषत। अयुक्तं तु विना देवीं दृष्टवद्भिश्च वानराः।।5.60.1।। समीपं गन्तुमस्माभी राघवस्य महात्मनः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तस्य his, तत् that, वचनम् words, श्रुत्वा on hearing, वालिसूनुः Vali's son, अभाषत said this, वानराः O vanaras, दृष्टवद्भिः even after seeing, अस्माभिः her, देवीं विना without Sita, महात्मनः great soul, राघवस्य to Rama, समीपम् near, गन्तुम् going, अयुक्तं तु not appropriate.

#### Explanation

After hearing Hanuman,  Vali's son said" O vanaras (Hanuman) having seen Sita it is not proper on our part to go without Sita to the great soul Rama.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

दृष्टा देवी न चानीता इति तत्र निवेदनम्।।5.60.2।। अयुक्तमिव पश्यामि भवद्भिः ख्यातविक्रमैः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ख्यातविक्रमैः known for valour, भवद्भिः you too, देवी godlike lady, दृष्टा seeing, न आनीता च not brought her, इति this, तत्र there, निवेदनम् tell, अयुक्तमिव not proper, पश्यामि I think.

#### Explanation

"O vanaras, you are known for your valour. I think, to tell Rama that we have seen Sita but not brought her is not proper".

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

न हि नः प्लवने कश्चिन्नापि कश्चित्पराक्रमे।।5.60.3।। तुल्य स्सामरदैत्येषु लोकेषु हरिसत्तमाः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हरिसत्तमाः noble vanaras, सामरदैत्येषु gods or demons, लोकेषु in the world, प्लवने in leaping, नः none, तुल्यः match, कश्चित् even few, न हि not indeed, पराक्रमेऽपि even in valour, कश्चित् are there, न no one.

#### Explanation

"O noble vanaras Indeed either among gods or among demons there is none who can match the vanaras in leaping and exhibiting valour.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

तेष्वेवं हतवीरेषु राक्षसेषु हनूमता।।5.60.4।। किमन्यदत्र कर्तव्यं गृहीत्वा याम जानकीम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तेषु hence, राक्षसेषु  among demons, हनूमता Hanuman, एवम् in that way, हतवीरेषु dead heroes, अत्र there, किम् what, अन्यत् other, कर्तव्यम् action, जानकीम् Janaki, गृहीत्वा get, याम here.

#### Explanation

"Hence, having killed the heroes among ogres what other task has Hanuman to do? Let us get Sita.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

तमेवं कृतसङ्कल्पं जाम्बवान् हरिसत्तमः।।5.60.5।। उवाच परमप्रीतो वाक्यमर्थवदर्थवित्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अर्थवित् knower of ways of execution of tasks, हरिसत्तमः noble vanara, जाम्बवान् Jambavan, परमप्रीतः very pleased, एवम् in that way, कृतसंकल्पम् determined, तम् him, अर्थवत् commnading way, वाक्यम् these words, उवाच spoke.

#### Explanation

"Jambavan, the noble vanara, skilled in the execution of tasks thus commanded in a determined manner:

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

न तावदेषा मतिरक्षमा नो यथा भवान् पश्यति राजपुत्र। यथा तु रामस्य मतिर्निविष्टा तथा भवान् पश्यतु कार्यसिद्धिम्।।5.60.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राजपुत्र O prince, भवान् you, यथा this way, पश्यति thought of, एषा also, मतिः to my mind, नः none, अक्षमा acceptable, न तावत् we are capable, तु you, रामस्य Rama's, मतिः mind, यथा this way, निविष्टा should act, तथा that way, भवान् you, कार्यसिद्धिम् to accomplish the work, पश्यतु do it.

#### Explanation

"O prince what you think is  acceptable to us also. Even though we are capable of achieving, knowing what is in Rama's mind, we should act only according to his command to accomplish the task. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये सुन्दरकाण्डे षष्टितमस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the sixtieth sarga of Sundarakanda of the holy Ramyana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 61

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

ततो जाम्बवतो वाक्यमगृह्णन्त वनौकसः। अङ्गदप्रमुखा वीरा हनुमांश्च महाकपिः।।5.61.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, अङ्गदप्रमुखाः Angada and other leaders, वीराः heroes, वनौकसः wanderers of the forest, vanaras, महाकपिः great monkey, हनुमांश्च and Hanuman, जाम्बवतः Jambavan's, वाक्यम् these words, अगृह्णन्त agreed upon.

#### Explanation

In the midst of all the leaders of the monkeys including Angada, Hanuman accepted Jambavan's advice.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

प्रीतिमन्तस्ततः सर्वे वायुपुत्रपरस्पराः। महेन्द्राद्रिं परित्यज्य पुप्लुवुः प्लवगर्षभाः।।5.61.2।। मेरुमन्दरसङ्काशा मत्ता इव महागजाः। छादयन्त इवाकाशं महाकाया महाबलाः।।5.61.3।। सभाज्यमानं भूतैस्तमात्मवन्तं महाबलम्। हनूमन्तं महावेगं वहन्त इव दृष्टिभिः।।5.61.4।। राघवे चार्थनिर्ववृत्तिं कर्तुं च परमं यशः। समाधाय समृद्धार्थाः सर्वेसिद्धिभिरुन्नता।।5.61.5।। प्रियाख्यानोन्मुखाः सर्वे सर्वे युद्धाभिनन्दिनः। सर्वे रामप्रतीकारे निश्चितार्था मनस्स्विनः।।5.61.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, मेरुमन्दरसङ्काशाः resembling Meru mountain, मत्ताः in rut, गजाः इव like elephants, आकाशम् sky, छादयन्तः इव as though covering the sky, महाकायाः of huge body, महाबलाः mighty, प्लवगर्षभाः bulls among leaping monkeys, सर्वे all, महेन्द्राद्रिम् from the summit of Mahendra mountain, परित्यज्य left, प्रीतिमन्तः very happily, वायुपुत्रपुरस्सराः led by the son of the Windgod, भूतैः all creatures, सभाज्यमानम् praised by, आत्मवन्तम् selfconfident, महाबलम् mighty, महावेगम् swift, हनूमन्तम् Hanuman, दृष्टिभिः seeing, वहन्तः इव like seeing without blinking, राघवे Rama's, अर्थनिर्ववृततिम् remaining in state of offering, परमम् supreme, यशः fame, कर्तुम् to do, समाधाय concentrating, समृद्धार्थाः better than others, कर्मसिद्धिभिः  accomplished ones, उन्नताः eager, पुप्लुवुः jumping vanaras, सर्वे all, प्रियाख्यानोन्मुखाः pleasantly talking among themselves, सर्वे all, युद्धाभिनन्दिनः anxious to wage a war, मनस्स्विनः determined, सर्वे all, रामप्रतीकारे to please, निश्चितार्थाः determined.

#### Explanation

Thereafter led by Hanuman, all the vanaras left Mahendra mountain and marched very happily. The vanaras were strong and huge like elephants and resembled mountain Meru. Moreover when they leaped up they seemed to cover the sky (so it was difficult to tell their numbers). Since Hanuman had accomplished the task, the vanaras were praising his strength, his swiftness and courage. They were looking at Hanuman without blinking their eyes, and seemed as though they were carrying him by their eyes. They were determined in their minds to please Rama. Remaining in a state of offering themselves, some of them more accomplished than others, collected together eager to wage war and talking pleasantly among themselves having resolved to assist Rama.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

प्रीतिमन्तस्ततः सर्वे वायुपुत्रपरस्पराः। महेन्द्राद्रिं परित्यज्य पुप्लुवुः प्लवगर्षभाः।।5.61.2।। मेरुमन्दरसङ्काशा मत्ता इव महागजाः। छादयन्त इवाकाशं महाकाया महाबलाः।।5.61.3।। सभाज्यमानं भूतैस्तमात्मवन्तं महाबलम्। हनूमन्तं महावेगं वहन्त इव दृष्टिभिः।।5.61.4।। राघवे चार्थनिर्ववृत्तिं कर्तुं च परमं यशः। समाधाय समृद्धार्थाः सर्वेसिद्धिभिरुन्नता।।5.61.5।। प्रियाख्यानोन्मुखाः सर्वे सर्वे युद्धाभिनन्दिनः। सर्वे रामप्रतीकारे निश्चितार्था मनस्स्विनः।।5.61.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, मेरुमन्दरसङ्काशाः resembling Meru mountain, मत्ताः in rut, गजाः इव like elephants, आकाशम् sky, छादयन्तः इव as though covering the sky, महाकायाः of huge body, महाबलाः mighty, प्लवगर्षभाः bulls among leaping monkeys, सर्वे all, महेन्द्राद्रिम् from the summit of Mahendra mountain, परित्यज्य left, प्रीतिमन्तः very happily, वायुपुत्रपुरस्सराः led by the son of the Windgod, भूतैः all creatures, सभाज्यमानम् praised by, आत्मवन्तम् selfconfident, महाबलम् mighty, महावेगम् swift, हनूमन्तम् Hanuman, दृष्टिभिः seeing, वहन्तः इव like seeing without blinking, राघवे Rama's, अर्थनिर्ववृततिम् remaining in state of offering, परमम् supreme, यशः fame, कर्तुम् to do, समाधाय concentrating, समृद्धार्थाः better than others, कर्मसिद्धिभिः  accomplished ones, उन्नताः eager, पुप्लुवुः jumping vanaras, सर्वे all, प्रियाख्यानोन्मुखाः pleasantly talking among themselves, सर्वे all, युद्धाभिनन्दिनः anxious to wage a war, मनस्स्विनः determined, सर्वे all, रामप्रतीकारे to please, निश्चितार्थाः determined.

#### Explanation

Thereafter led by Hanuman, all the vanaras left Mahendra mountain and marched very happily. The vanaras were strong and huge like elephants and resembled mountain Meru. Moreover when they leaped up they seemed to cover the sky (so it was difficult to tell their numbers). Since Hanuman had accomplished the task, the vanaras were praising his strength, his swiftness and courage. They were looking at Hanuman without blinking their eyes, and seemed as though they were carrying him by their eyes. They were determined in their minds to please Rama. Remaining in a state of offering themselves, some of them more accomplished than others, collected together eager to wage war and talking pleasantly among themselves having resolved to assist Rama.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

प्रीतिमन्तस्ततः सर्वे वायुपुत्रपरस्पराः। महेन्द्राद्रिं परित्यज्य पुप्लुवुः प्लवगर्षभाः।।5.61.2।। मेरुमन्दरसङ्काशा मत्ता इव महागजाः। छादयन्त इवाकाशं महाकाया महाबलाः।।5.61.3।। सभाज्यमानं भूतैस्तमात्मवन्तं महाबलम्। हनूमन्तं महावेगं वहन्त इव दृष्टिभिः।।5.61.4।। राघवे चार्थनिर्ववृत्तिं कर्तुं च परमं यशः। समाधाय समृद्धार्थाः सर्वेसिद्धिभिरुन्नता।।5.61.5।। प्रियाख्यानोन्मुखाः सर्वे सर्वे युद्धाभिनन्दिनः। सर्वे रामप्रतीकारे निश्चितार्था मनस्स्विनः।।5.61.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, मेरुमन्दरसङ्काशाः resembling Meru mountain, मत्ताः in rut, गजाः इव like elephants, आकाशम् sky, छादयन्तः इव as though covering the sky, महाकायाः of huge body, महाबलाः mighty, प्लवगर्षभाः bulls among leaping monkeys, सर्वे all, महेन्द्राद्रिम् from the summit of Mahendra mountain, परित्यज्य left, प्रीतिमन्तः very happily, वायुपुत्रपुरस्सराः led by the son of the Windgod, भूतैः all creatures, सभाज्यमानम् praised by, आत्मवन्तम् selfconfident, महाबलम् mighty, महावेगम् swift, हनूमन्तम् Hanuman, दृष्टिभिः seeing, वहन्तः इव like seeing without blinking, राघवे Rama's, अर्थनिर्ववृततिम् remaining in state of offering, परमम् supreme, यशः fame, कर्तुम् to do, समाधाय concentrating, समृद्धार्थाः better than others, कर्मसिद्धिभिः  accomplished ones, उन्नताः eager, पुप्लुवुः jumping vanaras, सर्वे all, प्रियाख्यानोन्मुखाः pleasantly talking among themselves, सर्वे all, युद्धाभिनन्दिनः anxious to wage a war, मनस्स्विनः determined, सर्वे all, रामप्रतीकारे to please, निश्चितार्थाः determined.

#### Explanation

Thereafter led by Hanuman, all the vanaras left Mahendra mountain and marched very happily. The vanaras were strong and huge like elephants and resembled mountain Meru. Moreover when they leaped up they seemed to cover the sky (so it was difficult to tell their numbers). Since Hanuman had accomplished the task, the vanaras were praising his strength, his swiftness and courage. They were looking at Hanuman without blinking their eyes, and seemed as though they were carrying him by their eyes. They were determined in their minds to please Rama. Remaining in a state of offering themselves, some of them more accomplished than others, collected together eager to wage war and talking pleasantly among themselves having resolved to assist Rama.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

प्रीतिमन्तस्ततः सर्वे वायुपुत्रपरस्पराः। महेन्द्राद्रिं परित्यज्य पुप्लुवुः प्लवगर्षभाः।।5.61.2।। मेरुमन्दरसङ्काशा मत्ता इव महागजाः। छादयन्त इवाकाशं महाकाया महाबलाः।।5.61.3।। सभाज्यमानं भूतैस्तमात्मवन्तं महाबलम्। हनूमन्तं महावेगं वहन्त इव दृष्टिभिः।।5.61.4।। राघवे चार्थनिर्ववृत्तिं कर्तुं च परमं यशः। समाधाय समृद्धार्थाः सर्वेसिद्धिभिरुन्नता।।5.61.5।। प्रियाख्यानोन्मुखाः सर्वे सर्वे युद्धाभिनन्दिनः। सर्वे रामप्रतीकारे निश्चितार्था मनस्स्विनः।।5.61.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, मेरुमन्दरसङ्काशाः resembling Meru mountain, मत्ताः in rut, गजाः इव like elephants, आकाशम् sky, छादयन्तः इव as though covering the sky, महाकायाः of huge body, महाबलाः mighty, प्लवगर्षभाः bulls among leaping monkeys, सर्वे all, महेन्द्राद्रिम् from the summit of Mahendra mountain, परित्यज्य left, प्रीतिमन्तः very happily, वायुपुत्रपुरस्सराः led by the son of the Windgod, भूतैः all creatures, सभाज्यमानम् praised by, आत्मवन्तम् selfconfident, महाबलम् mighty, महावेगम् swift, हनूमन्तम् Hanuman, दृष्टिभिः seeing, वहन्तः इव like seeing without blinking, राघवे Rama's, अर्थनिर्ववृततिम् remaining in state of offering, परमम् supreme, यशः fame, कर्तुम् to do, समाधाय concentrating, समृद्धार्थाः better than others, कर्मसिद्धिभिः  accomplished ones, उन्नताः eager, पुप्लुवुः jumping vanaras, सर्वे all, प्रियाख्यानोन्मुखाः pleasantly talking among themselves, सर्वे all, युद्धाभिनन्दिनः anxious to wage a war, मनस्स्विनः determined, सर्वे all, रामप्रतीकारे to please, निश्चितार्थाः determined.

#### Explanation

Thereafter led by Hanuman, all the vanaras left Mahendra mountain and marched very happily. The vanaras were strong and huge like elephants and resembled mountain Meru. Moreover when they leaped up they seemed to cover the sky (so it was difficult to tell their numbers). Since Hanuman had accomplished the task, the vanaras were praising his strength, his swiftness and courage. They were looking at Hanuman without blinking their eyes, and seemed as though they were carrying him by their eyes. They were determined in their minds to please Rama. Remaining in a state of offering themselves, some of them more accomplished than others, collected together eager to wage war and talking pleasantly among themselves having resolved to assist Rama.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

प्रीतिमन्तस्ततः सर्वे वायुपुत्रपरस्पराः। महेन्द्राद्रिं परित्यज्य पुप्लुवुः प्लवगर्षभाः।।5.61.2।। मेरुमन्दरसङ्काशा मत्ता इव महागजाः। छादयन्त इवाकाशं महाकाया महाबलाः।।5.61.3।। सभाज्यमानं भूतैस्तमात्मवन्तं महाबलम्। हनूमन्तं महावेगं वहन्त इव दृष्टिभिः।।5.61.4।। राघवे चार्थनिर्ववृत्तिं कर्तुं च परमं यशः। समाधाय समृद्धार्थाः सर्वेसिद्धिभिरुन्नता।।5.61.5।। प्रियाख्यानोन्मुखाः सर्वे सर्वे युद्धाभिनन्दिनः। सर्वे रामप्रतीकारे निश्चितार्था मनस्स्विनः।।5.61.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, मेरुमन्दरसङ्काशाः resembling Meru mountain, मत्ताः in rut, गजाः इव like elephants, आकाशम् sky, छादयन्तः इव as though covering the sky, महाकायाः of huge body, महाबलाः mighty, प्लवगर्षभाः bulls among leaping monkeys, सर्वे all, महेन्द्राद्रिम् from the summit of Mahendra mountain, परित्यज्य left, प्रीतिमन्तः very happily, वायुपुत्रपुरस्सराः led by the son of the Windgod, भूतैः all creatures, सभाज्यमानम् praised by, आत्मवन्तम् selfconfident, महाबलम् mighty, महावेगम् swift, हनूमन्तम् Hanuman, दृष्टिभिः seeing, वहन्तः इव like seeing without blinking, राघवे Rama's, अर्थनिर्ववृततिम् remaining in state of offering, परमम् supreme, यशः fame, कर्तुम् to do, समाधाय concentrating, समृद्धार्थाः better than others, कर्मसिद्धिभिः  accomplished ones, उन्नताः eager, पुप्लुवुः jumping vanaras, सर्वे all, प्रियाख्यानोन्मुखाः pleasantly talking among themselves, सर्वे all, युद्धाभिनन्दिनः anxious to wage a war, मनस्स्विनः determined, सर्वे all, रामप्रतीकारे to please, निश्चितार्थाः determined.

#### Explanation

Thereafter led by Hanuman, all the vanaras left Mahendra mountain and marched very happily. The vanaras were strong and huge like elephants and resembled mountain Meru. Moreover when they leaped up they seemed to cover the sky (so it was difficult to tell their numbers). Since Hanuman had accomplished the task, the vanaras were praising his strength, his swiftness and courage. They were looking at Hanuman without blinking their eyes, and seemed as though they were carrying him by their eyes. They were determined in their minds to please Rama. Remaining in a state of offering themselves, some of them more accomplished than others, collected together eager to wage war and talking pleasantly among themselves having resolved to assist Rama.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

प्लवमानाः खमाप्लुत्य ततस्ते काननौकसः। नन्दनोपमयासेदुर्वनं द्रुमलतायुतम्।।5.61.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, ते they, काननौकसः forestdwellers, monkeys, खम् sky, आफ्लुत्य rising up, प्लवमानाः started leaping, द्रुमलतायुतम् filled with trees and creepers, नन्दनोपमम् like the garden of Indra, वनम् garden, आसेदुः entered.

#### Explanation

"The monkeys leaped into the sky and entered the garden that resembled the garden of Indra filled with trees and creepers.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

यत्तन्मधुवनं नाम सुग्रीवस्याभिरक्षितम्। अधृष्यं सर्वभूतानां सर्वभूतमनोहरम्।।5.61.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अभिरक्षितम् wellprotected, सर्वभूतानाम् all creatures, अधृष्यम् difficult to access, सर्वभूतमनोहरम् enchanting to all beings, यत् that, सुग्रीवस्य Sugriva's, मधुवनं नाम Madhuvanam, तत् that.

#### Explanation

The Madhuvanam of Sugriva was wellprotected  and was difficult to access for the vanaras. It was enchating to all beings.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

यद्रक्षति महावीर्य स्सदा दधिमुखः कपिः। मातुलः कपिमुख्यस्य सुग्रीवस्य महात्मनः।।5.61.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महात्मनः great, कपिमुख्यस्य foremost of monkeys, सुग्रीवस्य Sugriva's, मातुलः maternal uncle, महावीर्यः of great valour, दधिमुखः Dadhimukha, कपिः monkey, यत् that which, सदा always, रक्षति protected.

#### Explanation

Dadhimukha, the foremost of all monkeys, of great valour and the maternal uncle of Sugriva was the caretaker of  the garden.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

ते तद्वनमुपागम्य बभूवुः परमोत्कटाः। वानरा वानरेन्द्रस्य मनः कान्ततमं महत्।।5.61.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ते those, वानराः vanaras, वानरेन्द्रस्य Vanara king's, मनःकान्ततमम् delighting to all, महत् extensive, तत् that, वनम् garden, उपागम्य on reaching, परमोत्कटाः highly rejoiced, बभूवुः became.

#### Explanation

On reaching the vanara king's extensive, delightful garden all the vanaras highly rejoiced.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

ततस्ते वानरा हृष्टा दृष्टवा मधुवनं महत्। कुमारमभ्ययाचन्त मधूनि मधुपिङ्गलाः।।5.61.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, मधुपिङ्गलाः honeycoloured monkeys, ते वानराः those vanaras, महत् large, मधुवनम् Madhuvanam, दृष्ट्वा seeing, हृष्टाः happy, कुमारम् young Angada, मधूनि honey, अभ्ययाचन्त sought permission.

#### Explanation

The honeycoloured monkeys felt happy on seeing the Madhuvanam and sought Angada's permission to drink honey.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

ततः कुमारस्तान् वृद्धान् जाम्बवत्प्रमुखान् कपीन्। अनुमान्य ददौ तेषां विसर्गं मधुभिक्षणे।।5.61.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, कुमारः young Angada, वृद्धान् old, तान् him, जाम्बवत्प्रमुखान् chief of monkeys Jambavan, कपीन् monkey, अनुमान्य permitting, तेषाम् them, मधुभक्षणे to drink honey, निसर्गम् leave, ददौ gave.

#### Explanation

Young Angada asked old Jambavan's permission for the vanaras to drink honey and Jambavan permitted them.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

ततश्चानुमता स्सर्वे सम्प्रहृष्टा वनौकसः। मुदिताः प्रेरिताश्चापि प्रनृत्यन्तोऽभवंस्तदा।।5.61.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, सर्वे all, वनौकसः forestdwellers, monkeys, अनुमताः having been permitted, सम्प्रहृष्टाः very happily, तदा then, प्रेरिताः motivated, मुदिताः rejoiced, प्रनृत्यन्तः started dancing, अभवन् they.

#### Explanation

Permitted thus to drink honey the forestdwelling monkeys were motivated. They rejoiced very happily and started dancing.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

गायन्ति केचित्प्रणमन्ति केचिन्नृत्यन्ति केचित्प्रहसन्ति केचित्। पतन्ति केचिद्विचरन्ति केचित्ल्पवन्ति केचित्प्रलपन्ति केचित्।।5.61.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

केचित् some, गायन्ति sang, केचित् some, प्रणमन्ति prostrated, केचित् few, नृत्यन्ति danced, केचित् some, प्रहसन्ति laughed, केचित् while others, पतन्ति fell down, केचित् some, विचरन्ति roamed, केचित् few, प्लवन्ति jumped up, केचित् some, प्रलपन्ति jumped up.

#### Explanation

Some sang, some prostrated on the ground, some danced, while some laughed, some jumped from the tree, some roamed about and some jumped up and down.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

परस्परं केचिदुपाश्रयन्ते परस्परं केचिदुपाक्रमन्ते। परस्परं केचिदुपब्रुवन्ते परस्परं केचिदुपारमन्ते।।5.61.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

केचित् some, परस्परम् to one another, उपाश्रयन्ते comforted, केचित् some, परस्परम् one another, उपाक्रमन्ते held, केचित् some, परस्परम् one another, उपब्रुवन्ते exchanged secrets, केचित् some, परस्परम् to one another, उपारमन्ते entertained.

#### Explanation

While some supported one another, some held each other, some exchanged secrets with one another, some entertained one another.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

द्रुमाद्द्रुमं केचिदभिद्रवन्ते क्षितौ नगाग्रान्निपतन्ति केचित्। महीतलात्केचिदुदीर्णवेगा महाद्रुमाग्राण्यभिसम्पतन्ति।।5.61.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

केचित् some, द्रुमात् from tree, द्रुमम् to another tree, अभिद्रवन्ते ran, केचित् some, नगाग्रात् from the top of tree jumped down, क्षितौ broken, निपतन्ति fell down, केचित् some, उदीर्णवेगाः very swift ones, महातलात् from the huge trees, महाद्रुमाग्राणि from the top of trees, अभिसम्पतन्ति fell down.

#### Explanation

While some ran from one tree to another, some jumped down from broken branches, and some swiftfooted ones fell down from the top of trees.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

गायन्तमन्यः प्रहसन्नुपैति हसन्तमन्यः प्ररुदन्नुपैति। रुदन्तमन्यः प्रणुदन्नुपैति नुदन्तमन्यः प्रणदन्नुपैति।।5.61.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

गायन्तम् singing, अन्यः others, प्रहनम् laughing, उपैति on who fell, हसन्तम् laughing, अन्यः others, प्ररुदन् went roaring aloud, उपैति fell, रुदन्तम् roaring, अन्यः others, प्रणुदन् pushing, उपैति fell on others, नुदन्तम् encouraged to do some thing, अन्यः others, प्रणदन् shouting, उपैति fell over.

#### Explanation

While one was singing, others approached him laughing while some were laughing others fell on them laughing excessively. While some were roaring, others went pushing them down. While some were encouraged to do something others shouted at them.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

समाकुलं तत्कपिसैन्यमासीन्मधुप्रसानोत्कटसत्त्वचेष्टम्। न चात्र कश्चिन्न बभूव मत्तो न चात्र कश्चिन्न बभूव तृप्तः।।5.61.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मधुप्रसानोत्कटसत्त्वचेष्टम् lost control due to drinking honeywine, तत् that, कपिसैन्यम् army of monkeys, समाकुलम् collected together, आसीत् quietly seated, अत्र there, कश्चित् not even one, मत्तः intoxicated, न बभूव not there, इति न not only this, अत्र there, कश्चित् none, तृप्तः satisfied, न बभूव इति न not there like that.

#### Explanation

The army of monkeys had lost control over their bodies due to drinking of honeywine. Not even one was quietly seated there. Not even one was not intoxicated. Not only that, none were satisfied.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

ततो वनं तत्परिभक्ष्यमाणं द्रुमांश्च विध्वंसितपत्रपुष्पान्। समीक्ष्य कोपाद्धधिवक्रनामा निवारयामास कपिः कपींस्तान्।।5.61.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः that, दधिवक्त्रनामा named Dadhimukha, कपिः monkey, परिभक्ष्यमाणम् drank all the honeywine, तत् that, वनम् garden, विध्वंसितपत्रपुष्पान् leaves, flowers, द्रुमांश्च and trees, समीक्ष्य seeing, कोपात् in anger, तान् कपीन् the monkeys, निवारयामास asked them to stop and get out.

#### Explanation

Beholding the destruction wrought by the vanaras to the garden, trees, leaves and flowers, and having drunk all the honey, Dadhimukha angrily asked them to stop and get out.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

स तैः प्रवृद्धैः परिभर्त्स्यमानो वनस्य गोप्ता हरिवीरवृद्धः। चकार भूयो मतिमुग्रतेजा वनस्य रक्षां प्रति वानरेभ्यः।।5.61.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

प्रवृद्धैः by the elderly, तैः by both, परिभर्त्स्यमानः reprimanded, वनस्य garden's, गोप्ता  caretaker, हरिवीरवृद्धः old monkey leader, उग्रतेजाः powerful one, वानरेभ्यः by vanaras, वनस्य garden's, रक्षांप्रति to protect, भूयः again, मतिम् in his mind, चकार thought over.

#### Explanation

The elderly Dadhimukha who was the powerful protector of the garden reprimanded them and devised yet again a plan to protect it.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

उवाच कांश्चित्परुषाणि धृष्टमसक्तमन्यांश्च तलैर्जघान। समेत्य कैश्चित्कलहं चकार तथैव साम्नोपजगाम कांश्चित्।।5.61.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कांश्चित् to some, परुषाणि in harsh words, उवाच spoken, अन्यांश्च to others, असक्तम् not to say, तलैः with the palm of the hand, धृष्टम् slapped, जघान hind part, समेत्य with good words, कैश्चित् with some, कलहम् quarrel, चकार made, तथैव in the same way, कांश्चित् some, साम्ना approached, उपजगाम in a conciliatory manner.

#### Explanation

He spoke harshly to some, to others he asked not to talk, some he slapped on the hind part with the palm of his hand. To some he spoke pleasantly and with some he quarrelled. And to others he spoke in a conciliatory manner.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

स तैर्मदात्संपरिवार्य वाक्यैर्भलाच्छ तेन प्रतिवार्यमाणैः। प्रधर्षितस्त्यक्तभयै स्समेत्य प्रकृष्यते चाप्यनवेक्ष्य दोषम्।।5.61.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तैर्मदात् in their drunkenness, अप्रतिवार्यवाक्यैः speaking in abusive language, तेन by them, बलात् by their strength, प्रतिवार्यमाणैः retaliated, त्यक्तभयैः devoid of fear, तैः those, प्रधर्षितः roared, सः that, दोषं च mistakes only, अनवेक्ष्य not seeing, सम्ये held, प्रकृष्यते च pulled.

#### Explanation

In their drunkenness they were using abusive language and with their strength they retaliated without fear. They invaded without considering their mistakes. They caught hold of Dadhimukha and pulled him.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

नखैस्तुदन्तो दशनैर्दशन्त स्तलैश्च पादैश्च समापयन्तः। मदात्कपिं तं कपय स्समग्रा महावनं निर्विषयं च चक्रुः।।5.61.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

समग्राः all of them together, कपयः that monkey, मदात् in their drunkenness, नखैः with nails, तुदन्तः scratched, दशनैः with teeth, दशन्तः bitten, तलैश्च with palms, पादैश्च legs, तं कपिम् the monkeys, समापयन्तः getting together, महावनम् great garden, निर्विषयम् completely, चक्रुः looted.

#### Explanation

In their drunkenness some monkeys scratched Dadhimukha violently with their nails, some bit him with their teeth and others slapped and kicked him with their palms and legs.Getting together they looted the garden completely. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये सुन्दरकाण्डे एकषष्टितम स्सर्गः।। Thus ends the sixtyfirst sarga of Sundarakanda of the holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 62

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

तानुवाच हरिश्रेष्ठो हनुमान्वानरर्षभः। अव्यग्रमनसो यूयं मधु सेवत वानराः।।5.62.1।। अहमावारयिष्यामि युष्माकं परिपन्थिनः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हरिश्रेष्ठः foremost of the monkeys, वानरर्षभः bull among vanaras, हनुमान् Hanuman, तान् them, उवाच spoke, वानराः vanaras, यूयम् looking at, अव्यग्रमनसः devoid of any fear in your mind, मधु honey, सेवत take, अहम् I, युष्माकम् those, परिपन्थिनः who object to your action, अवायिष्यामि will prevent.

#### Explanation

Hanuman, the foremost of the monkeys, bull among vanaras said, 'Enjoy the honey devoid of any fear. I shall prevent those who object to your action'.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

श्रुत्वा हनुमतो वाक्यं हरीणां प्रवरोऽङ्गदः।।5.62.2।। प्रत्युवाच प्रसन्नात्मा पिबन्तु हरयो मधु।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हनुमतः Hanuman, वाक्यम् these words, श्रुत्वा hearing, हरीणाम् to the monkeys, प्रवरः distinguished, अङ्गदः Angada, प्रसन्नात्मा very pleased, प्रत्युवाच replied, हरयः vanaras, पिबन्तु you may drink.

#### Explanation

Pleased with Hanuman's words, the distinguished Angada permitted the monkeys to drink.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

अवश्यं कृतकार्यस्य वाक्यं हनुमतो मया।।5.62.3।। अकार्यमपि कर्तव्यं किमङ्ग पुनरीदृशम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कृतकार्यस्य one who has accomplished the task, हनुमतः Hanuman, वाक्यम् these words, अकार्यमपि tells not to do, मया by me, अवश्यम् surely, कर्तव्यम् what is to be done, ईदृशं पुनः when he has spoken again, किमङ्ग what to say?

#### Explanation

Even if Hanuman, the achiever, forbids me to do something, I will do it. And now when he has permitted you, why hesitate ?

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

अङ्गदस्य मुखाच्छ्रुत्वावचनं वानरर्षभाः।।5.62.4।। साधुसाध्विति संहृष्टा वानराः प्रत्यपूजयन्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वानरर्षभाः bulls among vanaras, वानराः vanaras, अङ्गदस्य Angada's, मुखात् from the mouth, वचः spoken, श्रुत्वा having heard, संहृष्टाः very happy, साधु साध्विति good words, प्रत्यपूजयन् praised.

#### Explanation

Having heard the words that came from Angada's mouth, the bulls among vanaras praised him for his generous approval.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

पूजयित्वाङ्गदं सर्वे वानरा वानरर्षभम्।।5.62.5।। जग्मुर्मधुवनं यत्र नदीवेग इव द्रुमम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सर्वे all, वानरा vanaras, वानरर्षभम् to bull among vanaras, अङ्गदम् to Angada, पूजयित्वा having praised, मधुवनम् Madhuvanam, यत्र there, नदीवेगः fast flowing river, द्रुमम् इव like tree, जग्मुः went.

#### Explanation

The vanaras praised Angada, the bull among vanaras and went to Madhuvanam swiftly like a fastflowing river going to uproot a tree.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

ते प्रविष्टा मधुवनं पालानाक्रम्य वीर्यतः।।5.62.6।। अतिसर्गाच्च पटवो दृष्ट्वा श्रुत्वा च मैथिलीम्। पपुस्सर्वे मधु तदा रसवत्फलमाददुः।।5.62.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मैथिलीम् Mythili, दृष्ट्वा having seen, श्रुत्वा heard, अतिसर्गाच्च overjoyed with success, मधुवनम् Madhuvanam, प्रविष्टाः entered, पालान् guards, वीर्यतः violently, आक्रम्य attacked, तदा then, मधु Madhu, पपुः drank, रसवत् tasty, फलम् fruits, आददु: ate.

#### Explanation

Overjoyed at the news of Mythili, being seen, the vanaras entered Madhuvanam and attacked the guards violently, drank the honey and ate the tasty fruits.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

ते प्रविष्टा मधुवनं पालानाक्रम्य वीर्यतः।।5.62.6।। अतिसर्गाच्च पटवो दृष्ट्वा श्रुत्वा च मैथिलीम्। पपुस्सर्वे मधु तदा रसवत्फलमाददुः।।5.62.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मैथिलीम् Mythili, दृष्ट्वा having seen, श्रुत्वा heard, अतिसर्गाच्च overjoyed with success, मधुवनम् Madhuvanam, प्रविष्टाः entered, पालान् guards, वीर्यतः violently, आक्रम्य attacked, तदा then, मधु Madhu, पपुः drank, रसवत् tasty, फलम् fruits, आददु: ate.

#### Explanation

Overjoyed at the news of Mythili, being seen, the vanaras entered Madhuvanam and attacked the guards violently, drank the honey and ate the tasty fruits.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

उत्पत्य च ततस्सर्वे वनपालान् समागतान्। ताडयन्ति स्म शतश स्सक्तान्मधुवने तदा।।5.62.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तदा then, सर्वे all, मधुवने in Madhuvanam, शतशः in many ways, सक्तान् vanaras, समागतान् came together, वनपालान् guards protecting the garden, उत्पत्य risen up, ततः then, ताडयन्ति स्म struck.

#### Explanation

All the vanaras collected together, entered the Madhuvanam and attacked in many ways the guards protecting  the  garden.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

मधूनि द्रोणमात्राणि बाहुभिः परिगृह्य ते। पिबन्ति सहिता स्सर्वे निघ्नन्ति स्म तथापरे।।5.62.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ते सर्वे all of them, बाहुभिः many, द्रोणमात्राणि of huge size (large containers made of leather, a drona is a measure of two litres), मधूनि honey, परिगृह्य took hold of, सहिताः together, पिबन्ति  drinking, अपरे others, निघ्नन्ति स्म were preventing.

#### Explanation

All of them now gathered at one place and drank honey from large containers. Others were busy preventing the guards.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

केचित्पीत्वा प्रविध्यन्ति मधूनि मधुपिङ्गलाः। मधूच्छिष्टेन केचिच्च जग्मुरन्योन्यमुत्कटाः।।5.62.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मधुपिङ्गलाः honeycoloured, केचित् some, मधूनि honey, पीत्वा were drinking, प्रविध्यन्ति throwing, केचिच्च some, मधूच्छिष्ठेन chunks of honey combs, उत्कटाः intoxicated, अन्योन्यम् with each other, जग्मुः went about.

#### Explanation

The honeycoloured monkeys drank honey. Some threw large chunks of honey combs after drinking and some went about intoxicated.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

अपरे वृक्षमूले तु शाखां गृह्य व्यवस्थिताः। अत्यर्थं च मदग्लानाः पर्णान्यास्तीर्य शेरते।।5.62.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अपरे some others, शाखाम् branches of trees, गृह्य taking, वृक्षमूले at the root of trees, व्यवस्थिताः stayed, अत्यर्थम् on account of drinking, मदग्लानाः sweet drinks, पर्णानि leaves, आस्तीर्य spreading, शेरते lay down.

#### Explanation

Some took branches of trees and rested at the root of trees. Some totally languid lay down spreading leaves due to drinking of sweet drinks.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

उन्मत्तभूताः प्लवगा मधुमत्ताश्च हृष्टवत्। क्षिपन्ति च तथान्योन्यं स्खलन्ति च तथाऽपरे।।5.62.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मधुमत्ताः by drinking of honey, प्लवगाः vanaras, उन्मत्तभूताः like mad people, हृष्टवत् joyfully, अन्योन्यम् each other, क्षिपन्ति pushing each other, अपरे others, स्खलन्ति च were shaking unsteady.

#### Explanation

Intoxicated with drink, some behaved like mad people, some pushed others senselessly and some were unsteady and shaky.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

केचित् क्ष्वेलां प्रकुर्वन्ति केचित् कूजन्ति हृष्टवत्। हरयोमधुना मत्ताः केचित्सुप्ता महीतले।।5.62.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

केचित् some, क्ष्वेलाम् प्रकुर्वन्ति roared, केचित् some, हृष्टवत् happy, कूजन्ति cooing like the birds, मधुना by the drinking of honey, मत्ताः drowsy, केचित् हरयः some vanaras, महीतले on the ground, सुप्ताः slept.

#### Explanation

Some roared, some warbled happily, some felt drowsy, drinking honey and some dropped off to sleep on the ground.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

कृत्वा केचिद्धसन्त्यन्ये केचित्कुर्वन्ति चेतरत्। कृत्वा केचिद्वदन्त्यन्ये केचिद्बुध्यन्ति चेतरत्।।5.62.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

केचित् some, कृत्वा having done, हसन्ति laughing, अन्ये others, केचित् some, इतरत् different, कुर्वन्ति doing, केचित् some, कृत्वा having done, वदन्ति were telling others, अन्ये others, केचित् some, इतरत् something else, बुध्यन्ति thought of doing.

#### Explanation

Some made others laugh, some provoked others and some thought of doing something else having got over the intoxication.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

येऽप्यत्र मधुपाला स्स्युः प्रेष्या दधिमुखस्य तु। तेऽपि तैर्वानरैर्भीमैः प्रतिषिद्धा दिशो गताः।।5.62.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अत्र there, दधिमुखस्य Dadhimukha's, प्रेष्याः engaged, मधुपालाः guards of Madhuvanam, ये those, स्युः to cover, तेऽपि were also, भीमैः fierce, वानरैः vanaras, प्रतिषिद्धाः driven away, दिशः in all directions, गताः went.

#### Explanation

All the guards engaged in protecting the garden were driven away in all directions by the fierce vanaras.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

जानुभिस्तु प्रकृष्टाश्च देवमार्गं प्रदर्शिताः। अब्रुवन् परमोद्विग्ना गत्वा दधिमुखं वचः।।5.62.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

जानुभिः knees, प्रकृष्टाः drawn forth, देवमार्गम् towards the path of gods, प्रदर्शिताः exhibiting, परमोद्विग्नाः greatly disturbed, दधिमुखम् to Dadhimukha, गत्वा having gone, वचः these words, अब्रुवन् said.

#### Explanation

Some were dragged on their knees and tossed up. Some monkeys exhibited their private parts, showing indecent behaviour. Dadhimukha, highly disturbed at this, said:

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

हनूमता दत्तवरैर्हतं मधुवनं बलात्। वयं च जानुभिः कृष्टा देवमार्गं च दर्शिताः।।5.62.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हनूमता Hanuman, दत्तवरैर्हतं by whom they were permitted, वयं च and us, मधुवनं Madhuvanam, जानुभिः knees, बलात् forcefully, कृष्टा holding, देवमार्गं च  dragged to the private parts, दर्शिताः and showed.

#### Explanation

Permitted by Hanuman (to enter Madhuvanam and drink), they dragged one another forcefully tossed up  on their knees showing their private parts.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

ततो दधिमुख: क्रुद्धो वनपस्तत्र वानरः। हतं मधुवनं श्रुत्वा सान्त्वयामास तान् हरीन्।।5.62.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, तत्र there, वनपः guardian of the garden, दधिमुख: Dadhimukha, वानर: vanara, क्रुद्धः furious, मधुवनम् Madhuvanam, हतम् destoyed, श्रुत्वा having heard, हरीन् vanaras, सान्त्वयामास with soothing words.

#### Explanation

Then Dadhimukha, the guardian of the garden became furious on the vanaras. Having heard from the guards that the garden was destroyed, he said soothing words to them.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

इहागच्छत गच्छामो वानरान् बलदर्पितान्। बलेन वारयिष्यामो मधु भक्षयतो वयम्।।5.62.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इह here, आगच्चत come, गच्चामः will go, वयम् we, बलदर्पितान् proud of their power, मधु Madhu, भक्षयतः consume honey, वानरान् vanaras, बलेन forcibly, वारयिष्यामः will prevent them.

#### Explanation

"Come, we will all go there to Madhuvanam and prevent forcibly the vanaras who have consumed honey proud of their power.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

श्रुत्वा दधिमुखस्येदं वचनं वानरर्षभाः। पुनर्वीरा मधुवनं तेनैव सहसा ययुः।।5.62.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वीराः heroes, वानरर्षभाः bull among vanaras, दधिमुखस्य Dadhimukha's, इदम् this kind of, वचनम् words, श्रुत्वा having heard, सहसा quickly, तेनैव along with him, पुनः again, मधुवनम् Madhuvanam, ययुः went.

#### Explanation

"The heroes, bulls among vanaras, heard Dadhimukha and quickly entered Madhuvanam along with him.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

मध्ये चैषां दधिमुखः प्रगृह्य तरसा तरुम्। समभ्यधावद्वेगेन ते च सर्वे प्लवङ्गमाः।।5.62.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एषाम् their, मध्ये way, दधिमुखः Dadhimukha, तरसा energetic, तरुम् tree, प्रगृह्य taking up, वेगेन swiftly, समभ्यधावत् following, ते they, सर्वे all, प्लवङ्गमाः च the leaping vanaras.

#### Explanation

On the way, the energetic Dadhimukha took up a tree and ran to the leaping vanaras swiftly with guards following him.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

ते शिलाः पादपांश्चापि पर्वतांश्चापि वानराः। गृहीत्वाऽऽभ्यगमन् क्रुद्धा यत्र ते कपिकुञ्जराः।।5.62.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ते वानराः those vanaras, क्रुद्धाः furious, शिलाः rocks, पादपांश्चापि also trees, पर्वतांश्चापि even mountains, गृहीत्वा took up, ते they, कपिकुञ्जराः elephant of monkeys, यत्र there, अभ्यगमन् went.

#### Explanation

The furious vanara guards lifted rocks, trees and even mountains and went behind Dadhimukha the elephant among the vanaras.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

ते स्वामिवचनं वीरा हृदयेष्ववसज्य तत्। त्वरया ह्यभ्यधावन्त सालतालशिलायुधाः।।5.62.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वीराः heroic, ते they, तत् that, स्वामिवचनम् leader's words, हृदयेषु in their heart, अवसज्य rushed, सालतालशिलायुधाः Sala, Tala trees as weapons, त्वरया at once, अभ्यधावन्त ran.

#### Explanation

The heroic vanaras held Sala and Tala trees and rushed at once keeping their leader's words in mind.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

वृक्षस्थांश्च तलस्थांश्च वानरान् बलदर्पितान्। अभ्यक्रामंस्ततो वीराः पालास्तत्र सहस्रशः।।5.62.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, वीराः heroic, पालाः guards, सहस्रशः in thousands, वृक्षस्थांश्च on the trees, तलस्थांश्च under the trees, बलदर्पितान् proud of their power, वानरान् vanaras, अभ्यक्रामन् attacked.

#### Explanation

Then the heroic guards in their thousands fell on the vanaras proud of their power on and under the tala trees.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

अथ दृष्ट्वा दधिमुखं क्रुद्धं वानरपुङ्गवाः। अभ्यधावन्त वेगेन हनुमत्प्रमुखास्तदा।।5.62.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अथ and then, हनुमत्प्रमुखाः leader Hanuman, वानरपुङ्गवाः foremost of vanaras, तदा then, दधिमुखम् Dadhimukha's, क्रुद्धम् fury, दृष्ट्वा having seen, वेगेन swiftly, अभ्यधावन्त went there.

#### Explanation

Having seen the fury of Dadhimukha, Hanuman the leader of the vanaras went to him swiftly.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text
