तं सवृक्षं महाबाहुमापतन्तं महाबलम्। आर्यकं प्राहरत्तत्र बाहुभ्यां कुपितोऽङ्गदः।।5.62.26।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कुपितः angry, अङ्गदः Angada, सवृक्षम् with trees, महाबाहुम् strongarmed, महाबलम् powerful, आपतन्तम् coming, तम् him, आर्यकम् तत्र there, बाहुभ्याम् with both hands, प्राहरत् clasped tightly.

#### Explanation

Seeing the revered, powerful and strongarmed Dadhimukha rushing to him, the angry Angada caught him with both hands tightly.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

मदान्धश्च न वेदैनमार्यकोऽयं ममेति सः। अथैनं निष्पिपेषाशु वेगवद्वसुधातले।।5.62.27।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः he, मदान्धश्च blinded by arrogance, अयम् this, मम my, आर्यकः revered, इति this, एनम् therefore, न वेद not understnding that (I am his granduncle), अथ now, एनम् therefore, वसुधातले on the floor, वेगवत् forcibly, आशु him, निष्पिपेष pushed.

#### Explanation

"Blinded by arrogance, the prince pushed him (Dadhimukha) on the floor forcibly,  forgetting that he was his revered granduncle.

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

स भग्नबाहूरुभुजो विह्वलश्शोणितोक्षितः। मुमोह सहसा वीरो मुहूर्तं कपिकुञ्जरः।।5.62.28।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भग्नबाहूरुभुजः with shoulders and arms broken, विह्वलः battered, शोणितोक्षितः body drenched in blood, सः he, Dadhimukha, वीरः a hero, कपिकुञ्जरः elephant among monkeys, सहसा at once, मुहूर्तम् in a moment, मुमोह lost conciousness.

#### Explanation

The heroic Dadhimukha, an elephant among monkeys, with his shoulders and arms broken, and body drenched in blood lost his consciousness instantly.

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

स समाश्वस्य सहसा सङ्कृद्धो राजमातुलः। वानरान्वारयामास दण्डेन मधुमोहितान्।।5.62.29।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राजमातुलः maternal uncle of the king, सः he, सहसा at once, समाश्वस्य coming out of unconscious state, सङ्कृद्धः furious, मधुमोहितान् deluded by drinking, वानरान् vanaras, दण्डेन with a stick, वारयामास chased.

#### Explanation

At once Dadhimukha, the maternal uncle of the king became conscious and chased with a stick the intoxicated vanaras.

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

स कथञ्चिद्विमुक्तस्स्तैर्वानरैर्वानरर्षभः। उवाचैकान्तमाश्रित्य भृत्यान् स्वान् समुपागतान्।।5.62.30।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तैः they, वानरैः vanaras, कथञ्चित् with great difficulty, विमुक्तः escaped, सः he, Dadhimukha, वानरर्षभः bull among monkeys, एकान्तम् secluded, आश्रित्य reached, समुपागतान् returned, स्वान् them, भृत्यान् servants, उवाच said.

#### Explanation

Escaping from the vanaras with great difficulty, Dadhimukha,  a bull among monkeys, repaired to a secluded place and said to his servants:

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

एते तिष्ठन्तु गच्छामो भर्ता नो यत्र वानरः। सुग्रीवो विपुलग्रीवः सह रामेण तिष्ठति।।5.62.31।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एते let them, तिष्ठन्तु stay, नः our, भर्ता king, वानरः vanaras, विपुलग्रीवः one with broad neck, सुग्रीवः Sugriva, यत्र there, रामेण सह with Rama, तिष्ठति will be staying, गच्चामः let us go.

#### Explanation

"Let them stay here. Our broadnecked king Sugriva will be there with Rama. Let us go to him.

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

सर्वं चैवाङ्गदे दोषं श्रावयिष्यामि पार्थिवे। अमर्षी वचनं श्रुत्वा घातयिष्यति वानरान्।।5.62.32।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अङ्गदे Angada, सर्वम् all, दोषम् mistakes, पार्थिवे to the king, श्रावयिष्यामि will let him know, वचनम् these words, श्रुत्वा having heard, अमर्षि very furious, वानरान् vanaras, घातयिष्यति will put an end.

#### Explanation

None

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

इष्टं मधुवनं ह्येतत्पार्थिवस्य महात्मनः। पितृपैतामहं दिव्यं देवैरपि दुरासदम्।।5.62.33।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पितृपैतामहम् from grandfather and father, दिव्यम् wonderful, देवैरपि even for devatas, दुरासदम् forbidden, एतत् this, मधुवनम् Madhuvanam, महात्मनः great self, पार्थिवस्य to the king, इष्टं हि very dear.

#### Explanation

'Madhuvan has come down to us from our forefather's time. Dear to the king, its entry is prohibited even to the gods.

---

### Shloka 34

#### Shloka Text

स वानरानिमान् सर्वान् मधुलुब्धान् गतायुषः। घातयिष्यति दण्डेन सुग्रीवस्ससुहृज्जनान्।।5.62.34।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः सुग्रीवः that Sugriva, मधुलुब्धान् those greedy for honey, गतायुषः doomed, ससुहृज्जनान् their friends, इमान् these, सर्वान् all, वानरान् vanaras, दण्डेन with a stick, घातयिष्यति will be beaten.

#### Explanation

'Sugriva will chase all the vanaras and their friends greedy for honey. They will be punished with a stick. They are doomed'.

---

### Shloka 35

#### Shloka Text

वध्या ह्येते दुरात्मानो नृपाज्ञापरिभाविनः। अमर्षप्रभवो रोषस्सफलो नो भविष्यति।।5.62.35।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नृपाज्ञापरिभाविनः who have disobeyed the king's order, एते they, दुरात्मनः evilminded, वध्याः हि indeed deserve to be killed, अमर्षप्रभवः forbearance king, नः रोषः our fury, सफलः will result in success, भविष्यति will be.

#### Explanation

'Indeed those evilminded ones who have disobeyed the king's order deserve to be killed.That way our fury and forbearance will result in success'.

---

### Shloka 36

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्त्वा दधिमुखो वनपालान्महाबलः। जगाम सहसोत्पत्य वनपालैस्समन्वितः।।5.62.36।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाबलः very powerful, दधिमुखः Dadhimukha, वनसालान् garden protectors, एवम् in that way, उक्त्वा having said, सहसा at once, वनपालैः garden guards, समन्वितः along with them, उत्पत्य rising to the sky, जगाम went.

#### Explanation

Powerful Dadhimukha having said that to the guardians of the garden rose up to the sky and went off with them.

---

### Shloka 37

#### Shloka Text

निमेषान्तरमात्रेण स हि प्राप्तो वनालयः। सहस्रांशुसुतो धीमान् सुग्रीवो यत्र वानरः।।5.62.37।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः he, वनालयः forestdweller, सहस्रांशुसुतः to the son of Sungod, धीमान् wise, सुग्रीवः वानरः to the vanara Sugriva, यत्र there, निमेषान्तरमात्रेण in a moment's time, प्राप्तः reached.

#### Explanation

In a moment, Dadhimukha reached the place where wise Sugriva, the son of Sungod was there

---

### Shloka 38

#### Shloka Text

रामं च लक्ष्मणं चैव दृष्ट्वा सुग्रीवमेव च। समप्रतिष्ठां जगतीमाकाशान्निपपात ह।।5.62.38।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामं च Rama, लक्ष्मणं चैव and also Lakshmana, सुग्रीवमेव च and also Sugriva, दृष्ट्वा seeing, आकाशात् from the sky, समप्रतिष्ठाम् even, जगतीम् ground, निपपात landed.

#### Explanation

Seeing Rama, Lakshmana and also Sugriva from the sky he landed on an even ground.

---

### Shloka 39

#### Shloka Text

सन्निपत्य महावीर्यस्सर्वैस्तै: परिवारितः। हरिर्दधिमुखः पालैः पालानां परमेश्वरः।।5.62.39।। स दीनवदनो भूत्वा कृत्वा शिरसि चाञ्जलिम्। सुग्रीवस्य शुभौ मूर्ध्ना चरणौ पत्यपीडयत्।।5.62.40।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सर्वैः all, तैः पालैः those guards, परिवारितः the servants, पालानाम् king, परमेश्वरः lord, हरिः of monkeys, महावीर्यः great hero, सः दधिमुखः that Dadhimukha, दीनवदनः with a painful expression on the face, शिरसि head bowed down, अञ्जलिम् salutations with folded hands, कृत्वा having offered, सन्निपत्य surrounded by, सुग्रीवस्य Sugriva, शुभे auspicious, चरणौ feet, मूर्ध्ना touching with his forehead, पत्यपीडयत् painful obeisance.

#### Explanation

Dadhimukha, a great hero and king of the guards protecting Madhuvanam surrounded by his servants, with a sad expression on his face offered painful obeisance with folded hands bowing down his forehead at the feet of Sugriva. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये सुन्दरकाण्डे द्विषष्टितमस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the sixtysecond sarga of Sundarakanda of the holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 63

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

ततो मूर्ध्ना निपतितं वानरं वानरर्षभः। दृष्ट्वैवोद्विग्नहृदयो वाक्यमेतदुवाच ह।।5.63.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, वानरर्षभः bull among vanaras, मूर्ध्ना forehead, निपतितम् prostrated, वानरम् vanara, Dadhimukha, दृष्ट्वैव as soon as he saw, उद्विग्नहृदयः anxious at heart, एतत् these, वाक्यम् words, उवाच ह spoke.

#### Explanation

Feeling anxious on seeing Dadhimukha touching his feet with his forehead, Sugriva, the bull among vanaras said:

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

उत्तिष्ठोत्तिष्ठ कस्मात्त्वं पादयोः पतितो मम। अभयं ते भवेद्वीर सर्वमेवाभिधीयताम्।।5.63.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वीर hero, उत्तिष्ठ उत्तिष्ठ get up, get up, त्वम् you, कस्मात् why, मम my, पादयोः feet, पतितः fallen, ते to you, अभयम् give you protection, भवेत् you be, सर्वमेव from all, अभिधीयताम् you may tell.

#### Explanation

"O heroic Dadhimukha, get up. Tell me why you have fallen at my feet. I give you protection from all.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

स तु विश्वासितस्तेन सुग्रीवेण महात्मना। उत्थाय सुमहाप्राज्ञो वाक्यं दधिमुखोऽब्रवीत्।।5.63.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महात्मना great self, तेन सुग्रीवेण by Sugriva, विश्वासितः given assurance, सुमहाप्राज्ञः very wise, सः दधिमुखः that Dadhimukha, उत्थाय got up, वाक्यम् these words, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

When great Sugriva gave assurance to wise Dadhimukha, he got up and said:

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

नैवर्क्षरजसा राजन्न त्वया नापि वालिना। वनं निसृष्टपूर्वं हि भक्षितं तच्च वानरैः।।5.63.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राजन् O king, वनम्  garden, ऋक्षरजसा in Riksharaja's time, नैव निसृष्टपूर्वं हि or even before, त्वया your time, न not, वालिनापि even at the time of Vali, न not, तच्च accessible to go, वानरैः vanaras, भक्षितम् finished eating.

#### Explanation

"O king in your time or even before, during the time of Vali, that (Madhuvanam) was forbidden for entry. Now it has been laid waste by the trespassing vanaras.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

एभिः प्रधर्षिताश्चैव वानरा वनरक्षिभिः। मधून्यचिन्तयित्वेमान् भक्षयन्ति पिबन्ति च।।5.63.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एभिः they also, वनरक्षिभिः garden guards, प्रधर्षिताश्चैव even though resisted, वानराः vanaras, इमान् them, अचिन्तयित्वा not cared for, मधूनि honey, भक्षयन्ति finished eating, पिबन्ति च and drinking.

#### Explanation

"Even though the garden guards forcibly tried to prevent them they did not care and have devoured all the honey.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

शिष्टमत्रापविध्यन्ति भक्षयन्ति तथापरे। निवार्यमाणास्ते सर्वे भ्रुवो वै दर्शयन्ति हि।।5.63.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

शिष्टम् left over, अत्र there, अपविध्यन्ति thrown off, तथा similarly, अपरे others, भक्षयन्ति eating, ते सर्वे all, निवार्यमाणाः when we prevented, भ्रुवः eyebrows, दर्शयन्ति च raising to show (a sign of arrogance)

#### Explanation

"Some have thrown off the leftovers, when others were prevented from drinking off the honey, they raised their eyebrows (to show their grouch).

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

इमे हि संरब्धतरास्तथा तैस्सम्प्रधर्षिताः। वारयन्तो वनात्तस्मात्क्रुद्धैर्वानरपुङ्गवैः।।5.63.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

संरब्दतराः when enraged guards, तथा that way, तस्मात् from that, वनात् garden, वारयन्तः vanaras, इमे to go, क्रुद्धैः angry, तैः they, वानरपुङ्गवैः vanara leaders, सम्प्रधर्षिताः ill treated them.

#### Explanation

"When the guards asked the vanaras to leave the garden the enraged vanaras misbehaved with them.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

ततस्तैर्बहुभिर्वीरैर्वानरैर्वानरर्षभ। संरक्तनयनैः क्रोधाद्धरयः प्रविचालिताः।।5.63.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वानरर्षभ bull among vanaras, ततः then, क्रोधात् in fury, संरक्तनयनैः redeyed, वीरैः heroes, बहुभिः many, तैः them, वानरैः vanara guards, हरयः monkeys, प्रविचालिताः chased.

#### Explanation

"O Sugriva the vanara guards were chased away by the many heroic monkeys, angry and redeyed.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

पाणिभिर्निहताः केचित्केचिज्जानुभिराहताः। प्रकृष्टाश्च यथाकामं देवमार्गं च दर्शिताः।।5.63.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

केचित् some, पाणिभिः with the hands, निहताः struck, केचित् some, जानुभिः on the knees, आहताः beaten, यथाकामम् as they liked, प्रकृष्टाः dragged, देवमार्गम् private parts, दर्शिताः च showed.

#### Explanation

"Some were struck with hands, some beaten on their knees and were dragged as they liked and showed up their private parts.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

एवमेते हताश्शूरास्त्वयि तिष्ठति भर्तरि। कृत्स्नं मधुवनं चैव प्रकामं तैः प्रभक्ष्यते।।5.63.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्वयि you, भर्तरि when you are the king, तिष्ठति staying, एते these, शूराः heroes, एवम् in that way, हताः striking, तैः they, कृत्स्नम् on the earth, मधुवनं चैव Madhuvanam aslo, प्रकामम् at their will, प्रभक्ष्यते eating so.

#### Explanation

These heroes struck that way devoured the Madhuvanam on the earth at their will and devoured the honey even when you are there as king.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

एवं विज्ञाप्यमानं तं सुग्रीवं वानरर्षभम्। अपृच्छत्तं महाप्राज्ञो लक्ष्मणः परवीरहा।।5.63.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एवम् in that way, विज्ञाप्यमानम् having appealed, वानरर्षभम् bulls among vanaras, तं सुग्रीवम् to Sugriva, परवीरहा killer of enemies, महाप्राज्ञः very learned, लक्ष्मणः Lakshmana, अपृच्छत् enquired.

#### Explanation

While Dadhimukha appealed thus to wise Sugriva, Lakshmana the killer of enemies happened to see them and enquired:

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

किमयं वानरो राजन् वनपः प्रत्युपस्थितः। कं चार्थमभिनिर्दिश्य दुःखितो वाक्यमब्रवीत्।।5.63.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राजन् O king, वनप: protector of the garden, अयं वानरः this vanara, किम् why, प्रत्युपस्थितः came here?, दुःखितः sad, कम् why, अर्थम् for what, अभिनिर्दिश्य what is he telling, वाक्यम् these words, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

"O king why has this protector of the garden come here? Why is he sad? What is he narrating, asked Lakshmana.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्तस्तु सुग्रीवो लक्ष्मणेन महात्मना। लक्ष्मणं प्रत्युवाचेदं वाक्यं वाक्यविशारदः।।5.63.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महात्मना great prince, लक्ष्मणेन by Lakshmana, एवम् in that way, उक्तः having spoken, वाक्यविशारदः proficient in speech, सुग्रीवः Sugriva, इदम् these, वाक्यम् words, प्रत्युवाच replied.

#### Explanation

Great prince Lakshmana thus said, Sugriva the proficient speaker replied:

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

आर्य लक्ष्मण सम्प्राह वीरो दधिमुखः कपिः। अङ्गदप्रमुखैर्वीरैर्भक्षितं मधु वानरैः।।5.63.14।। विचित्य दक्षिणामाशामागतैर्हरिपुङ्गवैः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

आर्य O venerable one, लक्ष्मण Lakshmana, वीरः hero, कपिः monkey, दधिमुखः Dadhimukha, सम्प्राह to search, दक्षिणाम् southern, आशाम् hope, विचित्य having searched, आगतैः have come, हरिपुङ्गवैः monkey leaders, वीरैः heroic ones, अङ्गदप्रमुखैः Angada and others, वानरैः vanaras, मधु honey, भक्षितम् eaten.

#### Explanation

'O venerable Lakshmana Dadhimukha as complaining that the fruits and honey have been consumed by the vanara heroes, Angada and others who have come after their search for Sita in the southern direction.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

नैषामकृत्यानामीदृशस्स्यादुपक्रमः।।5.63.15।। आगतैश्च प्रमथितं यथा मधुवनं हि तैः। धर्षितं च वनं कृत्स्नमुपयुक्तं च वानरैः।।5.63.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

आगतैः having come, तैः those, वानरैः vanaras, मधुवनम् Madhuvanam, यथा that way, प्रमथितम् entered, कृत्स्नम् whole, वनम् garden, धर्षितम् broken, उपयुक्तं च right, एषाम् they that way, अकृतकृत्यानाम् if they had not accomplished their purpose, ईदृशः this way, उपक्रमः indulged, न स्यात् not done.

#### Explanation

"Those vanaras having entered Madhuvanam and broken the trees are quite right. Had they not accomplished their purpose they would not have indulged themselves that way.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

नैषामकृत्यानामीदृशस्स्यादुपक्रमः।।5.63.15।। आगतैश्च प्रमथितं यथा मधुवनं हि तैः। धर्षितं च वनं कृत्स्नमुपयुक्तं च वानरैः।।5.63.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

आगतैः having come, तैः those, वानरैः vanaras, मधुवनम् Madhuvanam, यथा that way, प्रमथितम् entered, कृत्स्नम् whole, वनम् garden, धर्षितम् broken, उपयुक्तं च right, एषाम् they that way, अकृतकृत्यानाम् if they had not accomplished their purpose, ईदृशः this way, उपक्रमः indulged, न स्यात् not done.

#### Explanation

"Those vanaras having entered Madhuvanam and broken the trees are quite right. Had they not accomplished their purpose they would not have indulged themselves that way.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

वनं यदाऽभिपन्नास्ते साधितं कर्म वानरैः। दृष्टा देवी न सन्देहो न चान्येन हनूमता।।5.63.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ते they, यदा like that, वनम् garden, अभिपन्नाः entered, वानरैं कर्म their duty, साधितम् had done, देवी queen, दृष्टा saw, सन्देहः doubt, न no, अन्येन others, न not, हनुमता Hanuman.

#### Explanation

"If they have trespassed into the garden that way, they might have done their duty. No doubt, Hanuman alone among them might have seen Sita.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

न ह्यन्यस्साधने हेतुः कर्मणोऽस्य हनूमतः। कार्यसिद्धिर्मतिश्चैव तस्मिन्वानरपुङ्गवे।।5.63.18।। व्यवसायश्च वीर्यं च श्रुतं चापि प्रतिष्ठितम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अस्य कर्मणः that duty, साधने capacity, हनूमतः Hanuman, अन्यः others, हेतुः the reason being, न हि not indeed, कार्यसिद्धि: capacity to accomplish, मतिश्चैव even wisdom, व्यवसायश्च even strenuous effort, वीर्यं च and virility, श्रुतं चापि enthusiasm, तस्मिन् for him, वानरपुङ्गवे to only the foremost of the monkys, प्रतिष्ठितम् are wellestablished.

#### Explanation

"Hanuman alone has the ability to do it, the reason being he has the capacity the wisdom, streneous effort, virility and enthusiasm to accomplish it. These attributes are wellestablished in him.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

जाम्बवान्यत्र नेता स्यादङ्गदश्च महाबलः।।5.63.19।। हनुमांश्चाप्यधिष्ठाता न तस्य गतिरन्यथा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

यत्र where, जाम्बवान् Jambavan, नेता is the leader, स्यात् remains, महाबलः strong, अङ्गदश्च Angada also, हनुमांश्च and Hanuman, अधिष्ठाता directing, तस्य that, गतिः will be done, अन्यथा otherwise, न not.

#### Explanation

Where Jambavan, Angada and Hanuman are leaders directing the task of the army it must have been accomplished.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

अङ्गदप्रमुखैर्वीरैर्हतं मधुवनं किल।।5.63.20।। वारयन्तश्च सहितास्तदा जानुभिराहताः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अङ्गदप्रमुखैः Angada and other leaders, वीरैः heroes, मधुवनम् Madhuvanam, हतं किल destroyed the garden, सहिताः along with others, वारयन्तः च when they were obstructed, तदा then, जानुभिः knees, आहताः beaten.

#### Explanation

None

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

एतदर्थमयं प्राप्तो वक्तुं मधुरवागिह।।5.63.21।। नाम्ना दधिमुखो नाम हरिः प्रख्यातविक्रमः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

नाम्ना named, दधिमुखो नाम Dadhimukha, प्रख्यातविक्रमः known for his valour, हरिः monkey, एतदर्थम् on account of it, वक्तुम् to tell, मधुरवाक् sweet tongued, इह here, प्राप्तः came.

#### Explanation

"Here is Dadhimukha, a vanara, known for his valour and sweet tongue. He came here this incident to tell.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

दृष्टा सीता महाबाहो सौमित्रे पश्य तत्त्वतः।।5.63.22।। अभिगम्य तथा सर्वे पिबन्ति मधु वानराः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाबाहो strongarmed, सौमित्रे O Saumitri, सीता Sita, दृष्टा would have seen, तत्त्वतः truly, पश्य seen, तथा so, वानराः vanaras, सर्वे all, अभिगम्य having arrived, मधु honey, पिबन्ति drank.

#### Explanation

"O strongarmed Saumitri the Vanaras must have seen Sita. Or else, they would not have arrived to drink honey.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

न चाप्यदृष्ट्वा वैदेहीं विश्रुताः पुरुषर्षभ।।5.63.23।। वनं दत्तवरं दिव्यं धर्षयेयुर्वनौकसः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पुरुषर्षभ bull among men, विश्रुताः renowned, वनौकसः forestdwellers, वैदेहीम् Vaidehi, अदृष्ट्वा not seen, दत्तवरम् that which was given as boon, दिव्यम् wonderful, वनम् garden, न धर्षयेयुः entered.

#### Explanation

"If the renowned vanaras had not seen Vaidehi, they would not have entered the wonderful garden granted as boon (by Brahma).

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

ततः प्रहृष्टो धर्मात्मा लक्ष्मणस्सह राघवः।।5.63.24।। श्रुत्वा कर्णसुखां वाणीं सुग्रीववदनाच्च्युताम्। प्राहृष्यत भृशं रामो लक्ष्मणश्च महाबलः।।5.63.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, सह राघवः also Rama, धर्मात्मा righteous, लक्ष्मणः Lakshmana, प्रहृष्टः very cheerful rejoiced, सुग्रीववदनात् Sugriva's mouth, च्युताम् delivered, कर्णसुखाम् pleasing to the ears, वाणीम् words, श्रुत्वा having heard, रामः Rama, प्राहृष्यत felt happy, महाबलः mighty strong, लक्ष्मणश्च even Lakshmana, भृशम् very, प्राहृष्यत happy.

#### Explanation

"Then righteous Rama and Lakshmana rejoiced to hear the words of Sugriva, which were pleasing to the ears.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

ततः प्रहृष्टो धर्मात्मा लक्ष्मणस्सह राघवः।।5.63.24।। श्रुत्वा कर्णसुखां वाणीं सुग्रीववदनाच्च्युताम्। प्राहृष्यत भृशं रामो लक्ष्मणश्च महाबलः।।5.63.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, सह राघवः also Rama, धर्मात्मा righteous, लक्ष्मणः Lakshmana, प्रहृष्टः very cheerful rejoiced, सुग्रीववदनात् Sugriva's mouth, च्युताम् delivered, कर्णसुखाम् pleasing to the ears, वाणीम् words, श्रुत्वा having heard, रामः Rama, प्राहृष्यत felt happy, महाबलः mighty strong, लक्ष्मणश्च even Lakshmana, भृशम् very, प्राहृष्यत happy.

#### Explanation

"Then righteous Rama and Lakshmana rejoiced to hear the words of Sugriva, which were pleasing to the ears.

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

श्रुत्वा दधिमुखस्येदं सुग्रीवस्सम्प्रहृष्य च। वनपालं पुनर्वाक्यं सुग्रीवः प्रत्यभाषत।।5.63.26।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सुग्रीवः one with beautiful neck, सुग्रीवः Sugriva, दधिमुखस्य Dadhimukha's, इदम् thus, श्रुत्वा having heard, सम्प्रहृष्य च very glad, पुनः again, वनपालम् protector of the garden, वाक्यम् these words, प्रत्यभाषत replied.

#### Explanation

Beautifulnecked Sugriva heard Dadhimukha, the protector of the garden, felt very glad and replied:

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

प्रीतोऽस्मि सोऽहं यद्भुक्तं वनं तैः कृतकर्मभिः। मर्षितं मर्षणीयं च चेष्टितं कृतकर्मणाम्।।5.63.27।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कृतकर्मभिः having performed the duty, तैः they, वनम् garden, यत् भुक्तम् for drinking that honey, सः अहम् I, प्रीतः अस्मि I am pleased, कृतकर्मणाम् having accomplished the task, मर्षणीयम् are to be excused, चेष्टितम् their behaviour, मर्षितम् excused.

#### Explanation

"They went to the garden to drink only after having performed their duty. I am glad about it. Since they have accomplished the purpose, they are to be excused for their behaviour. I have pardoned them.

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

इच्छामि शीघ्रं हनुमत्प्रधानान् शाखामृगांस्तान् मृगराजदर्पान्। द्रष्टुं कृतार्थान् सह राघवाभ्यां श्रोतुं च सीताधिगमे प्रयत्नम्।।5.63.28।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हनुमत्प्रधानान् led by Hanuman, मृगराजदर्पान् with the majesty of the lion, कृतार्थान् acomplished ones, तान् them, शाखामृगान् vanaras (who live on trees), राघवाभ्यां सह Rama and others, द्रष्टुम् to see, सीताधिगमेन to see Sita, प्रयत्नम् endeavour, श्रोतुं च to hear, इच्छामि desiring.

#### Explanation

None

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

प्रीतिस्फीताक्षौ सम्प्रहृष्टौ कुमारौ दृष्ट्वा सिद्धार्थौ वानराणां च राजा। अङ्गैः संहृष्टैः कर्मसिद्धिं विदित्वा बाह्वोरासन्नां सोऽतिमात्रं ननन्द।।5.63.29।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः he (Sugriva), वानराणां राजा king of vanaras, प्रीतिस्फीताक्षौ eyes filled with joy, सम्प्रहृष्टौ very happy, सिद्धार्धौ accomplished, कुमारौ young, दृष्ट्वा seeing, संहृष्टैः very glad, अङ्गैः limbs of the body, कर्मसिद्धिम् task has been accomplished, बाह्वोः shoulders, आसन्नाम् time has come, विदित्वा recognising, अतिमात्रम् highly, ननन्द delighted.

#### Explanation

"Beholding young Rama and Lakshmana extremely delighted, their eyes filled with joy out of fulfilment of the purpose, Sugriva who was also highly delighted felt an auspicious thrill on his limbs and knew that good time had come. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये सुन्दरकाण्डे त्रिषष्टितमस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the sixtythird sarga of Sundarakanda of the holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 64

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

सुग्रीवेणैवमुक्तस्तु हृष्टो दधिमुखः कपिः। राघवं लक्ष्मणं चैव सुग्रीवं चाभ्यवादयत्।।5.64.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सुग्रीवेण by Sugriva, एवम् in that way, उक्तः spoken, कपिः monkey, दधिमुखः Dadhimukha, हृष्टः pleased, राघवम् Rama, लक्ष्मणं चैव and also Lakshmana, सुग्रीवं च and Sugriva, अभ्यवादयत् offered salutations.

#### Explanation

Pleased with Sugriva's command (to send forth Angada and Hanuman) Dadhimukha offered salutations to Sugriva, Rama  and Lakshmana.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

स प्रणम्य च सुग्रीवं राघवौ च महाबलौ। वानरैः सहितः शूरैर्दिवमेवोत्पपात ह।।5.64.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः he, Dadhimukha, सुग्रीवम् to Sugriva, महाबलौ mighty strong, राघवौ च Rama, प्रणम्य offering obeisance, शूरैः heroes, वानरैः vanaras, सहितः followed by, दिवमेव to the sky, उत्पपात rose up.

#### Explanation

Dadhimukha offered his obeisance to Sugriva and the mighty Rama and Lakshmana  and rose up to the sky followed by the vanaras.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

स यथैवाऽगतः पूर्वं तथैव त्वरितं गतः। निपत्य गगनाद्भूमौतद्वनं प्रविवेश ह।।5.64.3।। स प्रविष्टो मधुवनं ददर्श हरियूथपान्। विमदानुत्थितान्सर्वान् मेहमानान्मधूदकम्।।5.64.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मधुवनम् Madhuvanam, प्रविष्टः entered, सः he, विमदान् intoxication, मदूदकम् honey and water, मेहमानान् voiding urine, सर्वान् all, हरियूथपान् troops of vanaras, ददर्श saw.

#### Explanation

Descending into Madhuvanam, Dadhimukha saw all the monkey troops free from intoxication (of honeywine).

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

स यथैवाऽगतः पूर्वं तथैव त्वरितं गतः। निपत्य गगनाद्भूमौतद्वनं प्रविवेश ह।।5.64.3।। स प्रविष्टो मधुवनं ददर्श हरियूथपान्। विमदानुत्थितान्सर्वान् मेहमानान्मधूदकम्।।5.64.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मधुवनम् Madhuvanam, प्रविष्टः entered, सः he, विमदान् intoxication, मदूदकम् honey and water, मेहमानान् voiding urine, सर्वान् all, हरियूथपान् troops of vanaras, ददर्श saw.

#### Explanation

Descending into Madhuvanam, Dadhimukha saw all the monkey troops free from intoxication (of honeywine).

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

स तानुपागमद्वीरो बद्ध्वा करपुटाञ्जलिम्। उवाच वचनं श्लक्ष्णमिदं हृष्टवदङ्गदम्।।5.64.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वीरः hero, सः he, करपुटाञ्जलिम् cupping his palms reverentially, बद्ध्वा holding, तान् him, उपागमत् returned, हृष्टवत् with joy, अङ्गदम् Angada, श्लक्ष्णम् conciliatory, इदं वचनम् these words, उवाच spoken.

#### Explanation

Heroic Dadhimukha went to Angada, holding his cupped palms reverentially and spoke these conciliatory words with joy:

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

सौम्य रोषो न कर्तव्यो यदेतत्परिवारितम्। अज्ञानाद्रक्षिभिः क्रोधाद्भवन्तः प्रतिषेधिताः।।5.64.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सौम्य prince of mild disposition, एतत् all this, यत् परिवारितम् that your companions, रोषः harsh, न कर्तव्यः not react, भवन्तः you, रक्षिभिः even the guards, अज्ञानात् out of ignorance, क्रोधात् fury, प्रतिषेधिताः have obstructed you.

#### Explanation

"O handsome one, do not react harshly at these guards who have obstructed your companions out of ignorance and fury.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

युवराजस्त्वमीशश्च वनस्यास्य महाबल। मौर्ख्यात्पूर्वं कृतो दोषस्तं भवान् क्षन्तुमर्हति।।5.64.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाबल mighty, त्वम् you, युवराजः heir apparent, अस्य वनस्य at this garden, ईशश्च king also, पूर्वम् earlier, मौर्ख्यात् out of foolishness, दोषः mistake, कृतः did, तम् to you, भवान् you, क्षन्तुम् be at peace, अर्हति is proper.

#### Explanation

"O mighty Angada you are heir apparent and also king. Out of foolishness I obstructed you. You ought to pardon me this mistake.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

आख्यातं हि मया गत्वा पितृव्यस्य तवानघ। इहोपयातं सर्वेषामेतेषां वनचारिणाम्।।5.64.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अनघ blameless, मया I, गत्वा gone, तव your, पितृव्यस्य elder brother of your father, सर्वेषाम् all, एतेषाम् your people, वनचारिणाम् forestdwellers, इह here, उपयातम् your arrival, आख्यातं हि indeed told.

#### Explanation

"O blameless prince I have already told your father's elder brother and all the forestdwellers about your arrival here.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

स त्वदागमनं श्रुत्वा सहैभिर्हरियूथपैः। प्रहृष्टो न तु रुष्टोऽसौ वनं श्रुत्वा प्रधर्षितम्।।5.64.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः he, एभिः these, सह with, त्वदागमनम् your arrival, श्रुत्वा hearing, प्रहृष्टः very glad, तु you, वनम् garden, प्रधर्षितम् destroying, श्रुत्वा having heard, असौ he was, न रुष्टः not angry.

#### Explanation

"He was not angry at your destruction of the garden. On the other hand, he was very glad to hear about your arrival.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

प्रहृष्टो मां पितृव्यस्ते सुग्रीवो वानरेश्वरः। शीघ्रं प्रेषय सर्वांस्तानिति होवाच पार्थिवः।।5.64.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ते पितृव्यः your father's brother, वानरेश्वरः lord of vanaras, पार्थिवः king, सुग्रीवः Sugriva, प्रहृष्टः happily, तान् सर्वान् all of them, शीघ्रम् quickly, प्रेषय send, इति this, माम् to me, उवाच ह said indeed.

#### Explanation

"Your father's brother, Sugriva, the lord of vanaras felt very glad and indeed asked me to send you quickly."

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

श्रुत्वा दधिमुखस्येदं वचनं श्लक्ष्णमङ्गदः। अब्रवीत्तान् हरिश्रेष्ठो वाक्यं वाक्यविशारदः।।5.64.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दधिमुखस्य to Dadhumukha, इदम् this, श्लक्ष्णम् very good, वचनम् these words, श्रुत्वा having heard, हरिश्रेष्ठः monkey leader, वाक्यविशारदः eloquent in speech, अङ्गदः Angada, तान् to them, वाक्यम् these words, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

At these good words from Dadhimukha, Angada, the monkey leader, an eloquent speaker said this:.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

शङ्के श्रुतोऽयं वृत्तान्तो रामेण हरियूथपाः। तत्क्षमं नेह नः स्थातुं कृते कार्ये परन्तपाः।।5.64.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

परन्तपाः scorcher of enemies, हरियूथपाः leader of monkeys, अयं वृत्तान्तः about this here, रामेण by Rama, श्रुतः having heard, शङ्के I presume, तत् that, कार्ये task, कृते having done, इह here, स्थातुम् to be here, नः for us, न क्षमम् not proper.

#### Explanation

"O scorcher of enemies I presume Rama has already heard the news of our arrival on accomplishment of the task. Hence it is not proper for us to tarry here.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

पीत्वा मधु यथाकामं विश्रान्ता वनचारिणः। किं शेषं गमनं तत्र सुग्रीवो यत्र मे गुरुः।।5.64.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वनचारिणः forestrangers, vanaras, यथाकामम् as they desire, मधु honey, पीत्वा having drunk, विश्रान्ताः rested, किम् what, शेषम् is left, मे my, गुरुः king, सुग्रीवः Sugriva, यत्र where, तत्र there, गमनम् have to go.

#### Explanation

"The vanaras of the forest have taken rest after drinking as much honey as they desired. What is left here to do? We have to go wherever Sugriva is."

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

सर्वे यथा मां वक्ष्यन्ति समेत्य हरियूथपाः। तथास्मि कर्ता कर्तव्ये भवद्भिः परवानहम्।।5.64.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सर्वे all, हरियूथपाः monkey troops, समेत्य assembled together, माम् me, यथा like that, वक्ष्यन्ति would tell, यथा in that way, कर्ता we are to do, अस्मि we, कर्तव्ये in our duty, अहम् I am, भवद्भिः as you say, परवान् have to do.

#### Explanation

Angada said to the vanara troops assembled, "Tell me what we have to do. I shall act as you wish.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

नाज्ञापयितुमीशोऽहं युवराजोऽस्मि यद्यपि। अयुक्तं कृतकर्माणो यूयं धर्षयितुं मया।।5.64.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I am, युवराजः heir apparent, अस्मि यद्यपि even though I am, आज्ञापयितुम् to order you, न ईशः not lord, कृतकर्माणः accomplished dones, यूयम् troops, मया by me, धर्षयितुम् ordering, अयुक्तम् not proper.

#### Explanation

"Even though I am heir apparent, it is not proper for me to order you.You have accomplished the purpose and to command you is not befitting on my part."

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

ब्रुवतश्चाङ्गदस्यैवं श्रुत्वा वचनमव्ययम्। प्रहृष्टमनसो वाक्यमिदमूचुर्वनौकसः।।5.64.16।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

एवम् in that way, ब्रुवतः when said, अङ्गदस्य by Angada, अव्ययम् priceless, वचनम् words, श्रुत्वा having heard, वनौकसः forest dwellers, प्रहृष्टमनः glad at heart, इदम् these, ऊचुः said.

#### Explanation

On hearing Angada's valuable words said in that way, the vanaras repled:

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

एवं वक्ष्यति को राजन् प्रभुस्सन्वानरर्षभ। ऐश्वर्यमदमत्तो हि सर्वोऽहमिति मन्यते।।5.64.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वानरर्षभ bull among vanaras, राजन् O king, प्रभुः सन् who is lord, कः who, एवम् in that way, वक्ष्यति said so, सर्वः all others, ऐश्वर्यमदमत्तः arrogant on account of prosperity, अहम् I, इति thus, मन्यते हि think .

#### Explanation

"O king O bull among monkeys we think you are an exception to those to whom prosperity brings arrogance.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

तव चेदं सुसदृशं वाक्यं नान्यस्य कस्यचित्। सन्नतिर्हि तवाख्याति भविष्यच्छुभयोग्यताम्।।5.64.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

इदं वाक्यम् these words, तव च you alone would have said, सुसदृशम् your humility, अन्यस्य in others, कस्यचित्  you only, न not, तव your, सन्नतिः humble, भविष्यच्छुभयोग्यताम् fit for a bright future, आख्याति speaks of.

#### Explanation

"You alone could speak such words. Your humility speaks of your fitness for a  bright future.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

सर्वे वयमपि प्राप्तास्तत्र गन्तुं कृतक्षणाः। स यत्र हरिवीराणां सुग्रीवः पतिरव्ययः।।5.64.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

प्राप्ताः come here, वयम् us, सर्वेऽपि all of us, हरिवीराणाम् vanara heroes, पतिः lord, अव्ययः one who is immortal, सः सुग्रीवः that Sugriva, यत्र to him, तत्र there, गन्तुम् to go, कृतक्षणाः ready at any moment.

#### Explanation

None

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

त्वया ह्यनुक्तैर्हरिभिर्नैव शक्यं पदात्पदम्। क्वचिद्गन्तुं हरिश्रेष्ठ ब्रूमः सत्यमिदं तु ते।।5.64.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हरिश्रेष्ठ best of the vanaras, Angada, त्वया your, अनुक्तै: order, हरिभिः vanaras, पदात् on foot, पदम् one step, क्वचित् not even, गन्तुम् to go, न शक्यम् not possible, सत्यम् it is true, इदम् this, ते to you, ब्रूमः appealed.

#### Explanation

"O Angada the best of vanaras without your command none will find it possible to put forward even a single step."

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

एवं तु वदतां तेषामङ्गदः प्रत्युवाच ह। बाढं गच्छाम इत्युक्त्वा खमुत्पेतुर्महाबलाः।।5.64.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तेषाम् those vanaras, एवम् that way, वदताम् spoken, अङ्गदः Angada, बाढम् very good, गच्छाम let us go, प्रत्युवाच ह replied, इति thus, उक्त्वा having spoken, महाबलाः powerful vanaras, खम् to the sky, उत्पेतुः rose up.

#### Explanation

Thus addressed by all the vanaras, Angada said 'Well, let us go'. Then the powerful vanaras rose up the sky.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

उत्पतन्तमनूत्पेतु स्सर्वे ते हरियूथपाः। कृत्वाकाशं निराकाशं यन्त्रोत्क्षिप्ता इवाचलाः।।5.64.22।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सर्वे all, ते हरियूथपाः vanara troops, यन्त्रोत्क्षिप्ताः  stones shattered by machines, अचलाः mountain, इव like, आकाशम् sky, निराकाशम् as if there is no sky, कृत्वा did, उत्पतन्तम् rising up, अनूत्पेतुः sprang up.

#### Explanation

All the vanara troops sprang up into the air as though there was no sky, like stones shattered by machines from the  mountains rise up.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

तेऽम्बरं सहसोत्पत्य वेगवन्तः प्लवङ्गमाः। विनदन्तो महानादं घना वातेरिता यथा।।5.64.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वेगवन्तः swift, ते प्लवङ्गमाः those monkeys, महानादम् roared aloud, विनदन्तः noise, वातेरिताः driven by the Wind, घनाः यथा like heavy clouds, सहसा at once, अम्बरम् to the sky, उत्पत्य went up.

#### Explanation

The swift monkeys roared aloud as though they went up the sky like thundering clouds driven by  the wind.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

अङ्गदे ह्यननुप्राप्ते सुग्रीवो वानराधिपः। उवाच शोकोपहतं रामं कमललोचनम्।।5.64.24।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अङ्गदे Angada, अननुप्राप्ते before he came, वानराधिपः lord of the vanaras, सुग्रीवः Sugriva, शोकोपहतम् stricken with grief, कमललोचनम् lotus eyed, रामम् Rama, उवाच said.

#### Explanation

Seeing the griefstricken, lotuseyed Rama, the lord of the vanaras Sugriva said this before the arrival of Angada:

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

समाश्वसिहि भद्रं ते दृष्टा देवी न संशयः। नागन्तुमिह शक्यं तैरतीते समये हि नः।।5.64.25।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

समाश्वसिहि trust me, ते to you, भद्रम् auspicious, देवी queen, दृष्टा have seen, संशयः doubt, न not, नः us, समये time, अतीते exceeded, तैः they, इह here, आगन्तुम् to come, न शक्यम् not possible.

#### Explanation

"Trust me, Rama. Be blessed. The vanaras have seen the divine lady. There is no doubt. It is not possible for them to come here after exceeding the time limit  (in their search for Sita).

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

न मत्सकाशमागच्छेत्कृत्ये हि विनिपातिते। युवराजो महाबाहुः प्लवतां प्रवरोऽङ्गदः।।5.64.26।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

युवराजः heir apparent, महाबाहुः strongarmed one, प्लवताम् leaping, प्रवरः endowed with good virtues, अङ्गदः Angada, कृत्ये mission, विनिपातिते has failed, मत्सकाशम् near me, नागच्छेत् will not come.

#### Explanation

"My heir apparent, the strongarmed Angada, and the best of the monkeys is endowed with virtues. He would not come near me if his mission had failed.

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

यद्यप्यकृतकृत्यानामीदृश स्स्यादुपक्रमः। भवेत्स दीनवदनो भ्रान्तविप्लुतमानसः।।5.64.27।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अकृतकृत्यानाम् in case they have not accomplished, उपक्रमः return, ईदृशः this way, यद्यपि स्यात् acted that way, सः he, दीनवदनः pathetic face, भ्रान्त विप्लुतमानसः perplexed and unsteady in mind, भवेत् would be.

#### Explanation

"In case the vanaras have returned without accomplishing the task, Angada would have assumed a pathetic face, perplexed and unsteady in mind.

---

### Shloka 28

#### Shloka Text

पितृपैतामहं चैतत्पूर्वकैरभिरक्षितम्। न मे मधुवनं हन्यादहृष्टः प्लवगेश्वरः।।5.64.28।। कौसल्यासुप्रजा राम समाश्वसिहि सुव्रत।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

प्लवगेश्वरः lord of monkeys, अहृष्टः not been happy, पितृपैतामहम् father and grandfather's time, पूर्वकैः ancestors, अभिरक्षितम् protected, मे मधुवनम् my Madhuvanam, न हन्यात् not destroy, कौसल्यासुप्रजाः Kausalya's son, सुव्रत one who pracitces good traditions, राम Rama, समाश्वसिहि trust me.

#### Explanation

"If Angada, lord of the vanaras had not been happy, he would not have destroyed the Madhuvanam of my father's and my grandfather's time. O Rama, follower of righteous practices, Kausalya has an excellent son in you. Trust me.

---

### Shloka 29

#### Shloka Text

दृष्टा देवी न सन्देहो न चान्येन हनूमता।।5.64.29।। न ह्यन्यः कर्मणो हेतुस्साधनेऽस्य हनूमतः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कर्मणो this task, हेतुस्साधनेऽस्य the reason for accomplishing, देवी Sita, दृष्टा saw, सन्देहः doubt, न  not, अन्येन not others, न not, हनुमता Hanuman.

#### Explanation

"Hanuman is the one who has accomplished this task. Who else can? No doubt, he has seen Sita.

---

### Shloka 30

#### Shloka Text

हनूमति हि सिद्धिश्च मतिश्च मतिसत्तमः।।5.64.30।। व्यवसायश्च वीर्यं च सूर्ये तेज इव ध्रुवम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मतिसत्तम best one among the wise, हनूमति Hanuman, सूर्ये Sun, तेजः इव like the luminosity, सिद्धिश्च capacity to succeed, मतिश्च intelligence, व्यवसायश्च effort, वीर्यं च and even courage, ध्रुवम् steadfast.

#### Explanation

"O Rama, the best among the wise just like the luminosity that abides in the Sun, capacity to succeed, energy, intelligence, even courage and steadfastness reside in Hanuman.

---

### Shloka 31

#### Shloka Text

जाम्बवान्यत्र नेता स्यादङ्गदश्च बलेश्वरः। हनुमांश्चाप्यधिष्ठाता न तस्य गतिरन्यथा।।5.64.31।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

यत्र there, जाम्बवान् Jambavan, नेता leader, स्यात् remains, महाबलः commander of army अङ्गदश्च and Angada, हनुमांश्च and Hanuman, अधिष्ठाता the guiding force, तस्य that, गतिः end result, अन्यथा otherwise, न not be.

#### Explanation

"Where Jambavan is the leader, Angada the commander of the army and Hanuman the guiding force, the end result will not be otherwise.

---

### Shloka 32

#### Shloka Text

मा भूश्चिन्तासमायुक्तस्सम्प्रत्यमितविक्रमः।।5.64.32।। ततः किलकिलाशब्दं शुश्रावासन्नमम्बरे। हनुमत्कर्मदृप्तानां नार्धतां काननौकसाम्।।5.64.33।। किष्किन्धामुपयातानां सिद्धिं कथयतामिव।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अमितविक्रमः extremely valiant, सम्प्रति at this time, चिन्तासमायुक्तः anxious, मा भूः do not, ततः at that time, हनुमत्कर्मदृप्तानाम् in the pride of Hanuman's success, नार्धताम् roaring, सिद्धिम् successfully completed the work, कथयतामिव talking, किष्किन्धाम् in Kishkinda, उपयातानाम् have reached, काननौकसाम् forestdwellers, vanaras, अम्बरे in the sky, आसन्नम् have arrived, किलकिलाशब्दम् chatterings, शुश्राव was heard.

#### Explanation

"You are extremely valiant and it is unbecoming of you to be anxious at this time Rama. Just then chatterings of the monkeys were heard from the sky as they roared in pride talking of Hanuman's achievement. Having successfully completed the errand, they have arrived at Kishkinda.

---

### Shloka 33

#### Shloka Text

मा भूश्चिन्तासमायुक्तस्सम्प्रत्यमितविक्रमः।।5.64.32।। ततः किलकिलाशब्दं शुश्रावासन्नमम्बरे। हनुमत्कर्मदृप्तानां नार्धतां काननौकसाम्।।5.64.33।। किष्किन्धामुपयातानां सिद्धिं कथयतामिव।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अमितविक्रमः extremely valiant, सम्प्रति at this time, चिन्तासमायुक्तः anxious, मा भूः do not, ततः at that time, हनुमत्कर्मदृप्तानाम् in the pride of Hanuman's success, नार्धताम् roaring, सिद्धिम् successfully completed the work, कथयतामिव talking, किष्किन्धाम् in Kishkinda, उपयातानाम् have reached, काननौकसाम् forestdwellers, vanaras, अम्बरे in the sky, आसन्नम् have arrived, किलकिलाशब्दम् chatterings, शुश्राव was heard.

#### Explanation

"You are extremely valiant and it is unbecoming of you to be anxious at this time Rama. Just then chatterings of the monkeys were heard from the sky as they roared in pride talking of Hanuman's achievement. Having successfully completed the errand, they have arrived at Kishkinda.

---

### Shloka 34

#### Shloka Text

ततश्श्रुत्वा निनादं तं कपीनां कपिसत्तमः।।5.64.34।। आयताञ्चितलाङ्गूलस्सोऽभवद्धृष्टमानसः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, सः कपिसत्तमः that great monkey, Sugriva, कपीनाम् of the monkeys, तं निनादम् that roar, श्रुत्वा having heard, आयताञ्चितलाङ्गूलः lifting and shaking the long tail, हृष्टमानसः extremely happy, अभवत् remained.

#### Explanation

The great Sugriva heard the roar of the monkeys and was extremely happy. He kept raising and shaking his long tail in joy.

---

### Shloka 35

#### Shloka Text

आजग्मुस्तेऽपि हरयो रामदर्शनकांक्षिणः।।5.64.35।। अङ्गदं पुरतः कृत्वा हनूमन्तं च वानरम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ते हरयः those joyful monkeys, कपिः monkeys, अङ्गदम् Angada, वानरम् vanaras, हनूमन्तम् Hanuman, पुरतः in front, कृत्वा having, रामदर्शनकांक्षिणः eager to have a look at Rama, आजग्मुः reached.

#### Explanation

The joyful monkeys with Angada and Hanuman leading them descended with eagerness to have a close look at Rama.

---

### Shloka 36

#### Shloka Text

तेऽङ्गदप्रमुखा वीराः प्रहृष्ठाश्च मुदान्विताः।।5.64.36।। निपेतुर्हरिराजस्य समीपे राघवस्य च।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अङ्गदप्रमुखाः Angada, the chief of the army of monkeys, ते वीराः the heroes, प्रहृष्टाश्च very joyfully, मुदा exuberant, अन्विताः descended, हरिराजस्य to the king of monkeys, राघवस्य च and Rama, समीपे near, निपेतुः landed.

#### Explanation

Angada the chief along with the heroic monkeys very joyfully and exuberantly descended close to Sugriva, the king of monkeys and Rama.

---

### Shloka 37

#### Shloka Text

हनुमांश्च महाबाहुः प्रणम्य शिरसा ततः।।5.64.37।। नियतामक्षतां देवीं राघवाय न्यवेदयत्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, महाबाहुः strongarmed, हनुमान् Hanuman, शिरसा with head bowed down, प्रणम्य offered salutations, देवीम् divine lady, नियताम् with her constant devotion, अक्षताम् sound in body, राघवाय to Rama, न्यवेदयत् reported.

#### Explanation

Then the strongarmed Hanuman with his head bowed down offered salutations and reported, 'Divine lady Sita with her constant devotion to Sri Rama is sound in body'.

---

### Shloka 38

#### Shloka Text

दृष्टा देवीति हनुमद्वदनादमृतोपमम्।।5.64.38।। आकर्ण्य वचनं रामो हर्षमाप सलक्ष्मणः।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सलक्ष्मणः Lakshmana, रामः Rama, हनुमद्वदनात् only Hanuman, अमृतोपमम् sweet at nectar, देवी दृष्टा इति Sita was seen, these, वचनम् words, आकर्ण्य hearing, हर्षम् delighted, आप remained.

#### Explanation

On hearing the nectarlike words from Hanuman that Sita was found, Rama and Lakshmana were delighted.

---

### Shloka 39

#### Shloka Text

निश्चितार्थं ततस्तस्मिन् सुग्रीवं पवनात्मजे।।5.64.39।। लक्ष्मणः प्रीतिमान् प्रीतं बहुमानादवैक्षत।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, लक्ष्मणः Lakshmana, तस्मिन् पवनात्मजे that son of the Windgod, निश्चितार्थम् surely, प्रीतम् very affectionately, सुग्रीवम् and Sugriva, बहुमानात् respectfully, अवैक्षत glanced.

#### Explanation

Sugriva and Lakshmana glanced at Hanuman respectfully with affection thinking surely Hanuman alone has succeeded.

---

## Sarga 65

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

ततः प्रस्रवणं शैलं ते गत्वा चित्रकाननम्। प्रणम्य शिरसा रामं लक्ष्मणं च महाबलम्।।5.65.1।। युवराजं पुरस्कृत्य सुग्रीवमभिवाद्य च। प्रवृत्तिमथ सीतायाः प्रवक्तुमुपचक्रमुः।।5.65.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः thereafter, ते they, चित्रकाननम् wonderful forest of Chitra, प्रस्रवणम् शैलम् to Prasravana mount, युवराजम् heir apparent, पुरस्कृत्य placing in front with respect, गत्वा having arrived, रामम् to Rama, महाबलम् mighty, लक्ष्मणं च and Lakshmana, शिरसा bowed down, प्रणम्य offered salutations, सुग्रीवम् to Sugriva, अभिवाद्य च offered obeisance, अथ and then, सीतायाः  regarding Sita's, प्रवृत्तिम् state, प्रवक्तुम् to tell, उपचक्रमुः began.

#### Explanation

Thereafter the vanaras arrived at the Prasrvana mount, with wonderful forests and bowed down to Sri Rama and mighty Lakshaman and Sugriva placing their leader, heir apparent in front. Then they began telling the story of Sita and her state.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

ततः प्रस्रवणं शैलं ते गत्वा चित्रकाननम्। प्रणम्य शिरसा रामं लक्ष्मणं च महाबलम्।।5.65.1।। युवराजं पुरस्कृत्य सुग्रीवमभिवाद्य च। प्रवृत्तिमथ सीतायाः प्रवक्तुमुपचक्रमुः।।5.65.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः thereafter, ते they, चित्रकाननम् wonderful forest of Chitra, प्रस्रवणम् शैलम् to Prasravana mount, युवराजम् heir apparent, पुरस्कृत्य placing in front with respect, गत्वा having arrived, रामम् to Rama, महाबलम् mighty, लक्ष्मणं च and Lakshmana, शिरसा bowed down, प्रणम्य offered salutations, सुग्रीवम् to Sugriva, अभिवाद्य च offered obeisance, अथ and then, सीतायाः  regarding Sita's, प्रवृत्तिम् state, प्रवक्तुम् to tell, उपचक्रमुः began.

#### Explanation

Thereafter the vanaras arrived at the Prasrvana mount, with wonderful forests and bowed down to Sri Rama and mighty Lakshaman and Sugriva placing their leader, heir apparent in front. Then they began telling the story of Sita and her state.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

रावणान्तः पुरे रोधं राक्षसीभिश्च तर्जनम्। रामे समनुरागं च यश्चायं समयः कृतः।।5.65.3।। एतदाख्यान्ति ते सर्वे हरयो रामसन्निधौ।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सर्वे all, ते हरयः those vanaras, रावणान्तः पुरे in the chambers of Ravana, रोधम् detention, राक्षसीभिः ogresses, तर्जनं च threatening, रामे to Rama, समनुरागं च her devotion, यः that which, अयम् thus, समयः time limit, कृतः set, एतत् all that, रामसन्निधौ in Rama's presence, आख्यान्ति told.

#### Explanation

The monkeys in Rama's presence narrated all about Sita's detention in Ravana's Ashoka garden, the ogresses threatening her and the time limit fixed by Ravana (for her survival).

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

वैदेहीमक्षतां श्रुत्वा रामस्तूत्तरमब्रवीत्।।5.65.4।। क्व सीता वर्तते देवी कथं च मयि वर्तते। एतन्मे सर्वमाख्यात वैदेहीं प्रति वानराः।।5.65.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वैदेहीम् Vaidehi, अक्षताम् not harmed, श्रुत्वा hearing, रामस्तु Rama too, उत्तरम् responded, अब्रवीत् asked, देवी divine lady, सीता Sita, क्व tell me, वर्तते she is, मयि to me, कथम् how, वर्तते is she, वानराः vanara, वैदेहीं प्रति her disposition towards, एतत् all that, सर्वम् every thing, मे to me, आख्यात asked.

#### Explanation

Hearing of Sita alive and not harmed, Rama asked the vanaras, 'How is Sita? How is she disposed to me? Tell me everything'.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

वैदेहीमक्षतां श्रुत्वा रामस्तूत्तरमब्रवीत्।।5.65.4।। क्व सीता वर्तते देवी कथं च मयि वर्तते। एतन्मे सर्वमाख्यात वैदेहीं प्रति वानराः।।5.65.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वैदेहीम् Vaidehi, अक्षताम् not harmed, श्रुत्वा hearing, रामस्तु Rama too, उत्तरम् responded, अब्रवीत् asked, देवी divine lady, सीता Sita, क्व tell me, वर्तते she is, मयि to me, कथम् how, वर्तते is she, वानराः vanara, वैदेहीं प्रति her disposition towards, एतत् all that, सर्वम् every thing, मे to me, आख्यात asked.

#### Explanation

Hearing of Sita alive and not harmed, Rama asked the vanaras, 'How is Sita? How is she disposed to me? Tell me everything'.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

रामस्य गदितं श्रुत्वा हरयो रामसन्निधौ। चोदयन्ति हनूमन्तं सीतावृत्तान्तकोविदम्।।5.65.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हरयः vanaras,रामस्य Rama's, गदितम् words spoken, श्रुत्वा hearing, सीतावृत्तान्तकोविदम् who was aware of Sita's position, हनूमन्तम् Hanuman, रामसन्निधौ in the presence of Rama, चोदयन्ति requested to tell.

#### Explanation

On hearing the words from Rama, the vanaras requested Hanuman who was aware of  Sita's position to tell Rama all about her.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

श्रुत्वा तु वचनं तेषां हनुमान्मारुतात्मजः। प्रणम्य शिरसा देव्यै सीतायै तां दिशं प्रति।।5.65.7।। उवाच वाक्यं वाक्यज्ञस्सीताया दर्शनं यथा।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मारुतात्मजः son of the Windgod, वाक्यज्ञः wise in speech, हनुमान् Hanuman, तेषाम् their, वचनम् words, श्रुत्वा hearing, देव्यै to the divine lady, सीतायै Sita, तां दिशं प्रति to the direction in which she was located, शिरसा with head down, प्रणम्य offered salutations, सीतायाः about Sita's, दर्शनम् finding, यथा these, वाक्यम् words, उवाच spoke.

#### Explanation

The son of the Wind god, Hanuman who was wise in speech heard the words of Rama, offered salutations to Sita in the direction in which she was located and narrated the story about her discovery.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

समुद्रं लङ्घयित्वाहं शतयोजनमायतम्।।5.65.8।। अगच्छं जानकीं सीतां मार्गमाणो दिदृक्षया।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अहम् I, शतयोजनम् a hundred yojanas, आयतम् wide, समुद्रम् ocean, लङ्घयित्वा having crossed, जानकीम् Janaki,सीताम् Sita, मार्गमाणः searching, दिदृक्षया with the intention of seeing, आगच्छम् reached.

#### Explanation

"Having crossed the sea consisting of a hundred yojanas in quest of Vaidehi, I found her.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

तत्र लङ्केति नगरी रावणस्य दुरात्मनः।।5.65.9।। दक्षिणस्य समुद्रस्य तीरे वसति दक्षिणे।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तत्र there, दक्षिणस्य समुद्रस्य on the southern side of the sea, दक्षिणे तीरे of the southern shore, दुरात्मनः evilminded, रावणस्य Ravana's, लङ्केति in Lanka, नगरी city, वसति there is.

#### Explanation

"On the southern shore of the ocean is situated the city of Lanka , ruled by the evilminded Ravana.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

तत्र दृष्टा मया सीता रावणान्तः पुरे सती।।5.65.10।। सन्न्यस्य त्वयि जीवन्ती रामा राम मनोरथम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

रामा Rama, तत्र there, रावणान्तः पुरे in the inner palace of Ravana, सती your wife, त्वयि your, मनोरथम्  her hopes, सन्न्यस्य giving up desires, जीवन्ती living, राम lovely, सीता Sita, मया by me, दृष्टा seen.

#### Explanation

"There in the inner palace of Ravana, I saw your lovely wife Sita, with all her hopes pinned on you, giving up all other desires.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

दृष्टा मे राक्षसीमध्ये तर्ज्यमाना मुहुर्मुहुः।।5.65.11।। राक्षसीभिर्विरूपाभी रक्षिता प्रमदावने।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

प्रमदावने in that beautiful garden, विरूपाभिः hideous, राक्षसीभिः rakshasas, रक्षिता guarded, मुहुर्मुहुः again and again, तर्ज्यमाना threatening, मे I, राक्षसीमध्ये in the midst of ogresses, दृष्टा saw.

#### Explanation

"I found her in the beautiful garden guarded by hideous ogresses threatening her again and again৷৷

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

दुःख मासाद्यते देवी तथाऽदुःखोचिता सती।।5.65.12।। रावणान्तः पुरे रुद्धा राक्षसीभि स्सुरक्षिता। एकवेणीधरा दीना त्वयि चिन्तापरायणा।।5.65.13।। अधःशय्या विवर्णाङ्गी पद्मिनीव हिमागमे। रावणाद्विनिवृत्तार्था मर्तव्यकृतनिश्चया।।5.65.14।। देवी कथञ्चित्काकुत्स्थ त्वन्मना मार्गिता मया।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तथा in that way, दुःखोचिता mind filled with grief, देवी divine lady, दुःखम्  grief, आपद्यते not deserve to experience, रावणान्तःपुरे  in the inner palace of Ravana, रुद्धा  detained, राक्षसीभि by ogresses, स्सुरक्षिता guarded by, एकवेणीधरा wearing a single braid( a mark of desolation),दीना pathetic, त्वयि your,सती wife,चिन्तापरायणा absorbed in your thought, अध: शय्या now lying on bare ground, हिमागमे in winter season, पद्मिनीव like the lotus, विवर्णाङ्गी  limbs turned pale, रावणात् by Ravana, विनिवृत्तार्था averse to Ravana, मर्तव्यकृन्तिश्चया determined to die, काकुत्स्थ Rama alone, त्वन्मनाः in her mind, देवी Sita, कथञ्चित्  somehow, मार्गिता found.

#### Explanation

"Sita, who did not deserve and yet was full of grief was detained by Ravana in his inner palace, guarded by ogresses. She had single braid (a sign of desolation), was pathetic,  and totally absorbed in your thought. She was lying on bare ground with her limbs turned pale, like lotus in winter. She was averse to Ravana and was determined to commit suicide. She has only Rama in her mind. Somehow I found her.

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

दुःख मासाद्यते देवी तथाऽदुःखोचिता सती।।5.65.12।। रावणान्तः पुरे रुद्धा राक्षसीभि स्सुरक्षिता। एकवेणीधरा दीना त्वयि चिन्तापरायणा।।5.65.13।। अधःशय्या विवर्णाङ्गी पद्मिनीव हिमागमे। रावणाद्विनिवृत्तार्था मर्तव्यकृतनिश्चया।।5.65.14।। देवी कथञ्चित्काकुत्स्थ त्वन्मना मार्गिता मया।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तथा in that way, दुःखोचिता mind filled with grief, देवी divine lady, दुःखम्  grief, आपद्यते not deserve to experience, रावणान्तःपुरे  in the inner palace of Ravana, रुद्धा  detained, राक्षसीभि by ogresses, स्सुरक्षिता guarded by, एकवेणीधरा wearing a single braid( a mark of desolation),दीना pathetic, त्वयि your,सती wife,चिन्तापरायणा absorbed in your thought, अध: शय्या now lying on bare ground, हिमागमे in winter season, पद्मिनीव like the lotus, विवर्णाङ्गी  limbs turned pale, रावणात् by Ravana, विनिवृत्तार्था averse to Ravana, मर्तव्यकृन्तिश्चया determined to die, काकुत्स्थ Rama alone, त्वन्मनाः in her mind, देवी Sita, कथञ्चित्  somehow, मार्गिता found.

#### Explanation

"Sita, who did not deserve and yet was full of grief was detained by Ravana in his inner palace, guarded by ogresses. She had single braid (a sign of desolation), was pathetic,  and totally absorbed in your thought. She was lying on bare ground with her limbs turned pale, like lotus in winter. She was averse to Ravana and was determined to commit suicide. She has only Rama in her mind. Somehow I found her.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

दुःख मासाद्यते देवी तथाऽदुःखोचिता सती।।5.65.12।। रावणान्तः पुरे रुद्धा राक्षसीभि स्सुरक्षिता। एकवेणीधरा दीना त्वयि चिन्तापरायणा।।5.65.13।। अधःशय्या विवर्णाङ्गी पद्मिनीव हिमागमे। रावणाद्विनिवृत्तार्था मर्तव्यकृतनिश्चया।।5.65.14।। देवी कथञ्चित्काकुत्स्थ त्वन्मना मार्गिता मया।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तथा in that way, दुःखोचिता mind filled with grief, देवी divine lady, दुःखम्  grief, आपद्यते not deserve to experience, रावणान्तःपुरे  in the inner palace of Ravana, रुद्धा  detained, राक्षसीभि by ogresses, स्सुरक्षिता guarded by, एकवेणीधरा wearing a single braid( a mark of desolation),दीना pathetic, त्वयि your,सती wife,चिन्तापरायणा absorbed in your thought, अध: शय्या now lying on bare ground, हिमागमे in winter season, पद्मिनीव like the lotus, विवर्णाङ्गी  limbs turned pale, रावणात् by Ravana, विनिवृत्तार्था averse to Ravana, मर्तव्यकृन्तिश्चया determined to die, काकुत्स्थ Rama alone, त्वन्मनाः in her mind, देवी Sita, कथञ्चित्  somehow, मार्गिता found.

#### Explanation

"Sita, who did not deserve and yet was full of grief was detained by Ravana in his inner palace, guarded by ogresses. She had single braid (a sign of desolation), was pathetic,  and totally absorbed in your thought. She was lying on bare ground with her limbs turned pale, like lotus in winter. She was averse to Ravana and was determined to commit suicide. She has only Rama in her mind. Somehow I found her.

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

इक्ष्वाकुवंशविख्यातिं शनैः कीर्तयतानघ।।5.65.15।। सा मया नरशार्दूल विश्वासमुपपादिता।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अनघ sinless one, नरशार्दूल tiger among men, इक्ष्वाकुवंशविख्यातिम् all about the glory of Ikshvaku race, शनैः slowly, कीर्तयता praised, मया by me, सा she, विश्वासम् confidence, उपपादिता to inspire.

#### Explanation

"O sinless one O tiger among men To inspire confidence in her I praised the glory of your Ikshvaku dynasty.

---

### Shloka 16

#### Shloka Text

तत स्सम्भाषिता देवी सर्वमर्थं च दर्शिता।।5.65.16।। रामसुग्रीवसख्यं च श्रुत्वा प्रीतिमुपागता। नियत स्समुदाचारो भक्तिश्चास्यास्तथा त्वयि।।5.65.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, देवी divine lady, सम्भाषिता talked, सर्वम् all,अर्थम् all facts,दर्शिता presented to her, रामसुग्रीवसख्यं च about the alliance of Rama and Sugriva, श्रुत्वा on hearing, प्रीतिम् affectionately, उपागता became, अस्याः her, समुदाचारः virtuous, नियतः fixed, तथा so also, त्वयि towards, भक्तिश्च and devotion.

#### Explanation

"When the divine lady talked to me, I presented all the facts about the alliance of Rama and Sugriva. On hearing me, virtuous Sita, whose devotion is fixed on you became delighted.

---

### Shloka 17

#### Shloka Text

तत स्सम्भाषिता देवी सर्वमर्थं च दर्शिता।।5.65.16।। रामसुग्रीवसख्यं च श्रुत्वा प्रीतिमुपागता। नियत स्समुदाचारो भक्तिश्चास्यास्तथा त्वयि।।5.65.17।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

ततः then, देवी divine lady, सम्भाषिता talked, सर्वम् all,अर्थम् all facts,दर्शिता presented to her, रामसुग्रीवसख्यं च about the alliance of Rama and Sugriva, श्रुत्वा on hearing, प्रीतिम् affectionately, उपागता became, अस्याः her, समुदाचारः virtuous, नियतः fixed, तथा so also, त्वयि towards, भक्तिश्च and devotion.

#### Explanation

"When the divine lady talked to me, I presented all the facts about the alliance of Rama and Sugriva. On hearing me, virtuous Sita, whose devotion is fixed on you became delighted.

---

### Shloka 18

#### Shloka Text

एवं मया महाभागा दृष्टा जनकनन्दिनी। उग्रेण तपसा युक्ता त्वद्भक्त्या पुरुषर्षभ।।5.65.18।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पुरुषर्षभ bull among men, उग्रेण formidable, तपसा austerity, त्वद्भक्त्या devotion to you, युक्ता filled with, महाभागा prosperous, जनकनन्दिनी delight of Janaka, मया by me, एवम् that way, दृष्टा seen.

#### Explanation

"O bull among men I saw Janaki,the delight of Janaka, a formidable lady filled with devotion to you and richly endowed with austerity.

---

### Shloka 19

#### Shloka Text

अभिज्ञानं च मे दत्तं यथा वृत्तं तवान्तिके। चित्रकूटे महाप्राज्ञ वायसं प्रति राघव।।5.65.19।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाप्राज्ञ very wise, चित्रकूटे in Chitrakuta, तव your, अन्तिके provided me, वायसं प्रति with an anecdote of a crow, यथा so also, वृत्तम् told, अभिज्ञानम् as an identification for you, मे to me, दत्तम् presented.

#### Explanation

"O wise one, with your ring given her, she told me an anecdote of a crow that took place at Chitrakuta.

---

### Shloka 20

#### Shloka Text

विज्ञाप्यश्च नरव्याघ्रो रामो वायुसुत त्वया। अखिलेनेह यद्धृष्टमिति मामाह जानकी।।5.65.20।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वायुसुत son of the Windgod, नरव्याघ्रः tiger among men, रामः Rama, त्वया your, इह here, यत् that which, दृष्टम् seen, अखिलेन should tell, विज्ञाप्यः should let him know, इति thus, जानकी Janaki, माम् me, आह told.

#### Explanation

(She said)'O son of the Windgod you should let Rama, the tiger among men know all that you have seen here.

---

### Shloka 21

#### Shloka Text

अयं चास्मै प्रदातव्यो यत्नात्सुपरिरक्षितः। ब्रुवता वचनान्येवं सुग्रीवस्योपशृण्वतः।।5.65.21।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सुग्रीवस्य while Sugriva, उपशृण्वतः within hearing of, एवम् that way, वचनानि these words, ब्रुवता that I said, यत्नात् the efforts, सुपरिरक्षितः kept with great care, अयं च and I, अस्मै this, प्रदातव्यः be presented.

#### Explanation

'Within hearing of Sugriva, tell him about your efforts. Present this(signet) to Rama carefully preserved by me.

---

### Shloka 22

#### Shloka Text

एष चूडामणिश्श्रीमान् मया सुपरिरक्षितः। मनश्शिलायास्तिलको गण्डपार्श्वे निवेशितः।।5.65.22।। त्वया प्रणष्ठे तिलके तं किल स्मर्तुमर्हसि।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

श्रीमान् prosperous, एषः this, चूडामणिः Chudamani, मया by me, सुपरिरक्षितः very carefully preserved, तिलके red mark on the forehead, प्रणष्ठे when it was, त्वया by you, मनश्शिलायाः with the stone pigment, तिलकः mark on the forehead, गण्डपार्श्वे on my forehead, निवेशितः किल decorated indeed, तम् स्मर्तुम् remind him, अर्हसि is proper.

#### Explanation

'This auspicious (jewel) Chudamani has been preserved by me very carefully. Remind him of the decorative red mark he painted with a stone pigment on my forehead, indeed, it is proper to remind him of this.

---

### Shloka 23

#### Shloka Text

एष निर्यातितश्श्रीमान्मया ते वारि सम्भवः। एतं दृष्ट्वा प्रहृष्यामि व्यसने त्वामिवानघ।।5.65.23।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अनघ sinless one, दिव्यः wonderful, एषः this, निर्यातितः sent to you, श्रीमान् prosperous one, वारिसम्भवः born in the ocean, व्यसने in sorrow, एतम् this (Chudamani), मया I, दृष्टवा gazing, त्वामिव you only, प्रहृष्यामि was feeling happy.

#### Explanation

'O sinless one tell Rama that this auspicious Chudamani born in the ocean is sent to him and I was gazing at it as if it was him (Rama) and feeling happy.

---

### Shloka 24

#### Shloka Text

जीवितं धारयिष्यामि मासं दशरथात्मज।।5.65.24।। ऊर्ध्वं मासान्न जीवेयं रक्षसां वशमागता।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

दशरथात्मज Dasaratha's son, मासम् one month, जीवितम् living, धारयिष्यामि will hold on to, रक्षसाम् these demons, वशम् captivated by, आगता completion, मासात् after a month, ऊर्ध्वम् more than, न जीवेयम् will not live.

#### Explanation

'O Son of Dasaratha I will hold on to life for a month. Captured by the demons, I will not live for more than a month'.

---

### Shloka 25

#### Shloka Text

इति मामब्रवीत्सीता कृशाङ्गी धर्मचारिणी।।5.65.25।। रावणान्तः पुरे रुद्धा मृगीवोत्फुल्ललोचना।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

कृशाङ्गी enfeebled lady, धर्मचारिणी following righteous ways, रावणान्तःपुरे in the inner palace of Ravana, रुद्धा detained मृगीव like a doe, उत्फुल्ललोचना eyes wide open in fear, सीता Sita, माम् to me, इति this, अब्रवीत् said.

#### Explanation

"With her limbs emaciated through austerities detained in Ravana's inner palace, eyes wide open in fear, Sita said this to me:

---

### Shloka 26

#### Shloka Text

एतदेव मयाख्यातं सर्वं राघव यद्यथा।।5.65.26।। सर्वथा सागरजले संतारः प्रविधीयताम्।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

राघव Rama, यत् that which, यथा like that, एतत् happened, सर्वमेव every thing, मया by me, ख्यातम् has been said, सर्वथा ever, सागरजले water of the ocean, सन्तारः means to cross the ocean, प्रविधीयताम् pay attention to.

#### Explanation

"O Rama I have spoken everything that has happened. We have to pay attention to the means of crossing the ocean."

---

### Shloka 27

#### Shloka Text

तौ जाताश्वासौ राजपुत्रौ विदित्वा तच्चाभिज्ञानं राघवाय प्रदाय। देव्या चाख्यातं सर्वमेवानुपूर्व्याद्वाचा सम्पूर्णं वायुपुत्त्र श्शशंस।।5.65.27।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वायुपुत्रः son of the Windgod, तौ राजपुत्त्रौ the two princes Rama and Lakshmana, जाताश्वासौ sighing in relief, विदित्वा knowing, तत् that, अभिज्ञानम् identification, राघवाय to Rama, प्रदाय communicated, देव्या by Sita, आख्यातम् having spoken, सर्वमेव everything, सम्पूर्णम् completely, आनुपूर्व्यात् in an orderly manner, वाचा spoken, शशंस  Hanuman.

#### Explanation

Coming to know that the two princes were sighing in relief, Hanuman presented the token of identification given by Sita to Rama, after having communicated in full the messsage of Sita in an orderly manner. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये सुन्दरकाण्डे पञ्चषष्टितमस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the sixtyfifth sarga of Sundarakanda of the holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 66

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्तो हनुमता रामो दशरथात्मज:। तं मणिं हृदये कृत्वा प्ररुरोद सलक्ष्मणः।।5.66.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हनुमता Hanuman, एवम् in that manner, उक्तः spoken, सलक्ष्मणः Lakshmana, दशरथात्मजः son of Dasaratha, रामः Rama, तं मणिम् that ornament, हृदये to the bosom, कृत्वा held, प्ररुरोद wept.

#### Explanation

Thus addressed by Hanuman, Rama held the token (Chudamani) against his bosom and wept with Lakshmana.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

तं तु दृष्ट्वा मणिश्रेष्ठं राघव श्शोककर्शितः। नेत्राभ्यामश्रुपूर्णाभ्यां सुग्रीवमिदमब्रवीत्।।5.66.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तं मणिश्रेष्ठम् the precious jewel, दृष्ट्वा seeing, राघवः Rama, शोककर्शितः overcome with sorrow, अश्रुपूर्णाभ्याम् filled with tears, नेत्राभ्याम् both eyes, सुग्रीवम् to Sugriva, इदम् thus, अब्रवीत् spoke.

#### Explanation

Seeing the precious jewel, Rama's eyes were filled with tears. Overwhelmed with grief, he spoke to Sugriva.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

यथैव धेनु स्स्रवति स्नेहाद्वत्सस्य वत्सला। तथा ममापि हृदयं मणिरत्नस्य दर्शनात्।।5.66.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वत्सला fond of its calf, धेनुः cow, वत्सस्य at the sight of calf, स्नेहात् out of love, यथैव in the same way, स्रवति distils milk from its teats, मम हृदयमपि my heart also, मणिरत्नस्य at this excellent jewel, दर्शनात् on seeing, तथा  so does.

#### Explanation

"Just as a cow fond of its calf begins to distil milk from its teats at the sight of the calf, so does my heart melt at the sight of this excellent jewel.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

मणिरत्नमिदं दत्तं वैदेह्याश्श्वशुरेण मे। वधूकाले यथाबद्धमधिकं मूर्ध्नि शोभते।।5.66.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

वधूकाले at the time of marriage, मूर्ध्नि to the head, आबद्धम् tied, यथा so, शोभते charming, इदम् this, मणिरत्नम् gem of ornament, मे श्वशुरेण my fatherinlaw, वैदेह्याः to Vaidehi, दत्तम् presented.

#### Explanation

"At the time of marriage, this jewel was presented by my father-in-law. Vaidehi wore it on her forehead and it looked charming.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

अयं हि जलसम्भूतो मणिस्सज्जनपूजितः। यज्ञे परमतुष्टेन दत्तश्शक्रेण धीमता।।5.66.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

जलसम्भूतः found in water, सज्जनपूजितः worshipped by elders of the family, अयं मणिः this jewel यज्ञे in sacrificial ceremony, परमतुष्टेन great joy, धीमता wise one, शक्रेण by Indra, दत्तः given.

#### Explanation

"This was found in water and was bestowed by Indra out of great joy on Janaka who had performed a grand sacrificial ceremony. So it was worshipped by elders of the family.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

इमं दृष्ट्वा मणिश्रेष्ठं यथा तातस्य दर्शनम्। अद्यास्म्यवगतस्सौम्य वैदेहस्य तथा विभोः।।5.66.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सौम्य noble, अद्य now, इमम् this, मणिश्रेष्ठम् gem of jewel, दृष्ट्वा when I see, तातस्य father's, तथा so also, विभोः king, वैदेहस्य of Videha, दर्शनम् seeing, यथा as if, अवगतः get.

#### Explanation

"O noble Sugriva now when I see this excellent jewel, I get direct sight of my father and king of Videha (in the same way as I obtained the sight of Sita).

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

अयं हि शोभते तस्याः प्रियाया मूर्ध्नि मे मणिः। अस्याद्य दर्शने नाहं प्राप्तां तामिव चिन्तये।।5.66.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

अयं मणिः this gem, मे प्रियायाः my beloved, मूर्ध्नि head, शोभते हि indeed shone, अद्य now, अस्य its, दर्शनेन mere look, ताम् her, प्राप्तामिव as if I am seeing, चिन्तये I think.

#### Explanation

"This jewel was shining on the head of my beloved. Now its mere look makes me feel that I am seeing her.

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

किमाह सीता वैदेही ब्रूहि सौम्य पुनः पुनः। पिपासुमिव तोयेन सिञ्चन्ती वाक्यवारिणा।।5.66.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सौम्य O handsome (Hanuman), पिपासुम् for a thirsty one, तोयेन इव like water, वाक्यवारिणा in lifegiving water of words, सिञ्चन्ती sprinkle, वैदेही Vaidehi, सीता Sita, किम् what else, पुनः पुनः again and again, आह to speak, ब्रूहि asked.

#### Explanation

"O handsome Hanuman Sita is like water for a thirsty person. Tell me again and again what Sita said to you. Sprinkle on me (lifegiving) water of her words in the form of message.

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

इतस्तु किं दुःखतरं यदिमं वारिसम्भवम्। मणिं पश्यामि सौमित्रे वैदेहीमागतां विना।।5.66.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सौमित्रे O Saumitri, वारिसम्भवम् found in water, इमं मणिम् this jewel, वैदेहीम् of Vaidehi, आगतां विना without her, यत् पश्यामि seeing this, इतः here, दुःखतरम् cause more sorrow, किम् what?

#### Explanation

"O Saumitri I see this jewel born (formed) under water but not her. What can be more sorrowful to me?

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

चिरं जीवति वैदेही यदि मासं धरिष्यति। क्षणं सौम्य न जीवेयं विना तामसितेक्षणाम्।।5.66.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सौम्य O dear!, वैदेही Vaidehi, मासम् one month, धरिष्यति यदि if she lives, चिरम् for long, जीवति living, सौम्य O dear, ताम् her, असितेक्षणां विना without the (enchanting) black-eyed, क्षणम् for a moment, न जीवेयम् I cannot live

#### Explanation

(Addressing Hanuman again) "O dear, if she can live for a month it is living for long. I would not live for a moment without the black-eyed Vaidehi.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

नय मामपि तं देशं यत्र दृष्टा मम प्रिया। न तिष्ठेयं क्षणमपि प्रवृत्तिमुपलभ्य च।।5.66.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मम my, प्रिया beloved, यत्र दृष्टा where you saw, मामपि me too, तं देशम् that place, नय take me, प्रवृत्तिम् where she is, उपलभ्य च and knowing, क्षणमपि even a moment, न तिष्ठेयम् not stay here.

#### Explanation

"Take me to the place you saw my beloved. Knowing where she is I cannot stay here even for a moment.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

कथं सा मम सुश्रोणी भीरुभीरु स्सती सदा। भयावहानां घोराणां मध्ये तिष्ठति रक्षसाम्।।5.66.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सदा always, भीरुभीरुः सती very timid, मम सुश्रोणी my fairhipped beloved, भयावहानाम् fearsome forms, घोराणाम् dreadful ones, रक्षसाम् demons, मध्ये midst, कथम् how, तिष्ठति does she remain.

#### Explanation

"She is always very timid. How can my fairhipped beloved remain in the midst of dreadful and fearsome demons?

---

### Shloka 13

#### Shloka Text

शारद स्तिमिरोन्मुक्तो नूनं चन्द्रं इवांबुधैः। आवृतं वदनं तस्या न विराजति राक्षसैः।।5.66.13।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

तिमिरोन्मुक्तः bereft of brightness, अम्बुदैः by rainclouds, चन्द्रः इव like the Moon, राक्षसैः by demons, आवृतम् surrounded by, तस्याः her, वदनम् countenance, न विराजति not shine, नूनम् surely.

#### Explanation

"Her face resembling the autumnal Moon bereft of its stain, though covered with rainclouds does not shine so bright.

---

### Shloka 14

#### Shloka Text

किमाह सीता हनुमंस्तत्त्वतः कथयाद्य मे। एतेन खलु जीविष्ये भेषजेनातुरो यथा।।5.66.14।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

हनुमान् Hanuman, सीता Sita, किम् what, आह to go, अद्य now, तत्त्वतः truly, मे I am, कथय told by her, आतुरः eager, भेषजेन यथा just as medicine does, एतेन that alone, जीविष्ये खलु indeed, keep me alive.

#### Explanation

"O Hanuman Tell me now truly what has been told by her. I am eager to know. Indeed, it will keep me alive just as medicne does. (I shall survive on the strength of her message)

---

### Shloka 15

#### Shloka Text

मधुरा मधुरालापा किमाह मम भामिनी। मद्विहीना वरारोहा हनुमन् कथयस्व मे।।5.66.15।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

मधुरा lovely one, मधुरालापा one who speaks sweetly, वरारोहा fairhipped one, मद्विहीना separated from me, मम भामिनी my beloved, किम् what, आह did she say, मे to me, कथयस्व you may narrate.

#### Explanation

That lovely Sita who speaks sweetly, that fairhipped beautiful one who has been separated from me, my beloved, what did she say? Please narrate. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये सुन्दरकाण्डे षट्षष्टितमस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the sixtysixth sarga of Sundarakanda of the holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

---

## Sarga 67

### Shloka 1

#### Shloka Text

एवमुक्तस्तु हनुमान् राघवेण महात्मना। सीताया भाषितं सर्वं न्यवेदयत राघवे।।5.67.1।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महात्मना great soul, राघवेण to Rama, एवम् that way, उक्तः spoken, हनुमान् Hanuman, सीतायाः Sita's, भाषितम् said, सर्वम् everything, राघवे to Rama, न्यवेदयत related.

#### Explanation

Thus asked by Sri Rama, Hanuman repeated everything spoken by Sita to Rama.

---

### Shloka 2

#### Shloka Text

इदमुक्तवती देवी जानकी पुरुषर्षभ। पूर्व वृत्तमभिज्ञानं चित्रकूटे यथातथम्।।5.67.2।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पुरुषर्षभ bull among men, देवी queen, जानकी Janaki, चित्रकूटे at Chitrakuta, पूर्ववृत्तम् incident of the past, अभिज्ञानम् as an identification, यथातथम् as it is, इदम् this, उक्तवती she said.

#### Explanation

"O bull among men queen Janaki related to me as a token of identification an incident  that had taken place at Chitrakuta in the past.

---

### Shloka 3

#### Shloka Text

सुखसुप्ता त्वया सार्धं जानकी पूर्वमुत्थिता। वायस स्सहसोत्पत्य विददार स्तनान्तरे।।5.67.3।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

त्वया सार्धम् along with you, सुखसुप्ता lying down happily, जानकी Janaki, पूर्वम् earlier, उत्थिता woke up, वायसः a crow, सहसा swiftly, उत्पत्य got up, स्तनान्तरे on her breast, विददार scratched.

#### Explanation

"When she was lying on your lap happily she awoke early and a crow came swiftly and scratched on her breast.

---

### Shloka 4

#### Shloka Text

पर्यायेण च सुप्तस्त्वं देव्यङ्के भरताग्रज। पुनश्च किल पक्षी स देव्या जनयति व्यथाम्।।5.67.4।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भरताग्रज elder brother of Bharata, त्वम् you, पर्यायेण woke up, देव्यङ्के on her lap, सुप्तः slept, सः पक्षी that crow, पुनश्च again, देव्याः Sita's, व्यथाम् hurting, जनयति किल started hurting.

#### Explanation

"O Bharata's brother, when Sita awoke from sleep you slept on her lap. The same crow again hurt her.

---

### Shloka 5

#### Shloka Text

पुनः पुनरुपागम्य विददार भृशं किल। ततस्त्वं बोधितस्तस्याश्शोणितेन समुक्षितः।।5.67.5।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पुनः पुनः again and again, उपागम्य coming over her, भृशम् violently, विददार किल started scratching, ततः then, त्वम् you, तस्याः her, शोणितेन by blood, समुक्षितः wet by shedding, बोधितः किल woke you up.

#### Explanation

Again and again the crow came to her and scratched her. She woke you up as you were getting wet with blood shed from her body.

---

### Shloka 6

#### Shloka Text

वायसेन च तेनैव सततं बाध्यमानया। बोधितः किल देव्या त्वं सुखसुप्तः परन्तप।।5.67.6।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

परन्तप scorcher of enemies, तेन by that, वायसेनैव by the crow, सततम् repeatedly, बाध्यमानया pained, देव्या by the divine lady, सुखसुप्तः sleeping happily, त्वम् you, बोधितः किल woke you up and informed you.

#### Explanation

"O scorcher of enemies pained by the crow hurting her repeatedly, while you were sleeping happily, she woke you up and informed you about it.

---

### Shloka 7

#### Shloka Text

तां तु दृष्ट्वा महाबाहो दारितां च स्तनान्तरे। आशीविष इव क्रुद्धो निश्वसन्नभ्यभाषथाः।।5.67.7।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाबाहो one with strong arms, स्तनान्तरे from the breasts, दारिताम् flowing, ताम् her, दृष्ट्वा seeing, क्रुद्धः furious, आशीविष इव like a serpent, निश्वसन् sighing, अभ्यभाषथाः you said this.

#### Explanation

"Seeing the blood flowing from her breasts you became furious like a hissing serpent and said:

---

### Shloka 8

#### Shloka Text

नखाग्रैः केन ते भीरु दारितं तु स्तनान्तरम्। कः क्रीडति सरोषेण पञ्चवक्त्रेण भोगिना।।5.67.8।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

भीरु timid, ते you, स्तनान्तरम् from the breasts, केन who, नखाग्रैः with the tip of the nails, दारितम् scratched, सरोषेण furious, पञ्चवक्त्रेण भोगिना fivehooded serpent, कः who, क्रीडति sported.

#### Explanation

"You are of timid nature. Who has scratched your breasts with the tip of the nail? Who is sporting with an enraged fivehooded serpent"?

---

### Shloka 9

#### Shloka Text

निरीक्षमाणस्सहसा वायसं समवैक्षथाः। नखै स्सरुधिरैस्तीक्ष्णैस्तामेवाभिमुखं स्थितम्।।5.67.9।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

निरीक्षमाणः seeing all over, सरुधिरैः with blood, तीक्ष्णैः sharp, नखैः nails, तामेव of her, अभिमुखम् in front of, स्थितम् stood, वायसम् crow, सहसा at once, समवैक्षथाः glanced.

#### Explanation

'Seeing all over, at once I glanced at the crow that stood in front of her with sharp bloodstained nails'.

---

### Shloka 10

#### Shloka Text

सुतः किल स शक्रस्य वायसः पततां वरः। धरान्तरचरश्शीघ्रं पवनस्य गतौ समः।।5.67.10।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

पतताम् among birds, वरः foremost, सः वायसः that crow, शक्रस्य Indra's, पुत्रः son, किल moving about, धरान्तरगतः all over the earth, शीघ्रम् swiftly, गतौ flying, पवनस्य windgod's speed, समः equal.

#### Explanation

'That crow, the foremost of the birds was the son of Indra moving about all over the earth swiftly. His speed matched the Windgod's.

---

### Shloka 11

#### Shloka Text

ततस्तस्मिन्महाबाहो कोपसंवर्तितेक्षणः। वायसे त्वं कृथाः क्रूरां मतिं मतिमतां वर।।5.67.11।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

महाबाहो longarmed one, मतिमताम् very wise one, वर reverred, त्वम् you, कोपसंवर्तितेक्षणः anguished, ततः then, तस्मिन् its, वायसे the crow's, क्रूराम् cruel, मतिम् in your mind, कृथाः decision took.

#### Explanation

'O longarmed, wise and revered Rama, then in anguish you took a cruel decision'.

---

### Shloka 12

#### Shloka Text

स दर्भं संस्तराद्गृह्य ब्रह्मास्त्रेण ह्ययोजयः। स दीप्त इव कालाग्निर्जज्वालाभिमुखः खगम्।।5.67.12।।

#### Transliteration

None

#### Translation

सः he, संस्तरात् from your mat, दर्भम् a blade of grass, गृह्य took out, ब्राह्मेण अस्त्रेण by the mantra of the weapon of Brahma, योजयत् chanted, सः he, दीप्तः blazed, कालाग्निरिव like the fire of the demon's day, द्विजम् bird, अभिमुखः towards the direction, जज्वाल hurled.

#### Explanation

'You took out a blade of grass from your mat and chanted the mantra to invoke the weapon of Brahma (Brahmastra) and hurled it on the crow. It blazed like the fire of the doom's day.

---

### Shloka 13
